pair.old: FOREWORD
pair.new: Foreword

pair.old: As a global car manufacturer focused on building high-quality vehicles with exceptional value, Kia is dedicated to providing you with a customer service experience that exceeds your expectations.
pair.new: As a global car manufacturer focused on building high-quality vehicles with exceptional value, Kia is dedicated to providing a customer service experience that exceeds your expectations.

pair.old: This Owner’s Manual is valid for all variants of your model, and describes all options, features, and equipment available, along with the maintenance needs. This manual may also describe optional equipment not purchased on your vehicle, country specifications, and functions and features not available in your region. Please always keep this manual in the vehicle for your and any subsequent owner’s reference.
pair.new: This Owner’s Manual is valid for all variants of your model. It describes all options, features, and equipment available, along with maintenance needs. This manual may also describe optional equipment not purchased on your vehicle, country specifications, and functions and features not available in your region. Please always keep this manual in the vehicle for your and any subsequent owner’s reference.

pair.old: Authorized Kia Dealerships provide factory-trained technicians, utilize recommended special service tools and supply genuine Kia replacement parts to help you maintain and service your Kia during your ownership.
pair.new: Authorized Kia dealerships provide factory-trained technicians, utilize recommended special service tools, and supply genuine Kia replacement parts to help you maintain and service your Kia during your ownership.

pair.old: Please drive safely and enjoy your Kia vehicle!
pair.new: Please drive safely, and enjoy your Kia vehicle!

pair.old: We want to help you get the greatest possible driving pleasure from your Kia. Your Owner’s Manual can assist you in many ways.
pair.new: We want you to enjoy your vehicle to the fullest. Your Owner’s Manual can assist you in many ways.

pair.old: Illustrations complement the words in this manual to best explain how to enjoy your Kia. By reading your manual, you learn about features, important safety information and driving tips under various road conditions.
pair.new: Illustrations accompany the text in this manual to best explain how to enjoy your Kia vehicle. By reading your manual, you learn about features, important safety information, and driving tips under various road conditions.

pair.old: The general layout of the manual is provided in the Table of Contents. Use the index when looking for a specific area or subject, it has an alphabetical listing of all information in your manual.
pair.new: The table of contents provides the general organization of the manual. Use the index when looking for a specific area or subject; it has an alphabetical listing of all information in your manual.

pair.old: Chapters: This manual has nine chapters plus an index. Each chapter begins with a brief list of contents so you can tell at a glance if that chapter has the information you want.
pair.new: This manual has 9 chapters plus an index. Each chapter begins with a brief list of contents so you can tell at a glance if that chapter has the information you want.

pair.old: You will find various WARNINGS, CAUTIONS, and NOTICES in this manual. These WARNINGS were prepared to enhance your personal safety. You should carefully read and follow ALL procedures and recommendations provided in these WARNINGS, CAUTIONS and NOTICES.
pair.new: You will find various WARNINGS, CAUTIONS, and NOTICES in this manual. These WARNINGS protect your personal safety. You should carefully read and follow ALL procedures and recommendations provided in these WARNINGS, CAUTIONS, and NOTICES.

pair.old: A NOTICE is used to address practices not related to physical injury.
pair.new: A NOTICE addresses practices not related to physical injury.

pair.old: Your new vehicle is designed to use only unleaded fuel having a pump octane number ((R+M)/2) of 87 (Research Octane Number 91) or higher. Do not use methanol blended fuels.
pair.new: Your new vehicle uses only unleaded fuel with a pump octane number ((R+M)/2) of 87 (Research Octane Number 91) or higher. Do not use methanol blended fuels.

pair.old: Your new vehicle is designed to obtain maximum performance with UNLEADED FUEL, as well as minimize exhaust emissions and spark plug fouling.
pair.new: Your new vehicle is designed to obtain maximum performance with UNLEADED FUEL, and minimize exhaust emissions and spark plug fouling.

pair.old: Never add any fuel system cleaning agents to the fuel tank other than what has been specified. (Consult an authorized Kia dealer for details.)
pair.new: Never add any fuel system cleaning agents to the fuel tank other than what has been specified. Consult an authorized Kia dealer for details.

pair.old: Tighten the cap until it clicks one time, otherwise the Check Engine light will illuminate.
pair.new: Tighten the cap until it clicks 1 time. Otherwise the Check Engine light illuminates.

pair.old: Using fuels that contain Silicone (Si), MMT (Methylcyclopentadienyl Manganese Tricarbonyl), Manganese (Mn) contained fuel, and other metallic additives may cause vehicle and engine damage or misfiring, poor acceleration, engine stalling, catalyst melting, clogging, abnormal corrosion, life cycle reduction, etc. Also, the Malfunction Indicator Lamp (MIL) may appear.
pair.new: Using fuels that contain Silicone (Si), Methylcyclopentadienyl Manganese Tricarbonyl (MMT), Manganese (Mn), and other metallic additives may cause vehicle and engine damage or misfiring, poor acceleration, engine stalling, catalyst melting, clogging, abnormal corrosion, life cycle reduction, and so on. The Malfunction Indicator Lamp (MIL) may illuminate if you use fuel with the additives listed.

pair.old: Fuels containing methanol (wood alcohol) should not be used in your vehicle. This type of fuel can reduce vehicle performance and damage components of the fuel system, engine control system and emission control system.
pair.new: Fuels containing methanol (wood alcohol) should not be used in your vehicle. This type of fuel can reduce vehicle performance and damage components of the fuel system, engine control system, and emission control system.

pair.old: Kia recommends that you use good quality gasolines treated with detergent additives such as TOP TIER detergent gasoline, which help prevent deposit formation in the engine. These gasolines will help the engine run cleaner and enhance performance of the emission control system.
pair.new: Kia recommends that you use good quality gasolines treated with detergent additives, such as TOP TIER detergent gasoline, which help prevent deposit formation in the engine. These gasolines will help the engine run cleaner and enhance performance of the emission control system.

pair.old: For more information on TOP TIER detergent gasoline, please go to the website (). For customers who do not use TOP TIER detergent gasoline regularly, and have problems starting or the engine does not run smoothly, additives that you can buy separately may be added to the gasoline.
pair.new: For more information on TOP TIER detergent gasoline, please go to the website (). For customers who do not use TOP TIER detergent gasoline regularly and have problems starting or running the engine smoothly, you can buy separative additives for the gasoline.

pair.old: If TOP TIER detergent gasoline is not available, one bottle of additive should be added to the fuel tank at every 8,000 miles (13,000 km) or every engine oil change. is recommended Additives are available from your authorized Kia dealer along with information on how to use them. Do not mix other additives.
pair.new: If TOP TIER detergent gasoline is not available, 1 bottle of additive should be added to the fuel tank every 8,000 miles (13,000 km) or every engine oil change. Additives are available from your authorized Kia dealer along with information on how to use them. Do not mix other additives.

pair.old: This vehicle should not be modified. Modification of your vehicle could affect its performance, safety or durability and may even violate governmental safety and emissions regulations.
pair.new: This vehicle should not be modified. Modification of your vehicle could affect its performance, safety, or durability and may even violate governmental safety and emissions regulations.

pair.old: In addition, damage or performance problems resulting from any modification may not be covered under the warranty.
pair.new: Damage or performance problems resulting from any modification may not be covered under the warranty.

pair.old: If you use an unauthorized electronic device, it may cause the vehicle to operate abnormally, wire damage, battery discharge and fire. For your safety, do not use unauthorized electronic devices.
pair.new: If you use an unauthorized electronic device, it may cause wire damage, battery discharge, fire, or abnormal vehicle operation. For your safety, do not use unauthorized electronic devices.

pair.old: Vehicle break-in process
pair.new: Breaking in your vehicle

pair.old: By following a few simple precautions for the first 600 miles (1,000 km) you may increase performance, economy and life of your vehicle.
pair.new: Following these simple precautions for the first 600 miles (1,000 km) may increase the performance, economy, and lifespan of your vehicle:

pair.old: While driving, avoid sudden acceleration
pair.new: Avoid sudden acceleration.

pair.old: Do not maintain a single engine speed for long periods of time, either fast or slow. Varying engine speeds is needed to properly break-in the engine.
pair.new: Do not maintain a single engine speed, either fast or slow, for long periods of time. Varying engine speeds is necessary to properly break in the engine.

pair.old: Fuel economy and engine performance may vary depending on your break-in process and stabilize after 4,000 miles (6,000 km). Engines may consume more oil during the break-in period.
pair.new: Depending on your break-in process, fuel economy and engine performance may vary and will stabilize after 4,000 miles (6,000 km). The engine may consume more oil during the break-in period.

pair.old: Risk of burns when parking or stopping vehicle
pair.new: Risk of burns when parking or stopping your vehicle

pair.old: When an engine idles at high speed with the rear side of the vehicle touching the wall, heat of the exhaust gas can cause fire and/or discoloration. Keep enough space between the rear part of the vehicle and the wall.
pair.new: When an engine idles at high speed with the rear side of the vehicle touching the wall, heat from the exhaust gas can cause fire or discoloration. Keep enough space between the rear part of the vehicle and the wall.

pair.old: Do not touch the exhaust/catalytic systems while engine is running or right after the engine is turned off. There is a risk of burns since the systems are extremely hot.
pair.new: Do not touch the exhaust or catalytic systems while the engine is running or has just been turned OFF. Because the systems are extremely hot, there is a risk of burns.

pair.old: As with all vehicles of this type, failure to operate this vehicle correctly may result in loss of control, an accident or vehicle rollover.
pair.new: As with all vehicles of this type, failure to operate this vehicle correctly may result in loss of control, an accident, or vehicle rollover.

pair.old: Specific design characteristics (higher ground clearance, track, etc.) give this vehicle a higher center of gravity than other types of vehicles. In other words, this vehicle is not designed for cornering at the same speeds as conventional 2-wheel drive vehicles.
pair.new: Specific design characteristics, such as higher ground clearance and track, give this vehicle a higher center of gravity than other types of vehicles. This vehicle is not designed for turning at the same speeds as conventional 2-wheel drive vehicles.

pair.old: Avoid sharp turns or abrupt maneuvers. Failure to operate this vehicle correctly may result in loss of control, an accident or vehicle rollover.
pair.new: Avoid sharp turns and abrupt maneuvers. Failure to operate this vehicle correctly may result in loss of control, an accident, or vehicle rollover.

pair.old: Outside rear view mirror
pair.new: Outside rearview mirror

pair.old: Wide-rear view camera
pair.new: Wide rearview camera

pair.old: Reversing lamp
pair.new: backup lamp

pair.old: Outside rear view mirror folding switch
pair.new: Outside rearview mirror folding switch

pair.old: Outside rear view mirror control switch
pair.new: Outside rearview mirror control switch

pair.old: Steering wheel tilt/telescopic lever/switch
pair.new: Tilt and telescopic steering wheel lever and switch

pair.old: Engine compartment
pair.new: Engine compartment overview

pair.old: Safety features of your Kia
pair.new: Safety features of your vehicle

pair.old: For the safety of the driver and passengers, you should become familiar with the vehicle’s safety features.
pair.new: For the safety of the driver and vehicle passengers, familiarize yourself with the vehicle’s safety features.

pair.old: You will find many safety precautions and recommendations throughout this section, and throughout this manual.
pair.new: You will find many safety precautions and recommendations throughout this section and manual.

pair.old: All children under age 13 should ride in your Kia properly restrained in a rear seat, not the front seat. Each infants and small children should be restrained in an appropriate child restraint. Larger children should use a booster seat with the lap/shoulder belt until they can use the seat belt properly without a booster seat. Please refer to applicable State and Federal laws for child seating requirements.
pair.new: All children under age 13 should be properly restrained in a rear seat, not the front seat. Each infant and small child should be restrained in an appropriate child restraint. Larger children should use a booster seat with the lap/shoulder belt until they can use the seat belt properly without a booster seat. Please refer to applicable State and Federal laws for child seating requirements.

pair.old: Driver distraction presents a serious and potentially deadly danger, especially for inexperienced drivers. Safety should be the first concern when behind the wheel and drivers need to be aware of the wide array of potential distractions, such as drowsiness, reaching for objects, eating, personal grooming, other passengers, and using mobile phones.
pair.new: Driver distraction presents a serious and potentially deadly danger, especially for inexperienced drivers. Safety should be the first concern when behind the wheel, and drivers need to be aware of the wide array of potential distractions, such as drowsiness, reaching for objects, eating, personal grooming, other passengers, and using mobile phones.

pair.old: ALWAYS set up your mobile devices (i.e., MP3 players, phones, navigation units, etc.) when your vehicle is parked or safely stopped.
pair.new: ALWAYS set up your mobile devices (such as MP3 players, phones, navigation units, and so on) when your vehicle is parked or safely stopped.

pair.old: ONLY use your mobile device when allowed by laws and when conditions permit safe use. NEVER text or email while driving. Most states have laws prohibiting drivers from texting. Some states and cities also prohibit drivers from using handheld phones.
pair.new: ONLY use your mobile device when allowed by law, and when conditions permit safe use. NEVER text or email while driving. Most states have laws prohibiting drivers from texting. Some states and cities also prohibit drivers from using handheld phones.

pair.old: NEVER let the use of a mobile device distract you from driving. You have a responsibility to your passengers and others on the road to always drive safely, with your hands on the wheel as well as your eyes and attention on the road.
pair.new: NEVER let the use of a mobile device distract you from driving. You have a responsibility to your passengers and others on the road to always drive safely, with your hands on the wheel and your eyes and attention on the road.

pair.old: Excessive speed is a major factor in crash injuries and deaths. Generally, the higher the speed, the greater the risk, but serious injuries can also occur at lower speeds. Never drive faster what is safe for current driving conditions, regardless of the maximum speed posted.
pair.new: Excessive speed is a major factor in crash injuries and deaths. Generally, the higher the speed, the greater the risk. Serious injuries can also occur at lower speeds. Never drive faster than what is safe for current driving conditions, regardless of the maximum speed posted.

pair.old: Having a tire blowout or a mechanical failure can be extremely hazardous. To reduce the possibility of such problems, check your tire pressures and conditions frequently, and perform all regularly scheduled maintenance.
pair.new: Having a tire blowout or a mechanical failure can be extremely hazardous. To reduce the possibility of such problems, check your tire pressure and condition frequently, and perform all regularly scheduled maintenance.

pair.old: There is no bench seat in the 2nd-row with vehicles equipped with 6 seating positions.
pair.new: Vehicles with 6 seating positions do not have a 2nd-row bench seat.

pair.old: Cushion extension (Driver’s seat)
pair.new: Cushion extension (driver’s seat)

pair.old: The actual feature in the vehicle may differ from the illustration.
pair.new: The actual features may differ from the illustration.

pair.old: There is no bench seat on rear 2nd-row with vehicle equipped with 6 seating positions.
pair.new: Vehicles with 6 seating positions do not have a 2nd-row bench seat.

pair.old: 2nd row seat
pair.new: 2nd row seat:

pair.old: 3rd row seat
pair.new: 3rd row seat:

pair.old: Uprighting a seat
pair.new: Raising a seat upright

pair.old: The driver must advise the passengers to keep seatbacks in upright positions whenever the vehicle is in motion. If a seat is reclined during an accident, the restraint system’s ability to restrain the passenger will be greatly reduced.
pair.new: The driver must advise the passengers to keep seatbacks in upright positions whenever the vehicle is in motion. If a seat is reclined during an accident, the restraint system’s ability to restrain the passenger is greatly reduced.

pair.old: Occupants should never sit on aftermarket seat cushions or sitting cushions. The passenger Occupant Detection System (ODS) may not operate properly, or the passenger’s hips may slide under the lap portion of the seat belt during an accident or a sudden stop.
pair.new: Occupants should never sit on aftermarket seat cushions. The passenger Occupant Detection System (ODS) may not operate properly, or the passenger’s hips may slide under the lap portion of the seat belt during an accident or a sudden stop.

pair.old: Never attempt to adjust the seat while the vehicle is moving. This could result in loss of control of your Kia.
pair.new: Never attempt to adjust the seat while the vehicle is moving. This could result in loss of control of your vehicle.

pair.old: Do not adjust the seat while wearing seat belts. Moving the seat forward will cause strong pressure on the abdomen.
pair.new: Do not adjust the seat while wearing seat belts. Moving the seat forward puts strong pressure on the abdomen.

pair.old: Do not stack or pile luggage or cargo higher than the seatback in the cargo area. In an accident the cargo could strike and injure a passenger. If objects are large, heavy or must be piled, they must be secured in the cargo area.
pair.new: Do not stack or pile luggage or cargo higher than the seatback in the cargo area. In an accident, the cargo could strike and injure a passenger. If objects are large, heavy, or must be piled, secure them in the cargo area.

pair.old: Be aware of wires or air vents when placing a seat cover or covering the seat with plastic cover. Short circuits and blocked vents can cause damage and could lead to fire.
pair.new: Be aware of wires or air vents when placing a seat cover or covering the seat with plastic. Short circuits and blocked vents can cause damage and could lead to fire.

pair.old: Feature of Seat Leather
pair.new: Seat leather

pair.old: Your car seats are upholstered with a combination of artificial and genuine leather. Genuine leather is made from the skin of an animal, which under goes a special process for use. Since it is a natural substance, each part differs in thickness or density. Wrinkles could appear depending on the temperature and humidity.
pair.new: Your car seats are upholstered with a combination of artificial and genuine leather. Genuine leather is made from the skin of an animal, which undergoes a special process for use. Since it is a natural substance, each part differs in thickness or density. Wrinkles could appear depending on the temperature and humidity.

pair.old: The seats are curved and the side supporting areas high, which provides driving comfort and stability.
pair.new: The seats are curved and the side supporting areas are high, which provides driving comfort and stability.

pair.old: Belts with metallic accessories, zippers and keys, may damage the seat fabric.
pair.new: Belts with metallic accessories, zippers, and keys may damage the seat fabric.

pair.old: Make sure not to get the seat wet. It may change the nature of the leather.
pair.new: Make sure not to get the seat wet. It may damage the leather.

pair.old: Bleach on clothes may contaminate the surface of the seat covering fabric.
pair.new: Bleach on clothes may stain the leather.

pair.old: The front seat can be adjusted by using the control levers located on the outside of the seat cushion.
pair.new: Use the control levers on the outside of the seat cushion to adjust the front seat.

pair.old: Adjust the seat before driving, and make sure the seat is locked securely by trying to move forward and backward without using the lever. If the seat moves, it is not locked properly.
pair.new: Adjust the seat before driving. Make sure the seat is locked securely by trying to move forward and backward without using the lever. If the seat moves, it is not locked properly.
Adjust the seat before driving. Make sure the seat is locked securely by trying to move forward and backward without using the lever. If the seat moves, it is not locked properly.

old.old: To recline the seatback:

new.new: To recline the seatback:

pair.old: Carefully lean back on the seat and adjust the seatback of the seat to the desired position.
pair.new: Carefully lean back on the seat and adjust the seatback to the desired position.

pair.old: To change the height of the seat cushion, push the lever upwards or downwards.
pair.new: To change the height of the seat cushion:

pair.old: To lower the seat cushion, push down the lever several times.
pair.new: To lower the seat cushion, push the lever down several times.

pair.old: To raise the seat cushion, pull up the lever several times.
pair.new: To raise the seat cushion, pull the lever up several times.

pair.old: The lumbar support can be adjusted by pressing the lumbar support switch on the side of the seat.
pair.new: Adjust the lumbar support by pressing the lumbar support switch on the side of the seat.
Adjust the lumbar support by pressing the lumbar support switch on the side of the seat.

pair.old: Press the front portion of the switch to increase support, or the rear portion of the switch, to decrease support.
pair.new: Press the front portion of the switch to increase support, or the rear portion of the switch to decrease support.
Press the front portion of the switch to increase support, or the rear portion of the switch to decrease support.

pair.old: The driver’s seat can be adjusted by using the control switches located on the outside of the seat cushion. Before driving, adjust the seat to the proper position so as to easily control the steering wheel, pedals and switches on the instrument panel.
pair.new: You can adjust the driver’s seat by using the control switches located on the outside of the seat cushion. Before driving, adjust the seat to the proper position so you can easily control the steering wheel, pedals, and switches on the instrument panel.

pair.old: Do not operate two or more power seat control switches at the same time. Damage to the power seat motor or electrical components may occur.
pair.new: Do not operate 2 or more power seat control switches at the same time. This may cause damage to the power seat motor or electrical components.

pair.old: Pull the rear portion of the control switch up to raise or press down to lower the seat cushion.
pair.new: Pull the rear portion of the control switch up to raise or press down to lower the rear part of the seat cushion.

pair.old: Press the front portion of the switch to raise the cushion extension and the rear portion of the switch to lower it.
pair.new: Press the front portion of the switch to move the cushion extension forward and the rear portion of the switch to move it back.

pair.old: Pull up the seatback recline lever.
pair.new: Lift the seatback recliner lever.

pair.old: Hold the lever and adjust the seatback of the seat to the position you desire.
pair.new: Hold the lever and adjust the seatback to the desired position.

pair.old: Walk-in seat (2nd row seat)
pair.new: Walk-in seat (3rd row seat)

pair.old: Route the seat belt webbing through the rear seat belt guide clip. After inserting the seat belt, tighten the belt webbing by pulling it up.
pair.new: Route the seat belt webbing through the 2nd row seat belt guide clip. Insert the seat belt and tighten the belt webbing by pulling it up.

pair.old: The 2nd row seat can also be unlocked by pulling the strap located beneath the 2nd row seat seatback. If the strap is located beneath the 2nd row seatback, there is a label attached to show where the strap is located.
pair.new: The 2nd row seat can also be unlocked by pulling the strap located beneath the 2nd row seat seatback. There is a label attached to the strap to show where it is located.

pair.old: Never attempt to adjust using the 2nd row seat walk-in switch or strap while the vehicle is moving or when the seat is occupied as the seat may suddenly move and cause the passenger in the seat to be injured.
pair.new: Never attempt to adjust using the 2nd row seat walk-in switch or strap while the vehicle is moving or when the seat is occupied, as the seat may suddenly move and cause injury to the passenger.

pair.old: The 2nd row seatback will be folded and push the seat to the farthest forward position.
pair.new: The 2nd row seatback will fold and push the seat to the farthest forward position.

pair.old: Sudden movement of the seats may result in injury. Use the strap only when the folding switch in the 2nd row (in the upper part of the second-row seatback or the outer part of the seat) does not work.
pair.new: Sudden movement of the seats may result in injury. Only use the strap when the folding switch in the 2nd row does not work. The folding switch is in the upper part of the 2nd row seatback or the outer part of the seat

pair.old: Insert the rear seat belt buckle in the pocket between the rear seatback and cushion and insert the rear seat belt webbing in the guide to prevent the seat belt from being damaged.
pair.new: Insert the rear seat belt buckle in the pocket between the rear seatback and cushion, and insert the rear seat belt webbing in the guide to prevent the seat belt from being damaged.

pair.old: Set the front seatback to the upright position and if necessary, slide the front seat forward.
pair.new: Set the front seatback to the upright position and, if necessary, slide the front seat forward.

pair.old: Objects carried on the folded down seatback should not extend higher than the top of the front seatbacks. This could allow cargo to slide forward and cause injury or damage during sudden stops.
pair.new: Objects carried on the folded down seatback should not extend higher than the top of the front seatbacks. Cargo that extends higher than the top of the front seatbacks could slide forward and cause injury or damage during sudden stops.

pair.old: Pull on the seatback folding lever (for 2nd row) or strap (for 3rd row), then fold the seat toward the front of the vehicle. When you return the seatback to its upright position, always be sure it has locked into position by pushing on the top of the seatback.
pair.new: Pull the seatback folding lever (for 2nd row) or strap (for 3rd row), then fold the seat toward the front of the vehicle.

pair.old: 2nd row seat folding switch is located on the right side of the 3rd row seat.
pair.new: The 2nd row seat folding switch is located on the right side of the 3rd row seat.

pair.old: The 2nd row seat back will be folded.
pair.new: The 2nd row seat back will fold.

pair.old: If you press the left switch, left side seat back and center seat back will be folded.
pair.new: If you press the left switch, the left side seat back and center seat back will fold.

pair.old: If you press the right switch, right side seat back will be folded.
pair.new: If you press the right switch, the right side seat back will fold.

pair.old: Do not fold the rear seatbacks down until the driver has positioned his seat properly. Doing so can cause the driver to operate the vehicle out of position and increase the risk of bodily injury in a sudden stop or collision.
pair.new: Do not fold the rear seatbacks down until the driver’s seat is positioned properly. Failure to heed this warning could cause the driver to operate the vehicle with the seat out of position, which increases the risk of bodily injury in a sudden stop or collision.

pair.old: Uprighting the seatback
pair.new: Raising the seatback upright

pair.old: When you return the seatback to its upright position, hold the seatback and return it slowly. If the seatback is returned without holding it, the back of the seat could spring forward, resulting in injury caused by being struck by the seatback.
pair.new: When you return the seatback to its upright position, hold the seatback and return it slowly. If the seatback is returned without holding it, the back of the seat could spring forward, striking the driver or passengers and resulting in injury.

pair.old: Do not allow the seat belt webbing or buckle to become pinched or caught in the rear seat.
pair.new: Do not allow the rear seat to pinch or catch the seat belt webbing or buckle.

pair.old: When you fold the rear seatback, insert the buckle between the rear seatback and cushion. Doing so can prevent the buckle from being damaged by the rear seatback.
pair.new: When you fold the rear seatback, insert the buckle between the rear seatback and cushion. Doing so can prevent the rear seatback from damaging the buckle.

pair.old: Cargo should always be secured to prevent it from being thrown about the vehicle in a collision and causing injury to the vehicle occupants. Do not place objects in the rear seats, since they cannot be properly secured and may hit the front seat occupants in a collision.
pair.new: Always secure cargo to prevent it from being thrown about the vehicle in a collision and causing injury to the vehicle occupants. Do not place objects in the rear seats, since they cannot be properly secured and may hit the front seat occupants in a collision.

pair.old: Make sure the engine is off, the shift lever is in P (Park) and the parking brake is securely applied whenever loading or unloading cargo. Failure to take these steps may allow the vehicle to move if the shift lever is inadvertently moved to another position.
pair.new: Make sure the engine is OFF, the shift lever is set to P (Park), and the parking brake is applied when loading or unloading cargo. Failure to take these steps may allow the vehicle to move if the shift lever is inadvertently set to a different gear.

pair.old: Headrest (for front seat)
pair.new: Front seat headrest

pair.old: Make sure the headrest locks in position after adjusting it to properly protect the occupants.
pair.new: To properly protect the occupants, make sure the headrest locks in position after adjusting it.

pair.old: If you recline the seatback towards the front with the headrest and seat cushion raised, the headrest may contact the sun visor or other parts of the vehicle.
pair.new: If you angle the seatback forward with the headrest and seat cushion raised, the headrest may contact the sun visor or other parts of the vehicle.

pair.old: The headrest may be adjusted forward to 3 different positions by pulling the headrest forward to the desired position.
pair.new: Adjust the headrest by pulling it forward to 1 of 3 different positions.

pair.old: To adjust the headrest to its furthest backwards position, pull it fully forward to the farthest position and release it.
pair.new: To adjust the headrest as far back as it will go, pull it all the way forward and release it.

pair.old: Raise headrest as far as it can go.
pair.new: Raise the headrest as far as it can go.

pair.old: Headrest (for rear seat)
pair.new: Rear seat headrest

pair.old: To maximize the effectiveness in case of accidents, the headrest should be adjusted so the middle of the headrest is as high as the center of gravity of an occupant’s head. Generally, the center of gravity of most people’s heads is similar with the height as the top of their eyes.
pair.new: To maximize effectiveness in case of accidents, the headrest should be adjusted so the middle of the headrest is level with the center of gravity of an occupant’s head. Generally, the center of gravity of most people’s heads is at the same height as the top of their eyes.

pair.old: Adjust the headrest as close to your head as possible. For this reason, the use of a cushion that holds the body away from the seatback is not recommended.
pair.new: Adjust the headrest as close to your head as possible. For this reason, we do not recommend using a cushion that holds your body away from the seatback.

old.title: Adjusting the height up and down (2nd row seat)
old.old: To raise the headrest, pull it up to the desired position (1). To lower the headrest, push and hold the release button (3) on the headrest support and lower the headrest to the desired position (2).

new.title: Adjusting the height up and down (2nd row seat)
new.new: To raise the headrest, pull it up to the desired position (1). To lower the headrest, push and hold the release button (3) on the headrest support and lower the headrest to the desired position (2).

pair.old: Adjustable armrest is located on each side of 2nd row seatback. To use the armrest, pull it forward from the seatback and adjust to desired angle. Pull it backward to relocate the armrest.
pair.new: An adjustable armrest is located on each side of 2nd row seatback. To use the armrest, pull it forward from the seatback and adjust it to desired angle. Pull the armrest backward to lock it in place.

pair.old: There is a double pocket (1) in the front seat back for storing simple books or atlases, and USB charger (2) (if equipped) for rear passengers.
pair.new: There is a double pocket (1) in the front seatback for storing simple books or atlases, as well as a USB charger (2) (if equipped) for rear passengers.

pair.old: Do not put heavy, stiff or sharp objects in the seatback pockets. In an accident they could contact and injure vehicle occupants.
pair.new: Do not put heavy, stiff, or sharp objects in the seatback pockets. In an accident, they could contact and injure vehicle occupants.

pair.old: There is a small pocket in the 2nd row seat cushion for storing simple objects.
pair.new: There is a small pocket in the 2nd row seat cushion for storing smaller objects.

pair.old: Seat belts are designed to bear upon the bony structure of the body and should be worn low across the front of the pelvis, chest and shoulders.
pair.new: Seat belts are designed to bear upon the skeletal structure of the body and should be worn low across the hips, chest, and shoulders.

pair.old: For maximum restraint system protection, the seat belts must always be used whenever the vehicle is moving.
pair.new: For maximum protection from the seat belts, always wear them when the vehicle is moving:

pair.old: A properly positioned shoulder belt should be positioned midway over your shoulder, across your collarbone.
pair.new: The proper position for the shoulder belt is midway over your shoulder and across your collarbone.

pair.old: Never wear the shoulder belt under your arm or behind your back. An improperly positioned shoulder belt cannot protect the occupant in the event of a crash.
pair.new: Never wear the shoulder belt under your arm or behind your back. An improperly positioned shoulder belt cannot protect you in the event of a crash.

pair.old: Any damage to webbing or hardware may cause serious injury or death in an accident. The entire seat belt assembly should be replace dwhen any part of the webbing or hardware is damaged.
pair.new: Any damage to webbing or hardware may cause serious injury or death in an accident. The entire seat belt assembly should be replaced when any part of the webbing or hardware is damaged.

pair.old: Seat belts should be adjusted as firmly as possible, consistent with comfort, to provide the protection for which they have been designed.
pair.new: Seat belts should be adjusted as firmly as possible, consistent with comfort, to provide maximum protection.

pair.old: A slack belt will greatly reduce the protection afforded to the wearer.
pair.new: A slack belt greatly reduces the protection afforded to the wearer.

pair.old: Care should be taken to avoid contamination of the webbing with polishes, oils and chemicals, and particularly battery acid. Use mild soap and water. The belt should be replaced if webbing becomes frayed, contaminated or damaged.
pair.new: Take care to avoid contaminating the webbing with polishes, oils, and chemicals, particularly battery acid. Use mild soap and water to clean the belt. Replace the belt if the webbing becomes frayed, contaminated, or damaged:

pair.old: No modifications or additions should be made by the user which could either prevent the seat belt adjusting devices from operating to remove slack or prevent the seat belt assembly from being adjusted to remove slack.
pair.new: Do not make any modifications or additions that could either prevent the seat belt adjusting devices from operating to remove slack or prevent the seat belt assembly from being adjusted to remove slack.

pair.old: When you fasten the seat belt, be careful not to latch the seat belt in buckles of other seats. It is very dangerous and you may not be properly protected.
pair.new: When you fasten the seat belt, be careful not to latch it in the buckles of other seats. It is very dangerous and you may not be properly protected.

pair.old: Do not fasten and unfasten the seat belt repeatedly while driving. This could cause loss of control, and an accident causing death, serious injury, or property damage.
pair.new: Do not fasten and unfasten the seat belt repeatedly while driving. This could cause loss of control and an accident resulting in death, serious injury, or property damage.

pair.old: Do not allow foreign material (gum, crumbs, coins, liquids, etc.) to obstruct the seat belt buckle orifice. This may prevent the seat belt from fastening securely.
pair.new: Do not allow foreign material (gum, crumbs, coins, liquids, and so on) to obstruct the seat belt buckle receptor. This may prevent the seat belt from fastening securely.

pair.old: As a reminder to the driver, the seat belt warning light will appear for approximately 6 seconds each time you turn the ENGINE START/STOP button ON regardless of belt fastening. If the seatbelt is not fastened, the warning chime will sound for about 6 seconds.
pair.new: As a reminder to the driver, the seat belt warning light displays for approximately 6 seconds each time you turn the ENGINE START/STOP button ON, regardless of whether the belt is fastened. If you do not fasten the seat belt, the warning chime sounds for about 6 seconds.

pair.old: If you start to drive without the seat belt fastened over approximately 5 mph (9 km/h) and less than approximately 12 mph (20 km/h), the corresponding warning light will appear. The warning light will turn off when the vehicle speed drops below approximately 5 mph (9 km/h).
pair.new: If you start to drive between approximately 5 mph (9 km/h) and 12 mph (20 km/h) without the seat belt fastened, the corresponding warning light appears. The warning light turns off when the vehicle speed drops below approximately 5 mph (9 km/h).

pair.old: As a reminder to the front passenger, the seat belt warning light will appear for approximately 6 seconds each time you turn the ENGINE START/STOP button ON regardless of belt fastening. If you start to drive without the passenger seat belt fastened when you drive over approximately 5 mph (9 km/h) and less than approximately 12 mph (20 km/h), the corresponding warning light will appear. The warning light will turn off when the vehicle speed drops approximately below 5 mph (9 km/h).
pair.new: As a reminder to the front passenger, the seat belt warning light appears for approximately 6 seconds each time you turn the ENGINE START/STOP button ON, regardless of whether the belt is fastened. If you start to drive between approximately 5 mph (9 km/h) and 12 mph (20 km/h) without the front passenger seat belt fastened, the corresponding warning light appears. The warning light turns off when the vehicle speed drops below approximately 5 mph (9 km/h).

pair.old: If you start to drive without the passenger seat belt fastened or you unfasten the seat belt when you drive approximately 12 mph (20 km/h) and faster, the warning light will blink and warning chime will sound for approximately 100 seconds. When the passenger seat belt is unfastened during driving, the warning light will appear when the speed is over approximately 5mph (9 km/h) and less than approximately 12 mph (20 km/h). When the speed is approximately 12 mph (20 km/h) and faster, the warning light will blink and warning chime will sound for approximately 100 seconds.
pair.new: If you start driving approximately 12 mph (20 km/h) or faster without the passenger seat belt fastened, or if the passenger seat belt is unfastened while driving, the warning light blinks, and the warning chime sounds for approximately 100 seconds.

pair.old: As a reminder to the rear passenger, the rear passenger’s seat belt warning lights will appear for approximately 6 seconds each time you turn the ignition switch ON regardless of belt fastening.
pair.new: As a reminder to the rear passengers, the seat belt warning light appears for approximately 6 seconds each time you turn the ENGINE START/STOP button ON, regardless of whether the belt is fastened.

pair.old: If you start to drive without the seat belt fastened, the seat belt warning light will blink for approximately 70 seconds.
pair.new: If you start to drive without the seat belt fastened, the seat belt warning light blinks for approximately 70 seconds.

pair.old: If you unfasten the seat belt when you drive under 12 mph (20 km/h), the seat belt warning light will blink for approximately 70 seconds.
pair.new: If you unfasten the seat belt when you drive under 12 mph (20 km/h), the seat belt warning light blinks for approximately 70 seconds.

pair.old: The seat belt automatically adjusts to the proper length only after the lap belt portion is adjusted manually so that it fits snugly around your hips. If you lean forward in a slow, easy motion, the belt will extend and let you move around. If there is a sudden stop or impact, however, the belt will lock into position. It will also lock if you try to lean forward too quickly.
pair.new: The seat belt automatically adjusts to the proper length only after you manually adjust the lap belt portion so that it fits snugly around your hips. If you lean forward in a slow, easy motion, the belt will extend and let you move around. If there is a sudden stop or impact, however, the belt will lock into position. It will also lock if you try to lean forward too quickly.

pair.old: You should place the lap belt portion as low as possible and snugly across your hips. If the lap belt is located too high on your waist, it may increase the chance of injury in the event of a collision.
pair.new: Place the lap belt portion as low as possible and snugly across your hips. If the lap belt is located too high on your waist, it may increase the chance of injury in the event of a collision.

pair.old: The arm closest to the seat belt buckle should be over the belt while the other arm should be under the belt as shown in the illustration. Never wear the seat belt under the arm closest to the door.
pair.new: The arm closest to the seat belt buckle should be over the belt, while the other arm should be under the belt, as shown in the illustration. Never wear the seat belt under the arm closest to the door.

pair.old: You can adjust the height of the shoulder belt anchor to one of 4 positions for maximum comfort and safety.
pair.new: You can adjust the height of the shoulder belt anchor to 1 of 4 positions for maximum comfort and safety.

pair.old: The height of the adjusting seat belt should not be too close to your neck. You will not be getting the most effective protection. The shoulder portion should be adjusted so that it lies across your chest and midway over your shoulder near the door and not your neck.
pair.new: The height of the adjusted seat belt should not be too close to your neck, or you will not get the most effective protection. The shoulder portion should be adjusted so that it lies across your chest and midway over your shoulder near the door, not your neck.

pair.old: To adjust the height of the seat belt anchor, lower or raise the height adjuster into an appropriate position.
pair.new: To adjust the height of the seat belt anchor, lower or raise the height adjuster into the appropriate position:

pair.old: Verify the shoulder belt anchor is locked into position at the appropriate height. Never position the shoulder belt across your neck or face. Improperly positioned seat belts can cause serious injuries in an accident.
pair.new: Verify that the shoulder belt anchor is locked into position at the appropriate height. Never position the shoulder belt across your neck or face. Improperly positioned seat belts can cause serious injuries in an accident.

pair.old: After a collision, the seat belt system should be inspected to ensure it is operating normally. Replace any belts that are not functioning appropriately.
pair.new: After a collision, the seat belt system should be inspected to ensure that it is operating normally. Replace any belts that are not functioning properly.

pair.old: The seat belt should be locked into the buckle on each seat cushion to be properly buckled.
pair.new: A properly buckled seat belt will be locked into the buckle on each seat cushion.

pair.old: Prior to buckling the rear seat belts, ensure the latch matches the seat belt buckle. Forcefully connecting the left or right seat belt to the center buckle will not secure the occupant in an accident.
pair.new: Prior to buckling the rear seat belts, ensure that the latch matches the seat belt buckle. Forcefully connecting the left or right seat belt to the center buckle will not secure the occupant in an accident.

pair.old: The rear seat belt buckles can be stowed in the pocket between the rear seatback and cushion when not in use.
pair.new: The rear seat belt buckles can be stowed in the pocket between the rear seatback and cushion when not in use:

pair.old: Route the seat belt webbing through the rear seat belt guides. It will help keep the belts from being trapped behind or under the seats.
pair.new: Route the seat belt webbing through the rear seat belt guides. This will help keep the belts from being trapped behind or under the seats.

pair.old: When pulling out to wear the seat belt, the tongue should be slowly pulled out of the seat belt guide so that the seat belt guide does not come off the trim.
pair.new: When pulling out the seat belt to wear it, slowly pull the tab out of the seat belt guide so that the guide does not come off the trim.

pair.old: Your Kia is equipped with pre-tensioner seat belts for the front driver and passenger, as well as the second row passengers.
pair.new: Your vehicle is equipped with pretensioner seat belts for the front driver and passenger, as well as the 2nd row passengers.

pair.old: The purpose of the pre-tensioner is to make sure that the seat belts fit tightly against the occupant’s body in certain collisions.
pair.new: The purpose of the pretensioner is to make sure that the seat belt fits tightly against the occupant’s body in certain collisions.

pair.old: The pre-tensioner seat belts may be activated in crashes where the collision is severe enough.
pair.new: The pretensioner seat belts may activate in crashes where the collision is severe enough.

pair.old: When the vehicle stops suddenly, or if the occupant tries to lean forward too quickly, the seat belt retractor will lock into position. In certain frontal collisions, the pre-tensioner will activate and pull the seat belt into tighter contact against the occupant’s body.
pair.new: When the vehicle stops suddenly, or if the occupant tries to lean forward too quickly, the seat belt retractor will lock into position. In certain frontal collisions, the pretensioner will activate and pull the seat belt into tighter contact against the occupant’s body.

pair.old: If the system senses excessive tension on the driver or passenger’s seat belt when the pre-tensioner system activates, the load limiter inside the retractor pre-tensioner will release some of the pressure on the affected seat belt.
pair.new: If the system senses excessive tension on the driver’s or passenger’s seat belt when the pretensioner system activates, the load limiter inside the retractor pretensioner will release some of the pressure on the affected seat belt.

pair.old: For your safety, be sure that the belt webbing is not loose or twisted and always sit properly on your seat.
pair.new: For your safety, be sure that the belt webbing is not loose or twisted, and always sit properly on your seat.

pair.old: The pre-tensioner may activate not only in a frontal collision but also in a side collision, if the vehicle is equipped with a side or curtain air bag.
pair.new: The pretensioner may activate in both a frontal or side collision if the vehicle is equipped with a side or curtain air bag.

pair.old: The seat belt pre-tensioner system consists mainly of the following components. Their locations are shown in the illustration:
pair.new: The seat belt pretensioner system consists of the following components. Their locations are shown in the illustration:

pair.old: Front retractor pre-tensioner assembly
pair.new: Front retractor pretensioner assembly

pair.old: Wash all exposed skin areas thoroughly after an accident in which the pre-tensioner seat belts were activated. The fine dust from the pre-tensioner activation may cause skin irritation and should not be inhaled for prolonged periods.
pair.new: Wash all exposed skin areas thoroughly after an accident in which the pretensioner seat belts were activated. The fine dust from the pretensioner activation may cause skin irritation and should not be inhaled for prolonged periods.

pair.old: Both the driver’s and front passenger’s seat belt pre-tensioner systems may be activated not only in certain frontal collisions, but also in certain side collisions or rollovers, if the vehicle is equipped with a side or curtain air bag.
pair.new: Both the driver’s and front passenger’s seat belt pretensioner systems may be activated not only in certain frontal collisions, but also in certain side collisions or rollovers if the vehicle is equipped with a side or curtain air bag.

pair.old: Because the sensor that activates the SRS air bag is connected with the pre-tensioner seat belt, the SRS air bag warning light on the instrument panel will appear for approximately 3 ~ 6 seconds after the ENGINE START/STOP button has been turned to the ON position, and then it should turn off.
pair.new: Because the sensor that activates the SRS air bag is connected with the pretensioner seat belt, the SRS air bag warning light on the instrument panel will appear for approximately 3–6 seconds after the ENGINE START/STOP button has been turned to the ON position. Then it should turn off.

pair.old: If the pre-tensioner seat belt system is not working properly, this warning light will appear even if there is not a malfunction with the SRS air bag. If the SRS air bag warning light does not appear when the ENGINE START/STOP button has been turned to the ON position, or if it remains appears after illuminating for approximately 3 ~ 6 seconds, or if it appears while the vehicle is being driven, have an authorized Kia dealer inspect the pre-tensioner seat belt and SRS air bag system as soon as possible.
pair.new: If the pretensioner seat belt system is not working properly, this warning light will appear even if there is not a malfunction with the SRS air bag. If the SRS air bag warning light does not appear when the ENGINE START/STOP button has been turned to the ON position, remains illuminated for approximately 3–6 seconds, or appears while the vehicle is being driven, have an authorized Kia dealer inspect the pretensioner seat belt and SRS air bag system as soon as possible.

pair.old: Do not attempt to service or repair the pre-tensioner seat belt system in any manner. Do not attempt to inspect or replace the pre-tensioner seat belts yourself. This must be done by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Do not attempt to service or repair the pretensioner seat belt system in any manner. Do not attempt to inspect or replace the pretensioner seat belts yourself. This must be done by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Hot pre-tensioner
pair.new: Hot pretensioner

pair.old: Do not touch the pre-tensioner seat belt assemblies for several minutes after they have been activated. When the pre-tensioner seat belt mechanism fires during a collision the pre-tensioner becomes hot and can burn you.
pair.new: Do not touch the pretensioner seat belt assemblies for several minutes after they have been activated. When the pretensioner seat belt mechanism fires during a collision, the pretensioner becomes hot and can burn you.

pair.old: Pre-tensioners are designed to operate only one time. After activation, pre-tensioner seat belts must be replaced. If the pre-tensioner must be replaced, contact an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Pretensioners are designed to operate only once. After activation, pretensioner seat belts must be replaced. Contact an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: All 50 states have child restraint laws. You should be aware of the specific requirements in your state. Child and/or infant seats must be properly placed and installed in the rear seat. For more information about the use of these restraints, refer to .
pair.new: All 50 states have child restraint laws. You should be aware of the specific requirements in your state. Child or infant seats must be properly placed and installed in the rear seat. For more information about the use of these restraints, refer to .

pair.old: Children who are too large for Child Restraint System should always occupy the rear seat and use the available lap/shoulder belts. The lap portion should be fastened and snug on the hips as low as possible. Check periodically to insure that the belt fits. A child’s squirming could put the belt out of position. Children are given the most safety in the event of an accident when they are restrained by a proper restraint system in the rear seat. If a larger child (over age 13) must be seated in the front seat, the child should be securely restrained by the available lap/shoulder belt and the seat should be placed in the rearmost position. Children age 13 and under should be restrained securely in the rear seat. NEVER place a child age 13 and under in the front seat. NEVER place a rear facing child seat in the front seat of a vehicle.
pair.new: Children who are too large for a Child Restraint System should always occupy the rear seat and use the available lap/shoulder belts. The lap portion should be fastened snugly on the hips as low as possible. Check periodically to ensure that the belt fits. A child’s movement could move the belt out of position. Children are safest in the event of an accident when they are restrained by a proper restraint system in the rear seat. If a larger child (over age 13) must be seated in the front seat, the child should be securely restrained by the available lap/shoulder belt, and the seat should be placed in the rearmost position. Children age 13 and under should be restrained securely in the rear seat. NEVER place a child age 13 and under in the front seat. NEVER place a rear-facing child seat in the front seat of a vehicle.

pair.old: If the shoulder belt portion slightly touches the child’s neck or face, try placing the child closer to the center of the vehicle. If the shoulder belt still touches their face or neck they need to be returned to a Child Restraint System.
pair.new: If the shoulder belt portion slightly touches the child’s neck or face, try placing the child closer to the center of the vehicle. If the shoulder belt still touches their face or neck, they need to be returned to a Child Restraint System.

pair.old: To reduce the chance of injuries in the event of an accident and to achieve maximum effectiveness of the restraint system, all passengers should be sitting up and the front and rear seats should be in an upright position when the vehicle is moving. A seat belt cannot provide proper protection if the person is lying down in the rear seat or if the front and rear seats are in a reclined position.
pair.new: To reduce the chance of injuries in the event of an accident and maximize the effectiveness of the restraint system, all passengers should be sitting up, and the front and rear seats should be in an upright position when the vehicle is moving. A seat belt cannot provide proper protection if the person is lying down in the rear seat, or if the front and rear seats are in a reclined position.

pair.old: Care of seat belts
pair.new: Seat belt care

pair.old: Seat belt systems should never be disassembled or modified. Care should be taken to assure that seat belts and belt hardware are not damaged by seat hinges, doors or abuse.
pair.new: Seat belt systems should never be disassembled or modified. Take care to ensure that seat belts and belt hardware are not damaged by seat hinges, doors, or abuse.

pair.old: Seatbelts can become hot in a vehicle that has been closed up in sunny weather. Please handle with care, as they could burn infants and children, if used abruptly.
pair.new: Seat belts can become hot in an enclosed vehicle in sunny weather. Please handle with care, as they could burn infants and children.

pair.old: Seat belts should be kept clean and dry. They can be cleaned by using a mild soap solution and warm water. Bleach, dye, strong detergents or abrasives should not be used because they may damage and weaken the fabric.
pair.new: Seat belts should be kept clean and dry. They can be cleaned by using a mild soap solution and warm water. Bleach, dye, strong detergents, or abrasives should not be used because they may damage and weaken the fabric.

pair.old: Infants and younger children must be restrained in an appropriate rear-facing or forward-facing Child Restraint System (CRS) that has first been properly secured to the rear seat of the vehicle Please refer to your state or federal laws for child seating requirements in the operation of a motor vehicle.
pair.new: Infants and younger children must be restrained in an appropriate rear-facing or forward-facing CRS that has first been properly secured to the rear seat of the vehicle. Please refer to your state or federal laws for child seating requirements in the operation of a motor vehicle.

pair.old: Children under age 13 must always ride in the rear seats and must always be properly restrained to minimize the risk of injury in an accident, sudden stop or sudden maneuver.
pair.new: Children under age 13 must always ride in the rear seats and must always be properly restrained to minimize the risk of injury in an accident, sudden stop, or sudden maneuver.

pair.old: Never install a child or infant seat on the front passenger’s seat. A child riding in the front passenger seat can be forcefully struck by an inflating airbag and get seriously injured.
pair.new: Never install a child or infant seat in the front passenger seat. A child riding in the front passenger seat can be forcefully struck by an inflating airbag and get seriously injured.

pair.old: Hot Child Restraint
pair.new: Hot Child Restraint System (CRS)

pair.old: A Child Restraint System can become very hot if it is left in a closed vehicle on a sunny day. Be sure to check the seat cover, buckles and latches before placing a child in the restraint system.
pair.new: A CRS can become very hot if it is left in an enclosed vehicle on a sunny day. Be sure to check the seat cover, buckles, and latches before placing a child in the restraint system.

pair.old: According to accident statistics, children are safer when properly restrained in the rear seats than in the front seat. Even with air bags, children can be seriously injured or killed. Children too large for a child restraint must use the seat belts provided.
pair.new: According to accident statistics, children are safer when properly restrained in the rear seats than in the front seat. Even with air bags, children can be seriously injured or killed. Children too large for a CRS must use the seat belts provided.

pair.old: All 50 states have child restraint laws which require children to travel in approved child restraint devices. The laws governing the age or height/weight restrictions at which seat belts can be used instead of child restraints differs among states, so you should be aware of the specific requirements in your state, and where you are traveling.
pair.new: All 50 states have child restraint laws that require children to travel in approved child restraint devices. The laws governing the age or height and weight restrictions at which seat belts can be used instead of child restraints differs among states. You should be aware of the specific requirements in your state and the states where you are traveling.

pair.old: A CRS is generally designed to be secured in a vehicle seat by lap belt portion of a lap/shoulder belt, or by a LATCH system in the rear seats of the vehicle.
pair.new: A CRS is generally designed to be secured in a vehicle seat by the lap belt portion of a lap/shoulder belt, or by a LATCH system in the rear seats of the vehicle.

pair.old: Child Restraint Installation
pair.new: Child Restraint System (CRS) Installation

pair.old: An improperly secured child restraint can increase the risk of serious injury or death in an accident. Always take the following precautions when using a Child Restraint System:
pair.new: An improperly secured CRS can increase the risk of serious injury or death in an accident. Always take the following precautions when using a CRS:

pair.old: Always follow the Child Restraint System manufacturer’s instructions for installation and use.
pair.new: Always follow the CRS manufacturer’s instructions for installation and use.

pair.old: Always properly restrain your child in the child restraint.
pair.new: Always properly restrain your child in the CRS.

pair.old: If the vehicle head restraint prevents proper installation of a child seat (as described in the Child Restraint System manual), the head restraint of the respective seating position shall be readjusted or entirely removed.
pair.new: If the vehicle head restraint prevents proper installation of a child seat (as described in the CRS manual), then readjust or entirely remove the head restraint from the seat.

pair.old: Do not use an infant carrier or a child safety seat that “hooks” over a seatback as it may not provide adequate protection in an accident.
pair.new: Do not use an infant carrier or a child safety seat that “hooks” over a seatback, as it may not provide adequate protection in an accident.

pair.old: A child restraint in the center seating position may also contact or push up against the safety belt buckles, which can damage the buckles and make them unusable or unsafe. Always check that the child restraint does not contact any of the safety belt buckles. Check the placement of the child restraint regularly to make sure that it has not shifted and come into contact with any of the safety belt buckles.
pair.new: A CRS in the center seating position may also contact or push up against the seat belt buckles, which can damage the buckles and make them unusable or unsafe. Always check that the CRS does not contact any of the seat belt buckles. Check the placement of the CRS regularly to make sure that it has not shifted and come into contact with any of the seat belt buckles.

pair.old: After an accident, have a Kia dealer check the Child Restraint System, seat belts, tether anchors and lower anchors.
pair.new: After an accident, have a Kia dealer check the CRS, seat belts, tether anchors, and lower anchors.

pair.old: Selecting a CRS
pair.new: Selecting a child restraint system (CRS)

pair.old: Select a child restraint based on your child’s height and weight. The required label or the instructions for use typically provide this information.
pair.new: Select a CRS based on your child’s height and weight. The required label or the instructions for use typically provide this information.

pair.old: Select a child restraint that fits the vehicle seating position where it will be used.
pair.new: Select a CRS that fits the vehicle seating position where it will be used.

pair.old: The American Academy of Pediatrics provides helpful fit and safety information about child restraints at www.healthychildren.org.
pair.new: The American Academy of Pediatrics provides helpful fit and safety information about child restraints at https://www.healthychildren.org.

pair.old: Never hold a child in your arms or lap when riding in a vehicle. The violent forces created during a crash will tear the child from your arms and throw the child against the car’s interior. Always use a Child Restraint System which is appropriate for your child’s height and weight.
pair.new: Never hold a child in your arms or lap when riding in a vehicle. The violent forces created during a crash will tear the child from your arms and throw them against the car’s interior. Always use an appropriate CRS for your child’s height and weight.

pair.old: Do not use one seat belt for two occupants at the same time. This will eliminate any safety benefit provided by the seat belt to the occupants.
pair.new: Do not use 1 seat belt for 2 occupants at the same time. This will eliminate any safety benefit provided by the seat belt to the occupants.

pair.old: child restraint system (CRS)typesThere are three main types of the CRS: rear-facing seats, forward-facing seats, and booster seats. They are classified according to the child’s age, height and weight.
pair.new: child restraint system (CRS)typesThere are 3 main types of the CRS: rear-facing seats, forward-facing seats, and booster seats. They are classified according to the child’s age, height, and weight.

pair.old: A rear-facing child seat provides restraint with the seating surface against the back of the child. The harness system holds the child in place, and in an accident, acts to keep the child positioned in the seat and reduces the stress to the neck and spinal cord.
pair.new: A rear-facing child seat provides restraint with the surface of the seat against the back of the child. The harness system holds the child in place, and in an accident, keeps the child positioned in the seat and reduces stress to the neck and spinal cord.

pair.old: All children under age one must always ride in a rear-facing infant child restraint.
pair.new: All children under age 1 must always ride in a rear-facing infant CRS.

pair.old: Continue to use a rear-facing child seat for as long as your child will fit within the height and weight limits allowed by the child seat manufacturer. It’s the best way to keep them safe. Once your child has outgrown the rear-facing child restraint, your child is ready for a forward-facing child restraint with a harness.
pair.new: Continue to use a rear-facing child seat for as long as your child will fit within the height and weight limits allowed by the child seat manufacturer. It’s the best way to keep them safe. Once your child has outgrown the rear-facing child restraint, your child is ready for a forward-facing CRS with a harness.

pair.old: A forward-facing child seat provides restraint for the child’s body with a harness. Keep children in a forward-facing child seat with a harness until they reach the top height or weight limit allowed by your child restraint’s manufacturer.
pair.new: A forward-facing child seat provides restraint for the child’s body with a harness. Keep children in a forward-facing child seat with a harness until they reach the top height or weight limit allowed by your CRS’s manufacturer.

pair.old: A booster seat is a restraint designed to improve the fit of the vehicle’s seat belt system. A booster seat positions the seat belt so that it fits properly over the lap of your child.
pair.new: A booster seat is a restraint that improves the fit of the vehicle’s seat belt system. A booster seat positions the seat belt so that it fits properly over the lap of your child.

pair.old: Keep your child in a booster seat until they are big enough to sit in the seat without a booster and still have the seat belt fit properly. For a seat belt to fit properly, the lap belt must lie snugly across the upper thighs, not the stomach. The shoulder belt should lie snug across the shoulder and chest and not across the neck or face. Children under age 13 must always ride in the rear seats and must always be properly restrained to minimize the risk of injury.
pair.new: Keep your child in a booster seat until they are big enough to sit in the seat without a booster and still have the seat belt fit properly. For a seat belt to fit properly, the lap belt must lie snugly across the upper thighs, not across the stomach. The shoulder belt should lie snugly across the shoulder and chest, not across the neck or face. Children under age 13 must always ride in the rear seats and be properly restrained to minimize the risk of injury.

pair.old: Installing a CRS
pair.new: Installing a child restraint system (CRS)

pair.old: Follow the instructions provided by the manufacturer when installing the child seat. Note these general steps when installing the seat to your vehicle:
pair.new: Follow the instructions provided by the manufacturer when installing the child seat. Note these general steps when installing the seat in your vehicle:

pair.old: After installing a child restraint to the vehicle, push and pull the seat forward and from side-to-side to verify that it is securely attached to the seat. A child restraint secured with a seat belt should be installed as firmly as possible. However, some side-to-side movement can be expected.
pair.new: After installing a child restraint in the vehicle, push and pull the seat forward and from side-to-side to verify that it is securely attached to the seat. A CRS secured with a seat belt should be installed as firmly as possible. However, some side-to-side movement can be expected.

pair.old: Secure the child in the child restraint.
pair.new: Secure the child in the CRS.

pair.old: Make sure the child is properly strapped in the child restraint according to the manufacturer instructions.
pair.new: Make sure the child is properly strapped in the CRS according to the manufacturer instructions.

pair.old: Lower anchors are metal bars built into the vehicle. There are two lower anchors for each LATCH seating position that will accommodate a child restraint with lower attachments.
pair.new: Lower anchors are metal bars built into the vehicle. There are 2 lower anchors for each LATCH seating position that will accommodate a CRS with lower attachments.

pair.old: To use the LATCH system in your vehicle, you must have a child restraint with LATCH attachments.
pair.new: To use the LATCH system in your vehicle, you must have a CRS with LATCH attachments.

pair.old: LATCH Lower Anchors
pair.new: LATCH lower anchors

pair.old: Never attempt to attach a LATCH equipped seat in the center seating position. LATCH lower anchors are only to be used in the left and right rear outboard seating positions. You may damage the anchors or the anchors may fail and break in a collision if the seat is in the center seating position.
pair.new: Never attempt to attach a LATCH-equipped seat in the center seating position. LATCH lower anchors are only to be used in the left and right rear outboard seating positions. You may damage the anchors, or the anchors may fail and break in a collision if the seat is in the center seating position.

pair.old: Lower nchor position indicator
pair.new: Lower anchor position indicator

pair.old: Securing a child restraint (CRS) with the LATCH anchors system
pair.new: Securing a child restraint system (CRS) with the LATCH anchors system

pair.old: To install a LATCH-compatible child restraint in either of the rear outboard seating positions:
pair.new: To install a LATCH-compatible CRS in either of the rear outboard seating positions:

pair.old: Move the seat belt buckle away from the lower anchors. Otherwise, the webbing or buckle can be damaged by the latch anchor, which can make them become unusable or unsafe.
pair.new: Move the seat belt buckle away from the lower anchors. Otherwise, the latch anchor can damage the webbing or buckle, which can make them unusable or unsafe.

pair.old: Move any other objects away from the anchors that could prevent a secure connection between the child restraint and the lower anchors.
pair.new: Move away any objects that could prevent a secure connection between the child restraint and the lower anchors.

pair.old: Place the child restraint on the vehicle seat, then attach the seat to the lower anchors according to the instructions provided by the child restraint manufacturer.
pair.new: Place the CRS on the vehicle seat, then attach the seat to the lower anchors according to the instructions provided by the child restraint manufacturer.

pair.old: Follow the child restraint instructions for properly adjusting and tightening the lower attachments on the child restraint to the lower anchors.
pair.new: Follow the CRS instructions for properly adjusting and tightening the lower attachments on the CRS to the lower anchors.

pair.old: Securing a CRS seat with “Tether Anchor” system
pair.new: Securing a child restraint seat with “Tether Anchor” system

pair.old: Route the Child Restraint System seat strap over the seatback.
pair.new: Route the CRS seat strap over the seatback.

pair.old: For vehicles with adjustable headrest, route the tether strap under the headrest and between the headrest posts, otherwise route the tether strap over the top of the seatback.
pair.new: For vehicles with adjustable headrests, route the tether strap under the headrest and between the headrest posts. Otherwise, route the tether strap over the top of the seatback.

pair.old: Connect the tether to the tether anchorage, then tighten the tether according to the instructions of your Child Restraint System’s manufacturer to firmly attach the Child Restraint System to the seat.
pair.new: Connect the tether to the tether anchor, then tighten the tether according to the instructions of your CRS’s manufacturer to firmly attach the CRS to the seat.

pair.old: Check that the child restraint is securely attached to the seat by pushing and pulling the seat forward and from side-to-side.
pair.new: Check that the CRS is securely attached to the seat by pushing and pulling the seat forward and from side-to-side.

pair.old: Securing a child restraint with a lap/shoulder belt
pair.new: Securing a CRS with a lap/shoulder belt

pair.old: When not using the LATCH system, all child restraints must be secured to a vehicle rear seat with the lap part of a lap/shoulder belt.
pair.new: When not using the LATCH system, all CRSs must be secured to a vehicle rear seat with the lap part of a lap/shoulder belt.

pair.old: All passenger seat belts move freely under normal conditions and only lock under extreme or emergency conditions (emergency locking mode). So, in order to secure a child restraint, you must manually pull the seat belt all the way out to shift the retractor to the “automatic locking” mode.
pair.new: All passenger seat belts move freely under normal conditions and only lock under extreme or emergency conditions (emergency locking mode). Therefore, to secure a CRS, you must manually pull the seat belt all the way out to shift the retractor to the “automatic locking” mode.

pair.old: Place the CRS on a rear seat and route the lap/shoulder belt around or through the child restraint, following the restraint manufacturer’s instructions.
pair.new: Place the CRS on a rear seat and route the lap/shoulder belt around or through the CRS, following the restraint manufacturer’s instructions.

pair.old: Even in vehicles with air bags, you and your passengers must always wear the safety belts provided in order to minimize the risk and severity of injury in the event of a collision or rollover.
pair.new: Even in vehicles with air bags, you and your passengers must always wear the seat belts provided to minimize the risk and severity of injury in the event of a collision or rollover.

pair.old: How does the air bag system operate?
pair.new: Air bag system operation

pair.old: Air bags are activated (able to inflate if necessary) only when the ENGINE START/STOP Button is in the ON Position and it can be activated within about 3 minutes after ignition off.
pair.new: Air bags are activated (able to inflate, if necessary) only when the ENGINE START/STOP Button is in the ON position, and they can be activated within about 3 minutes after the ignition is turned off.

pair.old: The appropriate air bags inflate instantly in the event of a serious frontal collision or side collision in order to help protect the occupants from serious physical injury.
pair.new: The appropriate air bags inflate instantly in the event of a serious frontal collision or side collision to help protect the occupants from serious physical injury.

pair.old: There is no single speed at which the air bags will inflate. Generally, air bags are designed to inflate based upon the severity of a collision and its direction, etc. Several factors determine whether the sensors produce an electronic deployment/inflation signal.
pair.new: There is no single speed at which the air bags will inflate. Generally, air bags are designed to inflate based on the severity and direction of a collision. Several factors determine whether the sensors produce an electronic deployment and inflation signal.
