pair.old: In order to help provide protection, the air bags must inflate rapidly. The airbag inflates extremely fast between the occupant and the vehicle structures before the occupant impacts the vehicle structures. This speed of inflation reduces the risk of serious or life-threatening injuries and is thus a necessary part of the air bag design. However, airbag inflation can also cause injuries which can include facial abrasions, bruises and broken bones. This is because the rapid inflation also causes the airbags to expand with a great deal of force.
pair.new: To help provide protection, the air bags must inflate rapidly. The airbag inflates extremely fast between the occupant and the vehicle structures before the occupant impacts the vehicle structures. This speed of inflation reduces the risk of serious or life-threatening injuries and is thus a necessary part of the air bag design. However, airbag inflation can also cause injuries, which can include facial abrasions, bruises, and broken bones. This is because the rapid inflation causes the airbags to expand with a great deal of force.

pair.old: Sit as far back as possible from the steering wheel while still maintaining comfortable control of the vehicle. A distance of at least 10 inch or inches (25 cm) from your chest to the steering wheel is recommended. Failure to do so can result in airbag inflation injuries to the driver.
pair.new: Sit as far back as possible from the steering wheel while still maintaining comfortable control of the vehicle. A distance of at least 10 inches (25 cm) from your chest to the steering wheel is recommended. Failure to do so can result in airbag inflation injuries to the driver.

pair.old: When inflated, the air bags make a loud noise and leave smoke and powder in the air inside the vehicle. This is normal and is a result of the ignition of the air bag inflator. After the air bag inflates, you may feel substantial discomfort in breathing due to the contact of your chest with both the seat belt and the air bag, as well as from breathing the smoke and powder. Open your doors and/or windows as soon as possible after impact to reduce discomfort and prevent prolonged exposure to the smoke and powder.
pair.new: When inflated, the air bags make a loud noise and leave smoke and powder in the air inside the vehicle. This is normal and results from the ignition of the air bag inflator. After the air bag inflates, you may inhale the smoke and powder and feel substantial discomfort breathing. You may also feel discomfort from your chest making contact with the seat belt and air bag. Open your doors and/or windows as soon as possible after impact to reduce discomfort and prevent prolonged exposure to the smoke and powder.

pair.old: Though smoke and powder are nontoxic, they may cause irritation to the skin (eyes, nose and throat, etc). Wash and rinse with cold water immediately and consult a doctor if the symptom persists.
pair.new: Though the smoke and powder are nontoxic, they may cause irritation to the skin, eyes, nose, and throat. Wash and rinse with cold water immediately and consult a doctor if the irritation persists.

pair.old: Do not touch the air bag storage area’s internal components immediately after airbag inflation. The air bag related parts in the steering wheel, instrument panel and the roof rails above the front and rear doors are very hot. Hot components can result in burn injuries.
pair.new: Do not touch the air bag storage area’s internal components immediately after airbag inflation. The air bag–related parts in the steering wheel, the instrument panel, and the roof rails above the front and rear doors are very hot. Touching hot components can result in burn injuries.

pair.old: Do not install a child restraint on the front passenger’s seat
pair.new: Do not install a Child Restraint System (CRS) on the front passenger’s seat

pair.old: Do not place front-facing child restraints in the front passenger’s seat. If the front passenger air bag inflates, it could cause serious or fatal injuries to the child.
pair.new: Do not place a front-facing CRS in the front passenger’s seat. If the front passenger air bag inflates, it could cause serious or fatal injuries to the child.

pair.old: Front driver/passenger’s seat belt buckle sensor
pair.new: Front driver’s/passenger’s seat belt buckle sensor

pair.old: The SRS air bag warning light on the instrument panel will appear for about 3 ~ 6 seconds after the ENGINE START/STOP button is turned to the ON position, after which the air bag warning light should go out.
pair.new: The SRS air bag warning light on the instrument panel appears for about 3–6 seconds after the ENGINE START/STOP button is turned to the ON position, after which the air bag warning light goes out.

pair.old: The light stays on after illuminating for approximately > 6 seconds.
pair.new: The light stays on after 6 seconds.

pair.old: Do not install or place any accessories on the steering wheel, instrument panel, or on the front passenger’s panel above the glove box in a vehicle. Such objects may become dangerous projectiles if the air bag deploys.
pair.new: Do not install or place any accessories on the steering wheel, instrument panel, or front passenger’s panel above the glove box in a vehicle. Such objects may become dangerous projectiles if the air bag deploys.

pair.old: Do not place any objects (an umbrella, bag, etc.) between the front door and the front seat. Such objects may become dangerous projectiles if the side airbag inflates.
pair.new: Do not place any objects (an umbrella, bag, and so on) between the front door and the front seat. Such objects may become dangerous projectiles if the side airbag inflates.

pair.old: If an air bag deploys, there may be a loud noise and powder released in the vehicle. These conditions are normal and are non-toxic. The air bags are packed in this fine powder. The dust generated during air bag deployment may cause skin or eye irritation as well as aggravate asthma for some persons. Wash all exposed skin areas thoroughly with cold water and a mild soap after an accident in which the air bags were deployed.
pair.new: If an air bag deploys, there may be a loud noise and powder released in the vehicle. These conditions are normal, and the powder is non-toxic. The air bags are packed in this fine powder. The dust generated during air bag deployment may cause skin or eye irritation, as well as aggravate asthma for some people. Wash all exposed skin thoroughly with cold water and a mild soap after an accident in which the air bags were deployed.

pair.old: The SRS can function only when the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON Position or approximately within 3 minutes after ignition off. The SRS is not working properly if any of the following situations occur:
pair.new: The SRS can function only when the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON position, or approximately 3 minutes after the ignition is turned off. The SRS is not working properly if any of the following situations occur:

pair.old: the SRS airbag warning light comes on while driving
pair.new: The SRS airbag warning light comes on while you are driving

pair.old: Before replacing a fuse or disconnecting a battery terminal, turn the ENGINE START/STOP button to the OFF position. Never remove or replace air bag related fuse(s) when the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON position as the SRS warning light to illuminate.
pair.new: Before replacing a fuse or disconnecting a battery terminal, turn the ENGINE START/STOP button to the OFF position. Never remove or replace air bag related fuse(s) when the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON position, as the SRS warning light will illuminate.

pair.old: The ODS is designed to detect the presence of a properly seated front passenger and determine if the passenger’s front air bag should be activate (may inflate) or not. Only the front passenger front air bag is controlled by the ODS.
pair.new: The ODS detects the presence of a properly seated front passenger and determine if the passenger’s front air bag should be activated (may inflate) or not. Only the front air bag for the front passenger is controlled by the ODS.

pair.old: Main components of the ODS
pair.new: Main components of the Occupant Detection System (ODS)

pair.old: An indicator light located on the overhead console which illuminate the words PASSENGER AIR BAG () indicates the front passenger air bag system is deactivated.
pair.new: An indicator light located on the overhead console illuminates the words PASSENGER AIR BAG () to indicate that the front passenger air bag system is deactivated.

pair.old: If the front passenger seat is occupied by a person other than what the system judges to be a properly seated adult (upright with the seatback in an upright position, centered on the seat cushion with their seat belt on, legs comfortably extended and feet on the floor), the PASSENGER AIR BAG () indicator will turn on and the front passenger’s air bag will not be able to inflate, if necessary, in frontal crashes.
pair.new: If the front passenger seat is occupied by a person other than what the system judges to be a properly seated adult (upright with the seatback in an upright position, centered on the seat cushion with their seat belt on, legs comfortably extended and feet on the floor), the PASSENGER AIR BAG () indicator will turn on and the front passenger’s air bag will not be able to inflate in frontal crashes.

pair.old: You will find the PASSENGER AIR BAG () indicator on the overhead console. This system detects the conditions 1 ~ 4 in the following table and activates or deactivates the front passenger air bag based on these conditions.
pair.new: You will find the PASSENGER AIR BAG () indicator on the overhead console. This system detects conditions 1–4 in the following table and activates or deactivates the front passenger air bag based on these conditions.

pair.old: Always be sure that you and all vehicle occupants are seated and restrained properly (sitting upright with the seat in an upright position, centered on the seat cushion, with the person’s legs comfortably extended, feet on the floor, and wearing the safety belt properly) for the most effective protection by the air bag and the safety belt.
pair.new: Always be sure that you and all vehicle occupants are seated and restrained properly (sitting upright with the seatback in an upright position, centered on the seat cushion, with the person’s legs comfortably extended, feet on the floor, and wearing the seat belt properly) for the most effective protection by the air bag and the seat belt.

pair.old: The ODS may not function properly if the passenger takes actions which can defeat the detection system. These include:
pair.new: The ODS may not function properly if the passenger takes actions that can defeat the detection system. These include:

pair.old: Putting legs on the dashboard or resting them on other locations which reduce the passenger weight on the front seat.
pair.new: Putting legs on the dashboard or resting them on other locations that reduce the passenger weight on the front seat.

pair.old: Improperly wearing the safety belt.
pair.new: Improperly wearing the seat belt.

pair.old: Conditions and operation of the front passenger ODS
pair.new: Conditions and operation of the front passenger Occupant Detection System (ODS)

pair.old: Condition detected by the occupant detection system
pair.new: Condition detected by the ODS

pair.old: The PASSENGER AIR BAG () indicator may turn on or off when a child above 12 months to 12 years old (with or without a CRS) sits in the front passenger seat. This is a normal condition.
pair.new: The PASSENGER AIR BAG () indicator may turn on or off when a child between the ages of 12 months and 12 years (with or without a CRS) sits in the front passenger seat. This is a normal condition.

pair.old: Do not install a child restraint seat in the passenger seat when the seat is heavily soaked with any type of liquid.
pair.new: Do not install a CRS in the passenger seat when the seat is heavily soaked with any type of liquid.

pair.old: Do not use car seat cushions that cover up the surface of the seat and aftermarket manufactured passenger seat heaters.
pair.new: Do not use car seat cushions that cover up the surface of the seat, or aftermarket manufactured passenger seat heaters.

pair.old: After conducting car interior cleaning using steam or detergent, the seat should be dried properly. Afterward, check for normal operation of the PASSENGER AIR BAG “OFF” and air bag warning lights.
pair.new: After cleaning the interior of the car using steam or detergent, dry the seat properly. Afterward, check for normal operation of the PASSENGER AIR BAG “OFF” and air bag warning lights.

pair.old: After the passenger seat has been removed or installed for repair purposes, check for normal operation of the PASSENGER AIR BAG () and air bag warning lights with a person seated or not seated in the passenger seat.
pair.new: After the passenger seat has been removed or installed for repair purposes, check for normal operation of the PASSENGER AIR BAG () and air bag warning lights with a person seated vs. not seated in the passenger seat.

pair.old: When the PASSENGER AIR BAG () symbol is appeared, the passenger air bag system will not operate. The passenger air bag system will operate when necessary if the symbol is not appeared.
pair.new: When the PASSENGER AIR BAG () symbol illuminates, the passenger air bag system will not operate. The passenger air bag system will operate when necessary if the symbol does not appear.

pair.old: Do not modify or replace the front passenger seat. Don’t place anything on or attach anything such as a blanket, front seat cover or aftermarket seat heater to the front passenger seat. This can adversely affect the Occupant Detection System.
pair.new: Do not modify or replace the front passenger seat. Do not place anything on or attach anything to the front passenger seat (such as a blanket, front seat cover, or aftermarket seat heater). This can adversely affect the ODS.

pair.old: Occupant Detection System
pair.new: ODS

pair.old: Riding in an improper position adversely affects the Occupant Detection System and may result in the deactivation of the front passenger airbag. It is important for the driver to instruct the passenger as to the proper seating instructions as contained in this manual.
pair.new: Riding in an improper position adversely affects the ODS and may result in the deactivation of the front passenger airbag. It is important for the driver to instruct the passenger about the proper seating instructions contained in this manual.

pair.old: Never sit on one side of the front passenger seat.
pair.new: Never sit on the side of the front passenger seat.

pair.old: Do not use car seat accessories such as thick blankets and cushions which cover up the car seat surface.
pair.new: Do not use accessories that cover up the car seat surface, such as thick blankets or cushions.

pair.old: Do not place electronic devices such as laptops or DVD players or heavy objects such as a large quantity of water bottles on the passenger seat.
pair.new: Do not place electronic devices (such as laptops or DVD players) or heavy objects (such as a large quantity of water bottles) on the passenger seat.

pair.old: Do not allow an adult passenger to ride in the front seat when the PASSENGER AIR BAG () indicator is illuminated, because the air bag will not deploy in the event of a collision. Have the passenger reposition in the seat. Failure to properly position yourself may lead to air bag deactivation resulting in air bag non-deployment in a collision. If the PASSENGER AIR BAG () indicator remains illuminated after repositioned properly and the vehicle is restarted, have the passenger move to the rear seat because the passenger’s front air bag will not deploy.
pair.new: Do not allow an adult passenger to ride in the front seat when the PASSENGER AIR BAG () indicator is illuminated, because the air bag will not deploy in the event of a collision. Have the passenger reposition in the seat. Failure to sit in the proper position may lead to air bag deactivation, resulting in air bag non-deployment in a collision. If the PASSENGER AIR BAG () indicator remains illuminated after the passenger is repositioned properly and the vehicle is restarted, have the passenger move to the rear seat because the passenger’s front air bag will not deploy.

pair.old: The PASSENGER AIR BAG () indicator does not illuminate if the front passenger seat is occupied. The occupant detection sensor will then classify the front passenger after several more seconds. Also, if the ENGINE START/STOP button is turned to the ON position when about 3 minutes have elapsed since the vehicle is in OFF position.
pair.new: The PASSENGER AIR BAG () indicator does not illuminate if the front passenger seat is occupied. The occupant detection sensor will then classify the front passenger after several more seconds. The indicator also does not illuminate if the ENGINE START/STOP button is turned to the ON position after about 3 minutes have elapsed since the vehicle was in the OFF position.

pair.old: Even though your vehicle is equipped with the Occupant Detection System, never install a Child Restraint System in the front passenger’s seat. A deploying air bag can forcefully strike a child resulting in serious injuries or death.
pair.new: Even though your vehicle is equipped with the ODS, never install a CRS in the front passenger’s seat. A deploying air bag can forcefully strike a child, resulting in serious injuries or death.

pair.old: If the ODS is not working properly, the Supplemental Restraint System (SRS) air bag warning light on the instrument panel will appear because the passenger’s front air bag is connected with the ODS. If there is a malfunction of the ODS the PASSENGER AIR BAG () indicator will not appear. In this case, the passenger’s front airbag will inflate in frontal impact crashes even if there is no occupant in the front passenger seat.
pair.new: If the ODS is not working properly, the Supplemental Restraint System (SRS) air bag warning light on the instrument panel will appear because the passenger’s front air bag is connected with the ODS. If there is a malfunction of the ODS, the PASSENGER AIR BAG () indicator does not appear. In this case, the passenger’s front airbag will inflate in frontal impact crashes, even if there is no occupant in the front passenger seat.

pair.old: Driver’s front air bag / Passenger’s front air bag
pair.new: Driver’s front air bag / passenger’s front air bag

pair.old: The purpose of the SRS is to provide the vehicle’s driver and/or the front passenger with additional protection of the seat belt system alone in case of a frontal impact of sufficient severity. The SRS uses sensors to gather information about the driver’s and front passenger’s seat belt usage and impact severity.
pair.new: The purpose of the SRS is to provide the vehicle’s driver and front passenger with additional protection besides the seat belt system alone in case of a sufficiently severe frontal impact. The SRS uses sensors to gather information about the driver’s and front passenger’s seat belt usage and the impact severity.

pair.old: These sensors provide the ability to control the SRS deployment based on whether the seat belts are fastened, and how severe the impact is.
pair.new: These sensors control the SRS deployment based on whether the seat belts are fastened and how severe the impact is.

pair.old: The advanced SRS offers the ability to control the air bag inflation with two levels. A first stage level is provided for moderate impacts. A second stage level is provided for severe impacts.
pair.new: The advanced SRS controls the air bag inflation with 2 levels: a first stage level for moderate impacts, and a second stage level for severe impacts.

pair.old: Modification to the seat structure is more likely to cause the air bag to deploy at a different level than should be provided.
pair.new: Modifying the seat structure is more likely to cause the air bag to deploy at the incorrect level.

pair.old: Manufacturers are required by government regulations to provide a contact point concerning modifications to the vehicle for persons with disabilities, which modifications may affect the vehicle’s advanced air bag system. That contact is Kia’s toll-free Customer Assistance center at 1-800-333-4542 (U.S. only). However, Kia neither endorses nor will it support any changes to any part or structure of the vehicle that could affect the advanced air bag system, including the ODS.
pair.new: Government regulations require manufacturers to provide a contact point concerning modifications made to the vehicle for people with disabilities, which may affect the vehicle’s advanced air bag system. That contact is Kia’s toll-free Customer Assistance center at 1-800-333-4542 (U.S. only). However, Kia neither endorses nor supports any changes to any part or structure of the vehicle that could affect the advanced air bag system, including the ODS.

pair.old: The front passenger seat, dashboard or door should not be replaced except by an authorized Kia dealer using original Kia parts designed for this vehicle and model. Any other such replacement or modification could adversely affect the operation of the Occupant Detection System and your advanced air bags.
pair.new: The front passenger seat, dashboard, and door should only be replaced by an authorized Kia dealer using original Kia parts designed for this vehicle and model. Any other such replacement or modification could adversely affect the operation of the ODS and your advanced air bags.

pair.old: Advanced air bags are combined with pretensioner seat belts to help provide enhanced occupant protection in frontal crashes. Front air bags are not intended to deploy in collisions in which sufficient protection can be provided by the seat belt.
pair.new: Advanced air bags are combined with pretensioner seat belts to help provide enhanced occupant protection in frontal crashes. Front air bags are not intended to deploy in collisions where the seat belt provides sufficient protection.

pair.old: Front air bags are not intended to deploy in side impacts, rear impacts or rollovers. When the frontal deployment threshold is satisfied in a side impact, the front air bags may deploy. Front air bags will not deploy in frontal impacts below the deployment threshold.
pair.new: Front air bags are not intended to deploy in side impacts, rear impacts, or rollovers. When the frontal deployment threshold is satisfied in a side impact, the front air bags may deploy. Front air bags will not deploy in frontal impacts below the deployment threshold.

pair.old: Do not tamper with or disconnect SRS wiring or other components of the SRS system. This could result in injury by accidentally deploying the air bags or rendering the SRS inoperative.
pair.new: Do not tamper with or disconnect SRS wiring or other components of the SRS system. This could result in injury, due to accidental deployment of the air bags or by rendering the SRS inoperative.

pair.old: No objects (such as an instrument panel cover, mobile phone holder, cup holder, perfume, stickers, etc.) should be placed over or near the air bag modules on the steering wheel, instrument panel, windshield glass or front passenger’s instrument panel above the glove box. Such objects could cause harm if the vehicle is in a collision severe enough to deploy the airbag.
pair.new: Do not place any objects (such as an instrument panel cover, mobile phone holder, cup holder, perfume, stickers, and so on) over or near the air bag modules on the steering wheel, instrument panel, windshield glass, or front passenger’s instrument panel above the glove box. Such objects could cause harm if the vehicle is in a collision severe enough to deploy the airbag.

pair.old: Your Kia is equipped with a side air bag in each front seat, 2nd row seat.
pair.new: Your vehicle is equipped with a side air bag in each front seat and 2nd row seat.

pair.old: The purpose of these air bags is to provide the vehicle’s driver and/or the front passenger and 2nd row outboard passengers with additional protection than that offered by the seat belts alone.
pair.new: The purpose of these air bags is to provide the vehicle’s driver, front passenger, and 2nd row outboard passengers with additional protection aside from what the seat belts offer alone.

pair.old: The side air bags are designed to deploy during certain side impact collisions, depending on the crash severity of impact.
pair.new: The side air bags are designed to deploy during certain side impact collisions depending on the crash severity of impact.

pair.old: For best protection from the side air bag system and to avoid being injured by the deploying side air bag, both front seat occupants should sit in an upright position with seat belts properly fastened. The driver’s hands should be placed on the steering wheel at the 9:00 and 3:00 positions. The passenger’s arms and hands should be placed on their laps.
pair.new: To maximize the protection offered by the side air bag system, and to avoid being injured by the deploying side air bag, both front seat occupants should sit in an upright position with seat belts properly fastened. The driver’s hands should be placed on the steering wheel at the 9:00 and 3:00 positions. The passenger’s arms and hands should be placed on their lap.

pair.old: Do not install any accessories, including seat covers, on the side or near the side air bag as this may affect the deployment of the side air bags.
pair.new: Do not install any accessories, including seat covers, on the side or near the side air bag, as this may affect the deployment of the side air bags.

pair.old: Do not place any objects over the air bag or between the air bag and you. Do not attach any objects around the area the air bag inflates such as the door, side door glass, front and rear pillar.
pair.new: Do not place any objects over the air bag or between the air bag and you. Do not attach any objects around the area where the air bag inflates, such as the door, side door glass, or front and rear pillar.

pair.old: Do not install any accessory on the side or near the side air bags.
pair.new: Do not install any accessory on or near the side air bags.

pair.old: They are designed to help protect occupants in certain side impacts and to help reduce the risk of ejection from the vehicle as a result of a rollover, especially when the seat belts are also in use.
pair.new: The curtain air bags are designed to help protect occupants from certain side impacts, and to help reduce the risk of ejection from the vehicle as a result of a rollover, especially when the seat belts are also in use.

pair.old: The curtain air bags are designed to deploy during certain side impact collisions, depending on collision severity, angle, speed and point of impact. However, when the side deployment threshold is satisfied in a frontal impact, side air bags may deploy.
pair.new: The curtain air bags are designed to deploy during certain side impact collisions depending on collision severity, angle, speed, and point of impact. However, when the side deployment threshold is satisfied in a frontal impact, the side air bags may deploy.

pair.old: Do not allow the passengers to lean their heads or bodies against the doors, extend arms on the doors, stretch their arms out of the window or place objects between the doors and passengers when they are seated on seats equipped with side impact and/or curtain air bags.
pair.new: Do not allow the passengers to lean their heads or bodies against the doors, extend arms on the doors, stretch their arms out of the window, or place objects between the doors and themselves when they are seated on seats equipped with side impact or curtain air bags.

pair.old: Do not place any objects over the air bag. Do not attach any objects around the area the air bag inflates such as the door, side door glass, front and rear pillar or roof side rail.
pair.new: Do not place any objects over the air bag. Do not attach any objects around the area where the air bag inflates, such as the door, side door glass, front and rear pillar, or roof side rail.

pair.old: If the installation location or angle of the sensors is altered in any way, the air bags may deploy when they should not or they may not deploy when they should.
pair.new: If the installation location or angle of the sensors is altered in any way, the air bags may deploy when they should not, or may not deploy when they should.

pair.old: Problems may arise if the sensor installation angles are changed due to the deformation of the front bumper, front end module, body or front doors where side collision sensors are installed. Have the vehicle checked and repaired by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Problems may arise if the sensor installation angles are changed due by misshaping of the front bumper, front end module, body, or front doors where side collision sensors are installed. Have the vehicle checked and repaired by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Installing bumper guards, side step or running boards, or replacing a bumper (or front door module) with non-genuine parts may adversely affect your Kia collision and air bag deployment performance.
pair.new: Installing bumper guards, side steps, or running boards, or replacing a bumper (or front door module) with non-genuine parts, may adversely affect your Kia collision and air bag deployment performance.

pair.old: Kia Genuine bumper guards/bumpers are guaranteed for quality and performance.
pair.new: Genuine Kia bumper guards/bumpers are guaranteed for quality and performance.

pair.old: There are many types of accidents in which the air bag would not be expected to provide additional protection.
pair.new: There are many types of accidents in which the air bag is not expected to provide additional protection.

pair.old: These include rear impacts, second or third collisions in multiple impact accidents, as well as low speed impacts.
pair.new: These include rear impacts, second or third collisions in multiple impact accidents, and low-speed impacts.

pair.old: Side and/or curtain air bags are designed to inflate when an impact is detected by side collision sensors depending on several factors, including the severity of impact resulted from a side impact collision.
pair.new: Side and curtain air bags are designed to inflate when an impact is detected by side collision sensors depending on several factors, including the severity of impact of the side collision.

pair.old: Similarly, although side and curtain air bags are designed to inflate in certain side impact collisions, they may inflate in other types of collisions where a side force is detected by the sensors. For instance, side air bag and/or curtain air bags may inflate if rollover sensors indicate the possibility of a rollover occurring (even if none actually occurs) or in other situations, including when the vehicle is tilted while being towed.
pair.new: Similarly, although side and curtain air bags are designed to inflate in certain side impact collisions, they may inflate in other types of collisions where a side force is detected by the sensors. For instance, side or curtain air bags may inflate if rollover sensors indicate the possibility of a rollover occurring (even if none actually occurs) or in other situations, including when the vehicle is tilted while being towed.

pair.old: Even if side and/or curtain air bags do not provide impact protection in a rollover, they will deploy to prevent ejection of occupants, especially those who are restrained with seat belts.
pair.new: Even if side or curtain air bags do not provide impact protection in a rollover, they will deploy to prevent ejection of occupants, especially those who are restrained with seat belts.

pair.old: Air bags may not deploy in certain low-speed collisions where the air bag would not add any benefit beyond the protection already offered by the seat belts.
pair.new: Air bags may not deploy in certain low-speed collisions where the air bag would not add any protection beyond what is already offered by the seat belts.

pair.old: Front air bags are not designed to inflate in rear collisions, because occupants are moved backward by the force of the impact. In this case, inflated air bags would not be able to provide any additional benefit.
pair.new: Front air bags are not designed to inflate in rear collisions because the force of the impact moves occupants backward. In this case, inflated air bags would not provide any additional protection.

pair.old: Front airbags may not inflate in side impact collisions, because passengers move in the direction of the collision. Thus, in side impacts, frontal airbag deployment would not provide additional occupant protection.
pair.new: Front airbags may not inflate in side impact collisions because passengers move in the direction of the collision. Therefore, in side impacts, frontal airbag deployment would not provide any additional protection.

pair.old: In an angled collision, the force of impact may direct the occupants in a direction where the air bags would not be able to provide any additional benefit, and thus the sensors may not deploy any air bags.
pair.new: In an angled collision, the force of impact may move the occupants in a direction where the air bags would not provide any additional protection; therefore, the sensors may not deploy any air bags.

pair.old: Just before impact, drivers often brake heavily. Such heavy braking lowers the front portion of the vehicle causing it to “ride” under a vehicle with a higher ground clearance. Air bags may not inflate in this “under-ride” situation because deceleration forces that are detected by sensors may be significantly reduced by such “under-ride” collisions.
pair.new: Just before impact, drivers often brake heavily. Such heavy braking lowers the front portion of the vehicle, causing it to “ride” under a vehicle with a higher ground clearance. Air bags may not inflate in this situation because “underride” collisions may significantly reduce the deceleration forces detected by the sensors.

pair.old: Airbags may not inflate if the vehicle collides with an object such as a utility pole or tree. This is because the point of impact is concentrated in one area and the full force of the impact is not delivered to the sensors.
pair.new: Airbags may not inflate if the vehicle collides with an object such as a utility pole or tree. This is because the point of impact is concentrated in a single area, and the full force of the impact is not delivered to the sensors.

pair.old: The Supplemental Restraint System (SRS) is virtually maintenance-free and so there are no parts you can safely service by yourself.
pair.new: The Supplemental Restraint System (SRS) is virtually maintenance-free, so there are no parts you can safely service by yourself.

pair.old: Any work on the SRS, such as removing, installing, repairing, or any work on the steering wheel, the front passenger’s panel, front seats and roof rails must be performed by an authorized Kia dealer. Improper handling of the SRS may result in serious personal injury.
pair.new: Any work on the SRS (such as removing, installing, repairing, or performing any work on the steering wheel, the front passenger’s panel, the front seats, and the roof rails) must be performed by an authorized Kia dealer. Improper handling of the SRS may result in serious personal injury.

pair.old: For cleaning the air bag pad covers, use only a soft, dry cloth or has been moistened with plain water. Solvents or cleaners could adversely affect the air bag covers and proper deployment of the system.
pair.new: For cleaning the air bag pad covers, use only a soft, dry cloth, or has been moistened with plain water. Solvents or cleaners could adversely affect the air bag covers and proper deployment of the system.

pair.old: Do not tamper with or disconnect SRS wiring or other components of the SRS system. Doing so could result in the accidental inflation of the air bag or render the SRS inoperative.
pair.new: Do not tamper with or disconnect SRS wiring or other components, as this could result in accidental air bag inflation or render the SRS inoperative.

pair.old: Always have the ignition off and wait for 3 minutes when your Kia is being towed. The side air bags may inflate if the vehicle is tilted such as when being towed because of the rollover sensors in the vehicle.
pair.new: Always have the ignition off and wait for 3 minutes before having your Kia towed. The side air bags may inflate if the vehicle is tilted when being towed because of the rollover sensors in the vehicle.

pair.old: Each seat belt is designed to restrain one occupant. If more than one person uses the same seat belt, they could be seriously injured or killed in a collision.
pair.new: Each seat belt is designed to restrain 1 occupant. If more than 1 person uses the same seat belt, they could be seriously injured or killed in a collision.

pair.old: Do not use any accessories on seat belts. Devices claiming to improve occupant comfort or reposition of the seat belt can reduce the protection provided by the seat belt and increase the chance of serious injury in a crash.
pair.new: Do not use any accessories on seat belts. Devices claiming to improve occupant comfort or reposition the seat belt can reduce the protection provided by the seat belt and increase the chance of serious injury in a crash.

pair.old: Sitting improperly or out of position can cause occupants to be shifted too close to a deploying air bag, strike the interior structure or be thrown from the vehicle resulting in serious injury or death.
pair.new: Sitting improperly or out of position can cause occupants to shift too close to a deploying air bag, strike the interior structure, or be thrown from the vehicle, resulting in serious injury or death.

pair.old: If you modify your vehicle by changing your vehicle’s frame, bumper system, front end or side sheet metal or ride height, this may affect the operation of your vehicle’s air bag system.
pair.new: If you modify your vehicle by changing your vehicle’s frame, bumper system, front end or side sheet metal, or ride height, this may affect the operation of your vehicle’s air bag system.

pair.old: The mechanical key and various remote functions are integrated into the smart key to provide convenience to the driver.
pair.new: The smart key integrates the mechanical key and various remote functions.

pair.old: Children copy adults and they could press the start button. The key would enable children to operate power windows or other controls, or even make the vehicle move, which could result in serious bodily injury or death.
pair.new: Children imitate adults, and they could press the start button. The key would allow children to operate power windows or other controls, or even make the vehicle move, which could result in serious bodily injury or death.

pair.old: The key code number is stamped on the key code tag attached to the key set. If you lose your keys, this number will enable an authorized Kia dealer to duplicate the keys easily. Remove the key code tag and store it in a safe place. Record the key code number and keep it in a safe and handy place, but not in the vehicle.
pair.new: The key code number is stamped on the key code tag attached to the key set. If you lose your keys, this number will allow an authorized Kia dealer to duplicate the keys easily. Remove the key code tag and store it in a safe place. Record the key code number and keep it in a safe, handy place outside the vehicle.

pair.old: Use only Kia original parts for the ignition key in your vehicle. If an aftermarket key is used, the ENGINE START/STOP button may not return to ON after START. The starter will continue to operate causing possible fire due to excessive current in the wiring.
pair.new: Use only Kia original parts for the ignition key in your vehicle. If you use an aftermarket key, the ENGINE START/STOP button may not return to ON after START. The starter will continue to operate, causing excessive current in the wiring, which could result in a possible fire.

pair.old: Leaving children unattended in a vehicle with the keys is dangerous even if the vehicle is ACC or ON position. Unattended children could press the start button and may operate power window, other controls, or even make the vehicle move, which could result in serious bodily injury or death. Never leave the keys in your vehicle with unsupervised children, when the vehicle is running.
pair.new: Leaving children unattended in a vehicle with the keys is dangerous, even if the vehicle is in ACC or ON. Unattended children could press the start button and may operate power windows or other controls, or even make the vehicle move, which could result in serious bodily injury or death. Never leave the keys in your vehicle with unsupervised children when the vehicle is running.

pair.old: With a smart key, you can lock or unlock a door and even start the vehicle without inserting the key.
pair.new: With a smart key, you can lock or unlock a door and even start the vehicle without inserting the key.

pair.old: The smart key works when it is within approximately 33 feet (10 m) from the vehicle. Antennae within the vehicle sense when the smart key is within range.
pair.new: The smart key works when it is within approximately 33 feet (10 m) of the vehicle. Antennae within the vehicle sense when the smart key is within range.

pair.old: Tinting the vehicle windows with film, especially metalized film, may interfere with the signal from the smart key, reducing its operating range.
pair.new: Tinting the vehicle windows with film, especially metalized film, may interfere with the smart key’s signal and reduce its operating range.

pair.old: smart keyoperationAll doors can be locked if the lock button is pressed while the key is within range of the vehicle. Once all doors are closed, the hazard warning lights will blink and the chime will sound once to indicate that all doors are locked.
pair.new: smart keyoperationAll doors will lock if you press the lock button while the key is within range of the vehicle. Once all doors are closed, the hazard warning lights will blink, and the chime will sound once to indicate that all doors are locked.

pair.old: If the lock button is pressed again within 4 seconds, the hazard warning lights will blink and the chime will sound once to confirm that the door is locked.
pair.new: If you press the lock button again within 4 seconds, the hazard warning lights will blink, and the chime will sound once to confirm that the door is locked.

pair.old: If any door remains open or if the key is out of range, the hazard warning lights (and/or the chime) will not signal. If all doors are closed after the lock button is pressed, the hazard warning lights will blink once.
pair.new: If any door remains open or if the key is out of range, the hazard warning lights (and/or the chime) will not signal. If all doors are closed after you press the lock button, the hazard warning lights will blink once.

pair.old: All doors will unlock if the unlock button is pressed once while the key is in range. The hazard warning lights will blink twice to indicate that all doors are unlocked.
pair.new: All doors will unlock if you press the unlock button once while the key is in range. The hazard warning lights will blink twice to indicate that all doors are unlocked.

pair.old: Note that the doors will re-lock automatically unless you open any door within 30 seconds of pressing the unlock button.
pair.new: Note that the doors will relock automatically if you do not open any door within 30 seconds of pressing the unlock button.

pair.old: If you attempt to lock or unlock the door by pressing the door lock/unlock button under any of the following conditions, the door will not be locked or unlocked.
pair.new: The door will not lock or unlock with the smart key’s lock/unlock button in the following situations:

pair.old: When you want to lock or unlock the door in the ACC or ON state.
pair.new: When the vehicle is in ACC or ON.

pair.old: When you want to lock a door in a car with one or more doors open.
pair.new: When you try to lock a door when 1 or more doors are open.

pair.old: Depending on the vehicle, the driver can activate or deactivate the 2-press unlock setting function. For detailed information, refer to the separately supplied infotainment system manual.
pair.new: Depending on the vehicle, the driver can activate or deactivate the 2-press unlock setting. For detailed information, refer to the separately supplied infotainment system manual.

pair.old: Tinting the vehicle windows with film, especially metalized film, may interfere with receiving frequency transmitted by the smart key, reducing its operating range.
pair.new: Tinting the vehicle windows with film, especially metalized film, may interfere with the smart key’s receiving frequency and reduce its operating range.

pair.old: If the smart key is inoperative due to exposure to water or liquids, it will not be covered by your manufacturer’s vehicle warranty.
pair.new: If the smart key is inoperative due to exposure to water or other liquids, it will not be covered by your manufacturer’s vehicle warranty.
If the smart key is inoperative due to exposure to water or other liquids, it will not be covered by your manufacturer’s vehicle warranty.

pair.old: You can start the vehicle while insist your Kia without inserting the key.
pair.new: You can start the vehicle while inside your Kia without inserting the key.

pair.old: When the battery of the smart key is discharged or the smart key does not operate normally, the door can be locked or unlocked using the mechanical key.
pair.new: When the battery of the smart key is discharged, or the smart key does not operate normally, you can lock or unlock the door using the mechanical key.

pair.old: To remove the mechanical key, pull the mechanical key protective cover (1) from the smart key.
pair.new: To remove the mechanical key, pull the mechanical key protective cover (1) off the smart key.

pair.old: The smart key is close to a radio transmitter which can interfere with normal operation of the smart key, such as a radio station or an airport.
pair.new: The smart key is close to a radio transmitter that can interfere with its normal operation, such as a radio station or an airport.

pair.old: The smart key is near a mobile two-way radio system
pair.new: The smart key is near a mobile 2-way radio system.

pair.old: The smart key is near a mobile phone, particularly if the phone is being used to make or receive calls or to send and receive messages. (Avoid placing the smart key adjacent to a mobile phone.)
pair.new: The smart key is near a mobile phone, particularly if the phone is being used to make or receive calls or to send or receive messages. Avoid placing the smart key adjacent to a mobile phone.

pair.old: When the smart key does not work correctly, try the spare or open and close the door with the mechanical key. Contact an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: When the smart key does not work correctly, try the spare, or open and close the door with the mechanical key. When your smart key does not work correctly, contact an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: If the smart key is near to your cell phone or smart phone, the signal from the smart key could be blocked by your cell phone or smart phone. This is especially important when the phone is active, such as when making calls, receiving calls, text messaging, and/or sending/receiving emails. Avoid placing the smart key and your cell phone or smart phone in the same pants or jacket pocket and maintain adequate distance between the two devices.
pair.new: If the smart key is near your cell phone or smartphone, the phone could be blocking the smart key’s signal. This is especially likely when the phone is active, such as when making or receiving calls, text messaging, and sending or receiving emails. Avoid placing the smart key and your cell phone or smartphone in the same pants or jacket pocket. Maintain adequate distance between the 2 devices.

pair.old: If the smart key is not moved for some time, the detection function (if equipped) of smart key operation will pause. Lift the smart key to activate the detection again.
pair.new: If you do not move the smart key for some time, its detection function (if equipped) will pause. Lift the smart key to reactivate the detection function.

pair.old: Loss of the smart key
pair.new: Losing the smart key

pair.old: If you happen to lose both smart key, you will not be able to start the vehicle. You should immediately contact your authorized Kia dealer for assistance (tow the vehicle, if necessary) to protect it from potential theft.
pair.new: If you happen to lose both smart keys, you will not be able to start the vehicle. You should immediately contact an authorized Kia dealer for assistance. Tow the vehicle if necessary to protect it from potential theft.

pair.old: mobile phones, cordless phones and battery chargers
pair.new: Mobile phones, cordless phones, and battery chargers

pair.old: When bringing a smart key onto an airplane, make sure you do not press any button on the key while inside the cabin. If you are carrying the key in your bag etc., make sure that the buttons cannot be pressed accidentally. If you press a button, the key may emit radio waves that could interfere with the operation of the aircraft.
pair.new: When bringing a smart key onto an airplane, make sure you do not press any button on the key while inside the cabin. If you are carrying the key in your bag or elsewhere, make sure that the buttons cannot be pressed accidentally. If you press a button, the key may emit radio waves that could interfere with aircraft operation.

pair.old: The smart key uses a 3 volt lithium battery which will normally last for several years.
pair.new: The smart key uses a 3V lithium battery, which normally lasts for several years.

pair.old: If you are unsure how to use or replace the battery, contact an authorized Kia dealer. NO ORIGINAL TEXT

new.new: If you are unsure how to use or replace the battery, contact an authorized Kia dealer.
To replace the smart key battery:

pair.old: Pry open the key battery cover gently using a thin tool.
pair.new: Gently pry open the key battery cover using a thin tool.

pair.old: Replace the old battery with a new battery (CR2450). When replacing the battery, make sure align the battery poles properly.
pair.new: Replace the old battery with a new battery (CR2450). When replacing the battery, make sure to align the battery poles properly.

pair.old: Install the battery in the reverse order of removal.
pair.new: Install the battery in the reverse order of removal.

pair.old: The immobilizer system protects your vehicle from theft. If an improperly coded key (or other device) is used, the vehicle’s power system is disabled.
pair.new: The immobilizer system protects your vehicle from theft. If an improperly coded key or other device is used, the vehicle’s power system is disabled.

pair.old: when ENGINE START/STOP button is placed in the ON position, the immobilizer system indicator should come on briefly, then go off. If the indicator starts to blink, the system does not recognize the coding of the key.
pair.new: When you place the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position, the immobilizer system indicator should come on briefly and then go off. If the indicator starts to blink, the system does not recognize the coding of the key.

pair.old: If the system repeatedly does not recognize the coding of the key, contact your Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the system repeatedly does not recognize the key’s coding, contact an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Do not attempt to alter this system or add other devices to it. Electrical problems could result that may make your vehicle inoperable.
pair.new: Do not attempt to alter or add other devices to this system, as this could result in electrical problems that may render your vehicle inoperable.

pair.old: Change the ENGINE START/STOP button to the ON position.
pair.new: Turn the ENGINE START/STOP button to the ON position.

pair.old: When starting the vehicle, do not use the key with other immobilizer keys around. Otherwise, the vehicle may not start or may stop soon after it starts. Keep each key separate in order to avoid a starting malfunction.
pair.new: When starting the vehicle, do not use the key with other immobilizer keys around. Otherwise, the vehicle may not start or may stop soon after it starts. Keep each key separate to avoid a malfunction.

pair.old: This system is designed to provide protection from unauthorized entry into the vehicle.
pair.new: The theft-alarm system provides protection from unauthorized entry into the vehicle.

pair.old: This system is operated in three stages:
pair.new: This system is operated in 3 stages:

pair.old: If triggered, the system provides an audible alarm with blinking of the hazard warning lights.
pair.new: If triggered, the system alarm sounds, and the hazard warning lights blink.

pair.old: Do not attempt to alter this system or add other devices to it.
pair.new: Do not attempt to alter or add other devices to this system.

pair.old: Park the vehicle and stop the engine. Arm the system as described below.
pair.new: After you park the vehicle, follow these steps to arm the Theft-alarm system:

pair.old: Turn off the engine.
pair.new: Press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the OFF position.
Press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the OFF position.

pair.old: Make sure that all doors, the hood and liftgate are closed and latched.
pair.new: Make sure that all the doors, the hood, and the liftgate are closed and latched.

pair.old: Lock the doors by pressing the button of the front outside door handle with the smart key in your possession.
pair.new: To lock the doors, press the button on the front outside door handle with the smart key in your possession.

pair.old: After completion of the steps above, the hazard warning lights will operate once to indicate that the system is armed. If any door (or liftgate) or hood remains open, the hazard warning lights and the chime will not operate and the theft-alarm will not arm. If all doors and liftgate and hood are closed after the lock button is pressed, the hazard warning lights blink once. The system can also be armed by locking the doors with the key from the front doors; however, the hazard warning lights will not blink using this method.
pair.new: After you complete these steps, the hazard warning lights blink once to indicate that the system is armed. If any door, the liftgate, or the hood remains open, the theft-alarm system will not arm, the hazard warning lights will not blink, and the chime will not sound. You can also arm the system by using the key to lock the doors; however, the hazard warning lights will not blink.

pair.old: Lock the doors by pressing the lock button on the smart key.
pair.new: Press the smart key’s lock button to lock the doors.

pair.old: The alarm will sound and the hazard lights will blink continuously for approximately 30 seconds if any of the following occurs while the system is armed.
pair.new: The alarm sounds and the hazard warning lights blink continuously for approximately 30 seconds if any of the following occur while the system is armed:

pair.old: A front or rear door is opened without using the smart key.
pair.new: You open a front or rear door without using the smart key.

pair.old: The liftgate is opened without using the smart key.
pair.new: You open the liftgate without using the smart key.

pair.old: The hood is opened.
pair.new: You open the hood.

pair.old: The horn will sound and the hazard warning lights will blink continuously for approximately 27 seconds, and repeat the horn 3 times unless the system is disarmed. To turn off the system, unlock the doors with the folding key or smart key.
pair.new: Unless the system is disarmed, the horn sounds, the hazard warning lights blink continuously for approximately 27 seconds, and then the horn sounds 3 times. To turn the system OFF, unlock the doors with the mechanical key or smart key.

pair.old: The system will be disarmed when:
pair.new: The system disarms when:

pair.old: The door unlock button is pressed.
pair.new: You press the door unlock button.

pair.old: The button of the front outside door is pressed while carrying the smart key.
pair.new: You press the button on the front outside door handle with the smart key in your possession.

pair.old: The engine is started. (within 3 seconds)
pair.new: You start the engine (within 3 seconds).

pair.old: After pressing the unlock button, the hazard warning lights will blink and the chime will sound twice (in smart key) to indicate that the system is disarmed.
pair.new: After you press the unlock button, the hazard warning lights will blink, and the chime will sound twice (in the smart key) to indicate that the system is disarmed.

pair.old: After pressing the unlock button, if any door (or liftgate) is not opened within 30 seconds, the system will be rearmed.
pair.new: After you press the unlock button, if you do not open any door (or liftgate) within 30 seconds, the system will rearm.

pair.old: If you lose your keys, consult your authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If you lose your keys, consult an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Do not change, alter or adjust the theft alarm system in your vehicle. Improper installation of the alarm system could damage the vehicle or cause the system malfunction.
pair.new: Do not change, alter, or adjust the theft-alarm system in your vehicle. Improper installation of the alarm system could damage the vehicle or cause the system to malfunction.

pair.old: Malfunctions caused by improper alterations, adjustments or modifications to the theft-alarm system are not covered by your vehicle manufacturer warranty.
pair.new: Malfunctions caused by improper alterations, adjustments, or modifications to the theft-alarm system are not covered by your vehicle manufacturer warranty.

pair.old: Know how to use the door lock so that you can lock or unlock the door if necessary.
pair.new: Know how to use the door lock so that you can lock or unlock the doors and liftgate.

pair.old: Carrying the smart key, you may lock and unlock the vehicle doors (and liftgate). Also, you may start the engine. Refer to the following for more details.
pair.new: With the smart key, you can lock and unlock the vehicle doors and liftgate. You can also start the engine. Refer to the following sections for more details.

pair.old: Pressing the button of the front driver side door handles with all doors closed and any door unlocked, locks all the doors. If all doors and engine hood are closed, the hazard warning lights will blink once to indicate that all doors are locked.
pair.new: With all doors closed and any door unlocked, press the button of the front driver side door handle to lock all the doors. If all doors and the engine hood are closed, the hazard warning lights will blink once to indicate that all doors are locked.

pair.old: Pressing the button of the front driver side door handle unlocks all the doors. The hazard warning lights blink twice to indicate that all doors are unlocked.
pair.new: Press the button of the front driver side door handle to unlock all the doors. The hazard warning lights blink twice to indicate that all doors are unlocked.

pair.old: The door handle button will only operate when the smart key is within 40 inches (1 m) from the outside door handle.
pair.new: The door handle button will only operate when the smart key is within 40 inches (1 m) of the outside door handle.

pair.old: Pull out the door handle.
pair.new: Pull on the door handle.

pair.old: Press the lever (1) located inside the bottom part of the cover with a key or flat-head screwdriver.
pair.new: Press the lever (1) located inside the bottom part of the cover (2) with a key or flat-head screwdriver.

pair.old: Push out the cover (2) while pressing the lever (only the driver’s door can be locked/unlocked). Only the driver’s door can be locked or unlocked.
pair.new: Push out the cover (2) while pressing the lever.

pair.old: Turn the key toward the rear of the vehicle to unlock and toward the front of the vehicle to lock. Only the driver’s door can be locked or unlocked.
pair.new: Turn the key toward the rear of the vehicle to unlock and toward the front of the vehicle to lock.

pair.old: You can operate door locks with the door lock handle or central door lock switch.
pair.new: You can operate door locks from inside the vehicle with the door lock handle or central door lock switch.

pair.old: If the inner door handle is pulled when the door is locked, the door will unlock and open.
pair.new: If you pull the inner door handle when the door is locked, the door will unlock and open.

pair.old: If the inner door handle is pulled once when the door is locked, the door will unlock.
pair.new: If you pull the inner door handle 1 time when the door is locked, the door will unlock.

pair.old: If the inner door handle is pulled once more, the door will open.
pair.new: If you pull the inner door handle 1 more time, the door will open.

pair.old: If a power door lock fails to function while you are in the vehicle, try one or more of the following techniques to exit:
pair.new: If a power door lock does not work while you are in the vehicle, try 1 or more of the following techniques to exit:

pair.old: Do not pull the inner door handle of driver’s (or passenger’s) door while the vehicle is moving.
pair.new: Do not pull the inner door handle of any door while the vehicle is moving.

pair.old: Door Lock
pair.new: Door lock

pair.old: Door Unlock
pair.new: Door unlock

pair.old: Doors indicating light
pair.new: Doors indicator light

pair.old: Operate by pressing the central door lock switch.
pair.new: Press the central door lock switch to operate the door locks.

pair.old: To lock all vehicle doors, press the central door lock switch (1) of driver and passenger side.
pair.new: To lock all vehicle doors, press the central door lock switch (1) on the driver and passenger side.

pair.old: To unlock all vehicle doors, press central door unlock switch (2) of driver and passenger side.
pair.new: To unlock all vehicle doors, press central door unlock switch (2) on the driver and passenger side.

pair.old: When all vehicle doors are locked, the indicator lights (3) on the front doors will turn off. If any door is unlocked, it would turn on.
pair.new: When all vehicle doors are locked, the indicator lights (3) on the front doors will turn off. If any door is unlocked, its indicator light will turn on.

pair.old: If any door is opened, the doors will not lock even though the central door lock switch is pressed.
pair.new: If any door is open, the doors will not lock, even when the central door lock switch is pressed.

pair.old: The doors should always be fully closed and locked while the vehicle is in motion to prevent accidental opening of the door. Locked doors will also discourage potential intruders when the vehicle stops or slows down.
pair.new: The doors should always be fully closed and locked while the vehicle is in motion to prevent the doors from accidentally opening. Locked doors will also discourage potential intruders when the vehicle stops or slows down.

pair.old: Be careful when opening doors and watch out for vehicles, motorcycles, bicycles or pedestrians approaching the vehicle in the path of the door. Opening a door when something is approaching can result in an accident to cause vehicle damage or serious injury.
pair.new: Be careful when opening doors and watch out for vehicles, motorcycles, bicycles, or pedestrians approaching the vehicle in the path of the door. Opening a door when something is approaching can result in an accident and cause vehicle damage or serious injury.

pair.old: Leaving your vehicle unlocked can invite theft or possible harm to you or others from someone hiding in your vehicle while you are gone. Always remove the ignition key, engage the parking brake, close all windows and lock all doors when leaving your vehicle unattended.
pair.new: Leaving your vehicle unlocked can lead to someone hiding in it while you are gone. This could result in theft or possible harm to you or others. Always remove the ignition key, engage the parking brake, close all windows, and lock all doors when you leave your vehicle unattended.

pair.old: Unattended children, the elderly or pets
pair.new: Unattended children, the elderly, or pets

pair.old: All doors will automatically lock after the vehicle speed exceeds 10 mph (15 km/h).
pair.new: All doors will automatically lock when the vehicle speed exceeds 10 mph (15 km/h).

pair.old: When this feature is set in the cluster or infotainment system screen, all the doors will be locked automatically when the vehicle is shifted out of P (Park) while the vehicle is running.
pair.new: When this feature is displayed on the instrument cluster or infotainment system screen, all doors lock automatically when the vehicle is shifted out of P (Park) while running.

pair.old: When this feature is set in the infotainment system screen, all the doors will beunlocked automatically when the vehicleis shifted back into P (Park) while thevehicle is ON.
pair.new: When this feature enabled on the infotainment system screen, all the doors unlock automatically when the vehicle is shifted back into P (Park) while it is ON.

pair.old: When this feature is set in the cluster or infotainment system screen, all the doors will be unlocked automatically when the vehicle is turned off.
pair.new: When this feature is displayed on the instrument cluster or infotainment system screen, all doors unlock automatically when the vehicle is turned OFF.

pair.old: The child safety lock is provided to help prevent children from accidentally opening the rear doors from inside the vehicle.
pair.new: The child-protector rear door lock helps prevent children from accidentally opening the rear doors from inside the vehicle.

pair.old: The child safety locks are located on the edge of each rear door. When the child safety lock is in the lock position (1), the rear door will not open if the inner door handle is pulled.
pair.new: The child safety locks are located on the edge of each rear door. When the child safety lock is in the lock position (1), the rear door will not open if you pull the inner door handle.

pair.old: To activate the child safety lock, insert a key (or screwdriver) into the hole and turn it clockwise to the lock position.
pair.new: To activate the child safety lock, insert a key or screwdriver into the hole and turn it clockwise to the lock position.

pair.old: If children accidentally open the rear doors while the vehicle is in motion, they could fall out and be severely injured or killed. To prevent children from opening the rear doors from the inside, the rear door safety locks should be used whenever children are in the vehicle.
pair.new: If children accidentally open the rear doors while the vehicle is in motion, they could fall out and be severely injured or killed. To prevent children from opening the rear doors from the inside, use the rear door safety locks whenever children are in the vehicle.

pair.old: The driver and passenger are responsible for the accident occurred while exiting the vehicle. Always check the surrounding before you exit the vehicle.
pair.new: The driver and passenger are responsible for any accident that occurs while exiting the vehicle. Always check your surroundings before you exit the vehicle.

pair.old: To deactivate the electronic child safety lock, push the electronic child safety lock switch one more time and then the indicator turns off.
pair.new: To deactivate the electronic child safety lock, push the electronic child safety lock switch 1 more time, and then the indicator turns off.

pair.old: Safe Exit Assist is operated when the electronic child safety lock is activated and Safe Exit Assist is selected in the instrument cluster. However, Safe Exit Assist does not automatically activate the electronic child safety lock system.
pair.new: Safe Exit Assist operates when the electronic child safety lock is activated and Safe Exit Assist is selected in the instrument cluster. However, Safe Exit Assist does not automatically activate the electronic child safety lock system.

pair.old: The electronic child safety lock is always on when the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON or ignition ON state and for approximately 3 minutes after the engine is turned off.
pair.new: The electronic child safety lock is always on when the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON or ignition ON state and for approximately 3 minutes after the engine is turned OFF.

pair.old: If your vehicle is equipped with the Electronic child safety lock, the Child-protector rear door locks, which are manually operated, are not provided.
pair.new: If your vehicle is equipped with the electronic child safety lock, the manually operated child-protector rear door locks are not included.

pair.old: If electronic child safety lock is activated, rear passenger cannot open or close the rear window also. For more details, refer to .
pair.new: The rear passenger cannot open or close the rear window while the electronic child safety lock is activated. For more details, refer to .

pair.old: If the Electronic child safety lock malfunctions, a message is displayed and a chime will sound.
pair.new: If the electronic child safety lock malfunctions, a message is displayed, and a chime sounds.

pair.old: If the electrical power door lock switch is not operating (e.g., dead car battery) the only way to lock the door(s) is with the mechanical key from the outside key hole.
pair.new: If the electrical power door lock switch is not operating (for example, if the car battery is dead), the only way to lock the door(s) is with the mechanical key from the outside key hole.

pair.old: Insert the key (or a screwdriver) into the emergency door lock hole and turn the key to the lock position as shown.
pair.new: Insert a key or screwdriver into the emergency door lock hole and turn the key to the lock position as shown.

pair.old: If the electrical power to door lock switch is not operating (e.g., dead car battery) and the liftgate is closed, you will not be able to open the liftgate until power is restored.
pair.new: If the electrical power to the door lock switch is not operating (for example, if the car battery is dead) and the liftgate is closed, you will not be able to open the liftgate until the power is restored.

pair.old: Rear Occupant Alert (ROA) System
pair.new: Rear occupant alert (ROA) system

pair.old: Make sure that all the windows are closed when ROA is operational. If the window is open, the alert may be activated by wind, insects, leaves and other movement in the rear seat area.
pair.new: Make sure that all the windows are closed when the ROA is operational. If the window is open, the alert may be activated by wind, insects, leaves, or other movement in the rear seat area.

pair.old: When you open the front door after opening and closing the rear door and turning off the engine, the “Check rear seats” warning message appears on the cluster.
pair.new: When you open the front door after opening and closing the rear door and turning off the engine, the “Check rear seats” warning message appears on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: After the 1st alert, the 2nd alert operates when any movement is detected in the rear seat after the driver’s door is closed and all the doors are locked. The horn will activate for about 25 seconds. If the system continues to detect a movement the alert operates up to 8 times.
pair.new: After the 1st alert, the 2nd alert operates when any movement is detected in the rear seat after the driver’s door is closed and all the doors are locked. The horn will activate for about 25 seconds. If the system continues to detect movement, the alert operates up to 8 times.

pair.old: The 2nd alert is activated only after the prior activation of the 1st alert.
pair.new: The 2nd alert is activated only after the 1st alert has activated.

pair.old: You can activate or deactivate the ROA from the infotainment system, the option can be found under the following menu:
pair.new: You can activate or deactivate the ROA on the infotainment system screen as follows:

pair.old: Press the SETUP button of the infotainment system.
pair.new: Tap the SETUP button on the infotainment system.

pair.old: If you do not want to use the ROA system, press OK button on the steering wheel when the 1st alert is displayed on the cluster. Doing so will deactivate the 2nd alert once.
pair.new: If you do not want to use the ROA system, press the OK button on the steering wheel when the 1st alert is displayed on the cluster. It deactivates the 2nd alert once.

pair.old: If boxes or objects are stacked in the vehicle, the system may not detect the obstacle. Also, the warning may generate if the box or object falls off.
pair.new: If boxes or objects are stacked in the vehicle, the system may not detect the obstacle. The warning may also activate if a box or object falls.

pair.old: The alert may activate with the doors locked due to car wash or surrounding vibration or noise.
pair.new: A car wash or other surrounding vibration or noise may activate the alert with the doors locked.

pair.old: Inside movement detection is stopped under remote start status.
pair.new: Remote start stops inside movement detection.

pair.old: Even if your vehicle is equipped with the Rear Occupant Alert (ROA) system, always make sure you check the rear seat before you exit the vehicle.
pair.new: Even if your vehicle is equipped with the ROA system, always make sure you check the rear seat before you exit the vehicle.

pair.old: The movement does not continue for a certain period of time or the movement is small.
pair.new: The movement is small or does not continue for a certain period of time.

pair.old: If a child sits on a vehicle seat without a child car seat.
pair.new: A child sits on a vehicle seat without a child car seat.

pair.old: Also, always be cautious of the passenger’s safety as the detection function and 2nd alert may not operate depending on the surrounding environment and certain conditions.
pair.new: Always be cautious of the passenger’s safety, as the detection function and 2nd alert may not operate depending on the surrounding environment and certain conditions.

pair.old: If the seat or child restraint seat moves away from the sensor due to seat position adjustment.
pair.new: The seat or child restraint seat moves away from the sensor when the seat’s position is adjusted.

pair.old: The door lock system may not work if the electrical system is compromised. Accordingly, please train children passengers regarding how to open the car door manually before an emergency situation arises. That way, they would be able to open the door manually in the event an emergency situation arises.
pair.new: The door lock system may not work if the electrical system is compromised. Please train child passengers to open the car door manually before an emergency situation arises.

pair.old: Declaration of conformity The radio frequency components (ROA Radar Sensor) complies:
pair.new: Declaration of conformity. The radio frequency components (ROA Radar Sensor) complies:

pair.old: Kia Digital Key 2 provides convenience to the driver, such as locking or unlocking the driver and passenger doors or the liftgate and turning on the vehicle with a smart phone or card key, without a smart key.
pair.new: Kia Digital Key 2 allows the driver to lock or unlock the driver’s door, passenger doors, or liftgate, and also turn the vehicle ON using a smartphone or card key without a smart key.

pair.old: Digital Key 2 (Smart Phone)
pair.new: Digital Key 2 (smartphone)

pair.old: How to register Digital Key 2 (Smart Phone)
pair.new: How to register Digital Key 2 (smartphone)

pair.old: Smartphone Set Up
pair.new: Smartphone set-up

pair.old: To use Digital Key 2 (Smart phone) function, install the Kia Access app on your smart phone, register your information and subscribe the service.
pair.new: To use the Digital Key 2 (smartphone) function, install the Kia Access app on your smartphone, register your information, and subscribe to the service.

pair.old: Smart Phone Registration
pair.new: Smartphone Registration

pair.old: Turn the vehicle on with the Smart key and make sure to keep the smart key inside the vehicle during digital key registration.
pair.new: Turn the vehicle on with the smart key, and make sure to keep the smart key inside the vehicle during digital key registration.

pair.old: After pressing Digital Key SettingsRegister on Kia Access app, place the backside of the smart phone on the in-vehicle charging pad.
pair.new: After tapping Digital Key SettingsRegister on the Kia Access app, place the backside of the smartphone on the in-vehicle charging pad.

pair.old: As long as the device is supported from additional vehicle services, the Digital Key can be registered wirelessly.
pair.new: As long as additional vehicle services support the device, you can register the digital key wirelessly.

pair.old: Select Save menu on the instrument cluster or on the infotainment system screen. The saving process will begin automatically.
pair.new: Tap Save on the menu on the instrument cluster or on the infotainment system screen. The saving process will begin automatically.

pair.old: When the digital key (smart phone) is saved, a message will appear on the instrument cluster or the infotainment system screen.
pair.new: When you save the digital key (smartphone), a message will appear on the instrument cluster or the infotainment system screen.

pair.old: Remove the smart phone from the in-vehicle authentication pad (wireless charging pad) and complete the saving process by following the instructions on the smart phone screen.
pair.new: Remove the smartphone from the in-vehicle charging pad and complete the saving process by following the instructions on the smartphone screen.

pair.old: When there is a digital key (smart phone) already saved in the vehicle, Delete All will be displayed on the Smartphone Key menu screen of the infotainment system and Delete will be displayed on the My Smartphone Key screen. If you want to save a digital key again, .
pair.new: When there is a digital key (smartphone) already saved in the vehicle, the Smartphone Key menu of the infotainment system will display Delete All and the My Smartphone Key screen will display Delete. If you want to save a digital key again, .

pair.old: Smart Phone
pair.new: Smartphone

pair.old: The digital key cannot be saved again while the vehicle’s digital key is saved in the owner’s smart phone. Save the digital key after deleting the digital key from the Kia Access App.
pair.new: You cannot save the digital key again while the vehicle’s digital key is saved in your smartphone. Save the digital key after deleting it from the Kia Access App.

pair.old: During the digital key 2 saving process, the process will cancel when:
pair.new: The Digital Key 2 saving process will cancel when:

pair.old: The smart phone is removed from the in-vehicle authentication pad (wireless charging pad)
