pair.new: You remove the smartphone from the in-vehicle charging pad

pair.old: Changing the infotainment system or instrument cluster screen
pair.new: You change the infotainment system or instrument cluster screen

pair.old: The vehicle is turned off
pair.new: You turn off the vehicle

pair.old: The gear is shifted
pair.new: You shift the gear

pair.old: As the in-vehicle authentication pad may not work smoothly depending on the internal structure of the smartphones, the digital key may not be registered. In this case, register the device by moving the smartphone to the left or right of the in-vehicle authentication pad (wireless charging pad).
pair.new: The in-vehicle authentication pad may not work smoothly depending on the internal structure of the smartphone, so the digital key may not be registered. In this case, register the device by moving the smartphone to the left or right of the in-vehicle authentication pad (wireless charging pad).

pair.old: How to Use the Digital Key 2 (Smart Phone)
pair.new: How to use the Digital Key 2 (smartphone)

pair.old: Locking/Unlocking the doors
pair.new: Locking and unlocking the doors
Locking and unlocking the doors

pair.old: If the driver touches the smart phone antenna to the driver’s or passenger’s door handle authentication pad for more than 2 seconds, the door will lock or unlock.
pair.new: If the driver touches the smartphone antenna to the driver’s or passenger’s door handle authentication pad for more than 2 seconds, the door will lock or unlock.

pair.old: After unlocking the doors, the doors will automatically re-lock after 30 seconds unless a door is opened.
pair.new: After you unlock the doors, they will automatically relock after 30 seconds unless you open a door.

pair.old: If the smart phone digital key does not operate, move the smart phone more than 4 inches (0.1 m) from the door handle authentication pad and try it again.
pair.new: If the smartphone digital key does not operate, move the smartphone more than 4 inches (10 cm) from the door handle authentication pad and try it again.

pair.old: To lock or unlock the doors, carry your smartphone and touch the door handle lock/unlock sensor (curved area) on the outside door handle.
pair.new: To lock or unlock the doors, carry your smartphone and touch the door handle to the lock/unlock sensor (curved area) on the outside door handle.

pair.old: When you carry the smartphone supported by UWB and stay near the vehicle for about a few minutes, the door may not unlock automatically.
pair.new: When you carry a smartphone supported by UWB and stay near the vehicle for a few minutes, the door may not unlock automatically.

pair.old: If a tinting film containing metal components is applied, the digital key may not function properly.
pair.new: If you apply a tinting film containing metal components, the digital key may not function properly.

pair.old: When the Smart Key is in the vehicle
pair.new: The smart key is in the vehicle.
The smart key is in the vehicle

pair.old: The vehicle is in the ACC or ON position
pair.new: The vehicle is in the ACC or ON position.

pair.old: Attempting to lock the door when more than one door, hood, liftgate is opened
pair.new: You attempt to lock the door while more than 1 door, the hood, or the liftgate is open.

pair.old: How to delete Digital Key (Smart Phone)
pair.new: How to delete digital key (smartphone)

pair.old: Turn the vehicle on with the smart key and make sure to keep the smart key inside the vehicle during the digital key (smartphone) deleting process.
pair.new: Turn the vehicle on with the smart key and make sure to keep the smart key inside the vehicle during the digital key (smartphone) deletion process.

pair.old: To delete all digital keys
pair.new: To delete all digital keys:

old.old: Select Digital Keys

new.new: Select Digital Keys

old.old: Select Smartphone key

new.new: Select Smartphone key

old.old: Select Shared keys

new.new: Select Shared keys

old.old: Select Delete all

new.new: Select Delete all

pair.old: To delete one digital key
pair.new: To delete 1 digital key:

pair.old: Select Digital Keys
pair.new: Select Digital keys.

pair.old: Select Smartphone key
pair.new: Select Smartphone key.

pair.old: Select Delete
pair.new: Select Delete.

pair.old: If the owner’s smart phone has been changed, the new smart phone can be registered after only deleting the previous Digital Key 2 (Smart Phone).
pair.new: If you change smartphones, you can only register the new smartphone after deleting the previous Digital Key 2 (smartphone).

pair.old: Digital Key 2 (Card Key)
pair.new: Digital key 2 (card key)

pair.old: How to register Digital Key 2 (Card Key)
pair.new: How to register Digital Key 2 (card key)

old.old: To use the card key as a digital key 2, register the card key in your vehicle system in accordance with the following procedures.
To use the card key as a Digital Key 2, register the card key in your vehicle system according to the following procedures:

new.new: To use the card key as a Digital Key 2, register the card key in your vehicle system according to the following procedures:

pair.old: Enter the vehicle with two smart keys.
pair.new: Enter the vehicle with 2 smart keys.

pair.old: 1) In-vehicle authentication pad (wireless charging pad)
pair.new: 1) Wireless charging pad

pair.old: Check if Use menu is activated.
pair.new: Check if the Use menu is activated.

pair.old: With the vehicle on, touch SetupVehicleDigital KeysNFC Card keyUse on the infotainment system.
pair.new: With the vehicle on, tap SetupVehicleDigital keysNFC card keyUse on the infotainment system.

pair.old: With the vehicle on, place the card key on the wireless charging pad and press the Save menu on the infotainment system screen. The saving process will begin automatically.
pair.new: With the vehicle on, place the card key on the wireless charging pad, and tap Save on the infotainment system screen. The saving process will begin automatically.

pair.old: When the digital key (card key) is saved, a message will appear on the infotainment system screen.
pair.new: When the digital key (card key) is saved, a message appears on the infotainment system screen.

pair.old: When there is a digital key (card key) already saved in the vehicle, the Save menu is disabled. If you want to save a digital key again, refer to and follow the deleting procedure first before saving a digital key.
pair.new: When there is a digital key (card key) already saved in the vehicle, the Save menu is disabled. If you want to save a digital key again, refer to and follow the procedure for deleting an existing digital key first before saving a new digital key.

pair.old: To register the digital key (card key), the two smart keys must be in the vehicle.
pair.new: To register the digital key (card key), the 2 smart keys must be in the vehicle.

pair.old: How to use the Digital Key 2 (Card Key)
pair.new: How to use the Digital Key 2 (card key)

pair.old: The driver can lock and unlock the door by touching the card key on the door handle. The vehicle can be started by placing the card key on the charging pad.
pair.new: The driver can lock and unlock the door by touching the card key to the door handle. The driver can start the vehicle by placing the card key on the charging pad.

pair.old: After locking the door, make sure to check its locked state. After unlocking the doors, the doors will automatically re-lock after 30 seconds unless a door is opened.
pair.new: After locking the door, make sure to check its locked state. Unlocked doors will automatically relock after 30 seconds unless you open a door.

pair.old: It works only if the center of the NFC card key touches the NFC authentication area of the vehicle door handle.
pair.new: The lock works only if the center of the NFC card key touches the NFC authentication area of the vehicle door handle.

pair.old: When touching a smartphone NFC antenna to the center of the outside door handle authentication pad, the doors will not lock with a beep sound under the following conditions:
pair.new: When you touch a smartphone NFC antenna to the center of the outside door handle authentication pad, the doors will not lock with a beep sound under the following conditions:

pair.old: When the vehicle is in ACC or ON position
pair.new: The vehicle is in the ACC or ON position

pair.old: When one or more doors, hood or liftgate are open
pair.new: 1 or more doors, the hood, or the liftgate are open

pair.old: How to delete Digital Key 2 (Card Key)
pair.new: How to delete Digital Key 2 (card key)

pair.old: Turn the vehicle on with the smart key and make sure to keep the smart key inside the vehicle during the card key deleting process.
pair.new: Turn the vehicle ON with the smart key. Make sure to keep the smart key inside the vehicle during the card key deletion process.

pair.old: With the vehicle on, place the card key on the in-vehicle authentication pad. With the vehicle on, touch SetupVehicleDigital keyNFC card keyDelete on the infotainment system.
pair.new: With the vehicle ON, place the card key on the in-vehicle authentication pad. With the vehicle ON, tap SetupVehicleDigital keyNFC card keyDelete on the infotainment system.

pair.old: When the smartphone registered in the vehicle is linked with user profile, the vehicle will automatically operate (door lock/unlock with digital key, etc.) according to the linked user profile setting. User profile linking and personalization are available for the total of two drivers.
pair.new: When you link the smartphone registered in the vehicle with a user profile, the vehicle will automatically operate (for example, the door will lock and unlock with the digital key) according to the linked user profile settings. User profile linking and personalization are available for a total of 2 drivers.

pair.old: Linking / Unlinking profile
pair.new: Linking and unlinking profile
When the wipers no longer clean adequately, the blades may be worn or cracked, and require replacement.

pair.old: Select SetupUser profileProfile settingLink Digital Key (Smartphone) on the infotainment system settings menu.
pair.new: Tap SetupUser profileProfile settingLink digital key (smartphone) on the infotainment system settings menu.

pair.old: If you select Link, the registered phone number’s digital key and the user profile will link. Select Link according to the instruction.
pair.new: If you select Link, the registered phone number’s digital key and the user profile will link. Select Link according to the instructions.
If you tap Link, the registered phone number’s digital key will link to the user profile. Tap Link according to the instructions on the infotainment system screen.

pair.old: When the process is complete, the message ‘Digital Key Link Complete.’ will appear on the infotainment system screen.
pair.new: When the process is complete, the message “Digital Key Link Complete” will appear on the infotainment system screen.
When the process is complete, the message “Digital Key Link Complete” appears on the infotainment system screen.

pair.old: Click the Link button and select the smartphone you want to link from the smartphone list displayed on the screen. Link it according to the instructions.
pair.new: Tap the Link button, and select the smartphone you want to link from the smartphone list displayed on the screen. Link it according to the instructions on the infotainment system screen.

pair.old: No text change review in context.

Select SetupUser ProfileProfile SettingLink Digital Key (Smartphone) on the infotainment system. Unlink the smartphone in the User Profile settings. Unlink the smartphone in the User Profile settings. Unlinking is possible only when user profile is linked.
pair.new: Tap SetupUser profileProfile settingLink digital key (smartphone) on the infotainment system. Unlink the smartphone in the user profile settings.You can only unlink the smartphone when the user profile is linked.

pair.old: When unlinking is complete, the message 'Digital key is unlinked.' will appear on the infotainment system screen.
pair.new: When unlinking is complete, the message “Digital key is unlinked” will appear on the infotainment system screen.

pair.old: The user profile cannot be linked to both Driver 1 and Driver 2 that are connected to a single smartphone. Personalization will operate with the recently linked user profile, and the previously linked user profile will be automatically canceled.
pair.new: The user profile cannot be linked to multiple drivers (such as Driver 1 and Driver 2) that are connected to a single smartphone. Personalization will operate with the most recently linked user profile and automatically cancel the previously linked user profile.

pair.old: User profile can be linked when Digital Key is registered on the smartphone and the vehicle. The smartphone with another vehicle’s digital key cannot be linked.
pair.new: The user profile can be linked when the digital key is registered on the smartphone and the vehicle. The smartphone cannot be linked with another vehicle’s digital key.

pair.old: NFC card key cannot be linked with personalized profile.
pair.new: The NFC card key cannot be linked with a personalized profile.

pair.old: If you remove the smartphone from the charging pad before completing the user profile link, the linking process will not be completed normally.
pair.new: If you remove the smartphone from the charging pad before the user profile finishes linking, the linking process will not be completed.

pair.old: Once the user profile linked Digital Key in the smartphone is deleted, Digital Key 2 should be re-registered and personalized by linking the user profile again.
pair.new: Once the user profile linked to the digital key in the smartphone is deleted, reregister and personalize the digital key 2 by linking the user profile again.

pair.old: Touch the driver’s door handle with the profile linked smartphone to lock or unlock the doors (Personalization does not operate when locking or unlocking the front passenger door.).
pair.new: Touch the driver’s door handle with the smartphone linked to your profile to lock or unlock the doors. (Personalization does not operate when you lock or unlock the front passenger door.)

pair.old: The personalization function using the digital key can be operated after linking the digital key in the infotainment system profile menu.
pair.new: You can operate the personalization function using the digital key after you link the digital key in the infotainment system profile menu.

pair.new: Wireless charging ON/OFF

pair.new: Setting up temperature unit, Block air inflow/ Automatic ventilation, Window defroster ON/OFF

pair.new: Preferred navigation system volume

pair.old: CarPlay/Android Auto/MirrorLink On/Off
pair.new: CarPlay/Android Auto/MirrorLink ON/OFF

pair.old: For more information of personalization, refer to the infotainment system manual.
pair.new: For more information on personalization, refer to the infotainment system manual.

pair.old: If you leave the digital key in the vehicle after locking or unlocking the doors or starting the vehicle with the smart key, the doors can be locked with the central door lock. Always have the digital key with you.
pair.new: If you leave the digital key in the vehicle after locking or unlocking the doors or starting the vehicle with the smart key, you can lock the doors with the central door lock. Always have the digital key with you.

pair.old: Used vehicle/digital key 2 maintenance
pair.new: Digital Key 2 maintenance for a used vehicle

pair.old: Purchasing used vehicle
pair.new: Purchasing a used vehicle

pair.old: If any of the digital key devices (smartphone key, card key (if equipped)) are registered in the vehicle, the message “Digital key(s) active” will appear on the instrument cluster once when the vehicle is turned on after unlocking the vehicle door.
pair.new: If any of the digital key devices (such as a smartphone key or card key, if equipped) are registered in the vehicle, the message “Digital key(s) active” will appear on the instrument cluster once when you turn on the vehicle after unlocking the door.

pair.old: When purchasing a used car, be sure to check the message and delete the smart phone key and card key (if equipped) registered by the previous user. Be sure to notify an authorized Kia dealer when you purchase a used vehicle. Check whether the card key (if equipped) that came with the used vehicle operates properly. If the digital key (card key) (if equipped) does not work properly, delete the card key (if equipped) and register the smartphone key. Then re-register the card key (if equipped).
pair.new: When purchasing a used car, be sure to check the message and delete the smartphone key and card key (if equipped) registered by the previous user. Notify an authorized Kia dealer when you purchase a used vehicle. Check if the digital key (card key) that came with the used vehicle operates properly. If it does not work properly, delete it and register the smartphone key. Then reregister the card key.

pair.old: If you need to have your Digital Key 2 System repaired or replaced, the registered digital key (smartphone/card key) (if equipped) can be deleted depending on the type of maintenance.
pair.new: If you need to have your Digital Key 2 system repaired or replaced, the registered digital key (smartphone or card key, if equipped) may be deleted depending on the type of maintenance.

pair.old: Limitations of the System
pair.new: Limitations of the system

pair.old: Smartphone battery or the vehicle battery is discharged
pair.new: The smartphone battery or vehicle battery is discharged.

pair.old: NFC or Bluetooth is turned off in the smartphone settings
pair.new: You turn off the NFC or Bluetooth in the smartphone settings.

pair.old: A credit card overlaps the back of your smart phone, or a metal or thick smartphone case is used
pair.new: You use a metal or thick smartphone case, or a credit card overlaps the back of your smartphone.

pair.old: Using the card key (if equipped) with other cards, or using it in a wallet or card holder
pair.new: You use the card key (if equipped) with other cards or in a wallet or card holder.

pair.old: There is electronic interference by other vehicles, objects, etc.
pair.new: There is electronic interference by other vehicles or objects.

pair.old: There may be a communication error with Digital Key 2 NFC function if a metallic cover or communication device is attached to the smartphone. If there is a malfunction ofDigital Key 2, remove the cover attached to the smartphone and try again.
pair.new: There may be a communication error with the Digital Key 2 NFC function if a metallic cover or communication device is attached to the smartphone. If there is a Digital Key 2 malfunction, remove the cover attached to the smartphone and try again.

pair.old: The vehicle may not be controlled by the smartphone if any of the following occurs:
pair.new: The smartphone may not control the vehicle if any of the following occurs:

pair.old: Basic and necessary functions of the smartphone are operating (general call, urgent call, audio or contactless payment)
pair.new: Basic and necessary functions of the smartphone are operating (general call, urgent call, audio, or contactless payment).

pair.old: Using wireless earphone (general call, urgent call or audio)
pair.new: You are using wireless earphones (general call, urgent call, or audio).

pair.old: When Digital Key 2 app function is being limited due to smartphone default settings or app launch priority policy per manufacturer
pair.new: Digital Key 2 app function is limited due to the smartphone default settings or app launch priority policy per the manufacturer.

pair.old: Fingerprint authentication system allows the driver to have access to personal information, unlock profile and exit valet mode with an enrolled fingerprint.
pair.new: The fingerprint authentication system allows you to access your personal information, unlock your profile, and exit valet mode with an enrolled fingerprint.

pair.old: To use the system, the driver’s fingerprint must be enrolled in the driver’s profile. The drivers can set or delete their fingerprint through the infotainment system. Follow the following procedure.
pair.new: To use the system, the driver must enroll their fingerprint in their profile. The driver can set or delete their fingerprint through the infotainment system as follows:

pair.old: Enrolling fingerprint
pair.new: Enrolling your fingerprint

pair.old: Turn on the vehicle.
pair.new: Turn the vehicle ON.

pair.old: Select SetupUser ProfileDriver 1Fingerprint IdentificationSet/Delete FingerprintSet in the infotainment.
pair.new: Tap SetupUser profileDriver 1Fingerprint identificationSet/delete fingerprintSet on the infotainment system screen.

pair.old: Gently place your finger hat you would like to enroll on the fingerprint sensor (A) according to the instruction. Fingerprint authentication attempts with excessive force may fail
pair.new: Gently place the finger that you would like to enroll on the fingerprint sensor (A) according to the instruction. Fingerprint authentication attempts with excessive force may fail.

pair.old: Following the instructions, place several parts of your fingerprint until the scanning process is complete.
pair.new: Following the instructions, place different parts of your fingerprint on the fingerprint sensor until the scanning process is complete.

pair.old: Once the scanning process is completed, the message Saving fingerprint appears and the fingerprint enrollment process is complete.
pair.new: Once the scanning process is complete, the message “Saving fingerprint” appears, and the fingerprint enrollment process is complete.

pair.old: When the fingerprint enrollment process is completed in the vehicle, the status is displayed on the infotainment system.
pair.new: When the vehicle completes the fingerprint enrollment process, the infotainment system displays the status.

pair.old: The fingerprint enrollment process is canceled when the following condition occurs:
pair.new: The fingerprint enrollment process is canceled when the following conditions occur:
The fingerprint enrollment process is canceled when the following conditions occur:

pair.old: The infotainment system screen is altered.
pair.new: You alter the infotainment system screen.
You alter the infotainment system screen.

pair.old: The ENGINE START/STOP button turns on or off.
pair.new: You turn the ENGINE START/STOP button ON or OFF.
You turn the ENGINE START/STOP button ON or OFF.

pair.old: The gear is shifted and the vehicle is driven.
pair.new: You shift the gear and drive the vehicle.
You shift the gear and drive the vehicle.

pair.old: Deleting fingerprint
pair.new: Deleting your fingerprint

pair.old: Select SetupUser ProfileDriver 1Fingerprint IdentificationSet/Delete FingerprintDelete in the infotainment system screen.
pair.new: Tap SetupUser profileDriver 1Fingerprint identificationSet/delete fingerprintDelete on the infotainment system screen.

pair.old:
Once the fingerprint is deleted, the status is displayed on the infotainment system screen.
Once you delete the fingerprint, the infotainment system screen displays the status.

new.new: Once you delete the fingerprint, the infotainment system screen displays the status.

pair.old: The maximum of two fingerprint can be enrolled. Driver 1 and Driver 2 can enroll one fingerprint each.
pair.new: A maximum of 2 fingerprints can be enrolled. Driver 1 and Driver 2 can enroll 1 fingerprint each.

pair.old: Please remove all substances including protection film on the fingerprint sensor and enroll your fingerprint.
pair.new: Please remove all substances, including the protection film on the fingerprint sensor, before enrolling your fingerprint.

pair.old: Do not duplicate your fingerprint with anyone.
pair.new: Do not allow anyone to duplicate your fingerprint.

pair.old: Select: SetupUser ProfileDriver 1 (or Driver 2)Fingerprint Identification
pair.new: Tap SetupUser profileDriver 1 (or Driver 2)Fingerprint identification

pair.old: You cannot link a profile with the same fingerprint for Driver 1 and Driver 2. The personalization function works with the recently linked profile, and the previously linked profile will be automatically canceled.
pair.new: You cannot link a profile with the same fingerprint for Driver 1 and Driver 2. The personalization function works with the most recently linked profile, and the previously linked profile will be automatically canceled.

pair.old: Fingerprint authentication system may not work when:
pair.new: The fingerprint authentication system may not work when:

pair.old: Trying to authorize with unregistered fingerprint.
pair.new: You try to authorize the system with an unregistered fingerprint.

pair.old: Not touching the center of fingerprint authentication sensor.
pair.new: You are not touching the center of the fingerprint authentication sensor.

pair.old: The fingerprint is altered (e.g., damaged swollen, chapped.
pair.new: The fingerprint is altered (for example: damaged, swollen, or chapped).

pair.old: The sensor is touched too quickly.
pair.new: You touch the sensor too quickly.

pair.old: If fingerprint authentication fails over 5 consecutive times, fingerprint enrollment and deletion may be temporarily restricted. You should try it later or try other methods such as inputting your password.
pair.new: If fingerprint authentication fails more than 5 times consecutively, fingerprint enrollment and deletion may be temporarily restricted. Try it later, or try other methods (such as entering your password).

pair.old: When you visit an authorized Kia dealer for repairs parts due to fingerprint authorization system or related part failure, your registered fingerprint might be deleted. Have your smart key with you when you visit an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: When you visit an authorized Kia dealer for repairs due to a failure of the fingerprint authorization system related parts, your registered fingerprint might be deleted. Have your smart key with you when you visit an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: If you turn the vehicle on or off while proceeding the fingerprint authorization, the process will not complete.
pair.new: If you turn the vehicle ON or OFF during fingerprint authorization, the process will not complete.

pair.old: If you purchase a used vehicle, you should confirm and delete all of the enrolled fingerprints in Driver 1 and Driver 2 profile.
pair.new: If you purchase a used vehicle, confirm and delete all of the enrolled fingerprints in the Driver 1 and Driver 2 profiles.

pair.old: Integrated Memory System
pair.new: Integrated memory system

pair.old: Integrated Memory System for the driver’s seat is provided to store and recall the following memory settings with a simple button operation.
pair.new: Integrated memory system for the driver’s seat allows you to store and recall the following memory settings with a simple button operation.

pair.old: Outside rear view mirror position
pair.new: Outside rearview mirror position

pair.old: This could result in loss of control, and an accident causing death, serious injury, or property damage.
pair.new: This could result in a loss of control and an accident causing death, serious injury, or property damage.

pair.old: Shift to P (Park) while the Start/Stop button is in the ON position.
pair.new: Shift to P (Park) while the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON position.
Shift to P (Park) while the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON position.

pair.old: Adjust the driver’s seat position, outside rearview mirror position, steering wheel position, and head-up display height to the desired position.
pair.new: Adjust the driver’s seat, outside rearview mirror, steering wheel, and heads-up display height to the desired positions.

pair.old: Hold the button (1 or 2). The system will beep once and notify you ‘Driver 1 (or 2) settings saved’ will appear on the infotainment screen.
pair.new: Hold the button (1 or 2). The system will beep once and notify you.“Driver 1 (or 2) settings saved” will appear on the infotainment screen.

pair.old: Press the desired memory button (1 or 2). The system will beep once, and then the driver’s seat position, outside rearview mirror position, steering wheel position, and head-up display height will automatically adjust to the stored positions.
pair.new: Press the desired memory button (1 or 2). The system will beep once, and then the driver’s seat, outside rearview mirror, steering wheel, and heads-up display height will automatically adjust to the stored positions.

pair.old: Driver 1 (or 2) settings applied” will appear on the infotainment screen.
pair.new: “Driver 1 (or 2) settings applied” will appear on the infotainment screen.

pair.old: Seat easy access will move the driver’s seat and steering wheel automatically as follows:
pair.new: Seat easy access moves the driver’s seat and steering wheel automatically as follows:

pair.old: The driver’s seat will move as follows when the Start/Stop button is in the OFF position with the gear in P (Park) and the driver’s door open.
pair.new: The driver’s seat will move as follows when the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the OFF position with the gear in P (Park) and the driver’s door open.

pair.old: Driver seat: Moves rearward depending on the distance selected from the Settings menu in the infotainment system. However, the driver’s seat may not move rearward if there is not enough space between the driver’s seat and the rear seats.
pair.new: The driver’s seat moves backward depending on the distance selected in the infotainment system’s Settings menu. However, the driver’s seat will not move if there is not enough space between the driver’s seat and the rear seats because the seat is already back.

pair.old: You can set the Seat Easy Access function from the Settings menu in the infotainment system screen. Tap SetupVehicle SettingsSeatSeating Easy AccessDriver Seat Easy AccessNormal/Extended/Off.
pair.new: You can set the Seat Easy Access function from the infotainment system’s Settings menu. Tap SetupVehicle SettingsSeatSeating Easy AccessDriver Seat Easy AccessNormal/Extended/Off.

pair.old: Resetting the System
pair.new: Resetting the system

pair.old: Take the following procedures to reset integrated memory system, when it does not operate properly.
pair.new: Follow these procedures to reset the integrated memory system when it does not operate properly.

pair.old: Resetting integrated memory system
pair.new: Resetting the integrated memory system

pair.old: Stop the vehicle and open the driver’s door with the Start/Stop button in the ON position and the vehicle shifted to P (Park).
pair.new: Stop the vehicle and open the driver’s door with the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position and the gear shifted to P (Park).

pair.old: Press the memory button 1 (or 2) and push forward the driver’s seat movement switch over 2 seconds simultaneously.
pair.new: For 2 seconds, simultaneously press the memory button 1 (or 2) and push the driver’s seat movement switch forward.

pair.old: While resetting integrated memory system
pair.new: While resetting the integrated memory system

pair.old: Resetting starts with a notification sound.
pair.new: A notification will sound.

pair.old: The driver’s seat and seatback is adjusted to the rearward position with the notification sound.
pair.new: The driver’s seat and seatback are adjusted backward when the notification sounds.

pair.old: The driver’s seat and seatback is re-adjusted to the default position (central position) with the notification sound. However, in the following cases, the resetting procedure and the notification sound may stop.
pair.new: The driver’s seat and seatback readjusts to the default position (central position) with the notification sound. However, in the following cases, the resetting procedure and the notification sound may stop:

pair.old: The memory button is pressed.
pair.new: You press the memory button.

pair.old: The seat control switch is operated.
pair.new: You operate the seat control switch.

pair.old: The gear is shifted out of P (Park).
pair.new: You shift the gear out of P (Park).

pair.old: The driving speed exceeds 3 mph (5 km/h).
pair.new: You drive faster than 3 mph (5 km/h).

pair.old: The driver’s door is closed.
pair.new: You close the driver’s door.

pair.old: While integrated memory system is being reset, if the resetting and notification sound stops incompletely, restart the resetting procedure again.
pair.new: If resetting and the notification sound stop before the integrated memory system reset process is complete, restart the procedure.

pair.old: Make sure that there is no objects around the driver’s seat in advance of resetting the integrated memory system.
pair.new: Make sure that there are no objects around the driver’s seat before resetting the integrated memory system.

pair.old: After resetting the integrated memory system, the adjustment for the driver seat must be stored again to recall the memory position.
pair.new: After resetting the integrated memory system, you must store the adjustment for the driver’s seat again to recall the memory position.

pair.old: If you drive with the liftgate open, you will draw dangerous exhaust fumes into your vehicle which can cause serious injury or death to vehicle occupants.
pair.new: If you drive with the liftgate open, you will draw dangerous exhaust fumes into your vehicle, which can cause serious injury to or the death of the vehicle occupants.

pair.old: Only the liftgate is unlocked if the liftgate unlock button on the transmitter or smart key is pressed for approximately 1 second.
pair.new: If you press the liftgate unlock button on the smart key for approximately 1 second, only the liftgate unlocks.

pair.old: If unlocked, the liftgate can be opened by pressing the handle and pulling it up.
pair.new: If the liftgate is unlocked, you can open it by pressing the handle and pulling it up.

pair.old: Once the liftgate is opened and then closed, the liftgate locks automatically. (All doors must be locked.)
pair.new: Once you open and close the liftgate, it locks automatically. All doors must be locked.

pair.old: In cold and wet climates, door lock and door mechanisms may not work properly due to freezing conditions.
pair.new: In cold and wet climates, the door lock and door mechanisms may not work properly due to freezing conditions.

pair.old: Make certain that you close the liftgate before driving your vehicle. Possible damage may occur to the liftgate gas lifters and attached hardware if the liftgate is not closed prior to driving.
pair.new: Make sure to close the liftgate before driving your vehicle. Possible damage to the liftgate gas lifters and attached hardware may occur if you do not close the liftgate prior to driving.
Make sure to close the liftgate before driving your vehicle. Possible damage to the liftgate gas lifters and attached hardware may occur if you do not close the liftgate prior to driving.

pair.old: To close the liftgate, lower and push down the liftgate firmly. Make sure that the liftgate is securely latched.
pair.new: To close the liftgate, lower and push the liftgate down firmly. Make sure that the liftgate is securely latched.

pair.old: Make sure your hands, feet and other parts of your body are safely out of the way before closing the liftgate.
pair.new: Make sure your hands, feet, and other parts of your body are safely out of the way before closing the liftgate.

pair.old: The liftgate lid should be always kept completely closed while the vehicle is in motion. If it is left open or ajar, poisonous exhaust gases may enter the car and serious illness or death may result.
pair.new: You should always keep the liftgate lid completely closed while the vehicle is in motion. If you leave the liftgate open or ajar, poisonous exhaust gases may enter the car and serious illness or death may result.

pair.old: Make sure nothing is near the liftgate latch and striker while closing the liftgate. It may damage the liftgate’s latch.
pair.new: To avoid damage to the liftgate’s latch, make sure nothing is near the latch and striker while closing the liftgate.

pair.old: To unlock and open the liftgate manually from inside the luggage compartment, perform the following procedure:
pair.new: To unlock and open the liftgate manually from inside the luggage compartment, follow these steps:
To unlock and open the liftgate manually from inside the luggage compartment, follow these steps:

pair.old: Insert a long, flat object, such as a key into the opening at the bottom of the liftgate.
pair.new: Insert a long, flat object (such as a key) into the opening at the bottom of the liftgate.
Insert a long, flat object (such as a key) into the opening at the bottom of the liftgate.

pair.old: Push the liftgate to open.
pair.new: Push the liftgate to open it.
Push the liftgate to open it.

pair.old: No one should be allowed to occupy the cargo area of the vehicle at any time. The cargo area is a very dangerous location in the event of a crash.
pair.new: No person should be allowed to occupy the cargo area of the vehicle at any time. The cargo area is a very dangerous location in the event of a crash.

pair.old: Make sure there are no people or objects around the liftgate before opening or closing the liftgate. Wait until the liftgate is open fully and stopped before loading or unloading cargo from the vehicle.
pair.new: Make sure there are no people or objects around the liftgate before opening or closing it. Wait until the liftgate has opened fully and stopped moving before loading or unloading cargo from the vehicle.

pair.old: The power liftgate operates when the gear is in P (Park) with the vehicle running. However, the power liftgate will operate regardless of the gear position when the vehicle is off. Also, the liftgate can be opened only when vehicle speed is below 1.8 mph (3 km/h).
pair.new: The power liftgate operates when the gear is in P (Park) and the vehicle is running. However, the power liftgate will operate regardless of gear position when the vehicle is OFF. The liftgate can only be opened when vehicle speed is below 1.8 mph (3 km/h).

pair.old: For safety, before attempting to open or close the liftgate, make sure the vehicle is in P (Park).
pair.new: For safety purposes, make sure the gear is in P (Park) before attempting to open or close the liftgate.

pair.old: Make sure that there are no people or objects in the path of the power liftgate or smart liftgate prior to use. Serious injury, damage to the vehicle or damage to surrounding objects (for example, walls, ceilings, vehicles, etc.) may result if contact with the liftgate occurs.
pair.new: Make sure that there are no people or objects in the path of the power liftgate or smart liftgate prior to use. Contact with the liftgate may result in serious injury, damage to the vehicle, or damage to surrounding objects (for example: walls, ceilings, vehicles, and so on).

pair.old: The liftgate may not open or may close unintentionally injuring people around the liftgate under the following situation:
pair.new: In the following situations, the liftgate may not open or may close unintentionally, injuring people:

pair.old: There is a heavy object on the liftgate such as a bicycle carrier, ladder, etc.
pair.new: There is a heavy object on the liftgate (such as a bicycle carrier, ladder, and so on).

pair.old: Do not open the liftgate before removing snow or heavy object on the liftgate.
pair.new: Do not open the liftgate before removing snow or heavy objects from the liftgate.

pair.old: Do not close or open the liftgate manually. This may cause damage to the power liftgate. If it is necessary to close or open the liftgate manually when the battery is discharged or disconnected, do not apply excessive force.
pair.new: Do not close or open the liftgate manually. This may cause damage to the power liftgate. If you must close or open the liftgate manually when the battery is discharged or disconnected, do not apply excessive force.

pair.old: Do not operate the power liftgate more than 10 times continuously when the vehicle is not running. Use the power liftgate with the vehicle running when the power liftgate is used repeatedly to prevent battery discharge.
pair.new: When the vehicle is not running, do not operate the power liftgate more than 10 times continuously. To prevent battery discharge, only use the power liftgate repeatedly while the vehicle is running.

pair.old: Do not apply excessive force when the power liftgate is operating. Doing so could result in vehicle damage.
pair.new: Do not apply excessive force when operating the power liftgate. Doing so could damage the vehicle.

pair.old: Do not grab or hold on to the liftgate support struts at any time. Damage to the liftgate support struts could result. Deformation of the liftgate support struts may cause vehicle damage and personal injury may occur.
pair.new: Do not grab or hold on to the liftgate support struts at any time, as this could damage them. Deformation of the liftgate support struts may damage the vehicle and lead to personal injury.

pair.old: Do not operate the power liftgate under the following conditions. The power liftgate may not operate properly.
pair.new: Do not operate the power liftgate under the following conditions, as the power liftgate may not operate properly:

pair.old: One side of the vehicle is lifted to inspect the vehicle or change a tire
pair.new: When lifting 1 side of the vehicle to inspect it or change a tire.

pair.old: Parking on an uneven road such as a slope, etc.
pair.new: When parking on an uneven road, such as a slope.

pair.old: Close the liftgate completely and lock all doors and liftgate using the central door lock button before using an automatic car wash.
pair.new: Before using an automatic car wash, close the liftgate completely and lock all doors and the liftgate using the central door lock button.

pair.old: Do not spray high pressure water directly on the power liftgate outside open/close button. The liftgate may open unintentionally.
pair.new: Do not spray high pressure water directly on the outside power liftgate open/close button. The liftgate may open unintentionally.

pair.old: If the liftgate is not fully closed and vehicle speed is at or above 1.8 mph (3 km/h), a warning will sound 10 times. Immediately park the vehicle at a safe place, close the liftgate, and check that the liftgate open warning on the instrument cluster is turned off.
pair.new: If the liftgate is not fully closed and the vehicle speed is at or above 1.8 mph (3 km/h), a warning will sound 10 times. Immediately park the vehicle in a safe place, close the liftgate, and check that the liftgate open warning on the instrument cluster is turned off.

pair.old: In cold and wet climates, the outside power liftgate open/close button may not work properly due to freezing conditions. If this occurs, remove the ice before using the outside power liftgate open/close button or use the power liftgate open/close button on the smart key or the instrument panel.
pair.new: In cold and wet climates, the outside power liftgate open/close button may not work properly due to freezing conditions. If this occurs, remove ice from the outside power liftgate open/close button before using it, or use the power liftgate open/close button on the smart key or the instrument panel.

pair.old: Operating the power liftgate more than 5 times continuously could cause damage to the operating motor. If this occurs, the power liftgate will not operate to prevent the motor from overheating. If any of the power liftgate buttons are pressed to try to open the liftgate, the chime will sound 3 times, but the liftgate will remain closed. Allow the power liftgate system to cool for about 1 minute before operating the system again.
pair.new: Operating the power liftgate more than 5 times continuously could cause damage to the operating motor. If this occurs, the power liftgate will not operate to prevent the motor from overheating. If you press any of the power liftgate buttons to try to open the liftgate, the chime will sound 3 times, but the liftgate will remain closed. Allow the power liftgate system to cool for about 1 minute before operating the system again.

pair.old: Power liftgate open/close button (Smart key, Instrument panel)
pair.new: Power liftgate open/close button (smart key, instrument panel)

pair.old: Press the power liftgate open/close button for 1 second. The liftgate opens or closes with a warning sound. While the liftgate is opening or closing, press the button to stop liftgateoperation
pair.new: Press the power liftgate open/close button for 1 second. The liftgate opens or closes with a warning sound. While the liftgate is opening or closing, press the button to stop liftgate operation.

pair.old: If the smart key is not within operation range (approximately 10 m) from the vehicle, liftgate operation will stop with a warning sound for 5 seconds.
pair.new: If the smart key is not within operating range (approximately 10 m) from the vehicle, liftgate operation will stop with a warning sound for 5 seconds.

pair.old: Power liftgate open/close button (Outside the power liftgate)
pair.new: Power liftgate open/close button (outside the power liftgate)

pair.old: When the liftgate is closed, press the power liftgate open/close button to open the liftgate.
pair.new: When the liftgate is closed, press the power liftgate open/close button to open it.

pair.old: If the liftgate is unlocked, the liftgate will open or close with a warning sound when the power liftgate open/close button is pressed without carrying the smart key.
pair.new: If the liftgate is unlocked, it will open or close with a warning sound when you press the power liftgate open/close button without carrying the smart key.

pair.old: Power liftgate open/close button (Inside the power liftgate)
pair.new: Power liftgate open/close button (inside the power liftgate)

pair.old: During power liftgate operation if the power liftgate senses any obstacle, the liftgate will stop or will fully open. The automatic reverse feature may not operate properly, or it may operate unexpectedly under the following circumstances:
pair.new: During power liftgate operation, if the power liftgate senses any obstacle, the liftgate will either stop or fully open. The automatic reverse feature may not operate properly, or it may operate unexpectedly under the following circumstances:

pair.old: The automatic reverse feature may not detect the resistance if the detected resistance is below a certain level, or if the liftgate is almost fully closed near the latched position.
pair.new: The automatic reverse feature may not detect resistance if it is below a certain level, or if the liftgate is almost fully closed near the latched position.

pair.old: Never intentionally place any object or part of your body in the path of the power liftgate to make sure the automatic reverse feature operates. Serious injury, or damage to the vehicle or object may occur.
pair.new: Never intentionally place any object or part of your body in the path of the power liftgate to make sure the automatic reverse feature operates. This may result in serious injury or damage to the vehicle.

pair.old: The power liftgate may stop operating if the automatic reverse feature operates more than two times while attempting to open or close the liftgate. If this occurs, carefully open or close the liftgate manually, and then after 30 seconds try to operate the power liftgate automatically again.
pair.new: The power liftgate may stop operating if the automatic reverse feature operates more than 2 times while you attempt to open or close the liftgate. If this occurs, carefully open or close the liftgate manually, and then after 30 seconds, try to operate the power liftgate automatically again.

pair.old: To adjust the power liftgate speed, select SetupVehicleDoorPower Liftgate Opening SpeedNormal/Fast in the infotainment system. (Default setting is Fast)
pair.new: To adjust the power liftgate speed, tap SetupVehicleDoorPower liftgate opening speedNormal/Fast in the infotainment system. (The default setting is Fast.)

pair.old: To adjust the power liftgate opening height, select SetupVehicleDoorPower Liftgate Opening HeightFull Open/Level 3/Level 2/Level 1/User Height Setting in the infotainment system.
pair.new: To adjust the power liftgate opening height, tap SetupVehicleDoorPower liftgate opening heightFull Open/Level 3/Level 2/Level 1/User height setting in the infotainment system.

pair.old: See additional information in supplied Infotainment Manual.
pair.new: See additional information in the supplied infotainment manual.
See additional information in the supplied infotainment manual.

pair.old: Position the liftgate manually to the height you prefer.
pair.new: Position the liftgate manually to your preferred height.

pair.old: Press the power liftgate open/close inner button and outer button simultaneously until a chime sounds.
pair.new: Press the power liftgate open/close inner and outer buttons simultaneously until a chime sounds.

pair.old: Wait until the liftgate fully opens to complete resetting. If the liftgate stops before it is fully open, resetting cannot be completed.
pair.new: Wait until the liftgate fully opens to complete the reset. If the liftgate stops before it is fully open, the reset cannot be completed.

pair.old: If the power liftgate does not operate properly after the above procedure, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the power liftgate does not operate properly after you perform the preceding procedure, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Power Liftgate automatic close
pair.new: Power liftgate automatic close

pair.old: Using power liftgate automatic close feature
pair.new: Using the power liftgate automatic close feature

pair.old: When the liftgate is open, approach the detecting area, which is 20 ~ 40 inches (50 ~ 100 cm) from behind the liftgate with the smart key in your possession. Wait for approximately 3 seconds until the chime sounds.
pair.new: When the liftgate is open, approach the detecting area (20–40 in [50–100 cm] behind the liftgate) with the smart key in your possession. Wait approximately 3 seconds until the chime sounds.

pair.old: With the smart key, move away from the vehicle. The liftgate closes automatically with a chime sound.
pair.new: With the smart key in your possession, move away from the vehicle. The liftgate closes automatically with a chime sound.

pair.old: Setting power liftgate automatic close
pair.new: Setting the power liftgate automatic close feature

pair.old: With the engine on, select SetupVehicleDoorPower Liftgate to turn on Power Liftgate Auto Close and deselect to turn off the feature in the infotainment system.
pair.new: tap SetupVehicleDoorPower liftgate to turn on Power Liftgate Auto Close, and deselect to turn off the feature in the infotainment system.

pair.old: On a vehicle equipped with a smart key, the liftgate can be opened using the Smart Liftgate with Auto Open system.
pair.new: On a vehicle equipped with a smart key, you can open the liftgate using the Smart Liftgate with AUTO Open system.

pair.old: The liftgate can be opened with no-touch activation satisfying all the conditions below.
pair.new: You can open the liftgate with no-touch activation under the following circumstances:

pair.old: After 15 seconds when all doors are closed and locked
pair.new: After 15 seconds, when all doors are closed and locked

pair.old: Positioned in the detecting area for more than 3 seconds.
pair.new: When you are positioned in the detecting area for more than 3 seconds

pair.old: The Smart Liftgate with Auto Open does not operate when:
pair.new: The smart liftgate with AUTO open does not operate when:

pair.old: The smart key is detected within 15 seconds after the doors are closed and locked, and is continuously detected.
pair.new: The smart key is detected within 15 seconds after the doors are closed and locked, and is continuously detected afterward.

pair.old: The smart key is detected within 15 seconds after the doors are closed and locked, and 60 in (1.5 m) from the front door handles. (for vehicles equipped with Welcome Light)
pair.new: The smart key is detected within 15 seconds after the doors are closed and locked, and is 60 in (1.5 m) from the front door handles (for vehicles equipped with Welcome Light).

pair.old: The smart key is in the vehicle.
pair.new: The smart key is inside the vehicle.

pair.old: To activate the Smart Liftgate with Auto Open, go to SetupVehicleDoorSmart Liftgate in the infotainment system.
pair.new: To activate the smart liftgate with AUTO open, go to SetupVehicleDoorSmart liftgate in the infotainment system.

pair.old: Detect and Alert
pair.new: Detect and alert

pair.old: If you are positioned in the detecting area (20 ~ 40 in [50 ~ 100 cm] behind the vehicle) carrying a smart key, the hazard warning lights will blink and chime will sound to alert you the smart key has been detected and the liftgate will open.
pair.new: If you are positioned in the detecting area (20–40 in [50–100 cm] behind the vehicle) while carrying a smart key, the hazard warning lights will blink and the chime will sound to alert you that the smart key has been detected. The liftgate will open.

pair.old: Do not approach the detecting area if you do not want the liftgate to open. If you have unintentionally entered the detecting area and the hazard warning lights and chime starts to operate, leave the detecting area with the smart key. The liftgate will stay closed.
pair.new: Do not approach the detecting area if you do not want the liftgate to open. If you have unintentionally entered the detecting area and the hazard lights and chime start to operate, leave the detecting area with the smart key in your possession. The liftgate will stay closed.

pair.old: The hazard warning lights will blink and chime 6 times and then the liftgate will open.
pair.new: The hazard warning lights will blink and the chime will sound 6 times, and then the liftgate will open.

pair.old: Make sure there are no people or objects around the liftgate before opening or closing the liftgate.
pair.new: Make sure there are no people or objects around the liftgate before opening or closing it.

pair.old: Make sure to deactivate the Smart Liftgate with Auto Open when washing your vehicle. Otherwise, the liftgate may open inadvertently.
pair.new: Make sure to deactivate the smart liftgate with AUTO open when washing your vehicle. Otherwise, the liftgate may open accidentally.

pair.old: The key should be kept out of reach of children. Children may inadvertently open the Smart Liftgate with Auto Open while playing around the rear area of the vehicle.
pair.new: Keep the key out of reach of children. Children may accidentally open the smart liftgate with AUTO open while playing around the rear area of the vehicle.

pair.old: Liftgate lift
pair.new: Liftgate gas lifters

pair.old: If you press any button of the smart key during the Detect and Alert stage, the Smart Liftgate with Auto Open function will be deactivated.
pair.new: If you press any button on the smart key during the Detect and Alert stage, the smart liftgate with AUTO open function will deactivate.

pair.old: Make sure to be aware of how to deactivate the Smart Liftgate with Auto Open function for emergency situations.
pair.new: Make sure you know how to deactivate the smart liftgate with AUTO open function for emergency situations.

pair.old: If you press the door unlock button (2), the Smart Liftgate with Auto Open function will be deactivated temporarily. But, if you do not open any door for 30 seconds, the Smart Liftgate with Auto Open function will be activated again.
pair.new: If you press the door unlock button (2), the smart liftgate with AUTO open function will deactivate temporarily. But if you do not open any door for 30 seconds, the smart liftgate with AUTO open function will activate again.

pair.old: If you press the liftgate open button (3) for more than 1 second, the liftgate opens.
pair.new: If you press the liftgate open button (3) for more than 1 second, the liftgate will open.

pair.old: If you press the door lock button (1) or liftgate open button (3) when the Smart Liftgate with Auto Open function is not in the Detect and Alert stage, the Smart Liftgate with Auto Open function will not be deactivated.
pair.new: If you press the door lock button (1) or liftgate open button (3) when the smart liftgate with AUTO open function is not in the Detect and Alert stage, the smart liftgate with AUTO open function will not deactivate.

pair.old: Detecting area
pair.new: Detection area

pair.old: The Smart Liftgate with Auto Open operates with a welcome alert if the smart key is detected within 20 ~ 40 in (50 ~ 100 cm) from the liftgate.
pair.new: The smart liftgate with AUTO open function operates with a welcome alert if the smart key is detected within 20–40 in (50–100 cm) of the liftgate.

pair.old: The smart key is close to a radio transmitter such as a radio station or an airport which can interfere with normal operation of the transmitter.
pair.new: The smart key is close to a radio transmitter, such as a radio station or an airport, which can interfere with normal operation of the transmitter.

pair.old: The smart key is near a mobile two way radio system or a mobile phone.
pair.new: The smart key is near a mobile two-way radio system or a mobile phone.

pair.old: The vehicle is slantingly parked on a slope or unpaved road, etc.
pair.new: The vehicle is parked on a slant, a slope, an unpaved road, and so on.

pair.old: The hood serves as a cover for the engine compartment.
pair.new: The hood is a cover for the engine compartment.

pair.old: Open the hood if maintenance work needs to be performed in the engine compartment or if you need to look at the compartment.
pair.new: Open the hood if you need to look at the engine compartment or have maintenance work performed in the compartment.

pair.old: Pull the release lever to unlatch the hood. The hood should slightly pop open.
pair.new: Pull the release lever to unlatch the hood. The hood will pop open slightly.

pair.old: Go to the front of the vehicle, raise the hood slightly, push the secondary hood release lever (1) to the left and lift the hood (2).
pair.new: Go to the front of the vehicle, raise the hood slightly, push the secondary hood release lever (1) to the left, and lift the hood (2).

pair.old: Hold the hood open with the support
pair.new: Prop the hood open with the support rod.

pair.old: After driving, the engine compartment and support rod will be hot. Grasp the support rod in the area wrapped in rubber to prevent burns.
pair.new: After you drive, the engine compartment and support rod will be hot. Grasp the support rod in the area wrapped in rubber to prevent burns.

pair.old: The support rod must be inserted completely into the hole provided whenever you inspect the engine compartment. This will prevent the hood from falling and possibly injuring you.
pair.new: You must insert the support rod completely into the hole provided whenever you inspect the engine compartment. This will prevent the hood from falling and possibly injuring you.

pair.old: The hood warning message will appear on the LCD display when hood is open.
pair.new: The hood warning message will appear on the LCD display when the hood is open.

pair.old: The warning chime will operate when the vehicle is being driven at or above 2 mph (3 km/h) with the hood open.
pair.new: The warning will chime when you drive the vehicle at or above 2 mph (3 km/h) with the hood open.

pair.old: Gloves, rags or any other combustible material must be removed from the engine compartment.
pair.new: Gloves, rags, or any other combustible material must be removed from the engine compartment.

pair.old: Lower the hood until it is about 12 in. (30 cm) above the closed position and let it drop. Make sure that it locks into place.
pair.new: Lower the hood until it is about 12 in (30 cm) above the closed position and let it drop. Make sure that it locks into place.

pair.old: Check that the hood has engaged properly.
pair.new: Check that the hood has latched properly.

pair.old: Before closing the hood, ensure that all obstructions are removed from the hood opening. Closing the hood with an obstruction present in the hood opening may result in severe personal injury or property damage.
pair.new: Before closing the hood, make sure that all obstructions are removed from the hood opening. Closing the hood with an obstruction present in the hood opening may result in severe personal injury or property damage.

pair.old: Do not leave gloves, rags or any other combustible material in the motor compartment. Doing so may cause a heat-induced fire.
pair.new: Do not leave gloves, rags, or any other combustible material in the motor compartment. Doing so may cause a heat-induced fire.

pair.old: Always double check to be sure that the hood is firmly latched before driving away. If it is not latched, the hood could fly open while the vehicle is being driven, causing a total loss of visibility, which may result in an accident.
pair.new: Always double check to be sure that the hood is firmly latched before driving away. If it is not latched, the hood could fly open while you drive the vehicle, causing a total loss of visibility, which may result in an accident.

pair.old: The vehicle’s fuel filler door must be opened and closed by hand from outside the vehicle.
pair.new: You must open and close the vehicle’s fuel filler door by hand from outside the vehicle.

pair.old: Turn the engine off.
pair.new: Turn the engine OFF.

pair.old: Ensure the driver’s door is unlocked for unlocking fuel filler door.
pair.new: Make sure the driver’s door is unlocked.

pair.old: Press the rear center edge of fuel filler door.
pair.new: Press the rear center edge of the fuel filler door.

pair.old: Pull the fuel filler door (1) out to fully open.
pair.new: Pull the fuel filler door (1) out to fully open it.

pair.old: Closing the fuel filler door
pair.new: Closing and locking the fuel filler door

pair.old: To install the fuel tank cap, turn it clockwise until it “Clicks”.
pair.new: To install the fuel tank cap, turn it clockwise until it clicks.

pair.old: Close the fuel filler door by pressing rear center edge of the fuel filler door.
pair.new: Close the fuel filler door by pressing its rear center edge.

pair.old: The doors of this vehicle are equipped with power windows that can be operated by a switch.
pair.new: The doors of this vehicle are equipped with power windows that you can operate with a switch.

pair.old: Automatic power window up*/down*
pair.new: Automatic power window up or down*

pair.old: ENGINE START/STOP button must be in the ON position for power windows to operate.
pair.new: The ENGINE START/STOP button must be in the ON position for the power windows to operate.

pair.old: Each door has a power window switch that controls the door’s window. The driver has a power window lock button which can block the operation of rear passenger windows. The power windows can be operated for approximately 3 minutes after ENGINE START/STOP button turned to the ACC or LOCK position. If the front doors are opened, the power windows cannot be operated even within the 3 minutes period.
pair.new: Each door has a power window switch that controls that door’s window. The driver has a power window lock button, which can block the operation of rear passenger windows. You can operate the power windows for approximately 3 minutes after turning the ENGINE START/STOP button to the ACC or LOCK position. If you open the front doors, you cannot operate the power windows, even within the 3-minute period.

pair.old: The driver’s door has a master power window switch that controls all the windows in the vehicle.
pair.new: If the window cannot close because objects are blocking it, remove the objects and close the window.

pair.old: If the window cannot be closed because it is blocked by objects, remove the objects and close the window.
pair.new: If the window cannot close because objects are blocking it, remove the objects and close the window.

pair.old: If you press the one-touch window button for micro adjustment, the glass will go down to a specific location to improve your convenience.
pair.new: If you press the 1-touch window button to make a small adjustment, the glass will go down to a specific location for your convenience.

pair.old: You can open and close windows using the power window switch.
pair.new: You can open and close the windows using the power window switch.

pair.old: To open or close a window, press down or pull up the front portion of the corresponding switch to the first detent position (5).
pair.new: To open or close a window, press down or pull up the front portion of the corresponding switch to the first position (5).

pair.old: Pressing or pulling up the power window switch momentarily to the second detent position (6) completely lowers or raises the window even when the switch is released. To stop the window at the desired position while the window is in operation, pull up or press down and release the switch.
pair.new: Momentarily pressing down or pulling up the power window switch to the second position (6) completely lowers or raises the window, even when you release the switch. To stop the window at the desired position while it is moving, pull up or press down and release the switch.

pair.old: If the power window does not operate normally, the automatic power window system must be reset as follows:
pair.new: If the power window does not operate normally, reset the automatic power window system as follows:

pair.old: Press ENGINE START/STOP button to the ON position.
pair.new: Move the ENGINE START/STOP button to the ON position.

pair.old: If the upward movement of the window is blocked by an object or part of the body, the window will detect the resistance and will stop upward movement. The window will then lower approximately 11.8 inches (30 cm) to allow the object to be cleared.
pair.new: If an object or part of the body blocks the upward movement of the window, the window will detect the resistance and stop moving up. The window will then move down approximately 11.8 inches (30 cm) to allow you to clear the object.

pair.old: If the window detects the resistance while the power window switch is pulled up continuously, the window will stop upward movement and then lower approximately 1 inch (2.5 cm).
pair.new: If the window detects resistance while you pull up the power window switch continuously, the window will stop moving up, then move down approximately 1 inch (2.5 cm).

pair.old: The automatic reverse feature for the window is only active when the “auto up” feature is used by fully pulling up the switch. The automatic reverse feature will not operate if the window is raised using the halfway position on the power window switch.
pair.new: The automatic reverse feature for the window is only active when you use the “auto up” feature by fully pulling up the switch. The automatic reverse feature will not operate if you raise the window using the halfway position on the power window switch.

pair.old: Always check for obstructions before raising any window to avoid injuries or vehicle damage. If an object less than 0.16 inch (4 mm) in diameter is caught between the window glass and the upper window channel, the automatic reverse window may not detect the resistance and will not stop and reverse direction.
pair.new: To avoid injuries or vehicle damage, always check for obstructions before raising any window. If an object less than 0.16 inch (4 mm) in diameter is caught between the window glass and the upper window channel, the automatic reverse window may not detect the resistance and will not stop and reverse direction.

pair.old: The automatic reverse feature doesn’t activate while resetting the power window system. Make sure body parts or other objects are safely out of the way before closing the windows to avoid injuries.
pair.new: The automatic reverse feature doesn’t activate while you reset the power window system. Make sure body parts or other objects are safely out of the way before closing the windows to avoid injuries.

pair.old: The front passenger’s control can operate the front passenger’s power window.
pair.new: The front passenger’s control can operate their own power window.

pair.old: The rear passengers’ control cannot operate the rear passenger’s power window.
pair.new: The rear passengers’ controls cannot operate the rear passengers’ power windows.

pair.old: If the power window lock switch is operated (indicator turns on), rear passenger cannot open the rear door (if equipped with the Electronic Child Safety Lock System). For more details, refer to .
pair.new: If you operate the power window lock switch (indicator turns on), the rear passenger cannot open the rear door (if equipped with the Electronic Child Safety Lock System). For more details, refer to .

pair.old: To prevent possible damage to the power window system, do not open or close two windows or more at the same time. This will ensure the longevity of the fuse.
pair.new: To prevent possible damage to the power window system, do not open or close 2 or more windows at the same time. This will ensure the longevity of the fuse.

pair.old: Always double check to make sure all arms, hands, heads and other obstructions are safely out of the way before closing a window.
pair.new: Always double check to make sure all arms, hands, heads, and other obstructions are safely out of the way before closing a window.

pair.old: Do not allow children play with the power windows. Keep the driver’s door power window lock button in the LOCK position (pressed). SERIOUS INJURY can result from unintentional window operation by the child.
pair.new: Do not allow children to play with the power windows. Keep the driver’s door power window lock button in the LOCK position (pressed). SERIOUS INJURY can result from a child unintentionally operating a window.

pair.old: Do not extend heads or any limbs outside the window while the vehicle is in motion.
pair.new: Do not extend your head or any limbs outside the window while the vehicle is in motion.

pair.old: You can still control the windows movement with the engine turned off.
pair.new: You can still control the windows ’ movement with the engine turned OFF.

pair.old: Press the Door Unlock button (1) for more than 3 seconds. The window moves down after the doors are unlocked, as long as you press the door unlock button (1). The window movement stops, when you release the door unlock button (1).
pair.new: Press the Door Unlock button (1) for more than 3 seconds. The window moves down after the doors unlock, as long as you press the door unlock button (1). The window movement stops when you release the door unlock button (1).

pair.old: If vehicle is left unattended after operating the Remote window opening function, theft may be possible. In addition, please use caution there might be a malfunction due to the inflow of water while raining.
pair.new: If you leave the vehicle unattended after operating the remote window opening function, theft may occur. In addition, please use caution, as there might be a malfunction due to the inflow of water while it rains.

pair.old: The remote window opening function may abruptly stop, when you move away from your vehicle during operation. Stay close to your vehicle, while monitoring the window movement.
pair.new: The remote window opening function may abruptly stop when you move away from your vehicle during operation. Stay close to your vehicle while monitoring the window movement.

pair.old: One of the windows may stop operating, when the window is interrupted by certain force. However, the other windows will keep operating. Thus, you should make sure that all windows are opened.
pair.new: One of the windows may stop operating when the window is interrupted by force. However, the other windows will keep operating. Make sure that all windows are opened.

pair.old: Be careful when using the remote window openings function, as the doors will be unlocked.
pair.new: Be careful when using the remote window opening function, as the doors will be unlocked.

pair.old: If your vehicle is equipped with a sunroof, you can slide or tilt your sunroof with the sunroof switch located on the overhead console.
pair.new: If your vehicle is equipped with a sunroof, you can slide or tilt it with the sunroof switch located on the overhead console.

pair.old: The sunroof can only be operated when ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON or START position.
pair.new: You can only operate the sunroof when the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON or START position.

pair.old: Even when leaving the vehicle for a short time, turn off the ignition and carry the key to prevent children from operating the sunroof.
pair.new: When leaving the vehicle for a short time, turn off the ignition and carry the key to prevent children from operating the sunroof.

pair.old: Do not sit on the roof. Sitting on a roof or lifting heavy objects can damage the sunroof.
pair.new: Do not sit on or place heavy objects on the sunroof, as this can cause damage.

pair.old: Push the sunroof switch rearward to the first detentposition, the power sunshade automatically slides open.
pair.new: Push the sunroof switch backward to the first position. The power sunshade slides open.

pair.old: Push the sunroof switch forward to the first detentposition, the power sunshade automatically closes. However, if the sunroof glass is open, the glass will close first.
pair.new: Push the sunroof switch forward to the first position. The power sunshade closes. The glass closes first if the sunroof is already open.

pair.old: Do not pull or push the power sunshade by hand as such action may damage the power sunshade or cause it to malfunction.
pair.new: Do not pull or push the power sunshade by hand, as this may damage the power sunshade or cause it to malfunction.

pair.old: Wrinkles formed on the power sunshades are normal due to material characteristic.
pair.new: Wrinkles that form on the shade are normal for the material used.

pair.old: Tilt open/close
pair.new: Tilt open or close

pair.old: Push the sunroof switch upward, the sunroof glass tilts open. However, if the power sunshade is closed, the sunshade will open first.
pair.new: Push the sunroof switch upward. The sunroof glass tilts open. If the power sunshade is closed, it opens.

pair.old: Push the sunroof switch upward or forward when the sunroof glass is tilt opened, the sunroof glass automaticallycloses.
pair.new: Push the sunroof switch upward or forward when the sunroof glass is tilted open. The sunroof glass closes.

pair.old: Slide open/close
pair.new: Slide open or close

pair.old: Push the sunroof switch rearward to the first detentposition, the sunroof glass opens. However, if the power sunshade is closed, the power sunshade will open first.
pair.new: Push the sunroof switch backward to the first position. The sunroof glass opens. If the sunshade is closed, it opens before the glass.

pair.old: Push the sunroof switch forward to the first detentposition, the sunroof glass closes. However, if the sunroof glass is close, the power sunshade will close.
pair.new: Push the sunroof switch forward to the first position. The sunroof glass closes. If the sunroof glass is already closed, the sunshade closes.

pair.old: Push the sunroof switch forward or rearward to the second detentposition, the power sunshade and sunroof glass operate automatically (auto slide feature).
pair.new: Push the sunroof switch forward or backward to the second position. The power sunshade and sunroof glass both open (auto slide feature).

pair.old: If the power sunshade or sunroof glass senses any obstacle while it is closing automatically, it will reverse direction then stop at a certain position.
pair.new: If the power sunshade or sunroof glass senses any obstacle while it is closing automatically, it will reverse direction, then stop at a certain position.

pair.old: The auto reverse function may not work if an object thin or soft is caught between the sliding power sunshade or sunroof glass and sunroof sash.
pair.new: The auto reverse function may not work if there is a thin or soft object caught between the sliding power sunshade or sunroof glass and sunroof sash.

pair.old: Make sure heads, hands, arms or any other body parts or objects are out of the way before operating the sunroof. Body parts or objects may get caught causing injuries or vehicle damage.
pair.new: Make sure heads, hands, arms, or any other body parts or objects are out of the way before operating the sunroof. Body parts or objects may get caught, causing injuries or vehicle damage.

pair.old: Never deliberately use your body parts to test the automatic reversal function. The power sunshade or sunroof glass may reverse direction, but there is a risk of injury.
pair.new: Never deliberately use your body parts to test the automatic reversal function. There is a risk of injury, even if the glass or shade reverses direction.

pair.old: Do not continue to push the sunroof switch after the sunroof is fully opened, closed, or tilted. Damage to the sunroof motor could occur.
pair.new: Do not continue to push the sunroof switch after the sunroof is fully opened, closed, or tilted. This can damage the sunroof motor.

pair.old: Continuous operations such as slide open/close, tilt open/close, etc. may cause the motor or sunroof system to malfunction.
pair.new: Continuous operations, such as slide open or close, tilt open or close, and so on, may cause the motor or sunroof system to malfunction.

pair.old: Dust accumulated between the sunroof and roof panel can make noise. Open the sunroof and remove dust regularly using a clean cloth.
pair.new: Open the sunroof and remove dust between the sunroof and roof panel regularly using a clean cloth.

pair.old: Do not open or drive with the sunroof glass open immediately after rain or washing the vehicle. Water may wet the interior of the vehicle.
pair.new: Do not open the sunroof or drive with the sunroof glass open immediately after it rains or after you wash the vehicle. The interior of the vehicle may get wet.

pair.old: Do not extend any luggage outside the sunroof while driving. Vehicle damage may occur if the vehicle suddenly stops.
pair.new: Do not extend any luggage through the sunroof while driving. Vehicle damage may occur if the vehicle suddenly stops.

pair.old: Do not extend your head, arms, body parts or objects outside the sunroof while driving. Injuries may occur if the vehicle suddenly stops.
pair.new: Do not extend your head, arms, body parts, or objects through the sunroof while driving. Injuries may occur if the vehicle suddenly stops.

pair.old: In some circumstances resetting the sunroof operation may need to be performed. Some instances where resetting the sunroof may be required include:
pair.new: In some circumstances, you may need to reset the sunroof operation. Some of these instances include:

pair.old: When the 12-volt battery is either disconnected or discharged
pair.new: When the 12V battery is either disconnected or discharged

pair.old: If the sunroof one -touch AUTO OPEN/CLOSE operation is not functioning properly
