pair.new: If the sunroof 1-touch AUTO OPEN/CLOSE operation is not functioning properly

pair.old: Push the switch forward until the power sunshade and sunroof glass moves slightly. Then release the switch.
pair.new: Push the switch forward until the power sunshade and sunroof glass move slightly. Then release the switch.

pair.old: Once again push and hold the sunroof switch forward until the sunroof glass slides open and closed. Do not release the switch until the operation is completed.
pair.new: Once again, push and hold the sunroof switch forward until the sunroof glass slides open and closed. Do not release the switch until the operation is completed.

pair.old: If the sunroof does not reset when the vehicle battery is disconnected or discharged, or the sunroof fuse is blown, the sunroof may not operate normally.
pair.new: If the sunroof does not reset when the vehicle battery is disconnected or discharged, or if the sunroof fuse is blown, the sunroof may not operate normally.

pair.old: If the driver turns off the engine when the sunroof is not fully closed, the warning chime will sound for several seconds and the sunroof open warning will appear on the cluster LCD display.
pair.new: If the sunroof is not fully closed when the driver turns off the engine, a warning chime sounds for several seconds, and a warning appears on the instrument cluster display.

pair.old: Make sure the sunroof is closed fully when leaving your vehicle. If the sunroof is left open, rain or snow may wet the interior of the vehicle. Also, leaving the sunroof open when the vehicle is unattended may invite theft.
pair.new: Make sure the sunroof is closed fully when leaving your vehicle. If you leave the sunroof open, it may invite theft, and rain or snow may wet the interior.

pair.old: The steering wheel of this vehicle is equipped with the Electric Power Steering (EPS) system.
pair.new: The steering wheel of this vehicle has tilt and telescopic ability and a horn.

pair.old: If the engine is off or if the power steering system becomes inoperable, the vehicle may still be steered, but will require increased steering effort.
pair.new: If the engine is off or if the power steering system becomes inoperable, you may still steer the vehicle, but it will require increased steering effort.

pair.old: The EPS is controlled by the power steering control unit which senses the steering wheel torque and vehicle speed to command the motor.
pair.new: The power steering unit, which senses the steering wheel torque and vehicle speed to command the motor, controls the EPS.

pair.old: The steering effort becomes heavier as the vehicle’s speed increases and becomes lighter as the vehicle’s speed decreases for better control of the steering wheel.
pair.new: For better control of the steering wheel, the steering effort becomes heavier as the vehicle’s speed increases and lighter as the speed decreases.

pair.old: The following symptoms may occur during normal vehicle operation:
pair.new: The following behaviors may occur during normal vehicle operation:

pair.old: When ENGINE START/STOP button is the ON position, the steering wheel enters normal operation mode after diagnosing the EPS system (for about 3 seconds).
pair.new: When the ENGINE START/STOP button is the ON position, the steering wheel enters normal operation mode after diagnosing the EPS system (for about 3 seconds).

pair.old: A click noise may be heard from the EPS relay after turning the ENGINE START/STOP button is turned to the ON or OFF position.
pair.new: You may hear a click noise from the EPS relay after turning the ENGINE START/STOP button to the ON or OFF position.

pair.old: If the steering wheel is operated when the vehicle is not in motion or driven at a low speed, you may hear some noise.
pair.new: If you operate the steering wheel when the vehicle is not in motion or when driving at a low speed, you may hear some noise.

pair.old: If the EPS system does not operate normally, the warning light will appear or blink on the instrument cluster. If the power assistance of steering fails, you will need to use more force to steer.
pair.new: If the EPS system does not operate normally, the warning light appears or blinks on the instrument cluster. If the power assistance for steering fails, you will need to use more force to steer.

pair.old: Operating the steering wheel at lower temperatures may require more force and accompany noise. However, when the temperature increases, it returns to normal.
pair.new: Operating the steering wheel at lower temperatures may require more force and cause more accompanying noise. When the temperature increases, steering returns to normal.

pair.old: When the charging system warning light comes on due to the low voltage (when the alternator or battery does not operate normally or malfunctions), the steering wheel may require increased steering effort.
pair.new: When the charging system warning light comes on due to the low voltage (when the alternator or battery does not operate normally or malfunctions), you may need to use more force to steer.

pair.old: When jump starting the vehicle after battery discharge, the steering wheel may not function properly. It is a temporary situation due to low battery voltage, and upon stable battery charging, the steering wheel will function normally again. Please move the steering wheel around to make sure the steering wheel is functioning properly before driving the vehicle.
pair.new: When jump starting the vehicle after battery discharge, the steering wheel may not function properly. This is a temporary situation due to low battery voltage, and upon stable battery charging, the steering wheel will function normally again. Please move the steering wheel around to make sure it is functioning properly before driving the vehicle.

pair.old: If the EPS system does not work or an error occurs, the warning light on the instrument panel may be turned on or blink and it may require more force to operate the steering wheel. Hold the steering wheel more tightly than usual and operate with greater force. And then immediately pull your vehicle over to a safe place and have your vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the EPS system does not work or an error occurs, the warning light on the instrument panel may turn on or blink, and it may require more force to operate the steering wheel. Hold the steering wheel more tightly than usual and operate with greater force. Then immediately pull your vehicle over to a safe place and have it inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Tilt & telescopic steering wheel
pair.new: Tilt and telescopic steering wheel

pair.old: A tilt and telescopic steering wheel allows you to adjust the steering wheel before you drive. You can also raise it to give your legs more room when you exit and enter the vehicle.
pair.new: A tilt and telescopic steering wheel allows you to adjust the steering wheel before you drive. You can also raise the wheel to give your legs more room when you exit and enter the vehicle.

pair.old: Adjust the steering wheel to the desired angle (2) and distance forward/back (3).
pair.new: Adjust the steering wheel to the desired angle (2) and distance forward or back (3).

pair.old: Move the steering wheel, so it points toward your chest, not toward your face. Make sure you can see the instrument panel warning lights and gauges. After adjusting, pull up the lock.
pair.new: Move the steering wheel so that it points toward your chest, not toward your face. Make sure you can see the instrument panel warning lights and gauges. After adjusting, pull up the lock.

pair.old:
Pull up the lock-release lever to lock the steering wheel in place.
Push the steering wheel both up and down to be certain it is locked in position.

new.new: Push the steering wheel both up and down to make sure it is locked into position.

pair.old: Be sure to adjust the steering wheel to the desired position before driving.
pair.new: Adjust the steering wheel to the desired position before driving.

pair.old: Adjust the steering wheel angle (2) and position (3) with the switch (1)
pair.new: Adjust the steering wheel angle (2) and position (3) with the switch (1).

pair.old: While adjusting the steering wheel height, please do not push or pull it hard since the fixture can be damaged.
pair.new: Do not push or pull the steering wheel hard, or you may damage the fixture.

pair.old: When ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON position, pressing the heated steering wheel button warms the steering wheel. The indicator on the button will appear.
pair.new: When the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON position, pressing the heated steering wheel button warms the steering wheel. The indicator on the button illuminates.

pair.old: To turn the heated steering wheel off, press the button once again. The indicator on the button will turn off.
pair.new: To turn the heated steering wheel OFF, press the button again. The indicator on the button turns OFF.

pair.old: The heated steering wheel defaults to the OFF position whenever ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON position.
pair.new: Each time you start the vehicle, the heated steering wheel defaults to OFF.

pair.old: The heated steering wheel will turn off automatically approximately 30 minutes after the heated steering wheel is turned on.
pair.new: The heated steering wheel turns OFF approximately 30 minutes after it is turned on.

pair.old: When cleaning the heated steering wheel, do not use an organic solvent such as paint thinner, benzene, alcohol or gasoline. Doing so may damage the surface of the steering wheel.
pair.new: When cleaning the heated steering wheel, do not use an organic solvent such as paint thinner, benzene, alcohol, or gasoline. Doing so may damage the surface of the steering wheel.

pair.old: If the surface of the steering wheel is struck by a sharp object, damage to the heated steering wheel components could occur.
pair.new: If a sharp object strikes the surface of the steering wheel, it could damage the heated steering wheel components.

pair.old: If the steering wheel becomes too warm, turn the system off. The heated steering wheel may cause burns even at low temperatures, especially if used for a long time.
pair.new: If the steering wheel becomes too warm, turn the system OFF. The heated steering wheel may cause burns even at low temperatures, especially if used for a long time.

pair.old: To sound the horn, press the area indicated by the horn symbol on your steering wheel (see illustration).
pair.new: Press the area indicated by the horn symbol on your steering wheel to sound the horn (see illustration).

pair.old: The horn will operate only when this area is pressed. Check the horn regularly to be sure it operates properly.
pair.new: The horn operates only when you press this area. Check the horn regularly to be sure it operates properly.

pair.old: Do not strike the horn severely to operate it, or hit it with your fist. Do not press on the horn with a sharp - pointed object.
pair.new: Do not strike the horn with your fist. Do not press on the horn with a sharp or pointed object.

pair.old: This vehicle is equipped with inside and outside rear-view mirrors to provide views of objects behind the vehicle.
pair.new: This vehicle is equipped with inside and outside rearview mirrors to provide views of objects behind the vehicle.

pair.old: Inside rear-view mirror
pair.new: Inside rearview mirror

pair.old: Adjust the rear-view mirror so that the center view through the rear window is seen. Make this adjustment before you start driving.
pair.new: Adjust the rearview mirror so that you can see the center view through the rear window. Make this adjustment before you start driving.

pair.old: Do not place objects in the rear seat or cargo area which could interfere with your vision out the rear window.
pair.new: Do not place objects in the rear seat or cargo area that could interfere with your view out the rear window.

pair.old: Do not adjust the rear-view mirror while the vehicle is moving. This could result in loss of control.
pair.new: Do not adjust the rearview mirror while the vehicle is moving. This could result in losing control of the vehicle.

pair.old: Cleaning mirror
pair.new: Mirror cleaning

pair.old: When cleaning the mirror, use a paper towel or similar material dampened with glass cleaner. Do not spray glass cleaner directly on the mirror. It may cause the liquid cleaner to enter the mirror housing.
pair.new: To clean the mirror, use a paper towel or similar material dampened with glass cleaner. Do not spray glass cleaner directly on the mirror. It may cause the liquid cleaner to enter the mirror housing.

pair.old: Day/night rear-view mirror
pair.new: Day/night rearview mirror

pair.old: (A): Day, (B): Night
pair.new: 1: Day, 2: Night

pair.old: Remember that you lose some rear-view clarity in the night position.
pair.new: Remember that you lose some rearview clarity in the night position.

pair.old: The ECM automatically controls the glare from the headlights of vehicles behind you in nighttime or in low light driving conditions.
pair.new: The ECM automatically controls the glare from the headlights of vehicles behind you at night or in low-light driving conditions.

pair.old: For Telematics button function:
pair.new: Telematics buttons:

pair.old: Virtual assist button
pair.new: Virtual assist

pair.old: Kia Connect (Voice local search) button
pair.new: Kia Connect (voice local search)

pair.old: Roadside assist button
pair.new: Roadside assist

pair.old: During nighttime driving, this feature will automatically detect and reduce rear view mirror glare. The HomeLink Universal Transceiver allows you to activate your garage door(s), electric gate, home lighting, etc.
pair.new: During nighttime driving, this feature will automatically detect and reduce rearview mirror glare. The HomeLink Universal Transceiver allows you to activate your garage doors, electric gate, home lighting, and so on.

pair.old: The NVS Mirror automatically reduces glare by monitoring light levels in the front and the rear of the vehicle. Any objects that obstructs the light sensor will degrade the automatic dimming control feature.
pair.new: The NVS Mirror automatically reduces glare by monitoring light levels in the front and the rear of the vehicle. Any objects that obstruct the light sensor will negatively affect the automatic dimming control feature.

pair.old: The mirror defaults to the ON position each time whenever the vehicle is started.
pair.new: The mirror defaults to the ON position whenever you start the vehicle.

pair.old: The HomeLink Wireless Control System provides a convenient way to replace up to three handheld radio-frequency (RF) transmitters used to activate compatible devices such as gate operators, garage door openers, entry door locks, security systems, and home lighting.
pair.new: The HomeLink Wireless Control System provides a convenient way to replace up to 3 handheld radio-frequency (RF) transmitters that activate compatible devices such as gate operators, garage door openers, entry door locks, security systems, and home lighting.

pair.old: Before programming HomeLink to a garage door opener or gate operator, make sure that people and objects are out of the way of the device to prevent potential harm or damage. When programming a garage door opener, it is advised to park outside of the garage.
pair.new: Before programming HomeLink to work with a garage door opener or gate operator, make sure that people and objects are out of the way of the device to prevent potential harm or damage. When programming a garage door opener, it is advised to park outside of the garage.

pair.old: Do not use HomeLink with any garage door opener that lacks safety stop and reverse features as required by U.S. federal safety standards (this includes any garage door opener model manufactured before April 1, 1982). A garage door that cannot detect an object signaling the door to stop and reverse - does not meet current U.S. federal safety standards. For more information, contact HomeLink at www.homelink.com, or call HomeLink customer support at 1-800-355-3515.
pair.new: Do not use HomeLink with any garage door opener that lacks safety stop and reverse features as required by U.S. federal safety standards (this includes any garage door opener model manufactured before April 1, 1982). A garage door that cannot detect an object signaling the door to stop and reverse does not meet current U.S. federal safety standards. For more information, contact HomeLink at www.homelink.com, or call HomeLink customer support at 1-800-355-3515.

pair.old: It is also recommended that a new battery be replaced in the hand-held transmitter of the device being trained to HomeLink for quicker training and accurate transmission of the radio frequency.
pair.new: It is also recommended that you place a new battery into the hand-held transmitter of the device being trained to work with HomeLink for quicker training and accurate transmission of the radio frequency.

pair.old: It is recommended that a new battery be placed in the hand-held transmitter of the device being programmed to HomeLink for quicker training and accurate transmission of the radio-frequency signal.
pair.new: It is recommended that you place a new battery into the hand-held transmitter of the device being programmed to HomeLink for quicker training and accurate transmission of the radio-frequency signal.

pair.old: Place ENGINE START/STOP button to the ACC (Accessory) position for programming of HomeLink.
pair.new: Press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ACC position to program HomeLink.

pair.old: Press and release the HomeLink button (1), (2) or (3), you would like to program. The HomeLink indicator light (7) will flash orange slowly (if not, perform the steps of “Erasing HomeLink Buttons” section, and start over).
pair.new: Press and release the HomeLink button you would like to program (1, 2, or 3). The HomeLink indicator light (7) will slowly flash orange. If it does not, perform the steps in the “Erasing HomeLink Buttons” section, and start over.

pair.old: Position the garage door opener remote 1 ~ 3 inches (2 ~ 8 cm) away from the HomeLink buttons.
pair.new: Position the garage door opener remote 1–3 inches (2–8 cm) away from the HomeLink buttons.

pair.old: While the HomeLink indicator light (7) is flashing orange, press and hold the hand-held remote button. Continue pressing the hand-held remote button until the HomeLink indicator light (7) light changes from orange to green. You may now release the hand-held remote button.
pair.new: While the HomeLink indicator light (7) is flashing orange, press and hold the hand-held remote button. Continue pressing the hand-held remote button until the HomeLink indicator light (7) changes from orange to green. You may now release the hand-held remote button.

pair.old: Press and release the HomeLink button you are programming and observe the indicator light.
pair.new: Press and release the HomeLink button you are programming, and observe the indicator light.

pair.old: If the indicator light remains solid green, your device should operate when the HomeLink button is pressed. At this point, if your device operates, programming is complete.
pair.new: If the indicator light remains solid green, your device should operate when you press the HomeLink button. At this point, if your device operates, programming is complete.

pair.old: If the indicator light rapidly flashes green, firmly press, hold for two seconds and release the HomeLink button up to three times in a row slowly to complete the programming process. Do not press the HomeLink button rapidly. At this point if your device operates, programming is complete. If the device does not operate, continue with step 6.
pair.new: If the indicator light rapidly flashes green, firmly press the HomeLink button, hold it for 2 seconds, and release it up to three times in a row slowly to complete the programming process. Do not press the HomeLink button rapidly. At this point, if your device operates, programming is complete. If the device does not operate, continue with the next step.

pair.old: At the garage door opener motor, (security gate motor, etc.) locate the “Learn”, “Smart”, “Set” or “Program” button. This can usually be found where the hanging antenna wire is attached to the motor-head unit (see the device’s manual to identify this button). The name and color of the button may vary by manufacturer.
pair.new: At the garage door opener motor, security gate motor, or other system motor, locate the “Learn,” “Smart,” “Set,” or “Program” button. This can usually be found where the hanging antenna wire is attached to the motor-head unit (see the device’s manual to identify this button). The name and color of the button may vary by manufacturer.

pair.old: A ladder and/or second person may simplify the following steps.
pair.new: A ladder or second person may simplify the following steps.
A ladder or second person may simplify the following steps.

pair.old: Firmly press and release the “Learn”,“Smart”, “Set” or “Program” button. You now have up to 30 seconds in which to complete the next step.
pair.new: Firmly press and release the “Learn,” “Smart,” “Set,” or “Program” button. You now have up to 30 seconds to complete the next step.

pair.old: Return to the vehicle and firmly press, hold for two seconds and release, the HomeLink button up to three times in a row slowly. Do not press the HomeLink button rapidly. As soon as you see the garage door start to move, stop pressing any buttons until a few seconds after the garage door has come to a complete stop, regardless of position. At this point programming is complete and your device should operate when the HomeLink button is pressed and released.
pair.new: Return to the vehicle and firmly press the HomeLink button, hold it for 2 seconds, and release it up to 3 times in a row slowly. Do not press the HomeLink button rapidly. As soon as you see the garage door start to move, stop pressing any buttons until a few seconds after the garage door has come to a complete stop, regardless of position. At this point, programming is complete and your device should operate when you press and release the HomeLink button.

old.b: Two-Way Communication Programming (For select garage door openers)

new.b: Two-Way Communication programming (for select garage door openers)

pair.old: If your garage door opener has the “ myQ ” logo on its side, your opener has Two-Way Communication capability. HomeLink has the capability to establish Two-Way Communication with your garage door opener. HomeLink can receive and display “closing” or “opening” status messages from compatible garage door openers. At any time, HomeLink can also recall and display the last recorded status communicated by the garage door opener to indicate your garage door being “closed” or “opened”.
pair.new: If your garage door opener has the “ myQ ” logo on its side, your opener has Two-Way Communication capability. HomeLink has the capability to establish Two-Way Communication with your garage door opener. HomeLink can receive and display “closing” or “opening” status messages from compatible garage door openers. At any time, HomeLink can also recall and display the last recorded status communicated by the garage door opener to indicate your garage door being “closed” or “opened.”

pair.old: To check if your garage door opener is compatible with this feature, refer to www.homelink.com/compatible/two-way-Communication. If your garage door opener has this functionality, AND the Two-Way Communication indicators (4), (6) in the mirror appear while the garage door is opening/closing, then no further steps are needed. Two-Way Communication Programming is already complete. However, if your garage door opener has this functionality, AND the Two-Way Communication indicators (4), (6) in the mirror DO NOT appear while the garage door is opening/closing, use the following instructions to enable this functionality.
pair.new: To check if your garage door opener is compatible with this feature, refer to www.homelink.com/support. If your garage door opener has this functionality, AND the Two-Way Communication indicators (4), (6) in the mirror appear while the garage door is opening/closing, then no further steps are needed. Two-Way Communication programming is already complete. However, if your garage door opener has this functionality, AND the Two-Way Communication indicators (4), (6) in the mirror DO NOT appear while the garage door is opening/closing, use the following instructions to enable this functionality.

pair.old: In your vehicle, press and hold the programmed HomeLink button for 2 seconds, then release. Confirm that the garage door is moving. AFTER it stops, you will have one minute to complete the following steps:
pair.new: In your vehicle, press and hold the programmed HomeLink button for 2 seconds, then release it. Confirm that the garage door is moving. AFTER it stops, you will have 1 minute to complete the following steps:

pair.old: On your garage door opener in your garage, locate the “Learn” button (usually near where the hanging antenna wire is attached to the garage door opener). If there is difficulty locating this button, refer to the device’s owner’s manual.
pair.new: On the garage door opener in your garage, locate the “Learn” button (usually near where the hanging antenna wire is attached to the garage door opener). If you have difficulty locating this button, refer to the device’s owner’s manual.

pair.old: Return to the vehicle and firmly press and release the programmed HomeLink button to activate your garage door. The Two-Way Communication indicators (4), (6) flash in orange when the door is moving. Do not make any additional button presses until AFTER the garage door has come to a complete stop.
pair.new: Return to the vehicle and firmly press and release the programmed HomeLink button to activate your garage door. The Two-Way Communication indicators (4), (6) flash in orange when the door is moving. Do not press any additional buttons until AFTER the garage door has come to a complete stop.

pair.old: If your garage door opener has Two-Way Communication functionality, it is possible for HomeLink to stop functioning the garage door shortly after initial programming, if the Two-Way Communication Programming wasn’t properly completed. This usually happens after the first 10 times a programmed HomeLink button is pressed. If you experience this, completing the “Programming a New HomeLink Button” and “Two-Way Communication programming” will restore door operation.
pair.new: If your garage door opener has Two-Way Communication functionality and the Two-Way Communication programming wasn’t properly completed, HomeLink may stop operating the garage door shortly after initial programming. This usually happens after the first 10 times you press a programmed HomeLink button. If you experience this, completing the “Programming a New HomeLink Button” and “Two-Way Communication programming” procedures will restore door operation.

pair.old: Canadian radio-frequency laws require transmitter remote signals to “time-out” (or quit) after a couple seconds of transmission, which may not be long enough for HomeLink to pick up the signal during programming.
pair.new: Canadian radio-frequency laws require transmitter remote signals to “time out” (or quit) after a couple seconds of transmission, which may not be long enough for HomeLink to pick up the signal during programming.

pair.old: Press and release the desired programmed HomeLink button (1, 2 or 3).
pair.new: Press and release the programmed HomeLink button (1, 2, or 3).

pair.old: The HomeLink indicator (7) should light green, solid or flashing, and your programmed device should operate.
pair.new: The HomeLink indicator (7) should light green, solid, or flashing, and your programmed device should operate.

pair.old: Press and release one of the programmed HomeLink buttons (1, 2 or 3)
pair.new: Press and release 1 of the programmed HomeLink buttons (1, 2, or 3).

pair.old: The indicator (4) and (6) operates as below, if your garage door opener has Two-Way Communication functionality.
pair.new: The indicator (4) and (6) operates as follows if your garage door opener has Two-Way Communication functionality:

pair.old: If the indicator (4) flashes in Orange, it indicates that the garage door is “Closing”.
pair.new: If the indicator (4) flashes orange, the garage door is closing.

pair.old: The indicator (4) turns solid green once the garage door has closed.
pair.new: The indicator (4) will turn solid green once the garage door has closed.

pair.old: If the indicator (6) flashes in Orange, it indicates that the garage door is “Opening”.
pair.new: If the indicator (6) flashes orange, the garage door is opening.

pair.old: The indicator (6) turns solid green once the garage door has fully opened.
pair.new: The indicator (6) will turn solid green once the garage door has fully opened.

pair.old: If the indicator (4) or (6) does not turn to green, it indicates that the last status of garage door was not received properly. The HomeLink® mirror tries to receive the last known status of the garage door for a few seconds.
pair.new: If the indicator (4) or (6) does not turn green, the last status of the garage door was not received properly. The HomeLink mirror will try to receive the last known status of the garage door for a few seconds.

pair.old: HomeLink mirror with Two-Way Communication provides a way to view the last stored message from the garage door opener. In order to recall the last known status of the last activated device, press the buttons “1 and 2” OR “2 and 3” simultaneously.
pair.new: The HomeLink mirror with Two-Way Communication provides a way to view the last stored message from the garage door opener. To recall the last known status of the last activated device, press the buttons “1 and 2” OR “2 and 3” simultaneously.

pair.old: If the indicator (4) appears solid Green, it indicates that the last activated device was “closed” properly.
pair.new: If indicator (4) appears solid green, the last activated device was closed properly.

pair.old: If the indicator (6) appears solid Green, it indicates that the last activated device was “open” properly.
pair.new: If indicator (6) appears solid green, the last activated device was opened properly.

pair.old: 1) Erasing and Reprogramming a Single HomeLink Button:
pair.new: 1) Erasing and reprogramming a single HomeLink button:

pair.old: Press and hold the desired HomeLink button you want to re-program. DO NOT release the button.
pair.new: Press and hold the HomeLink button you want to reprogram. DO NOT release the button.

pair.old: The HomeLink indicator light (7) will appear solid green. Release the button as soon as the HomeLink indicator light (7) begins to flash orange, usually about 20 seconds.
pair.new: The HomeLink indicator light (7) will appear solid green. Release the button as soon as the HomeLink indicator light (7) begins to flash orange, which usually takes approximately 20 seconds.

pair.old: Proceed with the steps in the “Programming a New HomeLink Button” section.
pair.new: Proceed with the steps in the “Programming a new HomeLink button” section.

pair.old: If you do not complete the re-programming of a new device to the button, it will revert to the previously stored programming.
pair.new: If you do not complete the reprogramming of a new device to the button, it will revert to the previously stored programming.

pair.old: 2) The following instructions will erase ALL HomeLink® programming from ALL buttons:
pair.new: 2) Erasing ALL HomeLink programming from ALL buttons:

pair.old: Press and hold the buttons (1) and (3) simultaneously
pair.new: Press and hold buttons (1) and (3) simultaneously.

pair.old: The HomeLink indicator light (7) will appear solid Orange for about 10 seconds
pair.new: The HomeLink indicator light (7) will appear solid orange for about 10 seconds.

pair.old: Release the buttons once the HomeLink indicator light (7) changes to Green and flashes rapidly
pair.new: Release the buttons once the HomeLink indicator light (7) rapidly flashes green.

pair.old: Now all three HomeLink buttons (1), (2) and (3) are cleared of any programming
pair.new: Now all 3 HomeLink buttons (1), (2), and (3) are cleared of any programming.

pair.new: HomeLink 5 programming flow chart

pair.old: Digital Center Mirror
pair.new: Digital center mirror

pair.old: The Digital Center Mirror is a system that uses the camera on the rear of the vehicle and displays its image on the screen of the Digital Center Mirror. The Digital Center Mirror allows the driver to see the rear view despite obstructions, such as the headrest or luggage, ensuring rear visibility.
pair.new: The digital center mirror system uses the vehicle’s rear camera and displays its image on the screen of the digital center mirror. The digital center mirror ensures rear visibility by allowing the driver to see the rear view despite obstructions, such as headrests or luggage.

pair.old: Failure to follow the warnings and instructions for proper use of the Digital Center Mirror could result in serious accident.
pair.new: Failure to follow the warnings and instructions for proper use of the digital center mirror could result in a serious accident.

pair.old: The digital center mirror is a convenience feature and it is not a substitute for proper vehicle operation. The system has areas where objects cannot be viewed. Check the blind spot of the digital center mirror before vehicle operation. The driver is always responsible for safe driving.
pair.new: The digital center mirror is a convenience feature and is not a substitute for proper vehicle operation. The system has areas where objects cannot be viewed. Check the blind spot of the digital center mirror before vehicle operation. The driver is always responsible for safe driving.

pair.old: Do not operate the Digital Center Mirror while driving. Doing so can be a distraction and it could lose control of your vehicle and cause an accident or serious injury.
pair.new: Do not operate the digital center mirror while driving. Doing so can be a distraction and cause loss of control of your vehicle, an accident, or a serious injury.

pair.old: Do not operate the digital center mirror while driving. Doing so can be a distraction and cause loss of control of your vehicle, an accident, or serious injury.
pair.new: Do not operate the digital center mirror while driving. Doing so can be a distraction and cause loss of control of your vehicle, an accident, or a serious injury.

pair.old: Do not disassemble or modify the digital center mirror, the camera unit or wiring. It may result in an accident or fire. If you notice smoke or an odor coming from the digital center mirror, stop using the system immediately. Have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Do not disassemble or modify the digital center mirror, the camera unit, or the wiring. This may result in an accident or fire. If you notice smoke or an odor coming from the digital center mirror, stop using the system immediately and have it inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: When sunlight or high beams from following vehicles occurs, it may appear on the screen of the digital center mirror. Switch the system to the conventional rearview mirror mode appropriately.
pair.new: Sunlight or high beams from following vehicles may appear on the screen of the digital center mirror. Switch the system to the conventional rearview mirror mode appropriately.

pair.old: System component
pair.new: System components

pair.old: Displays icons, adjusting Brightness & Tilt
pair.new: Displays icons, adjusting brightness and tilt.

pair.old: Press to display the icon display area and select the item you want to adjust (Brightness & Tilt).
pair.new: Press to display the icon display area and select the item you want to adjust (Brightness and Tilt).

pair.old: For the operation of the “ HomeLink Universal Transceiver ”.
pair.new: For the operation of the HomeLink Universal Transceiver.

pair.old: The mode can be switched when the switch is in the ON position.

pair.new: Rearview mirrors

pair.old: The outside rear-view mirror is convex. Objects seen in the mirror are closer than they appear.
pair.new: The outside rearview mirror is convex. Objects seen in the mirror are closer than they appear.

pair.old: Use your interior rear-view mirror or direct observation to determine the actual distance of following vehicles when changing lanes.
pair.new: Use your interior rearview mirror or direct observation to determine the actual distance of following vehicles when changing lanes.

pair.old: Do not scrape ice off the mirror face; This may damage the surface of the glass. If ice restricts the movement of the mirror, do not force the mirror for adjustment. To remove ice, use a deicer spray, or a sponge or soft cloth with warm water.
pair.new: Do not scrape ice off of the mirror face, as scraping may damage the surface of the glass. If ice restricts the mirror’s movement, do not force the mirror to adjust its position. To remove ice, use either a de-icer spray or warm water and a sponge or soft cloth.

pair.old: If the mirror is jammed with ice, do not adjust the mirror by force. Use an approved spray de-icer (not radiator antifreeze) to release the frozen mechanism or move the vehicle to a warm place and allow the ice to melt.
pair.new: If the mirror is jammed with ice, do not adjust the mirror by force. Use an approved spray de-icer (not radiator antifreeze) to release the frozen mechanism, or move the vehicle to a warm place and allow the ice to melt.

pair.new: The position of the outside rearview mirror switch (1) determines whether or not the mirrors will move.

pair.new: Left or right: When you select either the L (Left) or R (Right) switch, both of the outside rearview mirrors move.

pair.old: Neutral: When neither switch is selected, the outside rear-view mirrors will not move.
pair.new: Neutral: When you do not select either switch, the outside rearview mirrors will not move.

pair.new: The outside rearview mirrors will automatically revert to their original positions in the following cases:

pair.old: The ENGINE START/STOP button is placed to either the LOCK/OFF position or the ACC position.
pair.new: You set the ENGINE START/STOP button set to either LOCK/OFF or ACC.

pair.old: The gear is shifted to any position except R (Reverse).
pair.new: You shift the gear to any position except R (Reverse).

pair.old: The remote control outside rear view mirror switch is not selected.
pair.new: You do not select the remote control outside rearview mirror switch.

pair.new: Set the shift lever to P (Park). Make sure that the vehicle is stopped and the mirrors are not working.

pair.old: Position the lever to L (left) or R (right) depending on the mirror that you want to adjust.
pair.new: Position the lever to L (left) or R (right) according to the mirror you want to adjust.

pair.old: Step on the brake pedal and shift the shift lever to R (Reverse).
pair.new: Engage the brake pedal and set the shift lever to R (Reverse).
Engage the brake pedal and set the shift lever to R (Reverse).

pair.new: When the rearview mirror finishes moving downward, adjust it by pressing the directional switches: ▼, ▲, ◀, ▶.

pair.new: To automatically save the adjusted angle, set the shift lever to a gear other than R (Reverse), change the rearview mirror selector lever to neutral, and wait for the mirror’s automatic return.

pair.new: Running out of fuel can put the vehicle occupants in danger.

pair.new: You must stop and obtain additional fuel as soon as possible if the warning light illuminates or when the gauge indicator comes close to the “E” (Empty) level.

pair.new: The odometer displays the total distance that the vehicle has been driven and should be used to determine when to perform periodic maintenance.

pair.new: Odometer range: 0–999,999 mi or 0–1,599,999 km.

pair.new: Distance range: 1–9,999 mi or 1–9,999 km.

pair.old: If the estimated distance is below 1 mile (1 km), the trip computer will display “---” as distance to empty.
pair.new: If the estimated distance is below 1 mile (1.6 km), the trip computer will display “---” as the distance to empty.

pair.old: If the level of the remaining fuel is more than three-quarters, more than 0.8 gallons (3 liters) of fuel must be refilled for the fuel gauge to change. In other cases, more than 1.6 gallons (6 liters) of fuel must be refilled for the vehicle to change the fuel gauge.
pair.new: If the remaining fuel level is above 3/4, you must add more than 0.8 gallons (3 liters) of fuel for the fuel gauge to change. If the remaining fuel level is below 3/4, you must add more than 1.6 gallons (6 liters) of fuel for the fuel gauge to change.

pair.old: The distance to empty may differ from the actual driving distance as it is an estimate of the available driving distance.

pair.new: The trip computer may not register additional fuel if you add fewer than 1.6 gallons (6 liters) of fuel to the vehicle.

pair.new: The fuel economy and distance to empty may vary significantly based on driving conditions, driving habits, and the condition of the vehicle.

pair.old: If any of the following conditions occurs, the mileage and days may be incorrect.
pair.new: Mileage and days may be incorrect in the following situations:

pair.old: This warning message is displayed if the tire pressure is low. The corresponding tire on the vehicle will be appeared.
pair.new: This warning message displays when the tire pressure is low and indicates which tire is low.

pair.old: You can activate or deactivate Wiper/Lights Display function from the User Settings mode in the cluster LCD display.
pair.new: You can activate or deactivate the Wiper/Lights Display function in the information cluster LCD display’s User Settings mode.
You can activate or deactivate the Wiper/Lights Display function in the information cluster LCD display’s User Settings mode.

pair.old: This warning message is displayed if the washer fluid level in the reservoir is nearly empty.
pair.new: This warning message displays when the washer fluid level in the reservoir is nearly empty.

pair.old: This warning is to warn the driver the road may be icy.
pair.new: This warning appears when the road may be icy.

pair.old: When the following conditions occur, the warning light (including outside temperature gauge) blinks 5 times and then remain on. A warning chime sounds once.
pair.new: When the temperature on the outside temperature gauge is below approximately 40°F (4°C), the warning light and outside temperature gauge will blink 5 times, then remain lit. A warning chime will sound once.

pair.old: If the icy road warning appears while driving, you should drive even more attentively and safely, refraining from speeding, rapid acceleration, sudden braking or sharp turning, etc.
pair.new: If the icy road warning appears while you are driving, you should drive even more attentively and cautiously. Refrain from speeding, rapid acceleration, sudden braking, sharp turning, and other potentially dangerous driving.

pair.old: When the engine oil level warning light appears, check whether maintenance schedule (Engine oil replacement) in owner’s manual has been followed before replenishing the oil. If not followed, the engine oil must be replaced.
pair.new: When the engine oil level warning light appears, ensure that you have followed the engine oil replacement maintenance schedule in the owner’s manual before replenishing the oil. If you have not followed the schedule, you must replace the engine oil.

pair.old: Engine oil change due soon. Reset oil life after oil change
pair.new: Engine oil change due soon. Reset oil life after oil change.

pair.old: Engine oil change due now. Reset oil life after oil change
pair.new: Engine oil change due now. Reset oil life after oil change.

pair.old: The vehicle can detect self-discharge of the battery due to over-current that is generated by unauthorized electrical devices such as dashboard camera (dash cam).
pair.new: The vehicle can detect self-discharge of the battery due to overcurrent generated by unauthorized electrical devices, such as a dashboard camera.

pair.old: If the warning continues even after external electrical devices are removed, have your vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the warning continues after you disconnect external electrical devices, have your vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: This warning message appears if you cannot operate the ENGINE START/STOP button when there is a problem with the ENGINE START/STOP button system.
pair.new: This warning message appears if you cannot operate the ENGINE START/STOP button due to a problem with the button system.

pair.old: It means that you could start the engine by pressing the E ENGINE START/STOP button once more.
pair.new: You can start the engine by pressing the ENGINE START/STOP button once more.

pair.old: If the warning appears each time you press the ENGINE START/STOP button, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the warning appears every time you press the ENGINE START/STOP button, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: This warning message appears if you press the ENGINE START/STOP Button while the warning message “Key not detected” is illuminated.
pair.new: This warning message appears if you press the ENGINE START/STOP button while the warning message “Key not detected” is illuminated.

pair.old: The immobilizer indicator light blinks.
pair.new: The immobilizer indicator light will blink.

pair.old: This warning message appears if the headlights are off when the surrounding is dark.
pair.new: This warning message appears if the headlights are OFF when you are in dark surroundings.

pair.old: The warning light and indicator light indicate a situation where the driver should be careful and whether the various functions are activated.
pair.new: The warning and indicator lights show whether various functions are activated and indicate situations in which the driver should be careful.

pair.old: Parking brake & brake fluid warning light
pair.new: Parking brake and brake fluid warning light

pair.old: Once you set the ENGINE START/STOP Button to the ON position.
pair.new: When you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position.
For approximately 3 seconds when you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position.

pair.old: It appears for approximately 3 seconds
pair.new: The light appears for approximately 3 seconds.

pair.old: It remains on if the parking brake is applied.
pair.new: The light remains on if the parking brake is applied.

pair.old: When the parking brake is applied.
pair.new: When you apply the parking brake.

pair.old: If the warning light appears with the parking brake released, the brake fluid level in the reservoir is low.
pair.new: If the warning light appears when you release the parking brake, the brake fluid level in the reservoir is low.

pair.old: If the brake fluid level in the reservoir is low:
pair.new: If the brake fluid level in the reservoir is low, follow these steps:

pair.old: With the engine stopped, check the brake fluid level immediately and add fluid as required (For more details, refer to ). Then check all brake components for fluid leaks. If any leak on the brake system is still found, the warning light remains on, or the brakes do not operate properly, do not drive the vehicle.
pair.new: With the engine stopped, check the brake fluid level immediately and add fluid as required (for more details, refer to ). Then check all brake components for fluid leaks. Do not drive the vehicle if you find a leak in the brake system, the warning light remains on, or the brakes do not operate properly.

pair.old: Have your vehicle towed to an authorized Kia dealer and inspected.
pair.new: Have your vehicle towed to and inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Your vehicle is equipped with dual-diagonal braking systems. This means you still have braking on two wheels even if one of the dual systems should fail.
pair.new: Your vehicle is equipped with dual-diagonal braking systems, which means you still have braking on 2 wheels even if one of the dual systems should fail.

pair.old: Parking brake & brake fluid warning light
pair.new: Parking brake and brake fluid warning light

pair.old: Driving the vehicle with a warning light ON is dangerous. If the Parking Brake & Brake Fluid Warning Light appears with the parking brake released, it indicates that the brake fluid level is low.
pair.new: Driving the vehicle with a warning light ON is dangerous. If the Parking brake and brake fluid warning light appears while the parking brake is released, it indicates that the brake fluid level is low.

pair.old: Once you set the ENGINE START/STOP button to the ON position.
pair.new: When you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position.
When you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position until the engine starts.
For approximately 3 seconds when you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position.
For approximately 3 seconds when you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position.

pair.old: When there is a malfunction with either the alternator or electrical charging system.
pair.new: When either the alternator or electrical charging system is malfunctioning.

pair.old: If there is a malfunction with either the alternator or electrical charging system:
pair.new: If either the alternator or electrical charging system is malfunctioning, follow these steps.

pair.old: If the engine pressure is low:
pair.new: If the engine oil pressure is low:

pair.old: Turn the engine off and check the engine oil level (For more details, refer to ). If the level is low, add oil as required.
pair.new: Turn the engine off and check the engine oil level (for more details, refer to ). If the level is low, add oil as required.

pair.old: If the warning light remains on after adding oil or if oil is not available, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer. Continued driving with the warning light on may cause engine failure.
pair.new: If the warning light remains on after you add oil, or if oil is not available, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer. Continuing to drive with the warning light on may cause engine failure.

pair.old: When engine oil pressure decreases due to insufficient engine oil, etc., the Engine Oil Pressure warning light will appear.
pair.new: When engine oil pressure decreases due to insufficient engine oil or another cause, the engine oil pressure warning light will appear.

pair.old: The enhanced engine protection system which limits engine power will be activated. When the engine oil pressure is restored, the warning light and the enhanced engine protection system will turn off after the engine is restarted.
pair.new: The enhanced engine protection system, which limits engine power, will activate. When the engine oil pressure is restored, the warning light and the enhanced engine protection system will turn off after you restart the engine.

pair.old: Do not continue driving with the engine overheated. Otherwise, the engine may be damaged.
pair.new: Do not continue driving when the engine is overheated. Otherwise, the engine may be damaged.

pair.old: If the engine is not stopped immediately after the engine oil pressure warning light is appears and stays on while the engine is running, serious engine damage may result.
pair.new: After the engine oil pressure warning light appears and stays on while the engine is running, serious engine damage may occur if you do not stop the engine immediately.

pair.old: When one or more of your tires are significantly under inflated. (The location of any underinflated tire are displayed on the LCD display).
pair.new: When 1 or more of your tires are significantly underinflated. The LCD display shows the location of any underinflated tire.)

pair.old: When there is a malfunction with a LED headlamp related part.
pair.new: When there is a malfunction with an LED headlamp related part.

pair.old: Icy Road Warning Light
pair.new: Icy road warning light

pair.old: Liftgate Open Warning Light This warning light appears:When the liftgate is not closed securely.
pair.new: Liftgate open warning light This warning light appears when the liftgate is not closed securely.

pair.old: When the engine automatically starts by ISG system, some warning lights (ABS, ESC, ESC OFF, EPS or Parking brake warning light) may turn on for a few seconds. This happens because of the low battery voltage. It does not mean the system is malfunctioning.
pair.new: When the engine starts automatically with the ISG system, warning lights such as the ABS, ESC OFF, EPS, or parking brake warning light may illuminate for a few seconds. This is due to the low battery voltage and does not indicate a system malfunction.

pair.old: The wipers and washers remove foreign substances from the windshield and rear window, helping to maintain visibility.
pair.new: The wipers and washers remove foreign substances from the windshield and rear window to help maintain visibility.

pair.old: AUTO *:Auto control wipe
pair.new: AUTO *: Automatically controlled wipe

old.b: B: Intermittent control wipe time adjustment/Auto control wipe time adjustment*

new.b: B: Intermittent control and AUTO control wipe time adjustment*

old.b: C: Wash with brief wipes (Pull lever towards you)

new.b: C: Wash with brief wipes (pull lever toward you)

old.b: E: Wash with brief wipes (Rear/Push lever away from you)

new.b: E: Rear wash with brief wipes (push lever away from you)

pair.old: Operate as follows when ENGINE START/STOP button is ON.
pair.new: When the ENGINE START/STOP button is ON, operate the windshield wipers as follows:

pair.old: MIST: For a single wiping cycle, move the lever to this position and release it. The wipers will operate continuously if the lever is held in this position.
pair.new: MIST: For a single wiping cycle, move the lever to this position and release it. If the lever is held in this position, wipers will operate continuously.

pair.old: OFF: Wiper is not in operation
pair.new: OFF: Wiper is not in operation.

pair.old: INT: Wiper operates intermittently at the same wiping intervals. Use this mode in light rain or mist. To vary the speed setting, turn the speed control knob.
pair.new: INT: Wiper operates intermittently at the same wiping intervals. Use this mode in light rain or mist. To change the speed setting, turn the speed control knob.

pair.old: If there is heavy accumulation of snow or ice on the windshield, do not drive and defrost the windshield for about 10 minutes, or until the snow and/or ice is removed before using the windshield wipers to ensure proper operation. If you do not remove the snow and/or ice before using the wiper and washer, it may damage the wiper and washer system.
pair.new: If there is a heavy accumulation of snow or ice on the windshield, do not drive until you defrost the windshield for about 10 minutes, or remove the snow and ice before using the windshield wipers to ensure proper operation. If you do not remove the snow and ice before using the wiper and washer, you may damage the wiper and washer system.

pair.old: If you operate the wipers while driving on snowy roads, the wipers may stop due to snow buildup on your windshield. This is normal and not a failure because it is one of our safety features to prevent vehicle accidents and wiper damage from overloading the wiper motor. If the wipers stop, remove snow accumulated on the top or bottom of windshield before using them.
pair.new: If you operate the wipers while driving on snowy roads, the wipers may stop due to snow buildup on your windshield. This is normal and not a failure, but a safety feature to prevent vehicular accidents and wiper damage from overloading the wiper motor. If the wipers stop, remove snow accumulated on the top or bottom of the windshield before using the wipers.

pair.old: Do not use the washer in freezing temperatures without first warming the windshield with the defrosters; the washer solution could freeze on the windshield and obscure your vision.
pair.new: Do not use the washer in freezing temperatures before warming the windshield with the defroster, as the washer solution could freeze on the windshield and obscure your view.

pair.old: Auto control
pair.new: AUTO control

pair.old: The rain sensor (A) located on the upper end of the windshield glass senses the amount of rainfall and controls the wiping cycle for the proper interval. The more it rains, the faster the wiper operates. When the rain stops, the wiper stops.
pair.new: The rain sensor (A), located on the upper end of the windshield, senses the amount of rainfall and controls the wiping cycle at the proper interval. The more it rains, the faster the wiper operates. When the rain stops, the wiper stops.

pair.old: To vary the speed setting, turn the speed control knob (B).
pair.new: To change the speed setting, turn the speed control knob (B).

pair.old: If the wiper switch is set in AUTO mode when the ENGINE START/STOP button is ON, the wiper will operate once to perform a self-check of the system. Set the wiper to OFF position when the wiper is not in use.
pair.new: If you set the wiper switch to AUTO when the ENGINE START/STOP button is ON, the wiper will operate once to check the system. Set the wiper to OFF when not in use.

pair.old: When the ENGINE START/STOP button is ON and the windshield wiper switch is placed in the AUTO mode, use caution in the following situations to avoid any injury to the hands or other parts of the body:
pair.new: When the ENGINE START/STOP button is ON and the windshield wiper switch is set to AUTO, follow these precautions to avoid any injury to your hands or other body parts:

pair.old: When washing the vehicle, set the wiper switch in the OFF position to stop the auto wiper operation.
pair.new: When washing the vehicle, set the wiper switch OFF to stop automatic wiper operation.

pair.old: The wiper may operate and be damaged if the switch is set in the AUTO mode while washing the vehicle.
pair.new: The wiper may operate and be damaged if you set it to AUTO while washing the vehicle.

pair.old: Do not remove the sensor cover located on the upper end of the passenger side windshield glass. Damage to system parts could occur and may not be covered by your vehicle warranty.
pair.new: Do not remove the sensor cover located on the upper end of the passenger side windshield glass. This may cause damage to the system parts that may not be covered by your vehicle warranty.

pair.old: When starting the vehicle in winter, set the wiper switch in the OFF position. Otherwise, wipers may operate and ice may damage the windshield wiper blades. Always remove all snow and ice and defrost the windshield properly prior to operating the windshield wipers.
pair.new: When starting the vehicle in winter, set the wiper switch OFF. Otherwise, the wipers may operate, and ice may damage the windshield wiper blades. Always remove all snow and ice and defrost the windshield properly before operating the windshield wipers.

pair.old: When tinting the windshield, be careful of any fluid getting into the sensor located in the top center of the front windshield. It may damage related parts.
pair.new: If you tint the windshield, be careful not to let any fluid get into the sensor in the top center of the front windshield, as the fluid may damage related parts.

pair.old: Operating windshield washers
pair.new: Windshield washer operation

pair.old: Move the wiper speed control switch to the OFF position.
pair.new: Turn the wiper speed control switch to OFF.

pair.old: Pull the lever gently toward you to spray washer fluid on the windshield and to run the wipers 1-3 cycles. Use this function when the windshield is dirty. The spray and wiper operation will continue until you release the lever.
pair.new: Gently pull the lever toward you to spray washer fluid on the windshield and run the wipers 1–3 cycles when the windshield is dirty. The spray and wiper operation will continue until you release the lever.

pair.old: Operating rear window wiper and washer switch
pair.new: Rear window wiper and washer switch operation

pair.old: The rear window wiper and washer switch is located at the end of the wiper and washer switch lever.
pair.new: The rear window wiper and washer switch is located at the end of the lever.

pair.old: Turn the switch to the desired position to operate the rear wiper and washer.
pair.new: To operate the rear wiper and washer, turn the switch to the desired position.

pair.old: This vehicle is equipped with a variety of lights to appear the interior and exterior of the vehicle.
pair.new: This vehicle is equipped with a variety of lights to illuminate both the interior and exterior of the vehicle.

pair.old: The purpose of this feature is to prevent the battery from being discharged if the lights are left in the ON position. The system automatically shuts off the parking lights after the engine is off and the driver’s door is opened.
pair.new: The purpose of this feature is to prevent the battery from discharging if the lights are left ON. The function automatically shuts off the parking lights once the engine is off and the driver’s door is opened.

pair.old: To keep the lights on turn the position lamps OFF and ON again using the headlamp switch on the steering column after the engine is turned off.
pair.new: To keep the lights on after turning off the engine, turn the position lamps OFF and ON again using the headlamp switch on the steering column.

pair.old: If you place the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ACC or OFF position with the headlamps ON, the headlamps (and/or parking lights) remain on for about 5 minutes. With the engine off if the driver’s door is opened and closed, the headlamps (and/or parking lights) will turn off after 15 seconds.
pair.new: If you press the ENGINE START/STOP button OFF or to ACC with the headlamps ON, the headlamps and parking lights will remain on for approximately 5 minutes.

pair.old: The headlights (and/or parking lights) can be turned off by pressing the lock button on the smart key twice or turning the light switch to the OFF or AUTO position. However, if you turn the light switch to the AUTO position when it is dark outside, the headlamps will not be turned off.
pair.new: You can turn off the headlamps and parking lights by pressing the smart key’s lock button twice or turning the light switch to the OFF or AUTO position. Keep in mind that the headlamps will not turn off if you turn the light switch to the AUTO position when it is dark outside.

pair.old: You can activate or deactivate the Headlamp Delay function from the User Settings Mode in the LCD display. For more details, refer to . If your vehicle is equipped with additional navigation, refer to the infotainment system manual separately supplied.
pair.new: You can activate or deactivate the Headlight Delay function in the LCD display’s User Settings mode. For more details, refer to . If your vehicle is equipped with additional navigation features, refer to the infotainment system manual supplied separately.

pair.old: If the driver exits the vehicle through door other than the driver’s door, the battery saver function will not operate and the headlight delay function will not turn OFF automatically.
pair.new: If the driver exits the vehicle through any door other than the driver’s, the battery saver function will not operate, and the Headlight Delay function will not turn off automatically.

pair.old: This may cause the battery to discharge. To avoid battery discharge, turn OFF the headlamps manually from the headlight switch before exiting the vehicle.
pair.new: In this case, the battery may discharge. To avoid battery discharge, turn the headlamps off manually using the headlight switch before exiting the vehicle.

pair.old: Daytime Running Light (DRL)
pair.new: Daytime running light (DRL)

pair.old: The DRL can be helpful in many different driving conditions, and it is especially helpful after dawn and before sunset.
pair.new: The DRL can be helpful in many different driving conditions, especially after dawn and before sunset.

pair.old: The light switch has a headlight and a position lamp position.
pair.new: The light switch has a headlight and a position lamp setting.

pair.old: To operate the lights, turn the knob at the end of the control lever to one of the following positions:
pair.new: To operate the lights, turn the knob at the end of the control lever to one of the following settings:

pair.old: OFF position
pair.new: OFF

pair.old: Auto light position
Position Lamp & Taillight
pair.new: Position lamp and taillight
Position lamp and taillight

pair.old: Headlight lamp position
pair.new: Headlamps ON

pair.old: lighting controlposition and tail lampWhen the light switch is in the lamp position, the front position lamp, taillight, and the license plate lamp will turn ON.
pair.new: lighting controlposition and tail lampFront position lamp, taillights, and license plate lamp are ON.

pair.old: The ENGINE START/STOP button must be in the ON position to turn on the headlights.
pair.new: The ENGINE START/STOP button must be ON to turn on the headlights.

pair.old: Headlight (Low Beam)
pair.new: Headlight (low beam)

pair.old: lighting controlhead light (low beam)When the light switch is in the headlight position, headlight (low beam), taillight and license plate lamp will turn ON.
pair.new: lighting controlhead light (low beam)Low beam headlight, taillight, and license plate lamp are ON.

pair.old: Auto light
pair.new: AUTO light

pair.old: lighting controlauto lightWhen the light switch is in the AUTO light position, the taillights and headlights will turn ON or OFF automatically depending on the amount of ambient light.
pair.new: lighting controlauto lightTaillights and headlights turn ON or OFF automatically depending on the amount of ambient light.

pair.old: Never place anything over the sensor (1) located on the instrument panel as this will ensure better auto-light system control.
pair.new: For optimal AUTO light setting performance, never place anything over the sensor (1) located on the instrument panel.

pair.old: Don’t clean the sensor using a window cleaner. The cleaner may leave a light film which could interfere with sensor operation.
pair.new: Do not clean the sensor with a window cleaner, as it may leave a thin film that could interfere with sensor operation.

pair.old: If your vehicle has window tint or other types of metallic coating on the front windshield, the Auto light system may not work properly.
pair.new: If your vehicle has a window tint or other metallic coating on the front windshield, the AUTO light system may not work properly.

pair.old: Operating high beam
pair.new: High beam operation

pair.old: To flash the headlights, pull the lever towards you.
pair.new: To flash the high beam headlamps, pull the lever toward you.

pair.old: It will return to the normal (low beam) position when released. The headlight switch does not need to be on to use this flashing feature.
pair.new: The lever will return to the normal (low beam) setting when released. The headlight switch does not need to be on to flash the high beam headlights.

pair.old: Operating turn signals and lane change signals
pair.new: Turn and lane change signal operation

old.old: The ENGINE START/STOP button must be on for the turn signals to function.

new.new: The ENGINE START/STOP button must be ON for the turn signals to function.

pair.old: To turn on the turn signals, move the lever up or down (A).
pair.new: To engage the turn signal, move the lever up or down (A).

old.old: The lever will return to the OFF position when released.

new.new: The lever will return to the OFF position when released.

old.old: If an indicator stays on and does not flash or if it flashes abnormally, 1 of the turn signal bulbs may be burned out and will require replacement.
If an indicator stays on and does not flash, or if it flashes abnormally, one of the turn signal bulbs may be burned out and need to be replaced.

new.new: If an indicator stays on and does not flash, or if it flashes abnormally, one of the turn signal bulbs may be burned out and need to be replaced.

pair.old: To activate a one-touch lane change function, move the turn signal lever slightly and then release it. The lane change signals will blink 3, 5 or 7 times.
pair.new: To use the one-touch lane change function, move the turn signal lever slightly, then release it. The lane change signals will blink 3, 5, or 7 times.

pair.old: You can activate or deactivate the One Touch Turn Signal function or choose the number of blinking (3, 5, or 7) by selecting User SettingsLightsOne Touch Turn Signal.
pair.new: You can activate or deactivate the one-touch lane change function or choose the length of blinking (3, 5, or 7 times) by selecting User SettingsLightsOne Touch Turn Signal.

pair.old: Operating front fog light
pair.new: Front fog light operation

pair.old: Fog lights are designed to provide improved visibility when visibility in fog, rain or snow, etc.
pair.new: Fog lights are designed to improve visibility in fog, rain, or snow.

pair.old:
The fog lights will turn on when the fog light switch (1) is turned to the on position after the headlamp is turned on.
To turn off the fog lights:
To turn off the fog lights, turn the fog light switch (1) to the ON position.
pair.new: To turn off the fog lights, turn the fog light switch (1) to the OFF position.

pair.old: When in operation, the fog lights consume large amounts of vehicle electrical power. Only use the fog lights when visibility is poor.
pair.new: The fog lights consume large amounts of vehicle electrical power when in operation. Only use the fog lights when visibility is poor.

pair.old: High Beam Assist is a function that automatically adjusts the headlight range (switches between high beam and low beam) depending on the brightness of detected vehicles and certain road conditions.
pair.new: The HBA function automatically adjusts the headlight range (switches between high beam and low beam) depending on the brightness of detected vehicles and certain road conditions.
