pair.old: High Beam Assist setting
pair.new: (HBA) setting

pair.old: With the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position, select SetupVehicleLightsHigh Beam Assist from the Settings menu to turn on High Beam Assist function.
pair.new: With the ENGINE START/STOP button ON, select SetupVehicleLightsHigh Beam Assist in the Settings menu to turn on the High Beam Assist function.

pair.old: For your safety, change the Settings after parking the vehicle at a safe location.
pair.new: For your safety, change the settings only after parking the vehicle at a safe location.
For your safety, change the settings only after parking the vehicle at a safe location.
For your safety, change the settings only after parking the vehicle at a safe location.
For your safety, change the settings only after parking the vehicle at a safe location.
For your safety, change the settings only after parking the vehicle at a safe location.
For your safety, change the settings only after parking the vehicle at a safe location.
For your safety, change the settings only after parking the vehicle at a safe location.

pair.old: High Beam Assist operation operation
pair.new: High Beam Assist (HBA) operation

pair.old: After selecting ’High Beam Assist’ in the Settings menu, High Beam Assist will operate by following the procedure below.
pair.new: After selecting High Beam Assist in the Settings menu, the function operates as follows:

pair.old: Place the headlight switch in the AUTO position and push the headlamp lever towards the instrument cluster. The High Beam Assist () indicator light will appear on the cluster and the function will be enabled.
pair.new: To enable the function, set the headlight switch to AUTO and push the headlamp lever toward the instrument cluster. The HBA () indicator light will appear on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: When the function is enabled, high beam will turn on when vehicle speed is above 20 mph (30 km/h). When vehicle speed is below 12 mph (20km/h), high beam will not turn on.
pair.new: When HBA is enabled, the high beam will turn on when the vehicle speed is above 20 mph (30 km/h). The high beam will not turn on when the vehicle speed is below 12 mph (20km/h).

pair.old: The High Beam () indicator light will appear on the cluster when high beam is on.
pair.new: The high beam () indicator light will appear on the cluster when the high beam is on.

pair.old: When High Beam Assist is operating, if the headlight lever or switch is used, the function operates as follow:
pair.new: If you use the headlight lever or switch when HBA is operating, the function operates as follows:

pair.old: If the headlight lever is pulled towards you when the high beam is off, the high beam will turn on without High Beam Assist canceled. When you let go of the headlight lever, the lever will move to the middle and the high beam will turn off.
pair.new: To turn the high beam on without canceling HBA, pull the headlight lever toward you when the high beam is off. When you let go of the headlight lever, it will move to the middle and the high beam will turn off.

pair.old: If you push the light switch towards the instrument cluster, high beam is turned on and High Beam Assist is released.
pair.new: To turn the high beam on and cancel HBA, push the light switch toward the instrument cluster.

pair.old: If the headlight switch is placed from AUTO to another position (headlight/position/off), High Beam Assist will turn off and the corresponding lamp will turn on.
pair.new: If you change the headlight switch from AUTO to another setting, HBA will turn off and the corresponding lamp will turn on.

pair.old: When High Beam Assist is operating, high beam switches to low beam if any of the following conditions occur:
pair.new: When HBA is operating, the headlight switches from high to low beam in the following situations:

pair.old: When the headlight of an oncoming vehicle is detected.
pair.new: When an oncoming vehicle headlight is detected.

pair.old: When the headlight or taillight of a motorcycle or a bicycle is detected.
pair.new: When the headlight or taillight of a motorcycle or bicycle is detected.

pair.old: High Beam Assist Malfunction and limitations
pair.new: High Beam Assist malfunction and limitations

pair.old: High Beam Assist malfunction
pair.new: High Beam Assist (HBA) malfunction

pair.old: When HBA is not working properly, the warning message will appear and the Master warning light () will appear on the cluster. Have the function be inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: When HBA is not working properly, the Master warning light () appears on the instrument cluster, along with a warning message that lasts for several seconds. Have the function inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Limitations of High Beam Assist
pair.new: High Beam Assist (HBA) limitations

pair.old: High Beam Assist may not work properly in the following situations:
pair.new: HBA may not work properly in the following situations:

pair.old: Light from a vehicle is not detected because of lamp damage, or because it is hidden from sight, etc.
pair.new: Light from another vehicle is not detected for any reason.

pair.old: Headlights are covered with dust, snow or water.
pair.new: The headlights are dirty with dust, snow, or water.

pair.old: Headlights are off but the fog lamps are on and etc.
pair.new: The headlight is off but the fog light is on.

pair.old: There is a lamp that has a similar shape as a vehicle’s lamp.
pair.new: Your vehicle detects a lamp that is shaped like a vehicle headlight.

pair.old: Headlights have been damaged or not repaired properly.
pair.new: The headlamps are damaged or incorrectly repaired.

pair.old: Headlights are not aimed properly.
pair.new: The headlamps are not aimed properly.

pair.old: Driving on a narrow curved road, rough road, uphill or downhill.
pair.new: You are driving on a narrow, curved, rough, or steep road.

pair.old: Vehicle in front is partially visible on a crossroad or curved road.
pair.new: You are driving on a crossroad or curved road and a vehicle in front of you is partially visible.

pair.old: There is a traffic light, reflecting sign, flashing sign or mirror ahead.
pair.new: There is a traffic light, reflective or flashing sign, or mirror ahead.

pair.old: There is a temporary reflector or flash ahead (construction area).
pair.new: There is a temporary reflector or flash ahead from a construction area.

pair.old: The road conditions are poor such as being wet, iced or covered with snow.
pair.new: Road conditions are wet, icy, snowy, or otherwise poor.

pair.old: A vehicle suddenly appears from a curve.
pair.new: A vehicle suddenly appears from around a curve.

pair.old: The vehicle is tilted from a flat tire or is being towed
pair.new: A vehicle is tilted because of a flat tire or is being towed.

pair.old: Light from a vehicle is not detected because of exhaust fume, smoke, fog, snow, etc.
pair.new: Light from a vehicle is not detected because of exhaust fumes, smoke, fog, snow, or other obstructions.

pair.old: HBA may not operate for 15 seconds after the vehicle is started or the front view camera is initialized or restarted.
pair.new: HBA may not operate for 15 seconds after you start the vehicle or after you initialize or restart the front view camera.

pair.old: When HBA does not operate normally, manually switch the headlight between high and low beam.
pair.new: When HBA does not operate normally, manually switch the headlights between high and low beam.

pair.old: The welcome system is a function that displays the surroundings or the interior when the driver approaches or exits the vehicle.
pair.new: The welcome system displays the surroundings or the interior when the driver approaches or exits the vehicle.

pair.old: When all the doors (and liftgate) are closed and locked, the door handle lamp will illuminate for about 15 seconds if any of the following is performed:
pair.new: When all the doors and the liftgate are closed and locked, the door handle lamp will illuminate for about 15 seconds if you perform any of the following actions:

pair.old: When the door unlock button is pressed on the smart key.
pair.new: You press the smart key’s door unlock button.
You press the smart key’s door unlock button.

pair.old: When the button of the outside door handle is pressed.
pair.new: You press the button on the outside door handle.
You press the button on the outside door handle.

pair.old: When you approach the vehicle with the smart key in possession.
pair.new: You approach the vehicle with the smart key in your possession.

pair.old: The headlights (and/or taillights) remain on for approximately 5 minutes after the ignition key is removed or turned to the ACC or LOCK position. If the driver’s door is opened and closed, the headlights will turn off after 15 seconds.
pair.new: The headlights and taillights remain on for approximately 5 minutes after you turn the ignition OFF or set the ENGINE START/STOP button to ACC or LOCK. If you open and close the driver’s door, the headlights will turn off after 15 seconds.

pair.old: The headlights can be turned off by pressing the lock button on the transmitter or smart key twice or turning off the light switch from the headlamp or Auto light position.
pair.new: You can turn the headlights OFF by pressing the lock button on the transmitter or smart key twice, or by turning the light switch to OFF.

pair.old: If you press the door lock button, the lamps will turn off immediately.
pair.new: If you press the smart key’s door lock button, the lamps will turn off immediately.

pair.old: This vehicle is equipped with lights throughout the vehicle to illuminate the interior.
pair.new: This vehicle is equipped with lights to illuminate its interior.

pair.old: Do not use the interior lights when driving in the dark. Accidents could happen because your view may be obscured by interior lights.
pair.new: Do not use the interior lights when driving in the dark. Accidents could happen because the interior lights may obscure your view.

pair.old: The interior lights automatically turn off approximately 20 minutes after the ENGINE START/STOP button is turned off, if the lights are in the ON position.
pair.new: If the interior lights are ON, they will automatically turn OFF approximately 20 minutes after you turn the ENGINE START/STOP button OFF.

pair.new: When you unlock the doors with a smart key, as long as you do not open the doors, the map light and interior light illuminate for approximately 30 seconds.

pair.new: If you open a door when the ENGINE START/STOP button is set to ACC or OFF, the map and interior lights stay on for approximately 20 minutes.

pair.old: The map lamp and cabin lamp will stay on continuously if the door is opened with the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position.
pair.new: If you open a door when the ENGINE START/STOP button is ON, the map and interior lights stay on continuously.

pair.old: The map lamp and cabin lamp will turn off immediately if the ENGINE START/STOP button is changed to the ON position or all doors are locked.
pair.new: When the ENGINE START/STOP button is pressed ON or all doors are locked, the map and interior lights turn off immediately.

pair.new: To turn DOOR mode OFF, press the DOOR button (2).

pair.new: (3): Press this switch to turn the front and rear interior lights ON and OFF.

pair.new: Always close the vanity mirror and turn it OFF when not in use. If you close the sun visor with the vanity light ON, this may damage the sun visor or cause the battery to discharge.

pair.new: The glove box lamp will illuminates when you open the glove box.

pair.new: Adjust the fan speed control and temperature control as desired.

pair.new: The refrigerant system should only be serviced by trained and certified technicians to ensure proper and safe operation.

pair.old: The air conditioning evaporator (cooling coil) should never be repaired or replaced with one removed from a used or salvaged vehicle and new replacement Mobile Air Conditioning (MAC) evaporators should be certified (and labeled) as meeting SAE Standard J2842.
pair.new: The air conditioning evaporator (cooling coil) should never be repaired or replaced with a cooling coil that was removed from a used or salvaged vehicle. Replacement Mobile Air Conditioning (MAC) evaporators should be certified (and labeled) as meeting SAE Standard J2842.

pair.old: When using the air conditioning system, monitor the temperature gauge closely while driving up hills or in heavy traffic when outside temperatures are high. A/C system operation may cause vehicle overheating. Continue to use the blower fan but turn off the A/C system if the temperature gauge indicates vehicle overheating.
pair.new: If you use the A/C system when outside temperatures are high, monitor the temperature gauge closely while driving up hills or in heavy traffic, as A/C system operation may cause the vehicle to overheat. If the temperature gauge indicates that the vehicle is overheating, you may continue to use the blower fan, but turn off the A/C system.

pair.old: The A/C system should only be used with the windows and sunroof closed to prevent condensation inside the vehicle that may cause damage to electrical components.
pair.new: To prevent condensation inside the vehicle that may damage electrical components, only use the A/C system with the windows and sunroof closed.

pair.new: For better control of the heating and cooling system, never place anything over the sensor located on the instrument panel.

pair.old: A/C automatic drying
pair.new: Air conditioning (A/C) automatic drying

pair.old: A/C Automatic Drying feature dries the moisture in the air conditioner and reduces air conditioner odor. The blower motor automatically operates after 30 minutes of the vehicle being turned off.
pair.new: The A/C automatic drying feature dries the moisture in the A/C and reduces A/C odor. The blower motor automatically operates after the vehicle has been turned off for 30 minutes.

pair.old: The A/C Automatic Drying feature can be turned on and off by selecting SetupClimateClimate FeaturesA/C Automatic Drying from the infotainment system.
pair.new: You can turn the A/C automatic drying feature ON and OFF by tapping SetupClimateClimate featuresA/C automatic drying on the infotainment system screen.

pair.old: When the A/C Automatic Drying feature is activated, the air conditioner sets the fan speed to the third level, selects Fresh mode, and directs the air flow to the floor.
pair.new: When you activate the A/C automatic drying feature, the air conditioner sets the fan speed to the third level, selects Fresh mode, and directs the air flow to the floor.

pair.old: The A/C automatic drying feature operates under the following conditions:
pair.new: The A/C automatic drying feature operates under the following conditions:

pair.new: The A/C automatic drying feature stops operating under the following conditions:

old.old: The ENGINE START/STOP button is pressed, or the vehicle is ON

new.new: The ENGINE START/STOP button is pressed, or the vehicle is ON

pair.old: The A/C Automatic Drying feature reduces air conditioner odors but may not remove all odors.
pair.new: The A/C automatic drying feature reduces A/C odors but may not remove all odors.

pair.old: The A/C Automatic Drying feature does not operate if the remaining battery level is insufficient to prevent battery discharge.
pair.new: To prevent battery discharge, the A/C automatic drying feature does not operate if the remaining battery level is insufficient.

pair.new: Smart temperature comfort control system (for driver’s seat)

pair.old: The temperature of the driver’s seat warmer, air ventilated seat, and heated steering wheel is automatically controlled depending on the inside and outside temperature of the vehicle when the vehicle is running. The heated steering wheel and seat warmer/ventilation defaults to the OFF position whenever the vehicle is restarted. If the auto. controls that use climate control settings is ON, the heated steering wheel and seat warmer/ventilation will turn on or off depending on the inside and outside temperature.
pair.new: When the engine is running and climate control is set to AUTO, the smart temperature comfort control system automatically controls the driver’s seat warmer, air ventilated seat, and heated steering wheel according to the temperature both inside and outside the vehicle.

pair.old: Select SettingsVehicleSeatHeating/VentilationAUTO Controls. That Use SettingsSteering wheel warmer/ Seat warmer/ventilationClimate controlSettingsSteering wheel warmerSeat warmerventilation on the infotainment screen.

pair.new: Turning scheduled ventilation control ON or OFF

Also, the starting time can be set within 24 hours. Schedule Ventilation Control operates only once when the feature is set.

pair.old: Heating and air conditioning manually
pair.new: Manual heating and air conditioning (A/C) operation

pair.old: The heating and cooling system can be controlled manually by touching buttons other than the AUTO button.
pair.new: The heating and cooling system can be controlled manually by pressing buttons other than the AUTO button.

pair.old: No text changes. Please review in the updated TOC from Phase 2 The system works sequentially according to the order of buttons selected. To manually operate the heating and A/C, follow these steps:

new.new: To manually operate the heating and A/C, follow these steps:

pair.old: Set the mode to the desired position.To improving the effectiveness of heating and cooling;
pair.new: Set the desired mode.

pair.old: Set the temperature control to the desired position.
pair.new: Set the temperature control as desired.

pair.old: Set the fan speed control to the desired speed. If air conditioning is desired, turn the A/C system on.
pair.new: Set the desired fan speed. Turn the A/C system on if desired.

pair.old: Touch the AUTO button to convert to fully automatic control of the system.
pair.new: Press the AUTO button to switch to the automatically controlled climate system.

pair.old: The mode selection button controls the direction of the air flow through the ventilation system.
pair.new: The mode selection button controls the direction of airflow through the ventilation system.

pair.old: Air flow is directed toward the upper body and face. Additionally, each outlet can be controlled to direct the air discharged from the outlet.
pair.new: Air flow is directed toward the upper body and face. You can control the direction of the airflow from each vent.

pair.old: Air flow is directed towards the face and the floor.
pair.new: Air flow is directed toward the upper body and face and the floor.

pair.old: Most of the air flow is directed to the floor, with a small amount of the air being directed to the windshield, side window defrosters and side air vents.
pair.new: Most of the airflow is directed to the floor, and a small amount is directed to the windshield, side window defrosters, and side air vents.

pair.old: Most of the air flow is directed to the floor and the windshield with a small amount directed to the side window defrosters and side air vents.
pair.new: Most of the airflow is directed to the floor and windshield, and a small amount is directed to the side window defrosters and side air vents.

pair.old: Most of the air flow is directed to the windshield with a small amount of air directed to the side window defrosters and side air vents.
pair.new: Most of the air flow is directed to the windshield, and a small amount is directed to the side window defrosters and side air vents.

pair.old: You can adjust the direction of air delivered from these vents using the vent control levers as shown.
pair.new: You can adjust the direction of a vent’s airflow using the vent control lever as shown.

pair.old: When set to the lowest temperature setting, the air conditioning will operate continuously.
pair.new: When set to the lowest temperature setting, the A/C will operate continuously.

pair.new: Syncing driver and passenger temperature

pair.old: You can switch the temperature mode from Celsius to Fahrenheit as follows:
pair.new: To switch the temperature scale between Celsius and Fahrenheit, press the AUTO button for at least 3 seconds while pressing the OFF button.

pair.new: Air conditioning (A/C)

pair.old: Press the A/C button to turn the air conditioning system on (indicator light will appear).
pair.new: To turn the A/C system ON, press the A/C button. The indicator light will illuminate.

pair.old: Press the button again to turn the air conditioning system off.
pair.new: To turn the A/C system OFF, press the button again.

pair.new: To turn the climate control system OFF, press the driver side temperature control knob.

pair.new: You can operate the mode and air intake control buttons as long as the ENGINE START/STOP button is ON.

pair.old: Clean Air
pair.new: Clean air

pair.old: When the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON position, the clean air function turns on automatically.
pair.new: The clean air function turns ON automatically when the ENGINE START/STOP button is ON.

pair.old: The clean air function turns off automatically, when the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the OFF position.
pair.new: The clean air function turns OFF automatically when the ENGINE START/STOP button is OFF.

pair.old: 3rd Row air conditioning
pair.new: 3rd row air conditioning A/C

pair.old: To turn on the 3rd row air conditioning control system:
pair.new: To operate the 3rd row air conditioning control system:

old.old: You can operate the 3rd row air conditioning system from the front row control panel. Changing the front row’s fan speed by touching the control button will automatically change the third row’s fan speed as well. When the front row air conditioning has been turned off and you want to stop the A/C in the third row, press the 3rd row air conditioning select button one more time. Then, the third row’s A/C will also turn off.

You can operate the 3rd row A/C from the front row control panel. Changing the front row’s fan speed automatically changes the third row’s fan speed. To turn the 3rd row A/C OFF after turning the front row A/C OFF, press the 3rd row A/C button again.

new.new: You can operate the 3rd row A/C from the front row control panel. Changing the front row’s fan speed automatically changes the third row’s fan speed. To turn the 3rd row A/C OFF after turning the front row A/C OFF, press the 3rd row A/C button again.

pair.old: The 3rd row A/C system can be separately controlled by the control buttons in the 3rd row. When the A/C is ON or OFF, the 3rd row A/C control button in the front row will turn ON or OFF, informing the front passengers on the third row’s status.
pair.new: You can also operate the 3rd row A/C separately using the control buttons in the 3rd row. The 3rd row A/C button on the front row control panel will reflect any changes you make to the 3rd row A/C on the 3rd row control buttons.

pair.old: The fan speed of the 3rd row air conditioning can also be separately controlled by turning the fan speed control knob.
pair.new: You can control the 3rd row A/C fan speed separately using the 3rd row fan speed control knob.

pair.old: The climate control air filter installed behind the glove box filters the dust or other pollutants that enter the vehicle from the outside through the heating and air conditioning system.
pair.new: The climate control air filter, installed behind the glove box, filters dust or other pollutants from outside that enter the vehicle through the heating and air conditioning (A/C) system.

pair.old: A: Outside air
pair.new: A: External air

pair.old: D: Blower
pair.new: D: Blower fan

pair.old: If dust or other pollutants accumulate in the filter over time, the air flow from the air vents may decrease. pair.new: Windshield defrosting and defogging

The difference between the temperature of the outside air and the windshield could cause the outer surface of the windshield to fog up, causing loss of visibility. Set the mode selection to the position and fan speed control to the lower speed.
pair.new: Do not use the or setting when running the air conditioner (A/C) in extremely humid weather. The temperature differential between outside air and the windshield can fog up the windshield’s outer surface and obscure visibility. Set the mode to and the fan speed control to its lowest speed.

pair.old: For maximum defrosting, set the temperature control to the extreme right/hot position and the fan speed control to the highest speed.
pair.new: For maximum defrosting, set the temperature control to its highest heat and the fan speed control to its highest speed.

pair.old: Auto Defogging System
pair.new: AUTO defogging system

pair.old: Auto defogging helps reduce the possibility of fogging up the inside of the windshield by automatically sensing the moisture on the inside of the windshield.
pair.new: Automatic defogging senses moisture on the inside of the windshield to reduce the likelihood of the windshield fogging up.

pair.old: The auto defogging system operates when the heater or air conditioning is on.
pair.new: The AUTO defogging system operates when the heating or air conditioning (A/C) is ON.

pair.old: When the auto defogging system is operating, the indicator will appear.
pair.new: The AUTO defogging indicator will appear when the system is operating.

pair.old: If high humidity is detected in the vehicle, the auto defogging system will be enabled.
pair.new: If high humidity is detected inside the vehicle, AUTO defogging will engage.

pair.old: The air intake control will change to Fresh mode under low outside temperature.
pair.new: The air intake control changes to external airflow when the outside temperature is low.

pair.old: The mode will change to defrost to direct airflow to the windshield.

pair.old: When the auto defogging system is canceled, the defrost button indicator will blink 3 times.
pair.new: When AUTO defogging cancels, the defrost button indicator blinks 3 times.

pair.old: When the auto defogging system is reset, the defrost button indicator will blink 6 times without a signal.
pair.new: When the AUTO defogging system resets, the defrost button indicator blinks 6 times without a signal.

pair.old: If you try to turn off the air conditioning when the Auto Defogging System is on, the indicator will blink 3 times and the air conditioning will not be turned off.
pair.new: If you try to turn the A/C OFF when AUTO defogging is ON, the indicator blinks 3 times and the A/C will not turn OFF.

pair.old: To maintain the effectiveness and efficiency of the auto defogging system, do not select Recirculation mode while the system is operating.
pair.new: To maintain the AUTO defogging system’s effectiveness and efficiency, do not select recirculated air intake while the system is operating.

pair.old: When the auto defogging system is operating, the fan speed adjustment knob, temperature adjustment knob and air intake control button are all disabled.
pair.new: When AUTO defogging is operating, the fan speed adjustment knob, temperature adjustment knob, and air intake control button are all disabled.

pair.new: Use the USB charger when the engine is running. Using the USB charger for prolonged periods of time with the engine OFF could cause the battery to discharge.

pair.new: Adjust the air conditioner or heater to the lowest level when using the power outlet.

pair.new: The driver’s seat floor mats should especially be checked, as unsecured mats may cause unintended acceleration or braking. Be sure to remove all the plastic film on the carpets before installing the mats.

pair.new: Do not stack floor mats on top of one another (for example, an all-weather rubber mat on top of a carpeted floor mat). Only a single floor mat should be installed in each position.

pair.new: Luggage net holder

pair.new: DO NOT over stretch the luggage net and ALWAYS keep your face and body out of the luggage net’s recoil path. Failure to comply with these instructions may result in severe facial injuries. DO NOT use the luggage net when the strap has visible signs of wear or damage.

pair.new: Maintain the vehicle ’s balance, and adjust the weight of luggage as far forward as possible.

pair.old: Exterior Features
pair.new: Exterior features

pair.old: If your vehicle has a roof rack, you can load cargo and accessories (e.g.,) on top of your vehicle.
pair.new: If your vehicle has a roof rack, you can load cargo and accessories (for example, a cargo carrier or roof tent) on top of your vehicle.

pair.old: The crossbars (if equipped) should be placed in the proper load carrying positions prior to placing items onto the roof rack.
pair.new: Place the crossbars (if equipped) in the proper load carrying positions before placing items onto the roof rack.

pair.old: When the roof rack is not being used to carry cargo, the crossbars may need to be repositioned if wind noise is detected.
pair.new: When you are not using the roof rack to carry cargo, you may need to reposition the crossbars if you detect wind noise.

pair.old: Never place load directly on the sunroof or the roof panels. They are not designed to carry loads and may break. Never let anything interfere with the movement of the sunroof. Do not operate the sunroof when carrying cargo or roof accessories.

new.new: Never place a load directly on the sunroof or the roof panels. They are not designed to carry loads and may break. Never let anything interfere with the movement of the sunroof.

pair.old: The Kia roof rack can support up to 880 lb (400 kg), evenly distributed (i.e.,side to side and front to back), but only when the vehicle is parked.
pair.new: The Kia roof rack can support up to 880 lb (400 kg) evenly distributed (side to side and front to back), but only when the vehicle is parked.

pair.old: Never drive your vehicle when combined weight of occupants and cargo exceeds GVWR. That can cause loss of control and an accident. Never drive your vehicle with more than 220 lb (100 kg) of cargo on the roof rack. Heavier loads may result in loss of control and rollover.

new.new: Never drive your vehicle when the combined weight of occupants and cargo exceeds GVWR. That can cause loss of control and an accident.

pair.old: Loads on the roof rack raise your vehicle’s center of gravity and may change the ways in which your vehicle handles. Avoid sudden starts and braking, sharp turns, abrupt maneuvers, and high speeds that may result in loss of control or rollover.
pair.new: Loads on the roof rack raise your vehicle’s center of gravity and may change the way that your vehicle handles. Avoid sudden starts and braking, sharp turns, abrupt maneuvers, and high speeds that may result in loss of control or rollover.

pair.old: Overloading the roof racks may also damage them.
pair.new: Overloading the roof racks may damage them.

pair.old: Overloading the vehicle may also damage the vehicle.
pair.new: Overloading the vehicle may damage it.

pair.old: Plan carefully before attempting to put cargo and accessories on the roof racks. Be sure the items can be secured and that you have the equipment to do so. Get help from those close to you whenever appropriate.
pair.new: Plan carefully before attempting to put cargo and accessories on the roof racks. Be sure that you have the equipment to secure the items. Get help from those close to you whenever appropriate.

pair.old: Be sure that the cargo and accessories will not extend too far forward, rearward, or sideways.
pair.new: Be sure that the cargo and accessories will not extend too far forward, backward, or sideways.

pair.old: Do not allow anything to obscure the view out the windshield or the front view camera. Do not allow anything to obscure the high mounted stop lamp.
pair.new: Do not allow anything to obscure the view out the windshield or the front view camera. Do not allow anything to obscure the high -mounted stop lamp.

pair.old: Park your vehicle on solid, level ground, place it in P (Park) and set the parking brake before loading and securing items.
pair.new: Park your vehicle on solid, level ground, place it in P (Park), and set the parking brake before loading and securing items.

pair.old: Before installing an accessory such as a cargo carrier or roof tent, make sure that the item is compatible with your Kia’s roof racks.
pair.new: Make sure an accessory is compatible with your vehicle’s roof racks before installing it.

pair.old: Contact the manufacturer or your Kia dealer if you need additional information.
pair.new: Contact the manufacturer or your Kia dealer if you need additional information when installing an accessory.

pair.old: In addition, when using a roof tent:
pair.new: When using a roof tent:

pair.old: Be sure that the weight of occupants does not exceed the load capacity of the roof racks.
pair.new: Be sure that the weight of the occupants does not exceed the load capacity of the roof racks.

pair.old: Confirm the roof tent is securely fastened to the cross bars and roof rails before using it.
pair.new: Confirm that the roof tent is securely fastened to the cross bars and roof rails before using it.

pair.old: Never leave the engine running with occupants in a roof tent. Never run an internal combustion engine (such as a generator), gas stove, gas lantern, or other device that burns fuel near an occupied roof tent or awning. Those emit carbon monoxide, which is colorless, odorless, and deadly.
pair.new: Never leave the engine running with occupants in a roof tent. Never run an internal combustion engine (such as a generator), gas stove, gas lantern, or other device that burns fuel near an occupied roof tent or awning. Those devices emit carbon monoxide, which is colorless, odorless, and deadly.

pair.old: Failure to follow the instructions, warnings and cautions above, and those of the accessory manufacturer, could result in damage to your vehicle, to your cargo, and to other road users. It could also result in an accident and in serious injury and death.
pair.new: Failure to follow the instructions, warnings, and cautions above, and those of the accessory manufacturer, could result in damage to your vehicle, to your cargo, and to other road users. It could also result in an accident and in serious injury and death.

pair.old: Using the infotainment/climate switchable controller

pair.new: Press the button on the switchable controller to select the infotainment system or climate control panel.

pair.old: Over-The-Air (OTA) Software Update
pair.new: Over-The-Air (OTA) software update

pair.old: The Over-The-Air software update feature allows you to wirelessly update software to the latest version. Using this feature, you can keep your vehicle system updated with the latest software.
pair.new: The OTA software update feature allows you to wirelessly update software to the latest version. Using this feature, you can keep your vehicle system updated with the latest software.

pair.old: The latest software can be downloaded automatically while driving. After the latest software has been successfully downloaded, you will
pair.new: The latest software can be downloaded automatically while driving. A notification appears on your phone or the vehicle screen that the software update is ready to install. The next time the vehicle is turned off, you can choose to start the update.

pair.old: After the vehicle is turned off, the vehicle system will allow you to start the update.
pair.new: After you turn the vehicle off, the vehicle system allows you to start the update.

pair.old: If you press the Start button on the screen, the vehicle will begin installing the update automatically. The following conditions must be satisfied:
pair.new: If you press the Update Now button on the screen, the vehicle begins installing the update automatically. The following conditions must be satisfied:

pair.old: The battery and system status are automatically checked by the vehicle.
pair.new: The vehicle automatically checks the battery and system status.

pair.old: After the update is complete, you will receive a notification on your phone or the vehicle screen that the software update is complete.
pair.new: You will receive a notification on your phone or the vehicle screen that indicates that the software update is complete.

pair.old: The screen turns off automatically after 3 minutes to save battery life. If the screen turns off automatically, you can check the update progress by pressing the ENGINE START/STOP button.
pair.new: To save battery life, the screen turns off automatically after 3 minutes. You can check the update progress by pressing the ENGINE START/STOP button.

pair.old: After the update starts, you can exit the vehicle.
pair.new: You can exit the vehicle after the update starts.

pair.old: The OTA software update feature is only available for Kia Connect service users.
pair.new: The Over-The-Air (OTA) software update feature is only available for Kia Connect service users.

pair.old: If the update or recovery fails, contacting an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the update or recovery fails, contact an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Observe the following restrictions during the update.
pair.new: Observe the following restrictions during the update:

pair.old: You cannot use the vehicle during the update. Be sure to have enough time for the update, and safely park the vehicle before starting the update process.
pair.new: You cannot use the vehicle during the update. Be sure you have enough time for the update, and safely park the vehicle before starting the update process.

pair.old: The vehicle cannot be charged. Charge your vehicle after completing the update.
pair.new: You cannot charge the vehicle. Charge your vehicle after completing the update.

pair.old: The Rear Occupant Alert may not work. Visually check if there are any occupant in the rear seat after the update starts.
pair.new: The Rear Occupant Alert may not work. Visually check if there are any occupants in the rear seat after the update starts.

pair.old: The update will automatically cancel if any vehicle conditions required for the update are changed before starting the update.
pair.new: The update is automatically canceled if any required vehicle conditions change before you start the update.

pair.old: Once the update has started, you cannot cancel the update.
pair.new: You cannot cancel the update after it has started.

pair.old: Note that the high-voltage-related module for charging the 12 V battery may activate during the software update.
pair.new: The high-voltage-related module for charging the 12V battery may activate during the software update.

pair.old: If the update is not complete successfully, contact Kia.
pair.new: Contact Kia if the update does not complete successfully.

pair.old: Vehicle signal strength, must be strong (above -82 dBm) to safely install any downloaded software.
pair.new: Vehicle signal strength must be strong (above -82 dBm) to safely install any downloaded software.

pair.old: If your vehicle is equipped with an Infotainment system, refer to the separately supplied manual for detailed information.
pair.new: If your vehicle is equipped with an infotainment system, refer to the separately supplied manual for detailed information.

pair.old: The shark fin antenna transmits and receives wireless signals such as AM/FM, Sirius XM, GNSS, etc.
pair.new: The shark fin antenna transmits and receives wireless signals such as AM/FM, Sirius XM, global navigation satellite system (GNSS), and so on.

pair.old: The signals for which antenna can transmit and receive vary by the vehicle options.
pair.new: The antenna can transmit and receive different signals based on vehicle options.

pair.old: Use the USB port to plug in an USB.
pair.new: Use the USB port to plug in a USB stick.

pair.old: AM and FM radio signals are broadcast from transmitter towers located around your city. They are intercepted by the radio antenna on your vehicle. This signal is then processed by the radio and sent to your vehicle speakers.
pair.new: Transmitter towers located around your city broadcast AM and FM radio signals. The radio antenna on your vehicle intercepts these signals. The radio processes these signals and sends them to your vehicle speakers.

pair.old: In some cases the signal coming to your vehicle may not be strong and clear.
pair.new: In some cases, the signal coming to your vehicle may not be strong and clear.

pair.old: This can be due to several factors, such as the distance from the radio station, closeness of other strong radio stations or the presence of buildings, bridges or other large obstructions in the area.
pair.new: This may be due to several factors, such as the distance from the radio station, the closeness of other strong radio stations, or the presence of buildings, bridges, or other large obstructions in the area.

old.title: FM reception
old.old: FM broadcasts are transmitted at high frequencies and do not bend to follow the earth’s surface. Because of this, FM broadcasts generally begin to fade within short distances from the station. FM signals are easily affected by buildings, mountains, and obstructions. This can lead to undesirable or unpleasant listening conditions which might lead you to believe a problem exists with your radio.

FM broadcasts are transmitted at high frequencies and do not bend to follow the earth’s surface. Because of this, FM broadcasts generally begin to fade within short distances from the station. FM signals are easily affected by buildings, mountains, and obstructions. The obstructions can cause unpleasant listening conditions. This is not a problem with your radio.

new.title: FM reception
new.new: FM broadcasts are transmitted at high frequencies and do not bend to follow the earth’s surface. Because of this, FM broadcasts generally begin to fade within short distances from the station. FM signals are easily affected by buildings, mountains, and obstructions. The obstructions can cause unpleasant listening conditions. This is not a problem with your radio.

pair.old: FM Radio station
pair.new: Radio station

pair.old: Fading - As your vehicle moves away from the radio station, the signal will weaken and sound will begin to fade. When this occurs, select another station with a stronger signal.
pair.new: Fading: As your vehicle moves away from the radio station, the signal will weaken, and the sound will begin to fade. When this occurs, select another station with a stronger signal.

pair.old: Flutter/Static - Weak FM signals or large obstructions between the transmitter and your radio can disturb the signal causing static or fluttering noises to occur. Reducing the treble level may lessen this effect until the disturbance clears.
pair.new: Flutter or static: Weak FM signals or large obstructions between the transmitter and your radio can disturb the signal, causing static or fluttering noises. Reducing the treble level may lessen this effect until the disturbance clears.

pair.old: Station Swapping - As an FM signal weakens, a more powerful signal near the same frequency may begin to play. This is because your radio is designed to lock onto the clearest signal. If this occurs, select another station with a stronger signal.
pair.new: Station swapping: As an FM signal weakens, a more powerful signal near the same frequency may begin to play. This is because your radio is designed to lock onto the clearest signal. If this occurs, select another station with a stronger signal.

pair.old: Using a mobile phone or a two way radio
pair.new: Using a mobile phone or 2-way radio

pair.old: When a mobile phone is used inside the vehicle, noise may be produced from the audio system. This does not mean that something is wrong with the audio equipment. Operate mobile devices as far from the audio equipment as possible.
pair.new: Using a mobile phone inside the vehicle may produce noise from the audio system. This does not mean that something is wrong with the audio equipment. Operate mobile devices as far from the audio equipment as possible.

pair.old: When using a communication system such as a mobile phone or a radio set inside the vehicle, a separate external antenna must be affixed. When a mobile phone or a radio set is used with only the internal antenna, it may interfere with the vehicle’s electrical system and adversely affect the safe operation of the vehicle.
pair.new: When using a communication system (such as a mobile phone or a radio set) inside the vehicle, you must affix a separate external antenna. When you use a mobile phone or radio set with only the internal antenna, it may interfere with the vehicle’s electrical system and adversely affect the safe operation of the vehicle.

pair.old: Do not use a Mobile phone while driving. Stop at a safe location to use a Mobile phone.
pair.new: Do not use a mobile phone while driving. Stop at a safe location to use a mobile phone.

pair.old: Driving while distracted can result in a loss of control that may lead to an accident, severe bodily injury, or death. The driver’s primary responsibility is in the safe and legal operation of a vehicle. Any use of handheld devices, other equipment, or vehicle systems that take the driver’s eyes, attention, and focus away from the safe operation of a vehicle are not permissible by law. These should never be used during the operation of the vehicle.
pair.new: Driving while distracted can result in a loss of control that may lead to an accident, severe bodily injury, or death. The driver’s primary responsibility is in the safe and legal operation of a vehicle. Any use of handheld devices, other equipment, or vehicle systems that take the driver’s eyes, attention, and focus away from the safe operation of a vehicle are not permissible by law. These devices should never be used during the operation of the vehicle.

pair.old: Declaration of Conformity
pair.new: Declaration of conformity

pair.old: The exhaust system should be checked whenever the vehicle is raised to change the oil or for any other purpose.
pair.new: Check the exhaust system whenever the vehicle is raised for changing the oil or any other purpose.

pair.old: If you hear a change in the sound of the exhaust or if you drive over something that strikes the underneath side of the vehicle, have the exhaust system checked as soon as possible by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If you hear a change in the sound of the exhaust, or if you drive over something that strikes the underbody of the vehicle, have the exhaust system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer as soon as possible.

pair.old: Do not inhale exhaust fumes or leave your engine running in an enclosed area for a prolonged time. Exhaust fumes contain carbon monoxide, a colorless and odorless gas that can cause unconsciousness and death by asphyxiation.
pair.new: Do not inhale exhaust fumes or leave your engine running in an enclosed area for a prolonged period of time. Exhaust fumes contain carbon monoxide, a colorless and odorless gas that can cause unconsciousness and death by asphyxiation.

pair.old: Do not drive with the liftgate open. Poisonous exhaust gases can enter the passenger compartment. If you must drive with the liftgate open proceed as follows:
pair.new: Do not drive with the liftgate open. Poisonous exhaust fumes can enter the passenger compartment. If you must drive with the liftgate open, proceed as follows:

pair.old: Open side vents.
pair.new: Open the side vents.

pair.old: Set the air intake control at “Fresh”, the air flow control at “Floor” or “Face”, and the fan at the highest speed.
pair.new: Set the air intake control to external, the air flow control to “Floor” or “Face,” and the fan to the highest speed.

pair.old: Before getting into the vehicle, you should examine the vehicle and its surroundings. After getting into the vehicle, you should check a number of things before driving.
pair.new: Before you get into your vehicle, examine the vehicle and its surroundings. After you get into your vehicle, check a number of things before driving, as described in this section.

pair.old: Before entering vehicle
pair.new: Before entering the vehicle

pair.old: Be sure that all windows, outside mirror(s), and outside lights are clean.
pair.new: Ensure that all windows, outside mirrors, and outside lights are clean.

pair.old: Fluid levels, such as engine oil, engine coolant, brake fluid, and washer fluid should be checked on a regular basis, at the exact interval depending on the fluid. Further details are provided in .
pair.new: Fluid levels, such as engine oil, engine coolant, brake fluid, and washer fluid, should be checked on a regular basis, at the specific recommended interval depending on the fluid. Further details are provided in .

pair.old: Focus on the road while driving. The driver’s primary responsibility is in the safe and legal operation of the vehicle. Use of any hand-held devices, other equipment or vehicle systems that distract the driver should not be used during vehicle operation.
pair.new: Focus on the road while driving. Your primary responsibility is in the safe and legal operation of the vehicle. Do not use any handheld devices, other equipment, or vehicle systems that could distract you while driving.

pair.old: Position the seat so that all controls are easily reached.
pair.new: Position the seat so that you can easily reach all controls.

pair.old: Adjust the inside and outside rear view mirrors.
pair.new: Adjust the inside and outside rearview mirrors.

pair.old: Check the operation of warning lights when the ENGINE START/STOP button is turned to the ON position.
pair.new: Check the operation of warning lights when the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON position.

pair.old: Release the parking brake and make sure the brake warning light is not on.
pair.new: Release the parking brake and make sure the Brake Warning Light is not ON.

pair.old: When you intend to park or stop the vehicle with the engine on, be careful not to depress the accelerator pedal for a long period of time. It may overheat the engine or exhaust system and cause fire.
pair.new: When you intend to park or stop the vehicle with the engine ON, be careful not to engage the accelerator pedal for a long period of time. It may overheat the engine or exhaust system and cause a fire.

pair.old: Always check the surrounding areas near your vehicle for people, especially children, before putting a vehicle into D (Drive) or R (Reverse).
pair.new: Always check the surrounding areas near your vehicle for people, especially children, before shifting the gear to D (Drive) or R (Reverse).

pair.old: Securely store items in your vehicle. When you make a sudden stop or turn the steering wheel rapidly, loose objects may drop on the floor and it could interfere with the operation of the foot pedals, possibly causing an accident.
pair.new: Securely store items in your vehicle. When you make a sudden stop or turn, loose objects may fall on the floorboard, interfere with the operation of the brake and accelerator pedals, and cause an accident.

pair.old: Always wear appropriate shoes when operating your vehicle. Unsuitable shoes (high heels, ski boots, sandals, etc.) may interfere with your ability to use the brake and accelerator pedals.
pair.new: Always wear appropriate shoes when operating your vehicle. Unsuitable shoes (such as high heels, ski boots, sandals, and so on) may interfere with your ability to engage the brake and accelerator pedals.

pair.old: The light will go off after about 30 seconds when the door is closed. It will also go off immediately when the theft-alarm system is armed.
pair.new: The light turns OFF about 30 seconds after you close the door. It also turns OFF immediately when you arm the theft-alarm system.

pair.old: Your vehicle is equipped with four different ignition positions.
pair.new: Your vehicle is equipped with 4 different ignition positions.

pair.old: To turn off the engine (START/RUN position) or vehicle power (ON position), press the ENGINE START/STOP button with the gear in the P (Park) position. When you press the ENGINE START/STOP button without the gear in the P (Park) position, the ENGINE START/STOP button will not change to the OFF position but to the ACC position.
pair.new: To turn OFF the engine, press the ENGINE START/STOP button and set it from the START/RUN position to the OFF position while the gear is in P (Park). To turn OFF the vehicle power, press the ENGINE START/STOP button and set it from the ON position to the OFF position while the gear is in P (Park). If you press the ENGINE START/STOP button without the gear in P (Park), its position will not change to OFF rather than ACC.

pair.old: The steering wheel locks when the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the OFF position to protect you against theft.
pair.new: To protect you from theft, the steering wheel locks when the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the OFF position.

pair.old: It locks when the door is opened.
pair.new: The steering wheel also locks when you open the door.

pair.old: If the steering wheel is not locked properly when you open the driver’s door, the warning chime will sound. Try locking the steering wheel again. If the problem is not solved, have the system checked by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the steering wheel does not lock properly when you open the driver side door, the warning chime sounds. Try locking the steering wheel again. If that does not solve the problem, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: In addition, if the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the OFF position after the driver’s door is opened, the steering wheel will not lock and the warning chime will sound. In such a situation, close the door. Then the steering wheel will lock and the warning chime will stop.
pair.new: Additionally, the steering wheel does not lock and the warning chime sounds if you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the OFF position after opening the driver side door. In this situation, close the door so that the steering wheel locks and the warning chime stops.

pair.old: If the steering wheel doesn’t unlock properly, the ENGINE START/STOP button will not work. Press the ENGINE START/STOP button while turning the steering wheel right and left to release the tension.
pair.new: If the steering wheel does not unlock properly, the ENGINE START/STOP button does not work. Press the ENGINE START/STOP button while turning the steering wheel right and left to release the tension.

pair.old: You can turn off the engine (START/RUN) or vehicle power ON, only when your Kia is not in motion.
pair.new: You can only turn the engine OFF or ON by pressing the ENGINE START/STOP button in the START/RUN position when your vehicle is not in motion.

pair.old: In an emergency while your Kia is in motion, you are able to turn the engine off and to the ACC position by pressing the ENGINE START/STOP button for more than 2 seconds or 3 times repeatedly within 3 seconds.
pair.new: If your vehicle is in motion during an emergency, you can turn the engine OFF by pressing the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ACC position for more than 2 seconds, or 3 times repeatedly within 3 seconds.

pair.old: To put your Kia into ACC, press the ENGINE START/STOP button while it is in the OFF position without depressing the brake pedal.
pair.new: To put your vehicle in ACC mode, press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the OFF position without engaging the brake pedal.

pair.old: If the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ACC position for more than 1 hour, the vehicle is turned off automatically to prevent battery discharge.
pair.new: If the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ACC position for more than 1 hour, your vehicle turns OFF automatically to prevent battery discharge.

pair.old: When your Kia is ON, the engine is running. To turn your Kia ON, press the ENGINE START/STOP button while it is in the ACC position without depressing the brake pedal.
pair.new: When your vehicle is ON, the engine is running. To turn your vehicle ON in ACC mode, press the ENGINE START/STOP button without engaging the brake pedal.

pair.old: The warning lights can be checked before the engine is started. Do not leave the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position for a long time. The battery may discharge, because the engine is not running.
pair.new: Warning lights appear before the engine starts so you can check them. Do not leave the ENGINE START/STOP button ON for a long period of time before starting the engine, as this may discharge the battery.

pair.old: To start the engine, depress the brake pedal and press the ENGINE START/STOP button with the gear in the P (Park) or the N (Neutral) position. For your safety, start the engine with the gear in the P (Park) position.
pair.new: To start the engine, engage the brake pedal and press the ENGINE START/STOP button with the gear in either P (Park) or N (Neutral). However, for your safety, always start the engine with the gear in P (Park).

pair.old: Never press the ENGINE START/STOP button while your Kia is in motion. This will result in loss of directional control and braking function, which could cause an accident.
pair.new: Never press the ENGINE START/STOP button while your vehicle is in motion. This results in the loss of directional control and braking function, which could cause an accident.

pair.old: Before leaving the driver’s seat, always make sure the gear is shifted to P (Park) position, set the parking brake fully and shut the engine off. Unexpected and sudden vehicle movement may occur if these precautions are not taken.
pair.new: Before you leave the driver’s seat, always make sure to shift the gear to P (Park), fully apply the parking brake, and press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the LOCK/OFF position. The vehicle may move suddenly and unexpectedly if you do not take these precautions.

pair.old: Never reach for the ENGINE START/STOP button or any other controls through the steering wheel while the vehicle is in motion. The presence of your hand or arm in the area could cause loss of vehicle control, an accident and serious bodily injury or death.
pair.new: Never reach for the ENGINE START/STOP button or any other controls through the steering wheel while the vehicle is in motion. The presence of your hand or arm in the area could result in loss of control of the vehicle, an accident, and serious bodily injury or death.

pair.old: Do not place any movable objects around the driver’s seat as they may move while driving, interfere with the driver and lead to an accident.
pair.new: Do not place any movable objects around the driver’s seat. These objects may move while driving, interfere with your driving, and cause an accident.

pair.old: Do not start the vehicle with the accelerator pedal engaged. The vehicle can move and lead to an accident.
pair.new: Do not start the vehicle with the accelerator pedal engaged. The vehicle can move and cause an accident.

pair.old: Wait until the engine rpm is normal. The vehicle may suddenly move if the brake pedal is released when the rpm is high.
pair.new: Wait until the engine rpm is normal. The vehicle may suddenly move if you release the brake pedal when the rpm is high.

pair.old: >When your Kia doors are opened or when the ENGINE START/STOP button is pressed your Kia will check for the smart key.
pair.new: When you open the vehicle doors or press the ENGINE START/STOP button, your vehicle will check for the smart key.

pair.old: If the smart key is not in your Kia, the " “ indicator and a message ”Key is not in your Kia" will appear on the instrument cluster and LCD window. If all doors are closed, the chime will sound for 5 seconds. The indicator or warning will turn off while your Kia is moving. Always have the smart key with you.
pair.new: If the smart key is not in your vehicle, the “ ” indicator and the message “Key is not in your Kia” appear on the instrument cluster and infotainment system screen. If all doors are closed, the chime sounds for 5 seconds. The indicator or chime turns OFF while your vehicle is moving. Always have the smart key with you.

pair.old: The engine will start only when the smart key is in your Kia. Never allow children, or any person who is unfamiliar with your Kia touch the ENGINE START/STOP button or related parts. Pushing the ENGINE START/STOP button while the smart key is in your Kia may result in unintended engine activation and/or unintended vehicle movement.
pair.new: The engine only starts when the smart key is in your vehicle. Never allow a child or any person who is unfamiliar with your vehicle to touch the ENGINE START/STOP button or related parts. Pressing the ENGINE START/STOP button while the smart key is in your vehicle may result in unintended engine operation or vehicle movement.

pair.old: If the engine stalls while your Kia is in motion, do not attempt to move the gear to the P (Park) position. If traffic and road conditions permit, you may put the gear in the N (Neutral) position while your Kia is still moving and press the ENGINE START/STOP button in an attempt to restart the engine.
pair.new: If the engine stalls while your vehicle is in motion, do not attempt to shift the gear to P (Park). If traffic and road conditions permit, you may shift the gear to N (Neutral) while your vehicle is still moving, and press the ENGINE START/STOP button to attempt to restart the engine.

pair.old: If the battery is weak or the smart key does not work correctly, you can start the engine by pressing the ENGINE START/STOP button with the smart key.
pair.new: If the battery is weak or the smart key does not work correctly, you can start the engine by pressing the ENGINE START/STOP button directly with the smart key.

pair.old: If the stop light fuse is blown, you cannot start the engine normally.
pair.new: If the brake light fuse is blown, you cannot start the engine normally.

pair.old: Replace the fuse with a new one. If not possible, you can start the engine by pressing the ENGINE START/STOP button for 10 seconds while it is in the ACC position. The engine can start without pressing the brake pedal, but for your safety, always the brake pedal before starting the engine.
pair.new: Replace the fuse with a new one. If this is not possible, start the engine by pressing the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ACC position for 10 seconds. The engine can start without the brake pedal engaged, but for your safety, always engage the brake pedal before starting the engine.

pair.old: Do not press the ENGINE START/STOP button for more than 10 seconds except when the stop light fuse is blown.
pair.new: Do not press the ENGINE START/STOP button for more than 10 seconds except when the brake light fuse is blown.

pair.old: Make sure the parking brake is firmly applied.
pair.new: Firmly apply the parking brake.

pair.old: Make sure the gear is shifted to P (Park) position. Depress the brake pedal fully.
pair.new: Shift the gear to P (Park). Engage the brake pedal fully.

pair.old: Press the ENGINE START/STOP button. It should be started without depressing the accelerator pedal.
pair.new: Press the ENGINE START/STOP button without engaging the accelerator pedal.

pair.old: Do not wait for the engine to warm up while your Kia remains stationary. Start driving at moderate engine speeds. (Steep accelerating and decelerating should be avoided.)
pair.new: Do not wait for the engine to warm up while your vehicle remains stationary. Start driving at moderate engine speeds. (Avoid rapid acceleration and deceleration.)

pair.old: Do not race or accelerate the engine immediately after starting.
pair.new: Do not race or accelerate the vehicle immediately after you start the engine.

pair.old: If the engine is cold, idle for several seconds to allow sufficient lubrication is applied in the turbocharger unit.
pair.new: If the engine is cold, idle it for several seconds to ensure that sufficient lubrication is applied within the turbocharger unit.

pair.old: After high speed or extended driving, which puts strain on the engine load, idle the engine about 1 minute before turning it off.
pair.new: After high speed or extended driving, which puts strain on the engine load, idle the engine for about 1 minute before turning it OFF.

pair.old: This idle time will allow the turbocharger to cool prior to shutting the engine off.
pair.new: This idle time allows the turbocharger to cool before you turn OFF the engine.

pair.old: Do not turn the engine off immediately after it has been subjected to a heavy load. Doing so may cause severe damage to the engine or turbocharger unit.
pair.new: Do not turn OFF the engine immediately after you have subjected it to a heavy load. Doing so may cause severe damage to the engine or turbocharger unit.

pair.old: Automatic transmission (Shift Lever)
pair.new: Automatic transmission (shift lever)

pair.old: Depress the brake pedal and the lock release button when shifting.
pair.new: Engage the brake pedal and the lock release button when you shift the gear.

pair.old: Press the unlock button when shifting.
pair.new: Press the unlock button when you shift the gear.
Press the unlock button when shifting gears.

pair.old: The shift lever can be shifted freely.
pair.new: You can shift the gear shift lever freely.

pair.old: The automatic transmission has 8 forward speeds and one reverse speed. The individual speeds are selected automaticall, depending on the balance between the fuel economy and the power.
pair.new: The automatic transmission has 8 forward speeds and 1 reverse speed. The transmission selects the individual speeds automatically depending on the balance between the fuel economy and the power.

pair.old: The first few shifts on a new vehicle may be somewhat abrupt if the battery has been disconnected. This is a normal condition, and the shifting sequence will adjust after shifts are cycled a few times by the TCM (Transmission Control Module) or PCM (Powertrain Control Module).
pair.new: The first few automatic gear shifts on a new vehicle may be somewhat abrupt if the battery has been disconnected. This is normal, and the shifting sequence adjusts after the TCM (Transmission Control Module) or PCM (Powertrain Control Module) cycles through the gear shifts a few times.

pair.old: For smooth operation, depress the brake pedal when shifting from N (Neutral) to a forward or reverse gear.
pair.new: For smooth operation, engage the brake pedal when shifting from N (Neutral) to a forward or reverse gear.

pair.old: Always check the surrounding areas near your vehicle for people, especially children, before shifting a vehicle into D (Drive) or R (Reverse).
pair.new: Always check the surrounding areas near your vehicle for people, especially children, before shifting the gear to D (Drive) or R (Reverse).

pair.old: Before leaving the driver’s seat, always make sure the shift lever is in the P (Park) position; then set the parking brake fully and shut the engine off. Unexpected and sudden vehicle movement can occur if these precautions are not followed in the order identified.
pair.new: Before you leave the driver’s seat, always make sure to shift the gear to P (Park), fully apply the parking brake, and press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the LOCK/OFF position. The vehicle may move suddenly and unexpectedly if you do not take these precautions.

pair.old: To avoid damage to your transmission, do not accelerate the engine in R (Reverse) or any forward gear position with the brakes on.
pair.new: To avoid damaging your transmission, do not accelerate while in R (Reverse) or in any forward gear position with the brakes engaged.

pair.old: Do not shift from N (Neutral) or P (Park) into D (Drive) or R (Reverse) when the engine is above idle speed.
pair.new: Do not shift the gear from N (Neutral) or P (Park) to D (Drive) or R (Reverse) when the engine is above idle speed.

pair.old: When stopped on an incline, do not hold your Kia stationary with engine power. Use the service brake or the parking brake.
pair.new: When stopped on an incline, do not use engine power to keep your vehicle stationary. Use the service brake or the parking brake.

pair.old: Do not rely on the transmission to keep your Kia parked on an incline. Use the parking brake.
pair.new: Do not rely on the transmission to keep your vehicle parked on an incline. Use the parking brake.

pair.old: LCD display for warning message
pair.new: Instrument cluster display for warning message

pair.old: A warning message is displayed on the LCD in a warning condition.
pair.new: The instrument cluster displays each of the following warning messages when applicable:

pair.old: The indicator in the instrument cluster displays the shift lever position when the ENGINE START/STOP is in the ON position.
pair.new: The indicator on the instrument cluster displays the gear shift lever position when the ENGINE START/STOP is in the ON position.

pair.old: Always come to a complete stop before shifting into P (Park). This position locks the transmission and prevents the drive wheels from rotating.
pair.new: Always come to a complete stop before shifting the gear to P (Park). This position locks the transmission and prevents the drive wheels from rotating.

pair.old: Shifting into P (Park) while your Kia is in motion will cause the drive wheels to lock. This can damage the transmission and cause you to lose control of your Kia.
pair.new: Shifting the gear to P (Park) while your vehicle is in motion causes the drive wheels to lock. This can damage the transmission and cause you to lose control of your vehicle.

pair.old: Do not use the P (Park) position in place of the parking brake. Always make sure the shift lever is latched in the P (Park) position and set the parking brake fully.
pair.new: Do not shift the gear to P (Park) instead of applying the parking brake. Always make sure the gear shift lever is in (Park) and fully apply the parking brake.

pair.old: Never leave a child unattended in a vehicle.
pair.new: Never leave a child unattended in your vehicle.

pair.old: The transmission may be damaged if you shift into P (Park) while the vehicle is in motion.
pair.new: Shifting the gear to P (Park) while the vehicle is in motion may damage the transmission.

pair.old: Use this position to drive your Kia backward.
pair.new: Shift the gear to R (Reverse) to drive your vehicle backward.

pair.old: Always come to a complete stop before shifting into or out of R (Reverse); you may damage the transmission if you shift into R while your Kia is moving forward, except as explained in .
pair.new: Always come to a complete stop before shifting the gear to or from R (Reverse). Shifting the gear to R (Reverse) while your vehicle is moving forward may damage the transmission, except as explained in .

pair.old: The wheels and transmission are not engaged. Your Kia will roll freely even on the slightest incline unless the parking brake or service brakes is applied.
pair.new: In N (Neutral), the wheels and transmission are not engaged. Your vehicle can roll freely, even on the slightest incline, unless you apply the parking brake or service brake.

pair.old: Always park the vehicle in “P” (Park) for safety and engage the parking brake. If left in “N” (Neutral), the vehicle may move and cause serious damage and injury.
pair.new: Always park the vehicle with the gear in P (Park) and apply the parking brake for safety. If you leave the gear in N (Neutral), the vehicle may move and cause serious damage and injury.

pair.old: After the ENGINE START/STOP button has been turned off, the Electronic Parking Brake (EPB) cannot be disengaged.
pair.new: After you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the OFF position, you cannot release the Electronic Parking Brake (EPB).

pair.old: For EPB equipped vehicles with AUTO HOLD function used while driving, if the ignition button has been turned “OFF”, the electronic parking brake will be engaged automatically. The AUTO HOLD function should be turned off before the ignition button is turned off.
pair.new: When you use the AUTO HOLD function while driving a vehicle equipped with EPB, if you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the OFF position, the EPB is applied automatically. Turn the Auto Hold function OFF before you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the OFF position.

pair.old: This is the normal forward driving position. The transmission will automatically shift through 8 gear sequence, providing the best fuel economy and power.
pair.new: D (Drive) is the normal forward driving gear shift position. The transmission automatically shifts through an 8 gear sequence to provide the best fuel economy and power.

pair.old: For extra power when passing another vehicle or climbing grades, depress the accelerator fully, at which time the transmission will automatically downshift to the next lower gear.
pair.new: For extra power when passing another vehicle or climbing uphill, engage the accelerator fully. The transmission automatically shifts down to the next lowest gear.

pair.old: Always come to a complete stop before shifting into D (Drive).
pair.new: Always come to a complete stop before shifting the gear to D (Drive).

pair.old: Manual mode is selected by pushing the shift lever from the D (Drive) position into the manual gate. To return to D (Drive) range operation, push the shift lever back into the main gate.
pair.new: Select manual mode by pushing the gear shift lever from D (Drive) into the manual gate. To return to D (Drive) range operation, push the gear shift lever back into the main gate.

pair.old: In manual mode, moving the shift lever backwards and forwards will allow you to make gearshifts rapidly. In contrast to a manual transmission, manual mode allows gearshifts with the accelerator pedal depressed.
pair.new: In manual mode, moving the gear shift lever back and forth allows you to shift gears rapidly. Unlike a manual transmission, manual mode allows you to shift gears while engaging the accelerator pedal.

pair.old: Up (+): Push the lever forward once to shift up one gear.
pair.new: Up (+): Push the lever forward once to shift up 1 gear.
Up (+): Push the lever forward once to shift up 1 gear.

pair.old: Down (-): Pull the lever backwards once to shift down one gear.
pair.new: Down (-): Pull the lever backward once to shift down 1 gear.
Down (-): Pull the lever backward once to shift down 1 gear.

pair.old: The driver must execute upshifts in accordance with conditions, taking care to keep the engine speed below the red zone.
pair.new: Shift the gear upward according to the driving conditions, taking care to keep the engine speed below the red zone.
Shift the gear upward according to the driving conditions, taking care to keep the engine speed below the red zone.

pair.old: Only the 8 forward gears can be selected. To reverse or park your Kia, move the shift lever to the R (Reverse) or P (Park) position as required.
pair.new: You can only select the 8 forward gears. To reverse or park your vehicle, move the gear shift lever to R (Reverse) or P (Park) as required.
You can only select the 8 forward gears. To reverse or park your vehicle, move the gear shift lever to R (Reverse) or P (Park) as required.

pair.old: Downshifts are made automatically when your Kia slows down. When your Kia stops, 1st gear is automatically selected.
pair.new: The gear shifts down automatically when you slow down your vehicle. When you stop your vehicle, the 1st gear is selected automatically.
The gear shifts down automatically when you slow down your vehicle. When you stop your vehicle, the 1st gear is selected automatically.

pair.old: When the engine rpm approaches the red zone shift points are varied to upshift automatically.
pair.new: When the engine RPM approaches the red zone, the gear shift points are varied to shift up automatically.
When the engine RPM approaches the red zone, the gear shift points are varied to shift up automatically.

pair.old: To maintain the required levels of vehicle performance and safety, the system may not execute certain gearshifts when the shift lever is operated.
pair.new: To maintain the required levels of vehicle performance and safety, the system may not execute certain shifts between gears when you operate the gear shift lever.
To maintain the required levels of vehicle performance and safety, the system may not execute certain shifts between gears when you operate the gear shift lever.

pair.old: When driving on a slippery road, push the shift lever forward into the + (up) position. This causes the transmission to shift into the 2nd gear which is better for smooth driving on a slippery road. Push the shift lever to the -(down) side to shift back to the 1st gear.
pair.new: When driving on a slippery road, push the gear shift lever forward into the Up (+) position so that the transmission shifts into 2nd gear, which helps with smooth driving on slippery roads. To shift back into 1st gear, push the gear shift lever into the Down (-) position.

When driving on a slippery road, push the gear shift lever forward into the Up (+) position so that the transmission shifts into 2nd gear, which helps with smooth driving on slippery roads. To shift back into 1st gear, push the gear shift lever into the Down (-) position.

pair.old: The paddle shift function is available when the shift lever is in the D (Drive) position or the manual mode.
pair.new: The paddle shift function is available when the gear shift lever is in D (Drive) or manual mode.
The paddle shift function is available when the gear shift lever is in D (Drive) or manual mode.

pair.old: With the shift lever in the D position
pair.new: With the gear shift lever in D (Drive)
With the gear shift lever in D (Drive)

pair.old: The paddle shift function will operate when your Kia speed is more than 6 mph (10 km/h).
pair.new: The paddle shift function operates when your speed is above 6 mph (10 km/h).
The paddle shift function operates when your speed is above 6 mph (10 km/h).

pair.old: Pull the [+] or [-] paddle shifter once to shift up or down one gear and the system changes from automatic mode to manual mode.
pair.new: Pull the [+] or [-] paddle shifter once to shift up or down 1 gear. The system changes from automatic mode to manual mode.
Pull the [+] or [-] paddle shifter once to shift up or down 1 gear. The system changes from automatic mode to manual mode.

pair.old: To change back to automatic shift mode from manual shift mode, do one of the following:
pair.new: To change from manual shift mode back to automatic shift mode, perform one of the following actions:
To change from manual shift mode back to automatic shift mode, perform one of the following actions:

pair.old: Pull the [+] paddle shifter for more than one second
pair.new: Pull the [+] paddle shifter for more than 1 second
Pull the [+] paddle shifter for more than 1 second

pair.old: Move the shift lever from D (Drive) to manual gate and return it to D position again
pair.new: Move the gear shift lever from D (Drive) to manual, and then return to D (Drive).
Move the gear shift lever from D (Drive) to manual, and then return to D (Drive).

pair.old: The manual shift mode also changes back to automatic shift mode in one of following situations
pair.new: Manual shift mode also changes back to automatic shift mode in any of following situations:
Manual shift mode also changes back to automatic shift mode in any of following situations:

pair.old: When the accelerator pedal is gently depressed for more than 6 seconds while driving
