pair.new: You gently engage the accelerator pedal for more than 6 seconds while driving.
You gently engage the accelerator pedal for more than 6 seconds while driving.

pair.old: When your Kia stops
pair.new: You stop the vehicle.
You stop the vehicle.

pair.old: With the shift lever in the manual mode
pair.new: With the gear shift lever in manual mode
With the gear shift lever in manual mode

pair.old: Pull the [+] or [-] paddle shifter once to shift up or down one gear.
pair.new: Pull the [+] or [-] paddle shifter once to shift up or down 1 gear.
Pull the [+] or [-] paddle shifter once to shift up or down 1 gear.

pair.old: If you pull the [+] and [-] paddle shifters at the same time, you cannot shift the gear.
pair.new: If you pull both paddle shifters at the same time, you cannot shift the gear.

pair.old: Depress and hold the brake pedal.
pair.new: Engage and hold the brake pedal.
Engage and hold the brake pedal.

pair.old: Start the engine or turn the ENGINE START/STOP button to the ON position.
pair.new: Start the engine or press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position.
Start the engine or press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position.

pair.old: Move the shift lever.
pair.new: Move the gear shift lever.
Move the gear shift lever.
Move the gear shift lever.

context.old: If the shift lever cannot be moved from the P (Park) position to the R (Reverse) position with the brake pedal depressed, continue to depress the brake, then do the following:
If you cannot move the gear shift lever from P (Park) to R (Reverse) with the brake pedal engaged, continue to engage the brake, then perform the following actions:

step.cmd: Place the ENGINE START/STOP button in the LOCK/OFF position.
Carefully remove the cap (1) covering the shift-lock release access hole.
Insert a tool (e.g., flathead screwdriver) into the access hole and press down on the tool.
Remove the tool from the shift lock override access hole then install the cap.

tasktroubleshooting.old: If the shift lever does not move even after performing this procedure, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
If the gear shift lever does not move even after you perform this procedure, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

context.new: If you cannot move the gear shift lever from P (Park) to R (Reverse) with the brake pedal engaged, continue to engage the brake, then perform the following actions:

step.cmd: Press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the LOCK/OFF position.
Carefully remove the cap (1) covering the shift-lock release access hole.
Insert a tool (such as a flathead screwdriver) into the access hole and press down on the tool.
Remove the tool from the shift lock override access hole, then replace the cap.

tasktroubleshooting.new: If the gear shift lever does not move even after you perform this procedure, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Never move the shift lever from P (Park) or N (Neutral) to any other position with the accelerator pedal depressed.
pair.new: Never move the gear shift lever from P (Park) or N (Neutral) to any other position with the accelerator pedal engaged.
Never move the gear shift lever from P (Park) or N (Neutral) to any other position with the accelerator pedal engaged.

pair.old: Never move the shift lever into P (Park) when your Kia is in motion.
pair.new: Never move the gear shift lever into P (Park) while the vehicle is in motion.
Never move the gear shift lever into P (Park) while the vehicle is in motion.

pair.old: Be sure the vehicle is completely stopped before you attempt to shift into R (Reverse) or D (Drive).
pair.new: Be sure you have completely stopped the vehicle before you attempt to shift the gear to R (Reverse) or D (Drive).
Be sure you have completely stopped the vehicle before you attempt to shift the gear to R (Reverse) or D (Drive).

pair.old: Never take your Kia out of gear and coast down a hill. This may be extremely hazardous. Always leave your Kia in gear when moving.
pair.new: Never shift the vehicle out of gear and coast down a hill. This may be extremely hazardous. Always leave the vehicle in gear while moving.
Never shift the vehicle out of gear and coast down a hill. This may be extremely hazardous. Always leave the vehicle in gear while moving.

pair.old: Do not “ride” the brakes. This can cause them to overheat and malfunction. When driving down a long hill, slow down and manually shift to a lower gear. When you do this, engine braking will help slow down your Kia.
pair.new: Do not “ride” the brakes. This can cause them to overheat and malfunction. Instead, when you are driving down a long hill, slow down and shift to a lower gear. When you do this, engine braking will help slow down the vehicle.

Do not “ride” the brakes. This can cause them to overheat and malfunction. Instead, when you are driving down a long hill, slow down and shift to a lower gear. When you do this, engine braking will help slow down the vehicle.

pair.old: Exercise extreme caution when driving on a slippery surface. Be especially careful when braking, accelerating or shifting gears. On a slippery surface, an abrupt change in vehicle speed can cause the drive wheels to lose traction and you may lose your Kia control.
pair.new: Exercise extreme caution when driving on a slippery surface. Be especially careful when braking, accelerating, or shifting gears. On a slippery surface, an abrupt change in the vehicle’s speed can cause the drive wheels to lose traction, resulting in a loss of control over the vehicle.

Exercise extreme caution when driving on a slippery surface. Be especially careful when braking, accelerating, or shifting gears. On a slippery surface, an abrupt change in the vehicle’s speed can cause the drive wheels to lose traction, resulting in a loss of control over the vehicle.

pair.old: Optimum vehicle performance and economy is obtained by smoothly depressing and releasing the accelerator pedal.
pair.new: Smoothly engage and release the accelerator pedal for optimal vehicle performance and fuel economy.
Smoothly engage and release the accelerator pedal for optimal vehicle performance and fuel economy.

pair.old: When driving uphill or downhill, always shift to D (Drive) for driving forward or shift to R (Reverse) for driving backwards, and check the gear position indicated on the cluster before driving. Driving in the opposite direction of the selected gear can lead to a dangerous situation by shutting off the engine and affecting the braking performance.
pair.new: When driving uphill or downhill, always shift the gear to D (Drive) for driving forward or R (Reverse) for driving backward. Check the gear position displayed on the instrument cluster before driving. Driving in the opposite direction of the selected gear can put you in danger by turning OFF the engine and affecting the braking performance.

When driving uphill or downhill, always shift the gear to D (Drive) for driving forward or R (Reverse) for driving backward. Check the gear position displayed on the instrument cluster before driving. Driving in the opposite direction of the selected gear can put you in danger by turning OFF the engine and affecting the braking performance.

pair.old: Always buckle-up! In a collision, an unbelted occupant is significantly more likely to be seriously injured or killed than a properly belted occupant.
pair.new: Always buckle up! In a collision, an unbelted occupant is significantly more likely to be seriously injured or killed than a properly belted occupant.
Always buckle up! In a collision, an unbelted occupant is significantly more likely to be seriously injured or killed than a properly belted occupant.

pair.old: Avoid high speeds when cornering or turning.
pair.new: Avoid high speeds when turning.
Avoid high speeds when turning.
Avoid high speeds when turning.

pair.old: Do not make quick steering wheel movements, such as sharp lane changes or fast, sharp turns.
pair.new: Do not make quick steering wheel movements (such as sharp lane changes or fast, sharp turns).
Do not make quick steering wheel movements (such as sharp lane changes or fast, sharp turns).
Do not make quick steering wheel movements (such as sharp lane changes or fast, sharp turns).
Do not make quick steering wheel movements (such as sharp lane changes or fast, sharp turns).

pair.old: The risk of a rollover is greatly increased if you lose control of your vehicle at highway speeds.
pair.new: Losing control of your vehicle at high speeds greatly increases the risk of a rollover.
Losing control of your vehicle at high speeds greatly increases the risk of a rollover.
Losing control of your vehicle at high speeds greatly increases the risk of a rollover.

pair.old: Losing control often occurs if two or more wheels drop off the roadway and the driver oversteers to reenter the roadway.
pair.new: Loss of control often occurs if 2 or more wheels drop off the road and you oversteer to reenter the road.
Loss of control often occurs if 2 or more wheels drop off the road and you oversteer to reenter the road.

pair.old: In the event your vehicle leaves the roadway, do not steer sharply. Instead, slow down before gradually reentering the travel lanes. Abruptly steering to regain the road risks loss of control, serious injury, and death.
pair.new: If your vehicle leaves the road, do not steer sharply. Instead, slow down before gradually reentering the travel lanes. Abruptly steering to reenter the road risks loss of control of the vehicle, serious injury, and death.

If your vehicle leaves the road, do not steer sharply. Instead, slow down before gradually reentering the travel lanes. Abruptly steering to reenter the road risks loss of control of the vehicle, serious injury, and death.

pair.old: If your vehicle becomes stuck in snow, mud, sand, etc. you may attempt to rock your Kia free by moving it forward and backward. Do not attempt this procedure if people or objects are anywhere near your Kia. During the rocking operation your Kia may suddenly move forward or backward as it becomes unstuck, causing injury or damage to nearby people or objects.
pair.new: If your vehicle becomes stuck in snow, mud, sand, and so on, then you may attempt to rock it free by moving it forward and backward. Do not attempt this procedure if people or objects are anywhere near the vehicle. The vehicle may suddenly move forward or backward as it becomes unstuck, causing injury or damage to nearby people or objects.

If your vehicle becomes stuck in snow, mud, sand, and so on, then you may attempt to rock it free by moving it forward and backward. Do not attempt this procedure if people or objects are anywhere near the vehicle. The vehicle may suddenly move forward or backward as it becomes unstuck, causing injury or damage to nearby people or objects.

pair.old:
No text changes, general question for consistency.
To move up a steep grade from a standing start: To move up a steep grade from a standing start,engage the brake pedal and perform the following actions:

new.new: To move up a steep grade from a standing start,engage the brake pedal and perform the following actions:

pair.old: Depress the brake pedal, shift the shift lever to D (Drive). Select the appropriate gear depending on load weight and steepness of the grade and release the parking brake. Depress the accelerator gradually after releasing the service brakes.
pair.new: Select the appropriate gear depending on load weight and steepness of the grade and release the parking brake. Engage the accelerator gradually after releasing the service brakes.
Select the appropriate gear depending on load weight and steepness of the grade and release the parking brake. Engage the accelerator gradually after releasing the service brakes.

pair.old: Depress the brake pedal and the lock release button when shifting.
pair.new: Engage the brake pedal and press the lock release button when shifting gears.

pair.old: The shift lever can be freely shifted.
pair.new: You can freely move the gear shift lever.

pair.old: To move the shift lever from/to P (Park) or between R (Reverse) and D (Drive), you must depress the brake pedal for your Kia to stand still.
pair.new: When moving the gear shift lever to or from P (Park), or between R (Reverse) and D (Drive), you must engage the brake pedal to keep your vehicle still.

pair.old: The dual clutch transmission has 8 forward speeds and one reverse speed.
pair.new: The dual clutch transmission has 8 forward speeds and 1 reverse speed.

pair.old: The individual speeds are selected automatically in the D (Drive) position.
pair.new: The individual speeds are selected automatically when the gear is in D (Drive).

pair.old: ALWAYS check the surrounding areas near your vehicle for people, especially children, before shifting a vehicle into D (Drive) or R (Reverse).
pair.new: Always check the surrounding areas near your vehicle for people, especially children, before shifting the gear to D (Drive) or R (Reverse).

pair.old: Before leaving the driver’s seat, always make sure the shift lever is in the P (Park) position, then set the parking brake, and place the ENGINE START/STOP button in the LOCK/OFF position. Unexpected and sudden vehicle movement can occur if these precautions are not followed.
pair.new: Before you leave the driver’s seat, always make sure to shift the gear to P (Park), fully apply the parking brake, and press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the LOCK/OFF position. The vehicle may move suddenly and unexpectedly if you do not take these precautions.

pair.old: When using Manual Shift Mode, use caution when shifting from a higher gear to a lower gear on slippery roads. This could cause the tires to slip and may result in an accident.
pair.new: In Manual shift mode, use caution when shifting from a higher gear to a lower gear on slippery roads. This could cause the tires to slip, which may result in an accident.

pair.old: To avoid damage to your transmission, do not try to accelerate with the shift lever in R (Reverse) or any forward gear position with the brake engaged.
pair.new: To avoid damaging your transmission, do not try to accelerate with the gear shift lever in R (Reverse) or any forward gear position while the brake is engaged.

pair.old: When stopped on a slope, do not hold the vehicle with accelerator pedal. Engage the service brake or the parking brake.
pair.new: When stopped on an incline, do not use engine power to keep your vehicle stationary. Use the service brake or the parking brake.

pair.old: The Dual Clutch Transmission gives the driving feel of a manual transmission yet provides the ease of a fully automatic transmission. Unlike a traditional automatic transmission, the gear shifting can be felt (and heard) on the dual clutch transmission
pair.new: The Dual clutch transmission feels like driving with a manual transmission with the ease of a fully automatic transmission. Unlike a traditional automatic transmission, you can feel and hear the gear shifting with a dual clutch transmission.

pair.old: Shift into Drive range and have fully automatic shifting, similar to a conventional automatic transmission.
pair.new: Shift into D (Drive) range for fully automatic gear shifting, similar to a conventional automatic transmission.

pair.old: Dual clutch transmission adopts wet type dual clutch, which is different from the torque converter of Automatic Transmissions, and has better acceleration performance when driving. However, initial launch may be a little bit slower than an Automatic Transmission.
pair.new: Dual clutch transmission uses wet type dual clutch, which is different from the torque converter of automatic transmissions and has better acceleration performance when you drive. However, the initial launch may be a bit slower than an automatic transmission.

pair.old: When rapidly accelerating at low vehicle speed, the engine could rev to a high rpm depending on vehicle drive condition.
pair.new: When you rapidly accelerate from a low speed, the engine could rev to a high rpm depending on the driving conditions.

pair.old: For a smooth launch uphill, depress the accelerator pedal smoothly.
pair.new: For a smooth climb uphill, engage the accelerator pedal smoothly.

pair.old: If you release the accelerator pedal at low vehicle speeds, you may feel a strong engine brake, which is similar to a manual transmission.
pair.new: If you release the accelerator pedal while driving at a low speed, you may feel a strong engine brake, which is similar to a manual transmission.

pair.old: When driving downhill, you may use Sports Mode to downshift to a lower gear to control your speed without using the brake pedal excessively.
pair.new: When driving downhill, use SPORT mode to shift down to a lower gear to control your speed without engaging the brake pedal excessively.

pair.old: When turning the engine on and off, you may hear clicking sounds as the system goes through a self-test. These are normal sounds for the Dual Clutch Transmission.
pair.new: When turning the engine ON and OFF, you may hear clicking sounds as the system goes through a self-test. These are normal sounds for the Dual clutch transmission.

pair.old: Always come to a complete stop before shifting into D (Drive) or R (Reverse).
pair.new: Always come to a complete stop before shifting the gear to D (Drive) or R (Reverse).

pair.old: Do not put the shift lever in N (Neutral) while driving.
pair.new: Do not put the gear shift lever in N (Neutral) while driving.

pair.old: If there is a transmission failure, your Kia may not move, and the position indicator (D, R) will blink on the cluster. At this time, have the system checked by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If there is a transmission failure, the position indicator (D, R) blinks on the instrument cluster, and your vehicle may not move. If this happens, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: This warning message is displayed when vehicle is driven slowly on a grade, and the vehicle detects that the brake pedal is not applied.
pair.new: The following warning message displays when you drive slowly on a steep grade and the vehicle detects that you have not engaged the brake pedal:

pair.old: Driving up hills or on steep grades:
pair.new: When driving up hills or on steep grades:

pair.old: To hold your Kia on an incline use the foot brake or the parking brake.
pair.new: Engage the brake pedal or apply the parking brake to hold your vehicle on an incline.

pair.old: When in stop-and-go traffic on an incline, keep some distance ahead before moving your Kia forward. Then hold your Kia on the incline with the foot brake.
pair.new: When driving in stop-and-go traffic on an incline, keep some distance ahead before moving your vehicle forward. Then hold your vehicle on the incline by engaging the brake pedal.

pair.old: If your Kia is held on a hill by applying the accelerator pedal or by creeping with brake pedal disengaged, the clutch and transmission may overheat, which can result in damage. At this time, a warning message will appear on the LCD display.
pair.new: If you hold your vehicle on an incline by engaging the accelerator pedal or by creeping uphill with brake pedal disengaged, the clutch and transmission may overheat, which can result in damage. If this happens, the instrument cluster displays a warning message.

pair.old: If the LCD warning is active, the foot brake must be applied.
pair.new: If the warning is active on the instrument cluster, you must engage the brake pedal.

pair.old: Ignoring the warnings can lead to damage to the transmission.
pair.new: Ignoring the warnings can result in damage to the transmission.

pair.old: Under certain conditions, such as repeated stop-and-go launches on steep grades, sudden acceleration, or other harsh driving conditions, the transmission clutch temperatures will increase quickly. This may cause the transmission clutch to overheat.
pair.new: Under certain conditions (such as repeated stop-and-go climbing on steep grades, sudden acceleration, or other harsh driving conditions), the transmission clutch temperatures increase quickly. This may cause the transmission clutch to overheat.

pair.old: When the clutch is overheated, the safe protection mode engages and the gear position indicator on the cluster blinks with a chime. “Transmission temp. is high! Stop safely” warning message will appear on the LCD display and driving may not be smooth.
pair.new: When the clutch is overheated, the vehicle engages safe protection mode, and the gear shift position indicator on the instrument cluster blinks and sounds a chime. The warning message “Transmission temp. is high! Stop safely” appears on the instrument cluster. Driving may not be smooth.

pair.old: Pull over to a safe location, stop the vehicle with the engine running, apply the brakes to shift the vehicle to P (Park), and allow the transmission to cool.
pair.new: Pull over to a safe location, stop the vehicle with the engine running, engage the brake pedal so that you can shift the gear to P (Park), and allow the transmission to cool.

pair.old: If you ignore this warning, driving condition may worsen. You may experience abrupt or frequent shifts, or jerkiness. To return to the normal driving condition, stop your Kia and apply the foot brake or shift into P (Park).
pair.new: If you ignore this warning, driving conditions may worsen. You may experience abrupt or frequent shifts or jerks. To return to normal driving conditions, stop your vehicle and engage the brake pedal or shift the gear to P (Park).

pair.old: Allow the transmission to cool for a few minutes with the engine on before driving off.
pair.new: Allow the transmission to cool for a few minutes with the engine ON before driving away.

pair.old: When possible, drive your Kia smoothly.
pair.new: Drive your vehicle smoothly when possible.
Drive your vehicle smoothly when possible.

pair.old: If you continue to drive your Kia continues and the clutch temperatures reach the maximum temperature limit, the “Transmission Hot! Park with engine on” warning will be displayed. When this occurs, the clutch is disabled until the clutch cools to normal temperatures.
pair.new: If you continue to drive your vehicle and the clutch reaches the maximum temperature limit, the “Transmission Hot! Park with engine on” warning is displayed. When this occurs, the clutch is disabled until it cools to normal temperatures.

pair.old: The warning will display a time to wait for the transmission to cool.
pair.new: The warning message displays a time to wait for the transmission to cool.

pair.old: Pull over to a safe location, stop your Kia with the engine running, apply the brakes and shift your Kia to P (Park), and allow the transmission to cool.
pair.new: Pull over to a safe location, stop your vehicle with the engine running, engage the brake pedal, shift the gear to P (Park), and allow the transmission to cool.
Pull over to a safe location, stop your vehicle with the engine running, engage the brake pedal, shift the gear to P (Park), and allow the transmission to cool.

pair.old: When the message “Trans cooled. Resume driving” appears, you can continue to drive your vehicle.
pair.new: You can continue to drive your vehicle when the message “Trans cooled. Resume driving” appears.
You can continue to drive your vehicle when the message “Trans cooled. Resume driving” appears.

pair.old: If any of the warning messages in the LCD display continue to blink, have the system checked by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If any of the warning messages on the instrument cluster continue to blink, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
If any of the warning messages on the instrument cluster continue to blink, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: The indicator in the instrument cluster displays the shift lever position when the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON position.
pair.new: The indicator on the instrument cluster displays the gear shift lever position when the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON position.

pair.old: Always come to a complete stop before shifting into P (Park).
pair.new: Always come to a complete stop before shifting the gear to P (Park).

pair.old: To shift from P (Park), you must depress firmly on the brake pedal and make sure your foot is off the accelerator pedal.
pair.new: To shift the gear from P (Park), engage the brake pedal firmly and make sure your foot is off the accelerator pedal.

pair.old: The shift lever must be in P (Park) before turning the engine off.
pair.new: The gear shift lever must be in P (Park) before you turn the engine OFF.

pair.old: Shifting into P (Park) while the vehicle is in motion may cause you to lose control of the vehicle.
pair.new: Shifting the gear to P (Park) while the vehicle is in motion may cause you to lose control of the vehicle.

pair.old: After the vehicle has stopped, always make sure the shift lever is in P (Park), apply the parking brake, and turn the engine off.
pair.new: After you stop the vehicle, always make sure the gear shift lever is in P (Park), apply the parking brake, and turn the engine OFF.

pair.old: Do not use the P (Park) position in place of the parking brake.
pair.new: Do not shift the gear to P (Park) instead of applying the parking brake.

pair.old: Use this position to drive the vehicle backward.
pair.new: Shift the gear to R (Reverse) to drive your vehicle backward.

pair.old: Always come to a complete stop before shifting into or out of R (Reverse); you may damage the transmission if you shift into R (Reverse) while the vehicle is in motion.
pair.new: Always come to a complete stop before shifting the gear to or from R (Reverse). Shifting the gear to R (Reverse) while your vehicle is in motion may damage the transmission.

pair.old: The wheels and transmission are not engaged.
pair.new: In N (Neutral), the wheels and transmission are not engaged.

pair.old: Do not shift into gear unless your foot is firmly on the brake pedal. Shifting into gear when the engine is running at high speed can cause the vehicle to move very rapidly. You could lose control of the vehicle and hit people or objects.
pair.new: Do not shift the gear unless your foot is firmly on the brake pedal. Shifting the gear when the engine is running at a high speed can cause the vehicle to move very rapidly. You could lose control of the vehicle and hit people or objects.

pair.old: After the ENGINE START/STOP button has been turned off, the electronic parking brake cannot be disengaged.
pair.new: After you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the OFF position, you cannot release the Electronic Parking Brake (EPB).

pair.old: For EPB (Electronic Parking Brake) equipped vehicles with AUTO HOLD function used while driving, if the ignition button has been turned “OFF”, the electronic parking brake will be engaged automatically. Therefore, AUTO HOLD function should be turned off before the ignition button is turned off.
pair.new: When you use the AUTO HOLD function while driving a vehicle equipped with EPB, if you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the OFF position, the EPB is applied automatically. Turn the AUTO HOLD function OFF before you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the OFF position.

pair.old: This is the normal driving position. The transmission will automatically shift through a 8 gear sequence, providing the best fuel economy and power.
pair.new: D (Drive) gear is the normal forward driving gear shift position. The transmission automatically shifts through an 8-gear sequence to provide the best fuel economy and power.

pair.old: For extra power when passing another vehicle or driving uphill, depress the accelerator pedal further until you feel the transmission downshift to a lower gear.
pair.new: For extra power when passing another vehicle or climbing uphill, engage the accelerator fully. The transmission automatically shifts down to the next lowest gear.

pair.old: To stop the vehicle during driving, please press brake pedal fully to prevent unintended movement.
pair.new: To stop the vehicle while driving, engage the brake pedal fully to prevent unintended movement.

pair.old: Whether the vehicle is stationary or in motion, manual mode is selected by pushing the shift lever from the D (Drive) position into the manual gate. To return to D (Drive) range operation, push the shift lever back into the main gate.
pair.new: Whether the vehicle is stationary or in motion, you can select Manual mode by pushing the gear shift lever from D (Drive) into the manual gate. To return to D (Drive) range operation, push the gear shift lever back into the main gate.

pair.old: In manual mode, moving the shift lever backwards and forwards will allow you to make gearshifts rapidly. In contrast to a manual transmission, Manual mode allows gearshifts with the accelerator pedal depressed.
pair.new: In manual mode, moving the gear shift lever back and forth allows you to shift gears rapidly. Unlike a manual transmission, manual mode allows you to shift gears while engaging the accelerator pedal.

pair.old: If the brake pedal is repeatedly depressed and released with the shift lever in the P (Park) position, a chattering noise near the shift lever may be heard. This is a normal condition.
pair.new: If you repeatedly engage and release the brake pedal with the gear shift lever in P (Park), you may hear a chattering noise and vibration near the gear shift lever. This is normal.

pair.old: Always fully depress the brake pedal before and while shifting out of the P (Park) position to avoid inadvertent motion of your Kia which could injure persons in or around your Kia.
pair.new: Always engage the brake pedal fully before and while moving the gear shift lever from P (Park) into another position to avoid inadvertent vehicle motion, which could injure people in or around the vehicle.

pair.old: If the shift lever cannot be moved from the P (Park) position to the R (Reverse) position with the brake pedal depressed, continue to depress the brake, then do the following:
pair.new: If you cannot move the gear shift lever from P (Park) to R (Reverse) with the brake pedal engaged, continue to engage the brake, then perform the following actions:

pair.old: Place the ENGINE START/STOP button in the LOCK/OFF position.
pair.new: Press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the LOCK/OFF position.

pair.old: Press and hold the lock release button on the shift lever.
pair.new: Press and hold the lock release button on the gear shift lever.

pair.old: If the shift lever does not move even after performing this procedure, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the gear shift lever does not move even after you perform this procedure, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Always use the parking brake. Do not depend on placing the transmission in P (Park) to keep the vehicle from moving.
pair.new: Always apply the parking brake. Do not depend on shifting the gear to P (Park) to keep the vehicle from moving.

pair.old: To move up a steep grade from a standing start:
pair.new: To move up a steep grade from a standing start, engage the brake pedal and perform the following actions:

pair.old: The All Wheel Drive (AWD) system delivers engine power to front and rear wheels for maximum traction.
pair.new: The All Wheel Drive (AWD) system delivers engine power to the front and rear wheels for maximum traction.

pair.old: AWD is useful when extra traction is required, such as when driving slippery, muddy, wet, or snow-covered roads.
pair.new: Use AWD when you require extra traction (for example, when driving on slippery, muddy, wet, or snowy roads).

pair.old: If the system determines there is a need for all wheel drive, the engine’s driving power will be distributed to all four wheels automatically.
pair.new: If the AWD system detects a need for all wheel drive, the engine’s driving power is automatically distributed to all 4 wheels.

pair.old: This vehicle is designed primarily for on road use although it can operate effectively off road. However, it was not designed to drive in challenging off-road conditions. Driving in conditions that exceed the vehicle’s intended design or the driver’s experience level may result in severe injury or death.
pair.new: Although this vehicle can operate effectively off road, it is designed primarily for on road driving. This vehicle is not designed to drive in challenging off road conditions. Driving in conditions that exceed the vehicle’s intended design or the driver’s experience level may result in serious injury or death.

pair.old: If the AWD warning light () stays on the instrument cluster, your vehicle may have a malfunction with the AWD system. When the AWD warning light () appears, have your vehicle checked by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the AWD warning light () remains illuminated on the instrument cluster, your vehicle may have an AWD system malfunction. When the AWD warning light () appears, have your vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: To reduce the risk of SERIOUS INJURY or DEATH:
pair.new: To reduce the risk of serious injury or death:

pair.old: Do not drive in conditions that exceed the vehicle’s intended design such as challenging off-road conditions.
pair.new: Do not drive in conditions that exceed your vehicle’s intended design, such as challenging off road conditions.

pair.old: AWD helps the vehicle to maintain its best driving performance by controlling the 4 wheels, the engine, the transmission and vehicle braking based on current road conditions such as snow, mud, sand, etc.
pair.new: AWD helps the vehicle maintain its best driving performance by controlling all 4 wheels, the engine, the transmission, and vehicle braking based on current road conditions (such as snow, mud, sand, and so on).

pair.old: Enhanced safety when driving straight.
pair.new: Enhances safety when driving straight.

pair.old: Improved performance when cornering.
pair.new: Improves performance when turning.

pair.old: Ensures operability in tough driving conditions such as snow, rain, sand, etc.
pair.new: Ensures operability in tough driving conditions, such as snow, rain, sand, and so on.

pair.old: Switching from/to SNOW MODE
pair.new: Switching into or out of SNOW MODE

pair.old: You can switch from DRIVE MODE to SNOW mode by turning the knob. If you turn the DRIVE/SNOW mode button again, your Kia will return to DRIVE MODE.
pair.new: To switch from DRIVE mode to SNOW mode, turn the DRIVE/SNOW mode knob. If you turn the knob again, your vehicle returns to DRIVE MODE.

pair.old: For safe All Wheel Drive (AWD) operation
pair.new: Safe All Wheel Drive (AWD) operation

pair.old: The on-road or off-road conditions that dictate All Wheel Drive use expose your vehicle to extreme stress compared to normal road conditions. Slow down and be ready for changes in the composition and traction of the surface under your tires. If you have any doubt about the safety of the conditions you are driving on, stop and consider the best way to proceed.
pair.new: Compared to normal road conditions, on road or off road conditions that necessitate using AWD expose your vehicle to extreme stress. When using AWD, slow down and be ready for changes in the composition and traction of the road surface. If you have any doubt about the safety of road conditions, stop driving and consider the best way to proceed.

pair.old: Do not drive in deep standing water or mud since such conditions can stall your engine and clog your exhaust pipes. Do not drive down steep hills since it requires extreme skill to maintain control of your Kia.
pair.new: Do not drive in deep standing water or mud, which can stall your engine and clog your exhaust pipes. Do not drive down steep hills that require extreme skill to maintain control of the vehicle.

pair.old: When you are driving up or down hills drive as straight as possible. Use extreme caution in going up or down steep hills, since your vehicle may roll over depending on the grade, terrain, and water/mud conditions.
pair.new: When you drive up or down a hill, drive as straight as possible. When you drive up or down a steep hill, be extremely cautious of vehicle rollover caused by the grade, terrain, water, or mud.

pair.old: Traversing steep hills can be extremely dangerous. This danger can come from slight changes in the wheel angle which can destabilize your Kia. Even if your Kia is maintains stability under power, it can become unstable if your Kia stops its forward motion. Your vehicle may roll over without warning and without time for you to correct a mistake that could cause serious injury or death.
pair.new: Traversing steep hills can be extremely dangerous due to slight changes in the wheel angle that can destabilize your vehicle. Your vehicle can become unstable if it stops its forward motion (even if your vehicle maintains stability under power). Your vehicle may roll over without warning or enough time for you to correct a mistake that could cause serious injury or death.

pair.old: You must learn how to corner in a AWD vehicle. Do not rely on your experience in conventional Front Wheel Drive (FWD) vehicles when cornering your Kia in AWD mode. You must drive slower in AWD.
pair.new: You must learn how to turn in an AWD vehicle. Do not rely on your experience with conventional Front Wheel Drive (FWD) vehicles when you turn your vehicle in AWD mode. You must drive more slowly when using AWD.

pair.old: Reduce speed when you turn corners. The center of gravity of AWD vehicles is higher than that of conventional FWD vehicles, making them more likely to roll over when you turn corners too fast.
pair.new: Reduce your speed when turning. AWD vehicles have a higher center of gravity than conventional FWD vehicles, and are more likely to roll over when you turn too quickly.

pair.old: Do not grab the inside of the steering wheel when you are driving on unpaved roads. You may hurt your arm by a sudden steering maneuver or from steering wheel rebound due to impact with objects on the ground. You could lose control of the steering wheel.
pair.new: When you are drive on an unpaved road, do not grab the inside of the steering wheel. If you do so, a sudden steering maneuver or steering wheel rebound caused by impact with objects on the ground may hurt your arm, and you could lose control of the steering wheel.

pair.old: Always hold the steering wheel firmly when you are driving on unpaved roads.
pair.new: Always hold the steering wheel firmly when you drive on unpaved roads.

pair.old: If you are driving in heavy wind, your Kia’s higher center of gravity decreases your steering control capacity and requires you to drive more slowly.
pair.new: If you drive in strong wind, your vehicle’s higher center of gravity lessens steering control, so you must drive more slowly.

pair.old: Drive slowly. If you are driving too fast in water, the water can get into the engine compartment and wet the ignition system, causing your vehicle to suddenly stop. If this happens and your vehicle is in a tilted position, your vehicle may roll over.
pair.new: When you drive through water, drive slowly. If you drive through water too fast, the water can enter the engine compartment, get the ignition system wet, and cause your vehicle to suddenly stop. If this happens when your vehicle is in a tilted position, your vehicle may roll over.

pair.old: Do not drive in water if the level is higher than the bottom of your Kia.
pair.new: Do not drive in water higher than the bottom of your vehicle.

pair.old: Check your brake condition once you are out of mud or water. Press the brake pedal several times as you move slowly until you feel normal braking forces return.
pair.new: Check the condition of your brakes after you drive through mud or water. To do so, engage the brake pedal several times (as you drive slowly) until you feel normal braking forces return.

pair.old: Shorten your scheduled maintenance interval if you drive in offroad conditions such as sand, mud or water (refer to ). Always wash your vehicle thoroughly after off-road use, especially the underside of your Kia.
pair.new: If you drive in off road conditions such as sand, mud, or water, shorten your scheduled maintenance interval (refer to ). Always wash your vehicle thoroughly, especially the underbody, after driving off road.

pair.old: Since the driving torque is always applied to the 4 wheels, the performance of the AWD vehicle is greatly affected by the condition of the tires. Be sure to equip your Kia with four tires of the same size and type.
pair.new: The condition of the tires greatly affects AWD vehicle performance because driving torque is always applied to all 4 wheels. Be sure to equip your vehicle with 4 tires of the same size and type.

pair.old: A full time AWD vehicle cannot be towed by an ordinary tow truck. Make sure that your Kia is placed on a flatbed truck for moving.
pair.new: An AWD vehicle cannot be towed by an ordinary tow truck. If your vehicle must be towed, make sure a flatbed truck is used.

pair.old: Avoid high cornering speed.
pair.new: Avoid high speeds when turning.

pair.old: The risk of rollover is greatly increased if you lose control of your vehicle at high speed.
pair.new: The risk of a rollover greatly increases if you lose control of your vehicle at highway speeds.

pair.old: In a collision, an unbelted person is significantly more likely to die compared to a person wearing a seat belt.
pair.new: In a collision, an unbelted person is significantly more likely to die than a person wearing a seat belt.

pair.old: Loss of control often occurs if two or more wheels drop off the roadway, and the driver oversteers to re-enter the roadway. If your vehicle leaves the roadway, do not steer sharply. Instead, slow down before pulling back into the travel lanes.
pair.new: Loss of control often occurs when 2 or more wheels leave the road and the driver overcorrects to reenter the road. If your vehicle leaves the road, do not steer sharply. Instead, slow down before you reenter the road.

pair.old: Mud or Snow
pair.new: Mud or snow

pair.old: If one of the front or rear wheels begins to spin in mud, snow, etc., your Kia can sometimes be driven out by engaging the accelerator pedal further; however, avoid revving the engine continuously at high rpm because doing so could damage the AWD system.
pair.new: If a front or rear wheel begins to spin in mud or snow, you can sometimes drive your vehicle out by further engaging the accelerator pedal. However, avoid continuously revving the engine at a high RPM, which could damage your vehicle’s AWD system.

pair.old: Maintain slow and constant speed. Depress the accelerator pedal slowly to ensure safe driving (wheel-slip prevention).
pair.new: Maintain slow, constant speed and engage the accelerator pedal slowly to ensure safe driving and prevent the wheels from slipping.

pair.old: Keep sufficient distance between your vehicle and your Kia in front of you.
pair.new: Maintain sufficient following distance from the vehicle ahead.

pair.old: Reduce speed and always check the road condition.
pair.new: Reduce speed and always check road conditions.

pair.old: Avoid speeding, rapid acceleration, sudden brake application, and sharp turns to prevent getting stuck.
pair.new: To prevent getting stuck, avoid speeding, rapid acceleration, sudden braking, and sharp turns.

pair.old: When your Kia is stuck in snow, sand or mud, the tires may not operate. This is to protect the transmission and not a malfunction.
pair.new: Your vehicle’s wheels may not operate when stuck in snow, sand or mud. This is to protect the transmission and is not a malfunction.

pair.old: When driving on extremely muddy and sandy roads, the transmission can overheat.
pair.new: The transmission can overheat when you drive on extremely muddy or sandy roads.

pair.old: When the transmission overheated, the safe protection mode engages and the “Transmission Hot! Park with engine on" warning message will appear on the LCD display with a chime.
pair.new: When the transmission overheats, safe protection mode activates, and the “Transmission Hot! Park with engine on” warning message appears on the instrument cluster and an audible warning sounds.

pair.old: If you ignore this warning, the driving condition may worsen. You may experience abrupt shifts, frequent shifts, or jerkiness. To return to the normal driving condition, stop your Kia by applying the brake, and shift into P (Park). Allow the transmission to cool for a few minutes with engine on, before driving off.
pair.new: Driving conditions may worsen if you ignore this warning. You may experience abrupt shifts, frequent shifts, and jerkiness. To return to normal driving conditions, you should park your vehicle and allow the transmission to cool for a few minutes (with the engine ON) before driving again.

pair.old: Tire Precautions
pair.new: Tire precautions

pair.old: When driving in all-wheel drive, driving force is applied to all tires and the driving performance of your Kia is greatly affected by the degree of tire wear:
pair.new: AWD applies driving force to all 4 wheels, which means the condition of the tires greatly affects driving performance:

pair.old: When replacing tires, be sure to equip all four tires with the same size, type, tread, brand and load-carrying capacity. Do not use tires and wheels with different sizes and types from the one originally installed on your vehicle. It can affect the safety and performance of your vehicle which could lead to steering failure or rollover causing serious injury.
pair.new: When replacing tires, be sure all 4 tires are of the same size, type, tread, brand, and load-carrying capacity. Do not equip your vehicle with tires of a different size or type than what was originally installed on your vehicle. This can affect the safety and performance of your vehicle, which could lead to steering failure or rollover and cause serious injury.

pair.old: Each tire should be checked monthly when cold and inflated to inflation pressure recommended your Kia on your Kia placard or tire inflation pressure label.
pair.new: When it is cold outside, you should check each tire monthly and maintain the inflation pressure recommended on your Kia placard or tire inflation pressure label.

pair.old: An AWD vehicle should never be towed with the wheels on the ground. Your vehicle must be towed with a wheel lift and dollies or flatbed equipment with all the wheels off the ground.
pair.new: An AWD vehicle should never be towed with wheels on the ground. Your vehicle must be towed with a wheel lift and dollies or flatbed equipment so that none of the wheels are on the ground.

pair.old: Refer to .
pair.new: For more information, refer to .

pair.old: The AWD vehicle cannot be towed with sling-type equipment. Use wheel lift or flatbed equipment.
pair.new: An AWD vehicle cannot be towed with sling-type equipment. Use a wheel lift or flatbed equipment.

pair.old: Your vehicle is equipped with tires designed to provide safe ride and handling capability. Do not use tires and wheels that are different in size and type from the originally installed ones. It can affect the safety and performance of your vehicle, which could lead to steering failure or rollover and serious injury. When replacing the tires, be sure to equip all four tires with the tire and wheel of the same size, type, tread, brand and load-carrying capacity.
pair.new: Your vehicle is equipped with tires that make handling and riding in your vehicle safer. Do not use tires or wheels that are different in size and type from the original ones. This can affect the safety and performance of your vehicle, which could lead to steering failure or a rollover and result in serious injury. When replacing the tires, be sure that all 4 tires and wheels are the same size, type, tread, and brand and have the same load-carrying capacity.

pair.old: Full-time AWD vehicles must be tested on a special four-wheel chassis dynamometer.
pair.new: AWD vehicles must be tested with a special 4-wheel chassis dynamometer.

pair.old: Never engage the parking brake while performing these tests.
pair.new: Never apply the parking brake while performing these tests.

pair.old: A full-time AWD vehicle should not be tested on a FWD roll tester. If a FWD roll tester must be used, perform the following:
pair.new: An AWD vehicle should not be tested with a FWD roll tester. If a FWD roll tester must be used, follow these steps:

pair.old: Check the tire pressures recommended for your vehicle.
pair.new: Check the recommended tire pressure for your vehicle.

pair.old: Keep away from the front of your Kia while the vehicle is in gear on the dynamometer. This is very dangerous as the vehicle can jump forward and cause serious injury or death.
pair.new: Stay away from the front of your vehicle while it is in gear on the dynamometer, as the vehicle can jump forward and cause serious injury or death.

pair.old: When lifting up the vehicle, do not operate front and rear wheels separately. All four wheels should be operated.
pair.new: When lifting the vehicle, do not operate the front and rear wheels separately. You should operate all 4 wheels.

pair.old: If you need to operate the front wheel and rear wheel when lifting your Kia, you should release the parking brake.
pair.new: If you need to operate the front and rear wheels when lifting your vehicle, release the parking brake.

pair.old: Your vehicle has power-assisted brakes, parking brake, and various braking systems for safe driving.
pair.new: Your vehicle has power-assisted brakes, a parking brake, and various braking systems for safe driving.

pair.old: Power brakes
pair.new: Power-assisted brakes

pair.old: If the power-assisted brakes lose power because of a stalled engine or some other reason, you can still stop your vehicle by applying greater force to the brake pedal than you normally would. The stopping distance, however, will be longer.
pair.new: If the power-assisted brakes lose power because of a stalled engine or some other reason, you can still stop your vehicle by engaging the brake pedal with greater force than you normally would. However, the required stopping distance will be greater.

pair.old: When the engine is not running, the reserve brake power is partially depleted each time the brake pedal is applied. Do not pump the brake pedal when the power assist has been interrupted.
pair.new: When the engine is not running, the reserve brake power is partially depleted each time you engage the brake pedal. Do not engage the brake pedal when the power assist has been interrupted.

pair.old: Pump the brake pedal only when necessary to maintain steering control on slippery surfaces.
pair.new: Only engage the brake pedal when necessary to maintain steering control on slippery surfaces.

pair.old: When stepping on the brake pedal under a certain driving or weather condition, you may hear a sound or some other noises. This is not a brake malfunction.
pair.new: When you engage the brake pedal under certain driving or weather conditions, you may hear noise. This is not a brake malfunction.

pair.old: When driving on the road to which deicing chemicals are applied, vehicle noises may be heard from the brake or abnormal abrasion of tires. You should operate additional braking so that you can remove the deicing chemicals on the brake disk and pad under a safe traffic condition.
pair.new: When driving on a road where de-icing chemicals have been applied, you may hear noises from the brake or abnormal abrasion of tires. Engage the brake pedal under safe traffic conditions to remove the de-icing chemicals from the brake disk and pad.

pair.old: Do not drive with your foot resting on the brake pedal. This will create abnormally high brake temperatures which can cause excessive brake lining and pad wear.
pair.new: Do not drive with your foot resting on the brake pedal. This creates abnormally high brake temperatures, which can cause excessive wear to the brake lining and pad.

pair.old: Avoid continuous application of the brakes when descending a long or steep hill by shifting to a lower gear. Continuous brake application will cause the brakes to overheat and could result in a temporary loss of braking performance.
pair.new: To avoid continuous engagement of the brake pedal when descending a long or steep hill, shift the vehicle to a lower gear. Continuous engagement of the brakes causes them to overheat and could result in a temporary loss of braking performance.

pair.old: Wet brakes may impair your Kia’s ability to safely slow down; your Kia may pull to one side when the brakes are applied. Applying the brakes lightly will indicate whether they have been affected in this way. Always test your brakes in this fashion after driving through deep water. To dry the brakes, apply them lightly while maintaining a safe forward speed until brake performance returns to normal.
pair.new: Wet brakes may impair your vehicle’s ability to slow down safely; your vehicle may drift to one side when you engage the brake pedal. Lightly engaging the brake pedal indicates whether the brakes have been affected in this way. Always test your brakes in this fashion after driving through deep water. To dry the brakes, engage them lightly while maintaining a safe forward speed until brake performance returns to normal.

pair.old: If service brakes fail to operate while your Kia is in motion, you can make an emergency stop with the parking brake. The stopping distance, however, will be much greater than normal.
pair.new: If the service brakes fail to operate while your vehicle is in motion, you can make an emergency stop by applying the parking brake. However, the required stopping distance will be much greater than normal.

pair.old: Avoid applying the parking brake to stop your Kia while it is moving except in an emergency. Applying the parking brake while your Kia is moving at normal speeds can cause a sudden loss of control of your Kia. If you must use the parking brake to stop your Kia, use great caution.
pair.new: Avoid applying the parking brake to stop your vehicle while it is moving except in an emergency. Applying the parking brake while your vehicle is moving at normal speeds can cause a sudden loss of control of your vehicle. If you must use the parking brake to stop your vehicle, use great caution.

pair.old: In the event the accelerator pedal becomes stuck or entrapped, apply steady and firm pressure to the brake pedal to slow your Kia and reduce engine power.
pair.new: If the accelerator pedal is stuck or entrapped, engage the brake pedal with steady and firm pressure to slow down your vehicle and reduce engine power.

pair.old: If you experience this condition, take the following steps:
pair.new: If this happens, follow these steps:

pair.old: Apply the brakes and bring your vehicle to a safe stop.
pair.new: Engage the brake pedal and bring your vehicle to a safe stop.

pair.old: Move the transmission to P (Park), switch the engine off and apply the parking brake.
pair.new: Shift the gear to P (Park), turn OFF the engine, and apply the parking brake.

pair.old: If none are found and the condition persists, have your vehicle towed to an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If you do not find any interference and the accelerator pedal is still stuck, have your vehicle towed to an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: When your brake pads are worn and new pads are required, you will hear a high-pitched warning sound from your front or rear brakes. You may hear this sound come and go or it may occur whenever you press the brake pedal.
pair.new: When your brake pads are worn and new pads are required, you will hear a high-pitched warning sound from your front or rear brakes. You may hear this sound come and go, or it may occur whenever you engage the brake pedal.

pair.old: Do not continue to drive with worn brake pads. Continuing to drive with worn brake pads can damage the braking system and result in costly brake repairs.
pair.new: Do not continue to drive your vehicle with worn brake pads. Driving with worn brake pads can damage the braking system and result in costly brake repairs.

pair.old: Do not ignore high pitched wear sounds from your brakes. If you ignore this audible warning, you will eventually lose braking performance, which could lead to a serious accident.
pair.new: Do not ignore high pitched sounds from your brakes indicating brake wear. If you ignore this sound, you will eventually lose braking performance, which could lead to a serious accident.

pair.old: Brake dust may accumulate on the wheels, even under normal driving conditions. Some dust is inevitable as the brakes wear and contributes to brake noise.
pair.new: Brake dust may accumulate on the wheels, even under normal driving conditions. Some dust is inevitable as the brakes wear, which contributes to noise from the brakes.

pair.old: Always set the parking brake before leaving your Kia.
pair.new: Always apply the parking brake before leaving your vehicle.

pair.old: Firmly depress the brake pedal.
pair.new: Firmly engage the brake pedal.
Firmly engage the brake pedal.

pair.old: Depress the parking brake pedal down as far as possible.
pair.new: Apply the parking brake pedal by pressing down as far as possible.

pair.old: To reduce the risk of SERIOUS INJURY or DEATH, do not operate the parking brake while your Kia is moving except in an emergency. It could damage the brake system and lead to an accident.
pair.new: To reduce the risk of serious injury or death, do not apply the parking brake while your vehicle is moving except in an emergency. Doing so could damage the brake system and cause an accident.

old.old: To release the parking brake:

new.new: To release the parking brake:

pair.old: Depress the parking brake pedal and it will release automatically.
pair.new: Press the parking brake pedal. It will release automatically.

pair.old: If the parking brake does not release or does not release all the way, have your vehicle checked by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the parking brake does not release or only partially releases, have your vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Whenever leaving your Kia or parking, always come to a complete stop and continue to depress the brake pedal. Make sure the gear is shifted to P (Park) position, apply the parking brake, and place the ENGINE START/STOP button in the LOCK/OFF position.
pair.new: When parking or leaving your vehicle, always come to a complete stop and continue to engage the brake pedal. Make sure to shift the gear to P (Park), apply the parking brake, and press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the LOCK/OFF position.

pair.old: Vehicles with the parking brake not fully engaged are at risk for moving inadvertently and causing injury to yourself or others.
pair.new: Vehicles without the parking brake fully applied are at risk for moving inadvertently and causing injury to yourself or others.

pair.old: NEVER allow anyone who is unfamiliar with your Kia to touch the parking brake. If the parking brake is released unintentionally, serious injury may occur.
pair.new: NEVER allow a passenger or anyone who is unfamiliar with your vehicle to touch the parking brake. If the parking brake is released unintentionally, serious injury may occur.

pair.old: Only release the parking brake when you are seated inside your Kia with your foot firmly on the brake pedal.
pair.new: Only release the parking brake when you are seated inside your vehicle with your foot firmly on the brake pedal.

pair.old: Do not apply the accelerator pedal while the parking brake is engaged. If you depress the accelerator pedal with the parking brake engaged, a warning will sound. Damage to the parking brake may occur.
pair.new: Do not engage the accelerator pedal while the parking brake is applied. If you do so, a warning will sound. Damage to the parking brake may occur.

pair.old: Driving with the parking brake on can overheat the braking system and cause premature wear or damage to brake parts. Make sure the parking brake is released and the Brake Warning Light is off before driving.
pair.new: Driving with the parking brake applied can overheat the braking system and cause premature wear or damage to the brake parts. Make sure the parking brake is released and the Brake Warning Light is OFF before driving.

pair.old: Check the brake warning light by turning the ENGINE START/STOP button to ON (do not start the engine). This light will appear when the parking brake is applied with the ENGINE START/STOP button in the START or ON position.
pair.new: Check the Brake Warning Light by pressing the ENGINE START/STOP button in the START or ON position (do not press the button to start the engine). The Brake Warning Light illuminates when you apply the parking brake while the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the START or ON position.

pair.old: Before driving, be sure the parking brake is fully released, and the brake warning light is off.
pair.new: Before driving, be sure the parking brake is fully released and the Brake Warning Light is OFF.

pair.old: If the brake warning light remains on after the parking brake is released while engine is running, there may be a malfunction in the brake system. Immediate attention is necessary.
pair.new: If the Brake Warning Light remains ON after you release the parking brake while the engine is running, there may be a brake system malfunction that requires immediate attention.

pair.old: If possible, cease driving your Kia immediately. If that is not possible, use extreme caution while operating your Kia and only continue to drive your Kia until you can reach a safe location or repair shop.
pair.new: If possible, stop driving your vehicle immediately. If that is not possible, use extreme caution while operating your vehicle, and only continue to drive it until you can reach a safe location or repair shop.

pair.old: After parking your Kia, apply the EPB to prevent your Kia from being moved by an external force.
pair.new: After parking your vehicle, apply the EPB to prevent any external force from moving your vehicle.

pair.old: Depress the brake pedal.
pair.new: Engage the brake pedal.

pair.old: Make sure the warning light comes on.
pair.new: Make sure the warning light illuminates.

pair.old: The EPB is applied automatically if the Auto Hold button is on when your Kia is turned off. If you pull up the EPB switch after your Kia is turned off, the EPB will be applied.
pair.new: If the Auto Hold button is ON when you turn OFF your vehicle, the EPB is applied automatically. If you pull up the EPB switch after you turn OFF your vehicle, the EPB is applied.

pair.old: Never leave children or animals unattended in your Kia. If you leave children unaccompanied in the vehicle, they may be able to set your Kia in motion by, for example:
pair.new: Never leave children or animals unattended in your vehicle. They may be able to set your vehicle in motion by:

pair.old: Shifting the transmission out of P (Park) position.
pair.new: Shifting the gear out of P (Park).

pair.old: On a steep incline or when pulling a trailer, if your Kia does not remain at a standstill, do as follows:
pair.new: If your vehicle does not remain at a standstill when you are on a steep incline or pulling a trailer, perform the following actions:

pair.old: Do not operate the EPB while your Kia is moving except in an emergency.
pair.new: Do not apply the EPB while your vehicle is moving except in an emergency.

pair.old: Shift to P (Park) on a slope
pair.new: You shift the gear to P (Park) while on a slope.

pair.old: Engine OFF while AUTO HOLD is enabled
pair.new: You turn the engine OFF while AUTO HOLD is enabled.

pair.old: When your Kia moves a bit in P (Park) position
pair.new: Your vehicle moves a bit while the gear is in P (Park).

pair.old: Conditions while AUTO HOLD is activated
pair.new: While AUTO HOLD is activated:

pair.old: Driver’s door is opened
pair.new: You open the driver side door.

pair.old: Hood is opened
pair.new: You open the hood.

pair.old: Liftgate is opened
pair.new: You open the liftgate.

pair.old: Vehicle stops for more than approximately 10 minutes on a steep slope
pair.new: You stop the vehicle for more than approximately 10 minutes while on a steep slope.

pair.old: Requested by other systems
pair.new: Other systems request application of the EPB.

pair.old: For EPB equipped vehicles with AUTO HOLD function used while driving, if the ENGINE START/STOP button has been turned OFF, the EPB will be engaged automatically. Therefore, the AUTO HOLD function should be turned off before the ENGINE START/STOP button is turned off.
pair.new: For EPB-equipped vehicles using the AUTO HOLD function while driving, the EPB is engaged automatically if you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the OFF position. Turn OFF the AUTO HOLD function before pressing the ENGINE START/STOP button in the OFF position.

pair.old: If there is a problem with the brake pedal while driving, emergency braking is possible by pulling up and holding the EPB switch.
pair.new: If there is a problem with the brake pedal while driving, pull up and hold the EPB switch for emergency braking.

pair.old: Braking is possible only while you are holding the EPB switch.
pair.new: Emergency braking is possible only while you are holding the EPB switch.

pair.old: Do not operate the EPB while your Kia is moving except in an emergency. Applying the EPB while your Kia is moving at normal speeds can cause a sudden loss of control of your Kia. If you must use the EPB to stop your Kia, use great caution in applying the brake.
pair.new: Do not apply the EPB while your vehicle is moving except in an emergency. Applying the EPB while your vehicle is moving at normal speeds can cause a sudden loss of control of your vehicle. If you must use the EPB to stop your vehicle, use great caution in applying the brake.

pair.old: During emergency braking by the EPB, the parking brake warning light will appear to indicate that the system is operating.
pair.new: During emergency braking by the EPB, the parking brake Warning Light indicates that the system is operating.

pair.old: If you notice a continuous noise or burning smell when the EPB is used for emergency braking, have your vehicle checked by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If you notice a continuous noise or burning smell when using the EPB for emergency braking, have your vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

old.old: Releasing the parking brake with EPB switch,
To release the parking brake with the EPB switch:

new.new: To release the parking brake with the EPB switch:

pair.old: Have the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position.
pair.new: Press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position.

pair.old: Press the brake pedal.
pair.new: Engage the brake pedal.

pair.old: Make sure the brake warning light goes off.
pair.new: Make sure the Brake Warning Light turns OFF.

pair.old: The EPB will display a warning message with sound under certain conditions:
pair.new: An EPB warning appears and an audible warning sounds in the following conditions:

pair.old: If you try to drive while engaging the accelerator pedal with the EPB applied, but the EPB doesn’t release automatically, a warning will sound and a message will appear.
pair.new: You try to drive by engaging the accelerator pedal with the EPB applied, but the EPB doesn’t release automatically.

pair.old: If the driver’s seat belt is not fastened and your Kia hood, driver’s door or liftgate is opened, a warning will sound and a message will appear.
pair.new: You open the driver side door, the hood, or the liftgate while the driver side seatbelt is unfastened.

pair.old: If there is a problem with your Kia, a warning may sound and a message may appear.
pair.new: If there is a problem with your vehicle, it may display a warning message with sound.

pair.old: Press the brake pedal and release EPB by pressing the EPB switch.
pair.new: Engage the brake pedal and release the EPB using the EPB switch.

pair.old: Never allow a passenger to touch the parking brake. If the parking brake is released unintentionally, serious injury may occur.
pair.new: Never allow a passenger or anyone who is unfamiliar with your vehicle to touch the EPB switch. If the parking brake is released unintentionally, serious injury may occur.

pair.old: All vehicles should always have the parking brake fully engaged when parked to avoid inadvertent movement of your Kia which can injure occupants or pedestrians.
pair.new: Always fully engage the parking brake when parked to avoid inadvertent movement of your vehicle, which can injure occupants or pedestrians.

pair.old: A click or electric brake motor whine sound may be heard while operating or releasing the EPB. This is normal and indicates that the EPB is functioning properly.
pair.new: You may hear a click or electric brake motor whine sound while engaging or releasing the EPB. This is normal and indicates that the EPB is functioning properly.

pair.old: When leaving your keys with a parking lot attendant or valet, make sure to inform him/her how to operate the EPB.
pair.new: When leaving your keys with a parking lot attendant or valet, inform them how to apply the EPB.

pair.old: The EPB may malfunction if you drive with the EPB applied.
pair.new: The EPB may malfunction if you drive with it applied.

pair.old: When you automatically release EPB by pressing the accelerator pedal, press it slowly.
pair.new: When you automatically release the EPB by engaging the accelerator pedal, engage it slowly.

pair.old: When the conversion from Auto Hold to EPB is not working properly a warning will sound and a message will appear.
pair.new: When the conversion from Auto Hold to EPB is not working properly, the system displays the following warning message with sound:

pair.old: Engage the brake pedal when the above message appears for the Auto Hold and EPB may not activate.
pair.new: When this warning message appears, engage the brake pedal to deactivate Auto Hold.

pair.old: If the EPB is applied while Auto Hold is activated because of an ESC signal, a warning will sound and a message will appear.
pair.new: If you apply the EPB while Auto Hold is activated because of an ESC signal, the system displays the following warning message with sound:

pair.old: This warning light appears if the ENGINE START/STOP button is changed to the ON position and goes off in approximately 3 seconds if the system is operating normally.
pair.new: If you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position, and the engine turns OFF after approximately 3 seconds while the system is operating normally, the following warning light appears:

pair.old: The EPB malfunction indicator may appear when the ESC indicator comes on to indicate that the ESC is not working properly, but it does not indicate a malfunction of the EPB.
pair.new: When the ESC indicator appears because the ESC is not working properly, the EPB malfunction indicator may also appear. This does not indicate a malfunction of the EPB.

pair.old: The EPB warning light may appear if the EPB switch operates abnormally. Shut the engine off and turn it on again after a few minutes. The warning light will turn off and the EPB switch will operate normally. If the EPB warning light remains on, have the system checked by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: The EPB warning light may appear if the EPB switch operates abnormally. Turn the engine OFF, then turn it back ON after a few minutes. The warning light disappears, and the EPB switch operates normally. If the EPB warning light remains ON, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: If the EPB warning light does not appear or blinks even though the EPB switch was pulled up, the EPB is not applied.
pair.new: If the EPB warning light blinks or does not appear, even when you pull up the EPB switch, this indicates that the EPB has not been applied.

pair.old: If the EPB warning light blinks when the EPB warning light is on, press the EPB switch, then pull it up. Press it back to its original position and pull it back up. If the EPB warning does not turn off, have the system checked by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the EPB warning light blinks, press and then pull up the EPB switch. Press it back into its original position and pull it back up. If the EPB warning light does not turn OFF, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: If the EPB does not release normally, take your vehicle to an authorized Kia dealer by loading the vehicle on a flatbed tow truck and have the system checked.
pair.new: If the EPB does not release normally, load your vehicle onto a flatbed tow truck and take it to an authorized Kia dealer to have the system inspected.
If the EPB does not release normally, load your vehicle onto a flatbed tow truck and take it to an authorized Kia dealer to have the system inspected.

pair.old: AUTO HOLD
pair.new: Auto Hold

pair.old: The Auto Hold is designed to maintain your Kia in a standstill even though the brake pedal is not pressed after you bring your Kia to a complete stop by pressing the brake pedal.
pair.new: The Auto Hold function keeps your vehicle still, even if you do not continue to hold your foot on the brake pedal after you have engaged it, to bring your vehicle to a complete stop.

pair.old: Press the brake pedal and start your Kia.
pair.new: Engage the brake pedal and start your vehicle.

pair.old: Press the Auto Hold button. The white AUTO HOLD indicator will come on indicating the system is in standby.
pair.new: Press the Auto Hold button. The white AUTO HOLD indicator light appears, indicating that the system is in standby.

pair.old: Before the Auto Hold will engage, the driver’s door and engine hood must be closed.
pair.new: Before the Auto Hold function engages, you must close the driver side door and engine hood.

pair.old: When coming to a complete stop by pressing the brake pedal, the AUTO HOLD indicator changes from white to green indicating the AUTO HOLD is engaged. Your Kia will remain at a standstill even if you release the brake pedal.
pair.new: When you come to a complete stop by engaging the brake pedal, the AUTO HOLD indicator light changes from white to green, indicating that Auto Hold is engaged. Your vehicle remains at a standstill even if you release the brake pedal.

pair.old: If EPB is applied, Auto Hold will be released.
pair.new: If the EPB is applied, Auto Hold will be released.

pair.old: If you press the accelerator pedal with the gear in D (Drive) or Manual mode, the Auto Hold will be released automatically and your Kia will start to move. The indicator changes from green to white indicating the Auto Hold is in standby and the EPB is released.
pair.new: If you engage the accelerator pedal with the gear in D (Drive) or Manual mode, the Auto Hold function is released automatically, and your vehicle starts to move. The indicator light changes from green to white, indicating that the Auto Hold function is in standby and the EPB is released.

pair.old: When driving from Auto Hold by pressing the accelerator pedal, always check your surrounding.
pair.new: When driving after releasing Auto Hold by engaging the accelerator pedal, always check your surroundings.

pair.old: Slowly press the accelerator pedal for a smooth launch.
pair.new: Engage the accelerator pedal slowly for a smooth forward motion.

pair.old: Canceling Auto Hold function
pair.new: Canceling Auto Hold operation

pair.old: To cancel the Auto Hold operation, press the Auto Hold switch. The AUTO HOLD indicator will turn off.
pair.new: To cancel Auto Hold operation, press the AUTO HOLD switch. The AUTO HOLD indicator turns OFF.

pair.old: To cancel the Auto Hold operation when your Kia is at a standstill, press the Auto Hold switch while pressing the brake pedal.
pair.new: To cancel Auto Hold operation when your vehicle is at a standstill, press the AUTO HOLD switch while engaging the brake pedal.

pair.old: The following are conditions when the Auto Hold will not engage (Auto Hold light will not turn green and the Auto Hold system remains in standby):
pair.new: Auto Hold does not engage under the following are conditions (the AUTO HOLD indicator light does not turn green, and the Auto Hold system remains in standby):

pair.old: The driver’s door is opened
pair.new: You open the driver side door.
You open the driver side door.

pair.old: The engine hood is opened
pair.new: You open the engine hood.
You open the engine hood.

pair.old: The gear is in P (Park)
pair.new: You shift the gear to P (Park).

pair.old: The EPB is applied
pair.new: You apply the EPB.

pair.old: The Auto Hold automatically switches to EPB under any of the following conditions (Auto Hold light remains white and the EPB automatically applies):
pair.new: Auto Hold automatically switches to EPB under any of the following conditions (the AUTO HOLD indicator light remains white and the EPB applies automatically):

pair.old: Your Kia is in a standstill for more than 10 minutes.
pair.new: Your vehicle is at a standstill for more than 10 minutes.

pair.old: Your Kia is standing on a steep slope.
pair.new: Your vehicle is at a standstill on a steep slope.

pair.old: Your Kia moved for a few seconds.
pair.new: You moved your vehicle for a few seconds.

pair.old: In these cases, the brake warning light comes on, the AUTO HOLD indicator changes from green to white, and a warning sounds and a message will appear to inform you that EPB has been automatically engaged. Before driving again, press foot brake pedal, check your surrounding and release parking brake manually with the EPB switch.
pair.new: In these cases, the Brake Warning Light appears, the AUTO HOLD indicator light changes from green to white, a warning sounds, and a message appears to inform you that the EPB has been applied automatically. Before driving again, engage the brake pedal, check your surroundings, and use the EPB switch to manually release the parking brake.

pair.old: If the AUTO HOLD indicator lights up yellow, the Auto Hold is not working properly. Take your vehicle and have the system checked by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the AUTO HOLD indicator light turns yellow, the Auto Hold function is not working properly. Have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: A click or electric brake motor whine sound may be heard while operating or releasing the EPB, but these conditions are normal and indicate that the EPB is functioning properly.
pair.new: You may hear a click or electric brake motor whine sound while engaging or releasing the EPB. This is normal and indicates that the EPB is functioning properly.

pair.old: The Auto Hold function will display a warning message with sound under certain conditions.
pair.new: The Auto Hold function displays a warning message with sound under the following conditions:

pair.old: When the EPB is applied from Auto Hold.
pair.new: When you apply the EPB from Auto Hold.

pair.old: When the conversion from Auto Hold to EPB is not working properly.
pair.new: When the conversion from Auto Hold to the EPB does not work properly.

pair.old: When this message is displayed, the Auto Hold and EPB may not operate. For your safety, press the brake pedal.
pair.new: When this message appears, the Auto Hold function and the EPB may not operate. For your safety, engage the brake pedal.
