pair.old: If you do not apply the brake pedal when you release the Auto Hold by pressing the [AUTO HOLD] switch, a warning will sound and a message will appear.
pair.new: If you do not engage the brake pedal when you release the Auto Hold function by pressing the AUTO HOLD switch, a warning sounds and a message appears.

pair.old: When you press the [AUTO HOLD] switch, if the driver’s door and engine hood are open, a warning will sound and a message will appear.
pair.new: When you press the AUTO HOLD switch while the driver side door and engine hood are open, a warning sounds and a message appears.

pair.old: Press the AUTO HOLD button after closing the driver’s door and engine hood.
pair.new: Press the AUTO HOLD switch after closing the driver side door and engine hood.

pair.old: ABS prevents the wheels from locking so your Kia remains stable and can still be steered.
pair.new: The ABS prevents the wheels from locking so that your vehicle remains stable and you can still steer it.

pair.old: ABS (or ESC) will not prevent accidents due to improper or dangerous driving maneuvers. Even though vehicle control is improved during emergency braking, always maintain a safe distance between you and objects ahead. Vehicle speeds should always be reduced during extreme road conditions. Your Kia should be driven at reduced speeds in the following circumstances:
pair.new: The ABS (or ESC) does not prevent accidents due to improper or dangerous driving maneuvers. Even though emergency braking improves vehicle control, always maintain a safe distance between your vehicle and objects ahead. Always reduce the vehicle speed during extreme road conditions or under the following circumstances:

pair.old: When driving on rough, gravel or snow-covered roads
pair.new: When driving on rough, gravel, or snow-covered roads

pair.old: When driving on roads where the road surface is pitted or has different surface heights.
pair.new: When driving on roads where the surface is pitted or has different heights

pair.old: Driving in these conditions increases the stopping distance for your vehicle.
pair.new: Driving in these conditions increases the required stopping distance for your vehicle.

pair.old: The ABS continuously senses the speed of the wheels. If the wheels are going to lock, the ABS repeatedly modulates the hydraulic brake pressure to the wheels.
pair.new: The ABS continuously senses the speed of the wheels. If the wheels are about to lock, the ABS repeatedly modulates the hydraulic brake pressure to the wheels.

pair.old: When you apply your brakes under conditions which may lock the wheels, you may hear a “tik-tik” sound from the brakes or feel a corresponding sensation in the brake pedal. This is normal and it means your ABS is active.
pair.new: When you engage the brake pedal under conditions that may lock the wheels, you may hear a “tik-tik” sound from the brakes or feel a corresponding sensation in the brake pedal. This is normal and means your ABS is active.

When you engage the brake pedal under conditions that may lock the wheels, you may hear a “tik-tik” sound from the brakes or feel a corresponding sensation in the brake pedal. This is normal and means your ABS is active.

pair.old: To obtain the maximum benefit from your ABS in an emergency, do not attempt to modulate your brake pressure and do not try to pump your brakes. Press your brake pedal as hard as possible to allow the ABS to control the force being delivered to the brakes.
pair.new: To obtain the maximum benefit from your ABS in an emergency, do not try to modulate your brake pressure or pump your brakes. Engage your brake pedal as hard as possible to allow the ABS to control the force being delivered to the brakes.

pair.old: A click sound may be heard in the engine compartment when your Kia begins to move after your Kia is started. These conditions are normal and indicate that the Anti-lock Brake System is functioning properly.
pair.new: You may hear a click sound when your vehicle begins to move after you start it. This is normal and indicates that the ABS is functioning properly.

pair.old: Even with the ABS, your vehicle still requires sufficient stopping distance. Always maintain a safe distance from a your Kia.
pair.new: Even with the ABS, your vehicle still requires sufficient stopping distance. Always maintain a safe distance from vehicles ahead.

pair.old: Always slow down when cornering. The ABS cannot prevent accidents resulting from excessive speeds.
pair.new: Always slow down when turning. The ABS cannot prevent accidents resulting from excessive speeds.

pair.old: On loose or uneven road surfaces, operation of the ABS may result in a longer stopping distance than for vehicles equipped with a conventional brake system.
pair.new: On loose or uneven road surfaces, operation of the ABS may result in a longer stopping distance than is required for vehicles equipped with a conventional brake system.

pair.old: The ABS warning light will stay on for approximately 3 seconds after the ENGINE START/STOP button is ON.
pair.new: The ABS warning light stays ON for approximately 3 seconds after you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position.

pair.old: The ABS will go through self-diagnosis and the light will turn off if everything is normal. If the light stays on, there may be a problem with the ABS. Contact and visit an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: The ABS goes through self-diagnosis, and the light turns OFF if everything is normal. If the light stays ON, there may be a problem with the ABS. Contact and visit an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: When you drive on a road having poor traction, such as an icy road, and have operated your brakes continuously, the ABS will be continuously active and the ABS warning light may appear. Pull your vehicle over to a safe place and stop your Kia.
pair.new: When you drive on a road with poor traction (such as an icy road), and you have engaged the brake pedal continuously, the ABS will be continuously active, and the ABS warning light may appear. Pull over to a safe place and stop your vehicle.

pair.old: Restart your Kia. If the ABS warning light turns off, then your ABS is normal. If it remains on you may have a problem with the ABS. Contact and visit an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Restart your vehicle. If the ABS warning light turns OFF, then your ABS is normal. If it remains ON, you may have a problem with the ABS. Contact and visit an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: When you jump start your vehicle because of a drained battery, your Kia may not run as smoothly and the ABS warning light may turn on at the same time. This happens because of low battery voltage. It does not mean the ABS has malfunctioned.
pair.new: When you jump start your vehicle because of a drained battery, your vehicle may not run as smoothly, and the ABS warning light may appear at the same time. This happens because of low battery voltage and does not indicate an ABS malfunction.

pair.old: Do not pump your brakes!
pair.new: Do not pump your brakes.

pair.old: Have the battery recharged before driving your Kia.
pair.new: Recharge the battery before driving your vehicle.

pair.old: The ESC is designed to stabilize your Kia during cornering maneuvers.
pair.new: The ESC system stabilizes your vehicle when turning.

pair.old: ESC applies the brakes on individual wheels and intervenes with your Kia management system to stabilize the vehicle.
pair.new: ESC engages the brakes on individual wheels and intervenes with your vehicle management system to stabilize your vehicle.

pair.old: ESC will not prevent accidents. Excessive speed in turns, abrupt maneuvers and hydroplaning on wet surfaces can still result in serious accidents.
pair.new: ESC does not prevent accidents. Excessive speed in turns, abrupt maneuvers, and hydroplaning on wet surfaces can still result in serious accidents.

pair.old: Only a safe and attentive driver can prevent accidents by avoiding maneuvers that cause your Kia to lose traction. Even with ESC installed, always follow all the normal precautions for driving - including driving at safe speeds for the conditions.
pair.new: Only a safe and attentive driver can prevent accidents by avoiding maneuvers that cause the vehicle to lose traction. Even with ESC installed, always follow all the normal precautions for driving, including driving at safe speeds for the road conditions.

pair.old: For maximum protection, always wear your seat belt. No system, no matter how advanced, can compensate for all driver error and/or driving conditions. Always drive responsibly.
pair.new: For maximum protection, always wear your seat belt. No system, no matter how advanced, can compensate for all driver errors or driving conditions. Always drive responsibly.
For maximum protection, always wear your seat belt. No system, no matter how advanced, can compensate for all driver errors or driving conditions. Always drive responsibly.

pair.old: The ESC is an electronic system designed to help the driver maintain vehicle control under adverse conditions. It is not a substitute for safe driving practices. Factors including speed, road conditions and driver steering input can all affect whether ESC will be effective in preventing a loss of control. It is still your responsibility to drive and corner at reasonable speeds and to leave a sufficient margin of safety.
pair.new: The ESC is an electronic system that helps you maintain control of your vehicle under adverse conditions. It is not a substitute for safe driving practices. Factors such as speed, road conditions, and driver steering input can all affect whether ESC is effective in preventing a loss of control. It is still your responsibility to drive and turn at reasonable speeds and to leave a sufficient margin of safety.

pair.old: A click sound may be heard in your Kia compartment when your Kia begins to move after your Kia is started. This is normal and is functioning properly.
pair.new: You may hear a click sound in your compartment when your vehicle begins to move after you start it. This is normal and indicates that the vehicle is functioning properly.

pair.old: ESC ON condition
pair.new: The ESC system operates under the following conditions:

pair.old: When the ENGINE START/STOP button is turned ON, ESC and ESC OFF indicator lights appear for approximately 3 seconds, then ESC is turned on.
pair.new: When you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position, ESC and ESC OFF indicator lights appear for approximately 3 seconds, and then the ESC system turns ON.

pair.old: Press the ESC OFF button for at least half a second after turning your Kia ON to turn ESC off. (ESC OFF indicator will appear). To turn the ESC on, press the ESC OFF button (ESC OFF indicator light will go off).
pair.new: After you turn your vehicle ON, press the ESC OFF button for at least half a second to turn the ESC system OFF (the ESC OFF indicator appears). To turn the ESC system ON, press the ESC OFF button again (the ESC OFF indicator light disappears).

pair.old: When starting your Kia, you may hear a slight ticking sound. This is the ESC performing an automatic system self-check and does not indicate a problem.
pair.new: When starting your vehicle, you may hear a slight ticking sound. This is the ESC performing an automatic system self-check and does not indicate a problem.

pair.old: When the ESC is in operation, the ESC indicator light blinks.
pair.new: When the ESC system is operating, the ESC indicator light blinks.

pair.old: When the ESC is operating properly, you can feel a slight pulsation in your Kia. This is normal.
pair.new: When the ESC is operating properly, you can feel a slight pulsation in your vehicle. This is normal.

pair.old: When moving out of the mud or driving on a slippery road, pressing the accelerator pedal may not cause your Kia rpm (revolutions per minute) to increase.
pair.new: When moving out of mud or driving on a slippery road, engaging the accelerator pedal may not increase your vehicle’s rpm.

pair.old: ESC operation off
pair.new: ESC operation OFF

pair.old: ESC off state 1
pair.new: ESC OFF state 1

pair.old: To turn off the traction control function and only operate the brake control function of the ESC, press the ESC OFF button (ESC OFF ) for less than 3 seconds and the ESC OFF indicator light (ESC OFF ) will appear.
pair.new: To turn OFF the traction control function and only operate the ESC’s brake control function, press the ESC OFF button (ESC OFF ) for less than 3 seconds. The ESC OFF indicator light (ESC OFF ) appears.

pair.old: ESC off state 2
pair.new: ESC OFF state 2

pair.old: To turn off the traction control function and the brake control function of the ESC, press the ESC OFF button (ESC OFF ) for more than 3 seconds. ESC OFF indicator light (ESC OFF ) will appear and ESC OFF warning chime will sound. The ESC function will not operate.
pair.new: To turn the ESC’s traction control and brake control functions OFF, press the ESC OFF button (ESC OFF ) for more than 3 seconds. The ESC OFF indicator light (ESC OFF ) appears and the ESC OFF warning chime sounds. ESC does not operate.

pair.old: When ENGINE START/STOP button is turned ON, the indicator light appears, then turn off if the ESC system is operating normally.
pair.new: When you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position, the ESC OFF indicator light appears and then disappears if the ESC system is operating normally.

pair.old: The ESC indicator light blinks whenever ESC is operating or appears when ESC fails to operate.
pair.new: The ESC indicator light blinks when ESC is operating, or appears when ESC fails to operate.

pair.old: The ESC OFF indicator light comes on when the ESC is turned off with the button.
pair.new: The ESC OFF indicator light appears when you use the button to turn the ESC system OFF.

pair.old: ESC should be turned on whenever possible.
pair.new: Keep the ESC system turned ON whenever possible.

pair.old: To turn ESC off while driving, press the ESC OFF button while driving on a flat road surface.
pair.new: To turn the ESC system OFF, press the ESC OFF button while driving on a flat road surface.

pair.old: Never press the ESC OFF button while ESC is active (ESC indicator light blinks).
pair.new: Never press the ESC OFF button while the ESC system is active (the ESC indicator light blinks).

pair.old: If ESC is turned off while ESC is active, your Kia may lose control.
pair.new: If you turn the ESC system OFF while it is active, your vehicle may lose control.

pair.old: When operating the vehicle on a dynamometer, ensure that the ESC is turned off (ESC OFF light appeared). If the ESC is left on, it may prevent the vehicle speed from increasing, and result in false diagnosis.
pair.new: When operating the vehicle on a dynamometer, ensure that the ESC system is turned OFF (the ESC OFF indicator light appears). If the ESC system is left ON, it may prevent the vehicle speed from increasing, resulting in a false diagnosis.

pair.old: Turning the ESC off does not affect ABS or brake system operation.
pair.new: Turning the ESC system OFF does not affect ABS or brake system operation.

pair.old: The DBC feature assists the driver to descend down a steep hill without having to depress the brake pedal.
pair.new: The DBC feature helps you descend down a steep hill without having to engage the brake pedal.

pair.old: The system automatically applies the brakes to maintain your Kia speed 2.5mph (4 km/h)–25 mph (40 km/h) and allows the driver to concentrate on steering your Kia downhill.
pair.new: The DBC system automatically engages the brakes to maintain your vehicle speed between 2.5mph (4 km/h) and 25 mph (40 km/h) and allows you to concentrate on steering your vehicle as you drive downhill.

pair.old: Always turn off the DBC on normal roads. The DBC might activate inadvertently from the standby mode when driving through speed bumps or making sharp curves.
pair.new: Always turn OFF the DBC system on normal roads. DBC might activate inadvertently from standby mode when you drive over speed bumps or make sharp turns.

pair.old: The DBC defaults to the OFF position whenever the ENGINE START/STOP button is placed in the ON position.
pair.new: The DBC system defaults to OFF when you press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position.

pair.old: Noise or vibration may occur from the brakes when the DBC is activated. The rear stop light comes on when DBC is activated.
pair.new: You may hear noise or feel vibration from the brakes when you activate the DBC system. The rear brake light turns ON when the DBC system is activated.

pair.old: Press the DBC button when vehicle speed is under 38 mph (60 km/h). The DBC system will turn ON and enter the standby mode.
pair.new: Press the DBC button when vehicle speed is below 38 mph (60 km/h). The DBC system turns ON in standby mode.

pair.old: The system does not turn ON if vehicle speed is over 38 mph (60 km/h).
pair.new: The system does not turn ON if the vehicle speed is above 38 mph (60 km/h).

pair.old: In the standby mode, It enters the operating mode when the following conditions are met.
pair.new: The DBC system moves from standby mode to operating mode under the following conditions:

pair.old: The road surface should be more than a certain angle of inclination
pair.new: The road surface is inclined above a certain angle.

pair.old: The accelerator pedal must not be depressed.
pair.new: The accelerator pedal is not engaged.

pair.old: Your Kia speed should be within 2.5 mph (4 km/h)–25 mph (40 km/h)
pair.new: Your vehicle speed is between 2.5 mph (4 km/h) and 25 mph (40 km/h) when moving forward.

pair.old: 1.5 mph (2.5 km/h)–5 mph (8 km/h) in case of backward movement
pair.new: Your vehicle speed is between 1.5 mph (2.5 km/h) and 5 mph (8 km/h) when moving backward.

pair.old: Within operating vehicle speed 2.5 mph (4 km/h)–25 mph (40 km/h), the driver can lower or raise your Kia speed by stepping on the brake pedal or accelerator pedal.
pair.new: When the vehicle speed is between 2.5 mph (4 km/h) and 25 mph (40 km/h), you can increase or decrease the speed by engaging the accelerator or brake pedal.

pair.old: In the activated mode, the DBC will temporarily deactivate under the following conditions:
pair.new: The DBC temporarily deactivates under the following conditions:

pair.old: The hill is not steep enough.
pair.new: The hill you are driving on is not steep enough.

pair.old: The accelerator pedal is depressed.
pair.new: The accelerator pedal is engaged.

pair.old: When your Kia speed is in the range of 25 mph (40 km/h)–38 mph (60 km/h)
pair.new: Your speed is between 25 mph (40 km/h) and 38 mph (60 km/h).

pair.old: If the above conditions are not met, the DBC will automatically activate again.
pair.new: If these conditions are not met, the DBC system automatically reactivates.

pair.old: The DBC will turn OFF under the following conditions:
pair.new: The DBC system turns OFF under the following conditions:

pair.old: The DBC button is pressed again.
pair.new: You press the DBC button again.

pair.old: When the accelerator pedal is depressed and your Kia speed exceeds 38 mph (60 km/h)
pair.new: You engage the accelerator pedal and your speed is above 38 mph (60 km/h).

pair.old: The DBC may not deactivate on steep inclines even though the brake or accelerator pedal is depressed.
pair.new: The DBC system may not deactivate on steep inclines, even when the brake or accelerator pedal is engaged.

pair.old: The DBC does not operate when:
pair.new: The DBC system does not operate when:

pair.old: If the DBC yellow indicator light appears, the system may have overheated or have malfunctioned. When the warning light appears even though the DBC system has cooled off, have your vehicle checked by an authorized Kia dealer as soon as possible.
pair.new: If the DBC indicator light turns yellow, the system may have overheated or malfunctioned. If the warning light appears even when the DBC system has cooled off, have your vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer as soon as possible.

pair.old: A vehicle has the tendency to roll back on a steep hill when it starts to go after stopping. The HAC prevents your Kia from rolling back by applying the brakes automatically for about 2 seconds.
pair.new: A vehicle has the tendency to roll backward when it starts moving after you have stopped it on a steep hill. The HAC system prevents your vehicle from rolling backward by engaging the brakes automatically for about 2 seconds.

pair.old: The brakes are released when the accelerator pedal is engaged or after about 2 seconds.
pair.new: The HAC system releases the brakes after about 2 seconds, or when you engage the accelerator pedal.

pair.old: The HAC is activated for about 2 seconds, so when your Kia is starting, always engage the accelerator pedal.
pair.new: The HAC system activates for about 2 seconds, so when you start your vehicle, always engage the accelerator pedal.

pair.old: Maintaining brake pressure on incline
pair.new: Maintaining brake pressure on an incline

pair.old: HAC does not replace the need to apply brakes while stopped on an incline. While stopped, make sure you maintain sufficient brake pressure to prevent your vehicle from rolling backward and causing an accident. Don’t release the brake pedal until you are ready to accelerate.
pair.new: HAC does not replace the need to engage the brake pedal when you are stopped on an incline. While stopped, make sure you maintain sufficient brake pressure to prevent your vehicle from rolling backward and causing an accident. Do not release the brake pedal until you are ready to accelerate.

pair.old: Your Kia VSM system provides further enhancements to vehicle stability and steering responses under the following conditions:
pair.new: The VSM system further enhances the vehicle stability and steering responses under the following conditions:

pair.old: when driving on a slippery road or
pair.new: When driving on a slippery road.

pair.old: when a change in the coefficient of friction between left and right wheels is detected.
pair.new: When the vehicle detects a change in the coefficient of friction between the left and right wheels.

pair.old: Tire/wheel size
pair.new: Tire and wheel size

pair.old: When replacing tires and wheels, make sure they are the same size as the original tires and wheels installed. Driving with varying tires or wheel sizes may diminish any supplemental safety benefits of the VSM system.
pair.new: When replacing tires and wheels, make sure they are the same size as the original tires and wheels. Driving with varying tire or wheel sizes may diminish any supplemental safety benefits of the VSM system.

pair.old: When the VSM is operating properly, you can feel a slight pulsation in your Kia and/or abnormal steering responses (EPS). This is normal.
pair.new: When the VSM system is operating properly, you may feel a slight pulsation in your vehicle or abnormal steering responses (EPS). This is normal.

pair.old: The VSM does not operate when:
pair.new: The VSM system does not operate under the following conditions:

pair.old: Driving on a banked road such as a gradient or incline
pair.new: You are driving on a banked road, such as a gradient or incline.

pair.old: Driving in reverse
pair.new: You are driving in reverse.

pair.old: ESC OFF indicator light () remains on the instrument cluster
pair.new: The ESC OFF indicator light () remains on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: EPS indicator light remains on the instrument cluster
pair.new: The EPS indicator light remains on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: VSM operation off
pair.new: VSM operation OFF

pair.old: If you press the ESC OFF button to turn off the ESC, the VSM will also cancel and the ESC OFF indicator light () appears.
pair.new: If you press the ESC OFF button to turn the ESC system OFF, the VSM system also cancels, and the ESC OFF indicator light () appears.

pair.old: To turn on the VSM, press the button again. The ESC OFF indicator light turns off.
pair.new: To turn the VSM system ON, press the ESC OFF button again. The ESC OFF indicator light turns OFF.

pair.old: VSM Management
pair.new: VSM management

pair.old: Drive carefully even though your vehicle has VSM. It can only assist you in maintaining control of your Kia under certain circumstances.
pair.new: Drive carefully even though your vehicle has VSM. It can only help you maintain control of your vehicle under certain circumstances.

pair.old: The VSM may deactivated even if you don’t cancel the VSM operation by pressing the ESC OFF button. It indicates that a malfunction has been detected somewhere in the Electric Power Steering system or VSM system. If the ESC indicator light () or EPS warning light remains on, take your vehicle and have the system checked by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: The VSM may deactivate even if you do not press the ESC OFF button to cancel VSM operation. This indicates that a malfunction has been detected somewhere in the EPS system or VSM system. If the ESC indicator light () or EPS warning light remains on, have your vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: The VSM is not a substitute for safe driving practices but a supplementary function only. It is the responsibility of the driver to always check the speed and the distance to a your Kia ahead. Always hold the steering wheel firmly while driving.
pair.new: The VSM is only a supplementary function, not a substitute for safe driving practices. It is your responsibility to always check the speed and distance from a vehicle ahead. Always hold the steering wheel firmly while driving.

pair.old: Your vehicle is designed to activate according to the driver’s intention, even with installed VSM. Always follow all the normal precautions for driving at safe speeds for the conditions – including driving in inclement weather on a slippery road.
pair.new: Your vehicle handles according to your intention, even with VSM installed. Always follow all the normal precautions for driving at safe speeds for the conditions, including driving in inclement weather on a slippery road.

pair.old: The TSA is operated as a vehicle stability control system. The TSA is designed to stabilize your Kia and trailer when the trailer sways or oscillates. There are various factors that make your Kia sway or oscillate.
pair.new: TSA operates as a vehicle stability control system. TSA stabilizes your vehicle and trailer when the trailer sways or oscillates due to various factors.

pair.old: Such incidents mostly happen at high speed, but there is also a risk of swaying when the trailer is affected by crosswinds, buffeting, or improper overloading.
pair.new: Swaying typically happens when you drive at a high speed, but there is also a risk of swaying when crosswinds, buffeting, or improper overloading affect the trailer.

pair.old: Factors of swaying include:
pair.new: The following factors can cause swaying:

pair.old: Sudden control of steering wheel
pair.new: Sudden movement or overcorrection of the steering wheel

pair.old: The TSA continuously analyzes the vehicle and trailer instability. When the TSA detects some sway, the brakes are applied automatically to stabilize your Kia. When the vehicle becomes stable, the TSA stops.
pair.new: TSA continuously analyzes the vehicle and trailer for instability. When TSA detects swaying, the system engages the brake automatically to stabilize your vehicle. When the vehicle becomes stable, TSA stops.

pair.old: The BAS provides additional pressure when the brake pedal is momentarily and strongly depressed in a situation where sudden braking is required while driving.
pair.new: The BAS provides additional pressure when you momentarily and strongly engage the brake pedal in situations where sudden braking is required.

pair.old: The BAS reduces the time for ABS control to activate, and consequently reduces the braking distance, by providing additional pressure up to the point of ABS intervention.
pair.new: The BAS reduces the time required for the ABS control to activate, and consequently reduces the braking distance, by providing additional pressure to the brakes up to the point of ABS intervention.

pair.old: When your Kia speed is more than 20 mph (30 km/h) and the ABS control is not activated.
pair.new: Your vehicle speed is above 20 mph (30 km/h) and the ABS control is not activated.

pair.old: When the brake pedal is fully depressed over a certain level.
pair.new: You have fully engaged the brake pedal over a certain level.

pair.old: When the friction of the road surface is above a certain level.
pair.new: The friction of the road surface is above a certain level.

pair.old: BAS operation off
pair.new: BAS operation OFF

pair.old: Your Kia speed is below 6 mph (10 km/h).
pair.new: Your speed is below 6 mph (10 km/h).

pair.old: The brake pedal is depressed over a certain road condition.
pair.new: You engage the brake pedal in certain road conditions.

pair.old: The system operates depending on driver’s driving habit, vehicle speed, the degree to which the brake pedal is depressed and the road surface condition.
pair.new: The system operates depending on your driving habits, the vehicle speed, the degree to which you engage the brake pedal, and the conditions of the road surface.

pair.old: Good braking practices help keep occupants safe and extend brake life.
pair.new: Good braking practices help keep occupants safe and extend the life of your brakes.

pair.old: Check to be sure the parking brake is not engaged, and the parking brake indicator light is off before driving.
pair.new: Check to be sure that the parking brake is not applied and the parking brake indicator light is OFF before driving.

pair.old: Driving through water may wet the brakes. They can also get wet when your Kia is washed. Wet brakes can be dangerous! Your vehicle will not stop as quickly if the brakes are wet. Wet brakes may cause your Kia to pull to one side.
pair.new: Driving through water may wet the brakes. The brakes can also get wet when you wash your vehicle. Wet brakes can be dangerous! Your vehicle does not stop as quickly when the brakes are wet. Wet brakes may cause your vehicle to drift to one side.

pair.old: To dry the brakes, apply the brakes lightly until the braking action returns to normal, taking care to your Kia always keep your Kia under control. If the braking action does not return to normal, stop as soon as it is safe to do so and visit an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: To dry the brakes, lightly engage the brake pedal until the braking action returns to normal, taking care to always keep your vehicle under control. If the braking action does not return to normal, stop as soon as it is safe to do so and visit an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Don’t coast down hills with your Kia out of gear. This is extremely hazardous. Keep your Kia. Always keep your Kia in gear, use the brakes to slow down, then shift to a lower gear so that vehicle braking will help you maintain a safe speed.
pair.new: Don’t coast down hills with your vehicle out of gear. This is extremely hazardous. Always keep your vehicle in gear, use the brakes to slow down, and then shift to a lower gear so that vehicle braking action helps you maintain a safe speed.

pair.old: Don’t “ride” the brake pedal. Resting your foot on the brake pedal while driving can be dangerous because the brakes might overheat and lose their effectiveness. It also increases the wear of the brake components.
pair.new: Don’t “ride” the brake pedal. Resting your foot on the brake pedal while driving is dangerous because it can cause the brakes to overheat and lose their effectiveness. It also increases wear on the brake components.

pair.old: If a tire goes flat while you are driving, apply the brakes gently and keep your Kia pointed straight ahead while you slow down. When you are moving slowly enough for it to be safe to do so, pull off the road and stop in a safe place.
pair.new: If a tire goes flat while you are driving, gently engage the brake pedal and keep your vehicle pointed straight ahead while you slow down. Pull off the road and stop in a safe place once you are moving slowly enough to do so.

pair.old: Be cautious when parking on a hill. Firmly engage the parking brake and place the shift lever in P. If your vehicle is facing downhill, turn the drive wheels into the curb to help keep your Kia from rolling.
pair.new: Be cautious when parking on a hill. Firmly apply the parking brake shift the gear to P (Park). If your vehicle is facing downhill, turn the drive wheels toward the curb to help keep your vehicle from rolling.

pair.old: If your vehicle is facing uphill, turn the drive wheels away from the curb to help keep your Kia from rolling. If there is no curb or if it is required by other conditions to keep your Kia from rolling, block the wheels.
pair.new: If your vehicle is facing uphill, turn the drive wheels away from the curb to help keep your vehicle from rolling. If there is no curb and other conditions require you to keep your vehicle from rolling, block the wheels.

pair.old: Under some conditions your parking brake can freeze in the engaged position. This is most likely when there is an accumulation of snow or ice around or near the rear brakes or if the brakes are wet. If there is a risk that the parking brake may freeze, apply it only temporarily while you put the shift lever in P (Parking) and block the rear wheels so your Kia cannot roll. Then release the parking brake.
pair.new: Under some conditions, your parking brake can freeze while applied. This is most likely to occur when the brakes are wet, or when there is an accumulation of snow or ice near the rear brakes. If there is a risk that the parking brake may freeze, only apply it temporarily while you shift the gear to P (Park). Block the rear wheels so your vehicle cannot roll. Then release the parking brake.

pair.old: Do not hold your Kia on an incline with the accelerator pedal. This can cause the transmission to overheat. Always use the brake pedal or parking brake.
pair.new: Do not use the accelerator pedal to hold your vehicle on an incline. This can cause the transmission to overheat. Always use the brake pedal or parking brake.

pair.old: Your Kia auto shut-off system is designed to automatically shut off your Kia after a certain time the driver sets to reduce fuel consumption and energy consumption of your Kia and to prevent carbon monoxide (CO) poisoning.
pair.new: The Vehicle Auto Shut-off system automatically turns your vehicle OFF after a certain amount of time (set by you) to reduce fuel and energy consumption and prevent carbon monoxide poisoning.

pair.old: Requirements for activation
pair.new: Activation requirements

pair.old: This system can be activated, when all of the following requirements are satisfied:
pair.new: The Vehicle Auto Shut-off system can be activated when all of the following requirements are satisfied:

pair.old: The ENGINE START/STOP button is in ON position.
pair.new: The ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON position.

pair.old: Your Kia is stopped and in P (Park) position.
pair.new: Your vehicle is stopped and the gear is in P (Park).

pair.old: The door is opened.
pair.new: The driver door is opened.

pair.old: The passenger’s seat is not occupied.
pair.new: The passenger seat is unoccupied.

pair.old: The system can be initialized and restarted under the following conditions:
pair.new: You can initialize and restart the Vehicle Auto Shut-Off system in the following conditions:

pair.old: When pressing and releasing the brake pedal.
pair.new: You engage and release the brake pedal.

pair.old: When the accelerator pedal is depressed.
pair.new: You engage the accelerator pedal.

pair.old: When manually resettings the timer.
pair.new: You manually reset the timer.

pair.old: When manually pressing the OK button on the steering wheel.
pair.new: You press the OK button on the steering wheel.

pair.old: Canceling your Kia Auto Shut-off system
pair.new: Canceling the Vehicle Auto Shut-off system

pair.old: The system will be canceled automatically when:
pair.new: The Vehicle Auto Shut-off system automatically cancels in the following conditions:

pair.old: Your Kia speed is over 2 mph (3 km/h).
pair.new: You are driving at a speed above 2 mph (3 km/h).

pair.old: Your Kia is shifted to D (Drive), R (Reverse) or N (Neutral).
pair.new: You shift the gear to D (Drive), R (Reverse), or N (Neutral).

pair.old: Your seat belt is fastened.
pair.new: You fasten your seat belt.

pair.old: The ISG system reduces fuel consumption by automatically shutting down the engine when your Kia is at a standstill. (For example; red light, stop sign and traffic jam)
pair.new: The ISG system reduces fuel consumption by automatically turning the engine OFF when your vehicle is at a standstill (for example, at a red light, stop sign, or traffic jam).

pair.old: The engine starts automatically as soon as the starting conditions are met.
pair.new: The engine automatically restarts as soon as starting conditions are met.

pair.old: The ISG is ON whenever the engine is running.
pair.new: ISG is ON whenever the engine is running.

pair.old: If you depress the brake pedal and your Kia comes to a stop with the ISG ON, the engine will stop automatically.
pair.new: If you engage the brake pedal and bring your vehicle to a complete stop while ISG is ON, the engine automatically stops.

pair.old: Stop your Kia completely by pressing the brake pedal when the gear is in the D (Drive) or N (Neutral) position.
pair.new: Bring your vehicle to a complete stop by engaging the brake pedal while the gear is in D (Drive) or N (Neutral).

pair.old: The engine will stop and the green AUTO STOP () indicator on the instrument cluster will appear.
pair.new: The engine stops and the green AUTO STOP () indicator appears on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: If you open the engine hood in auto stop mode, the following will happen:
pair.new: If you open the engine hood while your vehicle is in Auto Stop mode, the following occur:

pair.old: The ISG system will deactivate (the light on the ISG OFF button will appears).
pair.new: ISG deactivates and the ISG OFF button illuminates.

pair.old: A message will appear on the LCD display.
pair.new: A message appears on the instrument cluster:

pair.old: If you shift the gear from N to D (Manual mode) or R without depressing the brake pedal after stopping engine automatically, the engine does not restart automatically and a warning chime sounds. Press the brake pedal for auto start.
pair.new: If you shift the gear from N (Neutral) to D (Manual mode) or R (Reverse) after the engine stops automatically without engaging the brake pedal, the engine does not restart automatically and an audible warning sounds. To enable auto start, engage the brake pedal.

pair.old: When the engine stops automatically by ISG, the engine will restart if one of the following driver actions.
pair.new: When ISG automatically stops the engine, the engine restarts if you perform one of the following actions:

pair.old: Release the brake pedal.
pair.new: You release the brake pedal.

pair.old: Move the shift gear to the R (Reverse) position or the Manual mode while depressing the brake pedal.
pair.new: You shift the gear to R (Reverse) or D (Manual mode) while engaging the brake pedal.

pair.old: The engine will start and the green AUTO STOP indicator () on the instrument cluster will go out.
pair.new: The engine starts and the green AUTO STOP () indicator disappears from the instrument cluster.

pair.old: The engine will also restart automatically without any driver actions if the following occurs:
pair.new: The engine automatically restarts on its own if any of the following occur:

pair.old: The engine has stopped for about 5 minutes.
pair.new: The engine has stopped for approximately 5 minutes.

pair.old: Your Kia is shifted to P (Park) when Auto Hold is activated.
pair.new: You shift the gear to P (Park) when Auto Hold is active.

pair.old: The door is opened or the seatbelt is unfastened when Auto Hold is activated.
pair.new: You open the driver side door or unfasten your seat belt when Auto Hold is active.

pair.old: The EPB switch is pressed when Auto Hold is activated.
pair.new: You press the EPB switch when Auto Hold is active.

pair.old: The ISG will operate under the following condition:
pair.new: ISG operates under the following conditions:

pair.old: The driver’s seatbelt is fastened.
pair.new: The driver’s seat belt is fastened.

pair.old: The driver’s door and hood are closed.
pair.new: The driver side door and the hood are closed.

pair.old: Your Kia is driven over a constant speed and stops.
pair.new: You drive your vehicle at a constant speed and then stop.

pair.old: Your Kia is sufficiently warmed up.
pair.new: Your vehicle is sufficiently warmed up.

pair.old: The steering wheel is turned less than 180 degrees and then the vehicle stops.
pair.new: The steering wheel is turned less than 180 degrees before your vehicle stops.

pair.old: If the ISG system does not meet the operating conditions, the ISG system is deactivated. The light on the ISG OFF button will appear and a message “Auto Stop conditions not met” will appear on the LCD display.
pair.new: If the operating conditions are not met, ISG deactivates. The ISG OFF button illuminates and the message “Auto Stop conditions not met” appears on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: If the light or warning message appears continuously, please check the operating conditions.
pair.new: If the light or warning message appears continuously, check the operating conditions.

pair.old: Deactivating the ISG
pair.new: Deactivating ISG

pair.old: If you wish to deactivate the ISG, press the ISG OFF button. The light on the ISG OFF button will appear.
pair.new: To deactivate ISG, press the ISG OFF button. The light on the button illuminates.

pair.old: If you press the ISG OFF button again, the ISG will be activated and the light on the ISG OFF button will turn off.
pair.new: To activate ISG, press the ISG OFF button again. The light on the button disappears.

pair.old: The yellow AUTO STOP () indicator on the instrument cluster will stay on after blinking for 5 seconds.
pair.new: The yellow AUTO STOP () indicator blinks for 5 second then remains illuminated on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: The light on the ISG OFF button will appear.
pair.new: The light on the ISG OFF button illuminates.

pair.old: When the engine is in Idle Stop mode, it’s possible to restart the engine without taking any action. Before leaving your Kia or doing anything in the engine compartment, stop the engine by the ENGINE START/STOP button to the OFF position.
pair.new: When the engine is in Idle Stop mode, the engine can restart, even if you do not take any action. Before you leave your vehicle or do anything in the engine compartment, press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the OFF position to stop the engine.

pair.old: If the Absorbent Glass Mat (AGM) battery is reconnected or replaced, ISG function will not operate immediately. If you want to use the ISG function, the battery sensor needs to be calibrated for approximately 4 hours with the ignition off. After calibration, turn the engine on and off 2 or 3 times.
pair.new: If you reconnect or replace the Absorbent Glass Mat (AGM) battery, ISG does not operate immediately. Before ISG can activate, the battery sensor must calibrate for approximately 4 hours with the ignition OFF. After the battery sensor calibrates, turn the engine ON and OFF 2 or 3 times.

pair.old: If the engine was stopped automatically by ISG, Early Engine Restart can automatically restart the engine from ISG without driver action when the vehicle ahead pulls away and the front view camera detects the preceding vehicle’s movement.
pair.new: When ISG automatically stops the engine, Early Engine Restart can automatically restart the engine without driver action when the front view camera detects the vehicle ahead driving away.

pair.old: If the engine restarts automatically by the Early Engine Restart function, a message will appear on the LCD display (if equipped) when the “AUTO STOP” page on the instrument cluster is selected.
pair.new: If Early Engine Restart automatically restarts the engine, a message appears on the instrument cluster (if equipped) when you select the AUTO STOP page on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: Even when the preceding vehicle drives away, the Early Engine Restart function may not restart the engine in case of limitations of the front view camera which can detect the preceding vehicle’s movement.
pair.new: Even when the vehicle ahead drives away, Early Engine Restart may not restart the engine due to limitations of the front view camera and its ability to detect the movement of the vehicle ahead.

pair.old: Regarding the limitations of the front view camera, please refer to Limitations of Smart ISG features
pair.new: For more information on limitations of the front view camera, refer to Limitations of Smart ISG features.

pair.old: If the engine was turned off by ISG, it can be restarted anytime by releasing the brake pedal, regardless of Early Engine Restart.
pair.new: When ISG turns the engine OFF, you can restart it at any time (regardless of Early Engine Restart) by releasing the brake pedal.

pair.old: ISG inhibition at traffic signs (STOP/YIELD/Roundabout)
pair.new: ISG inhibition at traffic signs (STOP, YIELD, and Roundabout)

pair.old: ISG inhibition at traffic signs can deactivate the ISG system and keep the engine on when stopped at certain traffic signs (STOP/YIELD/Roundabout).
pair.new: Certain traffic signs (STOP, YIELD, and Roundabout) can inhibit and deactivate ISG so that the engine stays ON at those traffic signs.

pair.old: If a STOP, YIELD or Roundabout sign is detected and the ISG system is deactivated by ISG inhibition at traffic signs, a message will appear on the LCD display (if equipped) when the “AUTO STOP” page on the instrument cluster is selected.
pair.new: If your vehicle detects a STOP, YIELD, or Roundabout sign and ISG inhibition deactivates the system, a message appears on the instrument cluster (if equipped) when you select the AUTO STOP page.

old.old: B: Auto Stop paused. STOP sign detected

new.new: B: Auto Stop paused. STOP sign detected

pair.old: Even when stopped at a STOP, YIELD, or Roundabout sign, this feature may not deactivate the ISG system in case of limitations of the front view camera which is able to detect traffic signs ahead.
pair.new: Even when you stop at a STOP, YIELD, or Roundabout sign, ISG inhibition may not deactivate the system due to limitations of the front view camera and its ability to detect traffic signs ahead.

pair.old: Regarding the limitations of the front view camera, please refer to Limitations of Smart ISG features.
pair.new: For more information on limitations of the front view camera, refer to Limitations of Smart ISG features.

pair.old: Smart ISG features may not operate normally, or may operate unexpectedly, under the following circumstances:
pair.new: Smart ISG features may operate abnormally or unexpectedly under the following circumstances:

pair.old: The detecting sensor or the surroundings are contaminated or damaged
pair.new: The detection sensor or its surrounding areas are contaminated or damaged.
The detection sensor or its surrounding areas are contaminated or damaged.

pair.old: The temperature around the front view camera is high or low due to surrounding environment
pair.new: The temperature around the front-view camera is extremely high or low.

pair.old: The camera lens is contaminated due to tinted, filmed or coated windshield, damaged glass, or sticky foreign material (sticker, bug, etc.) on the glass
pair.new: The camera lens is obscured by a windshield tint, film, or coating, damaged glass, or objects on the glass such as a sticker or insect.
The camera lens is obscured by a windshield tint, film, or coating, damaged glass, or objects on the glass such as a sticker or insect.

pair.old: Moisture is not removed or frozen on the windshield
pair.new: There is moisture or ice on the windshield.
There is moisture or ice on the windshield.

pair.old: Driving in heavy rain or snow, or thick fog
pair.new: You are driving in heavy rain, snow, or thick fog.

pair.old: The field of view of the front view camera is obstructed by sun glare
pair.new: Glare from the sun obstructs the front view camera’s field of view.
Glare from the sun obstructs the front view camera’s field of view.
Glare from the sun obstructs the front view camera’s field of view.
Glare from the sun obstructs the front view camera’s field of view.

pair.old: Street light or light from an oncoming vehicle is reflected on the wet road surface, such as a puddle on the road
pair.new: Light from a street light or oncoming vehicle reflects off of a wet road surface, such as a puddle.

pair.old: Your vehicle is being towed
pair.new: Your vehicle is being towed.
Your vehicle is being towed.

pair.old: The brightness changes suddenly, for example when entering or exiting a tunnel
pair.new: The brightness changes suddenly (for example, when you enter or exit a tunnel).
The brightness changes suddenly (for example, when you enter or exit a tunnel).

pair.old: The brightness outside is low, and the headlamps are not on or are not bright
pair.new: The brightness outside is low, and the headlights are not ON or are not bright.

pair.old: Only part of the vehicle is detected
pair.new: Only part of the vehicle is detected.

pair.old: The vehicle in front is a bus, heavy truck, truck with a unusually shaped luggage, trailer, etc.
pair.new: The vehicle ahead is a bus, heavy truck, or vehicle with bulky cargo or a trailer.

pair.old: The vehicle in front has no tail lights, tail lights are located unusually, etc.
pair.new: The vehicle ahead does not have tail lights or has an unusual tail light configuration.

pair.old: The rear of the front vehicle is small or the vehicle does not look normal, such as when the vehicle is tilted, overturned, or the side of the vehicle is visible, etc.
pair.new: The rear of vehicle ahead is small, or the vehicle does not look normal, such as when the vehicle is tilted or overturned.

pair.old: The front vehicle’s ground clearance is low or high
pair.new: The vehicle ahead has ground clearance that is extremely low or high.

pair.old: A vehicle, pedestrian or cyclist suddenly cuts in front
pair.new: A vehicle, pedestrian, or cyclist suddenly cuts in ahead of you.

pair.old: The vehicle in front is detected late
pair.new: The vehicle ahead is detected late.

pair.old: The vehicle in front is suddenly blocked by an obstacle
pair.new: The vehicle ahead is suddenly blocked by an obstacle.

pair.old: The vehicle in front suddenly changes lane or suddenly reduces speed
pair.new: The vehicle ahead suddenly changes lanes or reduces speed.

pair.old: The vehicle in front is bent out of shape
pair.new: The vehicle ahead is bent out of shape.

pair.old: The vehicle in front steers in the opposite direction of your vehicle to avoid a collision
pair.new: The vehicle ahead steers in the opposite direction of your vehicle to avoid a collision.

pair.old: With a vehicle in front, your vehicle changes lane at low speed
pair.new: With a vehicle ahead, you change lanes while driving your vehicle at low speed.

pair.old: The vehicle in front is covered with snow
pair.new: The vehicle ahead is covered in snow.
The vehicle ahead is covered in snow.

pair.old: You are departing or returning to the lane
pair.new: You are departing or returning to the lane.
You are departing or returning to the lane.

pair.old: Unstable driving
pair.new: Your driving is unstable.
Your driving is unstable.
Your driving is unstable.

pair.old: You are on a roundabout and the vehicle in front is not detected
pair.new: You are driving in a traffic circle and the curvature of the road prevents detection of the vehicle ahead.

pair.old: You are continuously driving in a circle
pair.new: You are continuously driving in a circle.
You are continuously driving in a circle.

pair.old: The vehicle in front has an unusual shape
pair.new: The vehicle ahead has an unusual shape.

pair.old: Some traffic signs may not be detected by the front view camera under the following circumstances:
pair.new: The front-view camera may not detect some traffic signs under the following circumstances:

pair.old: If located uphill or downhill away from the vehicle
pair.new: The signs are located uphill or downhill away from your vehicle.

pair.old: If located multiple lanes away from the vehicle
pair.new: The signs are located multiple lanes away from your vehicle.

pair.old: If partially or entirely blocked or covered by another vehicle, tree, or any obstacles
pair.new: The signs are partially or entirely blocked or covered by another vehicle, a tree, or any other obstacle.

pair.old: If damaged or alternated by stickers, graffiti, etc.
pair.new: The signs are damaged or altered by stickers or graffiti.

pair.old: If turned away from the vehicle
pair.new: The signs are turned away from your vehicle.

pair.old: The drive mode may be selected according to the driver’s preference or road condition.
pair.new: You may select the drive mode according to preference or road condition.

pair.old: ECO mode: ECO mode helps improve fuel efficiency for eco-friendly driving.
pair.new: ECO mode helps improve fuel efficiency for eco-friendly driving.

pair.old: NORMAL mode: NORMAL mode provides smooth driving and comfortable riding.
pair.new: NORMAL mode provides smooth driving and a comfortable ride.

pair.old: SPORT mode: SPORT mode provides sporty but firm riding.
pair.new: SPORT mode provides sporty but firm riding.

pair.old: SMART mode: SMART mode automatically adjusts the driving mode in accordance with the driver’s driving habits.
pair.new: SMART mode automatically adjusts the driving mode according to your driving habits.

pair.old: SNOW mode: SNOW mode provides appropriately distribute your Kia’s traction forces and prevent wheel slippage when driving on snowy or slippery road.
pair.new: SNOW mode appropriately distributes traction forces and prevents wheel slippage when you drive on snowy or slippery roads.

pair.old: The drive mode will change to NORMAL mode when the engine is restarted. However, except when it is in ECO mode and SMART mode. ECO mode and SMART mode will be maintained, as selected when the engine is restarted.
pair.new: The drive mode defaults to NORMAL when you restart the engine unless you select ECO or SMART. If you select ECO mode or SMART mode, the drive mode is retained when you restart the engine.

pair.old: SMART mode selects the proper driving mode among ECO, NORMAL, and SPORT by determining the driver’s driving habits (i.e., Economic or Aggressive (Sport)) from the brake pedal depression or the steering wheel operation.
pair.new: SMART mode determines your driving habits (for example, economic or aggressive (sport)) using information about brake pedal engagement and steering wheel operation to select the appropriate driving mode (ECO, NORMAL, or SPORT).

pair.old: Turning the knob to activate SMART mode. When SMART mode is activated, the indicator appears on the instrument cluster.
pair.new: To activate SMART mode, turn the drive mode knob. The indicator appears on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: Whenever the engine is restarted, the Drive Mode will revert back to NORMAL mode. If SMART mode is desired, reselect SMART mode from the DRIVE MODE button. The drive mode defaults to NORMAL when you restart the engine. If you want to use SMART mode, turn the drive mode knob to select SMART mode. No text change made, please review previous sentence for conflict.

new.new: The drive mode defaults to NORMAL when you restart the engine. If you want to use SMART mode, turn the drive mode knob to select SMART mode.

pair.old: SMART mode automatically controls your Kia driving, such as gear shifting patterns and engine torque, in accordance with the driver’s driving habits.
pair.new: SMART mode automatically controls your vehicle’s driving, including gear shifting patterns and engine torque, according to your driving habits.

pair.old: When driving conservatively your Kia in SMART mode, the driving mode changes to ECO mode to improve fuel efficiency. However, the actual fuel efficiency may differ in accordance with the driving situations (i.e., upward/downward slope, vehicle deceleration/acceleration).
pair.new: When you drive conservatively in SMART mode, the driving mode automatically changes to ECO mode to help improve fuel efficiency. However, actual fuel efficiency may differ according to driving situations (such as gradients and vehicle deceleration or acceleration).

pair.old: When you drive your Kia in SMART mode and abruptly decelerate or sharply turning the driving mode changes to SPORT mode. This may adversely affect fuel economy.
pair.new: Fuel economy may be adversely affected if the driving mode automatically changes to SPORT mode due to abrupt deceleration or sharp turns.

pair.old: Various driving situations, which you may encounter in SMART mode
pair.new: Various driving situations you may encounter in SMART mode:

pair.old: The driving mode automatically changes to ECO mode after a certain period, when you gently depress the accelerator pedal. (Your driving is determined to be economic.)
pair.new: The driving mode automatically changes to ECO mode after a certain period, when you gently engage the accelerator pedal. (Your driving is determined to be economic.)

pair.old: The driving mode automatically changes from SMART ECO mode to SMART NORMAL mode after a certain period, when you sharply or repetitively depress the accelerator pedal.
pair.new: The driving mode automatically changes from SMART ECO mode to SMART NORMAL mode after a certain period of time if you engage the accelerator pedal gently.

pair.old: The driving mode automatically changes to SMART NORMAL mode with the same driving patterns, when your Kia is driven on an upward slope of a certain angle. The driving mode automatically returns to SMART ECO mode, when your Kia returns a level road.
pair.new: The driving mode automatically changes to SMART NORMAL mode, regardless of your driving behavior, if you drive on an upward slope of a certain angle. When you return to a level road, the driving mode automatically changes back to SMART ECO mode.

pair.old: The driving mode automatically changes to SMART SPORT, when you abruptly accelerate your Kia or repetitively operate the steering wheel. (Your driving is determined to be sporty.) In this mode, your vehicle drives in a lower gear for abrupt accelerating/decelerating and increases the engine brake performance.
pair.new: The driving mode automatically changes to SMART SPORT if you rapidly accelerate or repetitively operate the steering wheel. (Your driving is determined to be sporty.) In SMART SPORT mode, your vehicle drives in a lower gear to accommodate abrupt acceleration or deceleration and increase engine braking performance.

pair.old: You may still sense the engine braking performance, even when you release the accelerator pedal in SMART SPORT mode. This is because your vehicle remains in lower gear over a certain period for next acceleration. It is normal and not a malfunction.
pair.new: You may still sense engine braking performance when you release the accelerator pedal in SMART SPORT mode. This is because your vehicle remains in a lower gear over a certain period of time until the next acceleration. Sensing engine braking performance in this case is normal and is not a malfunction.

pair.old: The driving mode automatically changes to SMART SPORT mode in harsh driving situations. Under normal driving situations, the driving mode sets to be either in SMART ECO mode or in SMART NORMAL mode.
pair.new: The driving mode automatically changes to SMART SPORT mode in harsh driving conditions. In normal driving conditions, the driving mode is either SMART ECO mode or SMART NORMAL mode.

pair.old: Limitation of SMART mode
pair.new: Limitations of SMART mode operation

pair.old: The SMART mode may be limited in following situations. (The OFF indicator appears.)
pair.new: SMART mode may be limited in following situations (the OFF indicator appears):

pair.old: Cruise Control or Smart Cruise Control is activated:
pair.new: You activate Cruise Control or Smart Cruise Control:

pair.old: Cruise Control or Smart Cruise Control may deactivate the SMART mode when your Kia is controlled by the set speed of Cruise Control or Smart Cruise Control. (SMART mode is not deactivated just by activating Cruise Control or Smart Cruise Control.)
pair.new: Cruise Control or Smart Cruise Control may deactivate SMART mode when the set speed is controlling your vehicle. (Simply activating Cruise Control or Smart Cruise Control does not deactivate SMART mode.)

pair.old: The transmission oil temperature is either extremely low or extremely high:
pair.new: The transmission oil temperature is extremely low or high:

pair.old: The SMART mode can be active under normal driving situations. However, an extremely high/ low transmission oil temperature may temporarily deactivate the SMART mode, because the transmission condition is no longer in normal operation condition.
pair.new: SMART mode can be active under normal driving situations. However, an extremely high or low transmission oil temperature may temporarily deactivate SMART mode because the transmission is no longer operating in normal conditions.

pair.old: SPORT mode manages the driving dynamics by automatically adjusting the steering effort, the engine and transmission control logic for enhanced driver performance.
pair.new: SPORT mode manages driving dynamics to enhance driver performance by automatically adjusting the steering effort, engine, and transmission control logic.

pair.old: Whenever the engine is restarted, the Drive Mode will revert back to NORMAL mode. If SPORT mode is desired, reselect SPORT mode from the DRIVE MODE button.
pair.new: The drive mode defaults to NORMAL when you restart the engine. If you want to use SPORT mode, turn the drive mode knob to select SPORT mode.

pair.old: The engine rpm will tend to remain raised over a certain length of time even after releasing the accelerator
pair.new: Engine rpm tends to remain raised over a certain amount of time, even after releasing the accelerator pedal.

pair.old: Upshifts are delayed when accelerating
pair.new: Upshifts are delayed when you accelerate.

pair.old: In SPORT mode, the fuel efficiency may decrease.
pair.new: Fuel efficiency may decrease when you drive in SPORT mode.

pair.old: When the Drive Mode is set to ECO mode, the engine and transmission control logic are changed to maximize fuel efficiency.
pair.new: ECO mode manages the engine and transmission control logic to maximize fuel efficiency.

pair.old: Whenever the engine is restarted, the drive mode remains in ECO mode.
pair.new: When you restart the engine, the drive mode remains in ECO mode.

pair.old: Fuel efficiency depends on your driving habit and road condition.
pair.new: Fuel efficiency depends on your driving habits and the road conditions.

pair.old: The acceleration response may be slightly reduced as the accelerator pedal is depressed moderately.
pair.new: The accelerator pedal is only moderately engaged and the acceleration response may be slightly reduced.

pair.old: The air conditioner performance may be limited.
pair.new: Air conditioning performance may be limited.

pair.old: The shift pattern of the automatic transmission may change.
pair.new: The automatic transmission’s shift pattern may change.

pair.old: The above situations are normal conditions when ECO mode is activated to improve fuel efficiency.
pair.new: All of the above conditions are normal when ECO mode is activated.

pair.old: Limitation of ECO mode operation:
pair.new: Limitations of ECO mode operation

pair.old: If the following conditions occur while ECO mode is operating, the system operation is limited even though there is no change in ECO indicator.
pair.new: If the following conditions occur while ECO mode is operating, the system operation is limited, even though there is no change in the ECO indicator:

pair.old: The system will be limited until engine performance returns to normal.
pair.new: The system is limited until engine performance returns to normal.

pair.old: When driving up hill:
pair.new: When driving uphill:

pair.old: The system will be limited to gain power when driving uphill because engine torque is restricted.
pair.new: The system is limited to gain power when driving uphill because the engine torque is restricted.

pair.old: When the accelerator pedal is fully depressed for a few seconds:
pair.new: When you fully engage the accelerator pedal for a few seconds:

pair.old: The system will be limited, determing that the driver wants to speed up.
pair.new: The system is limited as it determines that the driver wants to speed up.

pair.old: Your vehicle’s fuel economy depends mainly on your driving style, where you drive and when you drive.
pair.new: Your vehicle’s fuel economy mainly depends on how, where, and when you drive.

pair.old: Each of these factors affects how many miles (kilometers) you can achieve from a gallon (a liter) of fuel. To operate your vehicle as economically as possible, use the following driving suggestions to help save money in both fuel and repairs:
pair.new: Each of these factors affect your miles per gallon (kilometers per liter) of fuel. To operate your vehicle as economically as possible and save money on both fuel and repairs, follow these driving suggestions:

pair.old: Take care of your tires. Keep them inflated to the recommended pressure. Incorrect inflation, either too much or too little, results in unnecessary tire wear. Check the tire pressures at least once a month.
pair.new: Take care of your tires. Keep them inflated to the recommended pressure. Incorrect inflation results in unnecessary tire wear. Check tire pressure at least once a month.

pair.old: Be sure that the wheels are aligned correctly. Improper alignment can result from hitting curbs or driving too fast over irregular surfaces. Poor alignment causes faster tire wear and may also result in other problems as well as greater fuel consumption.
pair.new: Be sure the wheels are correctly aligned. Hitting curbs or driving too fast over irregular surfaces can result in improper alignment. Poor alignment causes faster tire wear, and may result in other problems and greater fuel consumption.

pair.old: Keep your vehicle in good condition. For better fuel economy and reduced maintenance costs, maintain your vehicle in accordance with the maintenance schedule in . If you drive your vehicle in severe conditions, more frequent maintenance is required (Refer to ).
pair.new: Keep your vehicle in good condition. For better fuel economy and reduced maintenance costs, maintain your vehicle in accordance with the maintenance schedule in . If you drive your vehicle in severe conditions, more frequent maintenance is required. For more information, refer to .

pair.old: Don’t let the engine idle longer than necessary. If you are waiting (and not in traffic), turn off your engine and restart only when you’re ready to go.
pair.new: Don’t let the engine idle longer than necessary. If you are waiting (and not in traffic), turn OFF your engine and restart only when you’re ready to go.

pair.old: Don’t “lug” or “over-rev” the engine. Lugging is driving too slowly in too high a gear resulting in the engine bucking. If this happens, shift to a lower gear. Over-revving is racing the engine beyond its safe limit. This can be avoided by shifting at the recommended speeds.
pair.new: Don’t lug or over-rev the engine. Lugging is driving too slowly in too high of a gear, which results in the engine bucking. If this happens, shift to a lower gear. Over-revving is racing the engine beyond its safe limit. Avoid over-revving by shifting the gear at the recommended speeds.

pair.old: Fuel economy is less in crosswinds and headwinds. To help offset some of this loss, slow down when driving in these conditions.
pair.new: Fuel economy lessens in crosswinds and headwinds. To offset the decreased fuel economy, slow down when driving in these conditions.

pair.old: Engine off during motion
pair.new: Turning the engine OFF when in motion

pair.old: Never turn the engine off to coast down hills or anytime your Kia is in motion. The power steering and power brakes will not function properly without the engine running. In addition, turning off the ignition while driving could engage the steering wheel lock resulting in loss of steering. Keep the engine on and downshift to an appropriate gear for engine braking.
pair.new: Never turn the engine OFF to coast down hills, or any time your vehicle is in motion. The power steering and power brakes will not function properly if the engine is not running. In addition, turning the ignition OFF while driving could engage the steering wheel lock and result in loss of steering. Keep the engine ON and downshift to an appropriate gear for engine braking.

pair.old: If driving conditions deteriorate due to poor weather or road conditions, you should pay even more attention than usual to your driving.
pair.new: In poor weather or road conditions, you should drive even more carefully than usual.

pair.old: When hazardous driving conditions are encountered such as water, snow, ice, mud, sand, or similar hazards, follow these suggestions:
pair.new: When you encounter water, snow, ice, mud, sand, or similar hazards on the road, follow these suggestions:

pair.old: If stalled in snow, mud, or sand, use the second gear. Accelerate slowly to avoid spinning the drive wheels.
pair.new: If you are stalled in snow, mud, or sand, shift to the 2nd gear and accelerate slowly to avoid spinning the drive wheels.

pair.old: Use sand, rock salt, or other nonslip material under the drive wheels to provide traction when stalled in ice, snow, or mud.
pair.new: Use sand, rock salt, or another nonslip material under the drive wheels to provide traction when you are stalled in ice, snow, or mud.

pair.old: If it is necessary to rock your Kia to free it from snow, ice, sand, or mud, first turn the steering wheel right and left to clear the area around your drive wheels. Shift back and forth between R (Reverse) and any forward gear.
pair.new: If it is necessary to rock your vehicle to free it from snow, ice, sand, or mud, first turn the steering wheel right and left to clear the area around your drive wheels. Then shift the gear back and forth between R (Reverse) and any forward gear.

pair.old: Do not race the engine or spin the wheels. If you are still stuck after a few tries, have your Kia pulled out by a tow vehicle to avoid engine overheating and possible damage to the transmission.
pair.new: Do not rev the engine or spin the wheels. If you are still stuck after a few tries, have your vehicle towed out to avoid overheating the engine and causing possible damage to the transmission.

pair.old: Do not attempt to rock your Kia if people or objects are nearby. Your Kia may suddenly move forward or backwards as it becomes unstuck.
pair.new: Do not attempt to rock your vehicle if people or objects are nearby. Your vehicle may suddenly move forward or backward as it becomes unstuck.

pair.old: Prolonged rocking may cause vehicle overheating, transmission damage or failure, and tire damage.
pair.new: Prolonged rocking may cause overheating of the vehicle, transmission damage or failure, and tire damage.

pair.old: Do not spin the wheels, especially at speeds more than 35 mph (56 km/h). Spinning the wheels at high speeds when your Kia is stationary could overheat and damage tires, and the rotating wheels may disengage and injure bystanders.
pair.new: Do not spin the wheels, especially at speeds above 35 mph (56 km/h). Spinning the wheels at high speeds when your vehicle is stationary could overheat and damage the tires. The rotating wheels may detach and injure bystanders.

pair.old: ESC should be turned OFF prior to rocking your Kia.
pair.new: Turn the ESC function OFF before rocking your vehicle.

pair.old: Smooth cornering
pair.new: Smooth turning

pair.old: Avoid braking or gear changing in corners, especially when roads are wet. Ideally, turns should always be taken under gentle acceleration. If you follow these suggestions, tire wear will be held to a minimum.
pair.new: Avoid braking or shifting the gear while turning, especially when roads are wet. Ideally, you should always turn with gentle acceleration. Following these suggestions minimizes wear on your tires.

pair.old: Because night driving presents more hazards than driving in the daylight, follow these important tips:
pair.new: Because night driving is more hazardous than driving in the daylight, follow these important tips:

pair.old: Slow down and keep more distance between you and other vehicles, as it may be more difficult to see at night, especially in areas where there may not be any street lights.
pair.new: Slow down and keep more distance between you and other vehicles, as it may be more difficult to see at night, especially in areas without any street lights.

pair.old: Adjust your mirrors to reduce the glare from other driver’s headlamps.
pair.new: Adjust your mirrors to reduce the glare from other drivers’ headlights.

pair.old: Keep your headlights clean and properly aimed on vehicles not equipped with the automatic headlamp aiming feature. Dirty or improperly aimed headlamps will make it much more difficult to see at night.
pair.new: Keep your headlamps clean and properly aimed at vehicles that are not equipped with the automatic headlight aiming feature. Dirty or improperly aimed headlamps will make it much more difficult to see at night.

pair.old: Rain and wet roads can make driving dangerous, especially if you’re not prepared for the slick pavement.
pair.new: Rain and wet roads can make driving dangerous, especially if you are unprepared for the slick pavement.

pair.old: A few things to consider when driving in the rain:
pair.new: When driving in rain, consider the following:

pair.old: A heavy rainfall will make it harder to see and will increase the distance needed to stop your vehicle, so slow down.
pair.new: A heavy rainfall makes it harder to see and increases the required stopping distance for your vehicle, so slow down.

pair.old: Keep your windshield wiping equipment in good shape. Replace your windshield wiper blades when they show signs of streaking or missing areas on the windshield.
pair.new: Keep your windshield wiping equipment in good shape. Replace your windshield wiper blades when they show signs of streaking or miss areas of the windshield.

pair.old: If your tires are not in good condition, making a quick stop on wet pavement can cause a skid and possibly lead to an accident. Be sure your tires are in good shape.
pair.new: If your tires are not in good condition, stopping quickly on wet pavement can cause you to skid and possibly lead to an accident. Be sure your tires are in good shape.

pair.old: Turn on your headlamps to make it easier for others to see you.
pair.new: Turn ON your headlights to make it easier for others to see you.

pair.old: Driving too fast through large puddles can affect your brakes. If you must go through puddles, try to drive through them slowly.
pair.new: Driving too fast through large puddles can affect your brakes. If you must drive through puddles, try to drive through them slowly.

pair.old: If you believe your brakes are wet, apply them lightly while driving until normal braking operation returns.
pair.new: If you believe your brakes are wet, lightly engage the brake pedal while driving until braking operation returns to normal.

pair.old: If the road is wet enough and you are going fast enough, your vehicle may have little or no contact with the road surface and ride on the water. The best advice is SLOW DOWN when the road is wet.
pair.new: If the road is wet enough and you are driving fast enough, your vehicle may have little or no contact with the road surface and ride on the water. The best advice is to SLOW DOWN when the road is wet.

pair.old: The risk of hydroplaning increases as the depth of tire tread decreases, refer to.
pair.new: The risk of hydroplaning increases as the depth of tire tread decreases. Refer to .

pair.old: Avoid driving through flooded areas unless you are sure the water is no higher than the bottom of the wheel hub. Drive through any water slowly. Allow adequate stopping distance because brake performance may be affected.
pair.new: Avoid driving through flooded areas unless you are sure the water is no higher than the bottom of the wheel hub. Drive through any water slowly. Allow adequate stopping distance after driving through water because it may affect brake performance.

pair.old: After driving through water, dry the brakes by gently applying them several times while your Kia is moving slowly.
pair.new: After driving through water, dry the brakes by gently applying them several times while your vehicle is moving slowly.

pair.old: Adjust the tire inflation pressures to specification. Low tire inflation pressures will result in overheating and possible failure of the tires.
pair.new: Adjust the tire inflation pressures according to the specification. Low tire inflation pressures result in overheating and possible failure of the tires.

pair.old: Avoid using worn or damaged tires which may result in reduced traction or tire failure.
pair.new: Avoid driving with worn or damaged tires, which may result in reduced traction or tire failure.

pair.old: Under/over inflated tires
pair.new: Underinflated or overinflated tires

pair.old: Always check the tires for proper inflation before driving. Under inflated or overinflated tires can cause poor handling, loss of vehicle control, and sudden tire failure, leading to accidents, injuries, and death. For proper tire pressures, refer to .
pair.new: Always check the tires for proper inflation before driving. Underinflated or overinflated tires can cause poor handling, loss of vehicle control, and sudden tire failure, leading to accidents, injuries, and death. For proper tire pressures, refer to .

pair.old: Always check the tire tread before driving your vehicle. Worn-out tires can result in loss of vehicle control. Worn-out tires should be replaced as soon as possible. For further information and tread limits, refer to .
pair.new: Always check the tire tread before driving your vehicle. Worn out tires can result in loss of control of the vehicle. Replace worn out tires as soon as possible. For further information and tread limits, refer to .

pair.old: Fuel, engine coolant and engine oil
pair.new: Fuel, engine coolant, and engine oil

pair.old: High speed travel consumes more fuel than urban driving. Do not forget to check both the engine coolant and engine oil.
pair.new: High speed travel consumes more fuel than driving in urban areas. Do not forget to check both the engine coolant and engine oil.

pair.old: A loose or damaged drive belt may result in overheating of the engine.
pair.new: A loose or damaged drive belt may cause the engine to overheat.

pair.old: Severe weather conditions in the winter results in greater tire wear and other problems.
pair.new: Severe weather conditions in the winter result in greater tire wear and other problems.
