pair.old: The basic function for Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist is warned and controlled in the following way.
pair.new: Basic functions for Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist are:

pair.old: Collision warning
pair.new: Collision Warning

pair.old: Emergency braking
pair.new: Emergency Braking

pair.old: Collision Warning can alert the driver with the Forward Safety warning light blinking (), warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration.
pair.new: Collision Warning can alert you with Forward Safety warning light blinking (), a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration.

pair.old: The collision warning operates under the following vehicle speed conditions, depending on the vehicle in front.
pair.new: Collision Warning operates when the vehicle ahead moves within the following speed ranges:

pair.old: Vehicle or powered two-wheeler: 6 ~ 124 mph (10 ~ 200 km/h)
pair.new: The vehicle or motorcycle speed is approximately 6-124 mph (10-200 km/h).

pair.old: Pedestrian or cyclist: 6 ~ 53 mph (10 ~ 85 km/h)
pair.new: The pedestrian or cyclist speed is approximately 6-53 mph (10-85 km/h).

pair.old: Emergency braking will alert the driver with the Forward Safety warning light blinking (), warning message, an audible warning and steering wheel vibration.
pair.new: Emergency Braking can alert you with Forward Safety warning light blinking (), a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration.

pair.old: Emergency braking will be activated according to oncoming objects and danger levels in the following conditions.
pair.new: Emergency Braking is activated according to oncoming objects and danger levels under the following conditions:

pair.old: *: If Forward Collision Avoidance Assist judges that avoiding a collision is difficult even by changing the driving lane. The function operate range may decrease due to surroundings of the vehicle. (if equipped)
pair.new: *Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist may determine that avoiding a collision by changing lanes is difficult. The function’s operational range may decrease due to your vehicle’s surroundings (if equipped).

pair.old: Pedestrian or cyclist: 6 ~ 40 mph (10 ~ 65 km/h)
pair.new: Pedestrian or cyclist: 6-40 mph (10-65 km/h)

pair.old: The function operation range may decrease due to the front traffic condition or the surroundings of the vehicle.
pair.new: The function’s operational range may decrease due to the traffic ahead of or the surroundings of the vehicle.

pair.old: When driving at night, the performance of powered two-wheeler recognition is decreased, so the Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist system may be temporarily limited or may not work.
pair.new: When driving at night, the ability to detect motorcycles is decreased. Thus, the Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist system may temporarily be limited or not work.
When driving at night, the ability to detect motorcycles is decreased. Thus, the Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist system may temporarily be limited or not work.

pair.old: When the vehicle is stopped due to emergency braking, the warning message will appear on the cluster.
pair.new: When Emergency Braking stops your vehicle, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster.
When Emergency Braking stops your vehicle, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster.
When Emergency Braking stops your vehicle, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster.
When Emergency Braking stops your vehicle, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: For your safety, the driver should engage the brake pedal immediately and check the surroundings.
pair.new: For safety purposes, you should immediately engage the brake pedal and check the vehicle’s surroundings.
For safety purposes, you should immediately engage the brake pedal and check the vehicle’s surroundings.
For safety purposes, you should immediately engage the brake pedal and check the vehicle’s surroundings.
For safety purposes, you should immediately engage the brake pedal and check the vehicle’s surroundings.

pair.old: Brake control will end after the vehicle is stopped by emergency braking for approximately 2 seconds.
pair.new: Brake control ends approximately 2 seconds after Emergency Braking stops your vehicle.
Brake control ends approximately 2 seconds after Emergency Braking stops your vehicle.
Brake control ends approximately 2 seconds after Emergency Braking stops your vehicle.
Brake control ends approximately 2 seconds after Emergency Braking stops your vehicle.
Brake control ends approximately 2 seconds after Emergency Braking stops your vehicle.

pair.old: The warning sound during collision warning and emergency braking can be switched off by pressing the hazard warning light.
pair.new: Press the hazard lights button to turn OFF the warning sound during collision warning and emergency braking.
Press the hazard lights button to turn OFF the warning sound during collision warning and emergency braking.
Press the hazard lights button to turn OFF the warning sound during collision warning and emergency braking.

pair.old: The Junction turning function is warned and controlled in the following way.
pair.new: Junction Turning functions for Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist are:

pair.old: Collision Warning can alert the driver with the Forward Safety warning light blinking (), warning message, an audible warning and steering wheel vibration.
pair.new: Collision Warning can alert you with Forward Safety warning light blinking (), a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration.

pair.old: Collision warning will be activated in the following conditions:
pair.new: Collision Warning activates under the following conditions:

pair.old: Your driving speed: Approximately 6-19 mph (10-30 km/h)
pair.new: Your speed is approximately 6-19 mph (10-30 km/h).

pair.old: Oncoming vehicle speed: Approximately 19-44 mph (30-70 km/h)
pair.new: The oncoming vehicle speed is approximately 19-44 mph (30-70 km/h).
The oncoming vehicle speed is approximately 19-44 mph (30-70 km/h).

pair.old: Powered two-wheeler and cyclist speed: Approximately 9-44 mph (15-70 km/h)
pair.new: The motorcycle or cyclist speed is approximately 9-44 mph (15-70 km/h).
The motorcycle or cyclist speed is approximately 9-44 mph (15-70 km/h).

pair.old: Emergency braking can alert the driver with the Forward Safety warning light blinking (), warning message, an audible warning and steering wheel vibration. The brake assist can be activated and to help avoid a collision with an oncoming vehicle.
pair.new: Emergency Braking can alert you with the Forward Safety warning light blinking (), a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration. Emergency Braking may activate to help avoid a collision with an oncoming vehicle.

pair.old: Emergency braking will be activated in the following conditions.
pair.new: Emergency Braking activates in the following conditions:
Emergency Braking activates in the following conditions:
Emergency Braking activates in the following conditions:

pair.old: Your driving speed: 6 ~ 19 mph (10 ~ 30 km/h)
pair.new: Your speed is approximately 6-19 mph (10-30 km/h).

pair.old: The Direct Oncoming function is warned and controlled in the following way.
pair.new: Direct Oncoming functions for Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist are:

pair.old: Collision warning can alert the driver with the Forward Safety warning light () blinking, warning message, an audible warning and steering wheel vibration.
pair.new: Collision Warning can alert you with Forward Safety warning light blinking (), a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration.

pair.old: Collision Warning will be activated in following conditions.
pair.new: Collision Warning activates in the following conditions:

pair.old: Crossing vehicle speed: Approximately above 6 mph (10 km/h)
pair.new: The crossing vehicle speed is above approximately 6 mph (10 km/h).
The crossing vehicle speed is above approximately 6 mph (10 km/h).

pair.old: Emergency braking can alert the driver with the Forward Safety warning light () blinking, warning message, an audible warning and steering wheel vibration.
pair.new: Emergency Braking can alert you with Forward Safety warning light blinking (), a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration.

pair.old: Your driving speed: Approximately 19-80 mph (30 ~ 130 km/h)
pair.new: Your speed is approximately 19-80 mph (30-130 km/h).

pair.old: If your vehicle or the oncoming vehicle is not driving straight, Front Oncoming function warning and control may be late or may not operate.
pair.new: If your vehicle or the oncoming vehicle is not driving straight, the Front Oncoming function warning and control may be late or may not operate.

pair.old: When driving at night, the performance of t powered two-wheeler recognition is decreased, so the Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist system may be temporarily limited or may not work.
pair.new: When driving at night, the ability to detect motorcycles is decreased. Thus, the Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist system may temporarily be limited or not work.

pair.old: The Junction Crossing function is warned and controlled by the following level.
pair.new: Junction Crossing functions for Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist are:

pair.old: Collision Warning can alert the driver with the Forward Safety warning light () blinking, warning message, an audible warning and steering wheel vibration.
pair.new: Collision Warning can alert you with Forward Safety warning light blinking (), a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration.

pair.old: Forward collision warning will be activated in the following conditions.
pair.new: Collision Warning activates in the following conditions:

pair.old: Your driving speed: Approximately 6-34 mph (10-55km/h)
pair.new: Your speed is approximately 6-34 mph (10-55km/h).
Your speed is approximately 6-34 mph (10-55km/h).

pair.old: Crossing vehicle speed: Approximately 6-37 mph (10-60km/h)
pair.new: The crossing vehicle speed is approximately 6-37 mph (10-60km/h).

pair.old: The Forward Safety warning light () blinking, warning message, an audible warning and the steering wheel vibration that emergency braking will be assisted. The brake assist can be activated and help mitigate or avoid a collision with a vehicle, pedestrian cyclist, or powered two-wheeler.
pair.new: Emergency Braking can alert the you with Forward Safety warning light blinking (), a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration. Brake assist may activate to help avoid a collision with a vehicle, pedestrian, cyclist, or motorcycle.

pair.old: Crossing vehicle speed: Approximately 6-25 mph (10-40km/h)
pair.new: The crossing vehicle speed is approximately 6-25 mph (10-40km/h).

pair.old: If the collision angle with the crossing vehicle is beyond a certain range, Junction Crossing Warning and control may be late or may not operate.
pair.new: If the angle of possible collision with the crossing vehicle is beyond a certain range, Junction Crossing warning and control may be late or may not operate.

pair.old: The Lane-change oncoming function is warned and controlled in the following way.
pair.new: Lane-Change Oncoming functions for Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist are:

pair.old: Collision warning can alert the driver with the Emergency steering warning light () blinking, warning message, an audible warning and steering wheel vibration.
pair.new: Collision Warning can alert you with Emergency steering warning light blinking (), a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration.

pair.old: Collision warning will be activated in the following conditions.
pair.new: Collision Warning activates in the following conditions:

pair.old: Your driving speed: Approximately 25 ~ 90 mph (40 ~ 145 km/h)
pair.new: Your speed is approximately 25-90 mph (40-145 km/h).
Your speed is approximately 25-90 mph (40-145 km/h).

pair.old: Oncoming vehicle or powered two-wheeler speed: Approximately above 6 mph (10 km/h)
pair.new: The oncoming vehicle or motorcycle speed is above approximately 6 mph (10 km/h).
The oncoming vehicle or motorcycle speed is above approximately 6 mph (10 km/h).

pair.old: Relative speed: Approximately below 124 mph (200 km/h)
pair.new: The relative speed is below approximately 124 mph (200 km/h).
The relative speed is below approximately 124 mph (200 km/h).

pair.old: Emergency steering can alert the driver with the Emergency steering warning light () blinking, warning message, an audible warning and steering wheel vibration. The steering assist will be activated to help mitigate or avoid a collision with an oncoming vehicle or powered two-wheelers.
pair.new: Emergency Steering can alert you with Emergency Steering warning light blinking (), a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration. The Steering assist activates to help mitigate or avoid a collision with an oncoming vehicle or motorcycle.

pair.old: Emergency steering will be activated in following conditions.
pair.new: Emergency Steering activates in the following conditions:
Emergency Steering activates when your speed is approximately 25-53 mph (40-85 km/h).
Emergency Steering activates when your speed is approximately 40-47 mph (65-75 km/h).

pair.old: Lane-change side function is warned and controlled in the following way.
pair.new: Lane-Change Side functions for Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist are:

pair.old: Collision warning will alert the driver with the emergency steering warning light () blinking, warning message, an audible warning and steering wheel vibration. The warning light will appear on the outside rear-view mirror when the vehicle either on a right or left lane is detected from the rear.
pair.new: Collision Warning can alert you with the emergency steering warning light blinking (), a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration. The warning light appears on the outside rearview mirror a vehicle is detected in the right or left lane from the rear.

pair.old: Collision warning will be activated in following conditions.
pair.new: Collision Warning activates when your speed is approximately 25-90 mph (40-145 km/h).

pair.old: To warn the driver that emergency steering will be assisted, the emergency steering warning light ()blinking, warning message will appear on the cluster, an audible warning will sound and the steering wheel will vibrate.
pair.new: Emergency Steering can alert you with Emergency Steering warning light blinking (), a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration.

pair.old: The warning light on the outside rear-view mirror will appear when the vehicle on both lanes is detected from the rear. Steering will be assisted to avoid collision.
pair.new: The warning light on the outside rearview mirror appears when a vehicle is detected in the right or left lane from the rear. Emergency Steering will activate to avoid a collision.

pair.old: Lane-Change Side function does not operate if the oncoming vehicle from the front side is stopped.
pair.new: Lane-Change Side function does not operate if the oncoming vehicle is stopped.

pair.old: The detection range of the front-side and rear-side radars is based on the standard road width of ordinary roads. On narrow roads, warnings may be triggered by a vehicle or powered two-wheeler driving in the next lane. Conversely, on wide roads, radars may fail to recognize a vehicle or powered two-wheeler driving in the next lane and not issue warnings.
pair.new: The front-side and rear-side radar detection range is based on the width of standard roads. On narrow roads, warnings may be triggered by a vehicle or motorcycle driving in the next lane. Conversely, on wide roads, radars may fail to recognize a vehicle or motorcycle driving in the next lane.

pair.old: The lateral approaching vehicle response function is deactivated in the following situations:
pair.new: The Lane-Change Side function deactivates in the following situations:

pair.old: If you drive more than a certain distance into the next lane
pair.new: You drive more than a certain distance into the next lane.

pair.old: If you move away from the direction where there is a risk of collision
pair.new: You move away from the direction where there is a risk of collision.

pair.old: If the steering wheel is rotated rapidly
pair.new: You turn the steering wheel rapidly.

pair.old: If you step on the brake pedal
pair.new: You engage the brake pedal.

pair.old: If Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist is activated
pair.new: Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist activates.

pair.old: After the lateral approaching vehicle response function has been triggered or after changing lanes, be sure to move into the center of the lane. If you continue to drive along the edge of the lane without moving to the lane’s center, the lateral approaching vehicle response function will be deactivated.
pair.new: After the Lane-Change Side function is triggered or after you change lanes, be sure to move into the center of the lane. If you continue to drive along the edge of the lane, the Lane-Change Side function will deactivate.

pair.old: If the driver’s seat is on the left, a collision warning may be issued when turning left; if the driver’s seat is on the right, a collision warning may be issued when turning right.
pair.new: In a left-hand drive vehicle, a collision warning may sound when turning left. In a right-hand drive vehicle, a collision warning may sound when turning right.

pair.old: The Evasive Steering Assist function is warned and controlled in the following way.
pair.new: The Evasive Steering Assist function for Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist is Emergency Steering.

pair.old: Emergency steering will alert the driver with the emergency steering warning light () blinking, warning message, an audible warning and steering wheel vibration.
pair.new: Emergency Steering will alert you with Emergency steering warning light blinking (), a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration.
Emergency Steering can alert you with Emergency steering warning light blinking (), a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration.

pair.old: If there is a risk of collision with a vehicle, a pedestrian, a cyclist, or a powered two-wheeler in front, the steering will be assisted to help prevent collision when the driver steers the vehicle to avoid collision.
pair.new: If there is a risk of a collision with another vehicle or a pedestrian, cyclist, or motorcycle ahead of your vehicle, Emergency Steering will assist you in steering to help prevent a collision.

pair.old: If there is a risk of collision with a pedestrian, a cyclist, or a powered two-wheeler in front and your vehicle already exceeded the emergency braking speed limit, the steering will be assisted to help prevent collision when the driver steers the vehicle to avoid collision.
pair.new: If there is a risk of a collision with a pedestrian, cyclist, or motorcycle ahead of your vehicle and you have already exceeded the Emergency Braking speed limit, Emergency Steering will activate to help you avoid a collision.

pair.old: The steering wheel may turn automatically when emergency steering is operating.
pair.new: When Emergency Steering is operating, the steering wheel may turn automatically.

pair.old: Emergency steering will automatically cancel when risk factors disappear. If necessary, the driver must steer the vehicle.
pair.new: Emergency Steering automatically ends when risk factors disappear.

pair.old: Emergency steering may not operate or may cancel during operation if the steering wheel is held tight or steered in the opposite direction.
pair.new: Emergency Steering may deactivate during operation if the steering wheel is held tightly or steered in the opposite direction.

pair.old: When steering is assisted to avoid collision with a vehicle, pedestrian and cyclist, Evasive steering assist will be canceled if collisions with other objects (vehicle, powered two-wheeler, pedestrians, or cyclists) are expected.
pair.new: When steering is assisted to avoid a collision with another vehicle, a pedestrian, or a cyclist, Evasive steering assist will deactivate if collisions are expected with other vehicles, motorcycles, pedestrians, or cyclists.

pair.old: Evasive steering assist may not operate if space to avoid collision in the driving lane is insufficient.
pair.new: Evasive steering assist may not operate if there is insufficient space to avoid a collision.

pair.old: For more details on warning messages, refer to .
pair.new: For more information about warning messages, refer to .

pair.old: Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist does not operate in all situations or cannot avoid all collisions.
pair.new: Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist does not operate in all situations and cannot avoid all collisions.

pair.old: The driver should hold the responsibility to control the vehicle. Do not solely depend on Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist. Rather, maintain a safe braking distance, and if necessary, depress the brake pedal to reduce driving speed or to stop the vehicle.
pair.new: You are responsible for controlling the vehicle. Do not solely depend on Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist. Maintain a safe braking distance and manually engage the brake pedal to reduce speed or stop the vehicle.

pair.old: Never deliberately operate Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist on people, objects, etc. It may cause serious injury or death.
pair.new: Never deliberately drive toward people or objects. It may cause serious injury or death.

pair.old: Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate if the driver depresses the brake pedal to avoid collision.
pair.new: Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate if you manually break to avoid collision.

pair.old: Depending on the road and driving conditions, Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist may warn the driver late or may not warn the driver.
pair.new: Depending on the road and driving conditions, Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist may warn you late or not at all.

pair.old: During Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist operation, the vehicle may stop suddenly injuring passengers and shifting loose objects. Always have the seat belt on and keep loose objects secured.
pair.new: During Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist operation, the vehicle may stop suddenly, which can cause loose objects to shift and possibly injure passengers. Always fasten seat belts and secure loose objects.

pair.old: If any other function’s warning message is displayed or audible warning is generated, Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist warning message may not be displayed and audible warning may not be generated.
pair.new: Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist may not display a warning message or generate an audible warning if any other function’s warning message is displayed or any other function’s audible warning is generated at the same time.

pair.old: You may not hear the warning sound of Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist if the surrounding is noisy.
pair.new: You may not hear the warning sound of Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist if you are in noisy surroundings.
You may not hear the warning sound of Smart Cruise Control if your surroundings are noisy.

pair.old: Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist may turn off or may not operate properly or may operate unnecessarily depending on the road conditions and the surroundings.
pair.new: Depending on road conditions and surroundings, Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist may turn OFF or operate improperly or unnecessarily.

pair.old: During emergency braking, braking control by Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist will automatically cancel when the driver excessively depresses the accelerator pedal or sharply steers the vehicle.
pair.new: Emergency Braking activated by Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist will end automatically if you brake hard or turn the steering wheel sharply.

pair.old: The surroundings and pedestrians, cyclists and powered two-wheeler or other vehicles in front of you may affect the speed or detection range to operate Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist, resulting in Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist temporarily limited or disabled.
pair.new: Your surroundings may affect the speed or detection range of Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist, and result in the function being temporarily limited or disabled.

pair.old: Forward collision avoidance assistance operates under specific conditions, taking into account the status of the oncoming vehicle, powered two-wheeler and cyclist, driving direction, speed, and surrounding environment to judges the level of risk.
pair.new: Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist takes into account the oncoming vehicle’s direction, speed, and surroundings to judge risk.

pair.old: The function may be limited or deactivated in cases where the driving speed is excessively high or there is a significant speed difference between the vehicle and the oncoming powered two-wheelers or cyclists.
pair.new: Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist may not activate when driving at a high speed or when there is a significant difference in speed between your vehicle and oncoming vehicles.

pair.old: When a collision is imminent, the Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist may assist the driver with brakes if the driver fails to brake enough.
pair.new: When a collision is imminent, Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist may activate brake assist if you do not brake enough to avoid the collision.

pair.old: The images or colors on the instrument cluster may be different from those in this manual.
pair.new: The images or colors may display differently depending on the specifications of the instrument cluster or theme.

pair.old: When Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist is not working properly, the warning message will appear, and the Forward Safety warning light (), the Emergency Steering warning light () and the Master warning light () will appear on the cluster. Visit an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: When Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist is not working properly, the Master warning light (), the Forward Safety warning light (), and the Emergency Steering warning light () appear on the instrument cluster, along with a warning message that lasts for several seconds. Visit an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: When the windshield where the front view camera is located, front radar cover, bumper or sensor is covered with foreign material, such as snow or rain, it can reduce the detecting performance and temporarily limit or disable Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist.
pair.new: When foreign material (such as snow or rain) covers the windshield where the front view camera is located, the front radar cover, the bumper, or the sensor, it can reduce the detecting performance and temporarily limit or disable Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist.

pair.old: If this occurs a warning message, and the Forward Safety warning light (), the Emergency Steering warning light () and the Master warning light () will appear on the cluster.
pair.new: If this occurs, a warning message, the Forward Safety warning light (), the Emergency Steering warning light (), and the Master warning light () display on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist will operate properly when snow, rain or foreign material is removed.
pair.new: Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist operates properly when you remove foreign material (such as snow or rain) from the sensor.

pair.old: If Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist does not operate properly after obstruction (snow, rain, or foreign material) is removed (including trailer, carrier, etc. from the rear bumper), have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist still does not operate properly after you remove foreign material (such as snow or rain) from the sensor or obstructions (such as a trailer or carrier) from the rear bumper, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Even though the warning message or warning light does not appear on the cluster, Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist may not properly operate.
pair.new: Even when the warning message or warning light does not appear on the instrument cluster, Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly.

pair.old: You can check it in the service message of the utility information view of the cluster display window.
pair.new: You can check the Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist status in the service message of the utility information view of the instrument cluster display window.
You can check the Lane Keeping Assist status in the service message of the utility information view of the instrument cluster display window.
You can check the Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist status in the service message of the utility information view of the instrument cluster display window.
You can check the Intelligent Speed Limit Assist status in the service message of the utility information view of the instrument cluster.
You can check the Lane Following Assist status in the service message of the utility information view of the instrument cluster display window.
You can check the Reverse Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist status in the service message of the utility information view of the instrument cluster.

pair.old: Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate normally, or it may operate unexpectedly under the following circumstances:
pair.new: Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist may operate abnormally or unexpectedly under the following circumstances:

pair.old: A sensor or the its surroundings are contaminated or damaged
pair.new: A sensor is obscured or damaged.

pair.old: The temperature around the front- view camera is extremely high or low
pair.new: The temperature around the front-view camera is extremely high or low.

pair.old: The camera lens is obscured by a tinted, filmed or coated windshield, by damaged glass, or by foreign material (sticker, bug, etc.) on the glass
pair.new: The camera lens is obscured by a windshield tint, film, or coating, damaged glass, or objects on the glass such as a sticker or insect.

pair.old: There is moisture or ice on the windshield
pair.new: There is moisture or ice on the windshield.

pair.old: Heavy rain, snow, or fog obscure the camera’s view
pair.new: Heavy rain, snow, or fog obscures the camera’s view.

pair.old: Light is reflected off a wet road surface, such as a puddle in the road
pair.new: Light reflects off of a wet road surface, such as a puddle.

pair.old: An object is placed on the instrument panel
pair.new: There is an object on the instrument panel.
There is an object on the instrument panel.

pair.old: The surroundings are very bright (e.g., glare) or very dark (e.g., in a tunnel).
pair.new: Your vehicle’s surroundings are excessively bright or dark.

pair.old: The light changes suddenly, (e.g., when entering or exiting a tunnel)
pair.new: The light changes suddenly, such as when you enter or exit a tunnel.

pair.old: Dark conditions, when the headlamps are not on or are obscured, or the other vehicle has no taillights
pair.new: You are in dark conditions and your vehicle’s headlights are not on or are obscured.

pair.old: Only part of the vehicle, powered two-wheeler, pedestrian or cyclist is detected
pair.new: Another vehicle or a motorcycle, pedestrian, or cyclist is only partially visible.

pair.old: The vehicle in front is a bus, heavy truck, truck with an unusually shaped cargo, trailer, etc.
pair.new: The vehicle ahead is a bus, heavy truck, or vehicle with bulky cargo or a trailer.

pair.old: The vehicle or powered two-wheeler in front has no taillights or its, taillights are in unusual positions
pair.new: The vehicle or motorcycle ahead does not have tail lights or has an unusual tail light configuration.

pair.old: The other vehicle’s ground clearance is extremely low or high
pair.new: The vehicle’s ground clearance is extremely low or high.

pair.old: A vehicle, powered two-wheeler, pedestrian or cyclist suddenly cuts in front
pair.new: A vehicle, motorcycle, pedestrian, or cyclist suddenly crosses ahead of your vehicle.

pair.old: The front radar or its surroundings are is impacted, damaged, or out of position
pair.new: The front radar sensor is damaged or out of position.

pair.old: The temperature around the front radar is high or low
pair.new: The temperature near the front radar is high or low.
The temperature near the front radar is high or low.

pair.old: Driving in areas where there are few vehicles or structures (for example, desert, meadow, etc.)
pair.new: You are driving in areas where there are few vehicles or structures, such as a desert or meadow.

pair.old: Driving near areas containing metal substances, such as a construction zone, railroad, metal plates on the road.
pair.new: You are driving near areas with a lot of metal, such as a construction zone, railroad, or road with metal plates.

pair.old: The cyclist in front is on a bicycle made of material that does not reflect on the front radar’s signal
pair.new: A vehicle or bicycle ahead is made of a material that does not reflect the front radar’s signal well.

pair.old: The view of the vehicle or powered two-wheeler is suddenly blocked
pair.new: Your view of the vehicle or motorcycle ahead is suddenly obscured.

pair.old: The vehicle or powered two-wheeler in front suddenly changes a lane or suddenly reduces speed
pair.new: The vehicle or motorcycle ahead suddenly changes lanes or reduces speed.

pair.old: You are on a roundabout and the vehicle or powered two-wheeler in front is not detected
pair.new: You are driving in a traffic circle and the curvature of the road prevents detection of the vehicle or motorcycle ahead.

pair.old: The vehicle or powered two-wheeler in front is on a steep hill, out of range of the camera and radar
pair.new: You are driving on a steep hill, which puts the vehicle or motorcycle ahead out of range of your vehicle’s camera or radar.

pair.old: The illustration above shows the kinds of images that the front view camera and front radar are capable of detecting as a vehicle, powered two-wheeler, pedestrian and cyclist. The system may not detect a pedestrian or cyclist that is:
pair.new: The following illustration shows the kinds of images of a vehicle, motorcycle, pedestrian, or cyclist that the front view camera and front radar can detect:

pair.old: leaning over or is not fully walking upright
pair.new: Leaning over or not walking fully upright.

pair.old: The pedestrian or cyclist is wearing clothing or equipment that makes it difficult to detect
pair.new: Wearing clothing or equipment that makes them difficult to detect.

pair.old: The pedestrian or cyclist in front is moving very quickly
pair.new: Moving very quickly ahead of your vehicle.

pair.old: The pedestrian or cyclist in front is short or is posing a low posture
pair.new: Short or crouching.

pair.old: The pedestrian or cyclist in front has impaired mobility or moving intersected with the driving direction
pair.new: Moving opposite of the flow of traffic or has impaired mobility.

pair.old: There is a group of pedestrians, cyclists or a large crowd in front
pair.new: Part of a large crowd ahead of your vehicle.

pair.old: The pedestrian or cyclist is difficult to distinguish from the similarly shaped structure in the surroundings
pair.new: Difficult to distinguish from their surroundings.

pair.old: When driving in the following places
pair.new: The system may not detect a pedestrian or cyclist ahead when you are driving:

pair.old: Driving through steam, smoke, or shadow
pair.new: Through steam, smoke, or shadow.

pair.old: Driving through a tunnel or iron bridge
pair.new: Through a tunnel or covered bridge.

pair.old: Driving in large areas where there are few vehicles or structures (i.e., desert, meadow, suburb, etc.)
pair.new: In large areas where there are few vehicles or structures, such as a desert, field, or suburban area.

pair.old: Driving in a car park
pair.new: In a parking lot.

pair.old: Driving through tollgate, construction areas, partially paved roads, bumpy roads, speed bumps, etc.
pair.new: Through a tollgate, construction area, or rough or bumpy terrain.

pair.old: Driving near areas containing metal substances, such as a construction zone, railroad, etc.
pair.new: Near areas with a lot of metal, like a construction zone or railroad.
You are driving near areas with a lot of metal, such as a construction zone, railroad, or road with metal plates.

pair.old: Driving on an inclined road, curved road, etc.
pair.new: On a steep or curving road.
On a steep or curving road.

pair.old: Driving through a roadside with trees or street lights
pair.new: Through a roadside with trees or street lights.

pair.old: Driving through a narrow road where trees or grass are overgrown
pair.new: Along a narrow road with dense trees or overgrowth.

pair.old: There is interference by electromagnetic waves, such as driving in an area with strong radio waves or electrical noise
pair.new: Through electromagnetic waves, such an area with strong radio waves or electrical noise.

old.old: A snow chain, spare tire, or wheel of a different size is installed
With snow chains or a spare tire.

new.new: With snow chains or a spare tire.

pair.old: While driving over a certain speed, Lane Keeping Assist can detect lane markings (or road edges) and may warn you if your vehicle leaves the lane without using the turn signal and may assist with steering to prevent your vehicle departing from its travel lane.
pair.new: When you drive over a certain speed, Lane Keeping Assist can detect lane markings or road edges. Lane Keeping Assist may assist with steering to prevent the vehicle from leaving its lane, and may warn you if your vehicle leaves the lane without you using the turn signal.

pair.old: The front view camera is used as a detecting sensor to detect lane markings (or road edges).
pair.new: The front view camera detects lane markings or road edges.

pair.old: With the vehicle on, select SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceDriving Safety on the infotainment system.
pair.new: While the vehicle is ON, tap SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceDriving Safety on the infotainment system screen to change the Lane Safety settings.

pair.old: Lane Safety: When lane departure is detected, the system assists with steering to prevent leaving the lane, and if lane departure occurs, it alerts the driver with an audible sound and steering wheel vibration. If Lane safety is deselected, the indicator light () will be turned off.
pair.new: Lane Safety: When a potential lane departure is detected, the system assists with steering to prevent you from leaving the lane. If you leave the lane, the system alerts you with an audible sound and steering wheel vibration. If you deselect Lane Safety, the indicator light () turns OFF.

pair.old: When the trailer’s connector is plugged into your vehicle, Lane Keeping Assist automatically turns off. In this case, you cannot get help from Lane Keeping Assist. Pay extra attention when you drive when the function is inactive. (If a Kia genuine Trailer Kit that can determine whether a trailer is connected is used)
pair.new: When using a Kia genuine Trailer Kit and the connector is plugged into your vehicle, Lane Keeping Assist automatically turns OFF. In this case, you cannot get help from Lane Keeping Assist. Pay extra attention when you drive while the function is inactive. (A Kia genuine Trailer Kit can determine whether a trailer is connected.)

pair.old: By pressing the Lane Driving Assist button (), the Lane Keeping Assist will be turned off, and it will also deactivate the Lane Safety.
pair.new: Pressing the Lane Driving Assist button () turns OFF Lane Keeping Assist and deactivates Lane Safety.

pair.old: Warning Volume: Adjusts the volume of the warning sound.
pair.new: Warning Volume: Adjusts the volume of the warning sound. If you turn OFF the Warning Volume setting, the function may still warn you at a low volume for your safety.
Warning Volume: Adjusts the volume of the warning sound. If you turn OFF the Warning Volume setting, the function may still warn you at a low volume for your safety.
Warning Volume: Adjusts the volume of the warning sound. If you turn OFF the Warning Volume setting, the function may still warn you at a low volume for your safety.

pair.new: Lane Safety Audible Warning Off: Turns OFF the Lane Safety Audible Warning, even when both Warning Volume and Haptic Warning are ON.

The Warning Volume and Haptic Warning cannot be turned OFF at the same time. When you turn OFF one warning, the other turns ON. The Lane Safety Audible Warning Off can be set when both the Warning Volume and the Haptic Warning are on

new.new: The Warning Volume and Haptic Warning cannot be turned OFF at the same time. When you turn OFF one warning, the other turns ON.

pair.old: Turning Lane Keeping Assist On/Off
pair.new: Turning Lane Keeping Assist ON and OFF

pair.old: With the vehicle on, press and hold the Lane Driving Assist () button located on the steering wheel to turn on and off Lane Keeping Assist.
pair.new: To turn Lane Keeping Assist ON or OFF, press and hold the Lane Driving Assist () button on the steering wheel while the vehicle is ON.

pair.old: The gray or green indicator () on the cluster will light up if you turn on Lane Keeping Assist.
pair.new: The gray or green indicator () appears on the instrument cluster if you turn ON Lane Keeping Assist.

pair.old: If Lane Keeping Assist is standby, the gray indicator () will appear on the cluster.
pair.new: If Lane Keeping Assist is on standby, the gray indicator () appears on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: if Lane Keeping Assist is ready to operate, the green indicator () will appear on the cluster.
pair.new: If Lane Keeping Assist is ready to operate, the green indicator () appears on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: The Lane Keeping Assist function is warned and controlled in the following way.
pair.new: The Lane Keeping Assist function has the following warnings and controls:

pair.old: Lane departure warning is issued through a green indicator light on the cluster, a blinking indicator in the direction you departed from, a warning sound, and the steering wheel will vibrate.
pair.new: The Lane Departure Warning is issued through a green indicator light on the instrument cluster, a blinking indicator in the direction you departed from, an audible warning sound, and steering wheel vibration.

pair.old: Lane Departure Warning will be activated in the following conditions.
pair.new: The Lane Departure Warning activates when your speed is approximately 40-120 mph (60-200 km/h).

pair.old: The green () indicator light will blink on the cluster, and the steering wheel makes adjustments to keep vehicle inside the lane.
pair.new: The green indicator () light blinks on the instrument cluster, and the steering wheel makes adjustments to keep the vehicle inside the lane.

pair.old: Lane Keeping Assist will be activated in the following conditions.
pair.new: Lane Keeping Assist activates when your speed is approximately 40-120 mph (60-200 km/h).

pair.old: If the driver takes their hands off the steering wheel for several seconds, a warning message will appear on the cluster, and an audible warning will sound in stages.
pair.new: If you take your hands off the steering wheel for several seconds, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster, and an audible warning sounds in stages.

pair.old: The steering wheel may not be assisted if the steering wheel is held very tight or the steering wheel is steered over a certain degree.
pair.new: Steering assistance may not activate if you hold the steering wheel very tight or steer over a certain degree.
Lane Following Assist may not assist with steering if you hold the steering wheel very tight or steer it over a certain degree.

pair.old: Lane Keeping Assist does not operate at all times. It is the responsibility of the driver to safely steer the vehicle and to maintain the vehicle in its lane.
pair.new: Lane Keeping Assist does not operate at all times. It is your responsibility to safely steer the vehicle and maintain the vehicle in its lane.

pair.old: If the steering wheel is held very lightly, the hands-off warning message may appear because Lane Keeping Assist may not recognize that the driver has their hands on the steering wheel.
pair.new: If you hold the steering wheel very lightly, the hands-off warning message may appear because Lane Keeping Assist may not recognize that you have your hands on the steering wheel.

pair.old: Even though the steering is assisted by Lane Keeping Assist, the driver may control the steering wheel.
pair.new: Even when the steering is assisted by Lane Keeping Assist, you may still control the steering wheel.

pair.old: The steering wheel may feel heavier or lighter when the steering wheel is assisted by Lane Keeping Assist than when it is not.
pair.new: The steering wheel may feel heavier or lighter than usual when the steering wheel is being assisted by Lane Keeping Assist.

pair.old: If you select Highway Lane Change Assist, the green lane will appear on the cluster. (if equipped)
pair.new: If you select Highway Lane Change Assist, the green lane appears on the instrument cluster (if equipped).

pair.old: The images and colors in the instrument cluster may differ depending on the cluster type or theme selected from the settings menu.
pair.new: The images or colors may display differently depending on the specifications of the instrument cluster or theme.

The images or colors may display differently depending on the specifications of the instrument cluster or theme.
