pair.new: For more information about the instrument cluster, refer to .

pair.old: When Lane Keeping Assist is not working properly, a warning message will appear, the Master warning light (), and the yellow () indicator light will appear on the instrument cluster.
pair.new: When Lane Keeping Assist is not working properly, the Master warning light (), and the yellow indicator () light appear on the instrument cluster, along with a warning message, which lasts for several seconds.

pair.old: If this occurs, have the function inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If a warning message appears, have the Lane Keeping Assist function inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: If foreign materials such as snow or rain block the sensors or the windshield where the front view camera is located, the detecting performance may be reduced, resulting in Lane Keeping Assist temporarily limited or disabled.
pair.new: If foreign material (such as snow or rain) obscure the sensors or the windshield where the front view camera is located, it may affect detection and render Lane Keeping Assist temporarily limited or disabled.

pair.old: In this case, a warning message is displayed with the Master warning light () and the Lane safety warning lights () on the cluster. This is normal operation.
pair.new: In this case, a warning message displays with the Master warning light () and the Lane safety warning lights () on the instrument cluster. This is normal operation.

pair.old: Lane Keeping Assist will operate properly after cleaning snow, rain or foreign materials. Always keep it clean.
pair.new: Lane Keeping Assist will operate properly after you clean the snow, rain, or foreign material from the sensor. Always keep the sensor clean.

pair.old: If Lane Keeping Assist still does not operate properly after cleaning foreign materials (snow, rain, etc.) or removing obstructions (including trailer, carrier, etc. from the rear bumper), have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If Lane Keeping Assist still does not operate properly after you clean foreign materials (such as snow or rain) from the sensor or remove obstructions (such as a trailer or carrier) from the rear bumper, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Even though a warning message or warning light does not appear on the cluster, Lane Keeping Assist may not properly operate.
pair.new: Even when a warning message or warning light does not appear on the instrument cluster, Lane Keeping Assist may not operate properly.

pair.old: Even after starting the vehicle again, Lane Keeping Assist may not function properly when the obstruction or malfunction condition persists.
pair.new: Even after your start the vehicle again, Lane Keeping Assist may not function properly if the obstruction or malfunction persists.

pair.old: Lane Keeping Assist may not operate properly or may operate unexpectedly under the following circumstances:
pair.new: Lane Keeping Assist may operate abnormally or unexpectedly under the following circumstances:

pair.old: The lane markings (or road edges) are covered with rain, snow, dirt, oil, etc.
pair.new: The lane markings (or road edges) are covered with rain, snow, dirt, oil, and so on.

pair.old: The color of the lane marking (or road edges) is not distinguishable from the road
pair.new: The color of the lane marking (or road edge) is not distinguishable from the road.

pair.old: There are markings (or road edges) on the road near the lane or the markings (or road edges) on the road look similar to the lane markings (or road edges)
pair.new: There are markings (or road edges) near the lane, or the markings (or road edges) look similar to lane markings.

pair.old: The lane marking (or road edges) is indistinct or damaged
pair.new: The lane marking (or road edge) is indistinct or damaged.

pair.old: When the shadow of objects around the road (central reservation, crash barrier, noise barrier, surrounding bushes, etc.) or the shadow of a vehicle covers the lane.
pair.new: The shadow of objects around the road (central reservation, crash barrier, noise barrier, surrounding bushes, and so on) or the shadow of a vehicle obscures the lane.

pair.old: The lane number increases or decreases, or the lane markings (or road edges) are crossing
pair.new: The lane number increases or decreases, or the lane markings (or road edges) cross.

pair.old: There are more than two lane markings (or road edges) on the road
pair.new: There are more than two lane markings (or road edges).

pair.old: The lane markings (or road edges) are complicated or a structure substitutes for the lines, such as a construction area
pair.new: The lane markings (or road edges) are complicated, or a structure (such as in a construction area) substitutes for the lines.

pair.old: There are road markings, such as zigzag lanes, crosswalk markings and road signs
pair.new: There are road markings such as crosswalk markings or road signs.

pair.old: The lane suddenly disappears, such as at the intersection
pair.new: The lane suddenly disappears, such as at an intersection.

pair.old: The lane (or road width) is very wide or narrow
pair.new: The lane or road is very wide or narrow.

pair.old: There is a road edge without a lane
pair.new: There is a road edge without a lane.

pair.old: There is a boundary structure in the roadway, such as a tollgate, sidewalk, curb, etc.
pair.new: There is a boundary structure in the roadway, such as a tollgate, sidewalk, curb, and so on.

pair.old: The distance to the front vehicle is extremely short or the vehicle in front is covering the lane marking (or road edge)
pair.new: You are driving extremely close to the vehicle ahead, or it is covering the lane marking (or road edge).

pair.old: For more details on the limitations of the front view camera, refer to .
pair.new: For more information about the limitations of the front view camera, refer to .
For more information about the limitations of the front view camera, refer to .

pair.old: The driver should hold the responsibility to safely drive and control the vehicle. Do not solely rely on Lane Keeping Assist and drive dangerously.
pair.new: You are responsible for driving safely and controlling the vehicle. Do not rely solely on Lane Keeping Assist or drive dangerously.

pair.old: The operation of Lane Keeping Assist can be canceled or not work properly depending on road conditions and surroundings. Always be cautious while driving.
pair.new: Lane Keeping Assist may stop operating or may not work properly depending on road conditions and surroundings. Always be cautious while driving.

pair.old: Refer to . if the lane is not detected properly.
pair.new: Refer to if the lane is not detected properly.

pair.old: If the vehicle is driven at high speed, the steering wheel will not be controlled. The driver must always follow the speed limit when using Lane Keeping Assist.
pair.new: If you drive the vehicle at a high speed, the steering wheel will not be controlled. Always follow the speed limit when using Lane Keeping Assist.

pair.old: If any other function’s warning message is displayed or audible warning is generated, Lane Keeping Assist warning message may not be displayed and audible warning may not be generated.
pair.new: Lane Keeping Assist may not display a warning message or generate an audible warning if any other function’s warning message is displayed or audible warning is generated at the same time.

pair.old: You may not hear the warning sound of Lane Keeping Assist if the surrounding is noisy. Adjust the vehicle volume moderately and always pay attention to the surrounding.
pair.new: You may not hear the warning sound of Lane Keeping Assist if the surroundings are noisy. Moderately adjust the vehicle volume and always pay attention to the surroundings.

pair.old: The vehicle is not driven in the center of the lane when Lane Keeping Assist is turned on or right after changing a lane.
pair.new: The vehicle is not driven in the center of the lane or has just changed lanes while Lane Keeping Assist is ON.

pair.old: The vehicle is turning quickly on a curved road.
pair.new: The vehicle turns quickly on a curved road.

pair.old: The vehicle makes sharp lane changes.
pair.new: The vehicle changes lanes suddenly.

pair.old: Loading freight exceeding the maximum load or placing freight unevenly may undermine driving safety. This may also prevent Lane Keeping Assist from operating properly.
pair.new: Loading freight exceeding the maximum load or placing freight unevenly may impede safe driving. This may also prevent Lane Keeping Assist from operating properly.

pair.old: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist can detect approaching vehicles in the driver’s blind spot areas and warn you of a possible collision with a warning light and a warning sound. If there is a collision risk when exiting a parallel space, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may assist with braking your vehicle to help avoid a collision.
pair.new: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist can detect approaching vehicles in your blind spots and warn you of a possible collision using a warning light and an audible warning sound. If there is a collision risk when changing lanes, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may assist with braking your vehicle to help you avoid a collision.

pair.old: When you are driving forward out of a parking space, if Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist determines that there is a collision risk with an approaching vehicle in the blind spot, it can help avoid collision by applying the brake.
pair.new: When you are pulling out of a parking space, if Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist determines that there is a collision risk with an approaching vehicle in your blind spot, it can help you avoid a collision by applying the brakes.

pair.old: The detecting range may vary depending on the speed of your vehicle. Even if there is a vehicle in the blind spot area, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not warn you when you pass by at high speeds.
pair.new: Detection range may vary depending on the speed of your vehicle. Even if there is a vehicle in your blind spot area, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not warn you when passing at high speeds.

pair.old: Warning timing may vary depending on the speed of the vehicle approaching at high speed.
pair.new: The timing of the warning may vary if a vehicle is approaching at a high speed.

pair.old: Never disassemble the detecting sensor assembly, or cause any damage to it.
pair.new: Never disassemble or damage the detection sensor assembly.

pair.old: If the detecting sensor or near the sensor has been damaged or impacted in any way, even though a warning message does not appear on the cluster, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly. Have the function inspected by an authorized Kia dealer. Visit an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the detection sensor or the area near the sensor has been damaged or impacted in any way, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly, even if a warning message does not appear on the instrument cluster. Have the function inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: The genuine Kia rear bumpers which the Rear corner radar sensors are mounted are parts with quality and performance ensured. If arbitrarily applying paint on or changing the bumper, the Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not function properly. Use only Kia Genuine Parts or those of an equivalent standard with proven quality and performance to repair or replace the bumper.
pair.new: The genuine Kia rear bumpers where the rear corner radar sensors are mounted are parts with quality and performance ensured. If you arbitrarily apply paint to or change the bumper, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not function properly. Use only Kia Genuine Parts (or those of an equivalent standard with proven quality and performance) to repair or replace the bumper.

pair.old: Do not apply license plate frame or objects, such as a bumper sticker, film or a bumper guard near the rear corner radar.
pair.new: Do not apply a license plate frame, or objects such as a bumper sticker, film, or bumper guard, near the rear corner radar.

pair.old: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not work properly if the bumper has been replaced, or the surroundings of the rear corner radar has been damaged or paint has been applied.
pair.new: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not work properly if the bumper is replaced, the surroundings of the rear corner radar are damaged, or paint is applied.

pair.old: If a trailer, carrier, etc., is installed, it may adversely affect the performance of the rear corner radar or Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate.
pair.new: If you install a trailer, carrier, and so on, it may adversely affect the performance of the rear corner radar, or Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate.

pair.old: With the vehicle on, select SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceBlind-Spot Safety on the infotainment system.
pair.new: While the vehicle is ON, tap SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceBlind-Spot Safety on the infotainment system screen.

pair.old: Blind-Spot Safety: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist will warn and braking assist will be applied depending on the collision risk levels.
pair.new: Blind-Spot Safety: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist warns you and assists with braking depending on the collision risk levels.

pair.old: A: Blind-Spot Safety System is Off
pair.new: A: Blind-Spot Safety System is Off

pair.old: When activating Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist or restarting the vehicle with this function activated, the warning light on the outside rear-view mirror will appear for approximately 3 seconds.
pair.new: When you activate Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist or restart the vehicle with this function activated, the warning light on the outside rearview mirror appears for approximately 3 seconds.

pair.old: When the vehicle is restarted with Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist inactivated, a warning message will appear on the cluster.
pair.new: When you restart the vehicle without Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist activated, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: If Blind-Spot Safety is deselected, the driver should always be aware of the surroundings and drive safely.
pair.new: If you turn OFF Blind-Spot Safety, always be aware of your surroundings and drive safely.

pair.old: When the trailer’s connector is plugged into your vehicle, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist automatically turns off. In this case, you cannot get help from Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist. Pay extra attention when you drive when the function is inactive. (If a Kia genuine Trailer Kit that can determine whether a trailer is connected is used)
pair.new: When using a Kia genuine Trailer Kit and the connector is plugged into your vehicle, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist automatically turns OFF. In this case, you cannot get help from Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist. Pay extra attention when you drive while the function is inactive. (A Kia genuine Trailer Kit can determine whether a trailer is connected.)

pair.old: If the vehicle is restarted, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist will maintain the last setting.
pair.new: If you restart the vehicle, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist maintains its last setting.

pair.old: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist will warn and control as following operation.
pair.new: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist operates as follows:

pair.old: The warning light on the cluster, outside rear-view mirror and head-up display (if equipped) appear when the vehicle on both lanes is detected from the rear.
pair.new: The warning light on the instrument cluster, outside rearview mirror, and head-up display (if equipped) appear when a vehicle in either of the surrounding lanes is detected from the rear.

pair.old: A vehicle is detected in the following conditions.
pair.new: A vehicle is detected in the following conditions:

pair.old: Your driving speed: Above 12 mph (20 km/h)
pair.new: Your speed is above 12 mph (20 km/h).

pair.old: The speed of the vehicle in your blind spot area: Above 7 mph (10 km/h)
pair.new: The speed of the vehicle in your blind spot is above 7 mph (10 km/h).
The speed of the vehicle in your blind spot is above 7 mph (10 km/h).

pair.old: With the vehicle detection in use, Collision warning will alert the driver when the turn signal is activated to make a lane change with an adjacent car in the blind spot area.
pair.new: With vehicle detection in use, Collision Warning will alert you when you activate the turn signal to make a lane change while there is a car in your blind spot.

pair.old: Collision warning will alert the driver with the warning light on the cluster, outside rear-view mirror and head-up display (if equipped), audible warning and steering wheel vibration.
pair.new: Collision warning will alert you with the warning lights on the instrument cluster, outside rearview mirror, and heads-up display (if equipped), an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration.

pair.old: When the turn signal is turned off or you move away from the lane, the collision warning will be canceled and the function will return to Vehicle detection state.
pair.new: When you turn OFF the turn signal or move away from the lane, the collision warning cancels, and the function returns to vehicle detection state.

pair.old: Collision Warning operate in the following conditions.
pair.new: Collision Warning operates under the following conditions:

pair.old: Your driving speed: Above 25 mph (40 km/h)
pair.new: Your speed is above 25 mph (40 km/h).

pair.old: The detecting range of the front corner radar or rear corner radar is determined by a standard road width, therefore, on a narrow road, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may detect other vehicles two lanes over and warn you. In contrast, on a wide road, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not be able to detect a vehicle driving in the next lane and may not warn you.
pair.new: The detecting range of the front or rear corner radar is determined by a standard road width. Therefore, on a narrow road, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may detect other vehicles 2 lanes over and warn you. In contrast, on a wide road, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not be able to detect a vehicle driving in the next lane and may not warn you.

pair.old: When the hazard warning flasher is on, the collision warning by the turn signal will not operate.
pair.new: When the hazard warning flasher is ON, the collision warning next to the turn signal will not operate.

pair.old: Images or colors may be displayed differently depending on the instrument cluster specifications or theme.
pair.new: The images or colors may display differently depending on the specifications of the instrument cluster or theme.
The images or colors may display differently depending on the specifications of the instrument cluster or theme.

pair.old: The warning light on the outside rear-view mirror, head-up display (if equipped), an audible warning and the steering wheel vibration will warn the driver of a collision. It assists in braking control to prevent a collision with a vehicle approaching from the blind spot area.
pair.new: The warning light on the outside rearview mirror, head-up display (if equipped), an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration warn you of a collision. This function assists in braking control to prevent a collision with a vehicle approaching from your blind spot.

pair.old: Collision-Avoidance Assist will be activated in the following conditions.
pair.new: Collision-Avoidance Assist activates in the following conditions:

pair.old: Your driving speed: Below 2 mph (3 km/h)
pair.new: Your speed is below 2 mph (3 km/h).

pair.old: Speed of the vehicle in your blind spot area: Above 3 mph (5 km/h)
pair.new: The speed of the vehicle in your blind spot is above 3 mph (5 km/h).

pair.old: When the vehicle is stopped due to emergency braking, a warning message will appear on the instrument cluster. For your safety, the driver should depress the brake pedal immediately and check the surroundings.
pair.new: When the vehicle is stopped due to Emergency Braking, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster. For your safety, engage the brake pedal immediately and check the surroundings.

pair.old: If any other function’s warning message is displayed or audible warning is generated, BCA warning message may not be displayed and audible warning may not be generated.
pair.new: Blind-Spot Collision Avoidance may not display a warning message or generate an audible warning if any other function’s warning message is displayed or audible warning is generated at the same time.

pair.old: You may not hear the warning sound of Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist if the surroundings are noisy. Always pay attention and keep the vehicle volume at a moderate level.
pair.new: You may not hear the warning sound of Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist if your surroundings are noisy. Always pay attention and keep volume within the vehicle at a moderate level.

pair.old: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate if the driver applies the brake pedal to avoid collision.
pair.new: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate if you engage the brake pedal to avoid a collision.

pair.old: When Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist is operating, braking control by the function will automatically cancel when the driver excessively depresses the accelerator pedal or sharply steers the vehicle.
pair.new: When Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist is operating, the function’s braking control cancels automatically when you excessively engage the accelerator pedal or sharply steer the vehicle.

pair.old: During Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist operation, the vehicle may stop suddenly injuring passengers and shifting loose objects. Always have the seat belt on and keep loose objects secured.
pair.new: During Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist operation, the vehicle may stop suddenly, injuring passengers and shifting loose objects. Always fasten the seat belts and keep loose objects secured.

pair.old: Even if there is a problem with Blind- Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist, the vehicle’s basic braking performance will operate properly.
pair.new: If there is a problem with Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist, the vehicle’s basic braking performance will operate properly.

pair.old: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may warn the driver late or may not warn the driver depending on the road and driving conditions.
pair.new: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may warn you late or not at all, depending on the road and driving conditions.

pair.old: The responsibility for safe operation lies with the driver. Do not rely solely on Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist for driving and always check the surrounding conditions directly and drive safely.
pair.new: You are responsible for safe operation of the vehicle. Do not rely solely on Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist for driving. Always check your surroundings and drive safely.

pair.old: Never operate Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist on people, animal, objects, etc. It may cause serious injury or death.
pair.new: Never rely solely on Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance to avoid a collision with people, animals, or objects. It may cause serious injury or death.

pair.old: The ESC (Electronic Stability Control) warning light is on
pair.new: The ESC warning light is illuminated.
The ESC warning light is illuminated.
The ESC warning light is illuminated.

pair.old: ESC (Electronic Stability Control) is engaged in a different function
pair.new: ESC is engaged in a different function.
ESC is engaged in a different function.

pair.old: When Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist is not working properly, a warning message will appear on the cluster for several seconds, and the Master warning light () will appear on the cluster.
pair.new: When Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist is not working properly, the Master warning light () appears on the instrument cluster, along with a warning message, which lasts for several seconds. If this occurs, have Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: When the outside rear-view mirror warning light is not working properly, a warning message will appear on the cluster for several seconds, and the Master warning light () will appear on the cluster. If this occurs, have Blind Spot Collision Avoidance Assist inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: When the outside rearview mirror warning light is not working properly, the Master warning light () appears on the instrument cluster, along with a warning message, which lasts for several seconds. If this occurs, have Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Covering the rear bumper around the rear corner radar or sensor with foreign materials, such as snow or rain, or installing a trailer or carrier can reduce the detecting performance, resulting in Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist temporarily limited or disabled.
pair.new: When foreign material (such as snow or rain) covers the rear bumper near the rear corner radar or sensor, or when you install a trailer or carrier, it can reduce the detection performance and temporarily limit or disable Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist.

pair.old: A warning message and the Master warning light () are displayed on the cluster display, but it does not indicate a malfunction of Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist.
pair.new: A warning message and the Master warning light () display on the instrument cluster. This does not indicate a Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist malfunction.

pair.old: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist will operate properly when such foreign material or trailer, etc., is removed, and then the vehicle is restarted.
pair.new: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist operates properly after you remove foreign material (such as snow or rain) from the sensor or obstructions (such as a trailer or carrier) from the rear bumper then restart the vehicle.

pair.old: If Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist does not operate properly after it is removed, have Blind Spot Collision Avoidance Assist inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist still does not operate properly after you remove foreign material (such as snow or rain) from the sensor or obstructions (such as a trailer or carrier) from the rear bumper, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Even though the warning message does not appear on the cluster, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not properly operate.
pair.new: Even when a warning message does not appear on the instrument cluster, Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly.

pair.old: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not properly operate in an area (for example, open terrain) where any objects are not detected right after the vehicle is turned on, or when the detecting sensor is blocked with foreign material right after the vehicle is turned on.
pair.new: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly in an area (such as ample, open terrain) where any objects are not detected right after the you turn ON the vehicle, or when the detection sensor is blocked with foreign material right after you turn ON the vehicle.

pair.old: Turn off Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist to install or remove a trailer, carrier, or another attachment. Turn on Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist when finished.
pair.new: Turn OFF Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist to install or remove a trailer, carrier, or other attachment. Turn ON Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist when you are finished.

pair.old: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly, or it may operate unexpectedly under the following circumstances:
pair.new: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may operate abnormally or unexpectedly under the following circumstances:

pair.old: There is inclement weather, such as heavy snow and rain.
pair.new: There is inclement weather, such as heavy snow or rain.

pair.old: The detecting sensor is covered with snow, rain, dirt, etc.
pair.new: The detection sensor is covered with snow, rain, dirt, and so on.

pair.old: The ambient temperature around the detecting sensor is high or low.
pair.new: The ambient temperature around the detection sensor is very high or low.

pair.old: The detecting sensor is blocked while driving near a vehicle, pillar, or wall.
pair.new: The detection sensor is blocked while you drive near a vehicle, pillar, or wall.

pair.old: Driving on a highway ramp or through a tollgate.
pair.new: You are driving on a highway ramp or through a tollgate.

pair.old: The pavement abnormally contains metallic components (such as subway construction).
pair.new: The pavement contains abnormally metallic components (such as debris left from subway construction).

pair.old: There are fixed objects near the vehicle, such as sound barriers, guardrails, central dividers, entry barriers, street lights, signs, tunnels, walls, etc.
pair.new: There are fixed objects near the vehicle, such as sound barriers, guardrails, central dividers, entry barriers, street lights, signs, tunnels, walls, and so on.

pair.old: Driving on a narrow road with overgrown foliage.
pair.new: You are driving on a narrow road with overgrown foliage.

pair.old: Driving in wide open areas where there are few vehicles or structures
pair.new: You are driving in wide open areas where there are few vehicles or structures.
You are driving in wide open areas where there are few vehicles or structures.

pair.old: Driving on a wet road surface.
pair.new: You are driving on a wet road surface.

pair.old: Other vehicles are moving very close behind you, or are passing.
pair.new: Other vehicles are moving very close behind you or passing you.

pair.old: Other vehicles are moving very fast when passing.
pair.new: Other vehicles are moving very fast when passing you.

pair.old: You change lane.
pair.new: You change lanes.

pair.old: Your accelerate at the same time as a vehicle next to you.
pair.new: You and a vehicle next to you accelerate simultaneously.

pair.old: A vehicle in the next lane moves two lanes away from you, or when the vehicle two lanes away moves to the next lane from you
pair.new: A vehicle in the next lane moves 2 lanes away from you, or the vehicle 2 lanes away moves to the lane next to you.

pair.old: A trailer or carrier is installed around the rear corner radar
pair.new: You have installed a trailer or carrier near the rear corner radar.
You have installed a trailer or carrier near the rear corner radar.

pair.old: The bumper around the rear corner radar is covered with objects, such as a bumper sticker, bike rack, etc.
pair.new: You cover the bumper near the rear corner radar with objects such as a bumper sticker, bike rack, and so on.

pair.old: The bumper around the rear corner radar is impacted, damaged or the radar is out of position
pair.new: The bumper near the front or rear corner radar is damaged, or the radar is out of position.

pair.old: Your vehicle height is low or high due to heavy loads, abnormal tire pressure, etc.
pair.new: Your vehicle height is low or high due to heavy loads, abnormal tire pressure, and so on.
Your vehicle height is low or high due to heavy loads, abnormal tire pressure, and so on.

pair.old: When the following objects are detected:
pair.new: The following objects are detected:

pair.old: A motorcycle or bicycle is detected
pair.new: A motorcycle or bicycle.

pair.old: A vehicle such as a flat-bed trailer is detected
pair.new: A vehicle towing a flat-bed trailer.

pair.old: A large vehicle such as a bus or truck is detected
pair.new: A large vehicle, such as a bus or truck.

pair.old: A moving obstacle such as a pedestrian, animal, shopping cart or baby carriage is detected
pair.new: A moving obstacle, such as a pedestrian, animal, shopping cart, or stroller.

pair.old: A vehicle with low height such as a sports car is detected
pair.new: A shorter vehicle such as a sports car.

pair.old: The vehicle severely vibrates while driving over a bumpy road or pothole.
pair.new: The vehicle vibrates intensely while you drive over a bumpy road or pothole.

pair.old: Driving on a slippery surface such as snow, water, ice, etc.
pair.new: You drive on a slippery surface such as snow, water, ice, and so on.

pair.old: Tire pressure is low or is damaged
pair.new: Tire pressure is low or tires are damaged.

pair.old: The braking system has been modified
pair.new: The braking system has been modified.

pair.old: When steering abruptly
pair.new: You steer abruptly.

pair.old: Always pay attention to road and driving conditions.
pair.new: Always pay attention to the road and driving conditions.

pair.old: Driving where the road is merging/dividing
pair.new: Driving where the road is merging or dividing

pair.old: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly when driving where the road merges or divides. The function may not detect the vehicle in the next lane.
pair.new: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly when you drive where the road merges or divides. The function may not detect the vehicle in the next lane. Always pay attention to road and driving conditions.

pair.old: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly if interfered by strong electromagnetic waves.
pair.new: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly in areas with strong electromagnetic interference.

pair.old: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate for approximately 3 seconds after the vehicle is started, or the front view camera or rear corner radars are initialized.
pair.new: Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist may not activate for approximately 3 seconds after you start the vehicle or initialize the front view camera or rear corner radars.

pair.old: While your vehicle is stopped, and if Safe Exit Warning detects a vehicle approaching the rear corner of your vehicle and a passenger opens a door, Safe Exit Warning may warn you with a warning message and a warning sound to help avoid a collision.
pair.new: While your vehicle is stopped, Safe Exit Warning may help you avoid a collision using a warning message and warning sound if it detects a vehicle approaching the rear corner of your vehicle and a passenger opens a door.

While your vehicle is stopped, Safe Exit Assist may help you avoid a collision using a warning message and warning sound if it detects a vehicle approaching the rear corner of your vehicle and a passenger opens a door.

pair.old: Warning timing may vary depending on the speed of the approaching vehicle.
pair.new: The timing of the warning may vary, depending on the speed of the approaching vehicle.

pair.new: For more information about the rear corner radars, refer to .

While the vehicle is ON, tap SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceDriving SafetySafe Exit on the infotainment system screen.

pair.old: If Safe Exit is deselected, Safe Exit Warning cannot warn you. The driver should always be aware of unexpected and sudden situations that may occur.
pair.new: If you deselect Safe Exit, Safe Exit Warning cannot warn you. You should always be aware of your surroundings.

pair.old: When the trailer’s connector is plugged into your vehicle, Safe Exit Warning automatically turns off. In this case, you cannot get help from Safe Exit Warning. Pay extra attention when you drive when the function is inactive. (If a Kia genuine Trailer Kit that can determine whether a trailer is connected is used)

pair.old: If the vehicle is restarted, Safe Exit Warning will maintain the last setting.
pair.new: If you restart the vehicle, Safe Exit Warning maintains its last setting.

pair.old: The warning light on the outside rear view mirror will blink and a warning message will appear on the cluster, and an audible warning will sound.
pair.new: The warning light on the outside rearview mirror blinks, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster, and an audible warning sounds.

pair.old: Safe Exit Warning will warn under the following circumstances:
pair.new: Safe Exit Warning warns you under the following circumstances:

pair.old: Your driving speed: below 2 mph (3 km/h)
pair.new: Your speed is below 2 mph (3 km/h)

pair.old: The speed of the approaching vehicle from the rear: above 4 mph (6 km/h)
pair.new: The speed of the approaching vehicle from the rear is above 4 mph (6 km/h)

pair.old: If any other function’s warning message is displayed or audible warning is generated, Safe Exit Warning message may not be displayed and audible warning may not be generated.
pair.new: Safe Exit Warning may not display a warning message or generate an audible warning if any other function’s warning message is displayed or audible warning is generated at the same time.

pair.old: You may not hear the warning sound of Safe Exit Warning if the surroundings are noisy.
pair.new: You may not hear the warning sound of Safe Exit Warning if your surroundings are noisy.

pair.old: Safe Exit Warning does not operate in all situations or cannot prevent all collisions.
pair.new: Safe Exit Warning does not operate in all situations and cannot prevent all collisions.

pair.old: Safe Exit Warning may warn the driver late or may not warn the driver depending on the vehicle and driving conditions. Always check vehicle surroundings.
pair.new: Safe Exit Warning may warn you late or may not warn you depending on the vehicle and driving conditions. Always check the vehicle’s surroundings.

pair.old: You and your passengers are responsible for accidents that occur while exiting the vehicle. Always check the surroundings before you exit the vehicle.
pair.new: You and your passengers are responsible for accidents that occur while exiting the vehicle. Always check your surroundings before you exit the vehicle.
You and your passengers are responsible for accidents that occur while exiting the vehicle. Always check your surroundings before you exit the vehicle.

pair.old: After the vehicle is turned off, Safe Exit Warning operates for approximately 3 minutes, but turns off immediately if the doors are locked.
pair.new: After you turn OFF the vehicle, Safe Exit Warning operates for approximately 3 minutes, but turns OFF immediately if you lock the doors.

pair.old: When Safe Exit Assist is not working properly, a warning message will appear on the instrument cluster for several seconds, and the Master warning light () will appear on the cluster. If there is a malfunction in Safe Exit Assist, a warning message will be displayed on the instrument cluster display for a certain period, and the Master warning light () will turn on. If it does not work properly, visit an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: When Safe Exit Warning is not working properly, the Master warning light () appears on the instrument cluster, along with a warning message that lasts for several seconds. If the function does not work properly, visit an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: When the outside rear-view mirror warning light is not working properly, a warning message will appear on the instrument cluster for several seconds, and the Master warning light () will appear on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: Safe Exit Assist may not properly operate in an open terrain where any objects are not detected or when the detecting sensor is blocked with foreign material immediately after the vehicle is turned on, or when the detecting sensor is blocked with foreign material.

pair.old: You can check it in the service message of the utility information view of the instrument cluster display window.

pair.old: Turn off Safe Exit Assist to install or remove a trailer, carrier, or another attachment. Turn on Safe Exit Assist when finished.
pair.new: Turn OFF Safe Exit Warning to install or remove a trailer, carrier, or other attachment. Turn ON Safe Exit Warning when you are finished.

pair.old: Safe Exit Warning may not operate normally, or Safe Exit Warning may operate unexpectedly under the following conditions:
pair.new: Safe Exit Warning may operate abnormally or unexpectedly under the following circumstances:

pair.old: Exiting the vehicle where there is overgrown foliage
pair.new: You exit the vehicle where there is overgrown foliage.

pair.old: Exiting the vehicle where the road is wet
pair.new: You exit the vehicle where the road is wet.
You exit the vehicle where the road is wet.

pair.old: An approaching vehicle is moving very fast or very slow
pair.new: An approaching vehicle is moving very fast or very slow.
An approaching vehicle is moving very fast or very slow.

pair.old: Safe Exit Warning may not operate normally if interfered with by strong electromagnetic waves.
pair.new: Safe Exit Warning may not operate properly in areas with strong electromagnetic interference.

pair.old: Safe Exit Warning may not operate for approximately 3 seconds after the vehicle is restarted, or the rear corner radars are initialized.
pair.new: Safe Exit Warning may not activate for approximately 3 seconds after you restart the vehicle or initialize the rear corner radars.

pair.old: Even after starting the vehicle again, Safe Exit Warning may not function properly if the obstruction or malfunction condition persists.
pair.new: Even after you restart the vehicle, Safe Exit Warning may not function properly if an obstruction or malfunction persists.

pair.old: When the electronic child safety lock button is in the LOCK position and an approaching vehicle from the rear is detected, the electronic child safety lock () button will not unlock even if the driver presses the button to unlock the rear doors.
pair.new: When the electronic child safety lock button is in the LOCK position and Safe Exit Assist detects a vehicle approaching from the rear, the electronic child safety lock () button will not unlock, even if you press the button to unlock the rear doors.

pair.old: Warning timing may vary depending on the speed of approaching vehicle.
pair.new: The timing of the warning may vary depending on the speed of approaching vehicle.

pair.old: The driver should always be aware of his or her surroundings. If Exit Safety is deselected, Safe Exit Assist cannot assist you.
pair.new: Always be aware of your surroundings. If you deselect Exit Safety, Safe Exit Assist cannot assist you.

pair.old: When the trailer’s connector is plugged into your vehicle, Safe Exit Assist automatically turns off. In this case, you cannot get help from Safe Exit Assist. Pay extra attention when you drive when the function is inactive. (If a Kia genuine Trailer Kit that can determine whether a trailer is connected is used)
pair.new: When using a Kia genuine Trailer Kit and the connector is plugged into your vehicle, Safe Exit Assist automatically turns OFF. In this case, you cannot get help from Safe Exit Assist. Pay extra attention when you drive while the function is inactive. (A Kia genuine Trailer Kit can determine whether a trailer is connected.)

pair.old: If the vehicle is restarted, Safe Exit Assist will maintain the last setting.
pair.new: When you restart the vehicle, Safe Exit Assist maintains its last setting.

pair.old: Warning Volume: Adjusts the volume of the warning sound. If you turn off the Warning Volume, the function may warn you with a low volume.
pair.new: Warning Volume: Adjusts the volume of the warning sound. If you turn OFF the Warning Volume setting, the function may still warn you at a low volume for your safety.

pair.old: Safe Exit Assist warns under the following actions.
pair.new: Safe Exit Assist performs the following actions:

pair.old: The warning light on the outside rear-view mirror will blink and a warning message will appear on the instrument cluster, and an audible warning will sound.
pair.new: The warning light on the outside rearview mirror blinks, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster, and an audible warning sounds.

pair.old: Collision warning when exiting vehicle will warn under the following circumstances:
pair.new: Collision Warning alerts you when exiting the vehicle under the following circumstances:

pair.old: The speed of an approaching vehicle from the rear is above 4 mph (6 km/h)
pair.new: The speed of an approaching vehicle from the rear: above 4 mph (6 km/h)

pair.old: The warning light on the outside rear-view mirror will blink and a warning message will appear on the instrument cluster.
pair.new: The warning light on the outside rearview mirror blinks and a warning message appears on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: Safe Exit Assist linked to Electronic child safety lock will operate in the following conditions:
pair.new: The Safe Exit Assist linked to Electronic child safety lock operates under the following circumstances:

pair.old: Your driving speed is below 2 mph (3 km/h)
pair.new: Your speed is below 2 mph (3 km/h)

pair.old: The speed of an approaching vehicle from the rear: above 4 mph (6 km/h)
pair.new: The speed of a vehicle approaching from the rear: above 4 mph (6 km/h)

pair.old: For more details on Electronic child safety lock button, refer to .
pair.new: For more information about the Electronic child safety lock button, refer to .

pair.old: If the driver presses the Electronic child safety lock button again within 10 seconds after a warning message appears, Safe Exit Assist determines that the driver has unlocked the doors acknowledging the rear status. The electronic child safety lock will turn off (button indicator OFF) and the rear doors will unlock. Always check the surroundings before turning off the Electronic child safety lock button.
pair.new: If you press the Electronic child safety lock button within 10 seconds after a warning message appears, Safe Exit Assist determines that you have unlocked the doors while acknowledging the rear status. The Electronic child safety lock turns OFF (as shown on the button indicator) and the rear doors unlock. Always check your surroundings before pressing the Electronic child safety lock button.

pair.old: If a rear door is open from the outside, it will open regardless of Safe Exit Assist operation.
pair.new: If you attempt to open a rear door from the outside, it opens regardless of Safe Exit Assist operation.

pair.old: Change the Settings after parking at a safe location.
pair.new: For your safety, change the settings only after parking the vehicle at a safe location.

pair.old: If any other function’s warning message is displayed or audible warning is generated, Safe Exit Assist warning message may not be displayed and audible warning may not be heard.
pair.new: Safe Exit Assist may not display a warning message or generate an audible warning if any other function’s warning message is displayed or audible warning is generated at the same time.

pair.old: You may not hear the warning sound in surroundings.
pair.new: You may not hear the warning sound if your surroundings are noisy.

pair.old: Safe Exit Assist does not operate in all situations or cannot prevent all collisions.
pair.new: Safe Exit Assist does not operate in all situations and cannot prevent all collisions.

pair.old: Safe Exit Assist may warn the driver late or may not warn the driver depending on the road and driving conditions. Always check vehicle surroundings.
pair.new: Safe Exit Assist may warn you late or may not warn you depending on the road and driving conditions. Always check the vehicle’s surroundings.

pair.old: Never deliberately operate Safe Exit Assist. Doing so may lead to serious injury or death.
pair.new: Never solely rely on Safe Exit Assist when exiting your vehicle. Doing so may lead to serious injury or death.

pair.old: After the vehicle is turned off, Safe Exit Assist operates approximately for 3 minutes, but turns off immediately if the doors are locked.
pair.new: After you turn the vehicle OFF, Safe Exit Assist operates approximately for 3 minutes, but turns OFF immediately if you lock the doors.

pair.old: When Safe Exit Assist is not working properly, a warning message will appear on the instrument cluster for several seconds, and the Master warning light () will appear. If there is a malfunction in Safe Exit Assist, a warning message will be displayed on the instrument cluster for a certain period, and the Master warning light () will turn on. If it does not work properly, Have Safe Exit Assist inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: When Safe Exit Assist is not working properly, the Master warning light () appears on the instrument cluster, along with a warning message that lasts for several seconds. If it does not work properly, have Safe Exit Assist inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: When the rear bumper around the rear corner radar or sensor is covered with foreign material, such as snow, a trailer or carrier, it can reduce the detecting performance and temporarily limit or disable Safe Exit Assist.
pair.new: When foreign material (such as snow or rain) covers the rear bumper near the rear corner radar or sensor, or when you install a trailer or carrier, it can reduce the detection performance and temporarily limit or disable Safe Exit Assist.

pair.old: A warning message and the Master warning light () are displayed on the instrument cluster, but does not indicate a malfunction of Safe Exit Assist.
pair.new: A warning message and the Master warning light () display on the instrument cluster. This does not indicate a malfunction of Safe Exit Assist.

pair.old: Safe Exit Assist will operate properly when such foreign material is removed and the vehicle is restarted.
pair.new: Safe Exit Assist operates properly when you remove foreign material (such as snow or rain) from the sensor and restart the vehicle.

pair.old: If Safe Exit Assist does not operate properly after it is removed, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If Safe Exit Assist still does not operate properly after you remove foreign material (such as snow or rain) from the sensor, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Safe Exit Assist may not properly operate in an open area where objects are not detected or when the detecting sensor is blocked with foreign material immediately after the vehicle is turned on.
pair.new: Safe Exit Assist may not operate properly in open terrain where no objects are detected, or when the detection sensor is blocked with foreign material immediately after you turn the vehicle ON.

pair.old: Turn off Safe Exit Assist to install or remove a trailer, carrier, or other attachment. Turn on Safe Exit Assist when finished.
pair.new: Turn Safe Exit Assist OFF to install or remove a trailer, carrier, or other attachment. Turn Safe Exit Assist back ON when you are finished.

pair.old: Safe Exit Assist may not operate properly, or it may operate unexpectedly under the following circumstances:
pair.new: Safe Exit Assist may operate abnormally or unexpectedly under the following circumstances:

pair.old: Existing the vehicle where there is overgrown foliage
pair.new: You exit the vehicle where there is overgrown foliage.

pair.old: For more details on the limitations of the rear corner radar, refer to .
pair.new: For more information about the rear corner radar, refer to .
For more information about the rear corner radar, refer to .

pair.old: Safe Exit Assist may not operate properly if interfered with by strong electromagnetic waves.
pair.new: Safe Exit Assist may not operate properly if there is strong electromagnetic interference.

pair.old: Safe Exit Assist may not operate for approximately 3 seconds after the vehicle is started, or the rear corner radars are initialized.
pair.new: Safe Exit Assist may not activate for approximately 3 seconds after you restart the vehicle or initialize the rear corner radars.

pair.old: Even after starting the vehicle again, Safe Exit Assist may not function properly when the obstruction or malfunction condition persists.
pair.new: Even after you restart the vehicle, Safe Exit Assist may not function properly if the obstruction or malfunction persists.

pair.old: If you drive over the preset speed limit, Manual Speed Limit Assist operates (set speed limit will blink and chime will sound) until the vehicle speed returns within the speed limit.
pair.new: If you drive over the preset speed limit, Manual Speed Limit Assist operates (the set speed limit blinks and the chime sounds) until you return the vehicle speed within the speed limit.

pair.old: Setting speed limit
pair.new: Setting the speed limit

pair.old: Press and hold Driving Assist () button at the desired speed. The Speed Limit () indicator will appear on the cluster.
pair.new: Press and hold the Driving Assist () button while driving at the desired speed. The Speed Limit () indicator appears on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: Push the (+) switch up or (-) switch down and release it at the desired speed.
pair.new: Push the (+) switch up or (-) switch down and release it while driving at the desired speed.

pair.old: Push the (+) switch up or (-) switch down and hold it. The speed will increase or decrease to the nearest multiple of 5 mph (10 in km/h) at first, and then increase or decrease by 5 mph (10 km/h).
pair.new: Push the (+) switch up or (-) switch down and hold it. The speed increases or decreases to the nearest multiple of 5 mph (10 km/h) at first, then increases or decreases by 5 mph (10 km/h).

pair.old: The set speed limit will be displayed on the instrument cluster.
pair.new: The instrument cluster displays the set speed limit.
The set speed limit blinks and the chime sounds until you return the vehicle speed to the limit you set.

pair.old: The set speed limit will blink and chime will sound until you return the vehicle speed to the set limit.
pair.new: The set speed limit blinks and the chime sounds until you return the vehicle speed to the limit you set.

pair.old: When the accelerator pedal is not depressed beyond the pressure point, vehicle speed will maintain the set speed.
pair.new: When the accelerator pedal is not engaged beyond the pressure point, the vehicle maintains the set speed.

pair.old: The set speed may differ depending on the vehicle specifications. You may not increase the set speed above the maximum set speed.
pair.new: The set speed may differ depending on the vehicle specifications. You may not increase the set speed above the maximum set speed for your vehicle.

pair.old: Press the () switch to temporarily pause the set speed limit. The set speed limit will turn off but the Speed Limit () indicator will stay on.
pair.new: Press the () switch to temporarily pause the set speed limit. The set speed limit turns OFF, but the Speed Limit () indicator stays ON.

pair.old: To resume Manual Speed Limit Assist after the function was paused, operate the (+), (-), () switch.
pair.new: To resume Manual Speed Limit Assist after pausing the function, push the (+), (-), and () switches as follows:

pair.old: Turning off Manual Speed Limit Assist
pair.new: Turning OFF Manual Speed Limit Assist

pair.old: Press the Driving Assist () button to turn Manual Speed Limit Assist off. The Speed Limit () indicator will go off.
pair.new: Press the Driving Assist () button to turn Manual Speed Limit Assist OFF. The Speed Limit () indicator disappears.

pair.old: Always set the vehicle speed under the posted speed limit.
pair.new: Always set the vehicle speed below the posted speed limit.

pair.old: Keep Manual Speed Limit Assist off when the function is not in use, to avoid inadvertently setting a speed. Check that the Speed Limit () indicator is off.
pair.new: To avoid inadvertently setting a speed, keep Manual Speed Limit Assist OFF when not in use. Check that the Speed Limit () indicator is OFF.

pair.old: Manual Speed Limit Assist does not substitute for proper and safe driving. It is the responsibility of the driver to always drive safely and to be aware of unexpected and sudden situations. Pay attention to the road conditions at all times.
pair.new: Manual Speed Limit Assist is not a substitute for proper and safe driving. It is your responsibility to always drive safely and be aware of unexpected and sudden situations. Pay attention to the road conditions at all times.

pair.old: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist uses information from the detected road signs and uses the navigation system data to inform you of the posted speed limit and to help you maintain it.
pair.new: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist uses information from detected road signs and navigation system data to inform you of the posted speed limit and help you maintain it.

pair.old: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist may not operate properly if the function is used in other countries.
pair.new: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist may not operate properly if you use it in other countries.

pair.old: If a navigation is applied to your vehicle, the navigation needs to be regularly updated for Intelligent Speed Limit Assist to operate properly.
pair.new: If your vehicle has a navigation system, the system needs regular updates for Intelligent Speed Limit Assist to operate properly.

pair.new: The following picture shows the location of the detection sensor:
The following picture shows the location of the detection sensor:

pair.old: With the vehicle on, select SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceSpeed Limit on the infotainment system.
pair.new: While the vehicle is ON, tap SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceSpeed Limit on the infotainment system screen.

pair.old: Speed Limit Assist: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist will inform the driver of speed limit and additional road signs and warn the driver when the vehicle is driven faster than the speed limit. Intelligent Speed Limit Assist will also inform the driver to change set speed of Manual Speed Limit Assist or Smart Cruise Control to help the driver stay within the speed limit.
pair.new: Speed Limit Assist: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist informs you of the posted speed limit and additional road signs, and warns you when you drive the vehicle faster than the speed limit. Intelligent Speed Limit Assist also recommends changing the set speed of the Manual Speed Limit Assist or Smart Cruise Control functions to help you stay within the posted speed limit.

pair.old: Speed Limit Warning: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist will inform the driver of speed limit and additional road signs. Intelligent Speed Limit Assist will also warn the driver when the vehicle is driven faster than the posted speed limit.
pair.new: Speed Limit Warning: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist informs you of the posted speed limit and additional road signs. Intelligent Speed Limit Assist also warns you when you drive the vehicle faster than the posted speed limit.

pair.old: Off: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist will turn off.
pair.new: Off: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist turns OFF.

pair.old: Speed limit and Speed warning function operates based on an offset value added to the speed limit. Set the offset value to ’0’ to change or warn the speed according to the posted speed limit.
pair.new: The Speed Limit and Speed Limit Warning functions operate based on an offset value added to the speed limit. Set the offset value to “0” to change or warn you of the speed according to the posted speed limit.

pair.old: The setting of Speed limit offset is not reflected in Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control.
pair.new: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control does not reflect the setting of the Speed Limit Offset function.

pair.old: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist is warned and controlled by the following level.
pair.new: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist has the following warnings and controls:

pair.old: Displaying speed limit
pair.new: Displaying the speed limit
Displaying the speed limit

pair.old: Changing set speed
pair.new: Changing the set speed
Changing the set speed

pair.old: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist warning and control are described based on the Offset adjust to ’0’. For details on Offset setting, refer to .
pair.new: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist warnings and controls operate based on adjusting the Offset to “0”. For more information about adjusting the Offset setting, refer to .

pair.old: Speed limit information is displayed on the instrument cluster.
pair.new: The instrument cluster displays the speed limit information.

pair.old: If speed limit information of the road cannot be recognized, ’---’ sign will be displayed. Please refer to if the road signs are difficult to recognize.
pair.new: If Intelligent Speed Limit Assist cannot recognize speed limit information from the road, the instrument cluster displays the ‘---’ sign. Please refer to if the road signs are difficult for the function to recognize.

pair.old: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist provides additional road sign information in addition to speed limit. Additional road sign information provided may vary according to your region.
pair.new: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist provides other road sign information in addition to the posted speed limit. The additional road sign information provided may vary according to your region.

pair.old: The images and colors in the instrument cluster may differ depending on the instrument cluster type or theme selected from the settings menu.
pair.new: The images or colors may display differently depending on the specifications of the instrument cluster or theme.

pair.old: When driving at a speed higher than the displayed speed limit, the red speed limit indicator will appear.
pair.new: When you drive at a speed higher than the displayed speed limit, the red speed limit indicator appears.

pair.old: If the speed limit of the road changes during the operation of Manual Speed Limit Assist or Smart Cruise Control, an arrow in the direction of up or down is displayed to inform the driver that the set speed needs to be changed. The driver can change the set speed according to the speed limit by using the (+) or (–) switch on the steering wheel.
pair.new: If the posted speed limit on the road changes while you are operating Manual Speed Limit Assist or Smart Cruise Control, the instrument cluster displays an up or down arrow to inform you to change the set speed. You can change the set speed according to the speed limit by using the (+) or (–) switch on the steering wheel.

pair.old: Auto set speed change (if equipped with the navigation)
pair.new: Auto set speed change (if equipped with navigation)

pair.old: When operating Manual Speed Limit Assist or Smart Cruise Control, if the speed set by the driver is the same as the speed limit on the road, the set speed is automatically adjusted accordingly the speed limit afterwards. The auto set speed change function operates on roads with a speed limit of above 45 mph (70 km/h). When the function is activated, the set speed on the instrument cluster is displayed in green.
pair.new: When you operate Manual Speed Limit Assist or Smart Cruise Control, if the speed you set is the same as the posted speed limit, the set speed automatically adjusts according to the speed limit afterward. The auto set speed change function operates on roads with a speed limit of above 45 mph (70 km/h). When you activate the function, the set speed displays in green on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: When driving at a speed lower than the speed limit, set the offset under ’0’, or press (-) switch to decrease your set speed.
pair.new: When you drive at a speed lower than the speed limit, set the offset below “0”, or push the (-) switch to decrease your set speed.

pair.old: Even after changing the set speed according to the speed limit of the road, the vehicle can still be driven over the speed limit. If necessary, depress the brake pedal to reduce your speed.
pair.new: Even after you change the set speed according to the posted speed limit, you can still drive the vehicle over the speed limit. If necessary, engage the brake pedal to reduce your speed.

pair.old: If the posted speed limit is under 20 mph (30 km/h), the set speed change and auto set speed change function will not work.
pair.new: If the posted speed limit is under 20 mph (30 km/h), the set speed change and auto set speed change functions will not work.

pair.old: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist operates using the speed unit in the instrument cluster set by the driver. If the speed unit is set to a unit other than the speed unit used in your country, Intelligent Speed Limit Assist may not operate properly. (Except Australia/Russia)
pair.new: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist operates using the speed unit you set in the instrument cluster. If you set the speed unit to a different unit than the one used in your country, Intelligent Speed Limit Assist may not operate properly (except in Australia or Russia).

pair.old: For more details on Manual Speed Limit Assist operation, refer to .
pair.new: For more information about Manual Speed Limit Assist operation, refer to .

pair.old: For more details on Smart Cruise Control operation, refer to .
pair.new: For more information about Smart Cruise Control operation, refer to .

pair.old: When Intelligent Speed Limit Assist is not working properly, a warning message will appear on the cluster for several seconds, and the Master warning light () the Intelligent Speed Limit Assist indicator light () will appear on the cluster.
pair.new: When Intelligent Speed Limit Assist is not working properly, the Master warning light () and the Intelligent Speed Limit Assist indicator light () appear on the instrument cluster, along with a warning message that lasts for several seconds.

pair.old: When the windshield where the front view camera is located is covered with foreign material, such as snow or rain, it can reduce the detecting performance and temporarily limit or disable Intelligent Speed Limit Assist. If this occurs, a warning message and the Intelligent Speed Limit Assist indicator light () will appear on the cluster.
pair.new: When foreign material (such as snow or rain) covers the windshield where the front view camera is located, it can reduce the detection performance and temporarily limit or disable Intelligent Speed Limit Assist.

pair.old: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist will operate properly when snow, rain or foreign material is removed. Always keep it clean.
pair.new: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist operates properly when you remove foreign material (such as snow or rain) from the sensor. Always keep it clean.

pair.old: If Intelligent Speed Limit Assist does not operate properly after it is removed, have your vehicle checked by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If Intelligent Speed Limit Assist still does not operate properly after you remove foreign material (such as snow or rain) from the sensor, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Even though a warning message or light does not appear on the cluster, Intelligent Speed Limit Assist may not operate properly.
pair.new: Even when a warning message or warning light does not appear on the instrument cluster, Intelligent Speed Limit Assist may not operate properly.

pair.old: Even if restarting the vehicle with the sensors blocked or malfunctioned, Intelligent Speed Limit Assist may not properly operate as the function maintains the broken/covered state.
pair.new: If you restart the vehicle with the sensors blocked or malfunctioning, Intelligent Speed Limit Assist may not operate properly until you fix the broken or covered state.

pair.old: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist may not operate properly, or it may operate unexpectedly under the following circumstances:
pair.new: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist may operate abnormally or unexpectedly under the following circumstances:

pair.old: The road sign is contaminated or indistinguishable
pair.new: The road sign is dirty or difficult to read.

pair.old: The road sign is difficult to see due to bad weather, such as rain, snow, fog.
pair.new: The road sign is difficult to see due to inclement weather, such as rain, snow, or fog.

pair.old: The road sign is partially obscured by surrounding objects or shadow
pair.new: The road sign is partially obscured by surrounding objects or shadows.

pair.old: The road signs do not conform to the standard
pair.new: The road signs do not conform to the standard expected by Intelligent Speed Limit Assist.

pair.old: The text or picture on the road sign is different from the standard
pair.new: The text or picture on the road sign is different from the standard expected by Intelligent Speed Limit Assist.

pair.old: The road sign is installed between the main line and the exit road or between diverging roads
pair.new: The road sign is installed between the main road and the exit road, or between diverging roads.

pair.old: A sign is attached to another vehicle
pair.new: A sign is attached to another vehicle.

pair.old: The distance between the vehicle and the road signs is far
pair.new: The distance between the vehicle and the road signs is far.

pair.old: The vehicle encounters illuminating road signs
pair.new: The road signs are illuminated.

pair.old: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist incorrectly recognizes numbers or pictures in the street signs or other signs as the speed limit
pair.new: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist incorrectly recognizes numbers or pictures in the street signs or other signs as the speed limit.

pair.old: A road sign near the road you are driving is detected
pair.new: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist detects a sign on a nearby road.

pair.old: Multiple signs are installed close together
pair.new: Multiple signs are installed close together.

pair.old: The minimum speed limit sign is misrecognized
pair.new: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist misreads the minimum speed limit sign.

pair.old: The brightness changes suddenly, for example when entering or exiting a tunnel or passing under a bridge
pair.new: The brightness changes suddenly (for example, when you enter or exit a tunnel).

pair.old: Headlamps are not used or the brightness of the headlamps are weak at night or in the tunnel
pair.new: You are not using the headlights, or the brightness of the headlights is weak at night or in a tunnel.

pair.old: Road signs are difficult to recognize due to the reflection of sunlight, street lights, or oncoming vehicles
pair.new: Road signs are difficult to recognize due to the reflection of sunlight, street lights, or oncoming vehicles.

pair.old: The navigation information or GPS information contains errors.
pair.new: The navigation or GPS information contains errors.

pair.old: The driver does not follow the guide of the navigation.
pair.new: You do not follow the guidance of the navigation.

pair.old: Driving on a road that is sharply curved or continuously curved
pair.new: You are driving on a sharply or continuously curved road.

pair.old: Driving through speed bumps, or driving up and down or left to right on steep inclines
pair.new: You are driving over speed bumps, or driving up and down or left to right on steep inclines.

pair.old: The vehicle is shaking heavily
pair.new: The vehicle is shaking heavily.

pair.old: Driving on a new road
pair.new: You are driving on a new road.

pair.old: When driving on a road under construction.
pair.new: You are driving on a road under construction.

pair.old: When the navigation software updates during driving
pair.new: The navigation software updates while you are driving.

pair.old: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist is a supplemental function that can help the driver to comply with the speed limit on the road, and may not display the correct speed limit or control the driving speed properly.
pair.new: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist is a supplemental function that can help you comply with the posted speed limit. This function may not display the correct speed limit or control the driving speed properly.

pair.old: It is the responsibility of the driver to follow the speed limit.
pair.new: It is your responsibility to follow the speed limit.

pair.old: It may not operate for 15 seconds after the vehicle is started, or the front view camera is initialized or restarted.
pair.new: Intelligent Speed Limit Assist may not operate for 15 seconds after you start the vehicle or initialize or restart the front view camera.

pair.old: Inattentive Driving Warning function
pair.new: Driver Attention Warning function

pair.old: Driver Attention Warning monitors your driving pattern while driving. When the driver’s attention level is below a certain level, Driver Attention Warning recommends a break to help with safe driving.
pair.new: Driver Attention Warning monitors your driving pattern. When your attention is below a certain level, Driver Attention Warning recommends a break to help with safe driving.

pair.old: Leading Vehicle Departure Alert function will inform the driver when a detected vehicle in front departs from a stop.
pair.new: Leading Vehicle Departure Alert informs you when a detected vehicle ahead departs from a stop.

pair.old: Always keep the front view camera in good condition to maintain optimal performance of DAW.
pair.new: Always keep the front view camera in good condition to maintain optimal performance of Driver Assistance Warning.

pair.old: With the vehicle on, select SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceDriver Attention WarningLeading Vehicle Departure Alert on the infotainment system.
pair.new: While the vehicle is ON, tap SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceDriver Attention WarningLeading Vehicle Departure Alert on the infotainment system screen.

pair.old: The basic function of Driver Attention Warning is as follows.
pair.new: The basic function of Driver Attention Warning is Taking a break.

pair.old: The Inattentive Driving Warning light () blinking and warning message will appear on the instrument cluster and an audible warning will sound to suggest that the driver take a break, when the driver’s attention level is below a certain level.
pair.new: The Inattentive Driving Warning light () blinks, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster, and an audible warning sounds to suggest that you take a break when your attention level is below a certain level.

pair.old: Driver Attention Warning will not suggest a break when the total driving time is shorter than 4 minutes or 4 minutes has not passed after the last break was suggested.
pair.new: Driver Attention Warning will not suggest a break when the total driving time is shorter than 4 minutes, or before 4 minutes have passed since the function last suggested a break.

pair.old: Driver Attention Warning may suggest a break depending on your driving pattern or habits, even if you don’t feel fatigue.
pair.new: Driver Attention Warning may suggest a break depending on your driving pattern or habits, even if you do not feel fatigued.

pair.old: Driver Attention Warning is a supplemental function and may not be able to determine whether the driver is inattentive.
pair.new: Driver Attention Warning is a supplemental function and may not be able to determine whether you are being inattentive.

pair.old: If you feel fatigued, you should take a break at a safe location, even though there is no break suggestion by Driver Attention Warning.
pair.new: If you feel fatigued, you should take a break at a safe location, even if Driver Attention Warning does not suggest a break.

pair.old: When a detected vehicle in front departs from a stop, Leading Vehicle Departure Alert will inform the driver by displaying a warning message on the cluster and an audible warning will sound.
pair.new: When a detected vehicle ahead departs from a stop, Leading Vehicle Departure Alert will inform you by displaying a warning message on the instrument cluster and generating an audible warning sound.

pair.old: If any other function’s warning message is displayed or audible warning is generated, Leading Vehicle Departure Alert’s warning message may not be displayed and audible warning may not be generated.
pair.new: Leading Vehicle Departure Alert may not display a warning message or generate an audible warning if any other function’s warning message is displayed or an audible warning is generated at the same time.

pair.old: The driver should hold the responsibility to safely drive and control the vehicle.
pair.new: You are responsible for driving safely and controlling the vehicle.

pair.old: Leading Vehicle Departure Alert is a supplemental function and may not alert the driver whenever the front vehicle departs from a stop.
pair.new: Leading Vehicle Departure Alert is a supplemental function and may not alert you whenever the vehicle ahead departs from a stop.

pair.old: When Driver Attention Warning is not working properly, a warning message will appear on the cluster for several seconds, and the Master warning light () will appear on the cluster.
pair.new: When Driver Attention Warning is not working properly,the Master warning light () appears on the instrument cluster, along with a warning message that lasts for several seconds.

pair.old: When the windshield (where the front view camera is located), front radar cover, bumper or sensor is covered with foreign material, such as snow or rain, it can reduce the detecting performance and temporarily limit or disable Driver Attention Warning.
pair.new: When foreign material (such as snow or rain) covers the windshield where the front view camera is located, the front radar cover, the bumper, or the sensor, it can reduce the detecting performance and temporarily limit or disable Driver Attention Warning.

pair.old: A warning message, and the Master warning light () will appear on the cluster. This is normal operation.
pair.new: If this occurs, a warning message and the Master warning light () display on the instrument cluster. This does not indicate a malfunction of Driver Attention Warning.

pair.old: Driver Attention Warning will operate properly when snow, rain or foreign material is removed.
pair.new: Driver Attention Warning operates properly when you remove foreign material (such as snow or rain) from the sensor.

pair.old: If Driver Attention Warning does not operate properly after obstruction (snow, rain, or foreign material) is removed (including trailer, carrier, etc. from the rear bumper), visit an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If Driver Attention Warning still does not operate properly after you remove foreign material (such as snow or rain) from the sensor or obstructions (such as a trailer or carrier) from the rear bumper, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Even though a warning message or warning light does not appear on the instrument cluster, Driver Attention Warning may not properly operate.
pair.new: Even when a warning message or warning light does not appear on the instrument cluster, Driver Attention Warning may not operate properly.

pair.old: Driver Attention Warning may not properly operate in an open area where there is nothing to detect or the detecting sensor is covered with foreign material after turning ON the vehicle.
pair.new: Driver Attention Warning may not operate properly in an open area where there is nothing to detect, or when the detection sensor is covered with foreign material after you turn the vehicle ON.

pair.old: Driver Attention Warning may not work properly in the following situations:
