pair.new: Driver Attention Warning may operate abnormally or unexpectedly under the following circumstances:

pair.old: The vehicle is driven violently
pair.new: You are driving aggressively.

pair.old: The vehicle intentionally crosses over lanes frequently
pair.new: You frequently change lanes.

pair.old: The vehicle is controlled by Driver Assistance system, such as Lane Keeping Assist
pair.new: Other functions of the Driver Assistance system, such as Lane Keeping Assist, are controlling the vehicle.

pair.old: [A]: Your vehicle, [B]: Front vehicle
pair.new: [A]: Your vehicle, [B]: Vehicle ahead
[A]: Your vehicle, [B]: Vehicle ahead

pair.old: If a vehicle cuts in front of you, Leading Departure Alert may not operate properly.
pair.new: If a vehicle cuts in ahead of you, Leading Departure Alert may not operate properly.

pair.old: If a vehicle in front makes a sharp turn, such as to turn left or right or making a U-turn, etc., Leading Vehicle Departure Alert may not operate properly.
pair.new: If a vehicle ahead makes a sharp turn (to the left or right or a U-turn), Leading Vehicle Departure Alert may not operate properly.

pair.old: If the vehicle in front abruptly departs, Leading Vehicle Departure Alert may not operate properly.
pair.new: If the vehicle ahead abruptly departs, Leading Vehicle Departure Alert may not operate properly.

pair.old: When a pedestrian or bicycle is between you and the vehicle ahead
pair.new: When a pedestrian or cyclist is between you and the vehicle ahead

pair.old: If there is a pedestrian(s) or bicycle(s) in between you and the vehicle in front, Leading Vehicle Departure Alert may not operate properly.
pair.new: If there is a pedestrian or cyclist between you and the vehicle ahead, Leading Vehicle Departure Alert may not operate properly.

pair.old: If a vehicle parked in front drives away from you, Leading Vehicle Departure Alert may alert you that the parked vehicle is driving away.
pair.new: If a vehicle parked ahead drives away from you, Leading Vehicle Departure Alert may alert you that the parked vehicle is driving away.

pair.old: If you pass a tollgate or intersection with lots of vehicles or you drive where lanes are merged or divided frequently, Leading Vehicle Departure Alert may not operate properly.
pair.new: If you pass a tollgate or intersection with lots of vehicles, or if you drive where lanes merge or divide frequently, Leading Vehicle Departure Alert may not operate properly.

pair.old: Driver Attention Warning may not operate for 15 seconds after the vehicle is started, or the front view camera is initialized.
pair.new: Driver Attention Warning may not activate until 15 seconds after you restart the vehicle or initialize the front view camera.

pair.old: Blind-Spot View Monitor displays rear blind spots in the instrument cluster when the turn signal is turned on to help safely change lanes.
pair.new: To help you safely change lanes, Blind-Spot View Monitor displays rear blind spots in the instrument cluster when you use the turn signal.

pair.old: Wide-side view camera/Outside rear-view mirror
pair.new: Wide Side View camera and Outside Rear View mirror

pair.old: Wide-rear view camera
pair.new: Wide Rear View camera
Wide rear view camera

pair.old: With the vehicle on, select SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceDriving SafetyBlind-Spot View Monitor from the infotainment system screen to turn on Blind-Spot View Monitor and deselect to turn off the function.
pair.new: While the vehicle is ON, tap SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceDriving SafetyBlind-Spot View Monitor on the infotainment system screen to change Blind-Spot View Monitor settings.

pair.old: Blind-Spot View Monitor will turn on and off when the turn signal is turned on and off.
pair.new: Blind-Spot View Monitor turns ON and OFF when you turn the turn signal ON and OFF.

pair.old: When the left or right turn signal turns on, the image on the instrument cluster will turn on.
pair.new: When you turn the left or right turn signal ON, the image appears on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: Off conditions
pair.new: Non-operating conditions
Non-operating conditions
Non-operating conditions
Non-operating conditions

pair.old: Blind-Spot View Monitor will turn off when one of the following conditions are satisfied:
pair.new: Blind-Spot View Monitor turns OFF under one of the following conditions:

pair.old: When the turn signal is turned off.
pair.new: When you turn the turn signal OFF.

pair.old: When the hazard warning flasher is ON.
pair.new: When the hazard light is ON.

pair.old: When another important warning is displayed on the instrument cluster.
pair.new: When the instrument cluster displays another important warning.

pair.old: When Blind-Spot View Monitor is not working properly, the instrument cluster display flickers, or the camera image does not display normally, have Blind-Spot View Monitor inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: When Blind-Spot View Monitor does not work properly, the instrument cluster display flickers, or the camera image does not display normally, have Blind-Spot View Monitor inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Blind-Spot View Monitor may display objects at a different distance from what is shown on the screen due to the correction of the wide-side view camera images. Make sure to visually check the surroundings for safety.
pair.new: Blind-Spot View Monitor may display objects at a different distance from what is shown on the infotainment system screen due to the correction of the Wide Side View camera images. Make sure to visually check the surroundings for safety.

pair.old: Smart Cruise Control detects a vehicle ahead and can help maintain the distance from the vehicle ahead and the set speed.
pair.new: Smart Cruise Control can detect a vehicle ahead and help you maintain the appropriate following distance from it at the set speed.

pair.old: When Smart Cruise Control judges you are attempting to overtake a vehicle in front, Smart Cruise Control helps with accelerating.
pair.new: When Smart Cruise Control determines that you are attempting to overtake a vehicle ahead, the function helps with accelerating.

pair.old: Smart Cruise Control will operate based on the driver’s driving style, such as inter-vehicle distance, acceleration, reaction speed.
pair.new: Smart Cruise Control operates based on how you drive, taking into account distance between vehicles, acceleration, and reaction speed.

pair.old: For more details on the precautions of the front view camera and front radar, refer to .
pair.new: For more information about the front view camera and front radar, refer to .

pair.old: With the vehicle on, select SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceSmart Cruise Control on the infotainment system to set the distance, acceleration and the reaction speed.
pair.new: While the vehicle is ON, tap SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceSmart Cruise Control on the infotainment system screen to set the distance, acceleration, and reaction speed.

pair.old: With the vehicle on, if SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceSmart Cruise ControlBased on Driving Style is selected from the infotainment system screen, Smart Cruise Control will operate based on the driver’s driving style, such as vehicle distance, acceleration, reaction speed.
pair.new: While the vehicle is ON, tap SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceSmart Cruise ControlBased on Driving Style. If Based on Driving Style is selected on the infotainment system screen, Smart Cruise Control operates based how you drive, taking into account distance between vehicles, acceleration, and reaction speed.

pair.old: The driving style can be adjusted manually.
pair.new: You can adjust the driving style manually.

pair.old: If equipped with Based on Driving Style, Based on driving mode and Based on driving style can be selected from the infotainment system screen by selecting SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceSmart Cruise Control.
pair.new: If your vehicle is equipped with Based on Driving Style, you can select Based on driving mode and Based on driving style by tapping SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceSmart Cruise Control on the infotainment system screen.

pair.old: While Smart Cruise Control is operating with Based on driving style selected, if you press and hold the Vehicle Distance () button, based on driving mode will disabled. Press and hold the Vehicle Distance () button to change Smart Cruise Control to Based on driving style mode.
pair.new: While Smart Cruise Control is operating with Based on driving style selected, if you press and hold the Vehicle Distance () button, Based on driving mode is disabled. Press and hold the Vehicle Distance () button to change Smart Cruise Control to Based on driving style mode.

pair.old: Smart Cruise Control learns your driving styles only when drive the vehicle.
pair.new: Smart Cruise Control learns your driving style only when you drive the vehicle.

pair.old: When Based On Driving Style is disabled, your driving style (inter-vehicle distance, acceleration, response speed) remains at the same level as the Based On Driving Style.
pair.new: When Based On Driving Style is disabled, your driving style (distance between vehicles, acceleration, and response speed) remains at the same level as the Based On Driving Style function.

pair.old: When the Based On Driving Style is enabled or disabled, the displayed driving style (including inter-vehicle distance, acceleration, and response speed) may vary between different controlled styles.
pair.new: Whether Based On Driving Style is enabled or disabled, the displayed driving style (including distance between vehicles, acceleration, and response speed) may vary between different driving styles.

pair.old: The Warning Methods can be set with the vehicle on. Select SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceWarning MethodsWarning Volume from the settings menu in the infotainment system to change the following settings:
pair.new: To change the Warning Methods settings while the vehicle is ON, tap SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceWarning Methods on the infotainment system screen. You can change the following settings:

pair.old: Smart Cruise Control operates when the following conditions are satisfied.
pair.new: Smart Cruise Control operates under the following conditions:

pair.old: The gear is in D (Drive)
pair.new: The gear is in D (Drive).

pair.old: Your driving speed is within the operating speed range
pair.new: Your speed is within the operating range:

pair.old: 5 ~ 110 mph (10 ~ 180 km/h): when there is no vehicle in front
pair.new: 5–110 mph (10–180 km/h) when there is no vehicle ahead

pair.old: 0 ~ 110 mph (0 ~ 180 km/h): when there is a vehicle in front
pair.new: 0–110 mph (0–180 km/h) when there is a vehicle ahead

pair.old: ESC (Electronic Stability Control) or ABS is on
pair.new: Electronic Stability Control (ESC) or Anti-lock Braking System (ABS) is ON.

pair.old: The driver’s door is open
pair.new: The driver’s door is open.

pair.old: Engine RPM is high
pair.new: Engine RPM is high.

pair.old: EPB (Electronic Parking Brake) is applied
pair.new: Electronic Parking Brake (EPB) is engaged.

pair.old: ESC (Electronic Stability Control) or ABS is controlling the vehicle
pair.new: ESC or ABS is controlling the vehicle.

pair.old: Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist brake control is operating
pair.new: Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist brake control is operating.

pair.old: Remote Smart Parking Assist brake control is operating (if equipped)
pair.new: Remote Smart Parking Assist brake control is operating (if equipped).

pair.old: When stopped behind another vehicle, you can turn on Smart Cruise Control while the brake pedal is depressed.
pair.new: When you are stopped behind another vehicle, you can turn on Smart Cruise Control while the brake pedal is engaged.

pair.old: Overtake Acceleration Assist function
pair.new: Overtaking Acceleration Assist function

pair.old: Overtaking Acceleration Assist will operate when the turn signal indicator is turned on to the left (left-hand drive) or turned on to the right (right-hand drive) while Smart Cruise Control is operating, and the following conditions are satisfied:
pair.new: Overtaking Acceleration Assist operates when you turn the turn signal indicator ON to the left (left-hand drive) or the right (right-hand drive) while Smart Cruise Control is operating under the following conditions:

pair.old: Your driving speed is above 40 mph (60 km/h)
pair.new: Your speed is above 40 mph (60 km/h).

pair.old: A vehicle is detected in front of you
pair.new: A vehicle is detected ahead of you.

pair.old: Overtaking Acceleration Assist does not operate in the following conditions.
pair.new: Overtaking Acceleration Assist does not operate under the following conditions:

pair.old: The hazard warning flasher is on
pair.new: The hazard lights are ON.

pair.old: Vehicle speed is reduced to maintain distance with a vehicle in front
pair.new: You have reduced your speed to maintain following distance from a vehicle ahead.

pair.old: When the turn signal indicator is turned on to the left (left-hand drive) or turned on to the right (right-hand drive) while there is a vehicle ahead, the vehicle may accelerate temporarily. Always pay attention to the road conditions.
pair.new: When you turn the turn signal indicator ON to the left (left-hand drive) or the right (right-hand drive) while there is a vehicle ahead, your vehicle may accelerate temporarily. Always pay attention to the road conditions.

pair.old: Regardless of your country’s driving direction, Overtaking Acceleration Assist will operate when the conditions are satisfied. When using the function in countries with different driving direction, always check the road conditions.
pair.new: Regardless of your country’s driving direction, Overtaking Acceleration Assist operates when the conditions are satisfied. When using the function in countries with a different driving direction, always check the road conditions.

pair.old: Press the Driving Assist button to turn on Smart Cruise Control. The speed will be set to the current speed on the cluster.
pair.new: Press the Driving Assist button to turn Smart Cruise Control ON. The speed is set to the current speed on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: If there is no vehicle in front of you, the set speed will be maintained.
pair.new: If there is no vehicle ahead of you, the function maintains the set speed.

pair.old: If there is a vehicle in front of you, the speed may be adjusted to maintain the distance to the vehicle ahead. If the vehicle ahead accelerates, your vehicle will travel at a steady cruising speed after accelerating to the set speed.
pair.new: If there is a vehicle ahead of you, the function may adjust the speed to maintain the distance from that vehicle. If the vehicle ahead accelerates, your vehicle will travel at a steady cruising speed after accelerating to the set speed.

pair.old: If your vehicle speed is between 0 ~ 20 mph (0 ~ 30 km/h) when you press the Driving Assist button, the Smart Cruise Control speed will be set to 20 mph (30 km/h).
pair.new: If your vehicle speed is between 0–20 mph (0–30 km/h) when you press the Driving Assist button, the Smart Cruise Control speed will be set to 20 mph (30 km/h).

pair.old: Each time the button is pressed, the headway changes as follows:
pair.new: Each time you press the button, the minimum distance changes as follows:

pair.old: The distance is set to the last set distance when the vehicle is restarted, or when Smart Cruise Control was temporarily canceled.
pair.new: The distance is set to the last distance you set before you restarted the vehicle or temporarily canceled Smart Cruise Control.

pair.old: Push the (+) switch up and release it immediately. The set speed will increase by 1 mph (1 km/h) each time the switch is operated in this manner.
pair.new: Push the (+) switch up and release it immediately. The set speed increases by 1 mph (1 km/h) each time you operate the switch in this manner.

pair.old: Push the (+) switch up and hold it. The set speed will increase by 5 mph (10 km/h) each time the switch is operated in this manner.
pair.new: Push the (+) switch up and hold it. The set speed increases by 5 mph (10 km/h) each time you operate the switch in this manner.

pair.old: Check the driving condition before using the (+) switch. Driving speed may sharply increase when you push up and hold the (+) switch.
pair.new: Check the driving conditions before using the (+) switch. Speed may sharply increase when you push up and hold the (+) switch.

pair.old: Push the (-) switch down and release it immediately. The set speed will decrease by 1 mph (1 km/h) each time the switch is operated in this manner.
pair.new: Push the (-) switch down and release it immediately. The set speed decreases by 1 mph (1 km/h) each time you operate the switch in this manner.

pair.old: Push the (-) switch down and hold it. The set speed will decrease by 5 mph (10 km/h) each time the switch is operated in this manner.
pair.new: Push the (-) switch down and hold it. The set speed decreases by 5 mph (10 km/h) each time you operate the switch in this manner.

pair.old: Press the () switch or depress the brake pedal to temporarily cancel Smart Cruise Control.
pair.new: Press the () switch or engage the brake pedal to temporarily cancel Smart Cruise Control.

pair.old: To resume Smart Cruise Control after the function was canceled, operate the (+), (-) or () switch.
pair.new: To resume Smart Cruise Control after you cancel it, push the (+), (-), or () switches as follows:

pair.old: If you push the (+) switch up or (-) switch down, the set speed will be set to the current speed on the cluster.
pair.new: If you push the (+) switch up or (-) switch down, the set speed is set to the current speed on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: If you press the () switch, vehicle speed will resume to the preset speed.
pair.new: If you push the () switch, the vehicle resumes the preset speed.

pair.old: Check the driving condition before using the () switch. Driving speed may sharply increase or decrease when you press the () switch.
pair.new: Check the driving conditions before you push the () switch. Speed may sharply increase or decrease when you push the () switch.

pair.old: Turning off Smart Cruise Control
pair.new: Turning OFF Smart Cruise Control

pair.old: Press the Driving Assist button to turn Smart Cruise Control off.
pair.new: Press the Driving Assist button to turn Smart Cruise Control OFF.

pair.old: If your vehicle is equipped with Manual Speed Limit Assist, press and hold the Driving Assist button to turn off Smart Cruise Control. However, Manual Speed Limit Assist will turn on.
pair.new: If your vehicle is equipped with Manual Speed Limit Assist, press and hold the Driving Assist button to turn OFF Smart Cruise Control. However, this action turns ON Manual Speed Limit Assist.

pair.old: You can see the status of the Smart Cruise Control operation in the Driving Assist view on the instrument cluster. Refer to .
pair.new: You can see the status of Smart Cruise Control operation in the Driving Assist view on the instrument cluster. Refer to .

pair.old: Smart Cruise Control will be displayed as below depending on the status of the function.
pair.new: Smart Cruise Control is displayed as shown in the following image (depending on the status of the function):
Smart Cruise Control is displayed as shown in the following image (depending on the status of the function):

pair.old: When operating
pair.new: When operating:

pair.old: Whether there is a vehicle ahead and the selected distance level
pair.new: Shows whether there is a vehicle ahead and displays the selected distance level

pair.old: Set speed
pair.new: Displays the set speed

pair.old: Whether there is a vehicle ahead and the target vehicle distance
pair.new: Shows whether there is a vehicle ahead and displays the target vehicle distance

pair.old: When temporarily canceled
pair.new: When temporarily canceled:

pair.old: Your vehicle (gray)
pair.new: Shows your vehicle (gray)

pair.old: Previous set speed (gray)
pair.new: Displays the previous set speed (gray)

pair.old: Whether there is a vehicle ahead (gray)
pair.new: Shows whether there is a vehicle ahead (gray)

pair.old: The distance of the front vehicle on the instrument cluster is displayed according to the actual distance between your vehicle and the vehicle ahead.
pair.new: The instrument cluster displays the distance from the vehicle ahead according to the actual distance between that vehicle and yours.

pair.old: The target distance may vary according to the vehicle speed and the set distance level. If vehicle speed is low, even though the vehicle distance has changed, the change of the target vehicle distance may be small.
pair.new: The target distance may vary according to the vehicle speed and the set distance level. If the vehicle speed is low, even though the vehicle distance has changed, the change of the target vehicle distance may be small.

pair.old: The images or colors displayed on the cluster may vary depending on the instrument cluster specifications or themes.
pair.new: The images or colors may display differently depending on the specifications of the instrument cluster or theme.

pair.old: If you want to speed up temporarily without altering the set speed while Smart Cruise Control is operating, depress the accelerator pedal. While the accelerator pedal is depressed, the set speed, distance level and target distance will blink on the instrument cluster.
pair.new: If you want to speed up temporarily while operating Smart Cruise control without altering the set speed, engage the accelerator pedal. While the accelerator pedal is engaged, the set speed, distance level, and target distance blink on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: However, if the accelerator pedal is depressed insufficiently, the vehicle may decelerate.
pair.new: However, if you do not engage the accelerator pedal sufficiently, the vehicle may decelerate.

pair.old: Be careful when accelerating temporarily as, because the speed and distance is not controlled automatically even if there is a vehicle in front of you.
pair.new: Be careful when accelerating temporarily, as Smart Cruise Control does not control the speed and distance automatically, even if there is a vehicle ahead of you.

pair.old: When the Based on Driving Style is enabled, the instrument cluster will show the set vehicle distance level and target vehicle distance in white. It will also display the distance level and target distance based on your driving behavior.
pair.new: When Based on Driving Style is enabled, the instrument cluster displays the set vehicle distance level and target vehicle distance in white. It also displays the distance level and target distance based on your driving style.

pair.old: If Smart Cruise Control is temporarily canceled automatically, a warning message will appear on the instrument cluster, and an audible warning will sound to warn the driver.
pair.new: If Smart Cruise Control is temporarily canceled automatically, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster, and an audible warning sounds.

pair.old: Smart Cruise Control will be temporarily canceled automatically when:
pair.new: Smart Cruise Control is temporarily canceled automatically when:

pair.old: Your driving speed is above 120 mph (190 km/h)
pair.new: Your speed is above 120 mph (190 km/h)

pair.old: The vehicle is stopped for a certain period
pair.new: The vehicle is stopped for a certain period of time

pair.old: The accelerator pedal is continuously depressed for a certain period
pair.new: You continuously engage the accelerator pedal for a certain period of time

pair.old: The conditions for the Smart Cruise Control to operate are not satisfied
pair.new: The Smart Cruise Control operating conditions are not satisfied

pair.old: If Smart Cruise Control is temporarily canceled while the vehicle is at a standstill with the function activated, EPB (Electronic Parking Brake) maybe applied.
pair.new: If Smart Cruise Control is temporarily canceled while the vehicle is at a standstill with the function activated, EPB (Electronic Parking Brake) may be applied.

pair.old: When Smart Cruise Control is temporarily canceled, the distance to a vehicle in front will not be maintained. Always have your eyes on the road and, if necessary, depress the brake pedal to reduce your speed to maintain a safe distance.
pair.new: When Smart Cruise Control is temporarily canceled, the distance from the vehicle ahead will not be maintained. Always have your eyes on the road and, if necessary, engage the brake pedal to reduce your speed and maintain a safe distance.

pair.old: If the Driving Assist button, (+) switch, (-) switch or () switch is operated when Smart Cruise Control operating conditions are not satisfied, a warning message will appear on the instrument cluster and an audible warning will sound.
pair.new: If you operate the Driving Assist button, (+) switch, (-) switch, or () switch when the Smart Cruise Control operating conditions are not satisfied, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster, and an audible warning sounds.

pair.old: In traffic situation
pair.new: In traffic

pair.old: In traffic, your vehicle will stop if the vehicle ahead of you stops. If the vehicle ahead starts moving, your vehicle will start as well.
pair.new: In traffic, your vehicle will stop if the vehicle ahead of you stops. If the vehicle ahead starts moving, your vehicle will start moving, as well.

pair.old: After the vehicle has stopped and a certain time has passed, a warning message will appear on the instrument cluster. Depress the accelerator pedal or operate the (+) switch, (-) switch or () switch to start driving.
pair.new: After you have stopped your vehicle and a certain period of time has passed, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster. Engage the accelerator pedal or push the (+) switch, (-) switch, or () switch to start driving.

pair.old: In the following situation, a warning message will appear on the instrument cluster, and an audible warning will sound to warn the driver of road conditions ahead.
pair.new: When the function detects surrounding vehicles, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster, and an audible warning sounds to warn you about the road conditions ahead.

pair.old: Always pay attention to vehicles or objects that may suddenly appear in front of you, and if necessary, depress the brake pedal to reduce your speed to maintain a safe distance.
pair.new: Always pay attention to vehicles or objects that may suddenly appear ahead of you and, if necessary, engage the brake pedal to reduce your speed and maintain a safe distance.

pair.old: While Smart Cruise Control is operating, when a collision risk with a vehicle ahead is high, a warning message will appear on the cluster, an audible warning will sound and the steering wheel will vibrate to warn the driver.
pair.new: When there is a high collision risk with a vehicle ahead while Smart Cruise Control is operating, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster, an audible warning sounds, and the steering wheel vibrates.

pair.old: Always have your eyes on the road and, if necessary, depress the brake pedal to reduce your speed in order to maintain a safe distance.
pair.new: Always keep your eyes on the road and, if necessary, engage the brake pedal to reduce your speed and maintain a safe distance.

pair.old: The distance from a vehicle ahead is near or the speed of the vehicle in front is faster or similar to yours
pair.new: A vehicle ahead is near, or the speed of the vehicle ahead is above or similar to yours.

pair.old: The speed of a vehicle in front is very slow or is at a standstill
pair.new: The speed of a vehicle ahead is very slow or at a standstill.

pair.old: The accelerator pedal is depressed right after Smart Cruise Control is turned on
pair.new: You engage the accelerator pedal right after turning Smart Cruise Control ON.

pair.old: Smart Cruise Control does not substitute for proper and safe driving. It is your responsibility to always check the speed and distance to a vehicle ahead.
pair.new: Smart Cruise Control does not substitute for proper and safe driving. It is your responsibility to always check your speed and distance from a vehicle ahead.

pair.old: Smart Cruise Control may not recognize unexpected, sudden or complex situations, so always pay attention to driving conditions and control your vehicle speed.
pair.new: Smart Cruise Control may not recognize unexpected, sudden, or complex situations, so always pay attention to driving conditions and control the speed of your vehicle.

pair.old: Keep Smart Cruise Control off when the function is not in use to avoid inadvertently setting a speed.
pair.new: Keep Smart Cruise Control OFF when you are not using it to avoid inadvertently setting a speed.

pair.old: Do not open the door or leave the vehicle when Smart Cruise Control is operating, even if the vehicle is stopped.
pair.new: Do not open any door or leave the vehicle while Smart Cruise Control is operating, even if the vehicle is stopped.

pair.old: Keep a safe distance according to road conditions and vehicle speed. If the distance is too close when driving at high speed, a serious collision may result.
pair.new: Keep a safe distance according to the road conditions and vehicle speed. Driving at a high speed at too close a distance may result in a serious collision.

pair.old: Vehicle speed may decrease on an upward slope and increase on a downward slope.
pair.new: The vehicle speed may decrease on an upward slope and increase on a downward slope.

pair.old: Always be aware of emergent situations such as when a vehicle cuts in suddenly.
pair.new: Always be aware of unexpected situations, such as when a vehicle cuts into your lane suddenly.

pair.old: When towing a trailer or something similar, your vehicle may experience frequent shifting and high RPM and the performance of Smart Cruise Control may be compromised. Always drive with caution.
pair.new: When towing a trailer or something similar, your vehicle may experience frequent shifting and high RPM, and the performance of Smart Cruise Control may be compromised. Always drive with caution.

pair.old: Turn off Smart Cruise Control when your vehicle is being towed.
pair.new: Turn OFF Smart Cruise Control when your vehicle is being towed.

pair.old: Smart Cruise Control may not operate properly if interfered with by strong electromagnetic waves.
pair.new: Smart Cruise Control may not operate properly in areas with strong electromagnetic interference.

pair.old: Smart Cruise Control may not detect an obstacle in front and lead to a collision. Always look ahead to prevent unexpected and sudden situations from occurring.
pair.new: Smart Cruise Control may not detect an obstacle ahead, leading to a collision. Always look ahead to prevent unexpected and sudden situations from occurring.

pair.old: Vehicles moving in front of you with frequent lane changes may cause a delay in Smart Cruise Control reaction or may cause Smart Cruise Control to react to a vehicle that is actually in an adjacent lane. Always drive cautiously to prevent unexpected and sudden situations from occurring.
pair.new: Vehicles frequently changing lanes ahead of you may cause a delayed Smart Cruise Control reaction, or may cause Smart Cruise Control to react to a vehicle that is actually in an adjacent lane. Always drive cautiously to prevent unexpected and sudden situations from occurring.

pair.old: Always be aware of the surroundings and drive safely, even though a warning message does not appear or an audible warning does not sound.
pair.new: Always be aware of your surroundings and drive safely, even when a warning message does not appear or an audible warning does not sound.

pair.old: If any other function’s warning message is displayed or warning sound is generated, Smart Cruise Control warning message may not be displayed and warning sound may not be heard.
pair.new: Smart Cruise Control may not display a warning message or generate an audible warning if any other function’s warning message is displayed or audible warning is generated at the same time.

pair.old: The vehicle manufacturer is not responsible for any traffic violation or accident caused by you.
pair.new: The vehicle manufacturer is not responsible for any traffic violation or accident you cause.

pair.old: If your driving style changes, distance, acceleration and reaction speed may change.
pair.new: If your driving style changes, the distance, acceleration, and reaction speed may change.

pair.old: Your vehicle must be driven sufficiently to analyze your actual driving style, such as vehicle distance, acceleration and reaction speed.
pair.new: You must drive your vehicle sufficiently for it to analyze your actual driving style, including vehicle distance, acceleration, and reaction speed.

pair.old: Based on Driving Style may not reflect the driver’s driving style or driving conditions which could affect driving safety.
pair.new: Based on Driving Style may not reflect your driving style or the driving conditions, which could affect safe driving.

pair.old: If you are driving in special conditions, such as snow, rain, fog or steep hills, your vehicle may not be driven according to your driving style.
pair.new: If you are driving in inclement conditions, such as snow, rain, fog, or steep hills, Smart Cruise Control may not drive your vehicle according to your driving style.

pair.old: Smart Cruise Control may not operate for a few seconds after the vehicle is restarted or the front view camera or front radar is initialized.
pair.new: Smart Cruise Control may not operate for a few seconds after you restart the vehicle or initialize the front view camera or front radar.

pair.old: You may hear a sound when the brake is controlled by Smart Cruise Control.
pair.new: You may hear a sound when Smart Cruise Control engages the brake.

pair.old: Based on Driving Style may not reflect your driving style if it’s is not safe such as rapid acceleration.
pair.new: Based on Driving Style may not reflect your driving style in unsafe ways, such as rapid acceleration.

pair.old: Based on Driving Style does not reflect any driving style other than vehicle distance, acceleration and reaction speed.
pair.new: Based on Driving Style does not reflect any aspects of driving style besides vehicle distance, acceleration, and reaction speed.

pair.old: If there is a malfunction in Smart Cruise Control, a warning message will be displayed on the instrument cluster (and turned off after a certain period), and the Master warning light () will turn on.
pair.new: When Smart Cruise Control is not working properly, the Master warning light () appears on the instrument cluster, along with a warning message that lasts for several seconds. Have Smart Cruise Control inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: When the front radar cover or sensor is covered with snow, rain, or foreign material, it can reduce the detecting performance and temporarily limit or disable Smart Cruise Control.
pair.new: When foreign material (such as snow or rain) covers the front radar cover or sensor, it can reduce the detection performance and temporarily limit or disable Smart Cruise Control.

pair.old: A warning messages and the Master warning light () are displayed on the instrument cluster (and turn off after a certain period). This does not indicate a malfunction of Smart Cruise Control.
pair.new: A warning message and the Master warning light () display on the instrument cluster (and turn off after a certain period). This does not indicate a malfunction of Smart Cruise Control.

pair.old: Smart Cruise Control will operate properly when snow, rain or foreign material is removed. Always keep it clean.
pair.new: Smart Cruise Control operates properly when you remove foreign materials (such as snow or rain) from the sensor. Always keep it clean.

pair.old: Even though a warning message does not appear on the instrument cluster, Smart Cruise Control may not properly operate.
pair.new: Even when a warning message does not appear on the instrument cluster, Smart Cruise Control may not operate properly.

pair.old: Smart Cruise Control may not properly operate in an open area where there is nothing to detect, or the detecting sensor is covered with foreign material after turning ON your vehicle.
pair.new: Smart Cruise Control may not operate properly in an open area where there is nothing to detect, or when foreign material covers the detection sensor after you turn your vehicle ON.

pair.old: Smart Cruise Control may not operate normally under the following circumstances:
pair.new: Smart Cruise Control may operate abnormally or unexpectedly under the following circumstances:

pair.old: Washer fluid is continuously sprayed, or the wiper is on
pair.new: Washer fluid is continuously sprayed, or the wiper is ON.

pair.old: Streetlights or light from an oncoming vehicle is reflected on a wet road.
pair.new: Streetlights or light from an oncoming vehicle are reflected on a wet road.

pair.old: The temperature around the front view camera is high or low
pair.new: The temperature near the front view camera is very high or low.

pair.old: The surroundings are very dark, such as in a tunnel, etc.
pair.new: Your vehicle’s surroundings are excessively bright or dark.

pair.old: The brightness outside is low, and the headlight are not on or are not bright
pair.new: The brightness outside is low, and the headlights are not ON or are not bright.

pair.old: Driving in heavy rain, snow or thick fog
pair.new: You are driving in heavy rain, snow, or thick fog.

pair.old: Driving through steam, smoke or shadows
pair.new: You are driving through steam, smoke, or shadows.

pair.old: Only part of a vehicle is detected
pair.new: Only part of a vehicle ahead is detected.

pair.old: A vehicle in front has no taillights, taillights are atypically located, etc.
pair.new: A vehicle ahead has no tail lights or the tail lights are atypically located.

pair.old: The brightness outside is low, and the taillights are not on or are not bright
pair.new: The brightness outside is low, and the tail lights of a vehicle ahead are not on or are not bright.

pair.old: The rear of a front vehicle in front is small or does not look normal (for example, tilted, overturned, etc.)
pair.new: The rear of a vehicle ahead is small or does not look normal (for example, tilted or overturned).

pair.old: A front vehicle’s ground clearance is low or high
pair.new: A vehicle ahead has low or high ground clearance.

pair.old: A vehicle suddenly cuts in front
pair.new: A vehicle suddenly cuts in ahead of you.

pair.old: An object reflects off the front radar such as a guardrail, nearby vehicle, etc.
pair.new: Light from an object (such as a guardrail or nearby vehicle) reflects off the front radar.

pair.old: The bumper around the front radar is impacted, damaged, or the front radar is out of position
pair.new: The bumper near the front radar is impacted or damaged, or the front radar is out of position.

pair.old: The vehicle in front is made of material that does not reflect on the front radar
pair.new: The vehicle ahead is made of material that does not reflect on the front radar.

pair.old: Driving near a highway, interchange or tollgate
pair.new: You are driving near a highway, interchange, or tollgate.

pair.old: Driving on a slippery surface due to snow, rain, ice, etc.
pair.new: You are driving on a slippery surface due to snow, rain, ice, and so on.

pair.old: Driving on a curved road
pair.new: You are driving on a curved road.

pair.old: A vehicle in front is detected late
pair.new: A vehicle ahead is detected late.

pair.old: A vehicle in front is suddenly blocked by an obstacle
pair.new: A vehicle ahead is suddenly blocked by an obstacle.

pair.old: A vehicle in front suddenly changes a lane or suddenly reduces speed
pair.new: A vehicle ahead suddenly changes lanes or reduces speed.

pair.old: A vehicle in front is bent out of shape
pair.new: A vehicle ahead is bent out of shape.

pair.old: A vehicle in front’s speed is fast or slow
pair.new: A vehicle ahead is moving very fast or slow.

pair.old: With a vehicle in front, you change lane suddenly
pair.new: You change lanes suddenly when there is a vehicle ahead.

pair.old: A vehicle in front is covered with snow
pair.new: A vehicle ahead is covered with snow.

pair.old: You are on a roundabout (rotary) and a vehicle in front is not detected
pair.new: You are driving in a traffic circle and a vehicle ahead is not detected.

pair.old: Adverse road conditions cause excessive vehicle vibration
pair.new: Adverse road conditions cause excessive vehicle vibration.

pair.old: Driving in the following places
pair.new: You are driving:

pair.old: Driving in a parking lot
pair.new: In a parking lot.

pair.old: Driving through a construction area, unpaved road, partially paved road, uneven road, speed bumps, etc.
pair.new: Through a construction area, unpaved road, partially paved road, uneven road, speed bumps, and so on.

pair.old: Driving through a roadside with trees or streetlights
pair.new: By a roadside with trees or streetlights.

pair.old: Driving through a narrow road with overgrown foliage
pair.new: On a narrow road with overgrown foliage.

pair.old: There is interference from electromagnetic waves, such as driving in an area with strong radio waves or electrical noise
pair.new: Through electromagnetic waves, such as an area with strong radio waves or electrical noise.

pair.old: Driving through a tunnel or on an iron bridge
pair.new: You are driving through a tunnel or covered bridge.

pair.old: On curves, Smart Cruise Control may not detect a vehicle in the same lane and may accelerate to the set speed. Vehicle speed may rapidly decrease when a vehicle ahead is detected suddenly.
pair.new: On curves, Smart Cruise Control may not detect a vehicle in the same lane, and may accelerate to the set speed. Your vehicle speed may rapidly decrease when a vehicle ahead is detected suddenly.

pair.old: Select the appropriate set speed on curves and apply the brake pedal or accelerator pedal according to the road and driving conditions ahead.
pair.new: Select the appropriate set speed on curves, and engage the brake pedal or accelerator pedal according to the road and driving conditions ahead.

pair.old: Your vehicle speed can reduce due to a vehicle in an adjacent lane.
pair.new: Smart Cruise Control may reduce your vehicle speed due to the presence of a vehicle in an adjacent lane.

pair.old: Check that the road conditions permit safe operation of Smart Cruise Control and if necessary, depress the brake pedal to reduce your driving speed to maintain a safe distance
pair.new: Check that the road conditions permit safe operation of Smart Cruise Control. If necessary, engage the brake pedal to reduce your speed and maintain a safe following distance.

pair.old: During uphill or downhill driving, the Smart Cruise Control may not detect a moving vehicle in your lane and cause your vehicle to accelerate to the set speed. Vehicle speed will rapidly decrease when a vehicle ahead is detected suddenly.
pair.new: When you drive uphill or downhill, Smart Cruise Control may not detect a moving vehicle in your lane, causing your vehicle to accelerate to the set speed. Your vehicle speed will rapidly decrease when a vehicle ahead is detected suddenly.

pair.old: Select the appropriate set speed on inclines and apply the brake pedal or accelerator pedal according to the road and driving conditions ahead.
pair.new: Select the appropriate set speed on inclines, and engage the brake pedal or accelerator pedal according to the road and driving conditions ahead.

pair.old: Your vehicle,
pair.new: Your vehicle

pair.old: When a vehicle (2) moves into your lane from an adjacent lane, it cannot be detected by the sensor until it is in the sensor’s detection range. Smart Cruise Control may not immediately detect the vehicle when the vehicle changes lanes abruptly. You must maintain a safe braking distance and, if necessary, depress the brake pedal to reduce your driving speed in order to maintain a safe distance.
pair.new: When a vehicle (B) moves into your lane from an adjacent lane, the sensor cannot detect it until it is in the detection range. Smart Cruise Control may not immediately detect the vehicle when it changes lanes abruptly. You must maintain a safe braking distance and, if necessary, engage the brake pedal to reduce your speed.

pair.old: In the following cases, some vehicles in your lane cannot be detected by the sensor:
pair.new: The sensor cannot detect the following types of vehicles or objects:

pair.old: Vehicles offset to one side
pair.new: Vehicles driving to one side.

pair.old: Slow-moving vehicles or sudden-decelerating vehicles
pair.new: Slow-moving or suddenly-decelerating vehicles.

pair.old: Vehicles with higher ground clearance or vehicles carrying loads that stick out of the back of the vehicle
pair.new: Vehicles with higher ground clearance, or vehicles carrying loads that stick out of the back of the vehicle.

pair.old: Vehicles that have the front lifted due to heavy loads
pair.new: Vehicles with the front lifted due to heavy loads.

pair.old: Vehicles within approximately 6 ft (2m) from your vehicle
pair.new: Vehicles within approximately 6 ft (2m) of your vehicle.

pair.old: Oncoming vehicles
pair.new: Oncoming vehicles.

pair.old: Stopped vehicles
pair.new: Stopped vehicles.

pair.old: Vehicles with small rear profile, such as trailers
pair.new: Vehicles with a small rear profile, such as trailers.

pair.old: Narrow vehicles, such as motorcycles or, bicycles
pair.new: Narrow vehicles, such as motorcycles or bicycles.

pair.old: Specialty vehicles
pair.new: Specialty vehicles.

pair.old: Animals and pedestrians
pair.new: Animals and pedestrians.

pair.old: In the following cases, the vehicle in front cannot be detected by the sensor. Always pay attention to the road and driving conditions and drive safely. If necessary, adjust your vehicle speed.
pair.new: Always pay attention to the road and driving conditions and drive safely. If necessary, adjust your vehicle speed. The sensor cannot detect the vehicle ahead in the following cases:

pair.old: You are steering your vehicle
pair.new: You are steering your vehicle.

pair.old: Driving on narrow or sharply curved roads
pair.new: You are driving on narrow or sharply curved roads.

pair.old: Always pay attention to road and driving conditions while driving.
pair.new: Always pay attention to the road and driving conditions.
Always pay attention to the road and driving conditions.

pair.old: When a vehicle in front of you merges out of the lane
pair.new: When a vehicle ahead of you changes lanes

pair.old: When a vehicle in front of you merges out of the lane, Smart Cruise Control may not immediately detect the new vehicle that is now in front of you.
pair.new: When a vehicle ahead of you changes lanes, Smart Cruise Control may not immediately detect the new vehicle that is now ahead of you.

pair.old: Always look out for pedestrians when your vehicle is maintaining a distance with the vehicle ahead.
pair.new: Always look out for pedestrians when your vehicle is maintaining a set distance from the vehicle ahead.

pair.old: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control can help maintain safe speed depending on the road conditions by using information from the navigation system when driving on highways while Smart Cruise Control is operating.
pair.new: When driving on a highway with Smart Cruise Control operating, Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control (NSCC) uses navigation system information to help maintain a safe speed according to road conditions.

pair.old: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control is available only on controlled access road of certain highways.
pair.new: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control is available only on certain highways.

pair.old: Additional highways may be added by future navigation updates.
pair.new: Additional highways may be become available for Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control in future navigation updates.

pair.old: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control operates on main roads and highways, and does not operate on interchanges or junctions.
pair.new: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control operates on main roads and highways, but not interchanges or junctions.

pair.old: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control (NSCC) is a supplemental system and is not a substitute for safe driving. It is your responsibility to always check the speed and distance to the vehicle ahead. Always drive safely and use caution.
pair.new: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control (NSCC) is a supplemental system and not a substitute for safe driving. It is your responsibility to always monitor the speed of and distance from the vehicle ahead, and to drive safely and cautiously.

pair.old: If vehicle speed is high, Highway Curve Zone Auto Slowdown function will temporarily decelerate your vehicle or limit acceleration to help you drive safely on a curve based on the curve information from the navigation.
pair.new: When driving at a high speed on a curve, Highway Curve Zone Auto Slowdown uses navigation system information to temporarily slow down or limit the acceleration of your vehicle to help you drive safely.

pair.old: If there is a problem with Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control, the function cannot be set from the Settings menu.
pair.new: If there is a problem with Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control, you cannot turn the function ON in the settings menu.

pair.old: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control is ready to operate if all the following conditions are satisfied:
pair.new: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control works under the following conditions:

pair.old: Smart Cruise Control is operating
pair.new: You are operating Smart Cruise Control

pair.old: Driving on main roads of highways (or motorways)
pair.new: You are driving on the highway or main road

pair.old: For more details on how to operate Smart Cruise Control, refer to .
pair.new: For more information about operating Smart Cruise Control, refer to .

pair.old: When Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control operates, it will be displayed on the instrument cluster as follows:
pair.new: When Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control is operating, the instrument cluster displays the following indicators:

pair.old: If all the operating conditions are satisfied, the green () indicator will appear.
pair.new: If all the operating conditions are satisfied, the green () indicator appears.

pair.old: During speed control, the green () indicator will blink.
pair.new: During speed control, the green () indicator blinks.

pair.old: If Smart Cruise Control cannot be operated due to pause or rerouting, the gray () indicator will appear on the instrument cluster.
pair.new: If Smart Cruise Control cannot operate because you have paused it or it is rerouting, the gray () indicator appears on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: If the accelerator pedal is depressed, the white () indicator will blink on the instrument cluster.
pair.new: If you have engaged the accelerator pedal, the white () indicator blinks on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: A warning message will appear in the following circumstances:
pair.new: A warning message appears when the Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control is not able to slow your vehicle to a safe speed.

pair.old: Depending on the curve ahead on the highway, the vehicle will decelerate, and after passing the curve, the vehicle will accelerate to Smart Cruise Control set speed.
pair.new: The vehicle decelerates depending on the curve ahead on the highway. After passing the curve, the vehicle accelerates to the Smart Cruise Control set speed.

pair.old: The starting point of deceleration depends on the vehicle’s driving speed and the curvature of the road. The higher the driving speed, the earlier the deceleration start point.
pair.new: The deceleration starting point depends on driving speed and road curvature. The higher the speed, the earlier the deceleration starting point.

pair.old: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control limitations
pair.new: Limitations of Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control

pair.old: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control may not operate normally under the following circumstances:
pair.new: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control may operate abnormally or unexpectedly under the following circumstances:

pair.old: The navigation is not working properly.
pair.new: The navigation system is not working properly.

pair.old: Speed limit and road information in the navigation is not updated.
pair.new: The speed limit and road information stored in the navigation system is out of date.

pair.old: Map information is not transmitted due to infotainment system’s abnormal operation.
pair.new: Map information is not transmitted due to abnormal operation of the infotainment system screen.

pair.old: The map information and the actual road is different because of real-time GPS data or map information error.
pair.new: Map information differs from the actual road because of real-time GPS data or map information errors.

pair.old: The navigation searches for a route while driving.
pair.new: The navigation is searching for a route while you are driving.

pair.old: GPS signals are blocked in areas such as a tunnel.
pair.new: GPS signals are blocked, such as in a tunnel.

pair.old: A road that divides into two or more roads and rejoins.
pair.new: A road divides into two or more roads and rejoins.

pair.old: The driver goes off course the route set in the navigation
pair.new: You leave the route set in the navigation system.

pair.old: The route to the destination is changed or canceled by resetting the navigation.
pair.new: You change or cancel the route to the destination by resetting the navigation.

pair.old: The vehicle enters a service station or rest area.
pair.new: The vehicle enters a service or rest area.

pair.old: The navigation cannot detect the current vehicle position (for example, elevated roads, including overpasses adjacent to general roads or nearby parallel roads).
pair.new: The navigation system cannot detect the vehicle’s current position (for example, you are driving on elevated roads, including overpasses adjacent to general roads or nearby parallel roads).

pair.old: The navigation is updating while driving.
pair.new: The navigation system updates while you are driving.

pair.old: The navigation is restarting while driving.
pair.new: The navigation system restarts while you are driving.

pair.old: Driving on a road under construction.
pair.new: You are driving on a road under construction.

pair.old: Driving on a controlled road.
pair.new: You are driving on a controlled road.

pair.old: Driving in bad weather, such as heavy rain or snow.
pair.new: You are driving in bad weather, such as heavy rain or snow.

pair.old: Driving on a sharply curved road.
pair.new: You are driving on a sharply curved road.

pair.old: When there is a difference between the navigation set route (branch line) and the driving route (main road), the Highway Curve Zone Auto Slowdown may not operate until the driving route is recognized as the main road.
pair.new: When there is a difference between the navigation set route (branch line) and the driving route (main road), the Highway Curve Zone Auto Slowdown may not operate until the system recognizes the driving route as the main road.

pair.old: When the vehicle’s driving route is recognized as the main road by maintaining the main road instead of the navigation set route, Highway Curve Zone Auto Slowdown function will operate. Depending on the distance to the curve and the current vehicle speed, vehicle deceleration may be insufficient or decelerate rapidly.
pair.new: Highway Curve Zone Auto Slowdown recognizes the vehicle’s driving route as the main road when you maintain the main road instead of the navigation set route. Depending on the distance from the curve and current vehicle speed, the vehicle may decelerate rapidly, or deceleration may be insufficient.

pair.old: When there is a difference between the navigation route (main road) and the driving route (branch line), Highway Curve Zone Auto Slowdown function will operate temporarily based on the curve information on the main road.
pair.new: When the navigation route (main road) and driving route (branch line) differ, the Highway Curve Zone Auto Slowdown function temporarily operates based on the main road curve information.

pair.old: When it is determined that you are driving out of the route by entering the highway interchange or junction, Highway Curve Zone Auto Slowdown function will not operate.
pair.new: When the navigation system determines that you are driving off-route by entering the highway change or junction, Highway Curve Zone Auto Slowdown function does not operate.

pair.old: If there is no destination set on the navigation, Highway Curve Zone Auto Slowdown function will operate based on the curve information on the main road.
pair.new: If you do not set a navigation destination, Highway Curve Zone Auto Slowdown function operates based on the curve information on the main road.

pair.old: Even if you depart from the main road, Highway Curve Zone Auto Slowdown function may temporarily operate due to navigation information of the highway curve section.
pair.new: Even if you depart from the main road, Highway Curve Zone Auto Slowdown function may temporarily operate based on navigation information about the curved section of the highway.

pair.old: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control is not a substitute for safe driving practices, but a convenience function. Always have your eyes on the road, and it is the responsibility of the driver to avoid violating traffic laws.
pair.new: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control is a convenience function, not a substitute for safe driving practices. Always keep your eyes on the road. It is your responsibility to avoid violating traffic laws.

pair.old: The navigation’s speed limit information may differ from the actual speed limit information on the road. It is your responsibility to check the speed limit on the actual driving road or lane.
pair.new: The navigation’s speed limit information may differ from the actual speed limit. It is your responsibility to check the speed limit where you are driving.

pair.old: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control will automatically be canceled when you leave the highway main road. Always pay attention to road and driving conditions while driving.
pair.new: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control cancels automatically when you leave the highway or main road. Always pay attention to the road and driving conditions.

pair.old: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control may not operate due to the presence of lead vehicles and their driving conditions. Always pay attention to road and driving conditions while driving.
pair.new: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control may not operate due to the presence of vehicles ahead of yours and their driving conditions. Always pay attention to the road and driving conditions.

pair.old: After you pass through a tollgate on a highway, Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control will operate based on the first lane. If you enter one of the other lanes, Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control might not operate properly.
pair.new: After you pass through a highway tollgate, Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control operates based on the first lane. If you enter another lane, Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control might not operate properly.

pair.old: Your vehicle will accelerate if you depress the accelerator pedal while Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control is operating. The function will not decelerate your vehicle. If you depress the accelerator pedal too lightly, the vehicle may decelerate.
pair.new: Your vehicle accelerates if you engage the accelerator pedal while Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control is operating. The function does not decelerate your vehicle. If you engage the accelerator pedal too lightly, the vehicle may decelerate.

pair.old: If the driver accelerates and releases the accelerator pedal while Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control is operating, the vehicle may not decelerate sufficiently or may rapidly decelerate to a safe speed.
pair.new: If you engage and then release the accelerator pedal while Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control is operating, the vehicle may not decelerate sufficiently, or it may rapidly decelerate to a safe speed.

pair.old: If the curve is too large or too small, Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control may not operate.
pair.new: If the curve is too large or too small, Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control may not change the speed.

pair.old: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control is a supplemental function and is not a substitute for safe driving. It is your responsibility to always check the speed and distance to the vehicle ahead. Always drive safely and use caution.
pair.new: Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control is a supplemental function, not a substitute for safe driving. It is your responsibility to always check the speed and distance from the vehicle ahead. Always drive safely and cautiously.

pair.old: A time gap could occur between the navigation’s guidance and when Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control operation starts and ends.
pair.new: A time gap could occur between the navigation system’s guidance and when Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control operation starts and ends.

pair.old: The speed information on the instrument cluster and navigation may differ.
pair.new: The speed information on the instrument cluster may differ from the navigation.

pair.old: Even if you are driving at a speed lower than Smart Cruise Control set speed, acceleration may be limited by curved road sections ahead.
pair.new: Even if you are driving at a speed lower than the Smart Cruise Control set speed, acceleration may be limited by curved road sections ahead.

pair.old: If Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control is operating while leaving the main road to enter an interchange, junction, rest area, etc., the function may operate for a certain period.
pair.new: If Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control is operating while you leave the main road to enter an interchange, junction, rest area, and so on, the function may operate for a certain period.

pair.old: Deceleration by Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control may feel it is not sufficient due to road conditions such as uneven road surfaces and narrow lanes.
pair.new: Deceleration by Navigation-based Smart Cruise Control may feel insufficient due to road conditions such as uneven road surfaces and narrow lanes. If you feel the deceleration is insufficient, engage the brake pedal to slow the vehicle.

pair.old: This device complies with Part 15 of the FCC rules.
pair.new: This device complies with Part 15 of the FCC rules
This device complies with Part 15 of the FCC rules
This device complies with Part 15 of the FCC rules

pair.new: Lane Following Assist can detect lane markings or a vehicle ahead on the road and center your vehicle in the lane.

pair.old: The front view camera is used as a detecting sensor to detect lane markings and front vehicles.
pair.new: The front view camera detects lane markings and vehicles ahead.
