pair.old: When the trailer’s connector is plugged into your vehicle, Lane Following Assist automatically turns off. In this case, you cannot get help from Lane Following Assist. Pay extra attention when you drive when the function is inactive. (If a Kia genuine Trailer Kit that can determine whether a trailer is connected is used)
pair.new: When using a Kia genuine Trailer Kit and the connector is plugged into your vehicle, Lane Following Assist automatically turns OFF. In this case, you cannot get help from Lane Following Assist. Pay extra attention when you drive while the function is inactive. (A Kia genuine Trailer Kit can determine whether a trailer is connected.)

pair.old: Turning Lane Following Assist On/Off
pair.new: Turning Lane Following Assist ON or OFF

pair.old: With the vehicle on, shortly press the Lane Driving Assist button located on the steering wheel to turn on Lane Following Assist. The gray or green () indicator light will appear on the cluster.
pair.new: To turn Lane Following Assist ON, briefly press the Lane Following Assist button on the steering wheel while the vehicle is ON. The gray or green () indicator light appears on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: Press the Lane Driving Assist button again to turn off Lane Following Assist.
pair.new: To turn Lane Following Assist OFF, press the Lane Following Assist button again.

pair.old: The following warning message will appear on the LCD display.
pair.new: The following warning messages appear on the infotainment system screen:

pair.old: If the vehicle ahead and/or both lane markings are detected and Your driving speed is below 110 mph (180 km/h), the green () indicator light appears on the instrument cluster, and Lane Following Assist can help center the vehicle in the lane by assisting the steering wheel.
pair.new: If the front camera detects a vehicle ahead or lane markings when you are driving below 110 mph (180 km/h), the green indicator light () appears on the instrument cluster, and Lane Following Assist can assist the steering wheel to help center your vehicle in the lane.

pair.old: When the steering wheel is not assisted, the white () indicator light blinks and changes to gray.
pair.new: When the steering wheel is not being assisted, the white indicator light () blinks and changes to gray.

pair.old: When the driver takes off their hands from the steering wheel for a few seconds, a warning message will appear and an audible warning will sound in stages.
pair.new: When you take your hands off the steering wheel for a few seconds, a warning message appears and an audible warning sounds in stages.

pair.old: If the driver still does not have their hands on the steering wheel after the hands-off warning message will appears and Lane Following Assist be automatically canceled.
pair.new: If you do not return your hands to the steering wheel after the hands-off warning message appears, Lane Following Assist is automatically canceled.

pair.old: Lane Following Assist does not operate at all times. It is the responsibility of the driver to safely steer the vehicle and to maintain the vehicle in its lane.
pair.new: Lane Following Assist does not operate at all times. It is your responsibility to safely steer the vehicle and stay in your lane.

pair.old: The hands-off warning message may appear late depending on road conditions. Always have your hands on the steering wheel while driving.
pair.new: The hands-off warning message may appear late depending on road conditions. Always keep your hands on the steering wheel while driving.

pair.old: If gloves are worn or the steering wheel is held lightly, it may be perceived as not being held, resulting in the Hands-Off Warning being displayed.
pair.new: If you wear gloves or hold the steering wheel lightly, Lane Following Assist may not detect your hands on the steering wheel and may display the hands-off warning.

pair.old: When both lane markings are detected, the lane lines on the cluster will change from gray to white.
pair.new: When both lane markings are detected, the lane lines on the instrument cluster change from gray to white.

pair.old: If lane markings are not detected, steering wheel control by Lane Following Assist can be limited depending on whether a vehicle is in front or the driving conditions of the vehicle.
pair.new: If lane markings are not detected, steering wheel assistance by Lane Following Assist may be limited depending on whether a vehicle is ahead or on the driving conditions of the vehicle.

pair.old: Even though the steering is assisted by Lane Following Assist, the driver may control the steering wheel.
pair.new: You can control the steering wheel even when Lane Following Assist assists with steering.

pair.old: The steering wheel may feel heavier or lighter when the steering wheel is assisted by Lane Following Assist than when it is not.
pair.new: The steering wheel may feel heavier or lighter than usual when Lane Following Assist assists with steering.

pair.old: When Lane Following Assist is not working properly, a warning message will appear and the Master warning light () will appear on the instrument cluster.
pair.new: When Lane Following Assist is not working properly, the Master warning light () appears on the instrument cluster, along with a warning message that lasts for several seconds.

pair.old: If this occurs, have Lane Following Assist inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the warning message and Master warning light appear on the instrument cluster, have the Lane Following Assist function inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: For more details on Lane Following Assist warnings, refer to .
pair.new: For more information about Lane Following Assist warnings, refer to .

pair.old: Highway Driving Assist detect lanes and vehicles ahead and help maintain the distance from the vehicle ahead and the set speed and center your vehicle in the lane while driving on the highway.
pair.new: Highway Driving Assist can detect lane marking and vehicles ahead to help maintain your vehicle’s following distance and set speed as well as center your vehicle in the lane while driving on a highway.

pair.old: Highway Lane Change Assist function can help change lanes to the direction you operate the turn signal switch if the function determines that lane change is possible.
pair.new: Highway Lane Change Assist can help you change lanes in the direction you operate the turn signal switch (if the function determines a lane change is possible).

pair.old: Highway Driving Assist is available only on controlled access roads of certain highways.
pair.new: Highway Driving Assist is only available on controlled access roads on certain highways.

pair.old: A controlled access road indicates roads with limited entrances and exits that allow uninterrupted high speed traffic flow. Only passenger cars and motorcycles are allowed on controlled access roads.
pair.new: A controlled access road has limited entrances and exits that allow uninterrupted high speed traffic flow. Only passenger cars and motorcycles are allowed on controlled access roads.

pair.old: Highway Driving Assist operates on main roads of highways and does not operate on interchanges or junctions.
pair.new: Highway Driving Assist operates on highway main roads, not interchanges or junctions.

pair.old: For more details on the precautions of the detecting sensors, refer to .
pair.new: For more information on detection sensor precautions, refer to .

pair.old: With the vehicle on, touch or select SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceDriving Convenience on the infotainment system to set whether to use each function.
pair.new: While the vehicle is ON, tap SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceDriving Convenience on the infotainment system screen to set options for each function.

pair.old: If Highway Lane Change Assist is selected, it can help change lanes safely.
pair.new: If you select Highway Lane Change Assist, the function can help you change lanes safely.

pair.old: When the trailer’s connector is plugged into your vehicle, Highway Driving Assist automatically turns off. In this case, you cannot get help Highway Driving Assist. Pay extra attention when you drive when the function is inactive. (If a Kia genuine Trailer Kit that can determine whether a trailer is connected is used)
pair.new: When using a Kia genuine Trailer Kit and the connector is plugged into your vehicle, Highway Driving Assist automatically turns OFF. In this case, you cannot get help Highway Driving Assist. Pay extra attention when you drive while the function is inactive. (A Kia genuine Trailer Kit can determine whether a trailer is connected.)

pair.old: Highway Driving Assist should be selected to use Highway Lane Change Assist.
pair.new: To use Highway Lane Change Assist, Highway Driving Assist must be active.

pair.old: If there is a problem with the function, the settings cannot be changed. Have the function inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If there is a problem with the Highway Lane Change Assist function, you cannot change the settings and should have the function inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: If the vehicle is restarted, the functions will maintain their last settings.
pair.new: The Highway Driving Assist and Highway Lane Change Assist functions maintain their last settings when you restart the vehicle.

pair.old: You can see the status of the Highway Driving Assist operation in the Driving Assist view on the instrument cluster. Refer to .
pair.new: The Highway Driving Assist operation status is visible in the Driving Assist view on the instrument cluster. For more information, refer to .

pair.old: Highway Driving Assist will be displayed as below depending on the status of the function.
pair.new: The Highway Driving Assist status displays on the instrument cluster as follows:

pair.old: Green HDA: Operating state
pair.new: Green: Operating

pair.old: Gray HDA: Standby state
pair.new: Gray: Standby

pair.old: White HDA blink: Accelerator depressed state
pair.new: White blinking: Accelerator engaged

pair.old: None: Off state
pair.new: Unlit: OFF

pair.old: Highway Driving Assist operates when:
pair.new: Highway Driving Assist operates under the following conditions:

pair.old: When driving on available roads, press Drive Assist button to turn on Highway Driving Assist.
pair.new: Press the Drive Assist button to turn Highway Driving Assist ON while driving on controlled access roads.

pair.old: When entering the main roads of highways while Smart Cruise Control is operating, Driving Assist will not turn on if Lane Following Assist is turned off.
pair.new: When entering a highway main road while Smart Cruise Control is operating, Highway Driving Assist will not turn ON if Lane Following Assist is turned OFF.

pair.old: When Highway Driving Assist is operating, your vehicle will stop if a vehicle ahead of you stops. If a vehicle ahead of you starts moving within 30 seconds after the stop, your vehicle will start as well. In addition, after the vehicle has stopped and 30 seconds have passed, a message will appear on the instrument cluster. Depress the accelerator pedal or operate the (+) switch, (-) switch or () switch to start driving.
pair.new: When Highway Driving Assist is operating, your vehicle will stop if a vehicle ahead of you stops. If the stopped vehicle ahead of you starts moving within 30 seconds, your vehicle will start as well. If the stopped vehicle ahead does not start moving within 30 seconds, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster. To start driving, engage the accelerator pedal or operate the (+), (-), or () switch.

pair.old: If you take your hands off the steering wheel for several seconds, a warning message will appear and an audible warning will sound in stages.
pair.new: If you remove your hands from the steering wheel for a few seconds, a warning message appears and an audible warning sounds in two stages:

pair.old: If the driver still does not have their hands on the steering wheel after the hands-off warning, a warning message will appear and Highway Driving Assist will be automatically canceled.
pair.new: If you do not return your hands to the steering wheel after the hands-off warning message appears, Highway Driving Assist is automatically canceled.

pair.old: Driving speed limit
pair.new: Driving the speed limit

pair.old: When Highway Driving Assist is canceled by the hands-off warning, The driving speed will be limited.
pair.new: The driving speed is limited when the hands-off warning cancels Highway Driving Assist.

pair.old: While Driving Speed Limit function is operating, a warning message will appear on the cluster, and an audible warning will sound continuously.
pair.new: When the Driving Speed Limit function is operating, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster and an audible warning sounds continuously.

pair.old: When your vehicle speed is above 40 mph (60 km/h), if a vehicle around you is driving at a close distance, your vehicle will control steering in the opposite direction of the vehicle to assist in safe driving. If there are vehicles in both sides of the lane that are driving close to you, the function will not veer to the opposite side of the lane.
pair.new: If a vehicle near you is driving at a close distance when your vehicle speed is above 40 mph (60 km/h), your vehicle will control steering toward the opposite direction to assist with safe driving. If there are vehicles driving close to you on both sides of your lane, the function will not steer your vehicle to the opposite side of the lane.

pair.old: When the Smart Cruise Control is temporarily canceled while Highway Driving Assist is operating, Highway Driving Assist will be in the standby state. At this time, Lane Following Assist will operate properly.
pair.new: If Smart Cruise Control is temporarily canceled while Highway Driving Assist is operating, Highway Driving Assist switches to standby, but Lane Following Assist continues to operate.

pair.old: You can see the status of the Highway Lane Change Assist function operation in the Driving Assist view on the instrument cluster. Refer to .
pair.new: The Highway Lane Change Assist status is visible on the instrument cluster. For more information, refer to .

pair.old: Highway Lane Change Assist function will be displayed as below depending on the status of the function.
pair.new: The Highway Lane Change Assist status is displayed on the instrument cluster as follows:

pair.old: Green () on: Ready state
pair.new: Green (): Ready

pair.old: Green () blink: Operating state
pair.new: Green () blinking: Operating

pair.old: Gray () on: Standby state
pair.new: Gray (): Standby

pair.old: White () blink: Canceled state (display only a certain time)
pair.new: White () blinking: Canceled (displays for a short time after cancellation)

pair.old: The lane line is displayed identical to Highway Lane Change Assist indicator (1). However, the lane detection availability will be showed on Standby state.
pair.new: The lane line displays with the same color as the Highway Lane Change Assist indicator (1). However, the lane detection availability is shown on Standby.

pair.old: The green arrow is displayed when a certain amount of time has passed after the function has started operating, and until the lane change has completed.
pair.new: The green arrow appears when a certain amount of time has passed after the function starts operating and until the lane change is completed.

pair.old: Message is displayed when the function does not operate even though the turn signal lever is used.
pair.new: A message appears if Highway Lane Change Assist cannot operate when you use the turn signal lever.

pair.old: Message is displayed when the function is canceled while operating.
pair.new: A message appears when Highway Lane Change Assist is canceled while operating.

pair.old: Highway Lane Change Assist function will turn on when the following conditions are satisfied.
pair.new: To turn Highway Lane Change Assist ON, use the Driving Assist or Lane Driving Assist button.

pair.old: Turning on Highway Lane Change Assist
pair.new: Turning ON Highway Lane Change Assist

pair.old: While Highway Lane Change Assist function is on, the function will be ready to operate when all the following conditions are satisfied:
pair.new: Highway Lane Change Assist is ready to operate when the function is ON and the following conditions are satisfied:

pair.old: Your driving speed is above 20 mph (30 km/h).
pair.new: Your speed is above 20 mph (30 km/h).

pair.old: When driving at low speeds (20 ~ 40 mph (30 ~ 60 km/h)), your vehicle can only be operated if vehicles in the left and right adjacent lanes are recognized, and there is no risk of collision when changing lanes.
pair.new: When driving 20-40 mph (30-60 km/h), your vehicle recognizes other vehicles in the left and right adjacent lanes, and there is no risk of collision when changing lanes.

pair.old: Hands-off warning is not displayed on the instrument cluster.
pair.new: The instrument cluster does not display the hands-off warning.

pair.old: Hazard warning flasher is off
pair.new: Hazard lights are OFF.

pair.old: While Lane Change Assist function is turned on (indicator on), Lane Following Assist will not cancel even if the turn signal indicator or hazard warning flasher is operating.
pair.new: While the Lane Change Assist function is ON, Lane Following Assist will not cancel, even if the turn signal indicator or hazard lights are operating.

pair.old: Lane Change Assist function turns off automatically when driven in the following road conditions:
pair.new: Lane Change Assist automatically turns OFF when you drive in the following conditions:

pair.old: One driving lane
pair.new: There is 1 driving lane.

pair.old: Roads lacking physical center separation structures (such as guardrails)
pair.new: Roads lack physical center separations structures, such as guardrails.

pair.old: There is a pedestrian or cyclist on the road ahead
pair.new: A pedestrian or cyclist is on the road ahead.

pair.old: If the driving speed slows down to less than 15 mph (25 km/h) when it is in ready state, it will change to standby state.
pair.new: If you drive below 15 mph (25 km/h) when Lane Change Assist is ready to operate, the function’s status changes to standby.

pair.old: When driving at low speeds (20 ~ 40 mph (30 ~ 60 km/h)), the vehicle will change to standby state if it is not recognized by vehicles in the left and right adjacent lanes.
pair.new: If you drive 20-40 mph (30-60 km/h) and your vehicle does not detect other vehicles in the right or left adjacent lanes, the Lane Change Assist status changes to standby.

pair.old: If a risk of collision is detected, the system will change to standby state.
pair.new: The Lane Change Assist status changes to standby if a risk of collision is detected.

pair.old: The images or colors may be displayed differently depending on the specifications of the instrument cluster or theme.
pair.new: The images or colors may display differently depending on the specifications of the instrument cluster or theme.

pair.old: When Highway Lane Change Assist function turns off while operating, steering assist will be temporarily canceled. Always be cautious while driving.
pair.new: When Highway Lane Change Assist turns OFF, Steering Assist is temporarily canceled. Always be cautious when driving.

pair.old: Highway Lane Change Assist operating
pair.new: Highway Lane Change Assist operation

pair.old: Highway Lane Change Assist function will operate, when you push the turn signal lever to (A) or (B) position while the function is in the ready state () indicator is green), and all the following conditions are satisfied:
pair.new: Highway Lane Change Assist operates when the function’s status is ready, you push the turn signal lever to the (A) or (B) position, and the following conditions are satisfied:

pair.old: Your hands are on the steering wheel
pair.new: Your hands are on the steering wheel.

pair.old: There is no collision risk in the direction of lane change
pair.new: The lane change direction has no risk of collision.

pair.old: There is a two-lane road with broken lines of any colors in the direction of the lane change
pair.new: You are driving on a 2-lane road with broken lines of any color in the direction of the lane change.

pair.old: There are no Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist and Blind Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist warnings
pair.new: There are no Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist or Blind Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist warnings.

pair.old: You are driving in the middle of the lane (should not be driving close to one side of the lane)
pair.new: You are driving in the center of the lane, not close to one side.

pair.old: The road you are driving on, or the road you are about to change lane is a road that the function can operate
pair.new: The road you are on or where you are about to change lanes is a road that meets Highway Lane Change Assist’s operation requirements.

pair.old: When the turn signal lever is placed at (A) position, the Highway Lane Change Assist function is performed. After that, if the turn signal lever is placed in neutral, Highway Lane Change Assist function is canceled before stepping on the lane.
pair.new: When you turn the turn signal lever to the (A) position, Highway Lane Change Assist operates. If you then turn the turn signal lever to neutral, Highway Lane Change Assist cancels before crossing the lane line.

pair.old: The Highway Lane Change Assist function is not canceled after stepping on the lane, but when the lane change is complete, it is canceled and the turn signal turns off.
pair.new: Highway Lane Change Assist does not cancel after you cross the lane line, but the function does cancel and the turn signal turns OFF when you complete your lane change.

pair.old: When the turn signal lever is placed at B position for a certain period, the green arrow will appear. Even when the lever is released and returns to its original position, lane change will still be assisted.
pair.new: When you turn the turn signal lever to the (B) position for a certain amount of time, the green arrow appears. Highway Lane Change Assist will still operate if you release the turn signal lever back to its neutral position.

pair.old: While lane change is occuring, the turn signal indicator will blink even when not holding the turn signal lever. The turn signal indicator will turn off when the lane change is complete.
pair.new: While you are changing lanes, the turn signal indicator blinks even if you do not hold the turn signal lever. The turn signal indicator turns OFF when you complete your lane change.

pair.old: Highway Lane Change Assist function will be in the standby state when one of the ready state conditions is not satisfied, or when entering or driving on one of the following roads:
pair.new: Highway Lane Change Assist enters the standby state when 1 of its ready state conditions is not satisfied or when driving on the following types of roads:

pair.old: Road within a certain distance from the tollgate on the main road of the highway (or motorway)
pair.new: Main highway roads within a certain distance of a tollgate

pair.old: Road ahead ends without an interchange or junction
pair.new: Dead ends without an interchange or junction

pair.old: Road with sharp curves
pair.new: Sharply curved roads

pair.old: Road with narrow lanes
pair.new: Roads with narrow lanes

pair.old: The function will be canceled when:
pair.new: Highway Lane Change Assist is canceled when:

pair.old: The turn signal lever is turned in the opposite direction of lane change
pair.new: You set the turn signal lever to the opposite direction of the lane change.

pair.old: The steering wheel is steered sharply
pair.new: You turn the steering wheel too sharply.

pair.old: If the turn signal switch is moved to position (A) while the Lane Change Assist is operating and then returned to the N (Neutral) position before changing lanes
pair.new: You turn the turn signal lever to the (A) position while Lane Change Assist is operating, then return the turn signal lever to the neutral position before you change lanes.

pair.old: While the function is operating, it will cancel if one of the following occurs:
pair.new: Highway Lane Change Assist is canceled under any of the following circumstances:

pair.old: Highway Driving Assist is turned off
pair.new: You turn Highway Driving Assist OFF.

pair.old: Lane Following Assist or Smart Cruise Control is turned off or temporarily canceled
pair.new: You turn OFF or temporarily cancel Lane Following Assist or Smart Cruise Control.

pair.old: Hands-off warning message is displayed on the instrument cluster
pair.new: The instrument cluster displays the hands-off warning message.

pair.old: The turn signal lever is placed at (A) position
pair.new: You turn the turn signal lever to the (A) position.

pair.old: The hazard warning flasher is turned on
pair.new: You turn the hazard lights ON.

pair.old: Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist or Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist warning message is displayed
pair.new: The instrument cluster displays a Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist or Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist warning message.

pair.old: Possible collision is detected in the next lane, even though there are no Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist and Blind Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist warning
pair.new: Your vehicle detects a possible collision in an adjacent lane, even without Forward Collision-Avoidance Assist and Blind Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist warnings.

pair.old: The target lane disappears
pair.new: The target lane ends.

pair.old: The target lane is not detected
pair.new: Your vehicle does not detect the target lane.

pair.old: There is a problem with turn signal lights
pair.new: There is a problem with the turn signal lights.

pair.old: Highway Lane Change Assist function is off. (The function turns off when: it is turned off from the settings menu; when the road changes to a one-way road; when there is an intersection or crosswalk ahead; when you enter a road with no structure, such as a median strip, guardrail, etc., or; when there is a pedestrian or cyclist in the driving lane.)
pair.new: The function is OFF because you turned it OFF in the settings menu, the road changes to a one-way, there is an intersection or crosswalk ahead, there is a pedestrian or cyclist in the driving lane, or you enter a road with no structure, such as a median strip or guardrail.

pair.old: If a risk of collision is detected
pair.new: Your vehicle detects a risk of collision.

pair.old: While the function is operating, if it is canceled, depending on the driving conditions, the vehicle may drive to the middle of the driving lane or steering assist may stop. Always pay attention to the road and driving conditions.
pair.new: If you cancel Highway Lane Change Assist while it is operating, the vehicle may steer toward the center of the driving lane or steering assist may stop, depending on driving conditions. Always pay attention to the road and driving conditions.

pair.old: You are responsible for controlling the vehicle for safe driving.
pair.new: You are responsible for driving safely and controlling the vehicle.

pair.old: Always have your hands on the steering wheel while driving.
pair.new: Always keep your hands on the steering wheel while driving.

pair.old: Highway Driving Assist is a supplemental function that assists you in driving the vehicle. It is not a complete autonomous driving system. Always check road conditions take appropriate actions to drive safely.
pair.new: Highway Driving Assist is a supplemental function that assists you in driving the vehicle. It is not a completely autonomous driving system. Always check road conditions and take the appropriate actions to drive safely.

pair.old: Always have your eyes on the road. It is your responsibility to avoid violating traffic laws. Kia is not responsible for any traffic violations or accidents caused by you.
pair.new: Always keep your eyes on the road. It is your responsibility to avoid violating traffic laws. Kia is not responsible for any traffic violations or accidents you cause.

pair.old: Highway Driving Assist may not be able to recognize all traffic situations. Highway Driving Assist may not detect possible collisions due to its limitations. Always be aware of its limitations. Obstacles such as vehicles, motorcycles, bicycles, pedestrians, or unspecified objects or structures such as guardrails, tollgate, etc., that may collide with your vehicle may not be detected.
pair.new: Highway Driving Assist may not recognize all traffic situations. Highway Driving Assist may not detect possible collisions due to the function’s limitations. Always be aware of the function’s limitations. The function may not detect obstacles (such as vehicles, motorcycles, bicycles, pedestrians, or unspecified objects) or structures (such as guardrails or tollgates) that may collide with your vehicle.

pair.old: Highway Driving Assist will turn off automatically under the following situations:
pair.new: Highway Driving Assist automatically turns OFF in the following situations:

pair.old: Driving on roads that Highway Driving Assist does not operate, such as a rest area, intersection, junction, etc.
pair.new: When driving on roads where Highway Driving Assist is not operable, such as rest areas, intersections, junctions, and so on.

pair.old: The navigation does not operate properly such as when the navigation is updating or restarting.
pair.new: When the navigation system does not operate properly (for example, when it is updating or restarting).

pair.old: Highway Driving Assist may inadvertently operate or turn off depending on road conditions (navigation information) and surroundings.
pair.new: Highway Driving Assist may inadvertently operate or turn OFF depending on road conditions, navigation information, and surroundings.

pair.old: Lane Following Assist function may be temporarily disabled when the front view camera cannot detect lanes properly or the hands-off warning is on.
pair.new: The Lane Following Assist function may be temporarily disabled when the front view camera cannot detect lane lines properly or when the hands-off warning appears.

pair.old: You may not hear the warning sound of Highway Driving Assist in noisy surroundings.
pair.new: You may not hear the Highway Driving Assist warning sounds if your surroundings are noisy.

pair.old: If the vehicle is driven at a speed above a certain speed on a curve, your vehicle may drive to one side or may depart from the driving lane.
pair.new: If you drive your vehicle above a certain speed on a curve, your vehicle may veer to one side or depart from the driving lane.

pair.old: When you are towing a trailer or another vehicle, turn off Highway Driving Assist for safety reasons.
pair.new: If you are towing a trailer or another vehicle, turn Highway Driving Assist OFF for safety reasons.

pair.old: For your safety, please read the owner’s manual before using the Highway Driving Assist.
pair.new: For your safety, please read the owner’s manual before using Highway Driving Assist.

pair.old: Highway Driving Assist will not operate when the vehicle is started, or when the detecting sensors or navigation is being initialized.
pair.new: Highway Driving Assist will not operate when you start the vehicle or initialize the detection sensors or navigation.

pair.old: Highway Driving Assist and Highway Lane Change Assist may not operate properly, or it may not operate under the following circumstances:
pair.new: Highway Driving Assist and Highway Lane Change Assist may operate abnormally or unexpectedly under the following circumstances:

pair.old: The map information and the actual road is different because the navigation is not updated
pair.new: The map information differs from the actual road because the navigation system is out of date.

pair.old: The map information and the actual road is different because of real-time GPS data or map information error
pair.new: The map information differs from the actual road because of real-time GPS data or map information errors.

pair.old: The infotainment system is overloaded by simultaneously performing functions such as route search, video playback, voice recognition, etc.
pair.new: The infotainment system is overloaded due to simultaneously performing functions such as route search, video playback, voice recognition, and so on.

pair.old: GPS signals are blocked in areas such as a tunnel
pair.new: GPS signals are blocked when you drive in areas such as a tunnel.

pair.old: You veer off course, reset the navigation route by changing the destination (including route change according to real-time road traffic information) or cancel the route to the destination
pair.new: You veer off-course, reset the navigation route by changing the destination (including changing the route according to real-time traffic information), or cancel the route to the destination.

pair.old: The vehicle enters a service station or rest area
pair.new: The vehicle enters a service or rest area.

pair.old: The navigation cannot detect the current vehicle position (for example, elevated roads, including overpasses adjacent to general roads or nearby parallel roads)
pair.new: The navigation system cannot detect the vehicle’s current position (for example, you are driving on elevated roads, including overpasses adjacent to general roads or nearby parallel roads).

pair.old: If your vehicle fails to recognize white single dashed lane lines and road boundaries
pair.new: Your vehicle fails to recognize white single dash lane lines and road boundaries.

pair.old: If some lanes are temporarily restricted
pair.new: Some lanes are temporarily restricted.

pair.old: If there are no physical separation structures, such as a center median, in the middle of the road
pair.new: The middle of the road does not have a physical separation structure, such as a center median.

pair.old: If the lane you intend to change to is a bus lane or a variable lane
pair.new: The lane you intend to change to is a bus lane or variable lane.

pair.old: If you have a trailer, carrier, or other equipment attached
pair.new: You have a trailer, carrier, or other equipment attached to your vehicle.

pair.old: For more details of front camera, front radar, front corner radar and rear corner radar sensor, refer to .
pair.new: For more information on the front camera, front radar, front corner radar, and rear corner radar sensors, refer to .

pair.old: Rear View Monitor displays the area behind your vehicle to help with safe parking.
pair.new: The Rear View Monitor (RVM) displays the area behind your vehicle to help you park safely.

pair.old: With the vehicle on, select the setup icon () on the screen or SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceParking SafetyCamera Settings from the infotainment system screen to change the Rear View Monitor settings.
pair.new: While the vehicle is ON, tap the setup icon () or tap SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceParking SafetyCamera Settings on the infotainment system screen to change the Rear View Monitor settings.
While the vehicle is ON, tap the setup icon () or tap SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceParking SafetyCamera Settings on the infotainment system screen to change the Rear View Monitor settings.

pair.old: If Extended Rear View Monitor use is selected, the rear camera is displayed even when shifting from R (Reverse) to N (Neutral) or D (Drive).
pair.new: If you activate the Extended Rear View Monitor setting, the rear camera view continues to display when you shift from R (Reverse) to N (Neutral) or D (Drive).

pair.old: If Rear-view Parking Guidance in the display information is selected, Rear-view Parking Guidance and Top Rear-view Parking Guidance are displayed in the rear monitor.
pair.new: If you activate the Rear View Parking Guidance setting, Rear View Parking Guidance and Top Rear View Parking Guidance display on the rear monitor.

pair.old: The setting menu may not be available for your vehicle depending on vehicle specifications.
pair.new: Depending on your vehicle’s specifications, the settings menu may not be available.

pair.old: The horizontal lines of the Rear-view Parking Guidance indicate distances of 20 in (0.5 m), 40 in (1 m) and 91 in (2.3 m) from the vehicle.
pair.new: The horizontal lines displayed by Rear View Parking Guidance indicate distances from your vehicle of 20 in (0.5 m), 40 in (1 m), and 91 in (2.3 m).

pair.old: The horizontal scale of the Top View Parking Guidance indicates distances of liftgate opening distance and 60 in (1.5 m) from the vehicle.
pair.new: The horizontal scale displayed by Top View Parking Guidance indicates liftgate opening distances and the 60 in (1.5 m) distance from your vehicle.

pair.old: Press the Parking/View button (1) to turn on Rear View Monitor.
pair.new: To turn Rear View Monitor ON, press the Parking/View button (1).

pair.old: Press the button again to turn off the function.
pair.new: To turn Rear View Monitor OFF, press the Parking/View button (1) again.
Press the button again to turn Surround View Monitor OFF.

pair.old: Rear-view
pair.new: Rear view

pair.old: The Rear-view function will turn on under the following conditions:
pair.new: Rear View turns ON under the following conditions:

pair.old: Shifting the gear to R (Reverse).
pair.new: You shift the gear to R (Reverse).

pair.old: Pressing the Parking/View button (1) while P (Park), or N (Neutral) and the vehicle speed is slower than 6 mph (10 km/h)
pair.new: You press the Parking/View button (1) while the gear shift is in P (Park) or N (Neutral). If the gear is in N (Neutral), vehicle speed must be lower than 6 mph (10 km/h) to operate the Rear View function.

pair.old: Pressing the View switching button (2) with the Rear top view on the screen allows you to select rear top view, rear-view, or rear wide view.
pair.new: To select Rear Top View, Rear View, or Rear Wide View, press the View switching button (2) on the Rear Top View screen.

pair.old: The Rear-view while parking function will turn off under the following conditions while parking:
pair.new: Rear View turns OFF when you are parking under the following conditions:

pair.old: Shifting the gear to P (Park)
pair.new: You shift the gear to P (Park).
You shift the gear to P (Park).

pair.old: Pressing the Parking/View button (1)
pair.new: You press the Parking/View button (1).
You press the Parking/View button (1).
You press the Parking/View button (1).
You press the Parking/View button (1).

pair.old: Pressing the back button (3) on the rear monitor screen
pair.new: You press the back button (3) on the Rear View Monitor screen.
You press the back button (3) on the Rear View Monitor screen.

pair.old: Pressing the infotainment system operation button (4)
pair.new: You press the infotainment system operation button (4).
You press the infotainment system operation button (4).

pair.old: N (Neutral) or D (Drive) and the vehicle speed is faster than 6 mph (10 km/h)
pair.new: The gear is in N (Neutral) or D (Drive) and the vehicle speed is above 6 mph (10 km/h).

pair.old: Rear-view will not turn off when the vehicle is in R (Reverse).
pair.new: When the gear is in R (Reverse), Rear View does not turn OFF.

pair.old: Extended Rear View Monitor function maintains the rear-view of the vehicle when shifting the gear from R (Reverse) to N (Neutral) or D (Drive) to help you park safely.
pair.new: To help you park safely, the Extended Rear View Monitor function maintains the Rear View display when you shift the gear from R (Reverse) into N (Neutral) or D (Drive).

pair.old: Rear View Monitor will maintain when the following conditions are satisfied:
pair.new: Extended Rear View Monitor operates when you shift the gear from R (Reverse) into N (Neutral) or D (Drive) and your vehicle speed is below 6 mph (10 km/h).

pair.old: Extended Rear View Monitor function will turn off when one the following conditions are satisfied:
pair.new: Extended Rear View Monitor turns OFF under the following conditions:

pair.old: The vehicle speed is faster than 6 mph (10 km/h)
pair.new: Vehicle speed is above 6 mph (10 km/h).

pair.old: Rear top view
pair.new: Rear Top View

pair.old: The rear top view shows an image of the vehicle looking down from above, allowing you to determine the distance from the rear vehicle or object when parking.
pair.new: The Rear Top View displays an image of the vehicle from above to help you determine the space between your vehicle and another vehicle or object when you are parking.

pair.old: Press the Rear Top View button to turn on the Rear Top View.
pair.new: To turn the Rear Top View ON, press the Rear Top View button.

pair.old: In all views, the video will not turn off when your vehicle is in R (Reverse) mode.
pair.new: Rear View video will not turn OFF when the gear is in Reverse (R), regardless of which view you select.

pair.old: Rear parking guidelines are displayed in the rear-view and rear top view. Select SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceParking SafetyCamera SettingsDisplay InformationRear Parking Guidelines from the settings menu in the infotainment system to display this function. Rear parking guidelines are not displayed in the rear-view while parking.
pair.new: The Rear View and Rear Top View can display Rear Parking Guidelines. To display Rear Parking Guidelines, tap SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceParking SafetyCamera SettingsDisplay InformationRear Parking Guidelines on the infotainment system screen’s settings menu. Note that Rear Parking Guidelines are not displayed on the Rear View while parking.

pair.old: When Rear View Monitor is not working properly, the screen flickers, or the camera image does not display properly, visit an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Visit an authorized Kia dealer if the Rear View Monitor does not work properly, the screen flickers, or the camera image does not display properly.

pair.old: When the vehicle is idling for a long time or when the vehicle is parked in an indoor parking lot, exhaust fumes may temporarily blur the image.
pair.new: Exhaust fumes may temporarily blur the Rear View Monitor image if your vehicle idles for a long time or you park your vehicle in an indoor parking lot.

pair.old: The wide-rear view camera does not cover the complete area behind your vehicle. You should always check the rear area from the inside and outside rear-view mirror before parking or backing up.
pair.new: The Wide Rear View camera does not encompass the complete area behind your vehicle. Always check the area behind your vehicle using the inside and outside rearview mirrors before parking or backing up your vehicle.

pair.old: The distance shown on the screen may differ from the actual distance. This is because the image shown on Rear View Monitor is displayed by calibrating the image from the wide-rear view camera. When the vehicle is tilted by cargo loading, rear parking guidelines may not be correct. Make sure to directly check the vehicle’s surroundings for safety.
pair.new: Distance shown on the Rear View Monitor screen may differ from actual distance due to image calibration by the Wide Rear View camera. If your vehicle is tilted due to loaded cargo, Rear Parking Guidelines may be incorrect. When you are parking or driving, be sure to directly check your vehicle’s surroundings for safety.

pair.old: Always keep the wide-rear view camera lens clean. If the lens is covered with foreign material, it may adversely affect camera performance and Rear View Monitor may not operate properly. Do not use chemical solvents such as strong detergents containing high alkaline or volatile organic solvents (gasoline, acetone etc.). This may damage the camera lens.
pair.new: Always keep the wide-rear view camera lens clean to maintain optimal camera performance and Rear View Monitor operation. Do not clean the camera lens with chemical solvents that may damage it, including strong detergents that contain high alkaline or volatile organic solvents such as gasoline, acetone, and so on.

pair.old: Surround View Monitor can assist in parking by allowing the driver to see around the vehicle.
pair.new: Surround View Monitor (SVM) can assist you with parking by allowing you to see around the vehicle.

pair.new: Detection sensor

pair.new: 1 Wide Front View camera

pair.old: Display Contents: Specify information that will be displayed on the parking assistance screen.
pair.new: Display Contents: Choose which information displays on the parking assistance screen.

pair.old: Rear-view Parking Guidance is displayed in the rear-view when Rear-view Parking Guidance is selected.
pair.new: If you select Rear View Parking Guidance, the function displays in the Rear View.

pair.old: When Top View Parking Guidance is selected, it is displayed on the top view to the right of the surround view monitor screen when the front or rear top view is activated.
pair.new: If you select Top View Parking Guidance, the function displays on the top right of the Surround View Monitor screen when Front or Rear Top View activates.

pair.old: The setting menu may not be available for your vehicle depending on vehicle features and specifications.
pair.new: Depending on your vehicle’s features and specifications, the settings menu may not be available.

pair.old: The parking guide horizontal lines in the rear view indicate distances of 20 in (0.5 m), 40 in (1 m) and 91 in (2.3 m) from the vehicle.
pair.new: The horizontal Parking Guidance lines displayed on the Surround View Monitor indicate distances from your vehicle of 20 in (0.5 m), 40 in (1 m), and 91 in (2.3 m).

pair.old: The horizontal scale of the Top View Parking Lines indicates distances of 79 in (2 m) from the vehicle.
pair.new: The horizontal scale displayed by Top View Parking Lines indicates a distance of 79 in (2 m) from your vehicle.

pair.old: With the vehicle on, select SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceParking SafetySurround View Monitor Auto On from the infotainment system screen to use the function.
pair.new: While the vehicle is ON, tap SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceParking SafetySurround View Monitor Auto On on the infotainment system screen to use the function.

pair.new: For more information about Surround View Monitor Auto On, refer to .

pair.old: The front-view function displays the vehicle’s front situation on the navigation system according to the driver’s settings, assisting in safe driving.
pair.new: The front-view function assists with safe driving by displaying activity ahead of the vehicle on the navigation system, according to your selected settings.

pair.old: While parking, the front-view of the Surround View Monitor is activated under the following conditions:
pair.new: The Surround View Monitor’s Front-view function activates when you are parking under the following conditions:

pair.old: P (Park) or N (Neutral) or D (Drive) position with a speed slower than 6 mph (10 km/h), Pressing the Parking/View button (1).
pair.new: Your vehicle’s gear is in P (Park), N (Neutral), or D (Drive) at a speed below 6 mph (10 km/h), and you press the Parking/View button (1).

pair.old: Shifting from R (Reverse) to N (Neutral) or D (Drive) and the vehicle speed is slower than 6 mph (10 km/h).
pair.new: You shift the gear from R (Reverse) to N (Neutral) or D (Drive) at a speed below 6 mph (10 km/h).

pair.old: Forward Parking Distance Warning warns the driver when the vehicle is in D (Drive)
pair.new: Forward Parking Distance Warning warns you while the gear is set to D (Drive).

pair.old: *(If SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceParking SafetySurround View Monitor Auto On on the infotainment system selected)
pair.new: *(If you have selected SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceParking SafetySurround View Monitor Auto On on the infotainment system screen).

pair.old: Pressing the view switching button (2) on the Surround View Monitor to select the Top View, Front-view, Side-view, or Wide View.
pair.new: Press the view switching button (2) on the Surround View Monitor to select Top View, Front View, Side View, or Wide View.

pair.old: While parking, the front-view of the Surround View Monitor will be turned off under the following conditions:
pair.new: The Surround View Monitor’s front-view function turns OFF when you are parking under the following conditions:

pair.old: Shifting to P (Park) or R (Reverse)
pair.new: You shift the gear to P (Park) or R (Reverse).

pair.old: Pressing the back button (3) on the Surround View Monitor screen
pair.new: You press the back button (3) on the Surround View Monitor screen.
You press the back button (3) on the Surround View Monitor screen.

pair.old: Pressing the infotainment system power button (4)
pair.new: You press the infotainment system power button (4).
You press the infotainment system power button (4).

pair.old: Driving faster than 6 mph (10 km/h)
pair.new: You drive above 6 mph (10 km/h).
You drive above 6 mph (10 km/h).

pair.old: When the front-view is activated, the last used view is displayed.
pair.new: When the Surround View Monitor Front View activates, the last view you used displays.

pair.old: The rear-view function of the Surround View Monitor displays the vehicle’s rear situation on the navigation system according to the driver’s settings, assisting in safe parking.
pair.new: The Surround View Monitor’s Rear-view function assists you with safe parking by displaying activity behind the vehicle on the navigation system, according to your selected settings.

pair.old: 3D view function
pair.new: 3D View function

pair.old: 3D view function shows the vehicle in various angles. Press the 3D view icon on the Surround View Monitor screen to choose the angle. Press the 3D view icon again to reset the angle.
pair.new: The Surround View Monitor’s 3D View function displays the vehicle from various angles. To select the angle, press the 3D View icon on the Surround View Monitor. To reset the angle, press the 3D View icon again.

pair.old: The 3D view of the Surround View Monitor is activated under the following conditions:
pair.new: The Surround View Monitor’s 3D View function activates under the following conditions:

old.old: P (Park) or N (Neutral) or D (Drive) position with a vehicle speed slower than 6 mph (10 km/h), or when selecting the 3D view button (2) while the Surround View Monitor is activated in R (Reverse) mode.
You shift the gear to P (Park), N (Neutral), or D (Drive) at a speed below 6 mph (10 km/h).

new.new: You shift the gear to P (Park), N (Neutral), or D (Drive) at a speed below 6 mph (10 km/h).

pair.old: The 3D view function of the Surround View Monitor while parking will be turned off under the following conditions:
pair.new: The Surround View Monitor’s 3D View functions turns OFF when you are parking under the following conditions:

pair.old: Shifting from N (Neutral) or D (Drive) to P (Park)
pair.new: You shift the gear from N (Neutral) or D (Drive) to P (Park).

pair.old: The 3D view does not display parking guidelines.
pair.new: The 3D View does not display parking guidelines.

pair.old: The top view screen, which is displayed with a Front/Rear-view or 3D view, converts the original images entered from the four wide view cameras to provide a 360-degree image around the vehicle that is viewed down.
pair.new: The Top View screen, which displays the Front View, Rear View, or 3D View, converts the original footage captured by your vehicle’s 4 wide-view cameras to provide a 360-degree image of the vehicle from above.

pair.old: Top view is not displayed with front/rear wide view.
pair.new: Top View does not display when you select Front or Rear Wide View.

pair.old: Zoom in or out on the top view by pinching the top view area.
pair.new: Pinch the Top View area to zoom in or out.

pair.old: When Surround View Monitor is not working properly, or the screen flickers, or the camera image does not display normally, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Have your vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer if the Surround View Monitor does not work properly, the screen flickers, or the camera image does not display properly.

pair.old: The screen may display abnormally and an icon will appear at the top left side of the screen under the following circumstances:
pair.new: The screen may display abnormally and show an icon at the top left side under the following circumstances:

pair.old: The liftgate is opened.
pair.new: You open the liftgate.

pair.old: The driver or front passenger door is opened.
pair.new: You open the driver’s door, or the front passenger opens their door.

pair.old: The outside rear-view mirror is folded.
pair.new: You fold the outside rearview mirror.

pair.old: ALWAYS look around your vehicle to make sure there are no objects or obstacles before moving the vehicle. What you see on the screen may differ from the actual vehicle’s location.
pair.new: Always look for objects or obstacles around your vehicle before moving. What you see on the screen may differ from the vehicle’s actual location.

pair.old: The distance shown on the screen may differ from the actual distance. This is because the image shown on Surround View Monitor is displayed by calibrating the image from the wide-rear view camera. When the vehicle is tilted by cargo loading, rear parking guidelines may not be correct. Make sure to directly check the vehicle’s surroundings for safety.
pair.new: Distance shown on the Surround View Monitor screen may differ from actual distance due to image calibration by the Wide Rear View camera. If your vehicle is tilted due to loaded cargo, Rear Parking Guidelines may be incorrect. When you are parking or driving, be sure to directly check your vehicle’s surroundings for safety.

pair.old: Surround View Monitor is designed to be used on a flat surface. If used on roads with different heights such as curbs and speed bumps, the image on the screen may not look correct.
pair.new: The Surround View Monitor is designed to be used on a flat surface, and the image on the screen may not look correct when used on roads with varying heights, such as curbs and speed bumps.

pair.old: Always keep the camera lens clean. If the lens is covered with foreign material, it may adversely affect camera performance and Surround View Monitor may not operate normally. Do not use chemical solvents such as strong detergents containing high alkaline or volatile organic solvents (gasoline, acetone etc.). This may damage the camera lens.
pair.new: Always keep the camera lens clean to maintain optimal camera performance and Surround View Monitor operation. Do not clean the camera lens with chemical solvents that may damage it, including strong detergents that contain high alkaline or volatile organic solvents such as gasoline, acetone, and so on.

pair.old: The Surround View Monitor shows the surrounding images of the vehicle by cameras mounted on the vehicle. The image shown on the screen may look unnatural depending on the vehicle and the surrounding conditions. The Surround View Monitor can improve its image by calibrating image through daily driving.
pair.new: The Surround View Monitor displays images of your vehicle’s surroundings using cameras mounted on the vehicle. The images shown on the screen may look unnatural depending on the vehicle and its surrounding conditions. Daily driving can calibrate and improve the Surround View Monitor’s image.

pair.old: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist detects vehicles approaching from the rear left or right while your vehicle is reversing and warns you of a possible collision with a warning message and a warning sound. Also, Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist may assist with braking your vehicle to help avoid a collision.
pair.new: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist detects vehicles approaching the rear of your vehicle from the left or right while reversing and uses a warning message and sound to warn you of a possible collision. Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist may also assist with braking your vehicle to help you avoid a collision.

pair.old: Warning time may vary depending on the speed of approaching vehicle.
pair.new: The warning time may vary depending on the speed of approaching vehicle.

pair.old: Refer to the picture above for the detailed location of the detecting sensors.
pair.new: The following picture shows the location of the sensors:

pair.old: For more details on the precautions of the rear corner radar, refer to .
pair.new: For more information about the rear corner radar, refer to .

pair.old: With the vehicle on, select SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceParking SafetyRear Cross-Traffic Safety on the infotainment system screen to turn on Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist.
pair.new: While the vehicle is ON, tap SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceParking SafetyRear Cross-Traffic Safety on the infotainment system screen to change Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist settings.

pair.old: When you restart the vehicle, Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist will always turn on. If Rear Cross-Traffic Safety is deselected after restart, you should always be aware of the surroundings and drive safely.
pair.new: When you restart the vehicle, Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist always turns ON. If you deselect Rear Cross-Traffic Safety after restarting the vehicle, you should always be aware of the surroundings and drive safely.

pair.old: When the trailer’s connector is plugged into your vehicle, Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist automatically turns off. In this case, you cannot get help Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist. Pay extra attention when you drive when the function is inactive. (If a Kia genuine Trailer Kit that can determine whether a trailer is connected is used)
pair.new: When using a Kia genuine Trailer Kit and the connector is plugged into your vehicle, Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist automatically turns OFF. In this case, you cannot get help from Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist. Pay extra attention when you drive while the function is inactive. (A Kia genuine Trailer Kit can determine whether a trailer is connected.)

pair.old: The Warning Methods can be set with the vehicle on. Select SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceWarning Methods from the settings menu in the infotainment system to change the following settings
pair.new: To change the Warning Methods settings while the vehicle is ON, tap SetupVehicleDriver AssistanceWarning Methods on the infotainment system screen. You can change the following settings:

pair.old: Collision warning will alert you with a warning light on the outside rear-view mirror, a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration.
pair.new: Collision warning alerts you with a warning light on the outside rearview mirror, a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration.

pair.old: Collision warning will also appear on the infotainment system screen.
pair.new: Collision warning also appears on the infotainment system screen.
Collision Warning also appears on the infotainment system screen.

pair.old: Collision warning will operate when all the following conditions are satisfied:
pair.new: Collision warning operates when all of the following conditions are satisfied:

pair.old: The gear is shifted to R (Reverse) while your driving speed is below 5 mph (8 km/h)
pair.new: You shift the gear to R (Reverse) while your speed is below 5 mph (8 km/h).
You shift the gear to R (Reverse) while your speed is below 5 mph (8 km/h).

pair.old: The approaching vehicle is within approximately 82 ft (25 m) from the left and right side of your vehicle
pair.new: The approaching vehicle is approximately 82 ft (25 m) from the left or right side of your vehicle.

pair.old: The speed of the vehicle approaching from the left and right is above 3 mph (5 km/h)
pair.new: The speed of the vehicle approaching from the left or right is above 3 mph (5 km/h).
The speed of the vehicle approaching from the left or right is above 3 mph (5 km/h).

pair.old: If the operating conditions are satisfied, there will be a warning whenever the vehicle approaches from the left or right side even though your vehicle speed is 0 mph (0 km/h).
pair.new: If the operating conditions are satisfied, there will be a warning whenever a vehicle approaches from the left or right side even if your vehicle speed is 0 mph (0 km/h).

pair.old: The images and colors on the instrument cluster may differ depending on the instrument cluster type or theme selected from the instrument cluster.
pair.new: The images or colors may display differently depending on the specifications of the instrument cluster or theme.

pair.old: Collision warning will alert the driver with a warning light on the outside rear-view mirror, a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration.
pair.new: Collision Warning can alert you with a warning light on the outside rearview mirror, a warning message, an audible warning, and steering wheel vibration.

pair.old: Emergency braking will be assisted to help prevent collision with approaching vehicles from the left and right.
pair.new: Emergency Braking assists you to help prevent a collision with an approaching vehicle from the left or right.

pair.old: Emergency braking will operate when all the following conditions are satisfied:
pair.new: Emergency Braking operates when all of the following conditions are satisfied:

pair.old: The approaching vehicle is within approximately 5 ft (1.5 m) from the left and right side of your vehicle
pair.new: The approaching vehicle is approximately 5 ft (1.5 m) from the left or right side of your vehicle.

pair.old: Brake control ends when the conditions of the approaching vehicle from the rear left or right side are as below:
pair.new: Brake control ends under the following conditions:

pair.old: The approaching vehicle is out of the detecting range
pair.new: The approaching vehicle is out of the detecting range.

pair.old: The approaching vehicle passes behind your vehicle
pair.new: The approaching vehicle passes behind your vehicle.

pair.old: The approaching vehicle does not drive toward your vehicle
pair.new: The approaching vehicle does not drive toward your vehicle.

pair.old: The approaching vehicle speed slows down
pair.new: The approaching vehicle slows down.

pair.old: You depress the brake pedal with sufficient power
pair.new: You engage the brake pedal with sufficient power.

pair.old: Stopping vehicle and ending brake control
pair.new: Stopping the vehicle and ending brake control

pair.old: When your vehicle stops due to emergency braking, a warning message will appear on the instrument cluster.
pair.new: When Emergency Braking stops your vehicle, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: You should depress the brake pedal immediately and check your surroundings.
pair.new: You should manually brake immediately and check your surroundings.

pair.old: Brake control will end after your vehicle is stopped by emergency braking for approximately 2 seconds.
pair.new: Brake control ends approximately 2 seconds after emergency braking stops your vehicle.

pair.old: During emergency braking, braking control by Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist will automatically cancel when the driver excessively depresses the brake pedal.
pair.new: During emergency braking, Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist automatically cancels braking control if you excessively engage the brake pedal.

pair.old: If any other function’s warning message is displayed or audible warning is generated, Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist’s warning message may not be displayed and audible warning may not be heard.
pair.new: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist may not display a warning message or generate an audible warning if any other function’s warning message is displayed or audible warning is generated at the same time.

pair.old: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate if you apply the brake pedal to avoid collision.
pair.new: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate if you manually brake to avoid a collision.

pair.old: During Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist operation, your vehicle may stop suddenly injuring passengers and shifting loose objects. Always have seat belts on and keep loose objects secured.
pair.new: During Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist operation, your vehicle may stop suddenly, injuring passengers and shifting loose objects. Always have seat belts on and keep loose objects secured.

pair.old: Even if there is a problem with Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist, your vehicle’s basic braking performance will operate properly.
pair.new: Your vehicle’s basic braking performance operates properly even if there is a problem with Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist.

pair.old: When Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist is operating, its braking control will automatically cancel when you excessively depress the accelerator pedal.
pair.new: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist automatically cancels braking control if you excessively engage the accelerator pedal.

pair.old: It is your responsibility to control your vehicle. Do not solely depend on Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist. Maintain a safe braking distance, and if necessary, depress the brake pedal to reduce speed or to stop your vehicle.
pair.new: It is your responsibility to control your vehicle. Do not solely depend on Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist. Maintain a safe braking distance and, if necessary, engage the brake pedal to reduce speed or stop your vehicle.

pair.old: Never deliberately operate Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist on people, animals, objects, etc. It may cause serious injury or death.
pair.new: Never rely on Rear Cross-Traffic Collision Avoidance to avoid a collision with people, animals, or objects. This may cause serious injury or death.

pair.old: The brake control may not operate properly depending on the status of ESC (Electronic Stability Control).
pair.new: The brake control may not operate properly depending on the status of Electronic Stability Control (ESC).
The brake control may not operate properly depending on the status of Electronic Stability Control (ESC).

pair.old: ESC (Electronic Stability Control) is engaged with a different function
pair.new: ESC is engaged in a different function.

pair.old: If braking is assisted by Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist, you must immediately depress the brake pedal and check the surroundings.
pair.new: If Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist assists with braking, you must immediately engage the brake pedal and check your surroundings.

pair.old: After shifting the gear to R (Reverse), braking control will operate once for left and right vehicle approach.
pair.new: After you shift the gear to R (Reverse), braking control operates once each for vehicles approaching from the left or right.

pair.old: If Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist is not working properly, a warning message will appear on the instrument cluster for several seconds, and the Master warning light () will appear on the instrument cluster.
pair.new: If Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist is not working properly, the Master warning light () appears on the instrument cluster, along with a warning message that lasts for several seconds.

pair.old: Have the function be inspected by an authorized Kia dealer. Visit an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Have the function inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Have the function be inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Have the function inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: When the rear bumper around the rear-side radar or sensor is covered with foreign material, such as snow, rain, trailer or carrier, it can reduce the detecting performance and temporarily limit or disable Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist.
pair.new: When foreign material (such as rain or snow) covers the rear bumper near the rear-side radar or sensor, or when you install a trailer or carrier, it can reduce the detection performance and temporarily limit or disable Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist.

pair.old: If this occurs, a warning message will appear on the instrument cluster.
pair.new: If this occurs, a warning message appears on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist will operate properly when such foreign material or trailer, etc., is removed.
pair.new: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist operates properly when you remove foreign materials (such as snow or rain) from the sensor or obstructions (such as a trailer or carrier) from the rear bumper.

pair.old: If Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist does not operate properly after it is removed, have the function be inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist still does not operate properly after you remove foreign materials (such as snow or rain) from the sensor or obstructions (such as a trailer or carrier) from the rear bumper, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Even though a warning message does not appear on the cluster, Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly.
pair.new: Even though a warning message does not appear on the instrument cluster, Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly.

pair.old: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly in an open area where any substances are not detected after turning ON your vehicle.
pair.new: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly in an open area where no structures are detected after you turn ON your vehicle.

pair.old: Turn off Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist to install or remove a trailer, carrier, or another attachment. Turn on Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist when finished.
pair.new: Turn OFF Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist to install or remove a trailer, carrier, or other attachment. Turn ON Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist when you are finished.

pair.old: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly, or it may operate unexpectedly under the following circumstances:
pair.new: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist may operate abnormally or unexpectedly under the following circumstances:

pair.new: You are departing from areas with overgrown foliage.

pair.new: You are departing from areas where roads are wet.

pair.new: The speed of an approaching vehicle is fast or slow.

pair.new: Braking control may not work, and your attention is required in the following circumstances:

pair.new: The vehicle vibrates severely while you drive over a bumpy, uneven road or pothole.

pair.old: Driving on a slippery surface such as snow, rain, ice, etc.
pair.new: You drive on a slippery surface such as snow, rain, ice, and so on.

pair.old: The tire pressure is low or a tire is damaged
pair.new: The tire pressure is low or a tire is damaged.

pair.new: Remote Smart Parking Assist is operating (if equipped).

pair.old: Driving near a vehicle or structure
pair.new: You are driving near a vehicle or structure

pair.new: The vehicle is in a complex parking environment

pair.new: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist may detect vehicles that are parking or pulling out near your vehicle (for example, a vehicle leaving from beside your vehicle, a vehicle parking or pulling out behind your vehicle, a vehicle that is approaching your vehicle making a turn, and so on).

pair.old: The function may unnecessarily warn the driver and control the brake.
pair.new: The function may unnecessarily warn you and control the brake.

pair.old: When the vehicle is parked diagonally
pair.new: The vehicle is parked diagonally

pair.new: [A]: Vehicle

pair.new: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist may be limited when you back up diagonally and may not detect a vehicle approaching from the left or right. The function may not warn you or control the brakes when necessary.

pair.old: When the vehicle is on or near a slope
pair.new: The vehicle is on or near a slope

pair.new: You are pulling into a parking space where there is a structure

pair.new: [A]: Structure

pair.new: The vehicle is parked backward

pair.new: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist may detect vehicles passing behind you when you park in a parking space in reverse. The function may unnecessarily warn you and control the brake.

pair.new: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly if there is strong electromagnetic interference.

pair.new: Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist may not activate for 3 seconds after you start your vehicle or initialize the rear corner radars.

pair.old: Backward Safety: It warns or assists in braking when there is a high risk of collision with pedestrians or objects in the rear direction.
pair.new: Backward Safety: This function warns you or assists you with braking when there is a high risk of collision with pedestrians or objects behind your vehicle.

pair.old: When the trailer’s connector is plugged into your vehicle, Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist automatically turns off. In this case, you cannot get help Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist. Pay extra attention when you drive when the function is inactive. (If a Kia genuine Trailer Kit that can determine whether a trailer is connected is used)
pair.new: When using a Kia genuine Trailer Kit and the connector is plugged into your vehicle, Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist automatically turns OFF. In this case, you cannot get help from Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist. Pay extra attention when you drive while the function is inactive. (A Kia genuine Trailer Kit can determine whether a trailer is connected.)

pair.old: Backward safety will be selected and Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist settings will be retained whenever your vehicle is restarted.
pair.new: When you restart your vehicle, Backward Safety is selected and Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist settings are retained.

pair.old: The setting menu may not be available for your vehicle depending on the vehicle features and specifications.
pair.new: The Warning Methods setting menu may not be available on all vehicles.

pair.old: Reverse Parking Collision- Avoidance Assist operation
pair.new: Reverse Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist operation

pair.old: Turning On/Off
pair.new: Turning ON or OFF

pair.old: Press and hold the Parking Safety () button more than 2 seconds to turn the Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist on or off.
pair.new: Press and hold the Parking Safety button () for more than 2 seconds to turn the Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist ON or OFF.

pair.old: When detecting a risk of collision with pedestrians or objects behind the vehicle, the warning sound and steering wheel will vibrate and the warning appears in the cluster. If the Surround View Monitor is active, the Infotainment system screen also displays a warning.
pair.new: When Reverse Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist detects a risk of collision with pedestrians or objects behind the vehicle, an audible warning sounds, the steering wheel vibrates, and a warning message appears on the instrument cluster. If the Surround View Monitor is active, the infotainment system screen also displays a warning message.

pair.old: If a collision is urgent, the Reverse Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist controls the breaking of the vehicle.
pair.new: If a collision risk is urgent, Reverse Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist controls the vehicle’s braking.

pair.old: Select Backward safety from the Parking safety menu of the infotainment system. Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist is enabled when the following conditions are satisfied:
pair.new: Tap Backward Safety on the Parking Safety menu on the infotainment system screen. Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist is enabled under the following conditions:

pair.old: The liftgate and doors are closed
pair.new: The liftgate and doors are closed.

pair.old: The Electronic Parking Brake (EPB) is released
pair.new: The Electronic Parking Brake (EPB) is released.

pair.old: The gear is shifted to R (Reverse)
pair.new: The gear is shifted to R (Reverse).

pair.old: Your driving speed is below 6 mph (10 km/h) (detecting pedestrians)
pair.new: Your speed is below 6 mph (10 km/h) (detecting pedestrians).

pair.old: Your driving speed is below 1 mph (4 km/h) (detecting objects)
pair.new: Your speed is below 1 mph (4 km/h) (detecting objects).

pair.old: Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist components such as the wide-rear view camera and the rear ultrasonic sensors are in normal conditions
pair.new: Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist components (such as the wide-rear view camera and the rear ultrasonic sensors) are in normal condition.

pair.old: When Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist activates, a line appears at the bottom of the vehicle image in the instrument cluster.
pair.new: When Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist activates, a line appears at the bottom of the vehicle’s image in the instrument cluster.

pair.old: Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist operates only once after shifting the gear to R (Reverse). To reactivate Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist, shift the gear from another gear to R (Reverse).
pair.new: Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist operates only once after you shift the gear to R (Reverse). To reactivate Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist, shift from another gear to R (Reverse).

pair.old: If Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist detects a risk of collision around your vehicle with a pedestrian or an object, Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist will warn the driver with an audible warning and warning message on the instrument cluster. If the infotainment screen is on, a warning will appear on the screen.
pair.new: If Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist detects the risk of a collision between your vehicle and a pedestrian or object, Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist warns you with an audible warning and warning message on the instrument cluster. If the infotainment system screen is ON, a warning appears on the screen.

pair.old: Braking assist is released after 5 minutes. Immediately depress the brake pedal and check your surroundings.
pair.new: Braking assist releases after 5 minutes. Immediately engage the brake pedal and check your surroundings.

pair.old: Braking assist is also released under the following conditions when:
pair.new: Braking assist also releases under the following conditions:

pair.old: The gear is shifted to P (Park) or D (Drive)
pair.new: You shift the gear to P (Park) or D (Drive).

pair.old: The brake pedal is depressed with sufficient power
pair.new: You manually brake with sufficient power.

pair.old: The warning is released when shifting to ’P’ (Park), ’N’ (Neutral), or ’D’ (Drive).
pair.new: You release the warning by shifting to P (Park), N (Neutral), or D (Drive).

pair.old: When Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist is activated while reversing, braking control will be released after 5 minutes and the Electronic Parking Brake (EPB) will be engaged.
pair.new: When Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist activates while you are reversing, braking control is released after 5 minutes, and the Electronic Parking Brake (EPB) is engaged.
