pair.old: Reverse Parking Collision- Avoidance Assist malfunction and limitations
pair.new: Reverse Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist malfunction and limitations

pair.old: Reverse Parking Collision- Avoidance Assist malfunction
pair.new: Reverse Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist malfunction

pair.old: If there is a problem with Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist or related functions and parts, a warning message is displayed on the instrument cluster. If it still does not work properly, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If there is a problem with Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist or related functions and parts, a warning message displays on the instrument cluster. If the function still does not work properly, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: The wide-rear view camera and rear ultrasonic sensors detect objects around your vehicle. Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist may be temporarily limited or may not operate if snow, rain, foreign substances, etc. get on the sensor. You can check the detection sensor blind warning target (wide-rear view camera, rear ultrasonic sensor) in the service message of the utility information view in the instrument cluster display window. Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist works normally if you remove the contamination from the recognition sensor. Always keep it clean. If it still does not work properly even after decontamination is removed, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: The wide-rear view camera and rear ultrasonic sensors detect objects around your vehicle. Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist may be temporarily limited or may not operate if snow, rain, foreign substances, and so on obscure the sensor. You can check the detection sensor blind warning target (wide-rear view camera and rear ultrasonic sensor) in the service message of the utility information view in the instrument cluster. Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist works normally if you remove the contamination from the recognition sensor. Always keep it clean. If the function still does not work properly even after you remove the contamination, have the vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist may not assist braking or warn the driver even if there are pedestrians or objects under the following circumstances:
pair.new: Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist may not assist you with braking, even if there are pedestrians or objects behind the vehicle, under the following circumstances:

pair.old: There is a problem with the vehicle
pair.new: There is a problem with the vehicle:

pair.old: Any non-factory equipment or accessory is installed
pair.new: You install any non-factory equipment or accessory.

pair.old: Braking system components, such as brake discs and calipers, are modified
pair.new: You modify the braking system components, such as brake discs and calipers.

pair.old: Wheels are misaligned or suspension components are modified
pair.new: The wheels are misaligned, or the suspension components are modified.

pair.old: Accessories are attached to the steering wheel or steering components are modified
pair.new: You have attached accessories to the steering wheel or modified the steering components.

pair.old: Your vehicle is unstable due to an accident or other causes
pair.new: Your vehicle is unstable due to an accident or other causes.

pair.old: Bumper height or ultrasonic sensor installation has been modified
pair.new: You have modified the bumper height or ultrasonic sensor installation.

pair.old: If there is severe tilting of the overall height due to abnormal tire pressure or excessive loading in the cargo area
pair.new: The overall height of the vehicle is severely tilted due to abnormal tire pressure or excessive loading in the cargo area.

pair.old: Wide view camera(s) or ultrasonic sensor(s) are damaged
pair.new: The wide view cameras or ultrasonic sensors are damaged.

pair.old: Wide view camera(s) or the ultrasonic sensor(s) is covered with foreign material, such as snow, dirt, etc.
pair.new: The wide view cameras or ultrasonic sensors are covered with foreign material such as snow or dirt.

pair.old: A snow chain, spare tire, or wheel of a different size is installed
pair.new: You have installed a snow chain, spare tire, or wheel of a different size.

pair.old: There is a problem with the surroundings
pair.new: There is a problem with the surroundings:

pair.old: If there is a pattern on the road
pair.new: There is a pattern on the road.

pair.old: When the shadow on the ground or reflected light
pair.new: There is a shadow or reflected light on the ground

pair.old: Pedestrians or objects are nearby the vehicle’s path
pair.new: Pedestrians or objects are near the vehicle’s path.

pair.old: Driving through a narrow track or a parking space
pair.new: You are driving through a narrow track or a parking space.

pair.old: Driving on uneven roads such as unpaved roads, gravel roads, speed bumps or inclined roads, etc.
pair.new: You are driving on an uneven road such as an unpaved road, gravel road, speed bump, or inclined road.

pair.old: Wide view camera(s) is obscured by a light source or by inclement weather, such as heavy rain, fog, snow, etc.
pair.new: Wide view cameras are obscured by a light source or by inclement weather, such as heavy rain, fog, snow, and so on.

pair.old: The surroundings are very bright or very dark
pair.new: The surroundings are extremely bright or dark.

pair.old: Outside temperature is very high or very low
pair.new: Outside temperature is extremely high or low.

pair.old: The wind is either strong (above 12 mph (20 km/h)) or blowing perpendicular to the rear bumper
pair.new: The wind is either strong (above 12 mph (20 km/h)) or blowing perpendicular to the rear bumper.

pair.old: Objects generating excessive noise, such as vehicle horns, loud motorcycles or truck air brakes, are near your vehicle
pair.new: Objects that generate excessive noise (such as vehicle horns, loud motorcycles, or truck air brakes) are near your vehicle.

pair.old: An object that generates ultrasonic waves is nearby
pair.new: Objects that generate ultrasonic waves are near your vehicle.

pair.old: A wireless device with a transmission function operates near the rear ultrasonic sensor
pair.new: A wireless device with a transmission function is operating near the rear ultrasonic sensor.

pair.old: It is affected by another vehicle’s parking distance warning function
pair.new: Your vehicle is affected by another vehicle’s parking distance warning function.

pair.old: The road is slippery or inclined
pair.new: The road is slippery or inclined.

pair.old: There is a situation with pedestrians or objects
pair.new: There is a situation with pedestrians or objects:

pair.old: The pedestrians are difficult to detect
pair.new: The pedestrian is difficult to detect.

pair.old: There is ground height difference between your vehicle and the pedestrian
pair.new: There is ground height difference between your vehicle and the pedestrian.

pair.old: The image of a pedestrian in the wide-rear view camera is indistinguishable from the background
pair.new: The image of the pedestrian in the wide-rear view camera is indistinguishable from the background.

pair.old: The pedestrian is near the rear edge of the vehicle
pair.new: The pedestrian is near the rear edge of the vehicle.

pair.old: The pedestrian is not standing upright
pair.new: The pedestrian is not standing upright.

pair.old: The pedestrian is either too short or tall to detect
pair.new: The pedestrian is either too short or tall to detect.

pair.old: The pedestrian or cyclist is wearing clothing that easily blends into the background, making it difficult to detect
pair.new: The pedestrian is wearing clothing that easily blends into the background, making them difficult to detect.

pair.old: The pedestrian is wearing clothing that does not reflect ultrasonic waves
pair.new: The pedestrian is wearing clothing that does not reflect ultrasonic waves.

pair.old: Size, thickness, height, or shape of the object does not reflect ultrasonic waves (for example, a pole, bush, curbs, carts, edge of a wall, etc.)
pair.new: The object’s size, thickness, height, or shape does not reflect ultrasonic waves (for example, a pole, bush, curb, cart, the edge of a wall, and so on).

pair.old: The pedestrian or the object is moving
pair.new: The pedestrian or object is moving.

pair.old: The pedestrian or object is very close to the rear of your vehicle
pair.new: The pedestrian or object is very close to the rear of your vehicle.

pair.old: There is a large object such as a wall behind a pedestrian or the object
pair.new: There is a large object (such as a wall) behind the pedestrian or the object.

pair.old: The object is not located at the front or rear center of your vehicle
pair.new: The object is not located near the front or rear center of your vehicle.

pair.old: The object is not parallel to the rear bumper
pair.new: The object is not parallel with the rear bumper.

pair.old: The face of the object is not parallel to the bumper
pair.new: The face of the object is not parallel with the rear bumper.

pair.old: There is a situation with the driving conditions
pair.new: There is a situation with the driving conditions:

pair.old: You drive the vehicle immediately after shifting to R (Reverse) or D (Drive)
pair.new: You drive the vehicle immediately after shifting the gear to R (Reverse) or D (Drive).

pair.old: You accelerate or circle the vehicle
pair.new: You accelerate or drive in a circle.

pair.old: You drive immediately after starting the vehicle
pair.new: You drive immediately after starting the vehicle.

pair.old: The performance of Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist may vary under certain conditions. If vehicle speed is above 2 mph (4 km/h), Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist will provide collision avoidance assistance only when pedestrians are detected. Always look around and pay attention when driving your vehicle.
pair.new: The performance of Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist may vary under certain conditions. If the vehicle’s speed is above 2 mph (4 km/h), Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist will operate only when pedestrians are detected. Always look around and pay attention when driving your vehicle.

pair.old: Some objects may not be detected by the rear ultrasonic sensors due to the objects distance, size or material, all of which can limit the effectiveness of the sensors.
pair.new: Some objects may not be detected by the rear ultrasonic sensors due to the objects’ distance from the vehicle, size, or material, all of which can limit the effectiveness of the sensors.

pair.old: Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly or may operate unnecessarily depending on the road conditions and the surroundings.
pair.new: Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly or may operate unnecessarily depending on the road conditions and surroundings.

pair.old: Always keep the wide-rear view camera and ultrasonic sensors clean.
pair.new: Always keep the Wide Rear View camera and ultrasonic sensors clean.

pair.old: Do not spray the wide-rear view camera or the rear ultrasonic sensors or their surrounding area directly with a high pressure washer. It may cause the wide-rear view camera or the ultrasonic sensors to malfunction.
pair.new: Do not spray the Wide Rear View camera, the rear ultrasonic sensors, or their surrounding areas directly with a high pressure washer. This may cause the Wide Rear View camera or ultrasonic sensors to malfunction.

pair.old: Do not apply objects, such as a bumper sticker or a bumper guard, near the wide-rear view camera or ultrasonic sensors or apply paint to the bumper. Doing so may adversely affect the performance of Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist.
pair.new: Do not apply objects (such as a bumper sticker or bumper guard) near the Wide Rear View camera or ultrasonic sensors, or apply paint to the bumper. Doing so may adversely affect the performance of Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist.

pair.old: Never disassemble or impact the wide-rear view camera or the components of the ultrasonic sensor.
pair.new: Never disassemble or damage the Wide Rear View camera or the components of the ultrasonic sensor.

pair.old: Do not apply unnecessary force on the wide-rear view camera or the ultrasonic sensors. Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly if the wide-rear view camera or the ultrasonic sensor(s) are forcibly moved out of proper alignment. Visit an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Do not apply unnecessary force to the Wide Rear View camera or ultrasonic sensors. Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly if you forcibly move the Wide Rear View camera or ultrasonic sensors out of proper alignment. If Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist does not operate properly, visit an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Noise may be heard when sudden braking occurs to avoid a collision.
pair.new: You may hear noise when braking suddenly to avoid a collision.

pair.old: If any other warning sound such as the seat belt warning chime is already generated, Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist warning may not sound.
pair.new: If any other warning sound (such as the seatbelt warning chime) is already generated, the Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist warning may not sound.

pair.old: Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist may not work properly if the bumper has been damaged, replaced or repaired.
pair.new: Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist may not work properly if the bumper has been damaged, replaced, or repaired.

pair.old: Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly if interfered with by strong electromagnetic waves.
pair.new: Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist may not operate properly in areas with strong electromagnetic interference.

pair.old: Playing the vehicle audio system at high volume may prevent passengers from hearing Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist warning sounds.
pair.new: Playing the vehicle audio system at a high volume may prevent passengers from hearing Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist warning sounds.

pair.old: Check your brake fluid and brake pad conditions regularly. The brake performance may decrease depending on brake condition.
pair.new: Check your brake fluid and brake pad conditions regularly. The brake performance may decrease depending on the brakes’ condition.

pair.old: Turn off Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist when towing a trailer. If towing and moving in reverse, Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist will activate as it detects the trailer.
pair.new: Turn OFF Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist when towing a trailer. If you are towing and moving in reverse, Parking Collision-Avoidance Assist will detect the trailer and activate.

pair.old: A pedestrian is standing behind the vehicle
pair.new: A pedestrian is standing behind the vehicle.

pair.old: A large obstacle, such as a vehicle, is parked in the rear center of your vehicle
pair.new: A large obstacle, such as another vehicle, is parked in the rear center of your vehicle.

pair.old: The radio frequency components (Front Radar) complies:
pair.new: The radio frequency components (Front Radar) comply:

pair.old: The radio frequency components (Front Corner Radar/Rear Corner Radar) complies:
pair.new: The radio frequency components (Front Corner Radar/Rear Corner Radar) comply:

pair.old: When an emergency situation occurs while driving or when you park by the edge of the roadway, you must alert approaching or passing vehicles to be careful as they pass. For this, you should use the hazard warning flasher.
pair.new: When an emergency situation occurs while you are driving or when you park by the edge of the road, turn the hazard lights ON to alert approaching vehicles to be careful as they pass.

pair.old: Hazard warning flasher
pair.new: Hazard lights switch

pair.old: The hazard warning flasher serves as a warning to other drivers to exercise extreme caution when approaching, overtaking, or passing your vehicle.
pair.new: The hazard lights warn other drivers to exercise extreme caution when approaching, overtaking, or passing your vehicle.

pair.old: It should be used whenever emergency repairs are being made or when the vehicle is stopped near the edge of a roadway.
pair.new: Use the hazard lights when your vehicle is stopped near the edge of the road or undergoing emergency repairs.

pair.old: Depress the flasher switch with the ENGINE START/STOP button in any position. The flasher switch is located in the center fascia panel. All turn signal lights will flash simultaneously.
pair.new: Push the hazard lights switch with the ENGINE START/STOP button in any position. The hazard lights switch is located in the center front panel. All turn signal lights blink simultaneously.

pair.old: The hazard warning flasher operates whether your vehicle is running or not.
pair.new: The hazard lights switch operates whether your vehicle is running or not.

pair.old: The turn signals do not work when the hazard flasher is on.
pair.new: Turn signal lights do not work when hazard lights are ON.

pair.old: Care must be taken when using the hazard warning flasher while the vehicle is being towed.
pair.new: Be careful using the hazard lights while the vehicle is being towed.

pair.old: In an emergency, stay calm and take the following steps.
pair.new: In an emergency, stay calm and follow these steps:

pair.old: Reduce your speed gradually, keeping a straight line.
pair.new: Reduce your speed gradually, staying in a straight line.

pair.old: Turn on your hazard warning flasher.
pair.new: Turn your hazard lights ON.

pair.old: Try to start the vehicle again. If your vehicle will not start, contact an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Try to restart your vehicle. If your vehicle does not restart, contact an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: If the engine stalls at a crossroad or crossing
pair.new: If the engine stalls at a crossroad or junction

pair.old: Take your foot off the accelerator pedal and let the vehicle slow down while driving straight ahead.
pair.new: Remove your foot from the accelerator pedal and let the vehicle slow down while driving straight ahead.

pair.old: Do not apply the brakes immediately or attempt to pull off the road as this may cause a loss of control.
pair.new: Do not engage the brakes immediately or attempt to pull off the road, as this may cause a loss of control.

pair.old: When the vehicle has slowed to such a speed that it is safe to do so, brake carefully and pull off the road.
pair.new: When the vehicle has slowed to a safe speed, brake carefully and pull off the road.

pair.old: Drive off the road as far as possible and park on firm level ground.
pair.new: Drive off the road as far as possible and park on firm, level ground.

pair.old: When the vehicle is stopped, turn on your emergency hazard flashers, set the parking brake and put the transmission in P.
pair.new: When the vehicle is stopped, turn your hazard lights ON, apply the parking brake, and shift the gear to P (Park).

pair.old: Have all passengers get exit the vehicle. Be sure they all get out on the side of the vehicle that is away from traffic.
pair.new: Have all passengers exit the vehicle. Be sure they all exit on the side of the vehicle that is facing away from traffic.

pair.old: When changing a flat tire, follow the instruction provided later in this section.
pair.new: When changing a flat tire, follow the instructions provided later in this section.

pair.old: If the engine will not start
pair.new: If the engine does not start

pair.old: When the engine doesn’t start, first check to see how much fuel there is and whether the battery is discharged.
pair.new: If the engine does not start, first check to see how much fuel there is and whether the battery is discharged.

pair.old: If engine doesn’t turn over or turns over slowly
pair.new: If the engine does not turn over or turns over slowly

pair.old: Be sure the shift lever is in N (Neutral) or P (Park) and the emergency brake is set.
pair.new: Be sure the gear shift lever is in N (Neutral) or P (Park) and the emergency brake is applied.

pair.old: Turn on the interior light. If the light dims or goes out when you operate the starter, the battery is discharged.
pair.new: Turn the interior light ON. If the light dims or goes out when you operate the starter, the battery is discharged.

pair.old: If engine turns over normally but does not start
pair.new: If the engine turns over normally but does not start

pair.old: When the vehicle will not start because of low battery power, you may need to jump start the vehicle.
pair.new: When the vehicle does not start because of low battery power, you may need to jump start the vehicle.

pair.old: Jump-starting
pair.new: Jump starting
Jump starting instructions

pair.old: Connect cables in numerical order and disconnect in reverse order.
pair.new: Connect the cables in the numerical order shown in the following image (and disconnect them in the reverse order):

pair.old: Jump-starting can be dangerous if done incorrectly. To avoid harm to yourself or damage to your vehicle or battery, follow these jump-starting procedures. If in doubt, have a competent technician or towing service jump-start your vehicle.
pair.new: Jump starting can be dangerous if done incorrectly. To avoid harm to yourself or damage to your vehicle or battery, follow the jump starting procedures in this section. If in doubt, have a competent technician or towing service jump start your vehicle.

pair.old: Use only a 12-volt jumper system. You can damage a 12-volt starting motor, ignition system, and other electrical parts beyond repair by use of a 24- volt power supply (either two 12-volt batteries in series or a 24-volt motor generator set).
pair.new: Use only a 12-volt jumper system. You can damage a 12-volt starting motor, ignition system, and other electrical parts beyond repair by using a 24-volt power supply (either 2 12-volt batteries in a series or a 24-volt motor generator set).

pair.old: Keep all flames or sparks away from the battery. The battery produces hydrogen gas which may explode if exposed to flame or sparks.
pair.new: Keep all flames or sparks away from the battery. The battery produces hydrogen gas, which may cause an explosion if it is exposed to flame or sparks.

pair.old: If these instructions are not followed exactly, serious personal injury and damage to the vehicle may occur! If you are not sure how to follow this procedure, seek qualified assistance. Automobile batteries contain sulfuric acid. This is poisonous and highly corrosive. When jump starting, wear protective glasses and be careful not to get acid on yourself, your clothing or on the vehicle.
pair.new: If you do not follow these instructions exactly, serious personal injury and damage to the vehicle may occur. If you are not sure how to follow this procedure, seek qualified assistance. Automobile batteries contain sulfuric acid, which is poisonous and highly corrosive. When jump starting your vehicle, wear protective glasses and be careful not to get acid on yourself, your clothing, or the vehicle.

pair.old: Do not attempt to jump start the vehicle if the discharged battery is frozen or if the electrolyte level is low; the battery may rupture or explode.
pair.new: Do not attempt to jump start the vehicle if the discharged battery is frozen or the electrolyte level is low. The battery may rupture or explode.

pair.old: Do not allow the (+) and (-) jumper cables to touch. It may cause sparks. Do not allow the (+) and (-) jumper cables to touch each other. This may cause sparks. The battery may rupture or explode when you jump start with a low or frozen battery.

new.new: Do not allow the (+) and (-) jumper cables to touch each other. This may cause sparks.

pair.old: Do not connect the jumper cable from the negative terminal of the booster battery to the negative terminal of the discharged battery. This can cause the discharged battery to overheat and crack or degrade. Make sure to connect one end of the jumper cable to the negative terminal of the booster battery, and the other end to a metallic point, far away from the battery.
pair.new: Do not connect the jumper cable from the negative terminal of the booster battery to the negative terminal of the discharged battery. This can cause the discharged battery to overheat and crack or degrade. Connect one end of the jumper cable to the negative terminal of the booster battery, and the other end to a metallic point far away from the battery.

pair.old: Automobile batteries contain sulfuric acid. When jump starting your vehicle, be careful not to get sulfuric acid on yourself, your clothing, or on the vehicle. This acid is poisonous and highly corrosive.
pair.new: Automobile batteries contain sulfuric acid. When jump starting your vehicle, be careful not to get sulfuric acid on yourself, your clothing, or the vehicle. This acid is poisonous and highly corrosive.

pair.old: If the booster battery is in another vehicle, do not allow the vehicles to come in contact.
pair.new: If the booster battery is in another vehicle, do not allow the vehicles to come into contact.

pair.old: Turn off all unnecessary electrical loads.
pair.new: Turn OFF all unnecessary electrical loads.

pair.old: Connect the jumper cables in the exact sequence shown in the illustration.
pair.new: Connect the jumper cables in the exact sequence shown in the following illustration:

pair.old: No text changes. Image added.
Connect on end of a jumper cable to the positive terminal of the discharged battery (1).
pair.new: Connect one end of a jumper cable to the positive terminal of the discharged battery (1).

pair.old: Connect the other end to the positive terminal of the booster battery (2).
pair.new: Connect the other end of that jumper cable to the positive terminal of the booster battery (2).

pair.old: Proceed to connect one end of the other jumper cable to the negative terminal of the booster battery (3), then the other end to a ground - a solid, stationary, metallic point (for example, the engine lifting bracket) away from the battery (4).
pair.new: Connect one end of the other jumper cable to the negative terminal of the booster battery (3).
Connect the other end of that jumper cable to a ground: a solid, stationary, metallic point (such as the engine lifting bracket) away from the battery (4).

pair.old: Do not allow the jumper cables to contact anything except the correct battery terminals or the correct ground. Do not lean over the battery when making connections.
pair.new: Do not allow the jumper cables to contact anything except the correct battery terminals or the correct ground. Do not lean over the battery when connecting the jumper cables.

pair.old: If connected with the other vehicle, start the vehicle with the booster battery first and let it run at 2,000 rpm for several minutes.
pair.new: If you have connected the jumper cables with another vehicle, start the vehicle containing the booster battery first and let it run at 2,000 rpm for several minutes.

pair.old: Start the vehicle with the discharged battery.
pair.new: Start the vehicle containing the discharged battery.

pair.old: If the engine starts, disconnect one end of the negative terminal of the booster battery (3), then other end of the positive terminal of the booster battery (2) and the discharged battery (1).
pair.new: If the engine of the vehicle containing the discharged battery starts, disconnect the end of the jumper cable from the negative terminal of the booster battery (3). Then disconnect the other jumper cable from the positive terminal of the booster battery (2) and the discharged battery (1).

pair.old: If the cause of your battery discharging is not apparent, have your vehicle checked by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If it is not obvious why your battery was discharged, have your vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Make sure to connect one end of the jumper cable to the negative terminal of the booster battery, and the other end to a metallic point, far away from the battery.
pair.new: Make sure to connect one end of the jumper cable to the negative terminal of the booster battery, and the other end to a metallic point far away from the battery.

pair.old: Push-starting
pair.new: Push starting

pair.old: Vehicles equipped with automatic transmission or Dual Clutch Transmission (DCT) cannot be push-started. It can only be jump-started. Follow the directions in this section for .
pair.new: Vehicles equipped with automatic transmission or Dual Clutch Transmission (DCT) can only be jump started, not push started. Follow the instructions in this section for .

pair.old: Tow starting vehicle
pair.new: Tow starting the vehicle

pair.old: Shift the gear to P (Park) and set the parking brake.
pair.new: Shift the gear to P (Park) and apply the parking brake.

pair.old: If the air conditioning is on, turn it off.
pair.new: If the air conditioning is ON, turn it OFF.

pair.old: If engine coolant is leaking under the vehicle or steam is coming from underneath the hood, stop the engine. Do not open the hood until the coolant has stopped leaking or the steam has stopped.
pair.new: If engine coolant is leaking under the vehicle or steam is coming from underneath the hood, stop the engine. Do not open the hood until the coolant has stopped leaking or the steam has disappeared.

pair.old: If there is no visible loss of engine coolant and no steam, leave the engine running and check to be sure the engine cooling fan is operating.
pair.new: If there is no visible engine coolant leak and no steam, leave the engine running and check to make sure the engine cooling fan is operating.

pair.old: If the fan is not operating, turn the engine off.
pair.new: If the fan is not operating, turn the engine OFF.

pair.old: If it is not missing, check to see that it is tight.
pair.new: If the drive belt is not missing, check to make sure that it is tight.

pair.old: If the drive belt seems to be satisfactory, check for coolant leaking from the radiator, hoses or under the vehicle. (If the air conditioning had been in use, it is normal for cold water to be draining from it when you stop).
pair.new: If the drive belt seems satisfactory, check for coolant leaking from the radiator, hoses, or under the vehicle. (If you have been using the air conditioning, it is normal for cold water to drain from it when you stop).

pair.old: While the engine is running, keep hair, hands and clothing away from moving parts, such as the fan and drive belts, to prevent injury.
pair.new: While the engine is running, keep your hair, hands, and clothing away from moving parts (such as the fan and drive belts) to prevent injury.

pair.old: If the water pump drive belt is broken or engine coolant is leaking out, stop the engine immediately and call the nearest an authorized Kia dealer for assistance.
pair.new: If the water pump drive belt is broken, or if engine coolant is leaking, stop the engine immediately and call the nearest authorized Kia dealer for assistance.

pair.old: If you cannot find the cause of the overheating, wait until the engine temperature has returned to normal. If coolant has been lost, carefully add coolant to the reservoir to bring the fluid level in the reservoir up to the halfway mark.
pair.new: If you cannot find the cause of the engine overheating, wait until the engine temperature has returned to normal. If coolant has been lost, carefully add coolant to the reservoir to bring the fluid level up to the reservoir’s halfway mark.

pair.old: Proceed with caution, keeping alert for further signs of overheating. If overheating recurs, call an authorized Kia dealer for assistance.
pair.new: Proceed with caution, keeping alert for further signs of the engine overheating. If overheating recurs, call an authorized Kia dealer for assistance.

pair.old: Do not remove the engine coolant reservoir cap when the engine is hot. This may result in coolant blowing out of the opening and cause serious burns.
pair.new: Do not remove the engine coolant reservoir cap when the engine is hot. This may result in coolant blowing out of the opening and causing serious burns.

pair.old: Serious loss of coolant indicates there is a leak in the cooling system and this should be checked as soon as possible. Have your vehicle checked by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Serious loss of coolant indicates that there is a leak in the cooling system. This should be checked as soon as possible. Have your vehicle inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: When the engine overheats from low engine coolant, suddenly adding engine coolant may cause cracks in the engine. To prevent damage, add engine coolant slowly in small quantities.
pair.new: When the engine overheats due to low engine coolant, suddenly adding engine coolant may cause cracks in the engine. To prevent damage, add engine coolant slowly and in small quantities.

pair.old: The Tire Pressure Monitoring System (TPMS) detects the pressure of vehicle’s tires and displays it on the LCD display.
pair.new: The Tire Pressure Monitoring System (TPMS) detects the pressure of the vehicle’s tires and displays it on the instrument cluster.

pair.old: Low tire pressure telltale/TPMS malfunction indicator
pair.new: Low tire pressure or TPMS malfunction indicator light

pair.old: Low tire pressure position telltale (Shown on the LCD display)
pair.new: Low tire pressure position indicator light (shown on the instrument cluster)

pair.old: Tire pressure is displayed 1 ~ 2 minutes later after driving.
pair.new: The tire pressure indicator appears 1–2 minutes after you start driving.

pair.old: If tire pressure is not displayed when the vehicle is stopped, “Drive to display” message displays. After driving, check the tire pressure.
pair.new: If the tire pressure does not display when you stop the vehicle, the “Drive to display” message displays. After driving, check the tire pressure again.

pair.old: The tire pressure may change due to factors such as parking condition, driving style, and altitude above sea level.
pair.new: The tire pressure may change due to factors such as parking conditions, driving style, and altitude above sea level.

pair.old: The tire pressure shown on the dashboard may differ from the tire pressure measured by tire pressure gauge.
pair.new: The tire pressure shown on the instrument cluster may differ from the tire pressure measured by a tire pressure gauge.

pair.old: Low tire pressure warning may sound when a tire’s pressure unit is equal or lower than nearby tires. This is a normal occurrence, which is due to the change in tire pressure along with tire temperature.
pair.new: A low tire pressure warning may sound when a tire’s pressure unit is equal to or lower than the nearby tires. This is normal operation due to the change in tire pressure along with the tire temperature.

pair.old: Over-inflation or under-inflation can reduce tire life, adversely affect vehicle handling, and lead to sudden tire failure that may cause loss of vehicle control resulting in an accident
pair.new: Overinflation or underinflation of tires can reduce tire life, adversely affect vehicle handling, and lead to sudden tire failure that may cause you to lose control of your vehicle, resulting in an accident.

pair.old: Please review in context
pair.new: Please review in context

pair.old: If any of the below happens, have the system checked by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer under any of the following circumstances:

pair.old: The low tire pressure telltale/TPMS malfunction indicator does not appear for 3 seconds when the ENGINE START/STOP button is turned to the ON position or engine is running.
pair.new: The low tire pressure or TPMS malfunction indicator light does not illuminate for 3 seconds when the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON position or the engine is running.

pair.old: The TPMS malfunction indicator remains illuminated after blinking for approximately 1 minute.
pair.new: The TPMS malfunction indicator light remains illuminated after blinking for approximately 1 minute.

pair.old: The Low tire pressure position telltale remains illuminated.
pair.new: The low tire pressure position indicator light remains illuminated.

pair.old: Low tire pressure telltale
pair.new: Low tire pressure indicator light

pair.old: Low tire pressure position telltale
pair.new: Low tire pressure position indicator light

pair.old: If the telltale illuminates, immediately reduce your speed, avoid hard cornering and anticipate increased stopping distances. Stop and check your tires as soon as possible.
pair.new: If the indicator light illuminates, immediately reduce your speed, avoid hard turning, and anticipate increased stopping distances. Stop and check your tires as soon as possible.

pair.old: Inflate the tires to the proper pressure as indicated on the vehicle’s placard or tire inflation pressure label located on the driver’s side center pillar outer panel. If you cannot reach a service station or if the tire cannot hold the newly added air, replace the low pressure tire with the spare tire.
pair.new: Inflate the tires to the proper pressure indicated on the vehicle’s placard or on the tire inflation pressure label (located on the driver side center pillar outer panel). If you cannot reach a service station, or if the tire cannot hold the newly added air, replace the low pressure tire with the spare tire.

pair.old: The TPMS malfunction indicator and the Low Tire Pressure telltale may turn on and appear after restarting and about 20 minutes of continuous driving before you have the low pressure tire repaired and replaced on the vehicle.
pair.new: The TPMS malfunction and low tire pressure indicator light may illuminate after you restart the vehicle. After that, you have about 20 minutes of continuous driving before you must have the low pressure tire repaired and replaced.

pair.old: In winter or cold weather, the low tire pressure telltale may be appeared if the tire pressure was adjusted to the recommended tire inflation pressure in warm weather. It does not mean your TPMS is malfunctioning because the decreased temperature leads to a proportional lowering of tire pressure.
pair.new: In winter or cold weather, the low tire pressure indicator light may illuminate if you adjusted the tires to the recommended inflation pressure in warm weather. This does not mean your TPMS is malfunctioning; the decreased temperature leads to a proportional lowering of tire pressure.

pair.old: You should check the tire inflation pressure and adjust the tires to the recommended tire inflation pressure when driving your vehicle under the following conditions.
pair.new: Check the tire inflation pressure and adjust the tires to the recommended pressure under the following conditions:

pair.old: from a warm area to a cold area
pair.new: You are driving from a warm area to a cold area.

pair.old: from a cold area to a warm area
pair.new: You are driving from a cold area to a warm area.

pair.old: the outside temperature is extremely high or low
pair.new: The outside temperature is extremely high or low.

pair.old: When filling tires with more air, conditions to turn off the low tire pressure telltale may not be met. This is because a tire inflator has a margin of error in performance. The low tire pressure telltale will be turned off if the tire pressure is above the recommended tire inflation pressure.
pair.new: Filling tires with more air may not meet the conditions required to turn the low tire pressure indicator light OFF. This is because a tire inflator has a margin of error in performance. The low tire pressure indicator light turns OFF if the tire pressure is above the recommended tire inflation pressure.

pair.old: Do not drive on low pressure tires. Significantly low tire pressure can cause the tires to overheat and fail, making the vehicle unstable and result in increased braking distances and a loss of vehicle control.
pair.new: Do not drive on low pressure tires. Significantly low tire pressure can cause the tires to overheat and fail, making the vehicle unstable and resulting in increased braking distances and a loss of control.

pair.old: The low tire pressure telltale will appear after it blinks for approximately one minute when there is a problem with the TPMS.
pair.new: When there is a problem with the TPMS, the low tire pressure indicator light illuminates after blinking for approximately 1 minute.

pair.old: If the system correctly detects an underinflation warning at the same time as a system failure, it will appear both the TPMS malfunction and the low tire pressure position telltales (e.g., if the Front Left sensor fails, the TPMS malfunction indicator appears, but if the Front Right, Rear Left, or Rear Right tire is underinflated, the low tire pressure position telltales may appear together with the TPMS malfunction indicator.)
pair.new: If the system correctly detects an underinflation warning at the same time as a system failure, it will illuminate both the TPMS malfunction and low tire pressure position indicator lights. (For example, if the Front Left sensor fails, the TPMS malfunction indicator appears. However, if the Front Right, Rear Left, or Rear Right tire is underinflated, the low tire pressure position indicator lights may appear together with the TPMS malfunction indicator light.)

pair.old: Have the system checked by an authorized Kia dealer as soon as possible to determine the cause of the problem.
pair.new: Have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer as soon as possible to determine the cause of the problem:

pair.old: The TPMS malfunction indicator may illuminate if the vehicle is moving around electric power supply cables or radios transmitters such as at police stations, government and public offices, broadcasting stations, military installations, airports, or transmitting towers, etc. This can interfere with normal operation of the TPMS.
pair.new: The TPMS malfunction indicator light may illuminate if you drive your vehicle near electric power supply cables or radio transmitters (for example, at police stations, government and public offices, broadcasting stations, military installations, airports, transmitting towers, and so on). This can interfere with normal TPMS operation.

pair.old: The TPMS malfunction indicator may illuminate if the vehicle is equipped with snow chains or a personal electronic devices (such as a laptop computer, mobile charger, remote starter or navigation) is used in the vehicle. This can interfere with normal operation of the TPMS.
pair.new: The TPMS malfunction indicator light may illuminate if the vehicle is equipped with tire chains, or if you use a personal electronic device (such as a laptop computer, mobile charger, remote starter, or navigation system) in the vehicle. This can interfere with normal TPMS operation.

pair.old: If you have a flat tire, the Low Tire Pressure telltale will come on. Have the flat tire repaired by an authorized Kia dealer as soon as possible or replace the flat tire with the spare tire.
pair.new: If you have a flat tire, the low tire pressure indicator light appears. Have the flat tire repaired by an authorized Kia dealer as soon as possible, or replace the flat tire with the spare tire.

pair.old: Never use a puncture-repairing agent not approved by Kia to repair and/or inflate a low pressure tire. A sealant that is not approved by Kia may damage the tire pressure sensor.
pair.new: Never use a puncture-repairing agent not approved by Kia to repair or inflate a low pressure tire. A sealant that is not approved by Kia may damage the tire pressure sensor.

pair.old: Each wheel is equipped with a tire pressure sensor mounted inside the tire behind the valve stem. You must use TPMS specific wheels. Always have your tires serviced by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Each wheel is equipped with a tire pressure sensor mounted inside the tire behind the valve stem. You must use TPMS-specific wheels. Always have your tires serviced by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Even if you replace the low pressure tire with the spare tire, the Low Tire Pressure telltale will remain on until the low pressure tire is repaired and placed on the vehicle.
pair.new: Even if you replace the low pressure tire with the spare tire, the low tire pressure indicator light remains illuminated until you repair or replace the low pressure tire.

pair.old: After you replace the low pressure tire with the spare tire, the TPMS malfunction indicator may appear after a few minutes. This is because the TPMS sensor mounted on the spare wheel is not activated.
pair.new: The TPMS malfunction indicator light may appear a few minutes after you replace the low pressure tire with the spare tire. This is because the TPMS sensor mounted on the spare wheel is not activated.

pair.old: Once the low pressure tire is inflated to the recommended pressure and installed on the vehicle or the TPMS sensor mounted on the replaced spare wheel is activated by an authorized Kia dealer, the TPMS malfunction indicator and the low tire pressure telltale will turn off within a few minutes of driving.
pair.new: Once you inflate the low pressure tire to the recommended pressure and install it on the vehicle, or mount the TPMS sensor on the replaced spare wheel and have it activated by an authorized Kia dealer, the TPMS malfunction and low tire pressure indicator light turns OFF within a few minutes of driving.

pair.old: If the indicator has not disappeared after a few minutes of driving, please visit an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the indicator light does not turn OFF after a few minutes of driving, visit an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: If an original mounted tire is replaced with the spare tire, the TPMS sensor on the replaced spare wheel should be activated and the TPMS sensor on the original mounted wheel should be deactivated. If the TPMS sensor on the original mounted wheel located in the spare tire carrier still activates, the Tire Pressure Monitoring System may not operate properly. Have the tire with TPMS serviced or replaced by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If you replace an original tire with the spare tire, the TPMS sensor on the replaced spare wheel should be activated, and the TPMS sensor on the original wheel should be deactivated. If the TPMS sensor on the original wheel located in the spare tire carrier is still active, the TPMS may not operate properly. Have the tire with the TPMS serviced or replaced by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: You may not be able to identify a low pressure tire by simply looking at it. Always use a good quality tire pressure gauge to measure the tire’s inflation pressure. Please note that a tire that is hot (from being driven) will have a higher pressure measurement than a tire that is cold (sitting stationary for at least 3 hours or driven less than 1 mile (1.6 km) during that 3 hour period).
pair.new: You may not be able to identify a low pressure tire simply by looking at it. Always use a good quality tire pressure gauge to measure the tire’s inflation pressure. A tire that is hot (after being driven) will have a higher pressure measurement than a tire that is cold (after sitting stationary for at least 3 hours or being driven less than 1 mile (1.6 km) during that 3 hour period).

pair.old: Allow the tire to cool before measuring the inflation pressure. Always be sure the tire is cold before inflating to the recommended pressure.
pair.new: Allow the tire to cool before measuring the inflation pressure. Always be sure the tire is cold before inflating it to the recommended pressure.

old.old: A cold tire means the vehicle has been sitting for 3 hours or driven for less than 1 mile (1.6 km) in that 3 hour period.

new.new: A cold tire means the vehicle has been sitting for 3 hours or driven for less than 1 mile (1.6 km) in that 3 hour period.

pair.old: Never use tire sealant if your vehicle is equipped with a TPMS. A liquid sealant can damage the tire pressure sensors.
pair.new: Never use tire sealant if your vehicle is equipped with a TPMS. A liquid sealant can damage the tire pressure sensors, causing the following issues:

pair.old: The TPMS cannot alert you to severe and sudden tire damage caused by external factors such as nails or road debris.
pair.new: The TPMS cannot alert you to severe and sudden tire damage caused by external factors, such as nails or road debris.

pair.old: If you feel any vehicle instability, immediately take your foot off the accelerator, apply the brakes gradually and with light force, and slowly move to a safe position off the road.
pair.new: If you feel any vehicle instability, immediately take your foot off the accelerator pedal, engage the brakes lightly and gradually, and slowly move to a safe position off the road.

pair.old: Tampering with, modifying, or disabling the TPMS components may interfere with the system’s ability to warn the driver of low tire pressure conditions and/or TPMS malfunctions. Tampering with, modifying, or disabling the TPMS components may void the warranty for that portion of the vehicle.
pair.new: Tampering with, modifying, or disabling the TPMS components may interfere with the system’s ability to warn you of low tire pressure conditions or TPMS malfunctions. It may also void the warranty for that portion of the vehicle.

pair.old: If you have a flat tire, you can change the flat tire to a spare tire using tools.
pair.new: If you have a flat tire, you can use tools to replace it with a spare tire.

pair.old: Driving on a flat tire will cause permanent damage to the tire. Re-inflating a tire after it has been driven on while being severely underinflated or flat may cause a blowout and a serious crash. Never attempt to re-inflate a tire that has been driven on while severely underinflated or flat. In this case, repair or replace the flat tire as soon as possible.
pair.new: Driving on a flat tire causes permanent damage to the tire. Reinflating a tire after you drive on it while the tire is severely underinflated or flat may cause a blowout and a serious crash. Never attempt to reinflate a tire that has been driven on while severely underinflated or flat. In this case, replace the flat tire as soon as possible.

pair.old: Changing a tire can be dangerous. Follow the instructions in this section when changing a tire to reduce the risk of serious injury or death.
pair.new: Changing a tire can be dangerous. To reduce the risk of serious injury or death, follow the instructions in this section when you change a tire.

pair.old: Be careful as you use the jack handle to stay clear of the flat end. The flat end has sharp edges that could cause cuts.
pair.new: When using the jack handle, be careful to stay clear of the flat end, as it has sharp edges that could cause cuts.

pair.old: The jack, jack handle and wheel lug nut wrench are stored in the luggage compartment.
pair.new: The jack, jack handle, and wheel lug nut wrench are stored in the luggage compartment.

pair.old: Pull up the luggage compartment cover to reach this equipment.
pair.new: Pull the luggage compartment up to reach this equipment:

pair.old: The jack is provided for emergency tire changing only.
pair.new: The provided jack is only for emergency tire changes.

pair.old: To prevent the jack from “rattling” while the vehicle is in motion, store it properly.
pair.new: Properly store the jack to keep it from rattling when your vehicle is in motion.

pair.old: Follow jacking instructions to reduce the possibility of personal injury.
pair.new: To reduce the possibility of personal injury, follow the provided jacking instructions.

pair.old: Always move the vehicle completely off the road and onto the shoulder before changeinge a tire. Use the jack on firm level ground. If you cannot find a firm level place off the road, call a towing service company for assistance.
pair.new: Always move the vehicle completely off of the road and onto the shoulder before you change a tire. Use the jack on firm, level ground. If you cannot find a firm, level place off of the road, call a towing services company for assistance.

pair.old: Be sure to use the correct front and rear jacking positions on the vehicle; never use the bumpers or any other part of the vehicle for jacking support.
pair.new: Be sure to use the correct front and rear jacking positions for your vehicle. Never use the bumpers or any other part of the vehicle for jacking support.

pair.old: The vehicle can roll off the jack causing serious injury or death.
pair.new: Your vehicle can roll off of the jack and cause serious injury or death.

pair.old: Do not go under a vehicle that is supported by a jack.
pair.new: Do not go underneath a vehicle supported by a jack.

pair.old: Make sure any children present are in a secure place, away from the road and from the vehicle to be raised with the jack.
pair.new: Make sure any children present are in a secure place away from the road and the vehicle.

pair.old: Do not place any portion of your body under a vehicle that is only supported by a jack since the vehicle can easily roll off the jack. Use vehicle support stands.
pair.new: Never put any part of your body under a vehicle that is only supported by a jack, as the vehicle can easily roll off of the jack. Use vehicle support stands.

pair.old: Do not start or run the engine of the vehicle while it is on the jack. This may cause the vehicle to fall off the jack.
pair.new: Do not start or run the engine while your vehicle is on the jack, as it may cause the vehicle to fall off of the jack.

pair.old: To prevent the jack from “rattling” while the vehicle IS in motion, store it properly.
pair.new: Properly store the jack to keep it from rattling when your vehicle is in motion.

pair.old: Substantial design variations and the age of the retreaded tire casing structure can limit service life and have negative impact on road safety.
pair.new: Substantial design variations and the age of a retreaded tire’s casing structure can limit service life and negatively impact driving safety.

pair.old: Turn the wrench counterclockwise until the spare tire reaches the ground.
pair.new: Turn the wrench counterclockwise until the spare tire reaches the ground.

pair.old: After the spare tire reaches the ground, continue to turn the wrench counterclockwise, and draw the spare tire outside. Never rotate the wrench excessively. The spare tire carrier may be damaged.
pair.new: After the spare tire reaches the ground, continue to turn the wrench counterclockwise and draw the spare tire out. Never rotate the wrench excessively, as this may damage the spare tire carrier.

pair.old: Remove the retainer (1) from the center of the spare tire. During tire removal & installation, try and keep the retainer cable tight, to prevent backlashing/binding.
pair.new: Remove the retainer (1) from the center of the spare tire. During tire removal and installation, try to keep the retainer cable tight to prevent backlashing or binding.

pair.old: To store the spare tire:
pair.new: To store the spare tire, follow these steps:

pair.old: Do not touch the metal surface of the luggage compartment floor while the engine is operating or hot. It could result in serious bodily injury.
pair.new: Do not touch the metal surface of the luggage compartment floor while the engine is running or hot, as this could result in serious bodily injury.

pair.old: Turn the engine off and wait until it cools down or wear gloves to remove the spare tire from the luggage compartment.
pair.new: Turn the engine OFF and wait until it cools down, or wear gloves to remove the spare tire from the luggage compartment.

pair.old: Park on a level surface and apply the parking brake firmly. Shift the gear to P (Park), apply the parking brake, and turn the engine OFF.
pair.new: Park your vehicle on a level, firm surface, apply the parking brake, and turn the engine OFF.

pair.old: Activate the hazard warning flasher.
pair.new: Turn the hazard lights ON.

pair.old: Remove the wheel lug nut wrench, jack, jack handle, and spare tire from the vehicle.
pair.new: Remove the wheel lug nut wrench, jack, jack handle, and spare tire from the vehicle.

pair.old: Block both the front and rear of wheel that is diagonally opposite the jack position.
pair.new: Block both sides of the wheel diagonally opposite the jack position.

pair.old: To reduce the possibility of injury, be sure to only use the jack provided with your vehicle in the correct jack position. Never use any other part of the vehicle for jack support.
pair.new: To reduce the possibility of injury, only use the jack provided with your vehicle in the correct jack position. Never use any other part of the vehicle for jack support.

pair.old: To prevent vehicle movement while changing a tire, always set the parking brake fully, and always block the wheel diagonally, opposite the wheel being changed.
pair.new: To prevent vehicle movement while changing a tire, always fully apply the parking brake and block the wheel diagonally opposite the tire you are changing.

pair.old: All the wheels of the vehicle should be blocked, and no person should remain in a vehicle that is being jacked.
pair.new: You should block all of the wheels on the vehicle. No one should remain in the vehicle when you use the jack.

pair.old: Loosen the wheel lug nuts counterclockwise one turn each, but do not remove any nut until the tire has been raised off the ground.
pair.new: Loosen the wheel lug nuts counterclockwise one turn each, but do not remove any of the nuts until the tire is raised above ground.

pair.old: Insert the jack handle into the jack and turn it clockwise, raising the vehicle until the tire just clears the ground. This measurement is approximately 1.2 inches (30 mm). Before removing the wheel lug nuts, make sure the vehicle is stable and that there is no chance for movement or slippage.
pair.new: Insert the jack handle into the jack and turn it clockwise, raising the vehicle until the tire just clears the ground (approximately 1.2 inches (30 mm)). Before removing the wheel lug nuts, make sure the vehicle is stable and there is no chance of movement or slipping.

pair.old: Loosen the wheel nuts and remove them with your fingers. Slide the wheel off the studs and lay it flat so it cannot roll away. To put the wheel on the hub, pick up the spare tire, line up the holes with the studs and slide the wheel onto them.
pair.new: Loosen the wheel lug nuts and remove them with your hands. Slide the wheel off the studs and lay it flat so it cannot roll away. To put the wheel on the hub, pick up the spare tire, line the holes up with the studs, and slide the wheel into place.

pair.old: If this is difficult, tip the wheel slightly and line up the top hole in the wheel lined up with the top stud. Jiggle the wheel back and forth until the wheel can be slid over the other studs.
pair.new: If this is difficult, tip the wheel slightly and line up the top hole in the wheel with the top stud. Jiggle the wheel back and forth until you can slide the wheel over the other studs.

pair.old: To reinstall the wheel, hold it on the studs, put the wheel nuts on the studs and finger tighten them. Jiggle the tire to be sure it is completely seated, then finger tighten the nuts as much as possibl.
pair.new: To reinstall the wheel, hold it on the studs, place the wheel lug nuts on the studs, and tighten them by hand. Jiggle the tire to be sure it is completely seated, then tighten the nuts by hand as much as possible.

pair.old: Lower the vehicle to the ground by turning the wheel nut wrench counterclockwise.
pair.new: To lower the vehicle back to the ground, turn the wheel nut wrench counterclockwise.

pair.old: Position the wrench as shown in the drawing and tighten the wheel nuts. Be sure the socket is seated completely over the nut. Do not stand on the wrench handle or use an extension pipe over the wrench handle. Go around the wheel tightening every other nut until they are all tight. Double-check each nut for tightness. After changing wheels, have an authorized Kia dealer tighten the wheel nuts to their proper torque as soon as possible.
pair.new: Position the wrench as shown in the illustration above and tighten the wheel nuts. Make sure the socket is seated completely over the nut. Do not stand on the wrench handle or use an extension pipe over the wrench handle. Go around the wheel, tightening every nut. Double check each nut to be sure it is tight on the wheel. After you change a wheel, have an authorized Kia dealer tighten the wheel nuts to their proper torque as soon as possible.

pair.old: Whenever you install a wheel, remove any corrosion, dirt or foreign materials present on the mounting surfaces of the wheel or the surface of the wheel hub, brake drum or brake disc that contacts the wheel. Make sure to secure any fasteners that attach the rotor to the hub so they do not interfere with the mounting surfaces of the wheel. Installing wheels without correct metal-to-metal contact at the wheel mounting surfaces can cause the wheel nuts to loosen and the wheel to come off while your vehicle is in motion, resulting in loss of vehicle control, personal injury or death.
pair.new: When you install a wheel, be sure to remove any corrosion, dirt, or foreign materials present on the mounting surfaces of the wheel or the surface of the wheel hub, brake drum, or brake disc that contacts the wheel. Secure any fasteners that attach the rotor to the hub so they do not interfere with the mounting surfaces on the wheel. Installing wheels without correct metal-to-metal contact at the wheel mounting surfaces can cause the wheel nuts to loosen and the wheel to come off while your vehicle is in motion. This could result in loss of vehicle control, personal injury, or death.

pair.old: Steel wheel & aluminum alloy wheel:
pair.new: Steel wheel and aluminum alloy wheel:

pair.old: If you have a tire gauge, remove the valve cap and check the air pressure. If the pressure is lower than recommended, drive slowly to the nearest service station and inflate to the correct pressure. If it is too high, adjust it until it is correct. Always reinstall the valve cap after checking or adjusting tire pressure. If the cap is not replaced, air may leak from the tire. If you lose a valve cap, buy another and install it as soon as possible.
pair.new: If you have a tire gauge, remove the valve cap and check the air pressure. If the air pressure is lower than recommended, drive slowly to the nearest service station and inflate your tires to the correct pressure. If the air pressure is higher than recommended, adjust the air pressure until it is correct. Always reinstall the valve cap after you check or adjust tire pressure. If the cap is not reinstalled, air may leak from the tire. If you lose a valve cap, buy another and install it as soon as possible.

pair.old: After you have changed wheels, always secure the flat tire in its place and return the jack and tools to their proper storage locations.
pair.new: After you change a wheel, always secure the flat tire in its place and return the jack and tools to their proper storage locations.

pair.old: Make certain during wheel removal that the same nuts that were removed are reinstalled - or, if replaced, that nuts with metric threads and the same chamfer configuration are used. Your vehicle has metric threads on the wheel studs and nuts. Installation of a non-metric thread nut on a metric stud will not secure the wheel to the hub properly and will damage the stud so that it must be replaced.
pair.new: When you remove a wheel, make sure the same nuts that were removed are reinstalled. If you must replace a nut, make sure the replacement nut has metric threads and the same chamfer configuration as the original nut. If you install a nut without metric threads, it will not properly secure the wheel to the hub and will damage the stud (and you will have to replace the stud).

pair.old: Note that most lug nuts do not have metric threads. Be sure to use extreme care in checking for thread style before installing aftermarket lug nuts or wheels. If in doubt, consult an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Note that most lug nuts do not have metric threads. Be extremely careful and check the thread style before you install aftermarket lug nuts or wheels. If in doubt, consult an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: If the studs are damaged, they may lose their ability to retain the wheel. This could lead to the loss of the wheel and a collision resulting in serious injuries.
pair.new: If the studs are damaged, they may lose the ability to hold the wheels in place. This could lead to the loss of a wheel, and a collision resulting in serious injuries.

pair.old: Never use oil or grease on bolts or nuts because the nuts might come loose. The vehicle’s wheel could fall off, causing a crash.
pair.new: Never use oil or grease on bolts or nuts because the nuts might come loose and the wheel could fall off, causing a crash.

pair.old: Your vehicle is equipped with a compact spare tire. This compact spare tire takes up less space than a regular-size tire. This tire is smaller than a conventional tire and is designed for temporary use only.
pair.new: Your vehicle is equipped with a compact spare tire. This compact spare tire takes up less space than a regular size tire. This tire is smaller than a conventional tire and designed for temporary use only.

pair.old: You should drive carefully when the compact spare is in use. The compact spare should be replaced by the proper conventional tire and rim at the first opportunity.
pair.new: Drive carefully when you use the compact spare tire. Replace it with a proper conventional tire and rim as soon as possible.

pair.old: The operation of this vehicle is not recommended with more than one compact spare tire in use at the same time.
pair.new: Do not operate your vehicle with more than one compact spare tire in use at the same time.

pair.old: The compact spare tire is for emergency use only. Do not operate your vehicle on this compact spare at speeds over 50 mph (80 km/h). The original tire should be repaired or replaced as soon as possible to avoid failure of the spare, possibly leading to bodily injury or death.
pair.new: The compact spare tire is for emergency use only. Do not operate your vehicle on the compact spare at speeds over 50 mph (80 km/h). The original tire should be repaired or replaced as soon as possible to avoid failure of the spare, possibly leading to bodily injury or death.

pair.old: Check the inflation pressure after installing the spare tire. Adjust it tothe specified pressure, as necessary.
pair.new: Check the inflation pressure after you install the spare tire and adjust it to the specified pressure if necessary.

pair.old: Ensure that you drive slowly enough for the road conditions to avoid all hazards. Any road hazard, such as a pothole or debris, could seriously damage the compact spare.
pair.new: Drive slowly enough to avoid all hazards (according to road conditions). Any road hazard, such as a pothole or debris, could seriously damage the compact spare tire.

pair.old: Continuous use of this tire could result in tire failure, loss of vehicle control, and possible personal injury.
pair.new: Continuous use could result in tire failure, loss of vehicle control, and personal injury.

pair.old: Do not exceed the vehicle’s maximum load rating or the load-carrying capacity shown on the sidewall of the compact spare tire.
pair.new: Do not exceed your vehicle’s maximum load rating or the load carrying capacity shown on the sidewall of the compact spare tire.

pair.old: Do not take this vehicle through an automatic vehicle wash while the compact spare tire is installed.
pair.new: Do not go through an automatic car wash while using the compact spare tire.

pair.old: Do not use the compact spare tire on any other vehicle because this tire has been designed especially for your vehicle.
pair.new: Do not use the compact spare tire on any other vehicle. This tire has been designed specifically for your vehicle.

pair.old: The compact spare tire should neither be used on any other wheels, nor should standard tires, snow tires, wheel covers or trim rings be used with the compact spare wheel. If such use is attempted, damage to these items or other vehicle components may occur.
pair.new: Do not use the compact spare tire with any other wheels. Similarly, do not use standard tires, snow tires, wheel covers, or trim rings with the compact spare wheel. This may result in damage to these items or other vehicle components.

pair.old: The actual Jack label in the vehicle may differ from the illustration. For more detailed specifications, refer to the label attached to the jack:
pair.new: The actual jack label in your vehicle may differ from the illustration above. Refer to the jack label in your vehicle for these specifications:

pair.old: When using the jack, set your parking brake.
pair.new: When using the jack, apply your parking brake

pair.old: When using the jack, stop the engine.
pair.new: When using the jack, stop the engine

pair.old: Do not get under a vehicle that is supported by a jack.
pair.new: Do not get under a vehicle that is supported by a jack

pair.old: When supporting the vehicle, the base plate of the jack must be vertical under the lifting point.
pair.new: When supporting the vehicle, the base plate of the jack must be vertical under the lifting point

pair.old: Move the shift position to the P (Park) position.
pair.new: Shift the gear to P (Park)

pair.old: The jack should be used on firm level ground.
pair.new: The jack should be used on firm, level ground

pair.old: If emergency towing is necessary, have it done by an authorized Kia dealer or a commercial tow-truck service.
pair.new: If emergency towing is necessary, have your vehicle towed by an authorized Kia dealer or a commercial towing service.

pair.old: Proper lifting and towing procedures are necessary to prevent damage to the vehicle. The use of wheel dollies (1) or flatbed is recommended.
pair.new: Proper lifting and towing procedures are necessary to prevent damage to your vehicle. The use of wheel dollies (1) or a flatbed is recommended.

pair.old: 1: dollies
pair.new: 1: Wheel dollies

pair.old: If your vehicle is equipped with side and curtain air bag, set the ENGINE START/STOP button to ACC position when the vehicle is being towed.
pair.new: If your vehicle is equipped with side and curtain air bags, press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ACC position when the vehicle is being towed.

pair.old: The side and curtain air bag may deploy when the ENGINE START/STOP button is turned to the ON position and the rollover sensor detects the situation as a rollover.
pair.new: The side and curtain air bag may deploy when the ENGINE START/STOP button is in the ON position and the rollover sensor detects the situation as a rollover.

old.old: When towing your vehicle in an emergency without wheel dollies:
When you need to tow your vehicle in an emergency without wheel dollies, follow these steps:

new.new: When you need to tow your vehicle in an emergency without wheel dollies, follow these steps:

pair.old: Set the ENGINE START/STOP button to ACC position.
pair.new: Press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ACC position.

pair.old: Place the shift lever in N (Neutral).
pair.new: Shift the gear to N (Neutral).

pair.old: Failure to shift to N (Neutral) may cause internal damage to the vehicle.
pair.new: Failure to shift the gear to N (Neutral) may cause internal damage to your vehicle.

pair.old: If any of the loaded wheels or suspension components are damaged or the vehicle is being towed with the driven wheels on the ground, use a towing dolly under the driven wheels.
pair.new: If any of the loaded wheels or suspension components are damaged, or the vehicle is being towed with the driven wheels on the ground, use a towing dolly under the driven wheels.

pair.old: Do not tow the vehicle backwards with the driven wheels on the ground, as this may cause damage to the transmission.
pair.new: Do not tow the vehicle backwards with the driven wheels on the ground. This may damage the transmission.

pair.old: Attaching straps to the chassis, suspension or other parts of the body can cause damage.
pair.new: Attaching straps to the chassis, suspension, or other parts of the body can cause damage.

pair.old: Do not tow with sling-type equipment. Use wheel lift or flatbed equipment.
pair.new: Do not tow with sling type equipment. Use wheel lift or flatbed equipment.

pair.old: Do not tow the vehicle with four wheels in contact with the ground if it is a vehicle equipped with AT or DCT. The transmission will be seriously damaged. Make sure not to tow the vehicle connecting it with other vehicles including camper vans.
pair.new: If your vehicle is equipped with Automatic Transmission (AT) or Dual-Clutch Transmission (DCT), towing the vehicle with all 4 wheels in contact with the ground will seriously damage the transmission. Do not tow your vehicle while it is connected to other vehicles (including camper vans).

pair.old: If you tow the vehicle while the driven wheels are touching the ground, the vehicle motor may generate electricity and the motor components may be damaged or a fire may occur.
pair.new: If you tow the vehicle while the driven wheels are touching the ground, the motor may generate electricity and damage the motor components or cause a fire.

pair.old: When a vehicle fire occurs due to the battery, there is a risk of a second fire. Contact the fire department when towing the vehicle.
pair.new: When the battery causes a vehicle fire, there is a risk of a second fire. Contact the fire department when towing the vehicle.

pair.old: Exercise utmost care to prevent damage to your vehicle and injury whenever performing any maintenance or inspection procedures.
pair.new: Take the utmost care to prevent damage to your vehicle and injury when performing any maintenance or inspection procedures.

pair.old: Should you have any doubts concerning the inspection or servicing of your vehicle, have an authorized Kia dealer perform this work.
pair.new: If you have any doubts concerning the inspection or maintenance of your vehicle, have an authorized Kia dealer perform this work.

pair.old: Inadequate, incomplete or insufficient servicing may result in operational problems with your vehicle that could lead to vehicle damage, an accident, or personal injury.
pair.new: Inadequate, incomplete, or insufficient service may result in operational problems with your vehicle that could lead to vehicle damage, an accident, or personal injury.

pair.old: Have your vehicle maintained and repaired by an authorized Kia dealer. Authorized Kia dealers meet Kia’s high service quality standards and receive technical support from Kia to provide you with a high level of service satisfaction.
pair.new: Have your vehicle maintained and repaired by an authorized Kia dealer. Authorized Kia dealers meet Kia’s high service quality standards and receive technical support from Kia to provide you with a high level of satisfaction.

pair.old: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) has issued a general warning to all vehicle owners of all brands regarding the risks associated with vehicle underbody corrosion. From your initial purchase, take the following steps to prevent unsafe corrosion damage to your vehicle:
pair.new: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) has issued a general warning to all vehicle owners of all brands regarding the risks associated with vehicle underbody corrosion. After your initial purchase, take the following steps to prevent unsafe corrosion damage to your vehicle:

pair.old: Wash the undercarriage of your vehicle regularly during the winter and whenever your vehicle has been exposed to salts or chemicals.
pair.new: Wash the underbody of your vehicle regularly during the winter and whenever your vehicle has been exposed to salts or chemicals.
Wash the underbody of your vehicle regularly during the winter and whenever your vehicle has been exposed to salts or chemicals.

pair.old: Do a thorough washing of the undercarriage at the end of the winter.
pair.new: Wash the underbody thoroughly at the end of winter.
Thoroughly wash the underbody at the end of winter.

pair.old: Use professional service technicians or governmental inspection stations to annually inspect for corrosion.
pair.new: Have your vehicle inspected for corrosion annually by a professional service technician or at a governmental inspection station.
Have your vehicle inspected for corrosion annually by a professional service technician or at a governmental inspection station.

pair.old: Immediately seek an inspection of your vehicle if you become visually aware of corrosion flaking or scaling or if you become aware of a change in vehicle performance, such as soft or spongy brakes, fluids leaking, impairment of directional control, suspension noises or rattling metal straps.
pair.new: Have your vehicle inspected immediately if you see sighs of corrosion flaking or scaling, or if you become aware of a change in vehicle performance (such as soft or spongy brakes, fluids leaking, impairment of directional control, suspension noises, or rattling metal straps).

Have your vehicle inspected immediately if you see sighs of corrosion flaking or scaling, or if you become aware of a change in vehicle performance (such as soft or spongy brakes, fluids leaking, impairment of directional control, suspension noises, or rattling metal straps).

pair.old: NHTSA further advises that after a vehicle is 7 years old, it is essential that you take these indicated maintenance steps to ensure that you protect yourself from unsafe corrosion conditions.
pair.new: NHTSA further advises that after a vehicle is 7 years old, it is essential that you take these indicated maintenance steps to protect yourself from unsafe corrosion conditions.

pair.old: Improper or incomplete service may result in problems. This section gives instructions only for maintenance items that are easy to perform.
pair.new: Improper or incomplete service may result in problems. This section provides instructions only for maintenance items that are easy to perform.

pair.old: Several procedures can only be done by an authorized Kia dealer with special tools.
pair.new: Several procedures can only be performed by an authorized Kia dealer with special tools.

pair.old: Improper owner maintenance during the warranty period may affect warranty coverage. For details, read the separate Warranty & Consumer Information manual provided with the vehicle. If you’re unsure about any servicing or maintenance procedure, have it done by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Improper owner maintenance during the warranty period may affect warranty coverage. For details, read the separate Warranty and Consumer Information manual provided with the vehicle. If you’re unsure about any servicing or maintenance procedure, have it done by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Do not wear jewelry or loose clothing while working under the hood of your vehicle with the engine running.
