These items can become entangled in moving parts, if you must run the engine while working under the hood, make certain that you remove all jewelry (especially rings, bracelets, watches, and necklaces) and all neckties, scarves, and similar loose clothing before getting near cooling fans.
pair.new: Do not wear jewelry or loose clothing while working under the hood of your vehicle with the engine running. These items can become entangled in moving parts. If you must run the engine while working under the hood, be sure to remove all jewelry (especially rings, bracelets, watches, and necklaces) and all neckties, scarves, and similar loose clothing before getting near the cooling fans.

pair.old: Do not touch metal parts (including strut bars) while the vehicle is operating or hot. Doing so could result in serious bodily injury. Turn the vehicle off and wait until the metal parts cool down to perform maintenance work on the vehicle.
pair.new: Do not touch metal parts (including strut bars) while the vehicle is operating or hot. Doing so could result in serious bodily injury. Turn the vehicle OFF and wait until the metal parts cool down before you perform maintenance work on the vehicle.

pair.old: The following lists are vehicle checks and inspections that should be performed by the owner or an authorized Kia dealer indicated at the frequencies to help ensure safe, dependable operation of your vehicle.
pair.new: The following lists are vehicle checks and inspections that should be performed by the owner or an authorized Kia dealer at the indicated frequencies to help ensure safe, dependable operation of your vehicle.

pair.old: Any adverse conditions should be brought to the attention of your dealer as soon as possible.
pair.new: Bring any adverse conditions to the attention of your dealer as soon as possible.

pair.old: These owner maintenance checks are generally not covered by your warranty and you may be charged for labor, parts and lubricants used.
pair.new: These owner maintenance checks are generally not covered by your warranty, and you may be charged for labor, parts, and lubricants used.

pair.old: Look for low or under inflated tires.
pair.new: Check for low tire pressure or underinflated tires.

pair.old: Check if the front of the radiator and condenser are clean and not blocked with leaves, dirt or insects etc. If any of the above parts are extremely dirty or you are not sure of their condition, take your vehicle to an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Check to make sure that the front of the radiator and condenser are clean and not blocked with leaves, dirt, insects, and so on. If any of these parts are extremely dirty, or if you are not sure of their condition, take your vehicle to an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Check for vibrations in the steering wheel. Notice any increased steering effort or looseness in the steering wheel or change in its straight-ahead position.
pair.new: Check for vibrations in the steering wheel. Notice any increased steering effort, looseness in the steering wheel, or change in its straight-ahead position.

pair.old: Notice if your vehicle constantly turns slightly or “pulls” to one side when traveling on smooth, level road.
pair.new: Notice whether your vehicle constantly turns slightly, or if it “pulls” or drifts to one side, when traveling on a smooth, level road.

pair.old: When stopping, listen and check for unusual sounds, pulling to one side, increased brake pedal travel or “hard-to-push” brake pedal.
pair.new: When stopping, listen for unusual sounds and check for drifting to one side, increased brake pedal travel, or difficulty engaging the brake pedal.

pair.old: If any slipping or changes in the operation of your transmission occurs, take your vehicle to an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If you notice any slipping or changes in the operation of your transmission, take your vehicle to an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Check the operation of all exterior lights, including the stoplights, turn signals and hazard warning flashers.
pair.new: Check the operation of all exterior lights, including the brake lights, turn signal lights, and hazard lights.

pair.old: Check the inflation pressures of all tires, including the spare, for worn tires, uneven wear, or damage.
pair.new: Check the inflation pressures of all tires, including the spare. Also check for worn tires, uneven wear, or damage.

pair.old: Check the radiator, heater and air conditioning hoses for leaks or damage.
pair.new: Check the radiator, heater, and air conditioning hoses for leaks or damage.

pair.old: Check the windshield washer spray and wiper operation. Clean the wiper blades with clean cloth dampened with washer fluid.
pair.new: Check the windshield washer spray and wiper operation. Clean the wiper blades with a clean cloth dampened with washer fluid.

pair.old: Check the muffler, exhaust pipes, shields and clamps.
pair.new: Check the muffler, exhaust pipes, shields, and clamps.

pair.old: Check the lap/shoulder belts for wear and function.
pair.new: Check the lap and shoulder bands of the seat belt for wear and function.

pair.old: Lubricate the door, hood locks and latches.
pair.new: Lubricate the door, hood locks, and latches.

pair.old: Lubricate the door rubber weatherstrips.
pair.new: Lubricate the door rubber weather strips.

pair.old: Inspect and lubricate transmission linkage and controls.
pair.new: Inspect and lubricate the transmission linkage and controls.

pair.old: Visually inspect steering, suspension, and chassis components for damaged, loose, or missing parts or signs of wear.
pair.new: Visually inspect the steering, suspension, and chassis components for damaged, loose, or missing parts or signs of wear.

pair.old: Scheduled maintenance service precaution
pair.new: Scheduled maintenance service precautions

pair.old: Follow the Normal Maintenance Schedule if the vehicle is usually operated under none of the following conditions apply. If any of the following conditions apply, follow the Maintenance Under Severe Usage Conditions.
pair.new: Follow the Normal Maintenance Schedule if the vehicle is not usually operated under any of the following conditions. Follow the Maintenance Under Severe Usage Conditions if any of the following conditions apply:

pair.old: A: Repeatedly driving short distance of less than 5 miles (8 km) in normal temperature or less than 10 miles (16 km) in freezing temperature.
pair.new: A: You repeatedly drive short distances of less than 5 miles (8 km) in normal temperatures, or less than 10 miles (16 km) in freezing temperatures.

pair.old: B: Extensive engine idling or low speed driving for long distances.
pair.new: B: You idle the engine extensively or drive at low speeds for long distances.

pair.old: C: Driving on rough, dusty, muddy, unpaved, graveled or salt spread roads.
pair.new: C: You drive on rough, dusty, muddy, unpaved, graveled, or salt-treated roads.

pair.old: D: Driving in areas using salt or other corrosive materials or in very cold weather
pair.new: D: You drive in areas that use salt or other corrosive materials, or in very cold weather.

pair.old: E: Driving in heavy dust condition.
pair.new: E: You drive in extremely dusty conditions.

pair.old: F: Driving in heavy traffic area.
pair.new: F: You drive in areas with heavy traffic.

pair.old: G: Driving on uphill, downhill, or mountain roads repeatedly.
pair.new: G: You frequently drive on uphill, downhill, or mountain roads.

pair.old: H: Using for towing or camping and driving with loading on the roof
pair.new: H: You use your vehicle for towing or camping and drive with cargo loaded on the roof.

pair.old: I: Driving for patrol car, taxi, other commercial use of vehicle towing.
pair.new: I: You drive your vehicle as a patrol car, taxi, or for other commercial purposes.

pair.old: J: Frequently driving under high speed or rapid acceleration/deceleration
pair.new: J: You frequently drive at high speeds or rapidly accelerate and decelerate.

pair.old: K: Frequently driving in stop-and-go conditions.
pair.new: K: You frequently drive in stop-and-go traffic.

pair.old: If your vehicle operates under the following conditions, you should inspect, replace or refill more frequently or follow the ’Severe’ Maintenance schedule. After the periods or distance shown in the chart, continue to follow the prescribed maintenance intervals.
pair.new: If you operate your vehicle under these conditions, you should inspect your vehicle, replace parts, or refill fluids more frequently, or follow the Maintenance Under Severe Usage Conditions schedule. Follow the prescribed maintenance intervals for the periods of time or distances shown in the chart.

pair.old: After 10 years or 100,000 miles (150,000 km), we recommend using severe maintenance schedule.
pair.new: After 10 years or 100,000 miles (150,000 km), we recommend using the Maintenance Under Severe Usage Conditions schedule.

pair.old: The following maintenance services must be performed to ensure good emission control and performance. Keep receipts for all vehicle services to protect your warranty. Where both mileage and date are shown, the frequency of service is determined by whichever occurs first.
pair.new: You must perform the following maintenance services to ensure good emission control and performance. Keep receipts for all vehicle services to protect your warranty. Where both the mileage and date are shown, the frequency of service you need is based on whichever occurs first.

pair.old: I: Inspect and if necessary, adjust, correct, clean or replace.
pair.new: I: Inspect and, if necessary, adjust, correct, clean, or replace.

pair.old:
Fuel additives
Fuel additives
If TOP TIER Detergent Gasoline is not available, one bottle of additive is recommended. Additives are available from your authorized Kia dealer along with information on how to use them. Do not mix other additives.
pair.new:

pair.old:
Engine oil and engine oil filter
Engine oil and engine oil filter
As it is normal for engine oil to be consumed during driving, the engine oil level should be checked on regular basis.
The engine oil change interval for normal operating conditions is based on the use of the recommended engine specification. If the recommended engine oil specification is not used, then replace the engine oil according to the maintenance schedule under severe operating conditions.
Never add any additives to the engine oil. Engine oil additives can change its properties of engine oil and may cause serious engine failure.
pair.new:
Engine oil and engine oil filter
Engine oil and engine oil filter
A Check the engine oil level on a regular basis, as it is normal for the vehicle to consume engine oil while you drive.
The engine oil change interval for normal operating conditions is based on the use of the recommended engine oil specification. If you do not use the recommended engine oil specification, replace the engine oil according to the Maintenance Under Severe Usage Conditions schedule.
Never add any additives to the engine oil. Engine oil additives can change the properties of engine oil and may cause serious engine failure.

pair.old:
Coolant (Engine)
Coolant (Engine)
When adding coolant, use only deionized water or soft water for your vehicle and never mix hard water in the coolant filled at the factory. An improper coolant mixture can result in serious malfunction or engine damage.
pair.new:
Coolant (Engine)
Coolant (Engine)
When adding coolant, only use deionized water or soft water for your vehicle. Never mix hard water into the coolant filled at the factory. An improper coolant mixture can result in serious malfunction or engine damage.

pair.new: Brake lines, hoses, and connections

pair.new: Fuel lines, hoses, and connections

Drive belts (Engine)

Inspect the alternator, water pump and air conditioner drive belt and if necessary, repair or replace. Inspect drive belt tensioner, idler and alternator pulley and if necessary repair or replace.
pair.new:
Drive belts (Engine)
Drive belts (Engine)
Inspect the alternator, water pump, and air conditioner drive belt and, if necessary, repair or replace them. Inspect the drive belt tensioner, idler, and alternator pulley and, if necessary, repair or replace them.

Differential oil (rear) (AWD)
Differential oil (rear) (AWD)
If your vehicle has been submerged in water or in a flooded area, the fluids should be changed as a precaution.
pair.new:
Differential oil (rear) (AWD)
Differential oil (rear) (AWD)
If your vehicle has been in a flooded area or submerged in water, change the fluids as a precaution.

Transfer case oil (AWD)

pair.old: Fuel filter (Gasoline engine)
pair.new: Fuel filter (Gasoline engine)

pair.old: The fuel filter is normally maintenance free but periodic inspection is recommended during scheduled maintenance to look for conditions caused by poor fuel quality.
pair.new: The fuel filter does not normally require maintenance, but periodic inspection is recommended during scheduled maintenance to look for conditions caused by poor fuel quality.

old.old: I: Inspect and if necessary, adjust, correct, clean or replace.

new.new: I: Inspect and, if necessary, adjust, correct, clean or replace.
R: Replace or change.

pair.old: Engine oil and engine oil filter
pair.new: Engine oil and filter

pair.old: Brake discs, pads, and calipers
pair.new: Brake discs, pads and calipers

pair.old: The engine oil and filter should be changed at the intervals specified in the maintenance schedule. If the vehicle is being driven in severe conditions, more frequent oil and filter changes are required.
pair.new: Change the engine oil and filter at the intervals specified in the maintenance schedule. If you drive your vehicle in severe conditions, more frequent oil and filter changes are required.

pair.old: Inspect all drive belts for evidence of cuts, cracks, excessive wear or oil saturation and replace if necessary. Drive belts should be checked periodically for proper tension and adjusted as necessary.
pair.new: Inspect all drive belts for evidence of cuts, cracks, excessive wear, or oil saturation, and replace them if necessary. Periodically check the drive belts for proper tension, and adjust them if necessary.

pair.old: When you are inspecting the belt, place the ENGINE START/STOP button in the LOCK/OFF or ACC position.
pair.new: When you inspect the drive belt, press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the LOCK/OFF or ACC position.

pair.old: The fuel filter is considered to be maintenance free, but periodic inspection is recommended to be scheduled depending on the quality of fuel used.
pair.new: The fuel filter is considered maintenance-free, but periodic inspection is recommended depending on the quality of the fuel used.

pair.old: If there are safety risks such as fuel flow restriction, surging, loss of power, hard starting problem etc., replace the fuel filter immediately regardless of maintenance schedule and consult an authorized Kia dealer for details.
pair.new: If there are safety risks, such as fuel flow restriction, surging, loss of power, hard starting problems, and so on, replace the fuel filter immediately regardless of the maintenance schedule. Consult an authorized Kia dealer for details.

pair.old: Fuel lines, fuel hoses and connections
pair.new: Fuel lines, fuel hoses, and connections

pair.old: Check the fuel lines, fuel hoses and connections for leakage and damage. Have the fuel lines, fuel hoses and connections replaced by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Check the fuel lines, fuel hoses, and connections for leakage and damage. Have the fuel lines, fuel hoses, and connections replaced by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: The fuel tank and fuel cap should be inspected at those intervals specified in the maintenance schedule. Make sure that a new fuel tank or fuel cap is correctly replaced.
pair.new: Inspect the fuel tank and fuel cap at the intervals specified in the maintenance schedule. Make sure to correctly replace a fuel tank or fuel cap with a new one.

pair.old: Inspect the surface of hoses for evidence of heat and/or mechanical damage. Hard and brittle rubber, cracking, tears, cuts, abrasions, and excessive swelling indicate deterioration. Particular attention should be paid to examine those hose surfaces nearest to high heat sources, such as the exhaust manifold.
pair.new: Inspect the surface of hoses for evidence of heat or mechanical damage. Hard and brittle rubber, cracking, tears, cuts, abrasions, and excessive swelling indicate deterioration. Pay particular attention to the hose surfaces nearest to high heat sources, such as the exhaust manifold.

pair.old: Inspect the hose routing to ensure that the hoses do not contact with any heat source, sharp edges or moving component which might cause heat damage or mechanical wear. Inspect all hose connections, such as clamps and couplings, to make sure they are secure, and that no leaks are present. Hoses should be replaced immediately if there is any evidence of deterioration or damage.
pair.new: Inspect the hose routing to ensure that the hoses do not contact any heat source, sharp edges, or moving components that might cause heat damage or mechanical wear. Inspect all hose connections, such as clamps and couplings, to make sure they are secure and that no leaks are present. Replace hoses immediately if there is any evidence of deterioration or damage.

pair.old: Make sure to install new spark plugs of the correct heat range.
pair.new: Install new spark plugs for the correct heat range.

pair.old: When assembling parts, be sure to wipe the inside and outside of the boot bottom of the ignition coil and the insulator of the spark plug with a soft cloth to prevent contamination of the spark plug insulator.
pair.new: To prevent contamination of the spark plug insulator when assembling parts, use a soft cloth to wipe the inside and outside of the boot bottom of the ignition coil and the spark plug insulator.

pair.old: Check the cooling system components, such as the radiator, coolant reservoir, hoses and connections for leakage and damage. Replace any damaged parts.
pair.new: Check the cooling system components, such as the radiator, coolant reservoir, hoses, and connections for leakage and damage. Replace any damaged parts.

pair.old: The coolant should be changed at the intervals specified in the maintenance schedule.
pair.new: Change the coolant at the intervals specified in the maintenance schedule.

pair.old: Automatic transmission fluid should not be checked under normal usage conditions. Have the automatic transmission fluid changed by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Do not check the automatic transmission fluid under normal usage conditions. Have the automatic transmission fluid changed by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Visually check for proper installation, chafing, cracks, deterioration and any leakage. Replace any deteriorated or damaged parts immediately.
pair.new: Visually check for proper installation, chafing, cracks, deterioration, and any leakage. Replace any deteriorated or damaged parts immediately.

pair.old: Inspect the parking brake system including the parking brake lever (or pedal) and cables.
pair.new: Inspect the parking brake system, including the parking brake lever (or pedal) and cables.

pair.old: Check the brake fluid level in the brake fluid reservoir. The level should be between “MIN” and “MAX” marks on the side of the reservoir. Use only hydraulic brake fluid conforming to DOT 4 specification.
pair.new: Check the brake fluid level in the brake fluid reservoir. The level should be between the MIN and MAX marks on the side of the reservoir. Use only hydraulic brake fluid conforming to DOT 4 specifications.

pair.old: Visually inspect the exhaust pipes, muffler and hangers for cracks, deterioration, or damage. Start the engine and listen carefully for any exhaust gas leakage. Tighten connections or replace parts as necessary.
pair.new: Visually inspect the exhaust pipes, muffler, and hangers for cracks, deterioration, or damage. Start the engine and listen carefully for any exhaust gas leakage. Tighten connections or replace parts as necessary.

pair.old: Brake discs, pads and calipers >
pair.new: Brake discs, pads, and calipers

pair.old: Steering gear box, linkage & boots/lower arm ball joint
pair.new: Steering gear box, linkage, boots, and lower arm ball joint

pair.old: With the vehicle stopped and engine off, check for excessive free-play in the steering wheel.
pair.new: With the vehicle stopped and the engine OFF, check for excessive free-play in the steering wheel.

pair.old: Check the drive shafts, boots and clamps for cracks, deterioration, or damage. Replace any damaged parts and, if necessary, repack the grease.
pair.new: Check the drive shafts, boots, and clamps for cracks, deterioration, or damage. Replace any damaged parts and, if necessary, repack the grease.

pair.old: When checking engine oil, engine coolant, brake fluid, and washer fluid, always be sure to clean the area around any filler plug, drain plug, or dipstick before checking or draining any lubricant or fluid. This is especially important in dusty or sandy areas and when the vehicle is used on unpaved roads. Cleaning the plug and dipstick areas will prevent dirt and grit from entering the engine and other mechanisms that could be damaged.
pair.new: When you check the engine oil, engine coolant, brake fluid, and washer fluid, always be sure to clean the area around any filler plug, drain plug, or dipstick before checking or draining any lubricant or fluid. This is especially important in dusty or sandy areas, and when you use the vehicle on unpaved roads. Cleaning the plug and dipstick areas prevents dirt and grit from entering and damaging the engine and other mechanisms.

pair.old: Check the propeller shaft, boots, clamps, rubber coupling and center bearing rubber for cracks, deterioration, or damage. Replace any damaged parts and if necessary, repack the grease.
pair.new: Check the propeller shaft, boots, clamps, rubber coupling, and center bearing rubber for cracks, deterioration, or damage. Replace any damaged parts and, if necessary, repack the grease.

pair.old: Engine oil is used for lubricating, cooling, and operating various hydraulic components in the engine. Engine oil consumption while driving is normal, and it is necessary to check and refill the engine oil regularly. Also, check and refill the oil level within the recommended maintenance schedule to prevent deterioration of oil performance.
pair.new: Engine oil lubricates, cools, and operates various hydraulic components in the engine. Engine oil consumption while driving is normal, so it is necessary to check and refill the engine oil regularly. Check and refill the oil according to the recommended maintenance schedule to prevent deterioration of the oil performance.

pair.old: Check the engine oil following the below procedure:
pair.new: Check the engine oil according to the following procedure:

pair.old: Start the engine and allow it to reach normal operating temperature.
pair.new: Start the engine and allow it to reach the normal operating temperature.

pair.old: Turn the engine off, remove the oil filler cap and pull the dipstick out. Wait for 15 minutes for the oil to return to the oil pan.
pair.new: Turn OFF the engine, remove the oil filler cap, and pull out the dipstick. Wait for 15 minutes for the oil to return to the oil pan.

pair.old: Pull the dipstick out again and check the level. Check if the oil level is between the F-L line, and if it is below the L line, add enough oil to bring the level to F line.
pair.new: Pull the dipstick out again and check to see whether the oil level is between the F-L line. If it is below the L line, add enough oil to bring it to the F line.

pair.old: Wipe the dipstick clean and re-insert it fully.
pair.new: Wipe the dipstick clean and fully reinsert it.

pair.old: Be very careful not to touch the radiator hose when checking or adding engine oil as it may be hot enough to burn you.
pair.new: Be very careful not to touch the radiator hose when checking or adding engine oil, as it may be hot enough to burn you.

pair.old: When you wipe the oil level gauge, you should wipe it with a clean cloth. When mixed with debris, it can cause engine damage.
pair.new: When you wipe the oil level gauge, use a clean cloth. An oil level gauge with debris can damage the engine.

pair.old: Use a funnel to help prevent oil from being spilled on engine components.
pair.new: Use a funnel to help prevent you from spilling oil on the engine components.

pair.old: The engine oil consumption may increase while you break in your new vehicle. It will stabilize after driving 4,000 miles (6,000 km).
pair.new: Engine oil consumption may increase while you break in your new vehicle. It will stabilize after you drive 4,000 miles (6,000 km).

pair.old: The engine oil consumption can be affected by driving habits, climate conditions, traffic conditions, oil quality, etc. Therefore, it is recommended that you inspect the engine oil level regularly and refill it if necessary.
pair.new: Engine oil consumption can be affected by driving habits, climate conditions, traffic conditions, oil quality, and so on. Therefore, it is recommended that you inspect the engine oil level regularly and refill the oil, if necessary.

pair.old: The lubrication, rust prevention, cooling, and cleaning effect of the engine oil will gradually degrade during its use. Have the engine oil and filter changed by an authorized Kia dealer according to the Engine Oil Life Management System instructions or the maintenance schedule.
pair.new: The lubrication, rust prevention, cooling, and cleaning effect of the engine oil gradually degrades during its use. Have the engine oil and filter changed by an authorized Kia dealer according to the Engine Oil Life Management System instructions or the maintenance schedule.

pair.old: If the maintenance schedule to replace engine oil is exceeded, the engine oil performance may deteriorate, and the engine condition may be affected. Therefore, replace the engine oil according to the maintenance schedule.
pair.new: If you exceed the maintenance schedule to replace the engine oil, the engine oil’s performance may deteriorate, which may affect the engine’s condition. Therefore, replace the engine oil according to the maintenance schedule.

pair.old: To keep the engine in optimal condition, use the recommended engine oil and filter. If the recommended engine oil and filter are not used replace it according to the maintenance schedule under severe usage conditions.
pair.new: To keep the engine in optimal condition, use the recommended engine oil and filter. If you do not use the recommended engine oil and filter, replace them according to the Maintenance Under Severe Usage Conditions schedule.

pair.old: The purpose of the maintenance schedule for engine oil replacement is to prevent oil deterioration and it is irrelevant to oil consumption. Check and refill engine oil regularly.
pair.new: The purpose of the maintenance schedule for engine oil replacement is to prevent oil deterioration and it is irrelevant to oil consumption. Check and refill the engine oil regularly.

pair.old: Used engine oil may cause irritation or cancer of the skin if left in contact with the skin for prolonged periods of time. Used engine oil contains chemicals that have caused cancer in laboratory animals. Always protect your skin by washing your hands thoroughly with soap and warm water as soon as possible after handling used oil. Do not leave used engine oil within the reach of children.
pair.new: Used engine oil may cause skin irritation or cancer if left in contact with the skin for prolonged periods of time. Used engine oil contains chemicals that have caused cancer in laboratory animals. Always protect your skin by washing your hands thoroughly with soap and warm water as soon as possible after handling used engine oil. Keep used engine oil out of reach of children.

pair.old: When the oil pressure is low due to insufficient engine oil, the Engine Oil Pressure () warning light will appear.
pair.new: When the oil pressure is low due to insufficient engine oil, the Engine Oil Pressure warning light () appears.

pair.old: In addition, the enhanced engine protection system, which limits the engine’s power is activated and the Malfunction Indicator Lamp () will appear when the vehicle is driven in this state continuously.
pair.new: In addition, the enhanced engine protection system (which limits the engine’s power) activates, and the Malfunction Indicator Light () appears when you drive the vehicle in this state continuously.

pair.old: When the engine oil pressure is restored, the warning light and the enhanced engine protection system will turn off after the engine is restarted.
pair.new: When the engine oil pressure is restored, the warning light and the enhanced engine protection system turn OFF after you restart the engine.

pair.old: The engine oil is very hot immediately after the vehicle has been driven and can cause burns during replacement. Replace the engine oil after the engine oil has cooled down.
pair.new: The engine oil is very hot immediately after you drive the vehicle and can cause burns during replacement. Replace the engine oil after the engine oil has cooled down.

pair.old: Never add any additives to the engine oil. Engine oil additives can change its properties and may cause engine failure.
pair.new: Never use any additives in the engine oil. Additives can change the engine oil’s properties and cause engine failure.

pair.old: The high-pressure cooling system has a reservoir filled with year round antifreeze coolant. The reservoir is filled at the factory.
pair.new: The high-pressure cooling system has a reservoir filled with year-round antifreeze coolant. The reservoir is filled at the factory.

pair.old: Check the antifreeze protection and coolant level at least once a year, at the beginning of the winter season, and before traveling to a colder climate.
pair.new: Check the antifreeze protection and coolant level at least once a year (at the beginning of the winter season), and before traveling to a colder climate.

pair.old: Do not drive with no engine coolant. It may cause water pump failure and engine seizure, etc.
pair.new: Do not drive without engine coolant. This may cause water pump failure, engine seizure, and so on.

pair.old: Removing engine coolant reservoir cap
pair.new: Removing the engine coolant reservoir cap

pair.old: Never attempt to remove the engine coolant reservoir cap while the engine is operating or hot. Doing so might lead to cooling system and engine damage. Also, hot coolant or steam could cause serious personal injury.
pair.new: Never attempt to remove the engine coolant reservoir cap while the engine is operating or hot. Doing so might damage the cooling system and engine. Also, hot coolant or steam could cause serious personal injury.

pair.old: Turn the engine off and wait until it cools down. Use extreme care when removing the engine coolant reservoir cap. Wrap a thick towel around it and turn it counterclockwise slowly to the first stop. Step back while the pressure is released from the cooling system.
pair.new: Turn OFF the engine and wait until it cools down. Use extreme care when removing the engine coolant reservoir cap. Wrap a thick towel around it and turn it counterclockwise slowly to the first stop. Step back while the pressure is released from the cooling system.

pair.old: When you are sure all the pressure has been released, press down on the cap, using a thick towel, and continue turning counterclockwise to remove it.
pair.new: When you are sure that all of the pressure has been released, press down on the cap using a thick towel, and continue turning it counterclockwise to remove it.

pair.old: The electric motor (cooling fan) is controlled by engine coolant temperature, refrigerant pressure and vehicle speed. It may sometimes operate even when the engine is not running. Use extreme caution when working near the blades of the cooling fan so that you are not injured by a rotating fan blades. As the engine coolant temperature decreases, the electric motor will automatically shut off. This is a normal condition.
pair.new: The electric motor (cooling fan) is controlled by the engine coolant temperature, refrigerant pressure, and vehicle speed. The fan sometimes operates even when the engine is not running. To avoid injury from rotating cooling fan blades, use extreme caution when working near the blades. As the engine coolant temperature decreases, the electric motor automatically shuts OFF. This is normal operation.

pair.old: Check the condition and connections of all cooling system hoses and heater hoses. Replace any swollen or deteriorated hoses.
pair.new: Check the conditions and connections of all cooling system hoses and heater hoses. Replace any swollen or deteriorated hoses.

pair.old: The coolant level should be filled between MAX and MIN (F and L) marks on the side of the coolant reservoir when the engine is cool.
pair.new: The coolant level should be filled between the MAX and MIN (F and L) marks on the side of the coolant reservoir when the engine is cool.

pair.old: If the coolant level is low, add enough distilled (deionized) or soft water. Bring the level to MAX (F), but do not overfill.
pair.new: If the coolant level is low, add enough distilled (deionized) or soft water to bring the level to MAX (F). Do not overfill.

pair.old: If frequent additions are required, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If your vehicle requires frequent coolant refills, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Make sure the coolant cap is properly closed after refill of coolant. The engine could be overheated while driving.
pair.new: Make sure to properly close the coolant cap after refilling the coolant. Otherwise, the engine could overheat while you drive.

pair.old: Check if the engine coolant reservoir cap label is on straight.
pair.new: Make sure the engine coolant reservoir cap label is on straight.

pair.old: When adding coolant, use only deionized water or soft water for your vehicle and never mix hard water in the coolant filled at the factory. An improper coolant mixture can result in serious malfunction or engine damage.
pair.new: When adding coolant, use only deionized water or soft water for your vehicle. Never mix hard water into the coolant filled at the factory. An improper coolant mixture can result in serious malfunction or engine damage.

pair.old: DO NOT USE alcohol or methanol coolant or mix them with the specified coolant.
pair.new: Do not use alcohol or methanol coolant, or mix them with the specified coolant.

pair.old: For mixture percentage, refer to the following table.
pair.new: For mixture percentage, refer to the following table:

pair.old: Do not remove the engine coolant reservoir cap when the engine and radiator are hot. Scalding hot coolant and steam may blow out under pressure causing serious injury.
pair.new: Do not remove the engine coolant reservoir cap when the engine and radiator are hot. Scalding hot coolant and steam may blow out under pressure, causing serious injury.

pair.old: Put a thick cloth around the radiator cap before refilling the coolant to prevent the coolant from overflowing into engine parts such as the alternator.
pair.new: To prevent the coolant from overflowing into engine parts (such as the alternator), place a thick cloth around the radiator cap before refilling the coolant.

pair.old: Radiator coolant can severely obscure visibility when sprayed on the windshield and may cause loss of vehicle control or damage the paint and body trim.
pair.new: Radiator coolant can severely obscure visibility when sprayed on the windshield. This may cause a loss of vehicle control or damage the paint and body trim.

pair.old: Check the fluid level in the reservoir periodically. The fluid level should be between MAX and MIN marks on the side of the reservoir.
pair.new: Check the brake fluid level in the reservoir periodically. The fluid level should be between the MAX and MIN marks on the side of the reservoir.

pair.old: Before removing the reservoir cap and adding brake fluid, clean the area around the reservoir cap thoroughly to prevent brake fluid contamination.
pair.new: To prevent brake fluid contamination, clean the area around the reservoir cap thoroughly before removing the cap and adding brake fluid.

pair.old: Periodically check that the fluid level in the brake fluid reservoir is between MIN and MAX. The level will fall with accumulated mileage. This is a normal condition associated with the wear of the brake linings.
pair.new: Periodically check that the fluid level in the brake fluid reservoir is between MIN and MAX. The level falls as you accumulate mileage. This is a normal condition associated with wear on the brake linings.

pair.old: If the fluid level is excessively low, have the system checked by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the fluid level is excessively low, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Use only hydraulic brake fluid that conforms to DOT 4 specifications. (Refer to .)
pair.new: Only use hydraulic brake fluid that conforms to DOT 4 specifications. (Refer to .)

pair.old: In the event the brake system requires frequent additions of fluid, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the brake system requires you to add fluid frequently, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: When changing and adding brake fluid, handle it carefully. Do not let contact your eyes. If brake fluid should contact your eyes, immediately flush them with a large quantity of fresh tap water. Have your eyes examined by a doctor as soon as possible.
pair.new: When changing and adding brake fluid, handle it carefully. Do not let it come into contact with your eyes. If brake fluid comes into contact with your eyes, immediately flush them with a large quantity of fresh tap water. Have your eyes examined by a doctor as soon as possible.

pair.old: Do not allow brake fluid to contact the vehicle’s body paint, as paint damage will result. Brake fluid, which has been exposed to open air for an extended time should never be used as its quality cannot be guaranteed. It should be properly disposed. Don’t put in the wrong kind of fluid. A few drops of mineral-based oil, such as engine oil, in your brake system can damage brake system parts.
pair.new: Do not allow brake fluid to come into contact with the vehicle’s body paint, as it will damage the paint. Never use brake fluid that has been exposed to open air for an extended period of time, as its quality cannot be guaranteed. Properly dispose of it instead. Do not put the wrong kind of fluid into the brake fluid reservoir. A few drops of mineral-based oil (such as engine oil) in your brake system can damage the brake system parts.

pair.old: Filter replacement
pair.new: Replacing the air cleaner filter

pair.old: Air cleaner filter must be replaced when necessary and should not be washed.
pair.new: Replace the air cleaner filter when necessary, and do not wash it.

pair.old: You can clean the filter when inspecting the air cleaner compartment. Clean the filter by using compressed air.
pair.new: You can clean the filter when inspecting the air cleaner compartment. Use compressed air to clean the filter.

pair.old: Pull up the air cleaner cover (2) and open.
pair.new: Pull up the air cleaner cover (2) and open it.

pair.old: Assemble in reverse order.
pair.new: Reassemble the air cleaner filter parts and cover in the reverse order of disassembly.

pair.old: If the vehicle is operated in extremely dusty or sandy areas, replace the element more often than the usual recommended intervals. (Refer to )
pair.new: If you drive your vehicle in extremely dusty or sandy areas, replace the filter more often than the usual recommended intervals. (Refer to .)

pair.old: Do not drive with the air cleaner removed; this will result in excessive engine wear.
pair.new: Do not drive with the air cleaner filter removed. This results in excessive engine wear.

pair.old: When removing the air cleaner filter, be careful that dust or dirt does not enter the air intake, or damage may result.
pair.new: When removing the air cleaner filter, make sure that dust or dirt does not enter the air intake, as this can cause damage.

pair.old: Filter inspection
pair.new: Inspecting the climate control air filter

pair.old: The climate control air filter should be replaced according to the maintenance schedule. If the vehicle is operated in severely air-polluted cities or on dusty rough roads for a long period, it should be inspected more frequently and replaced earlier. When you replace the climate control air filter, replace it by performing the following procedure, and be careful to avoid damaging other components.
pair.new: Replace the climate control air filter according to the maintenance schedule. If you drive your vehicle in severely air-polluted cities or on dusty rough roads for a long period of time, have the filter inspected more frequently and replaced earlier. Perform the following procedure to replace the climate control air filter, and be careful to avoid damaging other components.

pair.old: With the glove box open, pull the support strap (1).
pair.new: With the glove box open, pull out the support strap (1).

pair.old: Reassemble in the reverse order of disassembly.
pair.new: Reassemble the climate control air filter parts and cover in the reverse order of disassembly.

pair.old: When replacing the climate control air filter install it properly. Otherwise, the system may produce noise and the effectiveness of the filter may be reduced.
pair.new: When you replace the climate control air filter, be sure to install it properly. Otherwise, you may hear noise from the system and notice a reduction in the filter’s effectiveness.

pair.old: When the wipers no longer clean adequately, the blades may be worn or cracked, and require replacement.
pair.new: When the wipers no longer clean the windshield adequately, the blades may be worn or cracked and require replacement.

pair.old: Contamination of either the windshield or the wiper blades with foreign matter can reduce the effectiveness of the windshield wipers. Common sources of contamination are insects, tree sap, and hot wax treatments used by some commercial car washes. If the blades are not wiping properly, clean both the window and the blades with a good cleaner or mild detergent, and rinse thoroughly with clean water.
pair.new: Contamination of either the windshield or the wiper blades with foreign substances can reduce the effectiveness of the windshield wipers. Common sources of contamination are insects, tree sap, and hot wax treatments used by some commercial car washes. If the blades are not wiping properly, clean both the windshield and the blades with a good cleaner or mild detergent, and rinse thoroughly with clean water.

pair.old: To prevent damage to the wiper arms or other components, do not attempt to move the wipers manually.
pair.new: To prevent damage to the wiper arms or other components, do not attempt to move the wipers manually.

pair.old: The use of a non-specified wiper blade could result in wiper malfunction and failure.
pair.new: The use of a non-specified wiper blade could result in wiper malfunction and failure.

pair.old: Do not allow the wiper arm to fall against the windshield, since it may chip or crack the windshield.
pair.new: Do not allow the wiper arm to fall against the windshield, as it may crack or chip the glass.

pair.old: Do not pull the wiper arm forward, since arm could chip hood paint.
pair.new: Do not pull the wiper arm forward, as the arm could chip the hood paint.

pair.old: Make sure that both blade assemblies are installed firmly by trying to pull it slightly.
pair.new: Make sure that both blade assemblies are installed firmly by trying to pull them slightly.

pair.old: Return the wiper arm onto the windshield.
pair.new: Return the wiper arms to the windshield.

pair.old: Turn ignition to the ON position and wiper arms will return to the normal operating position.
pair.new: Press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position to return the wiper arms to their normal operating position.

pair.old: Within 20 seconds after the vehicle ignition is OFF, move the wiper lever to MIST position for over 2 seconds until the wiper moves down to the bottom-middle part.
pair.new: Within 20 seconds after the vehicle’s ignition is OFF, move the wiper lever to the MIST position for more than 2 seconds until the wiper blade moves down to the bottom middle position.

pair.old: Lift up the wiper blade and pull the blade to remove it.
pair.new: Lift up the wiper blade and pull it to remove it.

pair.old: Make sure the blade assembly is installed firmly by trying to pull it slightly.
pair.new: Make sure that the blade assembly is installed firmly by trying to pull it slightly.

pair.old: If the replacement is correct place the wiper arm on the rear windshield, and turn the vehicle ignition to ON and operate the wipers to check the blade is installed correctly.
pair.new: Place the wiper arm on the rear windshield, press the ENGINE START/STOP button in the ON position, and operate the rear wiper to make sure the blade is installed correctly.

pair.old: To prevent damage to the wiper arms or other components, have the wiper blade replaced by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: To prevent damage to the wiper arm or other components, have the wiper blade replaced by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: If too much force is applied to the wiper arm while pulling the blade, the center part may be damaged.
pair.new: If you apply too much force to the wiper arm while pulling the blade, you may damage the center part.

pair.old: The wiper may not operate for approx. 10 seconds when the wiper is operated without washer fluid or the blades are frozen. This is not a malfunction. It is a wiper protection system activated by the motor overload circuit within the wiper motor.
pair.new: The wiper may not operate for approximately 10 seconds if you try to operate it without washer fluid or if the blades are frozen. This is not a malfunction. It is a wiper protection system activated by the motor overload circuit within the wiper motor.

pair.old: The front windshield should be cleaned with a hose and wiped with a clean towel with the wiper blades raised up. The wiper blades should be wiped clean when grease or wax is applied to the blades.
pair.new: With the wiper blades raised up, clean the front windshield with a hose and wipe it with a clean towel. Whenever grease or wax has been applied to the wiper blades, wipe them clean.

pair.old: Keep the battery securely mounted.
pair.new: Keep the battery securely installed.

pair.old: Keep the battery top clean and dry.
pair.new: Keep the top of the battery clean and dry.

pair.old: Rinse any spilled electrolyte from the battery immediately with a solution of water and baking soda.
pair.new: Immediately rinse any spilled electrolyte from the battery using a solution of water and baking soda.

pair.old: If the vehicle is not going to be used for an extended time, disconnect the battery cables.
pair.new: If you do not plan to use the vehicle for an extended period of time, disconnect the battery cables.

pair.old: The battery contains hydrogen -- a highly combustible gas which will explode if it contacts a flame or spark.
pair.new: The battery contains hydrogen, a highly combustible gas that will explode if it comes into contact with a flame or spark.

pair.old: Keep batteries out of the reach of children because batteries contain highly corrosive SULFURIC ACID and electrolytes. Do not allow battery acid to contact your skin, eyes, clothing or paint finish.
pair.new: Keep batteries out of reach of children because batteries contain highly corrosive sulfuric acid and electrolytes. Do not allow battery acid to come into contact with your skin, eyes, clothing, or your vehicle’s paint finish.

pair.old: Wear eye protection when charging or working near a battery. Always provide ventilation when working in an enclosed space.
pair.new: Wear eye protection when charging or working near a battery. Always have proper ventilation when working in an enclosed space.

pair.old: Always read the following instructions carefully when handling a battery.
pair.new: Always read instructions carefully when handling a battery.

pair.old: If any electrolyte gets into your eyes, flush your eyes with clean water for at least 15 minutes and get immediate medical attention. If electrolyte gets on your skin, thoroughly wash the contacted area. If you feel pain or burning sensation, get medical attention immediately.
pair.new: If electrolyte gets into your eyes, flush your eyes with clean water for at least 15 minutes and seek immediate medical attention. If electrolyte gets on your skin, thoroughly wash the affected area. If you feel pain or a burning sensation, seek immediate medical attention.

pair.old: An inappropriately disposed battery can be harmful to the environment and human health. Dispose of the battery according to your local law(s) or regulation.
pair.new: An inappropriately disposed battery can be harmful to the environment and human health. Dispose of the battery according to your local laws or regulations.

pair.old: Never touch the electrical ignition system while the vehicle is running. This system works with high voltage, which can “zap” you.
pair.new: Never touch the electrical ignition system while the vehicle is running. This system works with high voltage, which can shock you.

pair.old: Battery posts, terminals, and related accessories contain lead and lead compounds. Wash hands after handling.
pair.new: Battery posts, terminals, and related accessories contain lead and lead compounds. Wash your hands after handling these parts.

pair.old: Battery posts, terminals, and related accessories contain lead and lead compounds, chemicals known to the State of California to cause cancer, birth defects and reproductive harm. Batteries also contain other chemicals known to the State of California to cause cancer. Wash hands after handling.
pair.new: Battery posts, terminals, and related accessories contain lead and lead compounds, chemicals known to the State of California to cause cancer, birth defects, and reproductive harm. Batteries also contain other chemicals known to the State of California to cause cancer. Wash your hands after handling these parts.

pair.old: If the becomes discharged, either run the engine for at least 60 minutes driving or at idle. Alternatively, you may connect a fully automatic regulated charger to the engine compartment front jumper posts or at the battery in the liftgate.
pair.new: If the battery discharges, run the engine for at least 60 minutes, either driving or idling. Alternatively, you may connect a fully automatic regulated charger to the engine compartment front jumper posts or to the battery in the liftgate.

pair.old: When recharging the battery, observe the following precautions:
pair.new: When recharging the battery, take the following precautions:

pair.old: The battery must be removed from the vehicle and placed in an area with good ventilation.
pair.new: Remove the battery from the vehicle and place it in an area with good ventilation.

pair.old: Watch the battery during charging, and stop or reduce the charging rate in following cases:
pair.new: Watch the battery while it charges, and stop or reduce the charging rate in the following cases:

pair.old: the battery cells begin gassing (boiling) violently.
pair.new: The battery cells begin boiling violently.

pair.old: the electrolyte temperature of any cell exceeds 120 °F (49 °C).
pair.new: The electrolyte temperature of any cell is above 120 °F (49 °C).

pair.old: Wear eye protection when checking the battery during charging.
pair.new: Wear eye protection when you check the battery while it is charging.

pair.old: Before performing maintenance or recharging the battery, turn off all accessories and stop the vehicle.
pair.new: Before performing maintenance or recharging the battery, turn OFF all accessories and stop the vehicle.

pair.old: Turn off the battery charger main switch.
pair.new: Turn OFF the battery charger main switch.

pair.old: Absorbent Glass Mat (AGM) batteries are maintenance free and have the AGM battery serviced by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Absorbent Glass Mat (AGM) batteries are maintenance-free. Have your AGM battery serviced by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: For charging your AGM battery, use only fully automatic battery chargers that are specially developed for AGM batteries.
pair.new: To charge your AGM battery, use only fully automatic battery chargers that are specially developed for AGM batteries.

pair.old: When replacing the AGM battery, use parts for replacement from an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: When replacing the AGM battery, use replacement parts from an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Do not open or remove the cap on top of the battery. This may cause leaks of internal electrolytes that could result in severe injury.
pair.new: Do not open or remove the cap on top of the battery. This may cause internal electrolytes to leak, which could result in severe injury.

pair.old: The following items should be reset after the battery has been discharged or the battery has been disconnected.
pair.new: Reset the following items after the battery has been discharged or disconnected:

pair.old: Auto up/down window (Refer to )
pair.new: Auto up and down window (Refer to )

pair.old: 12V Parking lithium battery is added to secure stable operation power supply when functions such as OTA update, after-blow, and CCS are operated while parking.
pair.new: Your vehicle uses an additional 12V Parking lithium battery to secure stable operation power supply when you operate certain functions while parking, such as OTA updates, after-blow, and CCS.

pair.old: 12V Lithium Auxiliary Battery care
pair.new: 12V Lithium auxiliary battery care

pair.old: If the 12V lithium auxiliary battery detects an abnormality, such as high temperature or overcharging, a warning message is displayed in the cluster or a malfunction alert text is sent to the registered mobile phone number.
pair.new: If the 12V lithium auxiliary battery detects an abnormality (such as high temperature or overcharging), the instrument cluster displays a warning message, or the vehicle sends a malfunction alert text message to the registered mobile phone number.

pair.old: However, malfunction alert text is sent only to customers who subscribe to the Kia Connect service.
pair.new: Only customers who subscribe to the Kia Connect service receive a malfunction alert text message.

pair.old: If you receive a warning message in the cluster or a malfunction notification on your phone, In this case, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If you receive a warning message on the instrument cluster or a malfunction alert text on your phone, have the system inspected by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: For proper maintenance, safety, and maximum fuel economy, you must always maintain the recommended tire pressures and stay within the load limits and weight distribution recommended for your vehicle.
pair.new: For proper maintenance, safety, and maximum fuel economy, you must always maintain the recommended tire pressures and stay within the load limits and weight distribution recommended for your vehicle.

pair.old: All tire pressures should be checked when the tires are cold. “Cold Tires” means the vehicle has not been driven for at least three hours or driven less than 1 mile (1.6 km).
pair.new: Check all tire pressures when the tires are cold (after the vehicle has been sitting for at least 3 hours, or you haven’t driven it more than 1 mile (1.6 km) since you started it.)

pair.old: Recommended pressures must be maintained for the best ride, vehicle handling, and minimum tire wear.
pair.new: Maintain the recommended tire pressures for the best ride, vehicle handling, and minimum tire wear.

pair.old: For recommended pressure, refer to .
pair.new: For recommended tire pressures, refer to .

pair.old: All specifications (sizes and pressures) can be found on a label attached to the driver’s side center pillar.
pair.new: You can find all tire specifications (sizes and pressures) on the label attached to the driver side center pillar.

pair.old: Inflate your tires consistent with the instructions provided in this manual. Regularly check the tire inflation pressure, and correct it as needed, at least twice a month and before any long trips. If you fail to observe this precaution, you may be driving on underinflated tires, which may not only compromise your vehicle’s driving stability, but may also lead to tire damage and the risk of an accident. This risk is much higher on hot days and when driving for long periods at high speeds.
pair.new: Inflate your tires according to the instructions provided in this manual. Regularly check the tire inflation pressure, and correct it as needed at least twice a month and before any long trips. If you fail to take this precaution, you may be driving on underinflated tires, which may not only compromise your vehicle’s driving stability, but may also lead to tire damage and the risk of an accident. This risk is much higher on hot days and when driving at high speeds for long periods of time.

pair.old: Failure to maintain specified pressure may result in excessive wear, poor handling, reduced fuel economy, deformation of tire and/or wheel, harsh ride conditions, possibility for additional damage from road hazards, or result in tire failure.
pair.new: Failure to maintain the specified tire pressure may result in excessive wear, poor handling, reduced fuel economy, deformation of tires or wheels, harsh riding conditions, possible additional damage from road hazards, or tire failure.

pair.old: Always observe the following:
pair.new: Always follow these recommendations:

pair.old: Check tire pressure when the tires are cold. (After vehicle has been parked for at least three hours or hasn’t been driven more than 1 mile (1.6 km) since startup.)
pair.new: Check the tire pressure when the tires are cold (after the vehicle has been sitting for at least 3 hours, or you haven’t driven it more than 1 mile (1.6 km) since you started it.)

pair.old: Never overload your vehicle. Be careful not to overload a vehicle luggage rack if your vehicle is equipped with one.
pair.new: Never overload your vehicle. Be careful not to overload the luggage rack (if equipped).

pair.old: Warm tires normally exceed recommended cold tire pressures by 4 ~ 6 psi (28 ~ 41 kPa). Do not release air from warm tires to adjust the pressure or the tires will be underinflated.
pair.new: Warm tires normally exceed recommended cold tire pressures by 4–6 psi (28–41 kPa). Do not release air from warm tires to adjust the pressure, or the tires will be underinflated.

pair.old: Tire Inflation
pair.new: Tire inflation

pair.old: Overinflation or underinflation can reduce tire life, adversely affect vehicle handling, and lead to sudden tire failure. This could result in loss of vehicle control and potential injury.
pair.new: Overinflation or underinflation can reduce tire life, adversely affect vehicle handling, and lead to sudden tire failure. This could result in a loss of vehicle control and potential injury.

pair.old: Refer to the following for detailed definitions of the terms that are found in the tire description:
pair.new: Refer to the following list for detailed definitions of the terms found in the tire description:

pair.old: A rubber coated layer of cords that is located between the plies and the tread. Cords may be made from steel or other reinforcing materials.
pair.new: A rubber coated layer of cords located between the plies and the tread. Cords may be made from steel or other reinforcing materials.

pair.old: The weight of a motor vehicle with standard and optional equipment (including the maximum capacity of fuel, oil and coolant), but without passengers and cargo.
pair.new: The weight of a motor vehicle with standard and optional equipment (including the maximum capacity of fuel, oil, and coolant), but without passengers or cargo.

pair.old: A code molded into the sidewall of a tire signifying that the tire complies with the U.S. Department of Transportation motor vehicle safety standards. The DOT code includes the Tire Identification Number (TIN), an alphanumeric designator which can also identify the tire manufacturer, production plant, brand and date of production.
pair.new: A code molded into the sidewall of a tire signifying that the tire complies with the U.S. Department of Transportation motor vehicle safety standards. The DOT code includes the Tire Identification Number (TIN), an alphanumeric designator that can also identify the tire manufacturer, production plant, brand, and date of production.

pair.old: Gross Vehicle Weight Rating
pair.new: Gross Vehicle Weight Rating.

pair.old: Load ratings
pair.new: Load rating

pair.old: The sum of curb weight; accessory weight; vehicle capacity weight; and production options weight.
pair.new: The sum of the curb weight, accessory weight, vehicle capacity weight, and production options weight.

pair.old: The number of occupants a vehicle is designed to seat multiplied by 150 lbs. (68 kg).
pair.new: The number of occupants a vehicle is designed to seat multiplied by 150 lb (68 kg).

pair.old: The side of an asymmetrical tire that has a particular side that faces outward when mounted on a vehicle. The outward facing sidewall bears white lettering or bears manufacturer, brand, and/or model name molding that is higher or deeper than the same moldings on the inner facing sidewall.
pair.new: The side of an asymmetrical tire that faces outward when mounted on a vehicle. The outward facing sidewall bears white lettering or manufacturer, brand, or model name moldings that are higher or deeper than the same moldings on the inner facing sidewall.

pair.old: A mechanical device made of rubber, chemicals, fabric and steel or other materials, that, when mounted on an automotive wheel, provides the traction and contains the gas or fluid that sustains the load.
pair.new: A mechanical device made of rubber, chemicals, fabric, and steel or other materials that, when mounted on an automotive wheel, provides the traction and contains the gas or fluid that sustains the load.

pair.old: The combined weight of installed regular production options weighing over 5 lbs. (2.3 kg) more than the standard items which they replace, not previously considered in curb weight or accessory weight. Examples include heavy duty brakes, ride levelers, roof rack, heavy duty battery and special trim.
pair.new: The combined weight of installed regular production options weighing over 5 lb (2.3 kg) more than the standard items that they replace, not previously considered in curb weight or accessory weight. Examples include heavy duty brakes, ride levelers, roof rack, heavy duty battery, and special trim.

pair.old: Vehicle manufacturer’s recommended tire inflation pressure and shown on the tire placard.
pair.new: The vehicle manufacturer’s recommended tire inflation pressure, shown on the tire placard.

pair.old: A metal support for a tire and upon which the tire beads are seated.
pair.new: A metal support for a tire upon which the tire beads are seated.

pair.old: An alphanumeric code assigned to a tire indicating the maximum speed at which a tire can operate.
pair.new: An alphanumeric code assigned to a tire indicating the maximum speed at which the tire can operate.

pair.old: The friction between the tire and the road surface. The amount of grip provided.
pair.new: The friction between the tire and the road surface (the amount of grip provided).

pair.old: Narrow bands, sometimes called “wear bars,” that show across the tread of a tire when only 2/32 inch (1.6 mm) of tread remains.
pair.new: Narrow bands, sometimes called “wear bars,” that show across the tread of a tire when only 1/16 inch (1.6 mm) of tread remains.

pair.old: Uniform Tire Quality Grading Standards, a tire information system that provides consumers with ratings for a tire’s traction, temperature and treadwear. Ratings are determined by tire manufacturers using government testing procedures. The ratings are molded into the sidewall of the tire.
pair.new: Uniform Tire Quality Grading Standards, a tire information system that provides consumers with ratings for a tire’s traction, temperature, and tread wear. Ratings are determined by tire manufacturers using government testing procedures. The ratings are molded into the sidewall of the tire.

pair.old: The weight of designated seating positions multiplied by 150 lbs. (68 kg) plus the rated cargo and luggage load.
pair.new: The weight of the designated seating positions multiplied by 150 lb (68 kg) plus the rated cargo and luggage load.

pair.old: Load on an individual tire due to curb and accessory weight plus maximum occupant and cargo weight.
pair.new: The load on an individual tire due to curb and accessory weight plus the maximum occupant and cargo weight.

pair.old: Load on an individual tire that is determined by distributing to each axle its share of the curb weight, accessory weight, and normal occupant weight and driving by 2.
pair.new: The load on an individual tire, determined by distributing to each axle its share of the curb weight, accessory weight, and normal occupant weight and dividing by 2.

pair.old: Manufacturer or Brand name is shown.
pair.new: The sidewall shows the manufacturer or brand name.

pair.old: A tire’s sidewall is marked with a tire size designation. You will need this information when selecting replacement tires for your vehicle. The following explains what the letters and numbers in the tire size designation mean.
pair.new: A tire’s sidewall is marked with a tire size designation. You will need this information when selecting replacement tires for your vehicle. The following example explains what the letters and numbers in the tire size designation mean.

pair.old: P: Applicable vehicle type (tires marked with the prefix “P” are intended for use on passenger vehicles or light trucks; however, not all tires have this marking).
pair.new: P: Applicable vehicle type (tires marked with the prefix “P” are intended for use on passenger vehicles or light trucks; however, not all tires have this marking).

pair.old: 45: Aspect ratio. The tire’s section height as a percentage of its width.
pair.new: 45: Aspect ratio (the tire’s section height as a percentage of its width).

pair.old: 105: Load Index, a numerical code associated with the maximum load the tire can carry.
pair.new: 105: Load Index (a numerical code associated with the maximum load the tire can carry).

pair.old: Wheels are also marked with important information that you need if you ever have to replace one. The following explains what the letters and numbers in the wheel size designation mean.
pair.new: Wheels are also marked with important information that you need if you ever have to replace one. The following example explains what the letters and numbers in the wheel size designation mean.

pair.old: The chart below lists many of the different speed ratings currently being used for passenger vehicle tires. The speed rating is part of the tire size designation on the sidewall of the tire. This symbol corresponds to that tire’s designed maximum safe operating speed.
pair.new: The following chart lists many of the different speed ratings that are currently used for passenger vehicle tires. The speed rating is part of the tire size designation marked on the sidewall of the tire. This symbol corresponds to that tire’s designed maximum safe operating speed.

pair.old: Any tires that are over 6 years old, based on the manufacturing date, should be replaced by new ones. You can find the manufacturing date on the tire sidewall (possibly on the inside of the wheel), displaying the DOT code. The DOT code is a series of numbers on a tire consisting of numbers and English letters. The manufacturing date is designated by the last four digits (characters) of the DOT code.
pair.new: Any tires that are over 6 years old, based on the manufacturing date, should be replaced with new ones. You can find the manufacturing date on the tire sidewall (possibly on the inside of the wheel) displayed as part of the DOT code. The DOT code consists of a series of numbers and English letters on a tire. The manufacturing date is designated by the last 4 digits (characters) of the DOT code.

pair.old: The front part of the DOT means a plant code number, tire size and tread pattern and the last four numbers indicate week and year manufactured.
pair.new: The first part of the DOT code contains the plant code number, tire size, and tread pattern, and the last four numbers indicate week and year manufactured.

pair.old: DOT XXXX XXXX 1624 represents that the tire was produced in the 16th week of 2024.
pair.new: DOT XXXX XXXX 1624 means that the tire was produced in the 16th week of 2024.

pair.old: Replace tires within the recommended time frame. Failure to replace tires as recommended can result in sudden tire failure, which could lead to a loss of control and an accident.
pair.new: Replace tires within the recommended time frame. Failure to replace tires as recommended can result in sudden tire failure, which could lead to a loss of vehicle control and an accident.

pair.old: The number of layers or plies of rubber- coated fabric in the tire. Tire manufacturers also must indicate the materials in the tire, which include steel, nylon, polyester, and others. The letter “R” means radial ply construction; the letter “D” means diagonal or bias ply construction; and the letter “B” means belted-bias ply construction.
pair.new: Tire ply means the number of layers (or plies) of rubber-coated fabric in the tire. Tire manufacturers must list the materials in the tire, which include steel, nylon, polyester, and others. The letter “R” means radial ply construction, the letter “D” means diagonal or bias ply construction, and the letter “B” means belted-bias ply construction.

pair.old: This number is the greatest amount of air pressure that should be put in the tire. Do not exceed the maximum inflation pressure. Refer to for recommended inflation pressure.
pair.new: This number is the greatest amount of air pressure that should be put into the tire. Do not exceed the maximum inflation pressure. Refer to for the recommended inflation pressure.

pair.old: This number indicates the maximum load in kilograms and pounds that can be carried by the tire. When replacing the tires on the vehicle, always use a tire that has the same load rating as the factory installed tire.
pair.new: This number is the maximum load in kilograms and pounds that the tire can carry. When replacing the tires on the vehicle, always use a tire that has the same load rating as the factory-installed tire.

pair.old: Quality grades can be found where applicable on the tire sidewall between tread shoulder and maximum section width.
pair.new: You can find quality grades (where applicable) on the tire sidewall between the tread shoulder and the maximum section width.

pair.old: Tires degrade over time, even when they are not being used. Regardless of the remaining tread, we recommend that tires be replaced after approximately six (6) years of normal service. Heat caused by hot climate or frequent high loading conditions can accelerate the aging process.
pair.new: Tires degrade over time, even when you are not using them. Regardless of the remaining tread, we recommend that you replace the tires after approximately 6 years of normal service. Heat from hot climates or frequent high loading conditions can accelerate the aging process.

pair.old: The tread wear grade is a comparative rating based on the wear rate of the tire when tested under controlled conditions on a specified government test course. For example, a tire graded 150 would wear one-and-a-half times (1½) as well on the government course as a tire graded 100.
pair.new: The tread wear grade is a comparative rating based on the wear rate of the tire when tested under controlled conditions on a specified government test course. For example, a tire graded 150 would wear 1½ times as well on the government test course as a tire graded 100.

pair.old: The relative performance of tires depends upon the actual conditions of their use. Performance may depart significantly from the norm due to variations in driving habits, service practices and differences in road characteristics and climate.
pair.new: The relative performance of tires depends on the actual conditions of their use. Performance may depart significantly from the norm due to variations in driving habits, service practices, and differences in road characteristics and climate.

pair.old: These grades are molded on the sidewalls of passenger vehicle tires. The tires available as standard or optional equipment on your vehicle may vary with respect to grade.
pair.new: Tread wear grades are molded on the sidewalls of passenger vehicle tires. The tires available as standard or optional equipment on your vehicle may vary with respect to grade.

pair.old: Traction - AA, A, B & C
pair.new: Traction: AA, A, B, and C

pair.old: The traction grades, from highest to lowest, are AA, A, B and C. Those grades represent the tire’s ability to stop on wet pavement as measured under controlled conditions on specified government test surfaces of asphalt and concrete. A tire marked C may have poor traction performance.
pair.new: The traction grades from highest to lowest are AA, A, B, and C. These grades represent the tire’s ability to stop on wet pavement as measured under controlled conditions on specified government test surfaces of asphalt and concrete. A tire marked C may have poor traction performance.

pair.old: The traction grade assigned to this tire is based on straight-ahead braking traction tests, and does not include acceleration, cornering, hydroplaning, or peak traction characteristics.
pair.new: The traction grade assigned to this tire is based on straight-ahead braking traction tests and does not include acceleration, turning, hydroplaning, or peak traction characteristics.

pair.old: Temperature -A, B & C
pair.new: Temperature: A, B, and C

pair.old: The temperature grades are A (the highest), B and C representing the tire’s resistance to the generation of heat and its ability to dissipate heat when tested under controlled conditions on a specified indoor laboratory test wheel.
pair.new: The temperature grades from highest to lowest are A, B, and C, representing the tire’s resistance to the generation of heat and its ability to dissipate heat when tested under controlled conditions on a specified indoor laboratory test wheel.

pair.old: Sustained high temperature can cause the material of the tire to degenerate and reduce tire life, and excessive temperature can lead to sudden tire failure. The grade C corresponds to a level of performance which all passenger vehicle tires must meet under the Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standard No. 109. Grades B and A represent higher levels of performance on the laboratory test wheel than the minimum required by law.
pair.new: Sustained high temperature can cause the material of the tire to degenerate and reduce the life of the tire, and excessive temperature can lead to sudden tire failure. Grade C corresponds to the performance level that all passenger vehicle tires must meet under the Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standard No. 109. Grades B and A represent higher levels of performance on the laboratory test wheel than the minimum required by law.

pair.old: Use a good quality gauge to check tire pressure. You cannot tell if your tires are properly inflated simply by looking at them. Radial tires may look properly inflated even when they’re underinflated.
pair.new: Use a high-quality gauge to check tire pressure. You cannot tell if your tires are properly inflated simply by looking at them. Radial tires may look properly inflated even when they are underinflated.

pair.old: Check the tire inflation pressure when the tires are cold. “Cold” means your vehicle has been sitting or at least three hours or driven no more than 1 mile (1.6 km).
pair.new: Check the tire inflation pressure when the tires are cold (after the vehicle has been sitting for at least 3 hours, or you haven’t driven it more than 1 mile (1.6 km) since you started it.)

pair.old: Press the tire gauge firmly onto the valve to get a pressure measurement. If the cold tire inflation pressure matches the recommended pressure on the tire and loading information label, no further adjustment is necessary.
pair.new: Press the tire gauge firmly onto the valve to measure the pressure. If the cold tire inflation pressure matches the recommended pressure on the tire and loading information label, no further adjustment is necessary.

pair.old: If the pressure is low, add air until you reach the recommended amount.
pair.new: If the pressure is low, add air until you reach the recommended pressure.

pair.old: Tires with too much or too little pressure wear unevenly. This could result in poor handling, loss of vehicle control, and sudden tire failure leading to accidents, injuries, and even death. The recommended cold tire pressure for your vehicle can be found in this manual and on the tire label located on the driver’s side center pillar.
pair.new: Tires with too much or too little pressure wear unevenly. This could result in poor handling, a loss of vehicle control, and sudden tire failure, leading to accidents, injuries, and even death. The recommended cold tire pressure for your vehicle can be found in this manual and on the tire label located on the driver side center pillar.

pair.old: In addition to proper inflation, correct wheel alignment helps to decrease tire wear.
pair.new: In addition to proper inflation, correct wheel alignment helps to prevent wear on the tires.

pair.old: Make sure the newly installed tires are balanced correctly to increase vehicle ride comfort and tire life. In addition, always rebalance the tire when the tire is removed from the wheel.
pair.new: Make sure the newly installed tires are balanced correctly to increase vehicle ride comfort and tire life. In addition, always rebalance a tire when it is removed from the wheel.

pair.old: If you notice unusual tire wear or your vehicle pulling one way or the other, the alignment may need to be reset.
pair.new: If you notice unusual tire wear, or if your vehicle pulls or drifts in one direction or the other, you may need to have the alignment reset.

pair.old: If you notice your vehicle vibrating when driving on a smooth road, your wheels may need to be rebalanced.
pair.new: If you notice your vehicle vibrating when you drive on a smooth road, you may need to have your wheels rebalanced.

pair.old: To equalize tread wear, it is recommended that the tires be rotated every 8,000 miles (13,000 km) or sooner if irregular wear develops.
pair.new: To equalize tread wear, it is recommended that you rotate the tires every 8,000 miles (13,000 km) or sooner if irregular wear develops.

pair.old: When rotating tires, check for uneven wear and damage.
