Abnormal wear is usually caused by incorrect tire pressure, improper wheel alignment, out of-balance wheels, severe braking or severe cornering. Look for bumps or bulges in the tread or side of tire. Replace the tire if you find either of these conditions. Replace the tire if fabric or cord is visible. After rotation, be sure to bring the front and rear tire pressures to specification and check lug nut tightness. (proper torque is 79 ~ 94 lb-ft [11 ~ 13 kgf·m])
pair.new: When rotating the tires, check for uneven wear and damage. Abnormal wear is usually caused by incorrect tire pressure, improper wheel alignment, out-of-balance wheels, severe braking, or severe turning. Look for bumps or bulges in the tread or on the side of the tire. Replace the tire if you find either of these conditions. Replace the tire if fabric or cord is visible. After rotating the tires, be sure to bring the front and rear tire pressures to specification and check the tightness of the lug nuts (proper torque is 79–94 lb-ft [11–13 kgf·m]).

pair.old: Disc brake pads should be inspected for wear whenever tires are rotated.
pair.new: Inspect the disc brake pads for wear whenever you rotate the tires.

pair.old: Rotate radial tires that have an asymmetric tread pattern only from front to rear and not from right to left.
pair.new: Rotate radial tires with an asymmetric tread pattern only from front to rear and not from right to left.

pair.old: Do not mix bias ply and radial ply tires under any circumstances. This may cause unusual handling characteristics.
pair.new: Do not mix bias ply and radial ply tires under any circumstances. This may cause unusual handling.

pair.old: Tire traction can be reduced if you drive on worn tires, tires that are improperly inflated or on slippery road surfaces.
pair.new: Driving on worn tires, improperly inflated tires, or slippery road surfaces may reduce the tire traction.

pair.old: Tires should be replaced when tread wear indicators appear. Slow down whenever there is rain, snow or ice on the road to reduce the possibility of losing control of the vehicle.
pair.new: Replace the tires when the tread wear indicators appear. Slow down whenever there is rain, snow, or ice on the road to reduce the risk of losing control of the vehicle.

pair.old: If the tire is worn evenly, a tread wear indicator will appear as a solid band across the tread.
pair.new: If the tire is worn evenly, a tread wear indicator appears as a solid band across the tread.

pair.old: This shows there is less than 1/16 in (1.6 mm) of tread left on the tire. Replace the tire when this happens.
pair.new: This indicator shows that there is less than 1/16 in (1.6 mm) of tread left on the tire. Replace the tire when this indicator appears.

pair.old: The Anti-lock Brake System (ABS) works by comparing the speed of the wheels. The tire size affects wheel speed. When replacing tires, all 4 tires must use the same size originally supplied with the vehicle. Using tires of a different size can cause the ABS and ESC to work irregularly.
pair.new: The Anti-lock Brake System (ABS) works by comparing the speed of the wheels. The tire size affects the wheel speed. When replacing the tires, all 4 tires must be the same size as the original ones supplied with the vehicle. Using tires of a different size can prevent the ABS and ESC from working correctly.

pair.old: It is best to replace all four tires at the same time. If that is not possible, or necessary, then replace the two front or two rear tires as a pair. Replacing just one tire can seriously affect your vehicle’s handling.
pair.new: It is best to replace all 4 tires at the same time. If that is not possible or necessary, replace the 2 front or 2 rear tires as a pair. Replacing just 1 tire can seriously affect your vehicle’s handling.

pair.old: When replacing tires, use the same type of tires originally supplied with the vehicles to avoid impacting driving performance.
pair.new: When you replace the tires, use the same type of tires as the ones originally supplied with the vehicle to avoid impacting the driving performance.

pair.old: When replacing the metal wheels for any reason, make sure the new wheels are equivalent to the original factory units in diameter, rim width and offset.
pair.new: When replacing the metal wheels for any reason, make sure the new wheels are equivalent to the original factory units in diameter, rim width, and offset.

pair.old: A wheel with an incorrect size may adversely affect many things: wheel and bearing life, braking and stopping abilities, handling characteristics, ground clearance, body-to-tire clearance, snow chain clearance, speedometer and odometer calibration, headlamp aiming and bumper height.
pair.new: A wheel of the incorrect size may adversely affect many things, including wheel and bearing life, braking and stopping abilities, handling characteristics, ground clearance, body-to-tire clearance, tire chain clearance, speedometer and odometer calibration, headlamp aim, and bumper height.

pair.old: All season tires:
pair.new: All season tires

pair.old: Kia specifies all season tires on some models to provide good performance for use all year round, including snowy and icy road conditions.
pair.new: Kia specifies all season tires on some models to provide good performance for use all year round, including for snowy and icy road conditions.

pair.old: All season tires are identified by ALL SEASON and/or M+S (Mud and Snow) on the tire sidewall. Snow tires have better snow traction than all season tires and may be more appropriate in some areas.
pair.new: All season tires are identified by ALL SEASON or M+S (Mud and Snow) on the tire sidewall. Snow tires have better snow traction than all season tires and may be more appropriate in some areas.

pair.old: Summer tires:
pair.new: Summer tires

pair.old: Summer tire performance is substantially reduced in snow and ice. Summer tires do not have the tire traction rating M+S (Mud and Snow) on the tire side wall. if you plan to operate your vehicle in snowy or icy conditions, Kia recommends the use of snow tires or all season tires on all four wheels.
pair.new: Summer tire performance is substantially reduced in snow and ice. Summer tires do not have the tire traction rating M+S (Mud and Snow) on the tire side wall. If you plan to drive your vehicle in snowy or icy conditions, Kia recommends the use of snow tires or all season tires on all four wheels.

pair.old: Snow tires:
pair.new: Snow tires

pair.old: Snow tires should be installed on all four wheels; otherwise, poor handling may result.
pair.new: Install snow tires on all 4 wheels. Doing otherwise may result in poor handling.

pair.old: Snow tires should carry 4 psi (28 kPa) more air pressure than the pressure recommended for the standard tires on the tire label on the driver’s side of the center pillar, or up to the maximum pressure shown on the tire sidewall, whichever is less.
pair.new: Snow tires should carry 4 psi (28 kPa) more air pressure than the pressure recommended for standard tires on the tire label on the driver side center pillar, or up to the maximum pressure shown on the tire sidewall, whichever is less.

pair.old: Do not drive faster than 75 mph (120 km/h) when your vehicle is equipped with snow tires.
pair.new: Do not drive at speeds above 75 mph (120 km/h) when your vehicle is equipped with snow tires.

pair.old: Do not use summer tires at temperatures below 45 °F (7 °C) or when driving on snow or ice. At temperatures below 45 °F (7 °C), summer tires can lose elasticity, and therefore traction and braking power as well. Change the tires on your vehicle to winter or all-weather tires of the same size as the standard tires of the vehicle. Both types of tires are identified by the M+S (Mud and Snow) marking. Using summer tires at very cold temperatures could cause cracks to form, thereby damaging the tires permanently.
pair.new: Do not use summer tires at temperatures below 45 °F (7 °C) or when driving on snow or ice. At temperatures below 45 °F (7 °C), summer tires can lose elasticity, and therefore lose traction and braking power. Change the tires on your vehicle to winter or all season tires of the same size as the standard tires on the vehicle. Both types of tires are identified by the M+S (Mud and Snow) marking. Using summer tires at very cold temperatures could cause cracks to form and permanently damage the tires.

pair.old: Be sure that the chains are installed in accordance with the manufacturer’s instructions.
pair.new: Be sure to install the chains according to the manufacturer’s instructions.

pair.old: To minimize tire and chain wear, do not continue to use tire chains when they are no longer needed.
pair.new: To minimize tire and chain wear, do not continue to use tire chains when you no longer need them.

pair.old: When driving on roads covered with snow or ice, drive at speeds less than 20 mph (30 km/h).
pair.new: When driving on roads covered with snow or ice, drive at speeds below 20 mph (30 km/h).

pair.old: Use the SAE “S” class or wire chains.
pair.new: Use the SAE “S” class or wire chains.

pair.old: If you hear noise caused by chains contacting the body, retighten the chain to avoid contact with the vehicle body.
pair.new: If you hear noise caused by the chains making contact with the body of the vehicle, retighten the chains to avoid contact with the body.

pair.old: To prevent body damage, retighten the chains after driving 0.3 ~ 0.6 miles (0.5 ~ 1.0 km).
pair.new: To prevent damage to the body of the vehicle, retighten the chains after driving 0.3–0.6 miles (0.5–1.0 km).

pair.old: Use wire chains less than 0.47 inches (12 mm) to prevent damage to the chain’s connection.
pair.new: Use wire chains below 0.47 inches (12 mm) to prevent damage to the chains’ connection.

pair.old: Radial-ply tires provide improved tread life, road hazard resistance and smoother high-speed ride.
pair.new: Radial-ply tires provide improved tread life, road hazard resistance, and a smoother high-speed ride.

pair.old: The radial-ply tires used on this vehicle are of belted construction and are selected to complement the ride and handling characteristics of your vehicle. Radial-ply tires have the same load carrying capacity as bias-ply or bias belted tires of the same size, and use the same recommended inflation pressure.
pair.new: The radial-ply tires used on this vehicle are of belted construction and are selected to complement the vehicle’s ride and handling characteristics. Radial-ply tires have the same load carrying capacity as bias-ply or bias belted tires of the same size, and they have the same recommended inflation pressure.

pair.old: Do not mix radial-ply tires with bias-ply or bias belted tires. Any combinations of radial-ply and bias-ply or bias belted tires when used on the same vehicle will seriously deteriorate vehicle handling. Identical radial-ply tires should always be used as a set of four.
pair.new: Do not mix radial-ply tires with bias-ply or bias belted tires. Any combinations of radial-ply and bias-ply or bias belted tires when used on the same vehicle seriously deteriorate the vehicle’s handling. Always use identical radial-ply tires as a set of 4.

pair.old: Longer wearing tires can be more susceptible to irregular tread wear. Follow the tire rotation interval shown in this section to achieve the tread life potential of these tires. Cuts and punctures in radial-ply tires are repairable only in the tread area, because of sidewall flexing. Consult your tire dealer for radial-ply tire repairs.
pair.new: Longer wearing tires can be more susceptible to irregular tread wear. Follow the tire rotation interval shown in this section to achieve the tread life potential of these tires. Cuts and punctures in radial ply tires can only be repaired in the tread area because of sidewall flexing. Consult your tire dealer for radial-ply tire repairs.

pair.old: Low aspect ratio tires, whose aspect ratio is lower than 50, are provided for sporty looks.
pair.new: Low aspect ratio tires (tires whose aspect ratio is lower than 50) are provided for sporty looks.

pair.old: Because the low aspect ratio tires are optimized for handling and braking, it may be more uncomfortable to ride in and there is more noise compared to normal tires.
pair.new: Because low aspect ratio tires are optimized for handling and braking, it may be more uncomfortable to ride in vehicles equipped with them, and they make more noise than normal tires.

pair.old: Because the sidewall of the low aspect ratio tire is shorter than normal, the wheel and tire of the low aspect ratio tire is more easily damaged. Follow the instructions below:
pair.new: Because the sidewall of a low aspect ratio tire is shorter than normal, the wheel and tire are more easily damaged. Follow these recommendations:

pair.old: When driving on a rough road or off road, drive cautiously because tires and wheels may be damaged. After driving, inspect the tires and wheels.
pair.new: When driving on a rough road or off road, drive cautiously to avoid damaging the tires and wheels. After driving, inspect the tires and wheels.

pair.old: When passing over a pothole, speed bump, manhole, or curb stone, drive slowly so that the tires and wheels are not damaged.
pair.new: When passing over a pothole, speed bump, manhole, or curb stone, drive slowly to avoid damaging the tires and wheels.

pair.old: If the tire is impacted, inspect the tire condition or contact an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the tire sustains impact, inspect the tire’s condition or contact an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: To prevent damage to the tire, inspect the tire condition and pressure every 1,900 miles (3,000 km).
pair.new: To prevent damage to the tires, inspect their condition and pressure every 1,900 miles (3,000 km).

pair.old: It is not easy to visually recognize tire damage. If there is a hint of tire damage even though you cannot see the damage, have the tire checked or replaced because the tire damage may cause air leakage from the tire.
pair.new: It is not easy to visually recognize tire damage. If there is a hint of tire damage, even though you cannot see it, have the tire checked or replaced because damage may cause air to leak from the tire.

pair.old: If the tire is damaged by driving on a rough road, off road, pothole, manhole, or curb stone, it will not be covered by your warranty.
pair.new: If you damage the tires by driving on a rough road, off road, or over a pothole, manhole, or curb stone, they are not covered by your warranty.

pair.old: A vehicle’s electrical system is protected from electrical overload damage by fuses.
pair.new: Fuses protect a vehicle’s electrical system from electrical overload damage.

pair.old: This vehicle has 2 (or 3) fuse panels, one located in the driver’s side panel bolster, the other in the engine compartment near the battery.
pair.new: Your vehicle has 2 (or 3) fuse panels: one in the driver side panel bolster, and the others in the engine compartment near the battery.

pair.old: If any of your vehicle’s lights, accessories, or controls do not work, check the appropriate circuit fuse. If a fuse has blown, the element inside the fuse will melt.
pair.new: If any of your vehicle’s lights, accessories, or controls do not work, check the appropriate circuit fuse. If a fuse is blown, the element inside the fuse melts.

pair.old: If the electrical system does not work, first check the driver’s side fuse panel.
pair.new: If the electrical system does not work, check the driver side fuse panel first.

pair.old: If the replacement fuse blows, this indicates an electrical problem. Avoid using the system involved and immediately consult an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If a replacement fuse blows, this indicates an electrical problem. Avoid using the respective system and immediately consult an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Three kinds of fuses are used: blade type for lower amperage rating, cartridge type, and multi fuse for higher amperage ratings.
pair.new: Your vehicle uses 3 kinds of fuses: blade type for lower amperage rating, cartridge type, and multi fuse for higher amperage ratings.

pair.old: Never install a wire or aluminum foil instead of the proper fuse - even as a temporary repair. It may cause extensive wiring damage and a possible fire.
pair.new: Never install wire or aluminum foil in place of a proper fuse, even as a temporary repair. Doing so may cause extensive wiring damage and possibly fire.

pair.old: Do not use a screwdriver or any other metal object to remove fuses because they may cause a short circuit and damage the system.
pair.new: Do not use a screwdriver or any other metal object to remove fuses. Doing so may cause a short circuit and damage the electrical system.

pair.old: When replacing a fuse, turn the ignition ’OFF’ and turn off switches of all electrical devices then remove battery (-) terminal.
pair.new: When you replace a fuse, press the ENGINE START/STOP button and all other switches in the OFF position, then remove the battery (-) terminal.

pair.old: The actual fuse/relay panel label may differ from equipped items.
pair.new: The actual fuse or relay panel label may differ from equipped items.

pair.old: Do not remove fuses, relays and terminals fastened with bolts or nuts. The fuses, relays and terminals may be fastened incompletely, and it may cause a possible fire. If fuses, relays and terminals fastened with bolts or nuts are blown, consult with an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: Do not remove fuses, relays, and terminals fastened with bolts or nuts. The fuses, relays, and terminals may be fastened incompletely and could cause a possible fire. If fuses, relays, and terminals fastened with bolts or nuts are blown, consult an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: When replacing a blown fuse or relay, make sure the new fuse or relay fits tightly into the clips. Failure to tightly install the fuse or relay may cause damage to the wiring and electric systems.
pair.new: When replacing a blown fuse or relay, make sure the new fuse or relay fits tightly in the clips. Failure to tightly install a fuse or relay may cause damage to the wiring and electrical systems.

pair.old: If you directly connect the wire on the taillamp or replace the bulb which is over the regulated capacity to install trailers etc., the inner junction block can get burned.
pair.new: The inner junction block can burn if you directly connect the wire on the tail lamp or replace its bulb with one over the regulated capacity to install a trailer.

pair.old: All electrical repairs should be performed by authorized Kia dealerships using approved Kia parts. Using other wiring components, especially when retrofitting multimedia or a theft alarm system, phone or radio may cause vehicle damage and increase the risk of a vehicle fire.
pair.new: All electrical repairs should be performed by authorized Kia dealerships using approved Kia parts. Using other wiring components, especially when retrofitting a multimedia or theft alarm system, phone, or radio may cause vehicle damage and increase the risk of a vehicle fire.

pair.old: Do not rewire your vehicle in any way as doing so may affect the performance of several safety features in your vehicle. Rewiring your vehicle may also void your warranty and you may be responsible for any subsequent vehicle damage.
pair.new: Do not rewire your vehicle in any way. Doing so may affect the performance of several safety features in your vehicle. Rewiring your vehicle may also void your warranty, which makes you responsible for any subsequent vehicle damage.

pair.old:
No original text
To replace a fuse in the inner panel, follow these steps:

new.new: To replace a fuse in the inner panel, follow these steps:

pair.old: Turn the ENGINE START/STOP button and all other switches off.
pair.new: Press the ENGINE START/STOP button and all other switches in the OFF position.

pair.old: Open the fuse panel cover.
pair.new: Remove the fuse panel cover.

pair.old: Pull the suspected fuse straight out. Use the removal tool provided in the main fuse box in the engine compartment.
pair.new: Pull the suspected fuse straight out using the removal tool provided in the engine compartment’s main fuse box.

pair.old: Check the removed fuse; replace it if it is blown.
pair.new: Check if the fuse you removed is blown. If so, replace the fuse.

pair.old: Push in a new fuse of the same rating, and make sure it fits tightly in the clips.
pair.new: To replace the blown fuse, push in a new fuse of the same rating and make sure it fits tightly in the clips.

pair.old: If it fits loosely, consult an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the new fuse does not fit tightly, consult an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: If you do not have a spare, use a fuse of the same rating from a circuit you may not need for operating the vehicle, such as the cigarette lighter fuse.
pair.new: If you do not have a spare fuse, replace the blown fuse with one of the same rating from a circuit you may not need to operate the vehicle, such as the cigarette lighter fuse.

pair.old: If the headlamps or taillamps, stoplights, courtesy lamp, day time running lights (DRL) do not work and the fuses are OK, consult an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: If the headlamps, tail lamps, brake lamps, courtesy lamp, or daytime running lights (DRL) do not work and their fuses are not blown, consult an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Turn the ignition and all other switches off.
pair.new: Press the ENGINE START/STOP button and all other switches in the OFF position.

pair.old: Check the removed fuse; replace it if it is blown. To remove or insert the fuse, use the fuse puller in the engine compartment fuse panel.
pair.new: Pull the suspected fuse straight out using the fuse puller provided in the engine compartment fuse panel.
Check if the fuse you removed is blown. If so, use the fuse puller again to replace the fuse.

pair.old: Push in a new fuse of the same rating, and make sure it fits tightly in the clips. If it fits loosely, consult an authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: To replace the blown fuse, push in a new fuse of the same rating and make sure it fits tightly in the clips. If the new fuse does not fit tightly, consult an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: The electronic system may not function correctly even when the engine room and internal fuse box’s individual fuses are not disconnected. In such case the cause of the problem may be disconnection of the main fuse (BFT type), which is located inside the positive battery terminal (+) cap. Since the main fuse is designed more intricately than other parts, visit the nearest authorized Kia dealer.
pair.new: The electronic system may not function correctly, even when the engine room and internal fuse box’s individual fuses are not disconnected. In this case, the main fuse (BFT type), which is inside the positive battery terminal (+) cap, may be disconnected and causing the malfunction. Because the main fuse is designed more intricately than other parts, you should visit an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.new: Visually inspect the battery cap to make sure it is closed securely. If the battery cap is not securely latched, influx of moisture into the electrical system may damage the system.

pair.old: Inside the fuse/relay panel covers, you can find the fuse/relay label describing fuse/relay name and capacity.
pair.new: Inside the fuse/relay panel covers, you can find the fuse/relay label describing the fuse/relay name and capacity.

pair.old: If you park the vehicle around a stainless signboard or reflective building etc., the plastic exterior trim (bumper, spolier, trim, lights, outside mirror etc.) may be damaged by reflected sunlight from the external structure. To avoid damaging the plastic exterior trim, park the vehicle away from areas where there’s reflected light may or use a vehicle cover. (Depending on the vehicle, the type of exterior trim may differ.)
pair.new: If you park your vehicle near a stainless signboard or reflective building, the plastic exterior trim (bumper, spoiler, trim, lights, outside mirror, and so on) may be damaged by reflected sunlight. To avoid damaging the plastic exterior trim, park the vehicle away from areas with reflected light or use a vehicle cover. (Depending on the vehicle, the type of exterior trim may differ.)

pair.old: To help protect your vehicle’s finish from rust and deterioration, wash it thoroughly and frequently at least once a month with lukewarm or cold water.
pair.new: To help protect your vehicle’s finish from rust and deterioration, wash it thoroughly with lukewarm or cold water at least once a month.

pair.old: If you use your vehicle for off-road driving, you should wash it after each off-road trip. Pay special attention to the removal of any accumulation of salt, dirt, mud, and other foreign materials. Make sure the drain holes in the lower edges of the doors and rocker panels are kept clear and clean.
pair.new: If you use your vehicle for off road driving, wash it after each off road trip. Take special care to remove any accumulated salt, dirt, mud, and other foreign materials. Be sure to keep the drain holes in the lower edges of the doors and rocker panels clear and clean.

pair.old: Insects, tar, tree sap, bird droppings, industrial pollution and similar deposits can damage your vehicle’s finish if not removed immediately.
pair.new: Insects, tar, tree sap, bird droppings, industrial pollution, and similar deposits can damage your vehicle’s finish if not removed immediately.

pair.old: Even prompt washing with plain water may not completely remove all these deposits. A mild soap, safe for use on painted surfaces, may be used.
pair.new: Even prompt washing with plain water may not completely remove all of these deposits. Use a mild soap that is safe for painted surfaces.

pair.old: After washing, rinse the vehicle thoroughly with lukewarm or cold water. Do not allow soap to dry on the finish.
pair.new: After you wash the vehicle, rinse it thoroughly with lukewarm or cold water. Do not allow soap to dry on the finish.

pair.old: After washing the vehicle, test the brakes while driving slowly to see if they have been affected by water. If braking performance is impaired, dry the brakes by applying them lightly while maintaining a slow forward speed.
pair.new: After you wash the vehicle, test the brakes while driving slowly to see if they have been affected by water. If the braking performance is impaired, dry the brakes by engaging them lightly while maintaining a slow forward speed.

pair.old: Do not use strong soap, chemical detergents or hot water, and do not wash the vehicle in direct sunlight or when the body of the vehicle is warm.
pair.new: Do not use strong soap, chemical detergents, or hot water, and do not wash the vehicle in direct sunlight or when the body of the vehicle is warm.

pair.old: Be careful when washing the side windows of your vehicle, especially with high-pressure water. Water may leak through the windows and wet the interior.
pair.new: Be careful when washing the side windows of your vehicle, especially with high pressure water. Water may leak through the windows and wet the interior.

pair.old: Do not spray the camera, sensors or its surrounding area directly with a high-pressure washer. Shock applied from high pressure water may cause the device to not operate normally.
pair.new: Do not spray the camera, sensors, or their surrounding areas directly with a high-pressure washer. Shock from high-pressure water may affect these devices’ operation.

pair.old: Do not bring the nozzle tip close to boots (rubber or plastic covers) or connectors as they may be damaged if they contact high pressure water.
pair.new: Do not bring the nozzle tip close to the vehicle’s boots (rubber or plastic covers) or connectors, as contact from high-pressure water may damage them.

pair.old: Water washing in the engine compartment including high-pressure washing may cause failure of electrical circuits located in the engine compartment.
pair.new: Washing the engine compartment with water, including high-pressure washing, may cause the electrical circuits located in the engine compartment to fail.

pair.old: Never allow water or other liquids to contact electrical/electronic components and air ducts as this may damage them.
pair.new: Never allow water or other liquids to contact electronic components or air ducts, as this may damage them.

pair.old: After the vehicle has been washed, brake carefully while paying attention to the traffic conditions until the braking effect has been fully restored.
pair.new: After you wash the vehicle, brake carefully while paying attention to the traffic conditions until the braking function is fully restored.

pair.old: Wax the vehicle when water will no longer bead on the paint.
pair.new: Wax the vehicle when water no longer beads on the paint.

pair.old: Always wash and dry the vehicle before waxing. Use a good quality liquid or paste wax and follow the manufacturer’s instructions. Wax all metal trim to protect it and to maintain its luster.
pair.new: Always wash and dry the vehicle before waxing it. Use a high quality liquid or paste wax and follow the manufacturer’s instructions. Wax all metal trim to protect it and maintain its luster.

pair.old: Removing oil, tar, and similar materials with a spot remover will usually strip the wax from the finish. Be sure to rewax these areas even if the rest of the vehicle does not yet need waxing.
pair.new: Removing oil, tar, and similar materials with a spot remover usually strips the wax from the finish. Be sure to re-wax these areas, even if the rest of the vehicle does not yet need waxing.

pair.old: Drying vehicle
pair.new: Drying your vehicle

pair.old: Wiping dust or dirt off the body with a dry cloth will scratch the finish.
pair.new: Wiping dust or dirt off the body of the vehicle with a dry cloth will scratch the finish.

pair.old: Do not use steel wool, abrasive cleaners, acid detergents or strong detergents containing high alkaline or caustic agents on chrome-plated or anodized aluminum parts. This may result in damage to the protective coating and cause discoloration or paint deterioration.
pair.new: Do not use steel wool, abrasive cleaners, acid detergents, or strong detergents containing high alkaline or caustic agents on chrome-plated or anodized aluminum parts. This may result in damage to the protective coating and cause discoloration or paint deterioration.

pair.old: Deep scratches or stone chips in the painted surface must be repaired promptly. Exposed metal will quickly rust and may develop into a major repair expense.
pair.new: Repair deep scratches or chips in the painted surface promptly. Exposed metal rusts quickly and may develop into a major repair expense.

pair.old: If your vehicle is damaged and requires any metal repair or replacement, be sure the body shop applies anti-corrosion materials to the parts repaired or replaced.
pair.new: If your vehicle is damaged and requires any metal repair or replacement, be sure the body shop applies anti-corrosion materials to the parts that were repaired or replaced.

pair.old: Bright-metal maintenance
pair.new: Bright metal maintenance

pair.old: To remove road tar and insects, use a tar remover, not a scraper or other sharp object.
pair.new: To remove road tar and insects, use a tar remover (not a scraper or other sharp object).

pair.old: To protect the surfaces of bright metal parts from corrosion, apply a coating of wax or chrome preservative and rub to a high luster.
pair.new: To protect the surfaces of bright metal parts from corrosion, apply a coating of wax or chrome preservative and rub it to a high luster.

pair.old: During winter weather or in coastal areas, cover the bright metal parts with a heavier coating of wax or preservative. If necessary, coat the parts with non-corrosive petroleum jelly or other protective compound.
pair.new: During winter weather or in coastal areas, cover the bright metal parts with a heavier coating of wax or preservative. If necessary, coat the parts with non-corrosive petroleum jelly or another protective compound.

pair.old: Road salt and other corrosive chemicals are used in cold weather states to melt snow and prevent ice accumulation. If these chemicals are not regularly removed, they will corrode the vehicle underbody and, over time, damage many parts: the fuel lines, the fuel tank retention system, the suspension, the exhaust system, and even the body frame.
pair.new: Road salt and other corrosive chemicals are used in cold weather areas to melt snow and prevent ice accumulation. If you do not regularly remove these chemicals from your vehicle, they will corrode underbody and, over time, damage many parts: the fuel lines, the fuel tank retention system, the suspension, the exhaust system, and even the body frame.

pair.old: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) has warned all vehicle owners of all brands of the need to take the following steps:
pair.new: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) has warned vehicle owners of all brands to take the following steps:

pair.old: Clean the wheel when it has cooled.
pair.new: Clean the wheel when it is cool.

pair.old: Use only a mild soap or neutral detergent, and rinse thoroughly with water. Also, be sure to clean the wheels after driving on salted roads. This helps prevent corrosion.
pair.new: Use only a mild soap or neutral detergent and rinse thoroughly with water. Also, be sure to clean the wheels after driving on salted roads to help prevent corrosion.

pair.old: Do not use any alkaline or acid detergents It may damage and corrode the aluminum wheels coated with a clear protective finish.
pair.new: Do not use any alkaline or acid detergents. They may damage and corrode aluminum wheels coated with a clear protective finish.

pair.old: By using the most advanced design and construction practices to combat corrosion, we produce vehicles of the highest quality. This is only part of the job. To achieve the long-term corrosion resistance your vehicle can deliver, your cooperation and assistance is required.
pair.new: By using the most advanced design and construction practices to combat corrosion, Kia produces vehicles of the highest quality. However, this is only part of the job. Your assistance is required to achieve long-term corrosion resistance for your vehicle.

pair.old: Road salt, dirt and moisture that is allowed to accumulate underneath the vehicle.
pair.new: Road salt, dirt, and moisture that is allowed to accumulate underneath the vehicle.

pair.old: Removal of paint or protective coatings by stones, gravel, abrasion or minor scrapes and dents which leave unprotected metal exposed to corrosion.
pair.new: Removal of paint or protective coatings by stones, gravel, abrasions, or minor scrapes and dents that leave unprotected metal exposed to corrosion.

pair.old: If you live in an area where your vehicle is regularly exposed to corrosive materials, corrosion protection is particularly important. Some of the common causes of accelerated corrosion are road salts, dust control chemicals, ocean air and industrial pollution.
pair.new: If you live in an area where your vehicle is regularly exposed to corrosive materials, corrosion protection is particularly important. Some of the most common causes of accelerated corrosion are road salts, dust control chemicals, ocean air, and industrial pollution.

pair.old: Moisture creates the conditions in which corrosion is most likely to occur. Corrosion is accelerated by high humidity, particularly when temperatures are just above freezing. The corrosive material is kept in contact with the vehicle’s surface by moisture that evaporates slowly.
pair.new: Corrosion is most likely to occur in moist conditions. High humidity accelerates corrosion, particularly when temperatures are just above freezing. The corrosive material stays in contact with the vehicle’s surface because moisture evaporates slowly.

pair.old: Mud is particularly corrosive because it dries slowly and holds moisture in contact with the vehicle. Although the mud appears to be dry, it can still retain moisture and promote corrosion.
pair.new: Mud is particularly corrosive because it dries slowly and holds moisture in contact with the vehicle. Although the mud appears to be dry, it can still retain moisture and accelerate corrosion.

pair.old: High temperatures can also accelerate corrosion of parts that are not properly ventilated so the moisture can be dispersed. For all these reasons, it is particularly important to keep your vehicle clean and free of mud or accumulations of other materials. This applies not only to the visible surfaces but particularly to the underside of the vehicle.
pair.new: High temperatures can also accelerate corrosion of parts that are not well ventilated enough to disperse the moisture. For all of these reasons, it is particularly important to keep your vehicle clean and free of mud or accumulations of other materials. This applies not only to the visible surfaces, but particularly to the underbody of the vehicle.

pair.old: You can help prevent corrosion from starting by observing the following:
pair.new: You can help prevent corrosion by following these guidelines:

pair.old: The best way to prevent corrosion is to keep your vehicle clean and free of corrosive materials. Attention to the underside of the vehicle is particularly important.
pair.new: The best way to prevent corrosion is to keep your vehicle clean and free of corrosive materials. Attention to the underbody of the vehicle is particularly important.

pair.old: If you live in a high-corrosion area where road salts are used, near the ocean, areas with industrial pollution, acid rain, etc.—, you should take extra care to prevent corrosion. In winter, hose off the underside of your vehicle at least once a month and be sure to clean the underside thoroughly when winter is over.
pair.new: If you live in a high-corrosion area where road salts are used, near the ocean, or in areas with industrial pollution, acid rain, and so on, you should take extra care to prevent corrosion. In winter, hose off the underbody of your vehicle at least once a month, and be sure to clean the underbody thoroughly when winter is over.

pair.old: When cleaning underneath the vehicle, give particular attention to the components under the fenders and other areas that are hidden from view. Do a thorough job; just dampening the accumulated mud rather than washing it away will accelerate corrosion rather than prevent it. Water under high pressure and steam are particularly effective in removing accumulated mud and corrosive materials.
pair.new: When cleaning the underbody of the vehicle, pay particular attention to the components under the fenders and other areas that are hidden from view. Do a thorough job; just dampening the accumulated mud rather than washing it away will accelerate corrosion rather than prevent it. Water under high pressure and steam are particularly effective in removing accumulated mud and corrosive materials.

pair.old: When cleaning lower door panels, rocker panels and frame members, be sure that drain holes are kept open so that moisture can escape and not be trapped inside to accelerate corrosion.
pair.new: When cleaning the lower door panels, rocker panels, and frame members, be sure to keep the drain holes open so that moisture can escape instead of staying trapped inside where it can accelerate corrosion.

pair.old: Don’t park your vehicle in a damp, poorly ventilated garage. This creates a favorable environment for corrosion. This is particularly true if you wash your vehicle in the garage or drive it into the garage when it is still wet or covered with snow, ice or mud. Even a heated garage can contribute to corrosion unless it is well ventilated so moisture is dispersed.
pair.new: Don’t park your vehicle in a damp, poorly ventilated garage. This creates a favorable environment for corrosion. This is particularly true if you wash your vehicle in the garage or drive it into the garage when it is still wet or covered with snow, ice, or mud. Even a heated garage can contribute to corrosion unless it is well ventilated enough to disperse moisture.

pair.old: Scratches or chips in the finish should be covered with “touch-up” paint as soon as possible to reduce the possibility of corrosion. If bare metal is showing through, the attention of a qualified body and paint shop is recommended.
pair.new: Scratches or chips in the finish should be covered with touch-up paint as soon as possible to reduce the possibility of corrosion. If bare metal shows through, the attention of a qualified body and paint shop is recommended.

pair.old: Bird droppings: Bird droppings are highly corrosive and may damage painted surfaces in just a few hours. Always remove bird droppings as soon as possible.
pair.new: Bird droppings are highly corrosive and may damage painted surfaces in just a few hours. Always remove bird droppings as soon as possible.

pair.old: Don’t neglect the interior
pair.new: Do not neglect the interior

pair.old: Moisture can collect under the floor mats and carpeting and cause corrosion. Check under the mats periodically to be sure the carpeting is dry. Use particular care if you carry fertilizers, cleaning materials or chemicals in the vehicle.
pair.new: Moisture can collect under the floor mats and carpeting and cause corrosion. Check under the mats periodically to be sure the carpeting is dry. Be particularly careful if you carry fertilizers, cleaning materials, or chemicals in the vehicle.

pair.old: These should be carried only in proper containers and any spills or leaks should be cleaned up, flushed with clean water and thoroughly dried.
pair.new: Only carry these materials in proper containers. Clean up any spills or leaks, then flush the carpeting with clean water and thoroughly dry it.

pair.old: Prevent chemicals such as perfume, cosmetic oil, sun cream, hand cleaner, and air freshener from contacting the interior parts because they may cause damage or discoloration. If they do contact the interior parts, wipe them off immediately. If necessary, use a mixture of warm water and mild non-detergent cleaner (test all cleaners on a concealed area before use).
pair.new: Prevent chemicals such as perfume, cosmetic oil, sun cream, hand cleaner, and air freshener from contacting the interior parts of the vehicle because they may cause damage or discoloration. If these chemicals do contact the interior parts, wipe them off immediately. If necessary, use a mixture of warm water and mild non-detergent cleaner (test all cleaners on a concealed area before use).

pair.old: Never allow water or other liquids to contact electrical/electronic components inside the vehicle as this may damage them.
pair.new: Never allow water or other liquids to contact electronic components inside the vehicle, as this may damage them.

pair.old: When cleaning leather products (steering wheel, seats etc.), use neutral detergents or low alcohol content solutions. If you use high alcohol content solutions or acid/alkaline detergents, the color of the leather may fade or the surface may get stripped.
pair.new: When cleaning leather products (such as the steering wheel or seat), use neutral detergents or solutions with low alcohol content. If you use high alcohol content solutions or acid or alkaline detergents, they may fade the color or strip the surface of the leather.

pair.old: Vacuum the seat periodically to remove dust and sand on the seat. It will prevent abrasion or damage of the leather and maintain its quality.
pair.new: Vacuum the seats periodically to remove dust and sand from them. This prevents abrasion or damage to the leather and maintains its quality.

pair.old: Wipe the leather seat cover often with dry or soft cloth.
pair.new: Wipe the leather seat cover often with a soft, dry cloth.

pair.old: Sufficient use of a leather protective may prevent abrasion of the cover and helps maintain the color. Be sure to read the instructions and consult a specialist when using leather coating or protective agents.
pair.new: Sufficient use of a leather protective may prevent abrasion of the cover and help maintain the color. Be sure to read the instructions and consult a specialist when using leather coating or protective agents.

pair.old: Leather with bright colors (beige, cream beige) is easily contaminated and clear in appearance. Clean the seats frequently.
pair.new: Light-colored leather (such as beige or cream) is more easily and visibly contaminated. Clean the seats frequently.

pair.old: Avoid wiping with a wet cloth. It may cause the surface to crack.
pair.new: Avoid wiping the leather seats with a wet cloth. It may cause the surface to crack.

pair.old: Remove all contaminants instantly. Refer to instructions below for removal of each contaminant.
pair.new: Remove all contaminants immediately. To remove different contaminants, refer to the following instructions:

pair.old: Clean the fabric seats regularly with a vacuum cleaner considering the fabric material. If they are heavily soiled with beverage stains, etc., use a suitable interior cleaner. To prevent damage to seat covers, wipe off the seat covers down to the seams with a large wiping motion and moderate pressure using a soft sponge or microfiber cloth.
pair.new: Clean the fabric seats regularly with a vacuum cleaner. If they are heavily soiled with stains from beverages or other sources, use a suitable interior cleaner. To prevent damage to the seat covers, use a soft sponge or microfiber cloth to wipe them down to the seams with large wiping motions.

pair.old: Remove dust and loose dirt from interior surfaces with a whisk broom or a vacuum cleaner. If necessary, clean interior surfaces with a mixture of warm water and mild non-detergent cleaner (test all cleaners on a concealed area before use).
pair.new: Remove dust and loose dirt from interior surfaces with a whisk broom or a vacuum cleaner. If necessary, clean the interior surfaces with a mixture of warm water and mild non-detergent cleaner (test all cleaners on a concealed area before use).

pair.old: Remove dust and loose dirt from fabric with a whisk broom or vacuum cleaner. Clean with a mild soap solution recommended for upholstery or carpets. Remove fresh spots immediately with a fabric spot cleaner. If fresh spots do not receive immediate attention, the fabric can stain and its color can be affected. Its fire-resistant properties can be reduced if the material is not properly maintained.
pair.new: Remove dust and loose dirt from the fabric with a whisk broom or vacuum cleaner. Clean with a mild soap solution recommended for upholstery or carpets. Remove fresh spots immediately with a fabric spot cleaner. If fresh spots do not receive immediate attention, the fabric can stain and its color can be affected. The fire-resistant properties of fabric can be reduced if you do not properly maintain the material.

pair.old: Clean the belt webbing with any mild soap solution recommended for cleaning upholstery or carpet. Follow the instructions provided with the soap. Do not bleach or re-dye the webbing because this may weaken it.
pair.new: Clean the webbing of the lap and shoulder belt portions of the seat belt with any mild soap solution recommended for cleaning upholstery or carpet. Follow the instructions provided with the soap. Do not bleach or redye the webbing because this may weaken it.

pair.old: Cleaning the interior window glass
pair.new: Cleaning the lap and shoulder belt webbing

pair.old: If the interior glass surfaces fog up (covered with an oily, greasy or waxy film), they should be cleaned with a glass cleaner. Follow the directions on the glass cleaner container.
pair.new: If the interior glass surfaces fog up or are covered with an oily, greasy, or waxy film, clean them with a glass cleaner. Follow the directions on the glass cleaner container.

pair.old: Do not scrape or scratch the inside of the rear window. This may result in damage of the rear window defroster grid.
pair.new: Do not scrape or scratch the inside of the rear window. This may damage the rear window defroster grid.

pair.old: The emission control system of your vehicle is covered by a written limited warranty. See the warranty information contained in the Warranty & Consumer Information manual in your vehicle.
pair.new: The emission control system of your vehicle is covered by a written limited warranty. See the warranty information contained in the Warranty and Consumer Information manual in your vehicle.

pair.old: Your vehicle is equipped with an emission control system to meet all applicable emission regulations. There are three emission control systems, as follows:
pair.new: Your vehicle is equipped with an emission control system to meet all applicable emission regulations. There are 3 emission control systems:

pair.old: To ensure proper function of the emission control systems, have your vehicle inspected and maintained by an authorized Kia dealer in accordance with the maintenance schedule in this manual.
pair.new: To ensure proper function of the emission control systems, have your vehicle inspected and maintained by an authorized Kia dealer according to the maintenance schedule in this manual.

pair.old: Caution for the Inspection and Maintenance Test (With Electronic Stability Control (ESC) system)
pair.new: Caution for the Inspection and Maintenance Test (with Electronic Stability Control (ESC) system)

old.b: To prevent the vehicle from misfiring during dynamometer testing, turn the ESC off by pressing the ESC switch.

new.b: To prevent the vehicle from misfiring during dynamometer testing, turn OFF the ESC system by pressing the ESC switch.

old.b: After dynamometer testing is complete, turn the ESC system back ON by pressing the ESC switch again.

new.b: After dynamometer testing is completed, turn the ESC back on by pressing the ESC switch again.

pair.old: The Positive Crankcase Ventilation (PCV) system is to prevent air pollution caused by blow-by gases being emitted from the crankcase. This system supplies fresh filtered air to the crankcase through the air intake hose. Inside the crankcase, the fresh air mixes with blow-by gases, which then pass through the PCV valve into the induction system.
pair.new: The Positive Crankcase Ventilation (PCV) system prevents air pollution caused by the crankcase emitting blow-by gases. This system supplies fresh filtered air to the crankcase through the air intake hose. Inside the crankcase, the fresh air mixes with blow-by gases, which then pass through the PCV valve into the induction system.

pair.old: The ORVR system is designed to prevent fuel vapors from escaping into the atmosphere. The ORVR system is designed to allow the vapors from the fuel tank to be loaded into a canister while refueling at the gas station, preventing the escape of fuel vapors into the atmosphere.
pair.new: The ORVR system prevents fuel vapors from escaping into the atmosphere. The ORVR system loads the vapors from the fuel tank into a canister while you refuel the vehicle at a gas station, preventing the escape of fuel vapors into the atmosphere.

pair.old: Fuel vapors generated inside the fuel tank are absorbed and stored in the onboard canister. When the engine is running, the fuel vapors absorbed in the canister are drawn into the surge tank through the Purge Control Solenoid Valve (PCSV)
pair.new: The onboard canister absorbs and stores fuel vapors generated inside the fuel tank. When the engine is running, the fuel vapors absorbed in the canister are drawn into the surge tank through the Purge Control Solenoid Valve (PCSV).

pair.old: The PCSV is controlled by the Engine Control Module (ECM). When the engine coolant temperature is low during idling, the PCSV closes so that evaporated fuel is not drawn into the engine. After the engine warms up during ordinary driving, the PCSV opens to introduce evaporated fuel to the engine.
pair.new: The Engine Control Module (ECM) controls the PCSV. When the engine coolant temperature is low while you are idling, the PCSV closes so that evaporated fuel is not drawn into the engine. After the engine warms up while you drive, the PCSV opens to introduce evaporated fuel to the engine.

pair.old: The exhaust emission control system is a highly effective system which controls exhaust emissions while maintaining good vehicle performance.
pair.new: The exhaust emission control system is a highly effective system that controls exhaust emissions while maintaining good vehicle performance.

pair.old: This vehicle should not be modified. Modification of your vehicle could affect its performance, safety or durability and may even violate governmental safety and emissions regulations. Damage or performance problems resulting from any modification may not be covered under your warranty.
pair.new: This vehicle should not be modified. Modification of your vehicle could affect its performance, safety, or durability, and may even violate governmental safety and emissions regulations. Damage or performance problems resulting from modifications may not be covered under your warranty.

pair.old: If you use unauthorized electronic devices, it may cause the vehicle to operate abnormally, wire damage, battery discharge and fire. For your safety, do not use unauthorized electronic devices.
pair.new: If you use unauthorized electronic devices, they may cause abnormal vehicle operation, wire damage, battery discharge, and fire. For your safety, do not use unauthorized electronic devices.

pair.old: Carbon monoxide (CO) can be present with other exhaust fumes. If you smell exhaust fumes inside your vehicle, have it inspected and repaired immediately. If you suspect exhaust fumes are entering your vehicle, drive it only with all the windows fully open. Have your vehicle checked and repaired immediately by an authorized kia dealer.
pair.new: Carbon monoxide (CO) can be present with other exhaust fumes. If you smell exhaust fumes inside your vehicle, have it inspected and have the issue repaired immediately. If you suspect exhaust fumes are entering your vehicle, only drive it with all of the windows fully open. Have your vehicle inspected and repaired immediately by an authorized Kia dealer.

pair.old: Engine exhaust gases contain CO. Though colorless and odorless, it is dangerous and could be lethal if inhaled. Follow the instructions on this page to avoid CO poisoning.
pair.new: Engine exhaust gases contain CO. Though colorless and odorless, CO is dangerous and could be lethal if inhaled. Follow the instructions in this section to avoid CO poisoning.

pair.old: Do not operate the engine in confined or closed areas (such as garages) more than necessary to move your vehicle in or out of the area.
pair.new: Do not operate the engine in a confined or enclosed area (such as a garage) more than necessary to move your vehicle in or out of the area.

pair.old: When your vehicle is stopped in an open area for more than a short time with the engine running, adjust the ventilation system as needed to draw in outside air.
pair.new: When you stop your vehicle in an open area for more than a short period of time with the engine running, adjust the ventilation system as needed to draw in outside air.

pair.old: Never sit in a parked or stopped vehicle for any extended time with the engine running.
pair.new: Never sit in a parked or stopped vehicle for any extended period of time with the engine running.

pair.old: When the engine stalls or fails to start, excessive attempts to restart the engine may cause damage to the emission control system.
pair.new: If the engine stalls or fails to start, excessive attempts to restart it may damage the emission control system.

pair.old: Do not park, idle or drive the vehicle over or near flammable objects, such as grass, vegetation, paper, leaves, etc. A hot exhaust system can ignite flammable items under your vehicle.
pair.new: Do not park, idle, or drive the vehicle over or near flammable objects, such as grass, vegetation, paper, leaves, and so on. A hot exhaust system can ignite flammable items under your vehicle.

pair.old: Do not remove the heat sink around the exhaust system, do not seal the bottom of the vehicle and do not coat the vehicle for corrosion control. They may present a fire risk under certain conditions.
pair.new: Do not remove the heat sink around the exhaust system, do not seal the bottom of the vehicle, and do not coat the vehicle for corrosion control. Doing so may present a fire risk under certain conditions.

pair.old: The following precautions must be observed:
pair.new: Take the following precautions:

pair.old: Do not misuse or abuse the engine. Examples of misuse are coasting with the ignition off and descending steep grades in gear with the ignition off.
pair.new: Do not misuse or abuse the engine. Examples of misuse are coasting with the ignition OFF and descending steep grades in gear with the ignition OFF.

pair.old: Do not operate the engine at high idle speed for extended periods (5 minutes or more).
pair.new: Do not operate the engine at high idle speeds for extended periods of time (5 minutes or more).

pair.old: Perchlorate Material-special handling may apply, See https://dtsc.ca.gov/perchlorate.
pair.new: Perchlorate Material-special handling may apply. For more information, see https://dtsc.ca.gov/perchlorate.

pair.old: Notice to California Vehicle Dismantlers: Perchlorate containing materials, such as air bag inflators, seatbelt pre-tensioners and keyless remote entry batteries, must be disposed of according to Title 22 California Code of Regulations Section 67384.10 (a).
pair.new: Notice to California Vehicle Dismantlers: You must dispose of perchlorate containing materials, such as air bag inflators, seat belt pretensioners, and keyless remote entry batteries, according to Title 22 California Code of Regulations Section 67384.10 (a).

pair.new: Dual clutch transmission (DCT) fluid

pair.old: Use only specified genuine automatic transmission fluid. The use of non-specified (even marked as compatible with genuine) could result in shift quality deterioration, vibrations and transmission failure. (Refer to )
pair.new: Use only specified genuine automatic transmission fluid. Using a non-specified transmission fluid (even if marked as compatible with genuine) could result in shift quality deterioration, vibration, and transmission failure. Refer to .

pair.old: SK ATF SP4M-1, MICHANG ATF SP4M-1, S-OIL ATF SP4M-1, Kia Genuine ATF SP4M-1
pair.new: SK ATF SP4M-1, MICHANG ATF SP4M-1, S-OIL ATF SP4M-1, or Kia Genuine ATF SP4M-1

pair.old: Different type of coolant or water may damage the electrical component.
pair.new: Different types of coolant, or water, may damage electrical components.

pair.old: An Phosphate based ethylene glycol based coolant
pair.new: Phosphate based ethylene glycol based coolant

pair.old: Always clean the area around any filler plug, drain plug, or dipstick before checking or draining any lubricant. This is especially important in dusty or sandy areas and when the vehicle is used on unpaved roads. Cleaning the plug and dipstick areas will prevent dirt and grit from entering the engine and other mechanisms that could be damaged.
pair.new: Always clean the area around any filler plug, drain plug, or dipstick before you check or drain any lubricant (especially in dusty or sandy areas or if you drive on unpaved roads). Cleaning the plug and dipstick areas prevents dirt and grit from entering the engine or other mechanisms that could be damaged.

pair.old: Engine oil viscosity (thickness) affects fuel economy and cold weather operation (engine start and engine oil flow ability). Lower viscosity engine oils can provide better fuel economy and cold weather performance. Higher viscosity engine oils are required for satisfactory lubrication in hot weather.
pair.new: Engine oil viscosity (thickness) affects fuel economy and cold weather operation (including engine starting and engine oil flow ability). Lower viscosity engine oils can provide better fuel economy and cold weather performance. Higher viscosity engine oils are required for satisfactory lubrication in hot weather.

pair.old: When choosing an oil, consider the range of temperature your vehicle will be operated in before the next oil change. Select the recommended oil viscosity from the chart.
pair.new: When you choose an engine oil, consider the range of temperature you will operate your vehicle in before your next oil change. Select the recommended oil viscosity from the chart below.

pair.old: An engine oil displaying this American Petroleum Institute (API) Certification Mark conforms to the International Lubricant Specification Advisory Committee (ILSAC). It is recommended to only use engine oils that uphold this API Certification Mark.
pair.new: The American Petroleum Institute (API) Certification Mark denotes engine oils that conform to the International Lubricant Specification Advisory Committee (ILSAC). You should only use engine oils marked with the API Certification Mark.

pair.old: The VIN is the number used in registering your vehicle and in all legal matters pertaining to its ownership, etc.
pair.new: Use your Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) to register your vehicle and in all legal matters pertaining to ownership.

pair.old: The number is punched on the floor under the front passenger’s seat. To check the number, open the cover.
pair.new: The VIN is punched into the floor underneath the passenger seat. To check the VIN, open the cover.

pair.old: The VIN is also on a plate attached to the top of the dashboard. The number on the plate can easily be seen through the windshield from outside.
pair.new: The VIN is also on a plate attached to the top of the dashboard. You can see the VIN on the plate through the windshield from outside.

pair.old: The vehicle certification label attached on the driver’s side center pillar gives the vehicle identification number (VIN).
pair.new: The vehicle certification label located on the center pillar on the driver side also states the VIN.

pair.old: The engine number is stamped on the engine block as shown in the drawing.
pair.new: The engine number is stamped on the engine block as shown in the illustration below:

pair.old: A compressor label informs you the type of compressor your vehicle is equipped with such as model, supplier part number, production number, refrigerant (1) and refrigerant oil (2).
pair.new: The air conditioner compressor label states which type of compressor your vehicle is equipped with, along with relevant information such as the model, supplier, part number, production number, refrigerant (1), and refrigerant oil (2).

pair.old: For more details, refer to .
pair.new: For more information, refer to .

pair.old: Roadside Assistance is provided on all new current model year Kia vehicles from the date the vehicle is delivered to the first retail buyer or otherwise put into use (inservice date), whichever is earlier, for a period of 60 months or 60,000 miles, whichever is earlier, subject to the terms, conditions and exclusions set forth in the Kia Warranty and Consumer Information Manual applicable to your model year vehicle.
pair.new: Roadside Assistance is provided with all new current model year Kia vehicles from the date the vehicle is delivered to its first retail buyer or otherwise put into use (in-service date), whichever is earlier, for a period of 60 months or 60,000 miles, whichever is earlier, subject to the terms, conditions, and exclusions set forth in the Kia Warranty and Consumer Information Manual applicable to your vehicle’s model year.

pair.old: Kia America, Inc. reserves the right to limit or deny services or other benefits to any owner or driver when, in Kia America, Inc.’s judgment, the claims and/or service requests are excessive in frequency or type of occurrence.
pair.new: Kia America, Inc. reserves the right to limit or deny services or other benefits to any owner or driver when, in Kia America, Inc.’s judgment, the claims or service requests are excessive in frequency or type of occurrence.

pair.old: Available from 5:00 AM to 6:00 PM PST, Monday through Friday and is accessible by dialing 1-800-333-4542.
pair.new: Toll free consumer assistance is available from 5:00 AM to 6:00 PM PST Monday through Friday. Call 1-800-333-4542 to access toll free consumer assistance.

pair.old: For more information regarding available assistance, please refer to your Kia Warranty & Consumer Information Manual.
pair.new: For more information regarding available assistance, refer to your Kia Warranty and Consumer Information Manual.

pair.old: Available 24 hours a day, 365 days a year and is accessible by dialing 1-800-333-4542 or by pressing the RSA button (when enrolled into Kia Connect).
pair.new: Emergency roadside assistance is available 24 hours a day, 365 days a year and is accessible by calling 1-800-333-4542 or pressing the RSA button (if enrolled in Kia Connect).

pair.old: Please note that you must provide your Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) to verify coverage at the time of your call. The VIN can be found on the dashboard at the driver’s side, on the door jamb, your vehicle’s registration or proof of insurance card.
pair.new: You must provide your Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) to verify coverage when you call. Find your VIN on the driver side of the dashboard, the door jamb, your vehicle registration, or your proof of insurance card.

pair.old: Kia utilizes a network of over 30,000 roadside assistance providers. Should you accidentally run out of fuel, require a battery jump, or need help changing a tire, a Kia Roadside Assistance Representative will dispatch someone to deliver a small quantity of gas, change a flat tire with your inflated spare, or arrange a battery jump to allow you to proceed to your destination. We have access to a network of over 10,000 locksmiths should you become locked out of your Kia.
pair.new: Kia utilizes a network of over 30,000 roadside assistance providers. Should you accidentally run out of fuel, require a battery jump, or need help changing a tire, a Kia Roadside Assistance Representative will dispatch someone to deliver a small quantity of gas, change a flat tire with your inflated spare, or arrange a battery jump to allow you to proceed to your destination. We also have access to a network of over 10,000 locksmiths should you be locked out of your vehicle.

pair.old: Roadside Assistance benefits are not available for any Kia vehicle that has ever been or should have been issued a “salvage” title or similar “branded” title under any state’s law or has been declared a “total loss” or equivalent by a financial institution or insurance company.
pair.new: Roadside Assistance benefits are not available for any Kia vehicle that has ever been or should have been issued a “salvage” title or similar “branded” title under any state’s law or has been declared a “total loss” or equivalent by a financial institution or insurance company.

pair.old: Trip interruption expense benefits are provided if a warranty-related disablement occurs more than 150 miles from your home, and the repairs require more than 24 hours to complete. Reasonable reimbursement is included for meals, lodging, or rental vehicle expenses. Trip interruption coverage is limited to $100 per day subject to a three-day maximum limit per incident. You must contact the Kia Roadside Assistance Center to obtain pre-authorization of expenses. Once the Kia Roadside Assistance Center gives authorization for trip interruption benefits, they will assist you in making the necessary arrangements. Insurance deductibles, expenses, and claims paid by your insurance company or other providers are not eligible for reimbursement.
pair.new: Trip interruption expense benefits are provided if a warranty-related disablement occurs more than 150 miles from your home, and the repairs require more than 24 hours to complete. Reasonable reimbursement is included for meals, lodging, or rental vehicle expenses. Trip interruption coverage is limited to $100 per day subject to a 3-day maximum limit per incident. You must contact the Kia Roadside Assistance Center to obtain preauthorization of expenses. Once the Kia Roadside Assistance Center gives authorization for trip interruption benefits, they will assist you in making the necessary arrangements. Insurance deductibles, expenses, and claims paid by your insurance company or other providers are not eligible for reimbursement.

pair.old: If you plan to register your vehicle in a foreign country, you should confirm that it conforms to the regulations in that country. Even if you successfully register the vehicle in a foreign country, you may experience the following problems and should therefore consider the possibility of having to deal with them:
pair.new: If you plan to register your vehicle in a foreign country, make sure it conforms to the regulations in that country. Even if you successfully register your vehicle in a foreign country, consider the possibility of having to deal with the following problems:

pair.old: The fuel specified for your vehicle may be unavailable. If other than the specified fuel is used, it could cause damage to the engine, fuel injection system, and other fuel-related parts which may not be covered under your New Vehicle Emissions Limited Warranty.
pair.new: The specified fuel for your vehicle may be unavailable. Using other fuels could cause damage to the engine, fuel injection system, and other fuel related parts, which may not be covered under your New Vehicle Emissions Limited Warranty.

pair.old: There may not be an Authorized Kia Dealer in the area in which you plan to register your vehicle. This can cause trouble in obtaining services in a foreign country for any number of reasons.
pair.new: There may not be an authorized Kia dealer local to where you plan to register your vehicle, which can cause trouble in obtaining services for any number of reasons.

pair.old: We cannot assume any responsibility for problems that result from unsatisfactory service or lack of service outside of the United States.
pair.new: Kia America, Inc. cannot assume any responsibility for problems caused by unsatisfactory service or lack of service outside of the United States.

pair.old: Electrical Equipment (U.S. only)
pair.new: Electrical equipment (U.S. only)

pair.old: The electrical system of your vehicle is designed to perform under all reasonably expected operating conditions.
pair.new: The electrical system of your vehicle performs under all reasonably expected operating conditions.

pair.old: Before any additional electrical equipment is installed in your vehicle, consult an Authorized Kia Dealer, to ensure that you do not void your warranty.
pair.new: Before installing any additional electrical equipment in your vehicle, consult an authorized Kia dealer to make sure you don’t void your warranty.

pair.old: Certain electrical equipment, or the way in which it is installed, may adversely affect the operation of your vehicle, including such systems as the engine control system, audio system and electrical charging system and potentially void all or part of your warranty.
pair.new: Certain electrical equipment, or the way it is installed, may adversely affect vehicle operation (including systems such as engine control system, audio, and electrical charging) and potentially void all or part of your warranty.

pair.old: We assume no responsibility for any expense you may incur or for any malfunction of your vehicle or any of its components or systems that result from the installation of additional electrical equipment that is not supplied, or recommended for installation by, Kia.
pair.new: Kia America, Inc. assumes no responsibility for any expense you may incur or any malfunction of your vehicle, its components, or its systems resulting from the installation of additional electrical equipment that is not supplied or recommended for installation by Kia.

pair.old: If a mobile two-way radio system is installed improperly, or if an excessively powerful type of system is used, other electronic systems may be adversely affected. To avoid damage to your vehicle, consult an Authorized Kia Dealer concerning the proper equipment and installation.
pair.new: Improper installation of a mobile two-way radio system or installation of an excessively powerful mobile two-way radio system may adversely affect other electronic systems. To avoid damaging your vehicle, consult an authorized Kia dealer on proper equipment and installation.

pair.old: Read and follow all directions in this Owner’s Manual, particularly the information under the headings “NOTICE”, “CAUTION” and “WARNING”.
pair.new: Read and follow all directions in this owner’s manual, particularly information under the headings “NOTICE,” “CAUTION,” and “WARNING.”

pair.old: If, after reading this manual, you have any questions regarding the operation of your vehicle, safety issues and defects, please contact your Kia’s toll-free Consumer Assistance hot line as below:
pair.new: If you have any questions regarding the operation of your vehicle, safety issues, or defects after reading this manual, please contact Kia’s toll free Consumer Assistance hot line:

pair.old: The following publications are available on www.KiaTechinfo.com.
pair.new: The following publications are available at www.KiaTechinfo.com:

pair.old: This manual covers maintenance and recommended procedures for repair to engine and chassis components. It is written for the Journeyman mechanic, but is simple enough for most mechanically inclined owners to understand.
pair.new: This manual covers maintenance and recommended procedures for repairing engine and chassis components. This manual is written for the journeyman mechanic, but is simple enough for most mechanically inclined owners to understand.

pair.old: This manual complements the Service Manual by providing in-depth troubleshooting information for each electrical circuit in your vehicle.
pair.new: This manual is a companion to the Service Manual and provides in-depth troubleshooting information for each electrical circuit in your vehicle.

pair.old: This manual describes the safety features, overall features and operating procedures for the vehicle.
pair.new: This manual describes the safety features, overall features, and operating procedures of your vehicle.
